abaita / aboita, interj. (U), expr. surprise coupled with indignation.

aboita, see abaita.
aca [aaca], part. (U), (expr. negation) no; ~ noguo (nĩguo), indeed there is no doubt about it; ~-ni, (said to more than one person) certainly not!; cf. arĩ, naari.
rũacũ, n. (11), 1. chip, shiver, splinter; cf. rokaracũ, rũcacũ. 2. thin wand as used for partition; see 3. long piece of split sugarcane suitable for children; cf. rabũa. [see acũka]
acũhia, see icũhia,

(I1), (of foodstuffs) be just right, be in good condition for eating, be crisp, ripe, mellow, &c.; (of sugar-cane) be succulent, crisp, easily broken, ripe; (of beverages) taste well, be mellow; (of bread) be light, wel! cooked; (of soil) be dry, friable, in good condition for digging; cf. ondoka. acaacaka, dim. acũkanga, diff. acũgkanĩra, assoc. acũkia, caus.; kabura kau nĩko gaacũkĩtie igwa, that light rain has mellowed the sugar-cane. acũra, v.t. 1. make just right; make crisp, ripe, mellow; arĩa maanj~ ga, those who were giving me pleasure. 2., make (soil) dry, cracked.
-ag-!, v. infix, indic. the habitual aspect of an action in past, present, or future time ; nĩmaathom~a, they were regular students (in the past).

FULL_ENTRY: -ag-?, v. root extension, indic. an action that takes place while sth. else is happening; rut~a wĩra, get on with your work, we are going to leave you; thi~a, go on, I will follow.

aga, v.t. & i. (11), 1. lack, miss, fail to find; be in need of; be missing; ~ mwĩkĩre, be at a loss to know what to do; ~ tha (bata, kĩene, rũbutu), see under tha, &c.; ~ mat, see under matũ ; ~ thoni see under thoni; ~ mũndũ, give no consideration to sb. ; ignore, treat with contempt. 2. (used as aux. v. in neg. sentences) kw~ kuura irio itikũra, if it does not rain, the crops will not grow; kw~ Ũguo failing this I shall not go; cf. rega, tiga. agaaga, dim. aganga, diff. agana, rec. miss one another, fail to meet; treat each other contemptuously., Jtina na thĩ iti~ga, the buttocks and the ground cannot miss one another for ever (i.e. one is bound to come to rest some time). aganira, rec. app. agia, caus. make miss, cause not to have or find sth., deprive sth.; ~ gĩkaro, cause not to have an abode, rest, peace. fagia, caus. refl. deprive oneself of; ~ hinya, wear oneself out. agithia, d. caus. cause sb. to lack or miss sth., deprive sb. of sth.; ~ mũndũ gĩtĩo, cause a person to miss esteem, belittle. agĩka, st. pot. agĩra?, app. fail to find for; be without sth. for or at. refi. app.; ~ bata, feel worthless.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (only used in compound nouns) one who lacks
FULL_ENTRY: mwaga!, a-, n. (v), (only used in compound nouns) one who lacks: x. ~-hinya, weak, powerless, person. 2. ~-irĩ, (term of abuse) one without possessions or progeny; cf. ni-iri. 3. ~-thoni, one who lacks modesty, ill-mannered, disrespectful, person; cf. mũra-thoni. 4. ~-tha, one without compassion, humane feelings, pity. waga, n. cl. 14 (only in compounds): 1. ~-hinya, weakness. 2. ~-thoni, shamelessness, immodesty, rudeness, lack of manners; cf. riko. 3. ~-tha, mercilessness, callousness. [aga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (11), I1. a rack. 2. a gap
FULL_ENTRY: rũaga, (ndwaga), n. (11), I1. a rack. 2. a gap; ~-inĩ, empty, uninhabited country. kĩaga'!, (ci-), n. aug. of above.
kĩagaga [kĩagaaga], n. cl. 7 (vrr), uncertainty. [agaga]

FULL_ENTRY: waganu, mawaganu, n, (1V), I. vice, wickedness, naughtiness. 2. offence against custom, tradition, &c.;. lawlessness. [agana]

NEW_VALUE: transgress, go beyond the bounds of
FULL_ENTRY: agarara, v.t. (II), xĩ. transgress, go beyond the bounds of; pass over, miss out (by. oversight or inten

Kahiga gatiagararagwo nĩ man, an old stone is never passed over by water (the advice of the old is never overlooked). agaagarara, dim. agararanga, diff. agararana, rec. step, jump, over one another, others; transgress against others, one another. agararanira, app. rec. agararaniria, caus. app. rec. lift (person, things) over sth. together. agararia, caus. lift over sth., cause to pass, jump, over; pass sth. over; send, throw, over. agararithia, d. caus. assist sb. to go over the top of sth., &c. agararika, st. pot. agararira, app. go over the top of sth., at, because of. agarariria, caus. app.

FULL_ENTRY: mwage, mi-, n. (I1), I. small tree (Oncoba routledgei). 2. wild gourd plant (Sphaerosicyos); cf. (N.K.) mũhũri, mũigaigua. rĩage, ma-; kiage, ci-; n. 1. fruit of the above, the size of a small orange, made into snuff bottles; ball. 2. a small wild gourd which bears prolifically. ŨOrociara ta maage, (a nuptial blessing) may you be as fruitful as wild gourd plants.

(11) be good, nice, becoming (in quality or appearance), be goodlooking, look well, be suitable; be in order. agaagira, dim. improve, be, become, a little better. agaagiria, caus. dim. make still better. agiranga, diff. aganirĩra, inten. rec. be mutually becoming, suited, to each other; match; be agreeable to one another, get on well together. agiria, caus. make good, nice, right; make suitable, becoming; put right, cause to look fine, well, pretty, beautiful. ragiria, refl. caus. pretend to be good; ingratiate oneself. agĩrithia, d. caus. have sth. made suitable, good, &c. fagĩrithia, refl. d. caus. make oneself appear becoming; ingratiate oneself; cf. ĩcukumithia. st. pot. agĩrĩra, app. be fitting, suitable, seemly; suit, fit (sb., sth.); be suitable to, for; be convenient, becoming to (sb., sth.); nĩkwagĩrĩre gwĩkaga nguo, it behoves (us, &c.) to be doing that. agirirũo, pass. app. (esp. in past tense with perfect) ought; kũr? maũndũa twagĩrĩrao nĩ gwĩka, there are things we ought to do. agirĩria, caus. app. make good, suitable, fitting, for, at.
wagiriru, n. cl. 14 (X), fitness, suitability, rightness, appropriateness. lagira]
wagiru, n. cl, 14 (1v), niceness, beauty, goodness, prettiness. [agira]

aha, v.i. & t. (11), 1. gape, be agape for; (of dogs, &c.) hold mouth open (to snap at flies); ~ rohuho, (of door) be wide open to the breeze. 2. (of people) seek food when starving, look for sth. to eat or drink; athuuri acio matindaga makĩ~ njohi, those elders spend the whole day hunting for drink. 3. take a snack; cf. ĩgagũra, ĩtua nyoni, 4. yawn, swallow up, engulf. 5. (of river) overflow, spread out and engulf. ahaaha, dim. ahaabũra, conv. dim. ahahnũnka, neut. dim. ahanga, diff. ahanira, app. rec. go out together in search of. ahaniria, caus. app. rec. search for sth. together with sth. else. ahia, caus. cause to engage in greedy or acquisitive quest; uuma kw~ nda ĩno where have you been seeking to fill this maw of yours?; ~ maitho rũgongo rũrĩa, cast your eyes over yonder ridge. ahithia, d. caus. help sb. to get a snack; assist sth, to acquire sth. ahĩka, st. pot.; thaa igũkinya hau food is unobtainable at this hour of the day. ahĩkika, d. st. pot. ahĩkĩra, app. st. pot. ahira, app. get a snack for; ndwahĩre arata aku, get sth. to eat for your friends. tahira, refl. app.; tiga kw~, rorania na cianal, enough of filling your belly, attend to the children now! ahĩria, caus. app. appropriate, make free with, filch (food, beer, &c., meant for others), intercept what is passed round. ahũra, conv. 1r. break off (branch or twig at bifurcation); divide up, strip off (bananas from a bunch), wrench off (a limb from a sheep carcase); cf. kahũra, kohora. 2. make a fork, diversion, branch, path, or by-pass leading off an existing one. 3. take in an additional area of uncultivated land to enlarge a garden. ahũranga, diff. conv. ahũrana, rec. conv. ahũrania, caus. rec. conv, strip apart, wrench into separate parts. ahũranĩra, app. rec. conv. ahũrithia, d. caus. cony. ahũrĩka, st. pot. conv. ahũrira, app. conv. ahũririra, inten. app. cony, ahũka, neut. 1. (of branch or twig at bifurcation) break off; (of banana from a bunch, &c.) split, fall off ; kĩmwe gĩkĩ~ kĩama-inĩ, one group seceded from the Council ; cf. kohoka. 2. branch off, turn off, change direction at a fork in a path; (N.K.) make way, stand on one side; cf. ebũka, kerũra. ahũkanga, diff. neut. ahũkana, rec, neut. be divided, split off, fork, become split off (as water encountering a barrier). ahũkanira, assoc. neut. ahũkika, st. pot. neut. ahũkĩra, app. neut. branch off, at, because of; get split off and fall on to.
aha, interj. (1), expr. disgust, disapproval, disappointment, or annoyance.
mwaha, n. cl. 3 only (111); -a ~, odd single; cf. mwaga. [aha]

maaha?, n. cl. 6 only (111), 1. products of progeny, offspring; roaga ~, may you lack children to your name; e na ~ maingi, he has plenty of resources (personal or material). 2. snack; cf. kandũ. 3. gratuity (usually in the form of food given over and above a payment; cf. mbacici. 4. bifurcation; a branched yam tuber. mwaka, mi-, n, (111), 1. year; ~ wa mĩrongo ĩrĩ, yo920. 2. season; the names of the main divisions and their order vary according to altitude and local vagaries of climate; those set out here follow approximately chronological order where there are two growing seasons; see dictionary entries. 1. early part of the year: mũgaa, kagaa, kĩrnjangwe, mwaniathenge. 2. March to May: mbura-yanjahĩ, mũringo, kĩhu, gĩthananwa, mũgira-njara. 3. June to August: gathaatn, muoria-nyoni, gaciciũ, gathano, (N.K.) gathano-komũ. 4. September to December: mbura-ya-mwere, mũringo, drera-hũngũ, kanya-hũngũ, kĩya-kia-mũhĩa (mwere), kayakarongo, kĩhu, gĩthananwa, mũratho, njakoira, thano, themithũ, gatumu (tumu); cf. kĩmera, hĩndĩ, kahinda.

NEW_VALUE: take up, unpeg anything pegged down
FULL_ENTRY: amba!, v.t. (1), I. stretch, peg, out; ~ hema, pitch tent; ~ mũti igũrũ, crucify; ~ nyambo, plant sharpened stakes for defence, or in a game pit; ~ mathagu, (of birds) spread wings when at rest. 2. cast on stitches (in knitting, weaving). famba, refi. ambaamba, dim. ambanga, diff. ambana, rec. (formerly, with ref. to ituika ceremony) peg anti-social persons to ground covering them with heaps of dry grass and rubbish prior to burning them to death. ambanira, app. rec. ambanĩria, assoc. ambithia, d. caus, help to stretch, peg out. ambika, st. pot. ambira, app. ambũra, conv. 1. take up, unpeg anything pegged down; ~ kick a football high in the air. 2. undo (knitting, wearing apparel, &c.). ambũrũka, neut. conv. move in a stream at a fast pace; stream out, along. ambũrũrũka, neut. inten. spring up as in anger or threateningly, break out into angry resentful speech; be stirred up. ambũka, neut. get unpegged; become undone. ambarara, stc. inten. 1. stretch oneself up, stand on tiptoe; sit up, be erect. 2. be tilted up, be raised at one end or side; cf. hang’arara, andatara. 3. (of snakes, &c.) rear up, bridle up. 4. fare up, take offence. ambararia, caus. inten. I. raise up, elevate; ~ moko!, hands up! 2. cause to take offence. rambararia, refl. caus. inten. 1. draw oneself up, assume erect attitude, rear up, take offence. ambarũrũka, inten.; see ambata, ten. go up, come up, climb up; ~ ngoro, feel a rising of emotion, indignation; cf. thang’ata, haica. ambaambata, dim. ten. go or come up a little farther, rise a little, go on upwards, ambatia, caus, ten, take, bring, upwards, cause to rise, raise, hoist. ambatira, app. ten. go up at, along or through; go or come up higher, move upwards, raise in station, be promoted; ~ gacĩra gaka, go up along this path. ambatiria, caus. app. ten. ambatirira, inten, ten. ascend, climb up by, upon, or into; climb right up to the top. ambatirĩria, caus. inten. ten.

amba?, v.t. & i. (1), 1. be the first, start, begin; no nii nyambire ho, I was the first on the spot. Ndĩ~ga na magua, (the bee) does not begin with honeycombs. Thugo nĩcio ci~ga ceremonial gifts form the preliminary to marriage arrangements, 2. (freq. as aux. v. foll. by inf. or subj.) (do) first; wambire gũtema?, who made the first cut?; ~ first go. ambaamba, dim. 1. appear as if about to begin sth.; fidget, be impatient; (of birds) raise wings up and CESEN a am S I 3 SU down without leaving the ground; cf. amba mathagu. 2. heave, rise and fall (as in breathing). ambaambia, caus. dim. ; nĩ kĩ kĩrakw~?, what is worrying you? ambana, diff. ambana, rec, (esp. of quarrel, fight) start to do sth. to others, each other; start a row, go at each other. ambanirira, app. rec. begin to do sth. to others. ambanirĩria, assoc. begin together; begin on, start with. ambia, caus. be the first to do sth., begin (with) ~ drio, be the first to taste food. ambio, pass. Ka mwene nĩko kaambagio ira, it is his own child that the householder should decorate first with white chalk (at initiation). Mwarĩ wa magego ndaambagio mũcũ, a girl from a home where meat is eaten often should not be taken as a first wife. ambĩka, st. pot. used inten., start, initiate, a piece of work; make a beginning (on a piece of weaving). ambĩkwo, pass. st. pot.; kĩnngano gĩkĩ~ na mahoya, the meeting was opened with prayer. ambikira, app. st. pot. begin to do sth., at, for, in. ambira, app. start to do sth., for, at; initiate (a piece of knitting, weaving, &c.) for. ambirĩra, inten. begin a succession of acts, begin to do sth. first; begin with, on, at; ngw~ mũti ũyũ, I am going to begin with this tree (with others to come); nĩwe waambĩrĩre kũrĩma kagongo gaka?, were you the first to cultivate this bit of ridge? ambirĩria, caus. inten. begin, start off; start up (a car, &c.).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one of the four main rafters of a traditional round hut, erected to support the double ring of sticks at its top into which the upper ends of the closely set rafters are thrust
NEW_VALUE: minute spines, prickles, stings on sugar-cane leaves, nettles, caterpillars, which irritate the skin
FULL_ENTRY: nwamba!, mĩ-, n. (1), one of the four main rafters of a traditional round hut, erected to support the double ring of sticks at its top into which the upper ends of the closely set rafters are thrust; cf. (N.K.) motheco, nwamba?, mi-, n. (1), (coll.) a tough guy; cf. mici. ũamba, marũamba, n. (vii), 1. minute spines, prickles, stings on sugar-cane leaves, nettles, caterpillars, which irritate the skin. 2. a plant (Hibiscus diversifolius), wild cotton (rough with small prickles; produces natural rope). kĩrũamba, i-, n. I1. aug. of 1. above. 2. flower of 2. above. see mũimbaigũrũ.
maambarita, n. cl. 6 only, (1), 1. interior of roof; cf. mĩtirĩro. 2. space between wicker-work of grain store and its thatched roof.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: upward slope, rise in the ground.
NEW_VALUE: upward slope, rise in the ground
FULL_ENTRY: mwambatiriro, mi-, n. (1), upward slope, rise in the ground. [amba']

ORIGINAL_VALUE: the first man to settle in an area. rĩambi, ma-, n. the thin layer of unused cells outside an actual honeycomb. kĩambi, ci-, n. first (of a number of things, of a batch, series, &c.)
NEW_VALUE: the first man to settle in an area
FULL_ENTRY: mwambi, a-, n. (1), the first man to settle in an area. rĩambi, ma-, n. the thin layer of unused cells outside an actual honeycomb. kĩambi, ci-, n. first (of a number of things, of a batch, series, &c.); cf. kĩambĩko; see nyakiambi. [amba?]
mwambia, a-, n. (11), one who begins (e.g. in the spear-throwing game). ~ nacio tiwe mOrigia nacio, the one who begins things is not the one who ends up with them. [amba?]
riambĩko, ma-, n. (1), place, time, of commencement. kiambiko, ci-, n. point of commencement (of a piece of work, construction of article, weaving of string-bag, &c.). [amba?]
kĩambiro, ci-, n. (1), 1. commencement; beginning of a matter, case, project. 2. dealing with, doing of, a piece of work. [amba?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. a peg-stretched hide. 2. vertebra with meat attached, esp. one of the bones of the neck
FULL_ENTRY: kiambo, ci-, n. (1), 1. a peg-stretched hide. 2. vertebra with meat attached, esp. one of the bones of the neck; ~ kĩa section of vertebrae given to the elder to whom this part of the animal was allocated at a meat feast; ~ kĩa mũthaci, section of vertebrae given to the man who cuts up the vertebral column; ~ lines in order ready for the dance; hũra ~, prepare lines ready for the dance. [amba"]

NEW_VALUE: sharpened stick, tent peg, peg used in spreading out hides and skins to dry
FULL_ENTRY: rũambo, nyambo | maambo, n. (1), 1. sharpened stick, tent peg, peg used in spreading out hides and skins to dry. ~ ndarnũa, one peg cannot peg out an ox-hide. 2. hole in a hide in which a peg is put. Maitho ma arũme ti maambo, men’s eyes are not empty peg holes. [amba]

maambura, n. cl, 6 (1), 1. ceremonial accompanying certain ‘rites de passage’; sacred rituals (including coition between the father and the mother of the homestead concerned) connected with the normal events of family life, birth, second birth, puberty, initiation, circumcision, marriage, performed under the direction of senior elders or old women, supplemented at circumcision rites by the mnd mũgo wa ~; ruta ~, perform the rituals. Gĩkũ ti ~, death is not a happy family occasion (since the bereaved may not mix with others until after the completion of the death-cleansing rites). See igongona. 2. (N.K.) dance of young men and women, boys and girls, on the day before a circumcision; cf. matuumo. 3. menstrual period (during which none of above rites could be performed); ona ~, menstruate; oera mwana ~, (of parents) perform ceremony for a girl at her first menstruation; cf. mĩruru, mweri, ĩnenũra, ratha.,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cry out (as in pain, fear, despair)
FULL_ENTRY: ana, v.i. (1), cry out (as in pain, fear, despair); roar, scream, moan, yell; ũkũ~ wanel, you'll yell (for help) in vain! anaana, dim. ananga, diff. ananira, app. rec. ananiria, caus. app. rec. (of animals) low, bleat, for each other. ania!, caus. (used inten.) cry out; (of animals) low, bleat, cry. anithia, d. caus. 1. make bleat, low; make cry out. 2. give sharp thrashing. 3. sound, blow, ring (bugle, horn, bell); ~ mũcarica, crack a whip; ~ mbu, sound the alarm (by yelling). anika, st. pot. anĩra, app. cry out, roar, yell, at, because of. aniria, caus. app. (of animals) bleat, low, cry for, at; long for. anirira, inten. shout, cry out, cry out with a long and sustained call (as to sb. at a distance); announce, proclaim; ~ kĩhikanio, put up the banns. anũra, conv. 1. make cry out, yell, loudly. 2. give a very sharp thrashing; strike (esp. with a whip). 3. crack (whip) loudly. 4. (of animals) howl, bellow, bleat incessantly. 5. make (bugles, horns, &c.) blare. anũka, neut. 1. go on screaming, yelling. 2. howl; blare.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. larva of bee in honeycomb. 2. part of comb containing larvae
FULL_ENTRY: (rĩ-) maana, n. (111), 1. larva of bee in honeycomb. 2. part of comb containing larvae; see mwana.

NEW_VALUE: progeny of human beings, young ones, descendants
FULL_ENTRY: mwana', ci-, n. cl, 1/8 (11), 1. child, baby, young human being; young girl, girl of any age; ~ wa Ngai, child of God, child born with one limb missing; cf. kĩũgn; ka kũragitwo ~ wa Ngai?, (said when rain has gone on all day without sign of stopping) can it be that someone has killed Godd’s child?; ~ wa ira, child of yesterday, lacking in experience; ~ no ta ũrĩa ũngĩ, one child is just as (dear as) another; ~ aakuire nyina agĩkima, (said when asking for a taste) a child died while his mother was mashing food; see maũnduri. 2. (cl. 8) progeny of human beings, young ones, descendants; ciana cia (Gĩkũyũ na) Mũmbi, (children of G. and M.) the Kikuyu tribe. krana?, ci-, n. (mainly of animals) young; see ciana above. kĩmwana, i-, n. young fellow, recently initiated youth (not a full member of the young warrior group); (coll. among young men) mate, buddy. kaana, tw-, n. (a term of endearment) lass, lad, young one, small child; ~ ka rnyondo (rũkenge), baby. kamwana, tũ-, see kĩmwana above. wana, n. cl. I4, childhood; rĩa ~, see under ria.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: x1. appear youthful, renew one’s youth, become rejuvenated
NEW_VALUE: appear youthful, renew one’s youth, become rejuvenated
FULL_ENTRY: anakaha, v.i. (11), x1. appear youthful, renew one’s youth, become rejuvenated; (of a patched garment) be renovated; (of an coat) be renewed. 2. adorn oneself with war or fighting gear; cf. anaanakaha, dim. anakahanga, diff. anakahania, caus. rec. make each other, others, appear youthful; adorn others. anakahanira, assoc, 1. become youthful together. 2. put on fighting gear to use against each other. anakahia, caus. make youthful, adorn; rejuvenate; renew, renovate. ranakahia, refl. caus. restore oneself to a youthful appearance, adorn oneself. anakahika, st. pot. anakahira, app. become youthful in appearance, at, for; adorn oneself with fighting gear because of.
-anake, a. (11) youthful, lively, vigorous; cf. -ithi. [see anakaha]
kianana [kĩanaana], ci-, young, immature person; halfling. [ana]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: spoiling, destruction. [ananga]. nwanangiko, mĩ-, n. (111), spoiling, ruining, destruction, defacement, disfigurement. [ananga] nwananirio, mi-, n. (1), (of many beasts) bleating, lowing. [ana] nwani, n. (111), 1. term of affection used by women
NEW_VALUE: term of affection used by women
FULL_ENTRY: vanangi, n. cl. 14 (11), spoiling, destruction. [ananga]. nwanangiko, mĩ-, n. (111), spoiling, ruining, destruction, defacement, disfigurement. [ananga] nwananirio, mi-, n. (1), (of many beasts) bleating, lowing. [ana] nwani, n. (111), 1. term of affection used by women ; mwari wa ~, my dear. 2. greeting between women one of whom has a son married to the other’s daughter. 3. wa-~, nickname for hyena. nia?, v.t, (1), hold out, prepare (sth.) in readiness to receive; ~ moko, hold out your hands; ~ kanua, open your mouth (for sth. to be put in); cf. ara!. ananiria, caus. app. rec. hold out (hands, &c.) together. anithia, d. caus. anirĩria, inten. hold out sth. to catch sth, falling; ~ maĩ mũberethiinĩ na gĩkombe, put a cup under the tap to receive water.
mwania-thenge!, nĩ-; kaaniathenge, tw-; n. (1-1), shrub (Giant St. John’s Wort) (Hypericum roeperianum) (wood light, used for carving goat-bells).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: hang, place, a beehive on a tree.
NEW_VALUE: hang, place, a beehive on a tree
FULL_ENTRY: anĩka?, v.t. (1), hang, place, a beehive on a tree.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: that which spreads in the sun.
NEW_VALUE: that which spreads in the sun
FULL_ENTRY: -anĩki, a, (111), that which spreads in the sun. [anĩka!]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: place where clothes can be spread to dry.
NEW_VALUE: place where clothes can be spread to dry
FULL_ENTRY: wanĩko!, mawanĩko, n. (111), place where clothes can be spread to dry. [anika!]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: place where beehive is (or can be) hung or placed.
NEW_VALUE: place where beehive is (or can be) hung or placed
FULL_ENTRY: waniko?, mawaniko, n. (x1), place where beehive is (or can be) hung or placed. [anika?]
kiano, ci-, n. (1), 1. shaft of arrow; cf. mũgui, rcthuthi. 2. ikia na ~, (of a special ceremony involving the shooting of an arrow without a head into the bush) disown, outlaw; cf. ikta na kĩbnikũ.
mwano? | (mwana?), mi-, n. (1), (of a beer-addict, &c.) craving. [ana]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: flatten out, curve in or out (by beating or pressure)
FULL_ENTRY: anda, v.t. (11), flatten out, curve in or out (by beating or pressure); pat (small chapatties) in the hand, compress ; cause to feel heavy or oppressed ; ~ na nda ya belabour sb. with the flat of a sword; ~ kĩmere, rub down coarse mash in making gruel. andwo, pass.; ~ nĩ irio, be oppressed by a surfeit of food; ~ nĩ mai, be oppressed by a desire to defecate. tanda, refl. fill oneself out with food; see itatha. andaanda, dim. 1. beat, pound lightly. 2. (of a child, weak person, &c.) speak brokenly, with difficulty. andaandũra, conv. dim. make a little bigger. andaandũka, neut. dim. be a little older. andanga, diff. andana, rec., andanira, app. rec. andaniria, caus. app. rec. andithia, d. caus. have flattened out; cause to be oppressed, help to belabour with the flat of a sword. iandithia, refl. be choked by food; be belaboured (by a sword, &c.) through one’s own fault; pretend to be oppressed. andika!, st. pot. andira, app. andũra, conv. enlarge, expand, make bigger. andũka, neut. be, become enlarged, expanded, or bigger in size; (of field, river, skin pegged to dry) widen out; (of tree, &c.) grow upwards, outwards; (of sth. beaten out) expand, be expanded. andarara, stc. inten. I1. be curved, drooping (in the middle); (of a warped beam, suspended rope) be bent down, in; kand haandaraire, curve, convex slope. andararia, caus. stc. inten. andatũra, conv. ten. broaden, develop, improve; ~ mĩtũrire ya andũ, improve the social conditions of the people. andaandatũra, dim. conv. ten. andatara, stc. ten. 1. stand, sit, upright bracing one’s back; hollow the small of the back. 2. (of trees) be arched, bent in the middle; (of bridge, roof) be bowshaped, sag; cf. hocerera.

kĩanda, ci-, n. (1v), 1. valley; cf. ngurumo, gĩtuamba. 2. downward side of slope. mwanda, mi-, n. 1. hollow, depression; cf. kĩande, 2. fold in a hillside, shallow valley without permanent water; cf. irengeta. kaanda, tw-, n. dim. kamwanda, n. hollow in the back, waist. [anda]
mwanda?, mi-, n. (1v), (N.K.) tree (Lachnopylis congesta); cf. mũcorui.
kiandarũwa [kĩandarũwa], ci-, n. (x11), canvas groundsheet, tarpaulin, awning. [Sw. CHANDALUA]
mwandata, mi-, n. (1v), 1. enclosure for sheep and cattle; entrance to such an enclosure. 2. cowshed of European type. [cf. Ma. E-MUATATA]

kĩande, ci-, n. (u1), 1. shoulder, shoulder-joint. 2. ga ~, (N.K.) leaf-bladed spear ; cf. mberethi, kageci, kembo. wande, n. cl. 14 only, 1. flatness; cf. kĩmanda. 2. deep-seated swelling causing suppuration. [anda]
anderethi [aanderethi / aandereethi], ~, n. (XVII/XIV), address. [E.]
mwandĩki, a-, n. (x1), writer, editor, secretary, copyist; (Bibl.) scribe. wandĩki, n. cl. 14, writing. [andika]
maandiko, n. cl. 6 only (x1), written record(s}. [Sw. MAANDIKO]
mwandiko, mi-, n. (x1), writing. [Sw. MwannDIkoO]

NEW_VALUE: be restless, shift about, keep changing one’s position, move restlessly about
FULL_ENTRY: anga!, v.i. (1) 1. be restless, shift about, keep changing one’s position, move restlessly about; (of a person trying to avoid notice) dodge about. 2. (of a woman in childbirth) be in travail, 3. go from place to place exhibiting trophies from enemy killed in war; qualify as a warrior by blooding one’s spear on an enemy. 4. experiment, practice; tiga kw~ na ni!, don’t take liberties with me. angaanga, dim. anganga, diff. 1. move restlessly about. 2. (of animal) be in labour. angangika, st. pot. be unsettled by some specific cause, be at sixes and sevens; cf. angaĩka. anganĩra, app. rec. angia, caus. 1. carry round in triumph. 2. make restless. angithia, d. caus. make restless; ~ thĩ, keep boys singing and dancing the night before circumcision to prevent worry and sleep. angira, ʻapp. go about in search of; avail oneself of; flee to look for (weapons, hidingplace) urgently; ũkaangĩre ciana, go and get food for the children.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of witch doctor) protect (with magic rites), anganga, diff.
NEW_VALUE: (of witch doctor) protect (with magic rites), anganga, diff
FULL_ENTRY: anga?, v.t. (11), (of witch doctor) protect (with magic rites), anganga, diff. angana, rec, angithia, d. caus., angĩka, st. pot. angira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: expr. an element of doubt. 1. am I to conclude that?, perhaps, it may be that
NEW_VALUE: am I to conclude that?, perhaps, it may be that
FULL_ENTRY: anga, part. (U), expr. an element of doubt. 1. am I to conclude that?, perhaps, it may be that; ~ nĩathĩ?, has he really gone then?; ~ do you by any chance know me? ; ~ nĩ kenda, there appear to be nine. 2. expr. acceptance of a statement with reservations; nĩoka. Anga!, the elder has come. You don’t mean it! nonga (no anga), part. I do not suppose, I suppose we do not; ~ tũggkoenda we do not want misunderstanding between our families, do we? nongana [no anga na], part. what about? I do not know about; w? mwega? Ii, ~ wee, how are you? All right, thank you, but what about you? nongarĩ [no anga rĩ) / ningari [nĩ anga ri], part. is it the case that? (answer ‘no’ expected); ~ ithu tũmwĩrire athĩ?, surely we did not actually tell him to go?, we did not tell him to go, did we?
rĩanga, ma-, n. (111), trophy of war. [anga]
-angangi, a. (I1), restless, roving. [anga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. name of one of the two ruling generation-sets
NEW_VALUE: name of one of the two ruling generation-sets
FULL_ENTRY: Mwanei, n. cl. 1 only (11), 1. name of one of the two ruling generation-sets; see Ituĩka, Maina. 2. a man’s name.
angorũo, see under Kora.
maangũ, n. cl. 6 (v), leprous sores, leprosy.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: small tree (Millettia dura) (wood tough and useful for axe and hoe handles).
NEW_VALUE: small tree (Millettia dura) (wood tough and useful for axe and hoe handles)
FULL_ENTRY: mwangwa, mi-, n. (v), small tree (Millettia dura) (wood tough and useful for axe and hoe handles).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) spread by producing shoots or runners, have offshoots
NEW_VALUE: () spread by producing shoots or runners, have offshoots
FULL_ENTRY: anja!, v.i. (1), (N.K.) spread by producing shoots or runners, have offshoots; cf. tharama, njanja. anjaanja, dim. anjanga, diff. anjanĩra, app. rec. anjia, caus., anjithia, d. caus. anjira, app.

anja?, v.i. & t. (1), begin; cf. ambiriria. anjana, rec. 1. start a dispute, fight (with). 2. begin with, tackle first. anjania, caus. rec. begin, at the same time, together. anjanira, assoc. begin together. anjia, caus. begin first; w~ kũinũka?, who went home first? anjithia, d. caus. anjika, st. pot. be started. anjĩra, app. begin, for, at.
anjenda, ~, n. (1x), agenda for meeting, &c. [E.]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: poss. a. & pron. 2nd pers. pl. 1. your, yours
FULL_ENTRY: -anyu, p.a. (U), poss. a. & pron. 2nd pers. pl. 1. your, yours; kw~, at your home, in your homeland or district. 2. w~, (used in greetings); w~ (wa) mong’o, salutation between full members of family; w~ wakini, greeting to sb. of your age-group; w~ mama, greeting used by nephew and niece to maternal uncle who replies with the same words; see mama; w~ baba, greeting to paternal uncle; see baba.
-a0, p.a, (U), poss. a. & pron., their, theirs; with cl. 2 the aconcord is absorbed, atumia ao, their wives; kw~, at their home, at home.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. set out in a systematic manner or in line
NEW_VALUE: set out in a systematic manner or in line
FULL_ENTRY: ara', v.t. (1), 1. set out in a systematic manner or in line; ~ mũhari, (of people) be in line, put (things) in a row; ~ mĩconjo, make contours by laying down lines of garden refuse; ~ maraara, form up in ranks of four. 2. spread, spread out; ~ gĩtanda, ũrĩrĩ, spread beddings on the bed; ~ metha, lay a table; ~ gĩtambaya, spread the cloth (on); ~ rahĩ, stretch out arm (in supplication or to receive sth.), beg; (N.K.) plant (seeds); cf. handa; ~ thĩ, bring down, toss down; spread on the ground. 3. make a suspended structure; ~ gĩtara, build a platform (esp. as a watch-tower against millet-eating birds); ~ gĩcega, make a pen for fattening a ram; ~ thagama (ndaraca)}, make a bridge; ~ ngũ, stack firewood; see mhando. 4. establish, lay out, arrange; ~ kĩng'anga, establish a temporary marketing place, begin trading; ~ rũĩmbo, start an impromptu dance; ~ cira, start unnecessary argument, 5. set out in decorative manner; ~ mĩgathĩ, mĩnyoro, make ornaments (from threaded beads), chains (for wearing round the neck, wrists, and ankles); cf. haka mĩgathĩ, mĩnyoro; ~ thira, make a grass kilt; ~ kĩnyata, decorate (a belt, armlet, bracelet, &c.) with beads; ~ mĩrĩnga, wind wire round the wrist, arm, or leg for adornment. fara, refl. spread oneself, display oneself; sit sprawled; ~ na twa nyanja, fall down heavily; cf. temana. araara, dim, 1. be quick in setting out, &c. 2. (of a blind person, &c.) feel with the hands, touch all over; stroke, smooth down (cloth, fur, &c.); fondle, paw; fiddle with, feel for sth. in sb.’s pockets. 3. level up (ground), cultivate (garden) with immaculate care, faraarira, refl. app. dim. (of sheep-grazing, written or printed characters on a page, &c.) spread out uniformly, cover, spread all over. araarĩka, st. pot. dim. spread, be, all over the place. aranga, diff. arana, rec. be in order, in line. caus. rec. set out in a line anyhow; ~ andũ, interfere in other people’s private affairs; ~ ita, (njama), put warriors in battle array. aranira, app. rec., be arranged in lines or array for; stand or sit face to face to parley, fight, &c.; ~ ndia, sit opposite to one another for a feast or meal. aranĩria, caus. app. rec, set out in array for, arrange together. arithia, d. caus, assist in spreading, putting in order or arraying; help to sow or plant; make form up (in lines). arĩka, st. pot. be capable of being spread out or arranged. arira, app. I. spread sth. for, on; arrange at, for; sit round for. 2. instal, set up (machinery, &c.). iarira, refl. app. settle down (as in a new home), make oneself comfortable; spread sth. for oneself; heyarĩre, a well-set place. arĩrĩra, inten. app. set sth. on to sth. else, add sth. on to, sew (beads on to leather strap). arũra, conv. unspread, remove, take off; dismantle; unwind (wire); strip (a bed). arũka, neut. I1. become or be unspread, unwound; untwist; (of a bridge) become broken, break down; get dismantled, come off. 2. (used as aux. v.) begin to, start; akĩ~ kũmũhũra, he fell to beating him; cf. itika, harũka. arũkana, rec. neut. 1. fall upon each other (in argument or fight); cf. ambaniriria. 2. ~ na, come to grips with. arama, stc. be wide, be broad, be expansive, gape wide, be open wide. aramana, rec, stc. become, be wide open, become clear (of obstruction), be free of obstacles. aramia, caus. stc. widen, make broad, spread out. rfaramia, refl. caus. stc. spread oneself; prosper, become well off. aramũra, conv. stc. broaden, unfold, spread out; ~ itanya ria mũndũ, realize sb.’s hopes. aramũka, neut. stc. 1. be unrestricted, unimpeded. 2. become unfolded.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: dunghill, midden, place where household refuse is dumped.
NEW_VALUE: dunghill, midden, place where household refuse is dumped
FULL_ENTRY: kĩara!, ci-, n. (1), dunghill, midden, place where household refuse is dumped.

kĩara?, ci-, n. (111), finger, toe; ~ kĩrĩa (gĩtungu), ~ kĩa mwene mũcii, thumb; ~ kĩa muoroto, index finger; ~ gia kũgũr, toe; gĩa ~ kĩa, be well fortuned at, be gifted at; arĩ ~ kĩa Ũtonga, riches seem to come his way; arĩ ~ gĩa kũratha, he is a good shot, skilful at firing an arrow in the neck of a cow for blood-letting without endangering the life of the animal; mũndũ wa ~ gĩtune (kĩhin), a shedder of blood. kaara, tw-, n. small finger or toe; ~ ka mũira, the little finger; raria ~, cool one’s finger (by spitting on it when it is burned by sth. hot); see under hobanĩria. njara, ~, n. (N.K.), I. arm, hand. 2. in compound words: (a) ~-hiũ, one quick to wound or kill; (b) ~-mboko, sb. with a permanent crook in the arm; lazy person, chary of field work; (e) ~-ndune, mũnda wa ~-ndune,` one prone to shed blood, one who strikes to wound; cf. kĩara gĩtune. rũara!, ndwara, n., cl. 11/10, finger-, toe-, nail; claw; see gĩcara!. [ara!]
riara, n. cl. 5 only (1?), (N.K.) promptitude; cka na ~, come immediately; cf. rua.
rũara? (N.K.), see
Mwarabu, A-, n. (11), an Arab. kiarabu, n. the Arabic language.
kĩaragana, ci-, n. (11), level expanse, plain. [aragana]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who makes even, straightens out
FULL_ENTRY: mwaragania, a-, n. (11), one who makes even, straightens out; one who puts matters right, settles disputes, peace-maker; adjudicator, administrator. waragania, n. cl. 14, justice; ~ wa bũrũri, administration of justice, settling of people’s disputes. [aragana]
-araganu, a. (11), flat, level. [aragana]
mwarage, see mŨcarage. 1

araha, v.i. (1), 1. rest, come to rest; (of flocks) rest at midday ; (of people) take a siesta; (coll.) sit sprawled out. 2. (of water) lie, collect (in puddles, ponds, &c.). araaraha, dim. arahanga, diff. arahania, caus. rec. lay out, spread out, display, arrange; make, take a rest together; ~ ũhkoro (cira), present a matter (case) clearly, in an orderly manner. farahania, refl. caus, rec. sprawl at one’s ease, be idle, take one’s ease, arahanira, assoc. arahanĩria, caus. assoc. arahia, caus, make spread out, set out, lay out to view (things for sale at a market, &c.); ~ cira, form a court (by setting a ring). 2. cause (water) to lie, make, form pools. 3. take to rest; give a rest to workers esp. at noon; take (flocks) to midday rest in shade. arahithia, d. caus. arahĩka, st. pot. arahira, app. sit round for, at, near; settle down for, at. arahĩria, caus. app. arahũra, conv. arouse from rest or idleness; take or send back to work after midday rest; send flocks back to graze after midday rest. arahũka, rouse oneself, become active after inactivity; resume work after rest, get up; (of goats) return to pasture after morning rest.
(mw-), mĩaramano, n. (111), 1. opportunity, convenient time; ona ~, have an opportunity. 2. freedom from hindrances. [ara?]
-aramu, a. (111), 1. wide, broad. 2. well-to-do. [ara!]
mwara-nda, mi-, n. (1-1), anterior abdominal wall. [ara!]

mwarano | mwaro?, mĩ-, n. (1), 1. (N.K.) a planting; cia ~, sweet potatoes from a new planting; cf. mũhando. 2. feast of beer given by head of household in which initiation rites are to take place for the first time; cf. mathambo, mbato. [ara!]

maarara [maaraara], n. cl. 6 (viir), (esp. of animals) spots; cf. ihara; see maara. kĩarara [kĩaraara], ci-, n. 1. honeycomb (empty or full); cf. magua. 2. second stomach of ruminant (reticulum); cf. kĩraguma, kahu-kanini.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. wide expanse of more or less level country, area, portion
NEW_VALUE: wide expanse of more or less level country, area, portion
FULL_ENTRY: rũare, ndware, n. (I1), 1. wide expanse of more or less level country, area, portion; ~ ra mũgũnda, a big stretch of cultivated land. Gĩtarũrũ ~? Ikinya rĩa njogu, (riddle) winnowing tray on the plain; (ans.) an elephant’s footprint. 2. old name for Nyeri.

mwaria, a-, n. (11), 1. speaker; cf. muugi. 2. one who speaks; (in compounds): (a) ~-ma, one who speaks the truth; (b) ~-njarie, ~-kiarie, (N.K.) one who merely repeats what has already been said, who agrees with the previous speaker; (c) ~ -ũbuthi, pleasing, winning, tactful speaker. [aria?]
warie, (mawarie), n. (11), what has been said; speech, opinion. [aria?]

NEW_VALUE: name given to a fat man
FULL_ENTRY: -arii, a. (1v), 1. broad, wide. 2. kĩ~, (with cl. 1 concord) name given to a fat man. -araarii, a. dim. of above, rather wide, wider, broadish. [ara!]
warĩ, mawarii, n. (1v), 1. breadth, width, expanse. 2. expansion, prosperity. [ara!]
mwarimũ, a-, n. (1x), teacher. [Sw. MWALIMU]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: speech, conversation, talk, language, manner of speaking
NEW_VALUE: nickname for a talkative woman or girl
FULL_ENTRY: mwario, tni- ; njario); n. (1), speech, conversation, talk, language, manner of speaking; cf. mwaririe. nyarũario, ~, n. with cl. 1/2 concords, nickname for a talkative woman or girl. [aria?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: shrub (Dalbergia lactea) (has a large number of long straight stems which, when split, make supple withies used for wickerwork, trays, &c.
NEW_VALUE: shrub (Dalbergia lactea) (has a large number of long straight stems which, when split, make supple withies used for wickerwork, trays, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mwaritha, mi-, n. (x1), shrub (Dalbergia lactea) (has a large number of long straight stems which, when split, make supple withies used for wickerwork, trays, &c.; cf.
rũariũngũ, wariũngũ, mawariũngũ; (N.k.) riũngũ, mariũngũ; n. (1v), spleen. kiariũngũ, ci-, n. disease characterized by enlargement of the spleen (anthrax).

mwari, n, (11), r1. daughter, brothers daughter; ~ wakwa, ~-guo, ~-we, my, your, his daughter; ~ wa my, our, sister; ~ wa thoguo, your half-sister; ~ wa ithe, his, her, half-sister; ~ wa baba (mũkũrũ, mũnyinyi), my cousin (paternal); ~ wa baba, my step-sister; ~ wa nyũkwa, your sister; ~ wa nyina, his, her, sister; ~ wa tya, see iya; ~-wandune, see mwaki. 2. (N.K.) 1. adult, unmarried girl; cf. 3. (R.C.) nun. wamwari, n. cl. 1 only, nun; cf. wamathia. nyamwari, ~, n. I. member of family named after member of mother’s family. 2. (used mainly by children in a jocular sense) person (not necessarily female) who comes to beg help of any kind. 3. (arch.) young female friend taken into family but treated as of lower status because an incomer, not a genuine member of the family.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (formed from v. root -rĩ and a neutral subj. prefix) it being
FULL_ENTRY: ari, part. (U), (formed from v. root -rĩ and a neutral subj. prefix) it being; ruta wĩra ~ we, do that job yourself; nĩonire ta ~ wega akũmenyithie, he saw that it was good to inform you. atari, neg. of arĩ, it not being; ta ~, as if it were not.

NEW_VALUE: one skilled in winding on bangles and coils of personal adornment
FULL_ENTRY: mwariri, a-, n. (1), one skilled in winding on bangles and coils of personal adornment. [ara]
mwaririe, mĩ-; warĩrie, mawarĩrie; n. (11) manner of speaking, language; cf. [aria?]

(1x), 1. one who speaks for another or on his behalf, spokesman, representative, advocate; cf. mũciririri, mũhuhirĩria. ~ kĩrimũ ndaigaga mũthĩgi thĩ, a fool’s advocate cannot lay down his staff of office. 2. member of Legislative Council, Parliamentary representative; cf. mũteteri. [aria?]
kĩaro, ci-, n. (1) forced labour; labour task; work; ndathĩ ~, I am off to work; cf. wira.

FULL_ENTRY: mwaro!, mĩ-, n. (1), 1. one of the withies tied side by side across the poles of a traditional-type bed to form a base. Andũ mendaine makomagĩra ~ people who like one another do not mind sleeping on one ‘bed-plank’. 2. bridge-decking, transverse pole, plank, or beam of any structure. [ara!]
mwaro?, see mwarano.

NEW_VALUE: mass of rock, rock face, outcrop of rock
FULL_ENTRY: rũaro | (N.k.) rũara?, ndwaro, n. (1), (0 I1. mass of rock, rock face, outcrop of rock; cf. rũnyanjara. 2. expanse, width; ~ ra ikũmbĩ, floor of grainstore.
waru, see under waru,

arunya / arugunya, v.i. (1) 1. be agitated, fidget; rock, writhe, squirm. 2, cry in pain of childbirth, undergo labour., ararunya, dim. arunyanga, diff. arunyanira, assoc. arunyia, caus. make fidget, writhe. arunyithia, d. caus. arunyika, st. pot. go on being agitated, be writhing, fidgeting. arunyikia, caus. st. pot. arunyira, app.

NEW_VALUE: wellgrown young female sheep, ewe, not yet mature
FULL_ENTRY: mewati, mi-, n. (111), ewe that has not yet lambed. Mũndũ ũtathiaga athĩnjaga ~, the man who never travels slaughters an ewe., Cf. ng'ondu. kimwati-, giatĩ, ci-; n. 1. wellgrown young female sheep, ewe, not yet mature. 2. circumcised girl not yet arrived at puberty and therefore not wearing a woman’s skin skirt; see mũthuru. kamwati, tũ- ; gaati, tw- ; n. dim. of above; cf. kaharika.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (derived from an old v.. meaning ‘say’) so, it is said (so)

So it’s said; akĩgweta ~ marĩa thirikari Tikite, he referred to the mistakes the government is said to have made; ~ ndehe kĩ?, what did you say I was to bring?; ~ atĩa?, what did you say?; baba oiga atĩa? ~ ũthĩ, what did my father say ? That you are to go; see also atia, atiri, |

NEW_VALUE: listen to me, listen to this

(N.K.) 2. cylindrical wooden barrel with lids at both ends used for storage in the home. 3. (of road or railway) culvert. rũatũ, njatũ, n. 1. (N.K.), crack. 2. one half, side, of anything split in two, piece split off; atũra njat cut in two; cf. gĩcere. giatũ, ci-, n. division, portion cut off; slice; hole through marrow-bone; bore of fife, pipe. [ata]
mwatũka, mi-, n. (x), crack, fissure. [ata]

FULL_ENTRY: mwatha, mĩ-, n. (11), tree spurge (Euphorbia spp.). watha, mawatha, n. irritant white sap of this tree used for poisoned arrows and for making tattoo marks; see gĩca, muokia.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: yawn, gape, be wide open. athaathama, dim. athamanga, diff. athamania, caus. rec. make open wide, make sth. or others gape. athamia, caus. open wide, cause to gape
FULL_ENTRY: athama, v.i. athara, see hathara.

rũathatha [rũathaatha], njathatha, n. (VIII), 1. loud talker, talkative person; mbũri ya ~, a bleating goat. 2. frilled margin of goats or dog’s mouth; space between goats front and back teeth, or behind its teeth, by which it can be held; root of tongue; nyita mbũri ~, seize a goat by the back of the tongue (as when giving medicine or to stop bleating when stolen). 3. (cl. 10) bifurcations, forked branches; cf. maahũkanira, njaaga.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: prating, flow of words of talkative person.
NEW_VALUE: prating, flow of words of talkative person
FULL_ENTRY: rũathathĩ [rũathaathi], njathathĩ, n. (vu?), prating, flow of words of talkative person.
rũathe, njathe, n. (11), large timbertree (Canthium spp.).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: x. hunter and trapper. 2. name given to member of hunting fraternity which formerly roamed the forests on Kikuyu boundary
FULL_ENTRY: mwathi?, a-, n. (11), x. hunter and trapper. 2. name given to member of hunting fraternity which formerly roamed the forests on Kikuyu boundary; native hunter, Ndorobo; see wathi, mawathi, n. 1. the life of a hunter; matters relating to the Aathi. 2. art of hunting, shooting; skill, success, in hunting. 3. good aim, good shot. 4. piece of meat secured by good shooting.
athima, see thima.
rĩathimũra, ma- ; (N.K.) rĩathimũri, njathimũri; n. (11), sneeze. [athimũra]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sticky sap exuding from the stem of unripe bananas or from the palm-tree when cut.
NEW_VALUE: sticky sap exuding from the stem of unripe bananas or from the palm-tree when cut
FULL_ENTRY: maathina, n. cl. 6 (x), sticky sap exuding from the stem of unripe bananas or from the palm-tree when cut.
-athĩki, a. (1), obedient, subservient. [atha]
-athĩkĩri, a. (1) obedient; nĩ nii mũgw~, I am your obedient servant. [atha]

gĩathũ, ci-, n. (v), coccyx; extreme emaciation causing coccyx to be prominent and calloused. Riika rĩtihithagwo ~, you cannot hide anything (not even the thing you are ashamed of) from your age-group. wathũ, n. cl. 14, weakness, disability; cf. wathe, wonje.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of a path) depart from the main one. 2. go to the bush (esp. for easing purposes)
NEW_VALUE: (of a path) depart from the main one
FULL_ENTRY: athũka, v.i. (1), 1. (of a path) depart from the main one. 2. go to the bush (esp. for easing purposes); cf. kerũra, mia. athũkanira, assoc, athũkira, app. depart at (a point).
au-u-u / awu-u-u / aauuii, interj. (U), expr. 1. pain, astonishment, consternation. 2. feeling aroused by sight of pain, misfortune.
kĩaũ!, ci-, n, (1), 1. groin, 2. origin.

NEW_VALUE: place where soil is poor and nothing grows
FULL_ENTRY: kiaũ?, n. cl. 7 only (111); ~ gĩa thi, place where soil is poor and nothing grows. [see aũra]
awa', n. cl. xi only (vi), (arch.) my father; cf. baba.
awa-awa, ideo. (1/11), descr. of cry of bush baby.
aya-ya-ya, interj. (U), expr. reproof.

NEW_VALUE: warning cry of a goatbag to trespassing or unruly goats, @
FULL_ENTRY: ayũ, interj. (U), warning cry of a goatbag to trespassing or unruly goats, @
ayũhia, (N.K.), see icũhia, B
ũba, see under mba.

FULL_ENTRY: baa, ba-a .. . a, ideo. (U), descr. of: 1. the sound of running water or gentle steady rain. 2. sustained railing, upbraiding, burbling talk; gambira ~, keep on at (people). 3. the long soft rustle of grass or vegetation as a body passes swiftly through it; mahuti makoiga ~, the leaves rustled softly. 4. the sound of dry grain or beans being poured out. v.t. (1), 1. iron clothes; cf. hũra bathi. baacabaaca, dim. baacanga, diff. baacania, caus. rec. iron clothes anyhow. baacanĩra, app. rec. baacanĩria, caus. app. rec. baacithia, d. caus. help to iron clothes, have clothes ironed. baacithiria, app. d. caus. baacĩka, st. pot. be capable of being ironed. baacira, app.
mabaaciriro, n. cl. 6 (1), carefulness, making sure. [baaca?]
ibaage, ma-; mũbaage, mi-; n. (v), Mauritius Thorn (Caesalpina sepiaria) (used for hedges). mbaage, ~~, n. fruit and seeds of Mauritius Thorn (used for the African chess game, also in divining).

mũbaara, mi-, n. (vi), 1. bundle; ~ wa mbaki, small packet of snuff done up in dry banana bark; cf. 2. ~ wa aka, menstruation; cf. mogongo, mweri, mi-, n. (xi), eyeing one another, mutual appraisal; looking askance at one another. Mũikaranio noguo ~, staying together begets animosity. [baara]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: come to an agreement, make an arrangement.
NEW_VALUE: come to an agreement, make an arrangement
FULL_ENTRY: baatana, v.i. (I1), come to an agreement, make an arrangement. [Sw. PATANA]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. cl. 1/2 (v1), my father, my classificatory father
FULL_ENTRY: baba [baaba], (~), n. cl. 1/2 (v1), my father, my classificatory father; ~ me thingira, my father and classificatory fathers are in the men’s hut; ~ mũkũrũ, my senior paternal uncle; ~ mũnyinyi, my junior paternal uncle; cf. awa. 2. term of occasional address to God; ~ Our Father; cf. Ithe witũ.
kibaba [kĩbaaba], i-, n. (11), 1. tin-can; cf. gĩkobu, mikebe, gĩthoguo. 2. food ration for a workman (a measure of maize meal). kababa, tũ-, n. dim. of above. [Sw. KIBABA]

NEW_VALUE: the furious flaming of a fire, e
FULL_ENTRY: babababa, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1. the furious flaming of a fire, e.g. one into which fat is dripping. 2. the uprising of a flock of pigeons, the flapping of many wings, &c.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a pawpawtree. ibabai, ma-, pawpaw fruit. i-, n. (1), a dried-up, rotten gourd
FULL_ENTRY: mũbabai, mĩ-, n. (1), a pawpawtree. ibabai, ma-, pawpaw fruit. i-, n. (1), a dried-up, rotten gourd; cf. kĩhohoe. [babaya]

NEW_VALUE: pinch, grip with fingers, nip, clutch
FULL_ENTRY: babata, v.t. (11) pinch, grip with fingers, nip, clutch; wring (ears); grip with both hands (e.g. a child on both ears or by the back of both thighs to punish or intimidate); strip (web of feather from its quill); cf. bata, kunya. babatanga, diff. babatana, rec. babatithia, d. caus. cause to be pinched, gripped; assist sb. to pinch. babatĩka, st. pot. babatĩra, app. babatũra, conv. used inten. pull apart (e.g. the leafy envelope of a maize cob to examine the grains inside); pull at, pluck at, roughly, clumsily; stretch a tyre tube (when searching for a puncture).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be, become light, flabby, weak, soft, spongy, decayed
FULL_ENTRY: babaya, v.i. (1), be, become light, flabby, weak, soft, spongy, decayed; lose courage, be agitated; cf. moca, twekia. bababaya, dim. babayanga, diff. babayania, caus. rec. make one another soft, flabby, fearful. babayanĩra, assoc. babayanĩria, caus. assoc. babayia, caus. make flabby, weak, soft; frighten, make timid. babayithia, d. caus. babaika, st. pot. 1. become soft, light, yielding. 2. be anxious, worried, fearful, ill at ease; lose calmness, be taken aback, become agitated; cf. maka, gega. babaĩkia, caus. st. pot. babaikĩra, app. st. pot. babaĩra, app. become soft, weak, flabby, at, for. babairia, caus. app.

NEW_VALUE: crunch, break up ( hard or brittle) by chewing or grinding
FULL_ENTRY: babura / babura [babuura], v.t. caaya!, v.i. (1), 1. (of sb. in pain, &c.) moan, groan; wail, whimper. Mwendi ũthaka nda~ga, one who wants to look beautiful should not moan (while being groomed). Cf. cakaya, kaya’, cacaĩka. 2. squeak, creak; (of a door) grate on hinges. caayacaaya, dim. caayanga, diff. caayanira, assoc, groan together, wail over others; do sth. to each other, others, with extreme care to avoid hurting; treat others with consideration; ndacaayanagĩra akĩherithania, he is inconsiderate when inflicting punishment on others. caaiya [caayia], caus. caaithia [caayithia], d. caus. cause to whimper, groan, squeak; commiserate with sb. rcaaithia, refl. d. caus. groan without a cause, wail unnecessarily. caaĩka, st. pot. (used inten.) keep up a continuous low moaning or grumbling. caaira, app. groan, moan, wail, for, at; wail over sth.; do sth. with extreme care to avoid hurting; handle, use, sth. with extreme care. icaaira, refl. app. wail over oneself, indulge in self-pity ; be unduly careful about oneself. caairĩra, inten. do, handie, use (sth.) with double care so as to avoid hurting or causing damage. refl. inten. think too much about oneself.
caaya?, see kaaya.

NEW_VALUE: of the sound made when is slapped or struck hard as with a cane, whip, or open hand
FULL_ENTRY: caba, ideo. (1), descr. of the sound made when sth. is slapped or struck hard as with a cane, whip, or open hand; gambia ~, strike hard, slap.

cababindũki, ~, n. (1), fez, tarboosh. [Sw. CHAPABUNDUKI]

FULL_ENTRY: cabacaba?, v.i. (1), make a splashing sound, squelch. cabacabangia, dift. cabacabanira, assoc. cabacabia, caus. 1I. splash, cause to squelch, make a splashing sound (as when walking or wading through water), paddle (as children in a pool of water). 2. prepare (a beverage) carelessly; cf. cabacia. cabacabithia, d. caus. cabacabika, st. pot. cabacabĩra, app. cabacabiria, caus. app. splash, on, for, at.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of splashing sound as when paddling or wading through water
NEW_VALUE: of splashing sound as when paddling or wading through water
FULL_ENTRY: cabacaba, ideo. (1), descr. of splashing sound as when paddling or wading through water; gamba ~, (of water) splash, squelch.
cabaci, ~, n. (x), a chapattie. [Ind.]

cabacia, v.t. (1) 1. splash (water), paddle. 2. make sth. watery. Cf. cabacabia. cabacabacia, dim. cabacangia, diff. cabacabaniria, app. rec. cabacithia, d. caus. cabacĩka, st. pot. cabacĩria, app. splash (water), on, at, because of. cabacũra, conv. 1. make (sth.) watery. 2. disturb (still water), make (water) dirty by disturbing the sediment. cabacũka, neut.

FULL_ENTRY: cabi / cabicabi, ideo. (1), descr. of sound made when sth. strikes the surface of a liquid esp. water, splash, gambia ~, strike (meat broth with a swizzle stick when mixing it, &c.).

ĩcabuna, v.i. (refl.) (1), behave jauntily, naughtily, defiantly; show off, act conceitedly, in a rough manner; cf. ĩtia. ĩcabunanga, diff. icabunira, app. (of people, animals) behave defiantly, towards, at, in.
caburaita / taburaita, ~, n, (vi), typewriter. [E.]

caca, v.i. (1), 1. (of a person, tree, &c.) be slender; (of a piece of wood planed or a stick whittled too much) become attenuated, thin, weak; be weak, frail; peter out; (of crops) become lanky. 2. (of a debate spoiled by poor speakers, interruptions, &c.) become ineffectual. 3. be, become, tasteless; cf. gaca. cacanga, diff. cacania, caus, rec, spoil one another, make one another weak, ineffectual, impoverished, straggly; mĩũngano nĩcacanagia, (of dances, &c. taking place simultaneously) public gatherings detract from one another; mĩthaiga ya ago aingi ya~, the remedies of several medicine men weaken one another; cf. thararia. cacanira, assoc. cacia, caus. cut into srnall thin pieces, reduce in bulk; weaken, make ineffectual; water, whittle, down. cacithia, d. caus, (used inten.). cacĩka, st. pot. cacira, app. cacũra, conv. whittle down too much, make very thin; thin out (plants growing close together); remove (weeds from a crop which they are choking); cultivate (weedy plants) in order to improve them. cacũka, neut. 1. be attenuated, get too much whittled down; be very thin, 2. cease to be thin, be thinned out, well spaced.
gicaca, see under njaca.

cacaika, v.i. (1), 1. be plaintive, effusive, voluble, exuberant; indulge in self-pity; cf. cakaya. 2. be excited, ebullient, joyous; ~ ta nguya, behave in an excited manner (like flying termites). 3, (of fat) splutter, sizzle; (of meat being roasted) crackle. cacacacaĩka, dim. cacaĩkanga, diff. cacaĩkanĩra, assoc. r. indulge in self-pity together, be effusive to one another; show morbid concern over sb.’s well-being, be over anxious about others. 2. sizzle together. cacaikia, caus. 1. cause to indulge in self-pity; make plaintive, effusive, excited. 2, make sizzle; roast (meat). cacalkithia, d, caus. pretend to be deeply concerned about sb. else’s misfortune, join sb. else (pretentiously) in being plaintive, effuse, exuberant. kithia, refi. d. caus. be plaintive for no good reason. cacaĩkĩra, app. be plaintive, about, at; be unduly concerned over sb., sth.; indulge in selfpity over sth. ; be effusive about sth., sb. ĩcacaĩkira, refi. app. be unduly concerned about one’s own welfare H indulge in self-love. nũcacana (mi-) n. (v1); mt? wa ~, young tree, sapling. cacana [-caacaana], a. (v1), immature, green, young; kĩgwa gĩ~, unripe ugar-cane; mo~, young green tree, sapling, wand; cf. mũcacana. aci, see tcaci / gĩtati, i-, n, (11), showing off, acts of vanity, conceited behaviour, effusiveness, gush, affectation, enthusiasm, undue exuberance; ndigithia ~, spare me your effusiveness ; kai mbembe ici ti na ~-ĩ!, (of maize in a leaky bag which persists in running out despite repeated plugging) how annoying this maize is!; cf. njacĩ, mwitio, ngotoko. [see cacaika]

FULL_ENTRY: -cacu, a. (11), thin, slender. [caca] njaca?, n. (11), long thin wand, sapling, withy used for basket-making ; see cf. rti, [caca]

FULL_ENTRY: caha [caaha], v.t. (1), do carefully; handle with care, skill, caution; patronize, be solicitous to, sb. ; cf. cĩha, caya. rcaha, refl. take care of oneself (esp. in dressing), spruce oneself up, do one’s personal toilet fastidiously. cahacaha, dim. cahanga, diff. cahana, rec. cahithia, d. caus. cahika, st. pot. cahira, app. do sth. carefully, for, on account of, at; be anxious, solicitous, for; be concerned about. cahĩrira, inten. handle with extreme care (so as to avoid hurting), deal gently with; select, use, with care; cf. baarĩrira, cayĩrĩra. ĩcahĩrĩra, refil. inten. take undue care about oneself (esp. in dressing). cahũra, conv. 1. treat without care or compunction, inflict punishment upon. 2. strike smartly with a switch or whip, slap hard, give a sharp beating or spanking to; cf. tamũra, cathũra. cahũrana, rec. conv. cahũrithia, d. caus. conv. cahũrira, app. conv. punish severely, because of, at. cahi, see under njahi.

cahĩka [caahĩka], v.i. (1) simmer, sizzle; (of water in a pipe or kettle, beer or gruel fermenting with audible effervescence) gurgle; cf. cihĩka. cahacahĩka, dim. cahĩkanga, diff. cahĩkanĩra, assoc. cahĩkia, caus. cahikira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of irrigated land near a stream) be sodden, waterlogged. 2. (of crops) be stunted, blighted
NEW_VALUE: (of irrigated land near a stream) be sodden, waterlogged
FULL_ENTRY: canjama, v.i. (11), 1. (of irrigated land near a stream) be sodden, waterlogged. 2. (of crops) be stunted, blighted; be prevented from growing by too much rain, cold, drought. 3. (of a person) be sorrowful, downcast, afflicted; be filled with apprehension, consternation, &c.; cf. gaagaya. canjacanjama, dim. canjarmanga, diff. canjamia, caus. make sorrowful, desolate; afflict, dismay. canjamithia, d. caus. canjamika, st. pot. canjamira, app. canjamũra, conv. cheer up, gladden, brighten, make clear; cause to rejoice, give a feeling of well-being; relieve, invigorate; cf. kenia. canjaaamũrana, rec. conv. hearten one another. canjamũrithia, d. caus. conv. cause sb, to be gladdened, cheered up, invigorated. canjamũrĩka, st. pot. conv.; athitĩte ndangĩ~, he is so wrapped up in gloom that it is impossible to cheer him up. app. canjamũka, neut. 1. be glad in heart, rejoice; be brisk, bright; be invigorated (after a hot drink, the receipt of good news, &c.); take courage. 2. (of water) be clear. canjamnũkia, caus. neut. canjamũkira, app. neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. plant used as medicine or tonic. 2. a magic medicine used in the final stage of ceremonial purification (see ndahikanio) and in the initiation rites (see irua)
NEW_VALUE: plant used as medicine or tonic
FULL_ENTRY: mũcanjamũka, mi-, n. (XII), I. plant used as medicine or tonic. 2. a magic medicine used in the final stage of ceremonial purification (see ndahikanio) and in the initiation rites (see irua); cf. mũcagatha. [canjama] mi-, n. (11), gladness, lightness of heart. [canjama]
canjamũkũ, ideo. (11), descr. of: 1. persons becoming cheerful, lively. 2. beverages, snuff, &c., being stimulating. [see canjamũka]
gĩcanjara, i-, n. (1v), 1. tree with bare, widespread limbs. 2. yam with spread-out tubers. [see canjarika]

canjarĩka [canjaarĩka] / canjaĩka, v.i. (1), 1. be opened out, fully exposed; (of moonlight, sunlight, &c.) be bright, brilliant. 2. (of a goat laid on its back for slaughter, a boy or girl at circumcision with legs apart, &c.) be stretched, exposed to open view; cf. tagania. 3. (of people) be in public view, shine, excel (esp. in dances, competitions, &c.). icanjarika, refl. stretch oneself out, be sprawled out. canjacanjarika, dim. canjarĩkanga, diff. canjarĩkanĩra, assoc. canjarikia, caus. spread out, expose. canjarikĩka, st. pot. canjarĩkira, app. refl.; ~ rita, mwaki, expose oneself to the warmth, of the sun, of the fire.

FULL_ENTRY: icara [icaara], v.i. (refl.) gĩcara, i-, n. (111), r. swollen toe;! (N.K.) foot rot. 2. elephant’s trunk; cf. gĩoko. 3. (usu. with cl. o/r10 concords) ox, bull, ram, he-goat, with. long hooves. macara | maca?, n. cl. 6 only, x. diseased condition of goat's feet. 2. ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. tall timber-l tree (Olea hochstetteri), Kenya Oliv (yields hard wood of inferior quali but easier to saw and in better sizes than mũtamaiyũ (q.v.)
NEW_VALUE: tall timber-l tree (Olea hochstetteri), Kenya Oliv (yields hard wood of inferior quali but easier to saw and in better sizes than mũtamaiyũ (q
FULL_ENTRY: mũcarage, mi-, n. (111), 1. tall timber-l tree (Olea hochstetteri), Kenya Oliv (yields hard wood of inferior quali but easier to saw and in better sizes than mũtamaiyũ (q.v.); used for making troughs, pestles, &c.). 2. tree (Linociera welwitschii). njarage, ~, n. fruit of 1 above (red in colour, made into beads, used by boys as hair adornment in dances, see ngũcũ).

cararara, ideo. (1), expr.: 1. complete, easy, tearing, ripping open. 2. sound of sewing-machine; gambia ~, sew up sth. with a machine. 3. (of sun, moon, bright lamp, fire, &c.) extreme brightness; open-heartedness; thera ngoro ~, be light hearted. [see cararia]

cararia?, v.t. (1) sew sth. (with a machine). caracararia, dim. cararangia, diff. cararaniria, app. rec. ; ~ rũtumo rũrũ na ru, sew this and that seam together. cararithia, d. caus. cararika, st. pot. carariria, app.; ła ~ nguo please run this dress through your (sewing) machine.
rũcararia, njararia, n. (11), long tear, crack, long strip. [cararia*]
mũcarariki, a-, n. (1), open-hearted, cheerful, person. [see cararia']
mũcarariko, mĩ-, n. (1), gladness, cheerfulness. [see cararia!]
cari, ideo. (1/11/11), descr. of: 1. cracking, bursting, open; popping; gamba ~, burst open with a pop. 2. a sharp strike with a whip or switch. [see carika]

caria, v.t. (1), 1. seek, look, for; search; cf. etha, maatha. 2. pick a quarrel. caracaria, dim. carangia, diff. carania, rec. caraniria, caus. app. rec. seek for sth. for others, seek for sth. together with sb. else. carithia, d. caus. get sb. to look for sth.; help to find; cause to be sought for. carĩka, st. pot. carĩkĩra, app. st. pot. ; bũrũri wĩ ngũi ndũngĩ~ indo, in an unstable country one cannot become wealthy. carĩria, app. seek for sth., at, for; seek on behalf of; look, search, for sb. because of; birithi kĩ?, for what crime are the police after him? carĩrĩria, inten. seek, again, repeatedly, intently.
maũcaria, n. (11), 1. seeker, inquirer. 2. one attending Christian instruction classes. [caria]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: long whip which can be cracked (used at dances, for driving cattle, &c.).
NEW_VALUE: long whip which can be cracked (used at dances, for driving cattle, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũcarica, mĩ-, n. (1x), long whip which can be cracked (used at dances, for driving cattle, &c.).

icariũngũ, see

carika, v.i. (11), 1. burst open and spill out; (of castor-oil, bean, pods) pop; cf. monoka, cararika. 2. (of pimples, swellings) burst. 3. (of fire) crackle; appear suddenly. caracarika, dim. carikanga, diff. (of fire, &c.) keep on crackling and letting off sparks. carĩkanĩra, assoc. carikia, caus. 1. burst, crash, open; make pop, crackle. 2. stab, strike, suddenly; ~ na mũguĩ, shoot with an arrow (suddenly); ~ rũhĩ, give a whack with open palm. carĩkika, st. pot. carĩkira, app. get spilled, in, at, because of.
gacata, see under njata.

caũ, ideo. (1/11), descr. of: 1. bareness, openness (e.g. of an area denuded of trees). 2. aria ~, speak without reserve, blurt things out. [see caũka]
(ga-) (gĩ-) caũ', see under njaũ,

caũka, v.i. (11), 1. (of weather) clear up (after rain); (of sky, a room when lit up) become bright; cf. kĩa. 2. (of a person) be bright, clever, well-informed, well-educated; ~ ngoro, be open-hearted; cf. kũka. 3. (of the skin after a burn or scald) peel, slough, come, off, be bare; cf. monyoka. 4. (of beer, gruel) be wellbrewed, well-fermented, mellow, clear. caũkanga, diff. caũkanira, assoc. Caũkia, caus. 1. make bright, clear, mellow; gũthĩ nĩ gũcaũkagia mũndũ, travelling opens one’s eyes. 2. scald (causing skin to come off). caũkĩka, st. pot, caũkĩra, app. caũra, v.t. 1. brighten, illuminate, enlighten, 2. burn off (grass); (of a burn) remove skin; peel, tear, off; strip (meat) off (a bone); skin (a hide). 3. (coll.) give a sharp spanking, strike smartly; ~ na hĩ, beat up by striking smartly with open palm. caũrana, rec. caũrithia, d. caus. caũrika, st. pot. caũrĩra, app. inten. (conv.) scald badly. caũrũria, caus. inten. scald entirely, take the skin clean off. neut. inten. 1. be completely exposed. 2. (of hide) come clean off. 3. ~ ngoro, be hilariously happy. nũcaũko, mi-, n. (111), forwardness; wit; ~ (wa ngoro), light-heartedness, brightness of spirit, gladness, rapture; see kena, canjamũka, thera ngoro. [caũka] caũku, a. (1v), 1. bright, clever, uninhibited, witty, forward. 2. (of skin) burnt. 3. (of beer, gruel, &c.) mellow, fully brewed. [caũka] aũkg, ideo. (11), well, fully, brewed; cf. -caũku. aya, v.t. (1), make a short slit, nick, incision; cut into small strips; clip (off); cf. kaya?, kaaya. cayacaya, dim. cayanga, diff. cut to ribbons, slash about. cayangia, caus. diff. slash about, cut to ribbons. cayana, Tec. cayanira, app. rec. caiya [cayia], caus. cut into thin strips, make small slits, cut up. caithia [cayithia], d. caus. caĩka, st. pot. caira, app.
icebia, v.i. (refl.) (£), slip away, slink off; cf. cendũka, cehia. rcebacebia, dim. rcebangia, diff. app. slip away; slink off, at.
ceca, see cetha.
cece, ideo. kinya ~, tread gingerly (like a tortoise).

(coll.) go to blazes; (of a woman) go on the loose. ceeraceera, dim. ceeranga, diff. ceerania, caus. rec. keep in company of; take others for a walk round. ceeranira, assoc. visit each other. ceeria, caus. take sb. for a walk, visit, sightseeing; make (machinery) move, work., ceerithia, d. caus. ceereka, st. pot.; gũtingĩ~, it is impossible to take a walk today. ceerera, app. pay a visit to, call on sb.; take a walk at.

ceere, tũmiceere, n, 1, dim, of
above. 2. a small quantity of rice. [Sw. MCHELE]
gĩcege [giceege], i-, n. (v), raccoonlike animal. gacege, tũ-, n. hedgehog.

FULL_ENTRY: mũcegeni [mũceegeni], mi-, n. (VIII), 1. stinging creeper-plant ( Tragidla sp. urticaceae). 2. genet cat. njegeni, ~, n. I. stinging creeper (used for punishment; applied to external genitalia, male and female, as sexual stimulant; put into broth by young men to stimulate courage; used in ceremony for laying departed spirits when specially troublesome ; used as counterirritant on a child with stomachache). 2. genet cat; cf. mũcoroge (njoroge). gacegeni, tũn. 1. smal! stinging creeper. 2. small, fierce, type of mongoose with light-coloured spots.

cehe!, see coho,

cehũra, see tehũra.

cemũka, see camũka.
(cemba), cembũria, see cimba.

gĩcembũ, see gitembũ.
cembũra, see under tembũra.

(cena [ceena]), v.i. (1), gleam, be bright; cf. cenena. cenacena, dim. sparkle, glisten, brighten. cenacenia, caus. dim. cenanga, diff. cenangia, caus. diff. cenia, caus. cenena [ceneena], v.i. (1), 1. sparkle, glisten ; (of stars) twinkle; (of fire, &e.) glimmer, flash; (of eyes) blink; cf. menamena. 2. fof people) spread idle gossip, tell tales; be inquisitive, anxious to see what is going on. cenacenena, dim. cenacenenia, caus. dim. cenenanga, diff. cenenangia, caus. diff. cenenanira, assoc. I. twinkle together. 2. talk to others indiscreetly. cenenaniria, caus. assoc. cenenia, caus. I1. flash (light, torch) about, make twinkle, glisten. 2. (N.K.) produce plumes at end of maize cobs; cf. mira. 3. make sb. inquisitive, indiscreet, make go about spreading idle gossip. 'cenenithia, d. caus. assist sb. to flash sth., &c. ceneneka, st. pot, I. flash about; be capable of being flashed or made to twinkle. 2. go about telling tales, spread idle gossip; be indiscreet, inquisitive; be agog, anxious to see what is going on. cenenekera, app. st. pot. cenenera, app. ceneneria, caus. app.
gaceneni, see under njeneni.
ceni, ~, n. (1), chain, car chains. [E.]

(11), 1. cut off with a small quick stroke, cut through, sever; snip off, prune, clip, dock; cf. heria, riha, ceha, cauria, tinia, renga. 2. skim (liquids to remove scum or avoid sediments); cf. keera, cengacenga, dim. cengacengũria, caus. cony. dim. cenganga, diff. cengana, rec. I. snip., 2. skim, dole out. 3. gnaw haphazardiy. cengũranĩria, caus. app. rec. cony. cengũrithia, d. caus. conv. cengũrika, st. pot. conv. cengũriria, caus. app. conv.
