FULL_ENTRY: cenga, (ma-), n, cl. 14/6 (vini), I. rusty-coloured scum or sediment in stagnant water. 2. medicine used in witchcraft made from this scum or sediment. 3. paint (used for decorating shields) made from this red scum.

NEW_VALUE: wobble, quiver, limp, walk with unsteady gait
FULL_ENTRY: cengeca, v.i. (1) 1. wobble, quiver, limp, walk with unsteady gait; cf. cũngũca. 2. (of knee or ankle rattles, counters in diviner’s gourd, chains, &c.) jingle, rattle. 3. (of dance) go with swing or din, cengacengeca, dim. cengecanga, diff. cengecanira, app. rec. I1. limp together. 2. go with a swing in unison. cengecia, caus. cengeceka, st. pot. cengecera, app. cony. cengecũka, neut.

NEW_VALUE: a torn garment, rags, tatters, gacengera, tũ-, n, a snippet
FULL_ENTRY: gĩcengera, i-, n. (x1), large piece of torn cloth. macengera, n. cl. 6 only, a torn garment, rags, tatters, gacengera, tũ-, n, a snippet. [cenga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (used only in folk-tales and children’s songs) little bits and pieces of food which you approach by stealth and snatch
FULL_ENTRY: cengera-cema [~ -ceema], n. (X1-VII1), (used only in folk-tales and children’s songs) little bits and pieces of food which you approach by stealth and snatch; see cema.
(gĩcengerema), see gĩcunguruma.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: lance, wooden spear, pointed stick used in spearthrowing games
FULL_ENTRY: mũcengi, mi-, n. (11), lance, wooden spear, pointed stick used in spearthrowing games; see mbara! ; cf. (N.K.) mũthiri. gĩcengi, i-, n. 1. aug. of above. 2. name given to anything that snips off sth., a cutter. icengi, ma-, n. goad, pointed stick (as used in interdistrict fights). [cenga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. thick piece of hide used to reinforce the handle of a shield. 2. a patch of colour
NEW_VALUE: thick piece of hide used to reinforce the handle of a shield
FULL_ENTRY: gicengo, i-, n. (u1), 1. thick piece of hide used to reinforce the handle of a shield. 2. a patch of colour; a patch of dirt on a garment. gacengo, tũ-, n. dim. of above.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of an unfinished building, a gap in a wall, an open door or window, a broken window-pane
NEW_VALUE: of an unfinished building, a gap in a wall, an open door or window, a broken window-pane
FULL_ENTRY: ceng’ũ, ideo. (11/11), descr. of an unfinished building, a gap in a wall, an open door or window, a broken window-pane; wide open, yawning,
| agape; atigĩte ikũmbĩ rĩake ~, he has left his store wide open; tikara thi ~, sit down in the open.

iceng’ũ, ma-; giceng’ũ, i-, n. (VIL), 1. sth. left uncovered, unclosed; tiga nyũmba ~, leave house wide open; cf. gitengũ. gaceng’ũ, n. dim. of above. [ceng ũka] v.i. (11), 1. be gaping, clear, wide open; stand wide open, be yawning. 2. depart, clear out of the way; cf. cengũ. ceng’ũkana, rec. used inten, get completely exposed. ceng’ũkia, caus. ceng’ũkira, app. ceng’ũra, v.t. open wide, make a break in, make gape. ceng’'aceng’ũra, dim. ceng’'ũranga, diff. ceng’ũrana, rec. ceng’ũrania, caus. rec. ceng’ũranira, app. rec. ceng’ũraniria, caus. app. rec. Ceng’ũrithia, d. caus. ceng’ũrika, st. pot. ceng’ũrira, app. ceng’ũriria, app. rec.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: immature yam, one that is still white.
NEW_VALUE: immature yam, one that is still white
FULL_ENTRY: mũcenjerũũ, mi-, n. (x1), immature yam, one that is still white.

cenji-, ~; mũcenji, a-; n. (111), uncivilized, uncouth person; barbarian, savage. gĩcenji [gĩcenji]}, n. (used adv.) in an uncivilized manner; -a ~, uncivilized, primitive, crude; hika ~, be married by native custom (without either church or civil ceremony). ũcenji, n. cl. 14 only, lack of culture, barbarism, heathendom. [Sw. SHENZI]
cenjia, see cinjia.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. cultivate soil lightly, loosen top soil
NEW_VALUE: cultivate soil lightly, loosen top soil
FULL_ENTRY: cera, v.t, (1), 1. cultivate soil lightly, loosen top soil; turn up ground with a knife after clearing bush; cut light bush, weeds, down (preparatory to digging with hoe); ~ ngwacĩ, dig out a potato garden; cf. rĩma, cimba. 2. pick holes in sb.’s reputation, discredit, ridicule sb. (by uttering bad things about him); cf. cambia, umbũrũria. ceracera, dim, ceranga, diff. ceranira, app. rec, cerithia, d. caus. cereka, st, pot. cerera, app.

NEW_VALUE: of: the sound of cracking, breaking, esp. of a calabash
FULL_ENTRY: cere, ideo. (11/1), descr. of: x. the sound of cracking, breaking, esp. of a calabash. 2. the crack of a dry twig or leaf when trodden on; mũci cio ndũngĩgua ~, in that village you could not hear a sound not even a footfall; gamba ~, break in two. [see cereka]

mũcere, mĩ-, n. (vI), a mass of shattered objects. gĩcere, i-, n. a broken piece of sth., esp. of earthenware or calabash used by potters as a pallet. gacere, tũ-, n. dim. of above. [see cereka] [mũcereceri], &-, n. (1), a Seychellois., Cereceri, n. cl. 2 only, the Seychellois people. gĩrcereceri, n. cl. 7 only, language, customs, and habits of the Seychellois.

NEW_VALUE: of being off-white, dirty white, greyish, or of a dull light-coloured hue
FULL_ENTRY: cerehnkũ, ideo. (11), descr. of being off-white, dirty white, greyish, or of a dull light-coloured hue. [cerehũka]
cerehũra!, v.t. (11), 1. tear out; cf. tehũra, cehũra. 2. ladle out water from a pool with half-calabash. cerehũka, v.i. get torn.
cerehũra?, v.t. (1), (N.K.) make white, make lose colour; cf. theũra, erũhia, eregethũra. cerehũka, v.i. become bright, white, clean.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (esp. of gourds, calabashes, crockery) be badiy cracked
FULL_ENTRY: cereka, v.i. (11), (esp. of gourds, calabashes, crockery) be badiy cracked; crack through; cf. cere, atũka, methũka. ceracereka, dim. cerekanga, diff. cerekana, rec. used inten. break up into pieces, fall apart; (of a person) collapse ; theka kinya laugh until one collapses; nyua gũ~, be helplessly drunk. cerekanira, assoc, cerekania, caus. rec. cerekia, caus. Cerekithia, d. caus. cerekeka, st. pot. cerekera, app.

NEW_VALUE: of breaking up, falling apart of calabashes, beebarrels, snuff-boxes, clay pots, stones, &c
FULL_ENTRY: cerekanũ, ideo. (11), descr. of breaking up, falling apart of calabashes, beebarrels, snuff-boxes, clay pots, stones, &c. [cereka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: happen in respect of sb. before one is ready
FULL_ENTRY: cerera, v.t. (11), happen in respect of sb. before one is ready; catch out and involve in lateness ; happen before one is there or ready; ngari nĩ we have missed the train. cererũo, pass. be late; ~ nĩ, be late for. ceracerera, dim. ceracererũo, pass. dim. cererania, caus. rec. cereranira, app. rec, catch out and involve many people in lateness. cereria, caus. cererithia / cerithĩria, d. caus. cererithania / cerithaniria, rec. d. caus. cererera, inten. (of a meeting, &c.) happen much too early for one to be there. cerererũo, pass. inten. be very late.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. sth, white, a white-washed object
NEW_VALUE: a white ram, he-goat, ox, horse
FULL_ENTRY: gicerũ?, i-, n. (VII), I. sth, white, a white-washed object; mađnyũũ wa ~, chalk, lime. 2. (with cl. 9/10 concords) a white ram, he-goat, ox, horse. Mũhiki njaga atumaga ~, a naked man in his impatience makes the skin of a white goat into a garment. mũcerũ, mĩ-, n. (with cl. 9/10 concords) a white ewe. njerũ, ~, n. white female goat, sheep, cow. [see -erũ]
icetho [iceetho], ma-, n. (111), 1. display, game; (of sports) event. 2. (cl. 6) game, games, dance, sports ; organized display or competition; larks, amusements. [Sw. CHEZA]
cetho [ceetho], ~~, n. (1), chisel, carpenter’s chisel. [E.]

NEW_VALUE: when the stem begins with a vowel
FULL_ENTRY: ci, ideo. (U), descr. of: 1. pricking, piercing, e.g. with a thin arrow. 2. light stabbing and pricking with a lance, small knife, bodkin, or needle; ndoigto ~ n kaigua, I have been pricked by a small thorn. -, n. & p. prefix, (U), cl. 8, when the stem begins with a vowel; cf. i-.
-ci, stem of dem., cl. 8 and 10, (U).
-ci-, p. obj. infix, cl. 8 and 10o, (U).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: woman who has recently given birth to child, before she has got back to normal life
FULL_ENTRY: mũciairi, a-, n. (111), woman who has recently given birth to child, before she has got back to normal life; hiũhĩria ~, nurse a recently delivered mother. gaciairi, tũ-, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. wa- ~, (in folk-tales)mother. njiairi, ~, n. animal that has recently borne young. n. cl, 14 only. I1. white of egg; cf. maciarĩro, mĩruru. 2. condition of being a young mother.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. bear, bear fruit, give birth, give birth to
NEW_VALUE: bear, bear fruit, give birth, give birth to
FULL_ENTRY: ciara, v.t. & i. (11), 1. bear, bear fruit, give birth, give birth to; have a child, kid, calf, &c.; cf. gia mwana, ohoka. 2. adopt, take into blood brotherhood; see njiarano. ciaraciara, dim. ciaranga, diff. ciarana, rec. 1. make blood brotherhood, come into blood brotherhood, adopt sb. into one’s family or clan. 2. bear (each) a child to be named after each other. ciaranira, assoc. bear fruit, young ones, at the same time., Ciaraniria, caus. assoc. bear sth. together with sth. else, give birth to girl and boy twins. ciaranirira, inten. rec. bear children one after another. ciarithia, d. caus. 1r. deliver in childbirth; help to bear offspring, fruit; 2, (esp. of animals) cause to be conceived. 3. ~ Nhoro, exaggerate a story, add unwarranted glosses to a statement in course of transmission; cf. njung’ĩ. ciarithania, rec. d. caus. I1, act as a midwife. 2. graft (plants); cross breed. 3. cause to become one (as brothers), admit to blood brotherhood. 4. (with ref, to woodwork) join together, ciarithĩka, st. pot. d. caus. I. be capable of being delivered. 2. (of a tree) be fit to be grafted. ciarithiria, app. d. caus. 1. act as a midwife at, on behalf of. 2. help to bear fruit, offspring, at, for. ciarĩka, st. pot. 1. be capable of being born, produced, or grafted. 2. be suitable for being admitted into blood brotherhood. ciarira, app. ciaririra, inten. I. give a brother or sister to a previous child, give birth to another child following after a previous (living) one. 2. bear fruit after some other; bear offshoots. ciarĩrĩrũo, pass. inten.; kũra ũ~, (said as a blessing when a child sneezes) grow and you will get a sister (or brother), ciarũka, neut. cease to belong to a family or clan into which one has been adopted. ciarũkwo, pass, neut.; wega no tũ ~ nĩ mbari ya K., we should cease to be associated with Ks sub-clan, ciarũrũka, neut. inten. be prolific.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. a bearing of fruit, delivery in child-birth
FULL_ENTRY: iciara, ma-, n. (111), 1. a bearing of fruit, delivery in child-birth; mũtumia wa ~ rimwe, woman who has had one child. 2. (cl. 6) births, offspring, progeny; tua stop bearing, come to end of child-bearing. [ciara]
-ciare, a. (111), born. Kori ga~ karinĩ karĩ nda, the kid that is born is excelled by the one in the womb.
[ciara]
ciarera, see under rera,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: parent. njiari, ~, n. (of animal) one that has young, mother. ũciari, mociari [moociari], n. parenthood, progeny
FULL_ENTRY: mũciari, a-, n. (11), parent. njiari, ~, n. (of animal) one that has young, mother. ũciari, mociari [moociari], n. parenthood, progeny; igĩrĩra mũrũ wa nyina ~, raise up seed to deceased brother. [ciara]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: midwife (woman past child-bearing and ceremonial intercourse).
NEW_VALUE: midwife (woman past child-bearing and ceremonial intercourse)
FULL_ENTRY: mũciarithia, a-, n. (1v), midwife (woman past child-bearing and ceremonial intercourse). [ciara]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. sheep, goat, or cow bought before birth
FULL_ENTRY: gĩciaro, i-, n. (111), 1. sheep, goat, or cow bought before birth; still-born animal. 2. female genitalia; (of animals) vagina; cf. krino. rũciaro, njiaro, n. offspring, progeny, descendants; a generation; ~ ria a hen’s brood; cf, maciaro, n. cl. 6 only, produce; result(s), outcome, consequences. [ciara] (mi-), n. (1v), nature by birth, innate behaviour, natural endowment; Kamau e nũgĩ wa ~, K. is naturally gifted ; kĩrimũ kĩa ~, a fool by birth, a born fool. 2. manner of bearing fruit, giving birth, producing. [ciara]

iciariro, ma-, n. (III), 1. birthplace; point, time, at which sth. is produced. 2. (cl. 6) albumen, white of egg; afterbirth; cf. &ciairi, mĩruru, thigira. 3. (cl. 6) garments and leaves used for the purpose of delivery and afterwards destroyed. [ciara]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: that which has given birth, produced.
NEW_VALUE: that which has given birth, produced
FULL_ENTRY: ciaru, a. (111), that which has given birth, produced. [ciara]

nũciarũa, a-, n. (1v), one adopted into another clan; candidate for ceremony of second birth; see njiarano. rũciarũa, njiarũa, n. 1. generation, offspring. 2. genealogy, family tree, descendants; njiarũa na njtarũa, for all generations. [ciara]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: waistcoat. gacibaũ i gathibaũ, tũ-, n. dim, of above. nũcibe / mũcibĩ, mi-
NEW_VALUE: pointed stick esp. that at the top of a roof (taken down when the owner dies)
FULL_ENTRY: cibaũ [gĩcibaaũ] / gĩthibaũ [githibaaũ], i-, n. (v), waistcoat. gacibaũ i gathibaũ, tũ-, n. dim, of above. nũcibe / mũcibĩ, mi- ; njibe, ~; n. (v11), 1. pointed stick esp. that at the top of a roof (taken down when the owner dies). 2. skewer for roasting meat; cf. mũcobe. [see cibika] nũcibi, mi-, n. (vi), belt. [Sw. MSHIPI]

cibi, ideo. (1/11), descr. of: 1. penetration into sth., e.g. piercing with a pointed instrument, stabbing, pricking; cf, cini, ci. 2. diving under water. cCibi-cibi, ideo. descr. of: 1. repeated penetration, pricking. 2. the plunging of large numbers of frogs into water, of animals into tall grass, undergrowth, bush.

NEW_VALUE: the act of sudden penetration into , e
FULL_ENTRY: cibi, ideo. (11/1), descr. the act of sudden penetration into sth., e.g. when a duck dives into water, a spear is stuck in the ground. [cibĩka]

cibĩka, v.t. & i. (11), 1. (of ducks, &c.) dive, plunge; go, into, under, sth. 2. drive sth. into; pierce; fasten sth. with a skewer, jab, stick (a sharp instrument or weapon into); aa~ thĩ, he stuck the spear into the ground (still grasping it); see mũcibe; cf. cibĩ, cĩra. ĩcibĩka, refl. 1. plunge oneself into (river, bush, &c.). 2. (of a thrown weapon) stick in the ground. 3. stick sth. into oneself. cibacibika, dim. cibĩkanga, diff. cibĩkana, rec. (used inten.) get swiftly into sth., dive out of sight under sth., get completely submerged. cibĩkania, caus. rec. thrust sth. under, into; throw, plunge, into (river, water, &c.) forcibly. cibĩkanio, pass. caus. rec.; ar? gũ~, he has been forced under. cibikanira, assoc. plunge into, under, sth. together. cibĩkanĩria, caus. assoc. throw, drive, thrust, many objects together. cibĩkia, caus. throw, toss, fling, plunge, sth., into, under; make sth. disappear by plunging, thrusting, it, into, under; throw, toss, a sharp instrument (spear, knife, &c.) so that it sticks in. cibĩkithia, d. caus. cibĩkika, st. pot. cibĩkĩra, app. 1. drive sth. into sth. (e.g. skewer into meat when roasting, &c.). 2. dive, plunge, out of sight, at, because of; see cibi.
cibũ, ma-, n. cl. 1/2 or 6 (x1), chief. [E.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be glimpsed, appear momentarily or indistinctly
NEW_VALUE: look at oneself in a mirror (from vanity, &c
FULL_ENTRY: A [ciica], v.i. (1), be glimpsed, appear momentarily or indistinctly ; gleam, glimmer; become visible, show at a distance. cicacica, dim. cicanga, diff. cicanĩra, assoc. be glimpsed together. cicia, caus. I. shine out momentarily, give a glimpse of. 2. see sth, faintly, momentarily (at a distance), catch a glimpse of, peer at sth. fcicia, refl. caus. 1. look at oneself in a mirror (from vanity, &c.). 2. affect not to see sth. clearly. 3. (of a Kikuyu bride in traditional custom refusing to move towards her new home) affect stupidity, be uncompromising. cicithia, d. caus. cicĩka, st. pot. be visible at a distance, be just visible. cicĩra, app. 1. catch a glimpse of sth. at, from. 2. appear momentarily at, from. cicũka, neut. r. become fully visible, distinct; (of sky, bush, forest-land, &c.) become quite clear. 2. (of shaved head, boiled bones, &c.) become clean.
rũcici n. (1), 1. glimmer, glint, flash, brief glimpse. 2. stab of pain. [cica]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: x1. almost closed up (by disease}. 2. tender
FULL_ENTRY: -cici, a. (v11), x1. almost closed up (by disease}. 2. tender; (of eyes) watery.
mũcicia, (mĩ-), n. (x1), light rains, drizzle; time to plant potatoes. [cicia]

NEW_VALUE: of slow but continuous drizzle or spitting rain
FULL_ENTRY: cicicici, ideo. (1), descr. of slow but continuous drizzle or spitting rain. [cf. cicia]

cicingara, v.i. (I1), r. close up entirely; (of a path, clearance) become entirely overgrown; cf. cingara, hinga. 2. (of fire) die out, go out; dwindle, get extinguished. cicingaranira, assoc., cicingaria, caus. close up, extinguish; ~ kĩroto, extinguish the memory of a dream; ~ ngoma, extinguish a ‘spirit’ thought to be causing sickness (generally of children) (by pouring first beer then fat on a heated axe-head, and as each sizzles medicine man and family adjuring the spirit to depart); cf. cingamana, cicingarithia, d. caus. cicingarika, st. pot. cicingarira, app. close up, get extinguished, become dim, at, because of. cicingariria, caus. app.
gĩcicio [gĩciicio], i-, n. (111), 1. mirror, glass, glass-pane; cf. ngirathi. 2. (cl. 8) spectacles, eye-glasses; cf. mĩwani.

rũciciũ, (njiciũ), n. (v), drizzle, light rain; cf. rathuutũ. karũciciũ, tũ-, n. dim. of above., gaciciũ / gacicia [gaciciya], n. time of year associated with drizzle and cold, usually about July; light drizzle; cf. gathano; see mwaka. [cicia]
mũciciũ, mĩ-, n. (1v), a timber-tree (Premna maxima).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of a very wet road, floor of hut soaked with animal urine) be soppy, sloshy
NEW_VALUE: (of a very wet road, floor of hut soaked with animal urine) be soppy, sloshy
FULL_ENTRY: ciga, v.i. (1), 1. (of a very wet road, floor of hut soaked with animal urine) be soppy, sloshy; (of a person) be drenched; cf. cagaca, conjora. Cigaciga, dim. ciganga, diff. ciganiria, caus. assoc, make sloshy at the same time. cigia, caus. make sloshy, soppy; drench. cigĩka, st. pot. cigira, app.
gĩcigirĩra, i-, n. (v), r. island; island in middle of stream. 2. any circumscribed area; cf. gĩcigo. [cigirĩra]
mũcihiri, a-, n. (111), Arab labourer from S. Arabia; half-breed; cf. cotara, [Sw. MSHIHIRI]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (esp. of fruit, sugar-cane, cloth, &c. when wrung or squeezed) ooze, drip. 2. rain gently, drizzle, spit with rain
FULL_ENTRY: cihbĩka [ciihĩka], v.i. (1), 1. (esp. of fruit, sugar-cane, cloth, &c. when wrung or squeezed) ooze, drip. 2. rain gently, drizzle, spit with rain; cf. cicia, 3. grizzle, whimper, sob; cf. cahika. cihacihika, dim. cihĩkanga, diff. cihĩkanĩra, assoc., cihĩkia, caus. I. eject water, fluid, wind in small quantities. 2. make, grizzle, whimper, &c. cihikithia, d. caus. cihĩkĩka, st. pot. cihĩkĩra, app. 1. ooze, eject steam, water, air, at or for. 2. drizzle at, 3. whimper for.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: dead-nettle (Leonotis mollissima) (conspicuous by its orangered flowers, used for making medicine).
NEW_VALUE: flowers of above, much frequented by sun-birds
FULL_ENTRY: mũcii, mi-, n. (v), dead-nettle (Leonotis mollissima) (conspicuous by its orangered flowers, used for making medicine). njii, ~, n. 1. flowers of above, much frequented by sun-birds. 2. flowers of banana. icii, ma-, n. plant, leaf, and flower of dead-nettle.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. long string of small beads wound round and round the wrist to half-way up forearm
NEW_VALUE: long string of small beads wound round and round the wrist to half-way up forearm
FULL_ENTRY: gĩcii?, i-3, n. (v), 1. long string of small beads wound round and round the wrist to half-way up forearm ; cf. (N.K.) thang’a, mohunyo. 2. (N.K) woman’s neck ornament composed of beadwork on leather neckband with attached ostrich egg-shell pieces and long fine chains in front; cf. njema.

NEW_VALUE: of: continuance of an unsatisfactory, ailing condition
FULL_ENTRY: ci-i-i, ideo. (u), descr. of: r. continuance of an unsatisfactory, ailing condition; uga, gamba, ~, get weaker and weaker. 2. hissing of green firewood; sizzling, slow frying; mwaki Ũragamba o ~, the fire is just smouldering away. 3. obtuseness; gamba ~, stay stubbornly silent.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. stint, give less to one than to others. 2. give grudgingly
NEW_VALUE: stint, give less to one than to others
FULL_ENTRY: ciika, v.t. & i. (11), 1. stint, give less to one than to others. 2. give grudgingly ; be stingy. ciikaciika, dim. ciikanga, diff. ciikana, rec. give others grudgingly, stint one another. ciikanira, app. rec. or assoc. ciikithia, d. caus. ciikĩka, st. pot. ciikĩra, app. stinge, stint, &c., because of, at.
ciithi, ~, n. (1), cheese. [E.]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. homestead, collection of huts belonging to a man and his dependants
FULL_ENTRY: mũcii, mĩ-, n. (1), 1. homestead, collection of huts belonging to a man and his dependants; home, dwellingplace; town, city; cf. itnũra, gwitũ. 2. (coll) young girl. gacĩ, tũ-, n. little home; ĩgeria ~, set up house on one’s own, get married. kamũcii, tũ-, n. 2nd dim. I. a hamlet. 2. a charming young girl. Gmũcii, n. cl. 14 only, domestic matters. kĩũmũcĩ, ci-, n. domestic economy, affairs pertaining to homestead.
gacii?, see under rũi.
cĩka, v.t. (1), 1. put on a neophyte’s garment. 2. (of young women) put on a protective under-garment.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. first garment worn by girl after initiation. 2. calico skirt worn instead of leather one
NEW_VALUE: first garment worn by girl after initiation
FULL_ENTRY: gĩciiko, i-, n. (111), 1. first garment worn by girl after initiation. 2. calico skirt worn instead of leather one; cf. mũthuru; see kariko. 3. petticoat worn under frock. [cĩka]

cĩra [ciyĩra], v.t. (1), drive sth. forcibly into; put, stick forcibly into; plant, fill up, with; cf. thinĩkĩra. rciira, refl. plant at, dig, settle, oneself comfortably, in, at; ensconce oneself, take up a position at, be implanted in. ciaciira, dim. ciiranga, diff. cĩrana, rec. cĩranĩra, app. rec. drive into sth. together. cĩranĩria, caus. app. rec. ciirithia, d. caus. cĩrika, st.. pot. cĩrĩra, app. ĩciirira, refl. nĩwĩciiriire mũcii, the malady has got firm hold of the homestead.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of the cry of the kite. cika, see cinga. gĩciko, i-, n. (1), spoon. gaciko, tũ-, n. small spoon

(of excessive cold or frost) blast, shrivel. 4. hurt, offend (with words); cause confusion, unrest in (people, home, country, &c.). cinwo, pass.; ~ nĩ marakara, be consumed with anger. fcina, refl. cause grave harm to oneself. cinacina, dim. cinanga, diff. cinana, rec. cinanira, app. rec. cinaniria, caus. app. rec. burn (things), together. cinithia, d. caus. cinĩka!, st. pot. cinira, app.; nĩwe wanjinĩra you are the one who deprived me of my house by burning it. cinĩrĩra, inten. burn on to, into; brand with; melt (sealingwax, &c.) on to; burn sth. over, in, sth. else. cinĩrĩria, caus. inten. (N.K.) as inten.

FULL_ENTRY: gĩcina-bangi, (i-), n. (1-1), (coll.) name given to young men and women who have been initiated but have not yet attained fuli adult status; irresponsible youth. [cina]

cinĩka?, v.t. NEW_VALUE: help one another in arguing out a case
FULL_ENTRY: cira [ciira], v.i. & t. (N.K.) dispute with one another or with people, go to law with one another. ciranira, rec. app. assoc. 1. help one another in arguing out a case. 2. plot, conspire, against; plot to do sb. harm. ciraniria, caus. assoc. discuss, argue, two or more cases together, ciria, caus. (N.K.) bring a charge against, call to account; remonstrate with. fciria, refl. caus. think, ponder, consider, reflect, suppose, consider sth. cirithia, d. caus. prosecute, call to account, being a charge against; make, answer, a charge; bring to judgement, sit in judgement on; remonstrate with; assist in hearing, conducting, a dispute. refl. d. caus. ponder over sth., think very carefully ; ~ na ngoro, reason With oneself, battle with one’s conscience. cirithania, rec. d. caus. prosecute (people). cirithanĩria, prosecute others because of; hear, conduct, disputes, at, for. cirithĩka, st. pot. d. caus. be culpable, be suitable for a charge to be brought against one. cirithiria, app. d. caus, charge, for, at, prosecute because of. cirika, st. pot. be debatable, arguable. fcirika, refl. st. pot. be capable of being thought about; ũhoro wa thoni Ũtangĩ~, a shameful affair which does not bear thinking about. cirĩkĩra, app. st. pot. cirĩra, app. I1. make a case, argue, go to law, about; conduct a case, on behalf of, because of, at, for; rule, order, conduct the affairs of; ~ represent people in governing bodies. 2. prepare, ordain, decide, on sth.; fka ũndũ Ũtaũcirũre, do sth. unintentionally. 3. scheme, plot, against. ĩcirira, refl. app. defend oneself in a case, stand up for one’s rights. fciri-ria, refl. caus. app. suspect, think ill of, sb. cirirĩra, inten. plead on behalf of sb., conduct a case for (esp. sb. who is hard pressed), act as an advocate for sb. absent; argue in favour of, plead for, sth. fciririra, refl. inten. plead for oneself. cirũka, neut., be finished with a case (because decided or abandoned). cirũkwo, rĩu nĩnjirũkĩtwo nĩ ng'ania, so-and-so has now dropped his case against me. cirũrũka, inten. neut. be finished with a case entirely.

coka [cooka], v.i. & t. (1), I1. return; come, go, back; return to; ~ na kiu, get away with you; ~ na mũhuro, go down, to the bottom; ~ mũci, mondo, (get into a man’s home, pocket) act treacherously, deceitfully, behind sb.’s back (by betraying his confidence, making love to his wife, &c.). 2. (often used as aux. v. with ady. meaning) (do) again, then, after that; ndiga~ gũthĩ, I shall never go again; agĩ~ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, after that he told him this. 3. (used as prep.); ~ na gũkũ, back to here; ~ na from there upwards; kuuma ratiri igĩrĩ na igũrũ, from two pounds upwards; cf. njoka. refl. reexamine one’s action or speech, take a second thought, reconsider sth., think again; ~ ngoro, re-examine one’s motives, cokacoka, dim. go, come, back a little (more); withdraw a short distance. cokacokia, caus. dim. bring back a little; ~ thutha, pull, push, back a little. cokanga, diff. cokana, rec. (of parts of whole) go back, fit, into position. cokania, caus. rec, 1, put back into position, reassemble parts of sth., put back sth. disarranged; put back, reassemble sth. anyhow. 2. return together with, return at same time as; ~ na, go back together with. cokanira, assoc. cokanĩria, caus. assoc. I1. reply to each other, reply to others (letters, questions, &c.); return sth. to others, give back, take back to people, return to one another; gũthii na gũ~, go to and fro, 2. argue, cross words; ~ ciugo, altercate, wrangle. cokanirira, inten. rec. 1. come together in a group, congregate in one place. 2. resume relations, resume mutual activity (such as fighting). 3. contract, shrink. cokanĩriria, caus. inten. rec. bring (back) together, consolidate, collect up, amass; ~ nguo, put clothes decently around the body. fcokanĩrĩria, refl. caus. inten. rec. draw close(r) together. cokia, caus. 1. return, send sth. back; take, bring, back; restore, give back; ~ rigĩ (mũrango), shut a door; ~ mĩhũmũ, breathe in, recover breath; ~ mũndũ mũcii, perform sacrifice to restore sb. to his home after being left in bush to die and subsequently recovering, save sb. from an embarrassment, say or do sth. for sb. in difficulties; ~ ngorono ya mwana, return child’s butterfat, perform sexual acts which bring initiation ceremonial of Masai Guild to a close; ~ mwana ihu-inĩ, perform ceremony of secondbirth, see njiarano; ~ ithagumia, chew cud; ~ makinya kwa nyina, (of bride) retrace steps to mother (pay first ceremonial visit on eighth day after marriage carried pick-a-back by girl friends). 2. reply to, answer (question, letter, &c.); ~ macokio, bring back reply to message; ~ kĩũria, answer a question; ~ horo, report back. 3. (of maize plants) die back; ~ sniff, die back; ~ muunge, become cowed, cease to be aggressive. fcokia, refi. caus. r. grumble, complain, for being singled out, feel slighted, aggrieved, chagrined. 2. go, back, on one’s own, return unaided; ~ mwĩrĩ, repeat an action which is harmful to oneself, cokithia, d. caus. help sb. to return sth., cause sth. to be returned. cokeka, st. pot.; kĩũria gĩtangĩ~, an unanswerable question. cokera, app. 1. go, come back, for; return at, for; ~ njĩra, turn back on the way. 2. repeat, do again (and again); ~ njoko, see under njoko; ndiga ~, I shall not do it again, I repent. cokeria, caus. app. I. give, take, back to; return sth.; ~ ngaatho, thank, pay tribute to. 2. regain a normally plump condition (after illness, journey, &c.). 3. reply, answer. cokerithia, d. caus. app. I. repeat; say, do, same thing over again, do repeatedly; make repeat, do again; ta ~ rĩngĩ, say, sing, it again, strike him again, make another attempt; gũ~ ti ũkĩgu, it is not silly to ask again. 2. repeat what is said by another. cokerithiria, d. caus. inten. repeat all over again (a statement or story); cause to repeat because of. cokereka, st. pot. app. be suitable for being returned for, repeated. cokerera, inten. I. go back, return, to; resume sth. 2. (of disease) attack sb. again; come back on, revert to. 3. come, do, next; follow on; ambĩorĩria na nii njokerere, start and I shall follow suit. 4. go back to the sarne thing again and again, be narrow-minded, dull, stupid, dim-witted. 5. put blame on; accuse sb. for one’s failure, difficulties; ndaagaga wa gũ~, he always finds a scapegoat. cokererũo, pass. inten. be revisited by sth., be made a scapegoat; ~ nĩ have a relapse, have a recurrence of disease. cokereria, caus. inten. 1. bring together; gather up, into a heap; reassemble; ni kũndũ kũingi matũcokagiriria na tũtarĩ naguo, in many cases they put all the blame on us when we are not the cause of the trouble; ~ mbeca, save money. 2. repair, reconstruct; ~ mũndũ, restore sb. (after a lapse); ~ mwaki, mend a fire, draw logs together. cokoroka, neut. conv. I, return at once by the same way. 2. retract a promise, change one’s mind in respect of sth. cokorokwo, pass. neut. conv. have food delayed in preparation (owing to inadequate fire, &c.). rcokoroka, refl. neut. conv. also rcokorokwo, pass. refl. neut. conv. repent of a certain course of action, change one’s mind or intentions; cf. ĩricakwo. cokorokia, caus. neut. conv.; ~ makinya, retrace one’s steps (esp. on the second ceremonial return visit of a bride to parents’ home after the consummation of marriage; in N.K. and elsewhere she was led by a young girl holding the end of her walking-stick while her eyes were covered by a bunch of leaves).

(11), long mark, stripe, pand of colour, ribbon. mũcoora | mũcoore., mi-, n. stripe, streak; kĩgwa kĩa ~, sp. of striped sugar-cane; cf. nyagikaara. gĩcoora?, i-, n. aug. of above. nyamũcoora l nyamicoora | nyamgcoore | nyagacoore, ~, n. striped variety of sugar-cane.
mũcoore, see mŨcoora.

NEW_VALUE: youths’ clubhouse or hut, built at some distance from the homestead, to which young men and their girl partners resort (provided with many false entrances and concealed ways of escape for occupants if disturbed by irate parents)
FULL_ENTRY: icoorero, ma-, n. (1), 1. youths’ clubhouse or hut, built at some distance from the homestead, to which young men and their girl partners resort (provided with many false entrances and concealed ways of escape for occupants if disturbed by irate parents); cf. waguthũ, (wa)ndomo. 2. (N.K.) girls bed; cf. kĩrĩri. [coora?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who participates in a procession.

FULL_ENTRY: mnũcoori, &-, n. (1), one who participates in a procession. [coora"]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: v.i. (1), be very wet, soaking, drenched
FULL_ENTRY: cora?), v.i. (1), be very wet, soaking, drenched; cf. conjora, cagaca. coria, caus. wet thoroughly, soak, make sodden. coria, refl. coracoria, dim. corangia, diff. corania, rec. coraniria, assoc. Corithia, d. caus. cause sth. to be very wet, help sb. to make sth. sodden, very wet.

icorethi [icooreethi], ma-, n. (xv1), insurance. [E.]

FULL_ENTRY: cori-cori, ideo. (1), descr. of hubbub, everyone talking at once; cf, tori-tori.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: oryx antelope. mi-, n. (v1), a tapered end
FULL_ENTRY: coroa [cooroa], ~, n. (VI), oryx antelope. mi-, n. (v1), a tapered end; ngo ya ~, a tapering shield; cf. mcobe. [coroba]

NEW_VALUE: feet squelching in very wet mud
FULL_ENTRY: corocoro, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1. feet squelching in very wet mud. 2. disjointed, incoherent, muddled speech, repetitive like that of a weak-minded person; see njoore. [cora?]

NEW_VALUE: strip, belt, swathe of dug land, cut maize, mown grass
FULL_ENTRY: mũcorori [mũcoroori] / mũcororia [mũcorooria], mĩ-, n. (1), 1. strip, belt, swathe of dug land, cut maize, mown grass. 2. narrow garment, apron; cf. cũrũri. [cororia]

NEW_VALUE: cut a narrow strip of hide, cloth, &c

COrorangia, diff. cororanĩria, app. rec. cororithia, d. caus. cororeka, st. pot. cororeria, app.
rũcororia [rũcorooria], njororia, n. (11), strip of ground, &c.

NEW_VALUE: (of goatskin, cloth, ground, garden) a narrow strip
FULL_ENTRY: gacororua [gacoroorua], tũ-, n. (v), (of goatskin, cloth, ground, garden) a narrow strip. [see cororia]

FULL_ENTRY: mĩũcoruĩ [mũcoorui], mĩ-, n. (vi), 1. tree (Lachnopylis congesta). 2. tree (Bucdleia salvifolia) (wood used as soft wood for fire stick); cf. mwanda.
cotara, ma-, n. with cl. 1/2 concords (v), half-caste. [Sw. CHOTARA]
rũcoya, see under njoya.
icoya, ma-, n. (11), 1. banana leaf; macoya maakira, see under kira!; first leaves of maize, leaves of young maize plants; see irigũ.
cua', see cau,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of smart cutting (e.g. through a stick of sugar-cane or striking sb. with a switch or cane, &c.)
FULL_ENTRY: cua’, ideo. (U), descr. of smart cutting (e.g. through a stick of sugar-cane or striking sb. with a switch or cane, &c.); gambia ~, cut through, strike with switch.

NEW_VALUE: a close group, bunch, clump, knot, of people or trees
FULL_ENTRY: gĩcua, i-, n. (1) 1. a close group, bunch, clump, knot, of people or trees; a closely built collection of huts. 2. a large heap of rubbish or leaves; a bonfire. icua, ma-, n. bonfire of garden rubbish; mba ~, make a ceremonial fire of bones, grill of sticks, and leaves on which the goat has been skinned together with certain twigs of shrubs employed in the ritual; koria ~ na njohi, extinguish the embers with beer or ceremonially by drinking beer. gacua, tũ-, n. r1. dim. of above. 2. ~ ka andũ, a group of people; cf. gĩkundi.
gacuacui / gacnacũi, tũ-, n. (111), sp. of honey-eater. [cua!]

cuba [cuuba], ~ / ma-, cl. o/10 0r 14/6; mũcuba, mĩ-; n. (1), 1. bottle, glass jar; ~ wa cai, a flask of tea; cokia ~, (coll.) make sb. feel small, bring to ridicule in public. 2. (cl. 6} broken pieces of glass; ruta macuba, (of medicine man) take pieces of glass (supposed causes of pains and twinges) out of an ailing person’s body. gicuba, i-, n. broken piece of glass, glass bottle, jar. [Sw. CHUPA]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. break up, cut, split (sugar-cane, firewood, &c.) into short or smail pieces
FULL_ENTRY: cuca, v.t. (1), 1. break up, cut, split (sugar-cane, firewood, &c.) into short or smail pieces; cf. bebera?. 2. soften (food) by adding soft ingredients to hard ones. 3. pound maize or millet lightly (to remove husks before making gruel); add millet to maize to change the flavour. 4. (of weather) make wanting in energy, make lethargic; see boriga. refl. r. turn things over in the mind pretentiously. 2. behave, act, awkwardly. 3. be coy, shy; cf. una, ĩregithia. cucacuca, dim. cucanga, diff, cucana, rec, cucanira, app. rec. Cucaniria, caus. app. rec., cucaniriria, inten. caus. app. rec. pile up, heap together; cf. rũndanirira, hũranĩrĩra. cucithia, d. caus, cucĩka, st. pot. 1. be weary, exhausted (usually as a result of being in the sun); cf. noga. 2. be capable of being broken up, loosened, smashed. cucĩkĩra, app. st. pot. cucĩra, app. rĩcucĩra, refl. app. cucũra, conv, 1. add an ingredient to reduce the strength, alter the flavour (of gruel, &c.). 2. make (colours) fade. 3. kĩruru nigigũgũ~, the shade will relieve you of your weariness. cucũka, neut. (of a mixture) become right in consistency; (of a weary person) become refreshed; (of colour) fade, bleach, become washed out, dulled ; cf. theũka, ragatha, hia.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sound used in driving goats from the bush.
NEW_VALUE: sound used in driving goats from the bush
FULL_ENTRY: cu-cu ... -cCu, interj. (U), sound used in driving goats from the bush.

cucu-cucu, ideo. (1), descr. of: ir. spitting rain, drizzle; mbura ya ~, light drizzle; ndeto cia ~, persistent urgent, pestering, repetition; see njicii, heeba. 2. a derisive or deprecatory expression directed at sb. who is being presumptuous; ~ mwanake!!, softly young man!

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. make certain, be sure of one’s facts. fcucura, refl. show off, air one’s knowledge
NEW_VALUE: make certain, be sure of one’s facts
FULL_ENTRY: cucura [cuucuura], v.i. (11), 1. make certain, be sure of one’s facts. fcucura, refl. show off, air one’s knowledge; wĩrcucure, (said by a young man to a girl who has taken his fancy at a mixed dance) let me have a look at you, cucuranga, diff. cucurithia, d. caus. ; cf. thuthuria, iting’ia, tĩtĩrithia, rĩngĩthĩra. ĩcucurithia, refl. d. caus. cucurika, st. pot. cucurira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: throw, hurl, fling (a club, short stick, big knife, &c.) through the air
NEW_VALUE: throw, hurl, fling (a club, short stick, big knife, &c
FULL_ENTRY: cugucia, v.t. (1), throw, hurl, fling (a club, short stick, big knife, &c.) through the air; sve nyuguto; cf. ikia. cugacugucia, dim. cugucangia, diff. cugucania, rec. cugucaniria, app. rec. cugucithia, d. caus. cugucika, st. pot. cuguciria, app.
(gi) (mũ) cuguthi, see mũcaguthi.

cuha [cuuha], v.i. & t. (1), r. hang loosely, suspended; swing, hang; cf. cunjura. 2. play about with sb.; cf. thaaka. cuhacuha, dim. cubanga, diff. cuhana, rec. cuhania, caus, rec. hang things haphazardly. cuhanira, assoc. Cuhaniria, caus. assoc. cuhia, caus. cause to hang loosely, suspend, swing; cf. cuuria. fcuhia, refl. swing oneself; cf. ccuuria. cuhithia, d. caus. cuhĩka, st. pot. cuhĩra, app. cuhũra, conv. fcukũra, refi. conv. cuhũrithia, d. caus. conv. cuhũrũra, inten. conv. cuhũrũrũka, neut. conv. cuhũka, neut. (of sth. suspended) drop, fall; become unhung, unhooked; (of bird) swoop down, pounce, stoop; cf. cuurũka. cuhbũkira, app. neut. drop, pounce, at.

NEW_VALUE: two sticks, crutches (used by a cripple to swing himself along)
FULL_ENTRY: mũcuha / mũcuho [mũcuuho] / (N.k.) mũnjuha [mũnjuuha]l, mĩ-, n. (VIII), 1. swing; hanging branch on which children swing; aerial root; trailer, hanging rope or string; anything like a pendant, a hanging cluster of fruit, &c.; cf. 2. (cl. 4) two sticks, crutches (used by a cripple to swing himself along). [cuha]
gacui, see under njui.
rũcuiri, see njuiri,

cuka, v.i. (11), disembark, alight. cukanga, diff. cukanĩra, assoc. alight at the same time as. cukia, caus. unload, make alight. cukithia, d. caus. help sb. to unload, make alight. cukika, st. pot. cukira, app. [Sw. SHUKA]
Gĩcuka, n. cl. 7 or 15b (111), Nairobi. [cuka]
(gi-) (ga-) cuku, see mŨOgacikũ.

cukuma, v.t. (I) gnaw at; cf. thũgũna. cukumanga, diff. cukumithia, d. caus. (of a vendor) press sth. upon a reluctant person by urging its virtues; seek to fasien guilt, responsibility, on sb.; athuri nĩ maram~ Ũru ũcio, the elders are trying to get him to admit his crime. ĩrcukumithia, refl. d. caus. ingratiate oneself, curry favour with. ĩcukumithania, refl. rec. d. caus. curry favour, be over-officious; cf. endereria.

cuma [cuuma], v.t. (11), plunder, ransack (persons, premises); deprive sb. of sth., deny sb. sth. valuable; strip anything that is valuable (from a dying person, &c.); ~ mathaga, strip off personal ornaments. refi. deny oneself sth. cumacuma, dim. cumanga, dif. cumana, rec. cumithia, d. caus. cumĩra, app.; ithe ndaigana his father would not be drawn, declined to answer, took no interest. [cuuma], ~ | ma-, n. cl. 9/10 or 6 (1), 1. iron, steel; big piece of iron or steel; cf. kĩgera. 2. (cl. 6) parts of a machine. gĩcuma, i-, n. r. big piece of iron or steel. 2. part of a machine. [Sw. CHUMA]
gacuma, see under njuma,
mũcumaa, mi-, n. (111), candle. [Sw. MSHUMAA]

miũcumari! [macumaari], mi-, n. (1), nail. [Sw. MSUMARI] A- / Cumari, n. (1), Somali. Cumari, n. with loc. concords, Somaliland. gĩcumari [gicumaari], n. cl. 7 only, 1. Somali language. 2. Somali ways, customs, habits. [E.]
-cumikani, a. (111), that which tantalizes. [cumia]
ũcumikiri, n. cl 14 only (11, covetousness. [cumia]

cumbi, (ma-), n. cl. 14/6 (Iv), 1. salt, table-salt; ~ wa mahiga, rough salt, rock salt. 2. ĩkĩra ~, exaggerate, present a matter in a distorted or fanciful way in order to make it sound better or worse, put spice into a story; cf. kĩnigũ. [Sw. cHumvI]
mũcuna, mi-, n. (VIn), tree (Pavonia kraussiana).

FULL_ENTRY: cunia [cuunia], v.t. (11), x. twist (hair) into little knots (prior to transforming into ringlets). 2. distort (statements).
(i-) macungo?, n, (1), siftings from fiour. [cunga]
-cungucu, a. (1v), lanky, tall. [cunguca]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. cl. 14/6 (v), oddments, odds and ends
FULL_ENTRY: cungucungu, (ma-), n. cl. 14/6 (v), oddments, odds and ends; little things taken on a journey; miscellaneous small articles in a hut or house; scraps of stuff; little bits of meat cut by a butcher to make up weight. gacungucungu, tũ-, n. scrap, oddment; cf, gacingicingi. 2. (cl. 13) odds and ends, details, minutiae.
gĩcunji, i-, n. (1), piece, part, portion, section; cf. krenyũ. gacunji, tũ-, n. I1. dim. of above. 2. verse or other sub-division of a chapter. macunji, n. ci. 6 only, large portions.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of sb. afraid to speak but wishing to make his presence known) sit or stand silent or in silent appeal. ĩcunjuma, refl. cunjumanga, diff. cunjumanira, assoc, cunjumia, caus. hanker after, lick lips with desire
FULL_ENTRY: cunjuma, v.i. (11), (of sb. afraid to speak but wishing to make his presence known) sit or stand silent or in silent appeal. ĩcunjuma, refl. cunjumanga, diff. cunjumanira, assoc, cunjumia, caus. hanker after, lick lips with desire; ~ maitho, put on appealing expression wishing to be taken notice of; cf. cũnjũma.

cunjura [cunjuura], v.i. (1) 1. be suspended, loosely attached; hang downwards, trail behind, droop. 2. hang about, wait around for sth. (as hangers on at a feast hoping to get bits of meat given away); cf. cuha, cuuhirĩra. cunjacunjura, dim. cunjuranga, diff. cunjuranira, assoc. cunjuraniria, caus. assoc. cunjuria, caus. cause to hang downwards, make trail behind, suspend. cunjurithia, d. caus. cunjurĩka, st. pot. 1. be dangled, be capable of suspension. 2. show eagerness, desire, for sth. cunjurikĩra, app. st. pot.; ~ nyama, have a scarcely restrainable hankering after (meat, gruel, &c.); ~ irua, be all impatient for initiation. cunjurira, app. cunjuriria, caus. app. cunjurũka, neut. fall, come, down in jerks, suddenly with a crash (as a result of getting untied or losing grip or support).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a long piece of sth. broken off and hanging down, a tatter
NEW_VALUE: a long piece of broken off and hanging down, a tatter
FULL_ENTRY: mũcunjuri [mũcunjuuri], mĩ-, n. (vu), a long piece of sth. broken off and hanging down, a tatter; cf. mcũnjũri. [cunjura]
cunya, see tunya.

NEW_VALUE: of charred or burnt black, kĩa ~, be burnt out completely
FULL_ENTRY: curu', ideo. (1), descr. of sth. charred or burnt black, kĩa ~, be burnt out completely; ira ~, be pitch black (as sth. charred); cf. ciri?, cua, biũ, buru; see cura.

FULL_ENTRY: curu?, ideo. (1) descr. of a bird’s chirp or similar sounds; ~/ sound made by people calling a chicken; ndinatigua ~, I didpt hear a sound, not even a chirp; cf. ciri!.

NEW_VALUE: of prowling in eager search for
FULL_ENTRY: curucuru, ideo. (1), descr. of prowling in eager search for sth.; also of the individual cheeping of birds in early morning before the dawn chorus.

NEW_VALUE: cull, pick out, select (tender ends of sweet potato vines for planting as cuttings, protruding twigs of a hedge, poor twigs of coffee-tree for pruning, &c
FULL_ENTRY: curuma, v.t. (11), cull, pick out, select (tender ends of sweet potato vines for planting as cuttings, protruding twigs of a hedge, poor twigs of coffee-tree for pruning, &c.); cf. thuura, cagũra, thaũra, ceeha, cururuma, dim. curumanga, diff. curuma-nira, app. rec. curumaniria, caus. | app. rec. curumia, caus. curumithia, d. caus. curumika, st. pot.
