NEW_VALUE: search closely, look for carefully (among other things)
FULL_ENTRY: cururia [curuuria], v.t. (1), search closely, look for sth. carefully (among other things); cf. uruuria, caria. icururia, refl. search one’s heart, memory; cf. cthuthuria. cururangia, diff. cururana, rec, cururania, caus. rec. Cururaniria, caus. app. rec. cCururithia, d. caus, cururĩka, st. pot.
mũcururu, see thururu,.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of sound made by a horn, or a missile travelling in the air
NEW_VALUE: of sound made by a horn, or a missile travelling in the air
FULL_ENTRY: curururu, ideo. (1), descr. of sound made by a horn, or a missile travelling in the air; gambia ~, howl, throw, blow.

NEW_VALUE: one who betrays or informs against others, slanderer, backbiter, accuser of person or people in their absence
FULL_ENTRY: mũcuukani, a-, n. (x1), I1. one who betrays or informs against others, slanderer, backbiter, accuser of person or people in their absence. 2. name used to refer to devil, Satan. [cuuka]
rũcuukũ, see under njuukũ.
(gĩ-) (ga-) cuuri, see under njuuri.
gicuuri, i-, n. (vin), hanger-on, sponger.
gĩcuuru, see under njuuru:!,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of an object in flight or falling
NEW_VALUE: of an object in flight or falling
FULL_ENTRY: cuuu, ideo. (U), descr. of an object in flight or falling; akĩgamba rũgiri ~, he jumped clear over the fence; cf. huu; see ĩcuura.
icũ (N.K.), see iyũ.
(gi-) (i-) (ma-) cũa, see under rũa.
gacũacũi, see gacuacui.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) decorated frontlet or long apron covering the breast and reaching to the knees (worn formerly by giris and young men)
FULL_ENTRY: cũbi [cũbi], ma-, n. cl. 14/6 (1), (N.K.) decorated frontlet or long apron covering the breast and reaching to the knees (worn formerly by giris and young men); cf. ~ / (ma-); ~ | mocũ [moocũ]; n. (vr) (cl. 1/2 concords), 1. my grandmother, my grandmother’s co-wife; my classificatory grandmother. 2. term of address by grandmothers to their grandchildren. 3. ~-wa-njoka, sp. of heron. cũguo [cũguo], ~ | ma-; ũcũguo ~ / mocũguo [moocaũguo]; your grandmother. cawe [cũwe], ~, ma-; ũcũwe [ũcũaũwe], ~ / mocũwe [moocũwe]; his, her, grandmother. gacũcũ, tũ-, n. (a term of affection) dear granddaughter (grandmother).

NEW_VALUE: one who wanders all over the place
FULL_ENTRY: mũcũgi?, mi-, n. (11) one who wanders all over the place. [cũga]} mi-, n. (1v), bird’s neck; long neck of kettle, &c.; cf. mũkingo, mũcũri.
rũcũgi, njngi, n. (1v), (N.K.) clever person; smart, cunning, fellow; cf. njũhiga.
mũcũgo, mi-, n. (111), gadding about. ~ nĩ nguĩko, restless wandering ends in temporary attachments. [cũga]
cOfuo, see under CACNŨ,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: stem of bushpea (Cajanus) (when dried used as fuel). njngũ, ~, n. 1. seed of tree-, bushor pigeon-pea (used as food)
FULL_ENTRY: mũcũgũ, mĩ-, n. (11), stem of bushpea (Cajanus) (when dried used as fuel). njngũ, ~, n. 1. seed of tree-, bushor pigeon-pea (used as food); cf. minji, nyagĩkoro. 2. name given to female goat or cow of speckled colour, reddish with white flecks. gicũgũ, i-, n. I. whole pigeon-pea plant. 2. (usu. with cl. 9/10 concords) he-goat, ox or bull of speckled colour. icaagũ, ma-, n. I1. leaf of pea-plant. 2. (cl. 6) flecks of cojour, speckled appearance. gatũ-, n. 1. small pigeon-pea plant. 2. (thabuni wa) ~, rough blue and white mottled soap (used for washing clothes); cf. thandaraiti.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. small diurnal animal which dodges about like a hare
NEW_VALUE: small diurnal animal which dodges about like a hare
FULL_ENTRY: gacngũ?, tũ-, n. (111), 1. small diurnal animal which dodges about like a hare; cf. mbũkũ. 2. moving about, movement; cf. njnũgũ’. [cũga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: small woody herb, wild ancestor of pigeonpea (Indigofera)}ũ, of two kinds
FULL_ENTRY: mũcũggũcũgũ, mĩ-, n. (v), small woody herb, wild ancestor of pigeonpea (Indigofera)}ũ, of two kinds: i. growing in swamps. 2. growing on dry ground (twigs used as toothbrushes; sticks inserted in perforated ear-lobes of neophytes during initiation rites; roots make good whistle pipes). n. cl. rr, as above. v.i. (11), (of fruit or vegetables) turn yellow, red, or other colour in ripening; become meliow, be very ripe; (of skin) become light in hue as result of burn, abrasion, swelling, or boil; nĩ icũagũkĩte, the peas are ripe; cf. kũgũka, caũka. dim. cagũkanga, diff. cũgũkanira, assoc. ripen at the same time as sth. else; ripen all together. cnagũkia, caus. cũgũkira, app. Marignũ ma mũkari macũgũkagira mũgũnda, the bananas of a mean person ripen in the garden. cũgũra, v.t. I. cause (fruit) to ripen (by exposure to sun); cause (skin) to change colour (by action of boiling water, whipping, &c.). 2. (coll.) give a hiding to, thrash thoroughly; cf. hiũkia, cũtha, cathũra. icũgũra, refl. make oneself look young, spruce oneself up. dim. cũgũranga, diff. caagũrana, rec.
gĩcũgũma, see under njagũma.

gĩcũhi, i-, n. (1x), 1. ring, hook (of metal); ~ gĩa kĩara, finger-ring; ~ kĩa gũtũ, ear-ring worn by men; ~ kĩa mbugi, ring joining clapper to goat or cow-bell; ~ kĩa rĩtiena, earring to which pieces of brass chain are attached. 2. (cl. 8 only) ringed ornaments, necklets, armlets, &c. (worn particularly by old men and women, favourite playthings for children); icuhĩ cia matũ, large flat coils, usu. of brass wire, worn, hanging by strap from each ear, by women who have had a child circumcised, also sometimes by fathers of circumcised children; ndirĩ icuhĩ, I have no time to amuse you. gacũhi, ta-, n. dim. of above.
icũgi, see ibũi.
cũka, see thũka,

NEW_VALUE: aromatic plant (Ocimum) (found in open country, sweet-smelling flower)
FULL_ENTRY: gacũki', tũ-, n. (111), 1. aromatic plant (Ocimum) (found in open country, sweet-smelling flower). 2. a mutual term of salutation. 3. another plant, beloved of bees (put in empty hives to attract them). wagacũki, n. mutual term of salutation between those who have given each other a sprig of this plant.
gacũki?, see under njũki.
ũcũku (N.K.), see Ũthũku.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: take part in religious worship. cũakacũkũra, dim. ranga, diff. cũkũranĩra, assoc. worship together. cũkgrithia, d., caus. conduct religious worship
FULL_ENTRY: cũkũra, v.i. (11), take part in religious worship. cũakacũkũra, dim. ranga, diff. cũkũranĩra, assoc. worship together. cũkgrithia, d., caus. conduct religious worship; ~ mwana, give thanks after childbirth. cũkũrĩka, st. pot. cũkũrĩra, app. worship, at, for; give thanks (to God) for. [Sw. SHUKURU]
mũcũkũrithia, a-, n. (1v), (of Anglican Church) minister. [cũkũra]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: religious service, order of service, written text or prayer in the order of service.
FULL_ENTRY: icakũro, ma-, n.(u1), religious service, order of service, written text or prayer in the order of service. ũcũkũro, n. cl. 14 only, worship. fcũkũra]
cama, v.t. (11); ~ njuĩrĩ, decorate the hair.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. crouch (as one ready to flee or fight)
NEW_VALUE: crouch (as one ready to flee or fight)
FULL_ENTRY: ĩcama, v. refl. (11), 1. crouch (as one ready to flee or fight); sit on one’s haunches. 2. sit apart, sit huddled up (in a corner so that one is overlooked or passed over when sth. is being shared out); go without sth., come out empty handed; twoima ta twecama, we ended up in having nothing; cf. cũcũma, ĩhũhũma, konota, rcũmira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. small bone suspended from lock of hair on the forehead
NEW_VALUE: small bone suspended from lock of hair on the forehead
FULL_ENTRY: gicũmi, t- ; njami, ~; n. (111), 1. small bone suspended from lock of hair on the forehead; cf. gaciri. 2. tag of leather attached at the back of a girl’s leg-wire for cleaning inside. [cũma]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who makes blunders in dancing, gets out of step.
NEW_VALUE: one who makes blunders in dancing, gets out of step
FULL_ENTRY: gĩcũmbi, i-, n. (11), one who makes blunders in dancing, gets out of step.
gicambirũ, i-, n. (1), a solitary, desolate person.

NEW_VALUE: make a recalcitrant baby taste food
FULL_ENTRY: cũna, v.t. (1), lick, lick down; smooth, wipe, down (the surface of wet clay in pottery, &c.); ~ ithaga, lick an ornament (as a reminder to do sth.); ~ gĩtete, clean out a gourd; ~ ibuku, kiss the book (esp. the Bible) on taking an oath. mwaki na ĩga~, (a cow) gives birth to fire and still licks it. ~ mũndũ, (coll.) fawn on a person, behave servilely; cf. ramba. rcũna, refl. be conceited. caanacũna, dim. cũnanga, diff. cũnana, rec. 1r. lick one another (term used to describe certain form of oath). 2. behave obsequiously to others. app. rec, cũnia, caus. make lick; ~ mwana let the baby taste chewed banana. cũnithia, d. caus. assist sb. to lick, give sb. sth. to lick; ~ mwana thakame, anoint the childd’s lips with blood from the sacred animal at the ‘second birth’, see njiarano; ~ mwana irigũ (irio), feed the baby with chewed, mashed, food (esp. banana), cũnika, st. pot. cũnira, app. lick, at, for. cũnirira, inten. lick down into sth., smooth over again into place. cũniriria, caus. inten. 1. make a recalcitrant baby taste food. 2. make completely smooth by licking, wiping down, again. conv., bruise, abrade; peel off (banana, bark of tree, &c.); cf. ũnũra. fcũnũra, refl. r. bruise oneself; (of a banana, &c.) peel off (on its own). 2. brag, play big, give oneself airs. cũnũrana, rec. Conv. d. caus. conv. I1. have peeled off. 2. cause sb. to be bruised; get sb. given a good thrashing. cũnũrĩka, st. pot. cũnũrira, app. conv. cũnũrũra, inten. conv. strip, tear off (meat, &c.) from (bone, &c.), tear off vigorously. cũnũrũrũka, neut. inten. get stripped, torn, off from sth. caus. neut. tear, strip, off in long strips (as in skinning banana, stripping bark from green wand or stick). cũnũka, neut. become bruised, have an abrasion of the skin; be rough and peeling, have the skin removed. cũngũkana, rec. neut. become bruised, have skin removed altogether. cũnũkira, app. neut.
cna, ideo. (11), completely, entirely, altogether; niina indo ~, finish things up completely; cf. biũ, buru, kũna. mi-, n. (VII), a tree. icũna, ma-, n. leaf of above.
gĩcũnũnũ, see under
-cũnũre, a (11), bruised. [cũnũra]

NEW_VALUE: bow, nod, incline, the head to one another (as in a dance)
FULL_ENTRY: cũnga, v.t. & i. (11), nod, bow, incline the head; let head fall forward in drowsiness, doze; move head forward or backward in acknowledgement or respect; (of tree) bow, sway in the wind; ~ mohera, thank, pay respects; (N.K.) thank in an ungracious manner, return evil for good, cũngacũnga, dim. cũnganga, diff. cũnganira, assoc. & app. rec. 1. bow, nod, incline, the head to one another (as in a dance); doze together. 2. play up to another (as two people in collusion making their statements coincide or backing up one another’s arguments); signify approval together. cũngia, caus. make doze, make drowsy; cause to nod or sway. cũngithia, d. caus. make sth., sb., bow, nod. cũngika, st. pot. cũngira, app. nod, doze, bow, sway, at, for ; ~ mũgumo, (of boys before initiation ceremony) bow heads towards the sacred (fig) tree in acknowledgement; see thĩ mũgumo; thũ ũgacũngĩre mũtũndũ, (said to a person with mumps) go and jerk your head backwards at the mũtũndũ tree.
mũcũnga, mi-, n. nodding, dozing; wa ~, sleeping-sickness. [canga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: addition, sth. added into the bargain
FULL_ENTRY: macũngiriro, n. cl. 6 (11), addition, sth. added into the bargain; akĩmũgũtha na ~ akĩmũruma, he hit him then (to add insult to injury) he started to abuse him; na ~ akĩmũhe ciringi ikũmi, and (not content with that) he gave him ten shillings into the bargain; cf. gicũngiro. [cũnga]
gĩcũngĩro, i-, n. (111), addition, sth. over and above; cf. giuco; see macũngiriro.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. leaf of pumpkin plant. 2. (cl. 6) (N.K.) seeds and core of pumpkin
FULL_ENTRY: icũngũ, ma-, n. (Iv), 1. leaf of pumpkin plant. 2. (cl. 6) (N.K.) seeds and core of pumpkin; kĩnya na macũngũ, eat both gourd and seeds (two bitter things); bitterness, bitter trouble. ũcũngũ, n. cl. 14, slight bitterness from a new calabash ; suffering; cf. gana. v.i. (1), 1. quiver, wobble; cf. cengeca, 2. (of bridge, building, branch of tree when bird flies off) vibrate. 3. (of poor dancers) bounce up and down, walk with jerky movement. 4. (of long thin wand, fishing rod, long plank, &c.) be springy, jerky; cf. thũngũtha, caũga. cũngacũngũca, dim. cũngũcanga, diff. app. rec. move, jump, vibrate in a jerky manner; (of poor dancers) bounce up and down together out of step; cf. cibica. ngũcia, caus. rock, shake; make wobble, quiver, bounce. cithia, d. caus. cũngũcika, st. pot. cũngũcira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: bottom of back, tip of spinal cord.
NEW_VALUE: bottom of back, tip of spinal cord
FULL_ENTRY: gĩcũngũrũ, i-, n. (x1), bottom of back, tip of spinal cord.
gacũngũrũ, see under njũngũrũ.
gicangũrũrũ, see gicũngũrũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: wander around aimlessly. dim. (of flies, gnats) hover round, about
FULL_ENTRY: cũng’a!, v.i, (11), wander around aimlessly. dim. (of flies, gnats) hover round, about; (of sb. furtive, nervous) dodge, dart, about; cf. cang’a. cOũng’'acũng’ania, caus. rec, hover round after sth. cũng’acũng’anĩra, assoc. caus. cang’acũng’ira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sit oneself down, squat, sit huddled up
NEW_VALUE: (11), sit oneself down, squat, sit huddled up
FULL_ENTRY: icũng’a, v.i., (refl.) (11), sit oneself down, squat, sit huddled up; take a firm stand, plant oneself in an attitude of resistance; see gicũngwa; cf. ĩthuna. rcũng’ia, caus, sit by oneself, set oneself apart in a group. fcũn'ira, app. squat, at, because of.
cŨng’a?, see cong’a.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: orgiastic dance, imported from Mbeere, (in N.K. held at night) performed at the home of a girl shortly to be initiated
FULL_ENTRY: mũcũng’ũ, mi-, n. (vu), orgiastic dance, imported from Mbeere, (in N.K. held at night) performed at the home of a girl shortly to be initiated; see ndaiyũ, thauthi. ũng’ũti, ~; ma-; njũng’ũti, ~; n. usually with cl. 1/2 concords (111), man of might and courage; cf. karau, ikandau, ikano, ngũngũti.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (also called ~ wa airĩtu) a new dance of men and girls performed in the daytime (the leader being at one time a man, at another a girl).
FULL_ENTRY: mũcũng’ wa, mi-, n. (vIi), (also called ~ wa airĩtu) a new dance of men and girls performed in the daytime (the leader being at one time a man, at another a girl).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. cajole, coax, conciliate, talk over, get round
FULL_ENTRY: (i-) (gĩ-) cũng’wa, see under njũngwa. / cũnjũima, v.t. (II), I. cajole, coax, conciliate, talk over, get round; cf. anjanja. 2. pet, make much of (as a grandmother and her grandchild). canjacũnjũiga, dim. cũdiff. cũnjũigana, rec. cajole others, calm people down. cũnjũigithia, d. caus. cause sb. to be cajoled or talked round. cũnjũigika, st. pot. be easy to talk round, be conciliated. canjũigira, app.

[e] stranger). dim. Cũnjũmanga, diff. cũnjgũmana, rec. hold others tightly, embrace one another. cũnjũmithia, d. caus. get sth. held tightly; cause sth, to be compressed, constricted. mika, st. pot. app. constrict, compress, hold tightly, for, at.
mũcũra, mĩ-, n. (Vi1), (N.K.) sheath of sword; cf. njora.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: ponder over sth., weigh two or more things in the mind in order to choose
NEW_VALUE: ponder over , weigh two or more things in the mind in order to choose
FULL_ENTRY: cũrania [cũrania], v.t. (1), ponder over sth., weigh two or more things in the mind in order to choose; decide; consider very carefully, think over; cf. thuthuria, refi. make up one’s mind, decide on an alternative, debate in one’s mind in order to choose. caracarania, dim. cũraniria, app. ponder over a number of points.
mũcũlricũ mi-, n. (1v), I1. young stalk, stem. 2. loose end, tagend, loose bit of string. 3. small electrical connexion (not plugged in).
cũri, ~, n. (1), a thief. [Ind. CHOR]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. neck of calabash cut in half and used as handle or funnel
NEW_VALUE: neck of calabash cut in half and used as handle or funnel
FULL_ENTRY: mũcũri, mi-, n. (11), 1. neck of calabash cut in half and used as handle or funnel; cf. 2. lip, spout, of teapot, kettle, jug, oil-can, &c. 3. (of dress, utensil, &c.) narrow, pointed end, gĩcũri, i-, n. 1. long tapering end at rear of woman’s skirt extending almost to the ground. 2. (of paper, cloth, hide) narrow pointed end, corner, tag-end, cutting. 3. (of field) projecting corner. 4. taper, lapel, or similar attachment, gacũri, tũ-, n. dim. of above.

ũcũrũ, (ma-), n. (11), 1. gruel, porridge; ~ wa moũkio, traditional gruel made from pounded maize and/or millet soaked in water and left to set and ferment to flavour before boiling, served both as a liquid food and as a refreshment; ~ wa gĩthĩ, thin or thick porridge made from maize flour ; cf. ndite, gĩteke; see kia; nyua ~, drink gruel or porridge; kundia ~, hold the calabash for sb. (usu. a young man) while he sips the gruel (at the party given by a prospective bride and her friends to the prospective groom and his friends); rugĩra ~, prepare gruel for sb. (as a mark of respect or love); rutĩra ~, give gruel to a casual visitor as a mark of hospitality; koya ~, ask for the attention of a girl for friendship or marriage. 2. (cl. 6) smears of gruel or porridge; filth, rubbish, nonsense; haka macara, smear with gruel, make a person do filthy work, be rude, treat disrespectfully; ndikũhakwo macũrũ, I won't stand any nonsense. gicũrũ, (i-), n. aug. of above.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: narrow, tapering end, strip (of land, cloth, &c.). gicarũhi, i-, n. a tapering strip, a broad or blunt tapering end
NEW_VALUE: narrow, tapering end, strip (of land, cloth, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũcũrũhi, mĩ-, n. (vI), narrow, tapering end, strip (of land, cloth, &c.). gicarũhi, i-, n. a tapering strip, a broad or blunt tapering end; cf. gĩcũri, gacororua. gacũrũhi, tũ-, n, dim. of above.
mũcũrũndi | mũthgrũndi, mi-, n, (v), (N.K.) a long roll of sth.; cf. mothũrũriga.
mũcũrũriga, see mũthũrũriga.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: wanderer, loafer, one who lags behind.
NEW_VALUE: wanderer, loafer, one who lags behind
FULL_ENTRY: mũcũrũriki [mũcũrũrĩki], a-, n. (111), wanderer, loafer, one who lags behind.
mũcũrũriko [mũcũrũrĩko], (mi-), n. (11), wanderlust; loafing. [cũrũra]

cũrũrũka, v.i. (1), I. ooze and flow down; trickle, run, down; cf. nyũrũrũka. 2. (of bark, bast, &c.) strip, come off easily. cũracũrũrũka, dim. cũrũrũkanga, diff. cũrũrũkanira, assoc. cOrũrũkia, caus. I. make trickle down, flow, run down; weep (tears). 2. strip, tear off bark, &c.; cf. ũnũra. cũrũrũkira, app. flow down along; trickle down at, wet sth. by
. trickles. caus. app. weep, for, at; strip, tear off, for, at.

NEW_VALUE: liquids oozing and flowing down or trickling
FULL_ENTRY: cũrũrũrg, ideo. (1), descr. of 1. liquids oozing and flowing down or trickling. 2. stripping off of bark, from tree, or meat from bone., [see carũrũka]
mũcũthi!, mi-, n. (1), small shrub (Agelaea heterophylla).
mũcũthi?, mĩ-, n. (1) gravy broth; ~, it is rich, juicy.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sp. of smali snake, said to jump or spring at a person or animal. [cũtha] ideo. (1), 1. descr. of a sharp loud sound made by striking with a whip, cane, or withy
NEW_VALUE: of a sharp loud sound made by striking with a whip, cane, or withy
FULL_ENTRY: cũthi, ~, n. (1), sp. of smali snake, said to jump or spring at a person or animal. [cũtha] ideo. (1), 1. descr. of a sharp loud sound made by striking with a whip, cane, or withy; gambia ~, give a sharp stroke, whip hard. 2. used of speech; aria ~, make unreliable, erroneous statements; cf. cau. 3. as a word of command; ngũringa kĩ- ~!, I am going to strike very hard .. . smash! (words of a children’s game). ideo. descr. of the sound of a thin stick whipped through the air; gambia ~, strike, whip, lash repeatedly. [see cũtha] v.i. (1), oscillate, swing slowly back and forth, sway to and fro, swing up and down, play at ‘sea-saw’ ; cf. ũgũga. cũgacũga, dim. cũũganga, diff. assoc. swing together. canũgania, caus. rec. dash up and down after sb. caus. swing sth. up and down, swing, give a ‘see-saw’; lift up and down rhythmically (as when playing with a baby); send back and forth; ~ mũbira, pass the ball from player to player; ~ mwana, rock a child to sleep. cũgithia, d. caus. cũgika, st. pot. cũgira, app. caus. app. ũha!, v.t. (1), treat sb. partially, show favour to, please; cf. cũha, kenia, ĩccukumithania, ĩcũha, refi. flinch from a difficult task, spare oneself. caũhacũha, dim. cũhanga, diff. cũhana, rec. show consideration for, others, one another. cũhanira, app. rec. d. caus. have sb. treated partially. cũhika, st. pot. be amenable to partial treatment. app.; ũranjũhira kĩ?, why are you taking so much trouble over me?

ORIGINAL_VALUE: view, survey the landscape, keep watch from a look-out
FULL_ENTRY: cũha? / (N.K.) cũaũa, v.i. & t. (1), view, survey the landscape, keep watch from a look-out; cf. roora, cũthirĩria. dim. cũhanga, diff. cũhanira, assoc. keep watch together. cũhithia, d. caus. caũhika, st. pot. cũhira, app. keep watch, man a look-out. cũhirira, inten. keep a look-out for. v.t. (1), cut at an angle, on the slant; cut a hunk out of sth. ; cf. cũrũ-hũra. cũhũka, v.i. go off at an angle, slip in cutting; be cut, torn, on the slant, obliquely.
gacũi, see under rũi,

NEW_VALUE: come into sight, show up, come momentarily close to
FULL_ENTRY: gacũiro / (N.K.) gacũhiro, tũ-, n. (1), vantage-point, look-out; eminence from which approaching enemies can be seen. gĩcũiro, i-, n. aug. of above [see cũha?] v.t. (11), make good (what was previously bad, spoiled, maligned); clear, acquit. kũra, dim. cũkũranga, diff. cũaũkũrana, rec. cũkũrithia, d. caus. get sth. cleared, cause to be acquitted. cũkũrika, st. pot. cũkũrira, app. v.i. (1), 1. come into sight, show up, come momentarily close to. 2. glower at; cf. thandũka. cũnacũnia, dim. cũnangia, diff. cũnaniria, assoc. come into sight together, come close together momentarily; threaten one another by sign, grimace, gesture (sometimes together with words). cũniria, app. come, bring into, sight at, for; show up to; make a warning, threatening, gesture at sb., threaten by sign, grimace, gesture; stop short of sth., come momentarily close to.

NEW_VALUE: onlooker. Gũtirĩ mũici na ~, there is no difference between the thief and the onlooker (abettor)
FULL_ENTRY: cũthi, interj. (U), look! see!; cf. rora, one. [see cũtha] a-, n. (1v), onlooker. Gũtirĩ mũici na ~, there is no difference between the thief and the onlooker (abettor). [cũtha] see under cũcŨ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of smooth, slick movement, esp. of deportment
NEW_VALUE: of smooth, slick movement, esp. of deportment
FULL_ENTRY: cwa, ideo. (u), descr. of smooth, slick movement, esp. of deportment; cf. hwa. [see cũa]
cwe, ideo. (U), descr. of: 1. person’s sudden disappearance. 2. tearing of cloth. 3. grinding of millet for gruel; ũyũ ti ũcũrũ, nĩ ~, this is not (good) gruel, it is too thin.

cwee / cwii, ideo. (U), descr. of: 1. long, deliberate thrust. 2. gradual movement or ailing condition; aragambiao ~ nĩ kĩ?, what ails him? ; ~ ta mũcema, (of a sick or elderly person) he is gradually getting poorly. E

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 3rd pers. s. of v. ~-i, alternative form of -rĩ (q.v.) ‘to be’
FULL_ENTRY: e (U), 3rd pers. s. of v. ~-i, alternative form of -rĩ (q.v.) ‘to be’; ~ ka?, where is he (she)? ; also, with or without na, ‘to have’ ; ~ mbeca nyingi, he has plenty of money; cf. ari.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. clean out, scrape up (rubbish, dung, mud)
NEW_VALUE: clean out, scrape up (rubbish, dung, mud)
FULL_ENTRY: ea [eya], v.t. (1), 1. clean out, scrape up (rubbish, dung, mud); rake out, clean up (cattle-pen, watercourse, &c.); cf. cakũra. 2. dig out site in preparation for building; canalize a stream. eyeya, dim. eyanga, diff. eyanira, app. rec, eyithia, d. caus. eyeka, st. pot. eyera, app. eyũrũrũkia, caus. inten. rake, turn, up; churn through, make a mess of (cattle-pen, mud, &c.). eyũrũrũkania, caus. rec. inten. make a thorough mess of. eyũrũrũkiria, caus. app. inten.
ee [eeee], interj. (U), expr. doubt or disbelief; ~, ndũnathĩ!, surely you didn’t go!

ORIGINAL_VALUE: scoop, bore, drill, hollow out, gouge, dig out, scoop out (e.g. flesh and seeds of a gourd)
NEW_VALUE: scoop, bore, drill, hollow out, gouge, dig out, scoop out (e
FULL_ENTRY: ega', v.t. (1), scoop, bore, drill, hollow out, gouge, dig out, scoop out (e.g. flesh and seeds of a gourd); ~ kĩrangi, gouge out a hollow cylinder mole-trap ; ~ kĩnya, scoop out the inside of a green calabash in preparation for use, see mego; cf. aya, eya. egega [egeega], dim. eganga, diff. egana, rec. make digs at other people, provoke others. eganira, app. rec.; tiga kw~ tũhũ, cease provoking others for nothing. eganiria, caus. app. rec.; ~ inya ici, prepare these calabashes together. egithia, d. caus. egeka, st. pot. be hollowed out. egera, app. egũka, neut. develop a cavity, get hollow. v.i. (1), be tilted, askew; swerve; ũrathĩ wegete, it (the car) is moving tilted to one side; cf. era. egega [egeega], dim. wobble, totter, be unsteady, rock, stagger, sway; move from side to side (as in balancing); cf. erera. egeganga, diff. dim. egeganira, assoc. dim. egegia, caus. dim. make wobble, totter; shake, threaten to overthrow, put out of balance. egegithia, d. caus. dim. get sth. rocked. egegeka, st. pot. dim. mĩte gĩtingĩ~, it is absolutely firm, you cannot shake it. egegera, app. dim. totter because of or at. egia, caus, cause to be tilted, make swerve, sway. epeka, st. pot. egera; app. ; ria ri~, when the sun tilts (early afternoon time); cf. mrarahũko.

NEW_VALUE: of above used better, a little better
FULL_ENTRY: wega [weega], mawega, n. (IV), 1. goodness, grace, virtue; good, quality, virtue, rightness; beneficence; niceness, prettiness; thii na ~, go in peace, good-bye; tigwo na ~, remain in peace, good-bye; ni ~, that’s fine, thank you; nĩ ~ ũthĩ, please go. 2. (used adv.) well, satisfactorily, thoroughly, nicely, peacefully; for certain, properly, in right manner, outright. wegega [wegeega], n. cl. 14 only, dim. of above used adv. better, a little better; ~ kũri nũ, a little bit better than 1; see agira.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. good, nice, pleasant, satisfactory, suitable, fine. 2. beautiful, pretty
NEW_VALUE: good, nice, pleasant, satisfactory, suitable, fine
FULL_ENTRY: -ega, a. (1V), 1. good, nice, pleasant, satisfactory, suitable, fine. 2. beautiful, pretty ; k~ ta kũ, as pretty as anything (name given to a lady-bird). 3. kind. 4. in good condition; wĩ mw~, are you well?, how are you? -egega [egeega], a. a little bit good, nicer, better.
egathi, see under mwigathi.
mwegea, mi-, n. (XIII), 1. wild gourdvine (Momordica schimperiana); cf. (N.K.) mũhũri. 2. wild custard-apple. rĩegea, megea [meegea], n. fruit of above; cf. hũri, thũnjũ.
rũegekio [rũegeekio], njegekio [njegeekio], n. (11), mimicking of sb.’s words, sounds; echo. [egeka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: concern, pertain to, have to do with
NEW_VALUE: make related to, direct towards a particular end
FULL_ENTRY: epemai, v.t. (1), concern, pertain to, have to do with; belong to, be related to; kaĩ mekwenda gũthĩ na indo itamegemete?, do they indeed want to appropriate what does not belong to them?; ũtonga ndũkamw~ o na angĩka atĩra! wealth will never come his way whatever he does! (-egemii) / -egii, (alt. form of perf.) pertaining to, concerning; horo Ũcio ndũkw~, that is no concern of yours; cf. konii. egemana, rec. concern, pertain to, be related to one another; be associated with; moirĩtu ũyũ-rĩ, tondũ ekũgũrũo-rĩ, egemaine na ũ?, in this girl’s forthcoming marriage negotiations who is going to speak for her family? ; ũhoro Ũcio wegemaine na Ũ?, who is that statement directed against? egemania, caus. rec. relate sth. to, bring into relationship with, refer to; associate, compare sth. with. regemania, refl. associate oneself with; ~ na, keep close to, alongside of; cf. tania. egemia, caus, 1. make related to, direct towards a particular end. 2. divert sth. to overcome obstacle, negotiate sth. through obstacle, keep out of difficulty; w~ mũcumari yũ nĩũkũriira, if you drive this nail in at the correct angle it will hold firm. egemithia, d. caus. egemeka, st. pot. be capable of being corrected, repaired. egemera, app. be related to; be directed towards; no ni? thĩna ũnjegemeire, Ĩ am the only one that troubles are overtaking. egemeria, caus. app.
egema?, see ogoma,
egetha, v.i. (11), hiccup. egethanga, diff. egethanira, assoc. egethia, caus. cause to hiccup. egethera, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: hiccuping, an attack of hiccups, kregetho / kĩegetha, ci-, n.
FULL_ENTRY: mwegetho, mi-, n. (111), hiccuping, an attack of hiccups, kregetho / kĩegetha, ci-, n. I1. aug. of above. 2. a single hiccup. [egetha]
egi, see mwigi.
-egii, see egema!,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. scoop of pulp from inside gourd. 2. gourd seed. 3. (cl. 6) pulp seeds and core of gourd
NEW_VALUE: pulp seeds and core of gourd
FULL_ENTRY: rĩego, mego [meego], n. (1), 1. scoop of pulp from inside gourd. 2. gourd seed. 3. (cl. 6) pulp seeds and core of gourd; rĩa ~, see ria; cf. nyũngũ. rũego, njego, n. I. mass of pulp. 2. long wooden spoon for scraping out gourd.

eha, v.t. (1) make a notch, aperture; make a gap (in teeth) by removing the middle two (sometimes one) of the lower jaw (delirious persons and those who get lockjaw are fed with water and gruel through the gap); ~ hengero xa mwata, burn a hole through the lid of a bee-barrel for bees to go in and out and for inserting knife when opening the hive; ~ mũcũrĩ, cut a channel in the spout of half-calabash for pouring; ~ gĩturua, cut spaces between the legs of a wooden stool; see wehe, egũra; cf. aya, enga, ega. eheha [eheeha], dim. ehanga, ~ njora, shape tops of the wooden foundations of a scabbard; ~ maĩ, make a breast stroke in swimming. ehana, rec. ehanira, app. rec. ehaniria, caus. app. rec. ehithia, d. caus. get sb.’s teeth removed; have something notched, assist in notching. eheka, st. pot. ehera!, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (1v), grief, sorrow, sadness, stress, anxiety
FULL_ENTRY: kĩeha!, (ci-), n. (1v), grief, sorrow, sadness, stress, anxiety; gra, igua, ~, be sorrowful, anxious, agitated; rkĩra ~, make anxious, sorrowful; cf. thina, rũeha, (njeha), n. despondency, anxiety, worry; ndiri na ~ ria gĩkũ, I have no fear of death.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: parasitic plant (similar to mistletoe but with small red berries) growing on trees such as mũkoigo, mũkindũri, &c.
FULL_ENTRY: kĩeha?, ci-, n. (1v), parasitic plant (similar to mistletoe but with small red berries) growing on trees such as mũkoigo, mũkindũri, &c.; see thina; cf. krĩgwatĩrira.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who removes front incisors of lower jaw, dentist.
NEW_VALUE: one who removes front incisors of lower jaw, dentist
FULL_ENTRY: mwehani, ehani [eehani], n. (x1), one who removes front incisors of lower jaw, dentist. [eha]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. gap made by forcing out with awl the two middle teeth of lower jaw
NEW_VALUE: gap made by forcing out with awl the two middle teeth of lower jaw
FULL_ENTRY: wehe, mawehe, n. (11), 1. gap made by forcing out with awl the two middle teeth of lower jaw; cf. njarũmi; see gura. 2. notch cut in top end of pole to receive transverse stick or beam; notch in arrow shaft. kĩwehe, n. nickname given to one who has lost several teeth in middle of lower jaw. [eha]
ehu [eehu], see mwihu.
eka, see ika [ĩka].

mweke, mi-, n. (V), I. vacant space, gap; rou nĩnyonete ~ wa cira ny, now I see a way through this dispute; cf. ithenya. 2. opening, chance, opportunity; no mũĩhaka mabataro mao matuĩke nomo mekũgĩa na ~, their requirements only should be given time for attention; cf. mĩaramano, bathi!. 3. separateness, distinction; e ~, he is distinct from others; iga ~, put separately; cf. mwanya. kamweke, tũ-, n. 1. small space; ~ konagia mũndũ a chink is sufficient to show the way; ke ~, allow me a little space (in your paper). 2. a fine distinction. 3. chance, opportunity ; cf. bathi!, mĩaramano.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: fence using a stick or sword, mislead by stratagem, have designs on, distract sb.’s attention
NEW_VALUE: fence using a stick or sword, mislead by stratagem, have designs on, distract sb
FULL_ENTRY: ema?, v.t. (11), fence using a stick or sword, mislead by stratagem, have designs on, distract sb.’s attention; cf. memia. emema [emeema], dim. lure, beguile, decoy (an animal, &c.); trick, play, sport with (by little playful tricks); try to get sth. by cunning. ememana [emeemana], rec. dim. spar with one another. emanga, diff. emana, rec. fence against each other (with light weapons); use finesse against one another in a dispute, use stratagem, artifice; approach a subject obliquely without committing oneself. emanira, app. rec. use artifice, stratagem, finesse, against one another, for, at. emitkia, d., caus. assist sb, to fence sb.; help sb. to beguile, lure, decoy, sb. else. emeka, st. pot. be easily beguiled, tricked; ndangĩ~ nĩ mĩtũkĩ, he is so quick in his movements that it is impossible to fence against him, he is so quick-witted that it is not possible to beguile him. emera, app. emerũo, pass. app.; emeirno akĩntinĩrio indo ciake ciothe, he was tricked out of all his belongings.
eme (-rĩ), see amu (-ri).
mwemwe, see under -MwWe,

emba, v.i. & t. (1) 1. become, dim, duli; lose vigour; (of fire) die down; (of footmarks) become blurred, indistinct; (of a hole or pit) get filled up; (of a path) become overgrown, obliterated; (of a wound) close up, draw together; (of a hut) become disused, abandoned. 2. obliterate, make indistinct, cover up (a pit with branches, shrubs, leaves, &c., in order to catch animals or persons). embemba, dim. 1. quiver, agitate; (of a person) be restless as if about to do or say sth. 2. (of a path) begin to get overgrown or lost; (of a hole) become nearly filled up; cf. ambamba. embembwo, pass. dim.; ~ nĩ be itching to say sth. embembana, rec, dim. be, become, filled; (of liquids, flour, grain, &c.) be full to the brim; (of a space or area) be fuil of, covered with. embembania, caus. rec. dim. fill full, cover over; level off (loose earth, grain, &c.). embanga, diff. embia, caus, make dim, dull; cause to be indistinct, blurred, covered over. embeka, st. pot. embera, app. embũra, conv. cause to break out afresh; reopen a path; revive a fire by blowing or adding fuel. embũka, neut. (of a sore, wound, hole, &c.) reopen, break open afresh, open up; (of fire) revive, burn up again.
mwemba-ignũrũ, see mũimbaigũrũ.
mũembe, mĩ-, n. (1x), mango-tree. iembe, ma-, n. mango fruit. [Sw. EMBE]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: synonym for the prohibited mathirĩgn song (used in 1929 to mislead people).
NEW_VALUE: synonym for the prohibited mathirĩgn song (used in 1929 to mislead people)
FULL_ENTRY: mwembe, n. cl. 3 only (11), synonym for the prohibited mathirĩgn song (used in 1929 to mislead people).
wembe, (mawembe), n. (1v), safety razor, razor; cf. rgenji. kawembe, ta-, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. safetyrazor blade; cf. kanyũi. [Sw. WEMBE]

mwene, ene [eene], n. (1v), owner (of sth.); ~-nyaga, see under nyaga; ~gĩthaka, a land-owner; ~-hinya, (a name for God) Almighty, possessor of power; ~-itĩri, chief man of a populated area, esp. one who has rights over the local circumcision ground; Ngai ~-matĩri, God (the lord of wide expanses); ~ head of the family (any person in the family named after a person in the male line of descent; cf. gĩthũmba). Gra kuona gĩteaga kĩa ~, sth. come by easily makes one lose what one has earned.
-ene!, p.a. (III), belonging to sb. else, others; ndeto ci~, other people’s business.

-ene?, a. (1v), 1. possessing, owning; ng'ombe ny~ gacaũ, a cow with a calf; Ngai Mw~ (Ny~) hinya wothe, Almighty God; see mwene; mũndũ mw~ nyũmba, the house owner. 2. (used in conj. with the indep. pron.) self; ni mw~, I myself; ithu ene (ithuene), we ourselves; inyur ene (inyuene), you yourselves; mũti guo mw~, the tree itself; nyũmba yo ny~, the house itself. 3. belonging, pertaining, to; its own; mũti na mathangũ m~, the tree and its leaves; aacinire nyũmba na andũ ene, he burnt the house as well as the people in it. 4. as 3 but reinforcing a poss.; nĩ rũhiũ mw~, this is my own knife; ciana ici ti ciake mw~, these are not his own children; iga o gĩkombe na gathani gakĩo kĩ~, keep each cup with its proper saucer; ĩno nĩ yaku wee mw~?, is this your very own? 5. (coll.) kw~ kw~, ene kw~, among, between, selves; ithuĩ ene kw~, between ourselves.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: glow, flicker, glimmer. enenanga, dim. enenangia, caus. dim, enenanira, assoc. enenia, caus. make glow, flicker
NEW_VALUE: glow, flicker, glimmer. enenanga, enenangia, caus
FULL_ENTRY: enena [eneena], v.i. (11), glow, flicker, glimmer. enenanga, dim. enenangia, caus. dim, enenanira, assoc. enenia, caus. make glow, flicker; hold sth. up to view, display sth. ; ~ gĩcinga, wave a firebrand in the air to make it burn brightly or to see clearly; ~ broadcast a piece of news. fenenia, caus, refi. show off, display oneself in new clothes; display one’s qualities; walk with a swagger; cf. ronania, enenaniria, caus. app. rec.; ~ hin, wave swords about at one another threateningly. enenithia, d, caus. eneneka, st. pot. enenera, app. eneneria, caus. app., fiicker at, for; wave (a light, &c.) at, to.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. glimmer of light, glow (as of the coming of dawn or a nearly dead fire when blown up)
NEW_VALUE: glimmer of light, glow (as of the coming of dawn or a nearly dead fire when blown up)
FULL_ENTRY: mweneni [mweneeni], mĩ-, n.(VI11), 1. glimmer of light, glow (as of the coming of dawn or a nearly dead fire when blown up); light enough to see by; nĩguo tuone ~ harĩ ũhoro so that we may get some light on that subject. 2. a clear, open space seen from a distance. kĩeneni, ci-, n. 1. fire-brand. 2. electric torch. kamweneni, tũ- / tumieneni, n. glimmer, glimpse; chance, opportunity. [enena]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. clearance, small cleared patch. 2. grassy glade, open space in forest, grassland, or field
FULL_ENTRY: kĩeni, ci-, n. (11), 1. clearance, small cleared patch. 2. grassy glade, open space in forest, grassland, or field; plot, enclosure, of grass, grass green, grassy plain. 3. small uncultivated patch in garden. 4. area, part of country or town; cf. kĩengo, gĩcigirira. keni [keeni], tweni, n. dim. of above.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. want, like, love, be fond of, desire. 2. have a tendency towards (especially of actions in which there is no question of volition)
NEW_VALUE: want, like, love, be fond of, desire
FULL_ENTRY: enda, v.t. (1), I. want, like, love, be fond of, desire. 2. have a tendency towards (especially of actions in which there is no question of volition); ~ gũkua, be on the point of death; ~ gũciara, be about to give birth, or bear fruits. fenda, refl. be selfish, self-centred, think a lot of oneself, be self-satisfied, conceited; cf. ĩtĩa. endenda, dim. endanga, diff. endana, rec. love, love or like one another, love or like sb., people; andũ mendaine matiũranagia, people who love one another do not dwell on each other’s mistakes. endania, caus. rec. make people like or love one another. endanithia, d. caus. rec.; nĩwe wendanithirie Kamau na mũtumia wake, he was responsible for the reconciliation of K. and his wife. endanira, app. rec.; mendanĩre kĩ?, why do they love one another? endaniria, caus. app. rec. like, love, desire, or want sth., sb., together with sth. else, at the same time. endaniriria, inten. rec. exercise favouritism, partiality, be partial, favour sb. or some persons; cf. endereria. endithia,' d. caus. cause sb. to like sth., cause sth. to be liked, wanted, or loved. rendithia, refl. d. caus. seek favour, try to make a favourable impression, ingratiate oneself. endeka, st. pot. be lovable, likeable; be capable of being loved, liked, or wanted. endekana, rec. st. pot. be wanted, desired (by people in general), be much desired or admired, be wanted by sb. or other urgently; mind wonire ngero nĩar~ nĩ Birithi, the person who witnessed a certain accident is wanted at once by the police. endera, app. like or want sth. because of or at; ~ kĩndũ ngoga, like sth. for its charm or attractiveness ; ~ kĩndũ thĩna, like sth. because one has no choice, like sth. out of desperation. rendera, refl. app. please oneself, desire sth. or to do sth. of one’s own accord, choose for oneself. Ngoro ĩrĩaga kĩrĩa ĩyendeire, see ngoro; cf, enderera, inten. be partial towards, support sb., favour sb. endereria, caus. inten. speak favourably on behalf of sth., endeavour to have sth. regarded with favour, try to get sth. or sb. wanted, liked, desired; cf. gathĩrĩria. ĩendereria, refl. caus. inten. worm oneself into company where one is not wanted, ingratiate oneself (with), curry favour (with); cf. zcukumithania. enderereka, st. pot. inten. yield to recommendation, persuasion, be prevailed upon to agree; maũndũ macio matingĩ~, those things cannot be made attractive.
mwenda-mwago, see under mwaggo.
mwendaniriro | mwendaniririo, mĩ-, n. (1), partiality, favouritism. Maĩ ma gĩthima ~, Ũtarĩ wao akainũka atakunda, favouritism at the well results in a stranger going home without a drink. [enda]
wendano, mawendano; ci-; n. (1) mutual liking, affection; favouritism, partiality. rũendano, nyendano, n. strong mutual affection; extreme favouritism. [enda]
-ende, a. (11), beloved. [enda]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. tilt, lean over to one side, cant
NEW_VALUE: tilt, lean over to one side, cant
FULL_ENTRY: endema, v.i. (1), I. tilt, lean over to one side, cant; be tilted, lopsided; be out of line, swerve aside, lurch to one side, move sharply aside; ~ na swing to the left. 2. (of moon) be lopsided, be at the quarter. endendema, dim, endemanga, diff. endemanira, assoc. lean over together. endemia, caus. I. cause to lean over, tilt, shift, turn to one side; make lopsided (e.g. in hanging a beehive). 2. send off at a tangent; ~ ũhoro, digress. endemithia, d. caus. endemeka, st. pot. endemera, app. obviate, swerve to avoid sb., sth. endemeria, caus, app. tilt, move sth. sharply towards, at, because of.

enderetha / engeretha, v.t. (1), spell (words), spell out. enderethanga, diff. enderethanira, app. rec, enderethithia, d. caus. enderetheka, st. ciugo itangĩ~ Ũhoro, words that cannot be easily spelt. enderethera, app. [Sw. ENDELEZA]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. man who contracts a matrilocal, matrilineal marriage
NEW_VALUE: man who contracts a matrilocal, matrilineal marriage
FULL_ENTRY: mwendia-rũhiũ, mĩ-, n. (I-11), 1. man who contracts a matrilocal, matrilineal marriage; one who cannot pay customary ‘bride-price? through poverty but attaches himself to the family of a man with many daughters, one of whom he is allowed to treat as wife (he remains there and works for his father-in-law, the children belong to their maternal grandfather and his lineage, the eldest son being named after a maternal grandparent. Such marriage is always convertible into the normal patrilocal, patrilineal form by subsequent payment of bride-price). 2. unattached man who cohabits with a widow or, by permission, with a married woman. 3. one who helps a woman to clear herself of ‘uncleanness’ due to the death of her husband by having sexual intercourse with her, unless this is done by the younger brother who inherits her; cf. mũthambania. 4. stranger willing to perform ceremonial coition with a woman on one of the occasions prescribed by custom, e.g. if she bears twins or has a child which cuts the upper teeth first, or, in the case of a newly initiated girl, at the conclusion of the aggregation rites; see mbiro ya rũenji.

NEW_VALUE: expose by folding back, drawing apart, the cover(s) (esp. of a woman’s private parts)
FULL_ENTRY: enga, v.t. (11), part, draw apart, push apart; cut, fold, back in order to get at what is underneath; make a chink, parting, opening, remove the covering, expose; ~ magego, show the teeth, grin; ~ njuĩrĩ, part the hair; ~ maĩ, skim the water, (in swimming) paddle like a duck; cf, humbũria, heta. engenga, dim. draw apart, a bit more; push on with an effort, proceed by pushing aside the bushes; reke nyengenge, let me follow as best as I can; nĩ twengenge nduma, let’s push our way through the dark. enganga, diff. enganĩra, app. rec. cut, fold, draw back, sth. together. engithia, d. caus.; nĩ kĩramw~ magego?, what makes him grin? engeka, st. pot. engera, app. break through, out, at. engũra / engũria, (caus.) conv. 1. expose by folding back, drawing apart, the cover(s) (esp. of a woman’s private parts). 2. open (a door, &c.) a little, set ajar, part, draw aside, make an opening in; cf. emũria. engũrira, app. conv. engũriria, caus. app. conv. engũka, neut. I1. (esp. of a woman’s private parts) become exposed, uncovered. 2. get opened.

mwenge, see mweke. v.i. (1) waddle; (of a very fat person or animal, sb. with an abscess between the legs, &c.) strut along stiffly with feet wide apart; walk with a wiggle or straddling gait; cf. tang’a; see njengei. engengea, dim. engeanga, diff. engeanira, assoc. engeia fengeyia], caus. make waddle, or wiggle. engeithia, d. caus. engeeka, st. pot. engeera, app.
mwengeca, mĩ-, n. (XI) a boys’ dance.
mwengeo, mi-, n. (x1), straddling gait. [enga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) needy child belonging to neighbour or stranger. rũengerema, n. cl. rr only, teeming swarm of people
FULL_ENTRY: kĩengerema, ci-, n. (v), (N.K.) needy child belonging to neighbour or stranger. rũengerema, n. cl. rr only, teeming swarm of people; cf. rũngũyũ.
enguta / thenguta, v.i. (1) move away altogether; cf. ehera, ehuta. engenguta, dim, engutanga, diff. engutanira, assoc. engutaniria, caus. assoc. engutia, caus. engutithia, d, caus. engutira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: front apron worn by women and girls, pubic apron
FULL_ENTRY: mwengũ, mi-, n, (1v), front apron worn by women and girls, pubic apron; tũaũra hamwe na ~, be tied to one’s mother’s apron strings; cf. mũiraba, raba, mwehio. kamwengnũ, tũ- | tũmĩengũ, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. small pubic apron worn by men in ngurũ dance. [enga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) 1. that may be so, no doubt
NEW_VALUE: that may be so, no doubt
FULL_ENTRY: engwa / engwe, part. (U), (N.K.) 1. that may be so, no doubt; o? ~ kau, oh yes, I can believe that. 2. even, not even, at least; ndionete ~ kamwe, I haven’t found even one; ~ rĩmwe, at least once; cf. anga, o na.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. shave, cut sb.’s hair, clip, cut with shears or scissors
FULL_ENTRY: enja?, v.t. (11), 1. shave, cut sb.’s hair, clip, cut with shears or scissors; cf. kũrũta, hara; ~ cut sb.’s hair in European style (short back and sides); see kang’'au (kĩng'eũ). 2. deceive (by fair words), cheat, fleece. fenja, refl. enjenja, dim. enjanga, diff. enjana, rec. 1. shave one another, cut one another’s hair, shave people; igua (ngoma) ikĩ~, listen to (the spirits) shaving one another (said of unfamiliar noises in the dark or, ironically, of people quarrelling). enjanĩra, app. rec. enjaniria, caus. app. rec. enjithia, d. caus. 1. assist sb. to shave, get sb.’s hair cut. 2. cause sb. to be fleeced, deceived, deluded. enjeka, st. pot. be shavable; komba na igoti na ndũkenjekel, see komba. enjera, app. shave, cut hair, at, for. enjũra, conv. lay bare (by clearing away bush, wounding sb. on the head, removing the outer covering surface of sth.),. enjũka, neut. be, become, bald; (of animals) lose hair in patches; (of sky) become clear, unclouded; (of land under bush) get cleared.
rienja?, menja?, n. (111), a shaving. [enja?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of a multistemmed plant, &c.) thick strong part of stem immediately below the surface from which secondary roots grow, stool
FULL_ENTRY: kĩenja, ci-, n. (1), 1. (of a multistemmed plant, &c.) thick strong part of stem immediately below the surface from which secondary roots grow, stool; ~ kĩa irigũ, woody boss from which banana stems grow in a group; cf. gĩtina, gĩthuuna. 2. person of position, ability, and authority ; strong, immovable person; atuĩkĩre ~ kĩa mũtumia, she has become prosperous and commands much power; cf. kĩng’ara, nding’ũri.
kienja-marigũ, ci-, n. (1-1), sp. of locust; cf. see ngigi.

mwenjani, enjani [eenjani], n. (111), 1. barber, hairdresser; cf. kĩnyũthi. 2. extortioner, exorbitant person, trickster; ~ nĩenjagwo, the deceiver is sometimes deceived; cf. mũriani. wenjani, mawenjani, n. 1. the art of dressing hair. 2. fleecing, extortion, trickery; nduka iria irĩũ na ~ mũtheri, those shops are absolutely exorbitant. [enja?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: hard earth from under the hearth (used for making snuff bitter, mixed with goats urine, in absence of soda).
NEW_VALUE: hard earth from under the hearth (used for making snuff bitter, mixed with goats urine, in absence of soda)
FULL_ENTRY: kĩenja-riiko, ci-, n. (1-111), hard earth from under the hearth (used for making snuff bitter, mixed with goats urine, in absence of soda). [enja!]
kĩenjeku, ci-, n. (11), 1. score, groove. 2. name of an age-set dated (by Justin Itotia) 1898 (said to be due to deep scars in the penis at circumcision). [enja"]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. long tapering object, e.g. a stake
FULL_ENTRY: mwenjerere, mĩ-, n. (1v), I1. long tapering object, e.g. a stake; ~ wa mwanake, tall, slender young man. 2. (itimũ rĩa) ~, long narrow-bladed spear, Masai pattern.

rũenji, nyenji, n. (111), razor; nyenji cia ngoma, obsidian artefacts, flakes, washed out in the rains; see nyuranyura; cf. wembe, kanyũi. kĩenji, ci-, n. 1. female circumcising knife; cf. kahiũ ka irua. 2. (in mythology} ogre’s weapon, huge sharp razor.

mwenyere, mi-, n. (IV), I. tree (Cussonia spicata); cf. 2. timber-tree (Hagenia abyssinica); cf. mũthithikũ. Ũrĩ ngatha ~, you are an estimable person but undependable like this tree (which is easily broken and withers quickly when cut down).
enyogondi, see mwinyogondi.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: fragment, piece broken off, scrap of sth. hard and brittle
FULL_ENTRY: kienyũ, ci-, n. (v), fragment, piece broken off, scrap of sth. hard and brittle; ~ kĩa Ngai, a piece of God, a young unmarried man; cf. gĩcunji. kenyũ, twenyũ, n. small bit broken off, crumb, chip; twenyũ twenyũ, crumble into bits. ~ na ~ cioyanagĩra nda, crumblet by crumblet the bits pile up in the stomach. ~ koinaga mũro, a fragment (of stone) can break the digging stick. twenyenyũ, n. cl. 13 only, little tiny bits. [enyũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: break off a piece (from a lump or sth. hard or brittle)
NEW_VALUE: (of a portion of a lump or hard, firm, or brittle) break off, flake off
FULL_ENTRY: enyũra, v.t. (11), break off a piece (from a lump or sth. hard or brittle); divide (foodstuffs) by breaking off; cf. methũra, menyũra, una. enyenyũra, dim. enyũranga, diff. break, divide, into pieces. enyũrangana, rec. diff. divide (sugar-cane, &c.) between people, one another (by breaking into pieces). enyũrana, rec. share sth. awith sb., others. enyũrania, caus. rec. enyũranira, rec. app. share out sth. to others. enyũraniria, caus. app. rec. break off sth. together with sth. else at the same time; divide sth. between others, break sth. into two for. enyũrithia, d. caus. enyũrika, st. pot. enyũrĩra, app. divide sth. and share with, break off a piece for, at. enyũka, v.i. 1. (of a portion of a lump or sth. hard, firm, or brittle) break off, flake off. 2. die suddenly (as by heart failure or when cause is unapparent). enyũkanga, diff. break up into fragments., enyũkana, rec. break into two, become broken completely. enyũkika, st. pot. enyũkira, app. break up on to, because of; crumble over.

era, v.i. (1) swerve, oscillate; (of a locust, fast-moving car, &c.) fly, move, in a swerving or erratic manner; cf. egega, erereka, rera. erera [ereera], dim. move about from side to side; (of trees, corn, grass, &c., in the wind) sway backwards and forwards, to and fro; rock to and fro, oscillate, float about; (of a person) move about purposelessly from place to place, roam about. ereria [ereeria], caus. dim. make sway to and fro, oscillate, move erratically; cause to roam, loaf, be a vagabond. eranga, diff. eranĩra, assoc. erja, caus. ereka, st. pot. erera, app. ererũo, pass. app. be lonely, deseried; feel isolated, depressed, apprehensive.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: kĩa ~, (N.K.) anything lone, isolated, unprotected. [era] (mariera), n. atmosphere, space above ground
FULL_ENTRY: mwera?, mi-, n. (1), kĩa ~, (N.K.) anything lone, isolated, unprotected. [era] (mariera), n. atmosphere, space above ground; ~-inĩ, in space, in the air; nyam cia ~-inĩ, birds. kariera, (tũ-), n. a gentle breeze; cf. karũhuho. [era]
eramatiri, see mwiramatiri.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (11), bulrush or finger millet (small and grey)
NEW_VALUE: bulrush or finger millet (small and grey)
FULL_ENTRY: mwere, (mĩ-), n. (11), bulrush or finger millet (small and grey); mburu

ya ~, see mwere; ~, (said by a girl to an unacceptable suitor) nothing doing! ; ~ mũkonjore, parched, husked millet taken on long journeys or warlike expeditions as provision; ~ mũraya, tall variety of millet; cf. mũhĩa, mĩkombĩ, mwerũ; see mbaria. kiere, ci-, n. 1. single plant, stalk, or ear of millet. 2. irresolute person; ~ kĩa njĩra-inĩ, a nobody, to be ignored or treated anyhow, a laughing-stock, one easily seduced. kamwere, tũ-, n. sp. of bean; see mboco.

NEW_VALUE: make pale, make light in colour
FULL_ENTRY: eregethũra, v.t. (I1) make pale, make light in colour; cause to fade. ereregethũra, dim. eregethũranea, diff. eregethũrana, rec. eregethũrithia, d., caus. eregethũrika, st. pot. eregethũrira, app. eregethũka, v.i. be, become pale, fade, bleach; become whitish, freshlooking, healthy-looking; (of Europeans) be rosy, red; cf. thambũka, tutũka, theũka. eregethũkia, caus. eregethũkira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. face towards, incline, be moving, towards
NEW_VALUE: face, be opposite to, one another
FULL_ENTRY: ereka, v.i. & t. (11), I. face towards, incline, be moving, towards; werekete kũ?, where do you think you are going? 2. aim sth. at, direct sth. at or to. erereka [ereereka], dim. be leaning, inclined to one side, be deflected ; (of a falling tree, &c.) sway to one side. ererekia, caus. dim. make lean to one side, deflect, make sway to one side. erekanga, diff. erekana, rec, I1. face, be opposite to, one another; turn against, quarrel with, one another; nyũmba yakwa yerekaine na yake, my house faces his directly. 2. be in line with sth. else, be consistent, be logical; aragia maũndũ merekaine, say things in a consistent manner; cf. ringana, erekania, caus. rec. Ir. lay evenly, side by side, in the same direction, adjust, relate; ~ mĩaro, lay (sticks forming floor or mattress of a bed) evenly, parallel; ~ horo, set forth a matter in an orderly, logica! manner, point by point, speak lucidly; cf. ringania, arĩra. 2. follow sb. towards, go with, erekanira, app. rec. I1. face one another, be set in the same direction; face, go towards, face the same direction together. 2., face one another in a quarrel, be set against sb. or others. erekaniria, caus. app. rec. erekia, caus. make face towards, direct sth. towards. erekeka, st. pot. erekera, app. face sth., set one’s face towards; mũtino nĩnũtwerekeire!, misfortune is upon us!; nĩngũkw~!, I shall deal with you! erekeria, caus. app. make face, point, towards; lead, take, in a certain direction, erekerera, inten. 1. go direct, straight, undeviatingly; face sth. directly, be orientated towards. 2. bestraightforward, honest, righteous. erekereria, caus. inten. I. make sth. face, point at sb., make go towards. 2. express a wish that some misfortune or fortune may fall on sb.; ndũkanjerekererie mũtino, don’t wish me ill luck.

NEW_VALUE: a clear, direct speaker, one who gives an orderly account of , one who speaks to the point
FULL_ENTRY: mwerekania, erekania [eerekania], n. (1v); ~ wa ndeto, a clear, direct speaker, one who gives an orderly account of sth., one who speaks to the point. [ereka]
mwerekera, mĩ-, n. (x), 1. direction, course, drift, trend; cf. mberekera. 2. (as adv.) right in line, straight ahead; nyamũ ĩrĩ ~, ratha!, the beast is now right in the line of sight, shoot! [ereka]
-erekereru, a. (1v), straightforward, honest, proper. [ereka]
werekeri, n. cl. 14 (111), object, aim, policy. [ereka]
werekereru, n. cl. 14 (1v), straightforwardness, uprightness of conduct., [ereka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. standing water, pond. 2. (cl. 6) water collected in hollows of trees or leaves of arrowroot (where it moves about like quicksilver with the movement of the leaves
NEW_VALUE: water collected in hollows of trees or leaves of arrowroot (where it moves about like quicksilver with the movement of the leaves
FULL_ENTRY: rĩerera [riereera], merera [meereera], n. (vu), I. standing water, pond. 2. (cl. 6) water collected in hollows of trees or leaves of arrowroot (where it moves about like quicksilver with the movement of the leaves; said to be a remedy against anthrax). [era]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. tree (Conopharyngia holstii). 2. tree (Rauwolfia natalensis). njerere, ~, n. fruit of these trees, yielding a sticky latex, mixed with salt and fed to cattle. werere, mawerere, n. latex of above (used as bird-lime, spread on bowed stick and set up in place where birds bathe)
FULL_ENTRY: mwerere [mwereere], mi-, n. (Vii), 1. tree (Conopharyngia holstii). 2. tree (Rauwolfia natalensis). njerere, ~, n. fruit of these trees, yielding a sticky latex, mixed with salt and fed to cattle. werere, mawerere, n. latex of above (used as bird-lime, spread on bowed stick and set up in place where birds bathe); see tanuka; cf. ngunga.
-erereki, a. (11), loafing, wandering. [ereka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: leaning to one side, list, cant, slant.
NEW_VALUE: leaning to one side, list, cant, slant
FULL_ENTRY: mwerereko, mi-, n. (11), leaning to one side, list, cant, slant. [ereka]

mweri, mi-, n. (111), 1. moon, month ; thakara ta ~, be extremely beautiful; ~ warũgama, the new moon; cf. kĩmakia; ~ wĩ na rũhĩa rũnini (rũnene), first (last) quarter of the moon; ~ mũtukĩre, when the moon rises late, begins to wane ; ~ waiganana na riũa, at the full moon; ~, mata, (said when spitting towards the moon for luck); mũico wa ~, the end of the month; wĩra wa ~, a monthly paid job; ~ ũcio Ũngi, next, last, month but one; see ngatha ya baba. 2. menses, menstruation; ona ~, menstruate; cf. ona nyoni, mmenũra, rrindĩra, mũirũ, mũgongo. kamweri, (tũ-), n. 1. moon in early or late phase. 2. small haricot bean. nyamweri, ~, n. small haricot bean (sometimes used as a bead); see mboco; cf. kĩboro.

eria, v.t. (11), (N.K.) wash, clean (clothes, baby, plates, &c.) (with water); cf. thambia. feria, refl. bathe, wash oneself; cf. ĩnoga, ĩthamba. ereria [ereeria], dim. erangia, diff. eraniria, rec. app. wash together. erithia, d. caus. ereka, st. pot. ereria, app. wash at or for. ĩereria, refl. app. bathe at or for.
-eru, a. (11), bent, crooked. [era]
mwerũ, mi-, n. (11), 1. whiteness, brightness; gĩcango kĩa ~, bright brass wire; ciũnma cia ~, white beads; mboco cia ~, white beans. 2. sp. of millet.

werũ, mawerũ, n. (111), 1. whiteness, lightness (esp. of skin); newness; coka ~, become new again. 2. white place, patch. 3. open country, plain, grassland, parkland; uninhabited lands, wilderness, plains; ~-gatĩ, (N.K.) treeless plain. kawerũ, tũ-, n. lawn, glade.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. break wind after taking food, belch
NEW_VALUE: break wind after taking food, belch
FULL_ENTRY: erũka, v.i. (11), I1. break wind after taking food, belch; cf. eka, thebea. 2. (N.K.) overstep a limit, exceed, go beyond, surpass, be a bit more; ~ kĩndũ, have no use for, discard, sth. ; mbia mirioni, the money is more than a million. erergka, dim. erũkanga, diff. erũkanĩra, assoc. erũkia, caus. cause to exceed, go beyond, exceed; nĩtw~ thaa thita, we have gone beyond 12 noon. erũkika, st. pot. erũkira, app. erũrũka, inten. belch unrestrainedly.
mwerũrũko, mĩ-, n. (111), noisy belching after eating. [erũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: dared, sb. or sth. against which or whom liberties have been taken
FULL_ENTRY: -ete, a. (1v), dared, sb. or sth. against which or whom liberties have been taken; kaana g~ nĩ a child that has been made a door-mat by others; gwatonyuo nĩ Ĩ, nĩ gw~, has anybody gone in? Yes, sb. has braved it. [eta]
gĩeterero, (ci-), n. (111), I. patient waiting, expectancy ; endurance. ~ ti kĩnaino, patience is not anxiety; cf. kĩrigĩrĩro. 2. place of waiting. weterero, n. cl. 14, hope, expectation. [eterera]
eteta?, see under ita.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (111), daring, boldness, courage, presumption, rashness
FULL_ENTRY: weti, (maweti), n. (111), daring, boldness, courage, presumption, rashness; cf. ũtoi. [eta]
etia, see under mwitia.
etii, see mwitii.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. stubborn, courageous, daring, presumptuous. 2. that which appears to be conscious of power or maturity
FULL_ENTRY: -etu, a. (Iv), 1. stubborn, courageous, daring, presumptuous. 2. that which appears to be conscious of power or maturity ; wholesome, fully developed ; kĩhĩ gĩ~, a big boy ready for initiation; iguku rĩ~, a huge hump; ndũrũme no kaũ nĩ nj~ -ĩ!, (said by elders after having rejected a ram that was too small) this ram is really a big one (it will do for the sacrifice)! [eta]

etha?, v.i. (1), 1. make a dance step; see kĩbaata. 2. walk with a mincing, affected gait to draw attention and win the favours of the giris. ethetha [etheetha], dim. ethanga, diff. ethanĩra, assoc. ethithia, d. caus. ethera, app.
Ethaga, see Mwĩthaga.

mwethia, mi-, n. (11), 1. shrub (Crotalaria agatiflora); cf. mũthegethe. 2. short-lived shrub (Sesbania punctata) (seed used inside picture-rattle to produce characteristic rustling sound when rhythmically shaken). 3. woody herb (Aeschynomene telekii). 4. small tree (Calpurnia aurea); cf. mũcingiri.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. top of corm of edible arum (Colocasia) (which is cut off and replanted)
FULL_ENTRY: mweũ, mi-, n. (111), 1. top of corm of edible arum (Colocasia) (which is cut off and replanted); edible arum shoot; ~ wa ragai, sp. of edible arum; ~, cut an arum shoot for planting; see ndũma; cf. itũma, irũtũ. 2. (cl. 4) yawn, yawns ; ruta, uma, ikia, ~, yawn. G
rũga, nea, n. (1), I1. sinew, tendon, ligament; rkĩra nga, put in bonds, put under restraint, be firm, refuse to give way on any account; ohora nga, re

NEW_VALUE: core of a boil or carbuncle, pus mixed with blood
FULL_ENTRY: lease, pardon, acquit, go bail for, secure release by payment, help sb. out of a pecuniary difficulty. 2. (of a gun) trigger. kiga, i-, n. core of a boil or carbuncle, pus mixed with blood; cf. mahira, ngũcĩ, thakame.
mũgaa! | (N.K.) min. (1x), flat-topped, thorny tree (Acacia stenocarpa).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (1x), rainless hot period at end of one growing season and before the long or short rains begin
NEW_VALUE: rainless hot period at end of one growing season and before the long or short rains begin
FULL_ENTRY: mũgaa? | (N.K.) mũgaya?, (mi-), n. (1x), rainless hot period at end of one growing season and before the long or short rains begin; cf. mũratho; see mwaka. kagaa | kamũgaa, tũ-, n. warm days in the early part of the hot season before the rains begin; see mwaka.
mũgaaci, mĩ-, n. (1), 1. old type of blue bead. 2. sp. of sweet potato.
kĩgaaro, i-; ngaaro, ~; n. (11), (N.K.) young men’s quarters; cf. thingira.
kigaathirĩrio, i-, n. (x1), praise, commendation. [gaatha!]

NEW_VALUE: one who apof proves everything said, a yes-man
FULL_ENTRY: kagaatho, tũ-, n. (1) one who apof proves everything said, a yes-man. [gaatha!]
Mũgabi (N.k.), see Mũkabi.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. get spoilt, become tainted, because of the presence of moisture
NEW_VALUE: get spoilt, become tainted, because of the presence of moisture
FULL_ENTRY: gaca!, v.i. (1), 1. get spoilt, become tainted, because of the presence of moisture; (of foodstuffs) go watery inside; become sour, tasteless; lose freshness and flavour; cf. gagaya. 2. (of hard objects, teeth) become stained; cf. gunda, guma. acagaca, dim. become more and more watery, spoilt, stained in patches and/or in stages. gacagacia, caus. dim. I. cause to squelch. 2. cause to crackle, rustle. gacagacangia, caus. diff. dim. gacagacĩka, st. pot. dim. gacagacĩria, caus, app. dim. gacanga, diff. gacania, rec. cause one another to become watery, spoilt, stained. gacanira, rec. app. gacaniria, caus. assoc, acia, caus. make moist, soft; cause to go watery inside; cause to get spoilt or stained. gacithia, d. caus, gacĩka, st. pot. gacĩra, app. I1. get spoilt, stained, because of, at, in. 2. (of affairs) fit the circumstances. gacũra, conv. remove stains (off teeth). gacũka, neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: take a forfeit from, take sth. with conditions for repayment at a later time, mortgage
NEW_VALUE: take a forfeit from, take with conditions for repayment at a later time, mortgage
FULL_ENTRY: gaca?, v.t. (11), take a forfeit from, take sth. with conditions for repayment at a later time, mortgage; recover sth. stolen and take part of the recovered material as a reward; take on credit, offset a potential indebtedness; take as one’s due, perquisite; establish a claim on sth.; mũno ũri wa ndũgũ, (phrase used in riddle contests when one has been given a forfeit) I am not going to take a big forfeit because you are my dear friend; cf. ithũra. gacwo, pass. surrender a forfeit (for failing to answer a riddle, &c.). gacagaca, dim. gacanga, diff. gacana, rec. gacanira, rec. app. take a forfeit, from others, together, at, for. gacanĩria, caus. rec. app. gacithia, d. caus. assist sb. to take a forfeit, give sth. with conditions for repayment later; Maina nĩara~ ndegwa, M. is conducting negotiations for getting an ox on ‘tick’, gacithĩria, d. caus. app. ggacĩka, st. pot. gacĩra, app. take a forfeit, because of, at, on behalf of; see kĩgacwa.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of a single rattling, cracking, crackling sound
NEW_VALUE: of a single rattling, cracking, crackling sound
FULL_ENTRY: gaca, ideo. (1), descr. of a single rattling, cracking, crackling sound; gamba ~, rattle. gacagaca!, ideo. descr. of crackling, rustling, rattling sounds, footsteps on muddy ground, dry branches, bushes, or leaves, water squelching under grass; cf, cagaca.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: member of the Agacikũ clan. Njikũ, Gacikũ, n. praise-names used in songs and mild oaths
FULL_ENTRY: Mũgacikũ / Mũgacuku, A-, n. (111), member of the Agacikũ clan. Njikũ, Gacikũ, n. praise-names used in songs and mild oaths; see Wanjikũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sth. obtained on credit, credit terms
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgaco, i-, n. (11), sth. obtained on credit, credit terms; (N.K.) meat feast provided by each man of a group in turn; ndegwa ya ~, an ox obtained on credit terms for a feast; twarĩre ~, the (meat) feast we had was on credit; cf. kĩmandi, mũthaage. [gaca?]

gaga‘, n. cl. 14 only (1), (of a person) hard, harsh, hard-bitten; curmudgeon; ka wĩ what a hard person youare! mogaga [moogaga], n. 1. childish disobedience; selfwill, wilfulness; coercion; cf. ngaganyi. 2. anything wonderful; cause of amazement; cf. &rirũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be damp, chilly, clammy, feel cold
FULL_ENTRY: gaga?, v.i. (1), be damp, chilly, clammy, feel cold; (coll.) die of cold, hunger; cf. ng'ang'a. gagia, caus. make damp, clammy. gagithia, d, caus. make feel cold; cause to die through cold. gagira, app. gagũra, conv. I1. make warm, unclammy. 2. give food (esp. breakfast) to. ĩgagũra, conv. refi. have breakfast, take a morning meal. gagũnranga, conv. diff. hurry to give breakfast; ~ ciana ithii cukuru, be quick to offer breakfast to children that they may go to school. gagũrana, conv. rec. give breakfast to. gagũranira, conv. rec. app. give breakfast for, at. gagũrithia, conv. d. caus, have sb. served with breakfast. gagũrĩka, conv. st. pot. gagũrira, conv. app. give breakfast at, on behalf of. gagũka, neut. become dry; loose wetness, moisture.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of brittle objects, calabashes, &c.) crack all over, break up. gagacanga, diff. gagacania, caus, rec. gagacanira, app. rec. gagacithia, d. caus. gagacĩka, st. pot. (of anything brittle, springy) crackle, crack, break
FULL_ENTRY: gagaca, v.t. (11), (of brittle objects, calabashes, &c.) crack all over, break up. gagacanga, diff. gagacania, caus, rec. gagacanira, app. rec. gagacithia, d. caus. gagacĩka, st. pot. (of anything brittle, springy) crackle, crack, break; (of dry sticks, twigs) be brittle; (of a padlock being locked) click. gagacira, app. gagacũra, conv. widen (a gap); force (a board) apart along a crack or joint; straighten (a bent object, e.g. wire). gagacũka, neut. 1. be split more. 2. be straightened.
mũgagaca, mi-; kagagaca, tũ-; ngagaca, ~~; n. (1v), small red scavenger ants.

NEW_VALUE: () bad watery yam or rotten, shrunk potato
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgagaĩ / kĩgaga, i-, n. (x1), big, good-for-nothing, useless fellow. man. cl. 6, (N.K.) bad watery yam or rotten, shrunk potato. [gaga?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of wood, skin, earth) be, become, bone dry, dry all through. 2. (of beverages) be just right, perfectly mature
NEW_VALUE: (of wood, skin, earth) be, become, bone dry, dry all through
FULL_ENTRY: gagamũka, v.i. (11), 1. (of wood, skin, earth) be, become, bone dry, dry all through. 2. (of beverages) be just right, perfectly mature; cf. acũka. gagamcũkia, caus. gagamũkira, app.
gagania [gaagania], (N.K.), see gaya.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: force apart (with a wedge or lever)
FULL_ENTRY: gaganya, v,t. (11), force apart (with a wedge or lever); wrench; cf. genya, gegenya. agaganya | gagagaganya, dim. gaganyanga, diff. gaganyanira, app. rec. gaganyaniria, caus. app. rec. gaganyithia, d. caus. gaganyika, st. pot. gaganyira, app. force, into, onto; clip, close (a padlock), by force; cf. hihinyira. gaganyiria, caus. app. gaganyũra, conv. split open, force apart, straighten (coiled or bent wire). ĩgaganyũra, refl. conv. 1. (of things) force, split, apart, independently; break loose from. 2. (of persons) act arrogantly, boast themselves; cf. rata. gaganyũka, neut. r. become open, split; get separated, forced apart. 2. be refractory, insubordinate, contumacious; cf. nana, nanũka.
ũgaganyũ | Ũpaganyũki, see Ũpenyũ.

gagata, v.i. (I1), I. ferment; go sour, bad; get stale; (of a banana) be halfripe, sour to taste; cf. gaca, gagaya. 2. (of a person) have a stinging sensation (bodily or mentally), be smart. gagagata | gagagagata, dim. gagatanga, diff. gagatania, rec. gagatanira, assoc. gagatia, caus. gaggatithia, d. caus. gagatĩka, st. pot. gagatĩra, app. gagatiria, caus. app. gagatũra, conv. remove fermentation, make go back to normal state or flavour. gagatũka, neut. cease to be sour, stale,
rOCgagati, (ngagati), n. (1), I. sour, astringent, taste. 2. (N.K.) slight sourness. [gagata]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of potatoes, &c.) be bad through being left too long before harvesting or through being stored badly, become watery, lose freshness and flavour
FULL_ENTRY: gagaya?, v.i. (1), (of potatoes, &c.) be bad through being left too long before harvesting or through being stored badly, become watery, lose freshness and flavour; cf. gaca, gagata. agayanga, diff. gagayania, caus. rec. gagayanĩra, assoc. ggagayaniria, caus. assoc, gagaiya, caus. gagaithia, d. caus, gagaika, st. pot. gagaira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: larva of large beetle, white and fat with black or red head, which usually lays its eggs in thatch or rotting grass.
NEW_VALUE: larva of large beetle, white and fat with black or red head, which usually lays its eggs in thatch or rotting grass
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgagũ, i-, n. (v), larva of large beetle, white and fat with black or red head, which usually lays its eggs in thatch or rotting grass.

FULL_ENTRY: mũgaita, mi-, n. Mũganda | Mũkanda, A-, n. (11), a Muganda, citizen of Uganda. kĩganda? [kĩganda], n. the language of the people of Buganda, Luganda. Uganda, n. the country of Uganda.

mũgandu, mi-, n. (11), shrub (Tinnea ethiopica).

(1), be wrong way round, inside out, upside down. -garii, alt. form of perf.; horo ũ~, a onesided statement. garamia, caus.; ~ njira, make the road bend. garamithia, d. caus. garamika, st. pot. garamira, app. garamũka / garimũka, neut. 1. become right side up, right way round; roll right over, upside down; overturn. 2. become warped, twisted; have wrong side up. garamoũkana | garimũkana, rec. neut. turn over completely. garamũkia, caus. neut. garamũkithia, d. caus. neut. garaaamũkika, st. pot. neut. garamũkira, app. neut.

NEW_VALUE: turn (meat on spit, potatoes on fire, &c
FULL_ENTRY: gariũra, v.t. (1), 1. turn (meat on spit, potatoes on fire, &c.); turn over, turn round the right way up. 2. pull about, worry, harry, pester; make go hither and thither; steer, guide about; kiugo gĩa ki~ njoka, a word that stirs up a serpent (that makes one indignant). igariũra, refl. twist and turn oneself about, writhe. gariũrana, rec.; ~ na, manage, tackle; contend, cope, try conclusions, with; cf. hiũrania. gariũrania, caus. rec. turn sth. this way and that; disturb completely. gariũrithia, d. caus. gariũrika, st. pot. gariũrira, app. gariũka, v.i. I. be restless, unable to settle down; turn and toss, writhe in pain. 2. turn evidence against, break covenant with; betray, be unreliable; cf. garũka. dim. gariũkanga, diff. gariũkana, rec. turn over, inside out, completely; (Bibl.) be perverted. gariũkania, caus. rec. gariũkanĩra, app. rec. gariũkia, caus. I. turn over. 2. disturb, worry. gariũkithia, d. caus. gariũkira, app.
mũgariũra, a-, n. (1), one who turns. ~ ndaagaga ĩmwe ĩcura, he who tries to keep on turning two (maize cobs, &c., being roasted) never fails to burn one of them. [gariũra]

FULL_ENTRY: garnra, v.t. (I1), I. turn (round) the right way up or out. 2. change, make go back. 3. rotate (crops). igarũra, refl. 1. turn oneself round. 2. (of convalescent person) make a change of abode (to see if it will do one good). garũrana, rec. garũrania, caus. rec, exchange; turn right over, round; alternate. ĩggarũrania, refi. caus. rec. turn oneself over (as in bed). garũraniria, caus. app. rec. garũrithia, d. caus. garũrika, st. pot. garũrira, app. 1. change, turn, sth. over at, for; change to sth. else; nyoni ya~ ciugo ciayo, the bird has changed its note. igarũrira, ref. app. change one’s mind, opinion, attitude, ground; go back on one’s word, retract, try to back out. garũrũka, neut. used inten. turn about, round; change, alter, revolve; turn against. garũrũkana, rec. neut. 1. turn against one another. 2. change round, places. garũrũkania, caus. rec. neut. turn right round. garũrũkanĩra, app. rec. neut. garũrũkira, app. neut. turn round at, for; turn against sth., change one’s attitude towards. ĩgarũrũkiria, refl. caus. app. neut. 1. fight back, retaliate, resist; turn tables on, retrieve one’s fortunes, get one’s own back. 2. answer back, speak in one’s own defence. garũka, v.i. turn, be a turncoat, go back on sth., turn against. garũkana, rec. overturn, turn right over, capsize; cf. kurumana, turũkana, ng'aũka. garũkira, app. turn to, go over to the side of.
migarũrũku | magarũrũko, n. cl. 4 or cl. 6 (1), changes. [garũra]

gathirange, see gathirange.

FULL_ENTRY: gathika, v.t. (11), link, couple, make join, hook. gathagathĩka, dim. gathbĩkanga, diff. gathĩkana, rec. become linked up, coupled, hooked. gathĩkania, caus. rec. link up, couple together, hook together completely, anyhow. gathĩkanĩra, app. rec. become completely linked up together at. gathĩkanĩria, caus. app. rec, link up together for, at. gathikithia, d. caus. assist sb. to link up, &c., have linked up, cause to link up. gathĩkĩka, st. pot. get linked up. gathĩkĩkĩra, app. st. pot. gathikira, app. link up for, at. gathikirira, inten. add more links on to link, hook on to sth. at, for. gathũra, conv. bend, break, open (links, hooks, &c.) disjoint, separate; ~ maita mũgwanja, break open a ring (made by joining the thumb and the index finger) seven times (a ‘light’ oath). ĩgathũra, refi. dissociate oneself. gathũrana, rec. conv. gathũrania, caus. rec. conv. uncouple, unlink, separate apart completely. gathũrithia, caus. conv. gathũrĩka, st. pot. conv. athũrĩra, app. conv. gathũka, neut., 1. gaya, v.t. (1) 1. divide, share out. 2. inherit, receive a legacy by inheritance. igaya, refl. 1. apportion out one’s possessions before death, make one’s wili. 2. (coll.) say one’s prayers before dying; be extreme in one’s action. gayagaya, dim. gayanga, diff. gayana, rec. divide between selves, share. gayania, caus. rec. divide into portions; divide out, apportion to others; divide up; ~ ikũmi na igĩrĩ, divide ten by two. gayanira, app. rec. divide between selves at a point; share, at, because of. gayaniria, caus. app. rec. divide up together with sth. else. gaithia, d. caus. have divided up; assist sb. to divide. gaika, st. pot. be, become, divisible. gayĩkana | gaikana, rec. st. pot. be, become, divided up; be divisible into portions. gayikanĩra / gaĩkanira, app. rec. st. pot. be divisible together with sth. else. gayira / gaira, app. allot a portion to; divide up, at, because of ; leave a legacy to. ĩgayira / ĩgaira, refl. app. bequeath sth. to sb. pass. refl. app. have bequeathed to one, inherit. gayũra / gaũra, conv. separate up a group; take out a portion. gayũrana / gaũrana, rec. cony. gayũrania / gaũrania, caus. rec. conv. gayũka / gpaũka, neut. separate out from the rest of thel group. gayũkana / gaũkana, rec. neut, (of a whole group) separate out, get divided into groups; be, split up, divided, forked, parted. gayũkania / gaũkania, caus. rec. neut. separate out, place things in groups, kinds, units; split up into groups. gayũkanĩra / gaũkanira, app. rec. neut. separate out into groups at, because of. gayũkaniria / gaũkanĩria, caus. app. rec. neut,
mũgaya (N.K.), se mũgaa.

geca, v.t. & i. (11), 1. crack into pieces, destroy; break (a billet of firewood, &c.) into pieces (by striking end on ground). 2. break up with teeth (splitting bits off by sheer force); peck hard so as to break or tear; crack. 3. jag, dent (metal); hack down (bush), break up new ground. 4. consume (wealth); use improvidently; eat, drink, heartily, greedily, with gusto. 5. prate, chatter, prattle; click, creak, crack; cf. geka. gecageca, dim. I. split (wood, &c.} into small bits; destroy, break, incessantly. 2. go click-click, make a creaking, cracking sound; ~ magri, get your legs cracking and go. gecanga, diff. gecanira, app. rec, gecithia, d. caus. geceka, st. pot. gecera, app.
