geegenya, v.t. (11), force apart.

gega, v.i. (1), 1. be amazed, astounded, astonished; cf. gegea. 2, (of wet garment, grass, person’s body) be damp, cold, clammy; cf. gaga, gegagega, dim. geganga, diff. gegania, caus. rec. amaze others, each other; cause astonishment, astonish (people). gegia, caus. amaze, astonish. gegithia, d. caus. gegeka, st. pot. gegera, app. gegũra, conv, make cease to be amazed. gegũka, neut. cease to be amazed.
kĩgeganio | kĩgegania, i- ; igegania, ma-; n. (1) 1. that which causes amazement, astonishment, perplexity ; a marvel; cf. kĩmakanio. 2. (cl. 6) -a magegania, astonishing, marvellous. [gega]
gegeca / bebeca, v.t, (11), 1. crunch with teeth, noisily ; gnaw. 2. squander ; cf. geca, thethera,
kĩgegea / (N.x.) kĩgĩgia, i-, n. (1), a congenital fool, half-wit. [gegea]

NEW_VALUE: of a smug, pretentious style of talking
FULL_ENTRY: gegepgege, ideo. (1), descr. of a smug, pretentious style of talking.

gegenya, v.t. (11), 1. eat, chew, a bit off; cf. gaganya, gekenya, genya', gegeca, geca. 2. split apart; cf. gekenya. gegenyũra, conv. 1. break loose. 2. make perverse, gegenyũka, neut. be perverse, self-willed; break loose from control.

igego, ma-, n. (1), tooth; ~ gĩturi, wisdom tooth; magego ma mũtheko, front teeth; magego ma iria, milk teeth (referred to only as mbembe ngunũku q.v.); see eha, enyenya, bucuca; cf, gathandambũirũ, hũmĩ, ¿kamburu, kĩng'ethũ, kĩnng'uru, kĩni. kapego, tũ-, n. 1. small tooth; kainainaga ta ~ gekuuma, it shakes like a very loose tooth (about to come out). 2. ¥ vulgar term for clitoris; cf. kanyamũ, kang’ũthũ.
rũgeithi, see ngeithi,

NEW_VALUE: of leguminous creeping plant found growing in hedges and fed to goats, sheep, &c
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgeithio!, i- | ma-, n. (11), sp. of leguminous creeping plant found growing in hedges and fed to goats, sheep, &c.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a present handed over when greeting sb., or sent as a sign of greeting
NEW_VALUE: a present handed over when greeting sb
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgeithio?, i-, n. (u1), a present handed over when greeting sb., or sent as a sign of greeting; see ngeithi. [geithia]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cylindrical case for protecting ostrich plumes (used mainly by Akamba).
NEW_VALUE: cylindrical case for protecting ostrich plumes (used mainly by Akamba)
FULL_ENTRY: mũgeka?, mi-, n, (n1), cylindrical case for protecting ostrich plumes (used mainly by Akamba).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sth. given as acknowledgement of service rendered
NEW_VALUE: given as acknowledgement of service rendered
FULL_ENTRY: rũgeka, ngeka, n. (111), sth. given as acknowledgement of service rendered ; penalty, fine, paid to njama; (N.K.) fee paid by newly initiated medicine man to his instructors ; cf, thĩnjo, ngerenwa. [geka"]
igekea, see igigia.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: x. widen a gap, groove, split
FULL_ENTRY: gekenya, v.t., (11), x. widen a gap, groove, split; see gegenya. 2. straighten coiled wire. 3. (of a person) strive with another (in order to get him to amend his evil ways, e.g. by abusing him).
rũgeko!, ngeko!, n. (111), malediction.
rũgeko?, ngeko?, n. (1), creaking, rattling. [geka?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: r. be, become, decorated, adorned, bedecked, resplendent
NEW_VALUE: be, become, decorated, adorned, bedecked, resplendent
FULL_ENTRY: gema, v.i. (1), r. be, become, decorated, adorned, bedecked, resplendent; cf. thakara. 2. be armed, accoutred. 3. (coll.) be ‘lit up’, drunk. gemagema, dim., gemanga, diff. gemanira, assoc, pemaniria, caus. assoc. decorate sth, together with sth. else. gemia, caus. decorate, adorn, bedeck. rgemia, refi. caus. gemithia, d. caus. emeka, st, pot, gemera, app.
kĩgemero, i-, n. (1), 1. lip of calabash, cup, &c. 2. thickness of anything flat such as a slice of meat, rind of fruit, piece of cloth, leather, mattress, book. [gema]
(m&-), mĩgemio, n, (1), decorations, ornamental patterns. [gema]
nagenero, n, cl. 6 (1), casualties (in war).
mũgenerũo, a-, n. (1), host. [gena]

mũgeni, a-, n. (1), stranger, foreigner; visitor, guest, object of hospitality. kageni, tũ-, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. Macdonald Weed; see kang’ei. n. cl 14 only, condition of being away from home, of being a guest; tũra ~, live abroad, away from one’s own district; ~, go visiting away from home; gwata ~, welcome (as a visitor); see genda.

NEW_VALUE: go on a journey to, because of, for the purpose of visiting sb
FULL_ENTRY: genda, v.i. (11), travel, go on an expedition; cf. thii gendagenda, dim. gendanga, diff. gendanĩra, assoc. gendithia, d. caus. gendeka, st. pot, gendera, app. 1. go on a journey to, because of, for the purpose of visiting sb.; 2. scheme against, be adversary of; cf. cirira. 3. go by way of; cf. thiira. gendereria, caus. inten. I. go from place to place, from stage to stage; go on from evil to evil, crime to crime; cf. thĩrĩrĩra. 2. ~ ũhoro, prolong, draw out, carry on with a discussion.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: transverse binding-rod encircling the rafters of a traditional round hut at wall-head, like a purlin
NEW_VALUE: rafters, space between wall and roof (rats’ highway)
FULL_ENTRY: mũgenda-mbia, mĩ-, n. (1-1), transverse binding-rod encircling the rafters of a traditional round hut at wall-head, like a purlin ; cf. mũikio. magenda-mbia, n. cl. 6 only, rafters, space between wall and roof (rats’ highway); horizontal] supports for the rack in a traditional hut, see itara; tie beams in a square house, ceiling; ndagereire ~, I went through the rafters (had a narrow escape). [genda]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. cross cousin, child of mother’s brother or father’s sister
NEW_VALUE: cross cousin, child of mother’s brother or father’s sister
FULL_ENTRY: mũgendi?, a-, n. (1), 1. cross cousin, child of mother’s brother or father’s sister; ~ wa awa, wa ithe, paternal uncle, father’s brother; see baba, ithe. 2. one resembling, approximating to or of the same class, genus, &c. ; ~ wa nyam, man akin to beast; ngwenda kũgũra mbũri 5 o na ~ wa I0, I want to buy 5 goats or even up to 10.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. journey, trip. 2. trading expedition to Masai country
FULL_ENTRY: rũgendo, ng’endo, n. (1), 1. journey, trip. 2. trading expedition to Masai country; cf. thabari. ũngendo, n. cl. 14 only, place to go to; ~, he has nowhere to turn to, he is destitute.

genya, v.t. & i. (11), 1. nibble, gnaw; take small bites at, bite a bit off, sth. ; bore into, devour gradually; cf. ria, thegenya. 2. talk facetiously, sarcastically, to, of; cf. ceca, thaka. genyagenya, dim. genyagenyũra, conv. dim. bring nearer to breakingpoint. genyanga, diff. genyana, rec. 1. pull each other’s leg. 2. have an argument, altercation; bicker at one another. genyanira, app. rec. enyanĩria, assoc. & caus. app. I1. (of persons) talk jestingly at the same time. 2. eat (things) away at the same time. genyithia, d. caus. genyeka, st. pot. genyera, app. genyũra, conv. break off (piece of wire, metal, &c.), bend until sth. breaks, snaps; cf. enyũra. genyũrania, caus. rec. conv. break off completely. genyũranira, app. rec, cony. genyũraniria, caus. app. rec. cony. break off (things) at the same time. genyũrithia, d. caus. conv. genyũrika, st. pot. conv. genyũrira, app. conv. genyũka, neut. 1. (of bent metal, &c.) break, snap, off; (of joint) get dislocated, out of joint; cf. thenyũka. 2. feel a sharp stabbing pain (in joints or bones). 3. break away from control, refuse to comply with tradition; be self-willed, wayward, unruly. genyũkwo, pass. neut, 1. suffer dislocation. 2. have stabbing pains.

genyegenye, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1. nibbling, taking little nibbles. 2. squeak, short shrill sounds as of an unoiled hinge or spring. 3. sounds made with thce teeth as of a goat chewing potato vines or a cow chewing the cud; cf. gece. [genya]

NEW_VALUE: slab, block of brackish earth given to livestock to chew
FULL_ENTRY: mũgenyo, mi- ; kigenyo? | kĩgengo, i-, n. TII, 1. that which is eaten into; a large persistent ulcer, sore, canker; a lepers wound; cf. kĩronda, gĩtegenyũ. 2. place where stock eat salt or brackish earth; (N.K.) slab, block of brackish earth given to livestock to chew.

genyũ, ideo. (11), descr. of: 1. breaking, flaking, off. 2. sudden and complete breaking of a solid object into two or more parts; akĩgambia kĩrũthanju kĩrĩa o ~, he broke the big stick in two just like that; see enyũra. [genyũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (111), (of earth) subsoil with a fairly high clay content (used for plastering walls)
NEW_VALUE: (of earth) subsoil with a fairly high clay content (used for plastering walls)
FULL_ENTRY: kigenyũ, (i-), n. (111), (of earth) subsoil with a fairly high clay content (used for plastering walls); see tĩri.
Ũgenyũ, see under ng’'enyũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cf. rrainama. 2. do oneself harm, get oneself into serious trouble. geragera, dim, geranga, diff. gerana, rec.
NEW_VALUE: measure one thing by another, lay one thing against another for comparison
FULL_ENTRY: gera!, v.t. (1) zI. count, measure, reckon; nda~ga atĩ thaara nĩ kĩgwa, I thought the Napier grass was sugarcane; cf. tara, thima, 2. ~ ngero, commit an offence, cause grave harm; ~ ngero, cause serious damage to sb?’s property, body, reputation, hurt sb. grievously. ĩgera, ref. r. measure, test, oneself; ~ hinya na, try one’s strength on; rina rĩegera, when the sun begins to decline (about 3 p-m.), cf. rrainama. 2. do oneself harm, get oneself into serious trouble. geragera, dim, geranga, diff. gerana, rec.; ~ hinya (na), test one another’s strength, compete. gerania, caus, rec. test, tempt, others, each other. geranĩra, app. rec. geranĩria, caus. app. rec. test sth. together with sth. else; test others, each other. geria, caus. try, attempt; test, tempt, try out; ~mbarĩki, prepare a brew of castor-oil for initiation. igeria, refl. caus. test oneself with, try for oneself (by handling a tool, weapon, &c.). gerithia, d. caus.; ~ ngero, caus sb. to be hurt badly, ruined, assist sb. to commit a crime. gereka!, st. pot. gerekania, caus. st. pot. 1. measure one thing by another, lay one thing against another for comparison. 2. use a parable. igerekania, refl. caus. st. pot. imitate, liken oneself to. gerera, app. i. count, measure in, at, for. 2. do with care. ĩgerera, refl. app. be audacious, daring, over-confident in respect of sth. gereria, caus. app. test, try, tempt at, for, because of. fgereria, refl. caus. app. test oneself. gererera, inten. I. gauge, measure, carefully ; assess, act, with deliberation; do sth. with moderation; ~ thaa ciakinya Ũke, watch out for the right time to come. 2. mete out sth. that is due to sb. 3. cause grave damage at, for. gerereria, caus. inten. 1. make as if to do sth.; show how to do sth. by demonstration. 2. try the feel, weight, of sth. 3. express hope that sth. good or evil may befall sb.; ~ mũndũ gĩkũ, wish death to a person; cf. athirĩria, thĩgĩrĩria.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: go by way of, pass along
NEW_VALUE: iron, piece of iron (formerly in use as currency)
FULL_ENTRY: gera?, v.t. (1), go by way of, pass along; cf. hĩtũka. geranga, diff. geranĩra. app. rec. geraniria, caus. app. rec. make (others) go along sth. together. geria, caus. make go along a certain way; ~ njĩra mũgũnda, make a road across the garden; ~ mũtokaa njira gatagatĩ, drive the car in the middle of the road. gerithia, d. caus. gereka?, st. pot. be passable. gerekera, app. st. pot. gerera, app. go by, pass along at, in; guothe mara~, whichever way they go. gereria, caus., app. gererithia, d. caus. app. put through a practical demonstration, teach by causing to do, train (in earnest or make-believe). gerereka, st. pot. app. gererera, inten. i-, n. (1), iron, piece of iron (formerly in use as currency); metal in general; ~ kĩa copper; ~ gia ta, bowstick; cf. cuma, gĩkama, rathuku, gĩcango, thahabu, betha. kagera, tũ-, n. (of a door) bolt; ~ ka matemo, see matemo; nyua ~, see nyua. [gera!]
kĩgeranio, i-, n. (1) examination (school or other test). [gera1]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: shrub (Hibiscus sp.) (used for bewitching an unknown thief, expelling sb. from his family, &c.
NEW_VALUE: shrub (Hibiscus ) (used for bewitching an unknown thief, expelling sb
FULL_ENTRY: mũgere, mĩ-, n. (vI), shrub (Hibiscus sp.) (used for bewitching an unknown thief, expelling sb. from his family, &c.; also for withies, ceremonial staves, notching).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: take aim at, bring into line with, point at
FULL_ENTRY: gereka?, v.t. (1), take aim at, bring into line with, point at; wing, put steadying tail feathers on, (arrow); cf. hũra cabaa, onereria. geragereka, dim. gerekanga, diff. gerekana, rec. gerekania, caus. rec. bring into line with each other; liken to, compare; see gera!. gerekanira, app. rec. gerekaniria, caus. app. rec. gerekithia, d. caus, gerekeka, st. pot. gerekera, app. gerekereria, caus. app. I1. point, aim (gun, arrow, spear, &c.) at. 2. wish sth., good or bad, to befall sb.; cf. gerereria (gera!).

NEW_VALUE: a wish (that good or bad may happen to sb
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgerekererio, i-, n. (1), a wish (that sth. good or bad may happen to sb.). [gereka]

NEW_VALUE: of creeping bush-plant, the leaves of which are used as spinach when food is scarce
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgerema, i-, n. (1x), sp. of creeping bush-plant, the leaves of which are used as spinach when food is scarce.
ũgerereri, n. cl. 14 (1), equity. [gera]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. prudence, careful use or administration
FULL_ENTRY: - [moogerero]), n. prudence, careful use or administration; cf. ngererero.,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who measures, a measurer. igeri, ma-, n. measure, size, quantity
NEW_VALUE: preciseness, clarity, ability to get to the point
FULL_ENTRY: mũgeri, a-, n. (1), one who measures, a measurer. igeri, ma-, n. measure, size, quantity; cf. mũigana. kĩgeri, i-, n. a measure, sth. to measure with. ũgeri! | geri, (mageri), n. 1. preciseness, clarity, ability to get to the point; kanua gaka karĩ ~, this fellow is an expert story-teller. 2. good aim, good eye; cf. wathi. [gera!]
ũgeri?, n. cl. 14 only (1); ~ wa ngero, crime, offences involving penalties, law-breaking. [gera!]
igeria, ma-, n. (1), a try, attempt. Mageria nomo mahota, tryings are succeedings. Cf. gerio. [gera!]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: candidate for trial, test, or examination
FULL_ENTRY: mũperio, a-, n. (11), candidate for trial, test, or examination; one who is tempted. mũgerio, mi-, n. I. sample, test sample; first portion of a new crop to be eaten, first-fruits ; ci mũtũrio. 2. greeting between persons who have shared some of the first green maize of theseason. igerio, ma-, n. 1.a special kind of temptation. 2. (cl. 6) trial, temptations; cf. igeria. kĩgerio, i-, n. examination, test; cf. kĩgeranio. [gera]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: measure, size. mũgero, mi-, n. measure, vessel used as a measure
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgero, i-, n. (1), measure, size. mũgero, mi-, n. measure, vessel used as a measure; ~ wa kĩhũri, a calabashful. [gera!]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. measure, measuring vessel, grain measured out
NEW_VALUE: (of medicine man) paraphernalia, medicaments, ingredients of medicine, stock in trade
FULL_ENTRY: mũgerũa, mĩ-, n, (1), I1. measure, measuring vessel, grain measured out; small quantity, sample; andũ acio matiri ~, those people are of no account, few in number., 2. (cl. 4) (of medicine man) paraphernalia, medicaments, ingredients of medicine, stock in trade; cf. mĩthooko.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: harvest, reap. refl. (of an abundant crop) reap itself. gethagetha, dim. gethanga, diff. gethanĩra, app. rec. gethaniria, caus. app. rec. reap (crops) together. gethithia, d. caus. getheka, st. pot. gethera, app. reap for, at, because of
FULL_ENTRY: getha', v.t. (1), harvest, reap. refl. (of an abundant crop) reap itself. gethagetha, dim. gethanga, diff. gethanĩra, app. rec. gethaniria, caus. app. rec. reap (crops) together. gethithia, d. caus. getheka, st. pot. gethera, app. reap for, at, because of; ~ macungwa gikabu, pluck the oranges and put them into the basket. getherera, inten. reap some of a crop; reap and leave derelict plants standing in the field; reap one out of a mixed crop; mwa~ waru ria-inĩ?, why did you reap the potatoes without clearing the ground of weeds?

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cause repeated throbbing, shooting pains (esp. in joints). gethwo, pass.
FULL_ENTRY: getha?, v.t. (11), cause repeated throbbing, shooting pains (esp. in joints). gethwo, pass.; ~ nĩ mahĩndĩ, be in constant pain in one’s back; cf. tuura, genũka. gethagetha, dim. gethanga, diff. gethanira, app. rec. gethithia, d. caus. getheka, st. pot. gethera, app. gethũra, conv. I1. dislocate, disjoint (esp. animal joints). 2. cause sharp pain in a joint. gethũka, neut. 1.(of inanimate things, esp. when dry) come apart; cf. gathũka, ahũka, kahũka, genyũka, gitũka. 2. (of bones, joints) ache, have a slight pain; be dislocated. gethũkwo, pass. neut. have a sharp pain; cf, genyũkwo.
(ni) getha, see (nĩ) kenda?,
magetha, n. cl. 6 only (1), harvest. [getha!]
kagethe, see under ngethe?,
mapgethe, n. cl. 6 (11), (N.K.) 1. unripe bananas; cf. macii. 2. the Anjirũ clan.

NEW_VALUE: small vermin animal, like a squirrel, 2
FULL_ENTRY: mũgethi, a-, n. (1), reaper. kĩgethi, i-, n. 1. small vermin animal, like a squirrel, 2. small harvesting knife. [getha"]
ghu, a strong expulsion of breath as at a pause in climbing a hill. ~? Mũka ũrĩ ihu na karĩma, (riddle) huu!; (ans.) a pregnant woman climbing a hill.
mũgi, mĩ-, n. (1), an under-shrub (Achyrospermum carvalhki).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. bother, disturb, trouble, harass. 2. ~ trample a field
FULL_ENTRY: gia [giya], v.t. (11), 1. bother, disturb, trouble, harass. 2. ~ trample a field; ~ gĩthaka, wade through bush. refl. r. be hesitant, have difficulty in making up one’s mind, have an inward struggle; be in two minds, noncommittal, evasive; do reluctantly. 2. ~ motondoinĩ, be stuck, struggle, in mud. giagia, dim. gianga, diff. giana, rec. wrestle with, struggle against, wrangle with, one another; ~ na mathina, face difficulties. giania, caus. rec. make (bush, &c.) entangled, bring confusion amongst. gianĩra, app. rec. struggle against sb., for (sth.), at (a place); ake mara~ indo ciake, his sons are squabbling over his wealth. gianiria, caus. app. rec. giithia, d. caus. gĩka, st. pot. be in difficulty; be oppressed, struggle along as one whose strength is inadequate, make futile efforts; ~ mũno nĩ toro, be completely overpowered by sleep; cf. giira, app. giũra, conv. I1. make regain composure, former position. 2. ruffle, trample; (of plants, bush) worry, bother. fgiũra, refi. conv. struggle unavailingly (to get load on to one’s back, &c.). giũranga, diff. conv. derange, pull about; tumble, ruffle, up; rummage among, tousle. giũrania, caus. rec. conv. make ruffled, entangle, bring into confusion; ~ irio, (of cattle) trample crops. giũka, neut. r. become unruffled, regain composure, 2. (of plants) stand erect after being trampled upon.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: break up (thin dry twigs, grass, straw for lighting fire, &c.).
NEW_VALUE: break up (thin dry twigs, grass, straw for lighting fire, &c
FULL_ENTRY: gica, v.t. (11), break up (thin dry twigs, grass, straw for lighting fire, &c.). gicagica, dim. gicagicanga, diff. dim. gicanga, diff. gicania, caus. rec, gicanĩka, st. pot. caus. rec. gicithia, d. caus. gicĩra, app.

NEW_VALUE: song in cryptic terms sung by young men from ridge to ridge
FULL_ENTRY: kagica, tũ-, n. song in cryptic terms sung by young men from ridge to ridge.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of state of compietion, absoluteness
FULL_ENTRY: gici, ideo. (1/11), descr. of state of compietion, absoluteness; kira ~, be absolutely silent; cf. ki.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: r. filth, disgusting mess, revolting sight
FULL_ENTRY: magigi, n. cl. 6 only (1), r. filth, disgusting mess, revolting sight; na ~, it is disgusting. 2. feeling of loathing.
gigia, see pegea.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: feel nervous at, be fearful of, dread, feel a dread of, have a horror of. ĩgigima, refl. be disgusted at
NEW_VALUE: feel nervous at, be fearful of, dread, feel a dread of, have a horror of
FULL_ENTRY: gigima, v.t. (11), feel nervous at, be fearful of, dread, feel a dread of, have a horror of. ĩgigima, refl. be disgusted at; feel suspicious, have qualms about; Ũcio nĩwatũma egigime, that rouses all his aversion.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: wrest (sth. held by another person), try to deprive by force
NEW_VALUE: wrest ( held by another person), try to deprive by force
FULL_ENTRY: giginya, v.t. (11), wrest (sth. held by another person), try to deprive by force; exploit; cf. huria, gaganya, hihinya. gigagiginya, dim. giginyanga, diff. giginyana, rec., try to wrest sth, from each other, wrest sth. from others, deprive each other or others by force, prey on each other, exploit others. giginyanira, app. rec. giginyaniria, caus. app. rec. exploit together, deprive at the same time. giginyithia, d. caus. giginyĩka, neut. giginyira, app. giginyiria, caus. app.

NEW_VALUE: taking away by force, robbery, ũtunyani
FULL_ENTRY: Ũgiginyani, n. cl. 14 (u1), 1. taking away by force, robbery, cf. ũtunyani. 2. forcing open, levering apart. [giginya]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who deprives others of their things.
NEW_VALUE: one who deprives others of their things
FULL_ENTRY: -giginyani, a. (111), one who deprives others of their things. [giginya]
mũgĩko [mũgiyiko], mi-, n. (111), 1. physical struggle, tussle, harassment. 2. mental worry, inward struggle, perturbation. [gia]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (1), condition of maturity, reliability, reasonableness
FULL_ENTRY: ũgima, (mogima [moogima]), n. (1), condition of maturity, reliability, reasonableness; integrity, truth; ka ~, use common sense, act responsibly.
-gima / (-gimi), a. (1), whole, sound; fully grown, adult; complete; see gimaha.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be, become grown up, of full adult status
FULL_ENTRY: gimaha, v.i. (1), be, become grown up, of full adult status; be full grown; cf. gimara. gimahanga, diff. gimacaus. diff. gimahania, caus. rec. gimahanĩra, assoc. gimahaniria, caus. assoc. gimahia, caus. gimahithia, d. caus. gimahira, app. act, behave in gentlemanly style to, towards, at. gimahũra, conv. 1. cause sb. to be ungentlemanly. 2. discipline sb.

egimara, v.i. (1), (rare) grow up, mature, attain adulthood. gimagimara, dim. gimaranga, diff. gimarania, caus. rec. gimaranira, app. rec. gimaraniria, caus. app. rec. gimaria, caus. gimarithia, d. caus. gimarira, app. gimarũka, neut. behave as if one was not an adult or a gentleman.,
ginũka, see gitũka.
gindaya (N.K.), see hinda.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. thatch, roof in, fix top of a structure, cover
NEW_VALUE: thatch, roof in, fix top of a structure, cover
FULL_ENTRY: gita, v.t. & i. (1), 1. thatch, roof in, fix top of a structure, cover; ~ na macai, use corrugated iron for roof. 2. (of dense tree foliage) become covered, closed, matted, be bushy, dense, closely packed, form a canopy; karũgiri kagitĩte, a compact, wellgrown, hedge. 3. be prosperous; become self-satisfied, obstinate; kou muuma kwa~-i?, is all going well where you come from? ĩgita, refl. defend oneself with a shield, sword, stick; parry a blow; dodge, ward off, a missile; guard oneself. gitagita, dim. gitagitia, caus. dim. 1. try to conceal, hush up, cover up partially, camouflage. 2. use an euphemism for an undesirable word. gitanga, diff. gitana, rec. (of thick foliage, &c.) become completely closed up. 2. be well thatched, securely covered. gitania, caus. rec, 1. thatch, roof in, carelessly. 2. conceal by inserting among other things or behind sth. else. ĩgitania, refl. caus. rec. hide oneself behind, link oneself with sth. or sb. for protection, security; cf. ĩgiritania. gitanĩra, assoc. or app. rec. gitaniria, caus. app. rec, thatch (things) together, gitia, caus. hide, conceal, screen, from view. refl. caus. set oneself apart, take shelter as from rain; keep oneself away from, be private, self-contained; ~ ũthiũ, hide one’s face. gitithia, d. caus. gitĩka, st. pot. gitĩkĩka, d. st. pot. gitira, app. thatch for, at; defend. fgitĩra, refl. app.; ~ mbura, shelter oneself from rain (with a banana leaf as umbrella). fgitĩria, ref. caus. app. hide oneself from. conv. remove roof. gitũka, neut. become unthatched, gitara, stc. 1. become completely and closely thatched, roofed in. 2. (of a place where sth, can be concealed) be secluded, sheltered.
-gitaru, a. (11), secluded, sheltered. [gita]

gia, v.t. & 1. (11), 1. have, be; possess, get; be at a place, in sth.; ~ hairũ, be in a fix; agĩre kũ?, where has he got to?; ndingienda agie nhoro-inĩ nyũ, I would not like him to be in this matter, affair; nrokana~ga, may you go on getting more (prosper); tkaya wa Ngai ũro~ na inyuĩ, may the peace of God be with you; ~ kaana, give birth; ~ indo, become rich; ~ kinya, become strong. 2. come to be, amount to, become; igiri ikĩrehwo igĩkĩ~ inya, two more were brought to make four; (as aux.) gũkua o amwendete ma, (her) affection for him lasted right to the day of her death; cf. ya, tuĩka. rec. fetch others (in turn). giranira, app. rec. FULL_ENTRY: gona, v.i. (1), I. (of a top) hum; cf. 2. become unconscious, be under influence of a spell; doze in sleep or sickness. gonania, caus. rec. gonanira, app. rec. gonaniria, caus. app. rec. make hum at the same time, sing or speak in deep sonorous voices together; spin tops at the same time. gonia, caus. I. hum, sing, or speak in deep sonorous tones; spin a top; ~ horo, say sth. in a deep emphatic way; ~ toro, sleep deeply and soundly; ~ rũimbo, sing in a deep melodious voice. 2. put to sleep (with anaesthetic). gonithia, d. caus. goneka, neut. gonera, app. goneria, caus. app.; ~ iriri, sleep soundly in bed. gonora, conv. unleash a deep and sonorous sound, hum with vigour; ~ rũĩmbo, sing heartily. gonoka, neut. 1. make a deep sustained sound or hum (as the sound of a dance being carried on with vigour); cf. ruruma, nogona. 2. regain Consciousness,

kĩgoni, i-, n. (1), 1. one in a deep sleep; (N.K.) one rendered unconscious. 2, feeble, debilitated person; cf. kĩonda. 3. medicine causing unconsciousness (used for purposes of theft or seduction). [gona]

NEW_VALUE: bend inwards tobreaking point, snap without severing
FULL_ENTRY: gondoka, v.i. (1) (of supple material, trees, arms, &c.) be bent inwards, snap; get broken without being completely severed; cf. kondoka, gotoka. gondagondoka, dim. gondokana, rec. become twisted entirely. gondokanĩra, app. rec. gondokaniria, caus. app. rec. gondokera, app. gondora, v.t. 1. bend inwards tobreaking point, snap without severing; ~ mũndũ, give a person a good thrashing. 2. drink copiously. gondorania, caus. rec. bend, break, twist, entirely without severing. gondorithia, d. caus. gondorera, app.
(ka-) (kĩ-) gondu, see under ng’ondu.

mũgongo, mi-, n. (1), 1. back; kuua na ~, carry on the back. Gũtiri wa nda na wa ~, there is not a child of the front and a child of the back (parents should have no favourites); cf. ngoro, mahindi, gĩcũngũrũ. 2. ridge; ~ wothe, all the people living on a ridge; ~ wothe wa Gikũyũ, the whole Kikuyu country-side. 3. menstruation; cf. mweri. rũgongo, ng’ongo, n. ridge of land, top of ridge, upper part of slope; section of country bounded by two rivers or valleys, forming a community; ~ na mcchuro, up and down. kagongo, tũ-, n. short, narrow, low ridge of land.
gongona, v.t. (11), sacrifice. gongonera, app. sacrifice for, on behalf of.

igongona [igongoona], ma-, n. (vI), I1. sacrifice, blood sacrifice; ritual use of beer, gruel, or honey; customary ceremony presided over by the head of the community, father of family, or medicine man, directed to the spirits of the ancestors, sometimes to God (the commonest cause being removal or avoidance of ritual pollution); ~ rĩa mũciairi, sacrifice of purification after childbirth; mbri ya ~, sacrificial lamb or goat; ruta ~, perform a sacrificial ceremony ; gĩa na ~, hold a sacrificial (or other) ceremony ; haka ngoma (Ngai) na ~, appease spirits (God) with sacrifice; mwene ~, giver of a sacrifice; mũrutĩrao-~, person for whom sacrifice is offered; mũruti-(wa-) ~, anyone officiating at a sacrificial ceremony; kĩa ~, anything used in a sacrifice; cf. ihaki. 2. any public or private ceremony, Masonic rite, military review, laying of a foundation stone, opening of a new institution, &c.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. place of offering, altar, place at which village sacrificial ritual is performed, marked by a branch of wild fig, olive (see mũĩkũngũgũ), taken when the village was moved and sometimes showing the position of the sacred stone sunk in the ground when the homestead was begun
NEW_VALUE: place of offering, altar, place at which village sacrificial ritual is performed, marked by a branch of wild fig, olive (see mũĩkũngũgũ), taken when the village was moved and sometimes showing the position of the sacred stone sunk in the ground when the homestead was begun
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgongona [kigongoona], i-, n. (v1), I1. place of offering, altar, place at which village sacrificial ritual is performed, marked by a branch of wild fig, olive (see mũĩkũngũgũ), taken when the village was moved and sometimes showing the position of the sacred stone sunk in the ground when the homestead was begun; place where any sacrifice or propitiatory offering is made, e.g. the bank of a river where the crossing presents danger or the open plain where wild animals abound; see igongona; cf. ihakĩro. 2. name of a plant; wa ~, sacred tree esp. the wild fig used in sacrificial ritual; thinga ~, rub the sex organ on sth.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: call sb. persistently by name (without active result), urge sb. to respond
FULL_ENTRY: gongora, v.t. (1), call sb. persistently by name (without active result), urge sb. to respond; o na mũgongore ndenyuria, beseech him as you may he will not stir. ĩgongora, refl. be self-important, bossy. gongoragongora, dim. gongoranga, diff. gongorania, caus. rec. gongoranira, app. rec. gongorithia, d. caus. gongoreka, st. pot.; o na ũngĩraara ũkimũgongora ndangĩ~, even if you call him the whole night long, he will not respond. gongorera, app. beseech sb. who will not respond for or at; kũng ongorera hingo ũigana atia?, how much longer are you going to go on calling me?
mũgongorũo / mũgongorũa, a-, n. (iv), sb. who is too obstinate to respond when called, a high-andmighty person. [gongora]

NEW_VALUE: of condition of being bent, twisted but not broken apart (with to a saw, a crippled foot, a sharp bend in a road, a court case, &c
FULL_ENTRY: gonyo, ideo. (1/11), descr. of condition of being bent, twisted but not broken apart (with ref. to a saw, a crippled foot, a sharp bend in a road, a court case, &c.). [gonya]

NEW_VALUE: of a bend or twist made worse by attempts to straighten it out
FULL_ENTRY: gonyokanũ, ideo. (11), descr. of a bend or twist made worse by attempts to straighten it out. [gonya]

gooca, v.t. (1), 1. praise, extol, eulogize; cf. kumia, gaatha. 2. address, call to, sb. persistently using honorific titles in order to get a response. ĩgooca, refl. boast, be conceited, have a high opinion of oneself; cf. goocagooca, dim. goocanga, diff. goocana, rec. goocania, caus. rec. goocanĩra, app. rec. goocaniria, caus. app. rec. praise (people) at the same time. goocithia, d. caus. igoocithia, refl. d. caus. gooceka, st. pot. goocera, app. ĩgoocera, refl. app.

NEW_VALUE: toro wa ~, an apology for a sleep, light fitful sleep, doze
FULL_ENTRY: igooce, (ma-), n. (VII); toro wa ~, an apology for a sleep, light fitful sleep, doze; ringwo nĩ ~, become sleepy (as when sitting by a fire), doze, be bleareyed from sleepiness. [gooca]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: r. act of praising, giving honour, glorifying
NEW_VALUE: act of praising, giving honour, glorifying
FULL_ENTRY: ũgooci, n. cl. 14 only (1), r. act of praising, giving honour, glorifying; praise, flattery. 2. style, quality ; praisename, [gooca]

rũgooco, (ngooco); ũgooco, cl. 14 only; n. (1), 1. repeated mention of sb.’s name; praise, laudation, flattery; -a ~, praiseworthy. 2. repeated calling of sb. until he chooses to respond. 3. a marvel; horo wa ~, an astounding affair; cf. [gooca]
igooti?, ma-, n. (1), coat. [E.]
kĩgooto, see under ngooto.
mũgoro, mi-, n. (1), a shrub (Halleria | lucida).
kagoro, see under ngware.,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: part of the intestine of a ruminant, passage from stomach to intestines.
NEW_VALUE: part of the intestine of a ruminant, passage from stomach to intestines
FULL_ENTRY: mũgorogoru, mi-, n. (v), part of the intestine of a ruminant, passage from stomach to intestines.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. dewlap, folds of skin under a sheep’s neck. 2. wattles of cock or turkey. 3. stupid, weak-witted person. 4. a name for the buibul
NEW_VALUE: dewlap, folds of skin under a sheep’s neck
FULL_ENTRY: kĩygorogoru / kĩgoragoru, i-, n. (v), I1. dewlap, folds of skin under a sheep’s neck. 2. wattles of cock or turkey. 3. stupid, weak-witted person. 4. a name for the buibul; see gĩcogoru (gĩcorogu); cf. gĩthnkũ.
mũgoromoko, mi-, n. (1), grunt, roar, growl. [goromoka]

NEW_VALUE: feel jealous, be stirred, concerned, troubled about
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgoru, n. (111); igua ~, feel jealous, be stirred, concerned, troubled about.
mũgoruma [mũgooruma], mi-, n. (vur), 1. big, inactive, man; sterile man; cf. 2. cia ~, watery potatoes that cannot be cooked.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. cl. 14|(6) (vur), 1. hard, black patch ir potato where tuber has been infected by worm or disease
NEW_VALUE: hard, black patch ir potato where tuber has been infected by worm or disease
FULL_ENTRY: gorũe [goorwe] / goroe, (ma-), n. cl. 14|(6) (vur), 1. hard, black patch ir potato where tuber has been infected by worm or disease; infected patch or streak in sugar-cane.2. canker,blemish, fault; miikarĩre yake ndĩrĩ ~, his conduct is beyond reproach. kagorũe, n. thutha wa ~, very early morning (while it is still dark).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of the sound of sth. breaking off violently from a joint
FULL_ENTRY: goto, ideo. (11/1), descr. of the sound of sth. breaking off violently from a joint; gamba ~, go snap. [see gotora] mi-, n. (1), 1. strip of dry banana bark. 2. ceremonial obstetric binder; foha ~, feel very hungry; cf. mathiori. igoto, ma-, n. 1. strip of banana bark used for roof and walls of temporary buildings, tying up snuff, bandaging, &c.); see irigũ. 2. -a ~, sth. small; gacai ka ~, a tiny speck of tea. ngoto, ~, 1. green flake of stem of banana palm. 2. encasing leaf, sheath, of flower and fruit bud; see kĩongoro. kagoto!, tũ-, n. a small bit of the bark of dry banana palm; a small bit of anything; cf. kohoya.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. small broken stone for making concrete or building
NEW_VALUE: small broken stone for making concrete or building
FULL_ENTRY: kagoto?, tũ-; kogoto, ~~; n. (1), 1. small broken stone for making concrete or building; concrete; cf., ngogoto. 2. (coll.) galore; andũ ~, masses of people; irto ~, lashings of food. [cf. Sw. KOKOTO]
mũgotha, see mŨgũtha.

NEW_VALUE: in a hurry (to buy a wife)
FULL_ENTRY: gothagotha, v.i. do hastily, hurriedly. gothagothania, caus. rec. do hastily in a slapdash manner, prepare precipitately to fight; collect (goats) in a hurry (to buy a wife); put up (a house, hut) in a hurry.
igothe, ma-; ũgothe, cl. 14 only; n. (i), anything remarkable, wonderful. Kahĩ nĩ magothe, a boy is sth. wonderful. Cf.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: brace oneself up, summon all one’s strength.
NEW_VALUE: brace oneself up, summon all one’s strength
FULL_ENTRY: ĩgothia, v. refl. (1), brace oneself up, summon all one’s strength.
kĩgotho, see ngotho.
gothũ, see under thũ.

goya, v.i. (1), droop, bow; bend over, down; tilt, swing about in a drooping position, sway, toss up and down; (of cock’s tail, ostrich plume, bamboos, palm fronds, &c.) nod; (of grass in flower) be bowed down; (of sick person) be weak, limp; ~ ta gĩkombi (of a shy person) hang head (like a ripe millet head); nĩnũgoe ta gĩkombi, (of men in the dance after drawing swords and twirling them in unison) let the dancers bow (like the ripe millet head). goyagoya, dim. swing about in a drooping position. goyanga, diff. goyania, caus. rec. goyanira, assoc. goyanĩria, caus. assoc. goiya, caus. 1. ~ Qhoro kahora, deal with a matter circumspectly. 2. ~ nyaga, make ostrich feathers dance on the head. 3. make (people, &c.) limp and weak. ĩgoiya, refl. caus. be bashful, abashed, coy; (of a girl when talking to future husband) hang the head. goithia, d. caus. goeka, st. pot., goera, app. goeria, caus. app. rĩgoeria, refi. caus. app. goerera, inten. droop, hang over, be bent downward; mũgoiyo nĩ Ũgoerere, let the dance be livened up. goereria, caus. inten. bend down gradually, carefully.
igua [iguwa], ma-, n. (1), 1. honeycomb; cf. yrana. 2. (cl. 6) honey cells; a section of comb. kagua, tũ-, n, small piece of honeycomb.
guagũka, see under
guamũka, v.i. (1), be consoled, comforted.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: get soaked, become soft after soaking.
NEW_VALUE: get soaked, become soft after soaking
FULL_ENTRY: guamba, v.i. (11), get soaked, become soft after soaking.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. a detachment of raiders, ‘smash and grab’ raid
NEW_VALUE: a detachment of raiders, ‘smash and grab’ raid
FULL_ENTRY: guamba, ma-, n. cl. 14/6 (vui), 1. a detachment of raiders, ‘smash and grab’ raid; skirmish, small-scale raid. 2. an expert thief.
maguandata, n. cl. 6 (1v), (N.Kk.) (arch.) strength; cf. mandacũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cow with a large white patch extending from the belly to the ridge of the back, on one side only.
FULL_ENTRY: guathi, ~, n. (11), cow with a large white patch extending from the belly to the ridge of the back, on one side only. kĩguathi, i-, n. usu. with cl. oj1o concords, ox of the same colour.
-guayu / -guau [guawu], a. (1v), indisposed. [guaya]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. (rare use) doubt, hesitation, caused by consideration of circumstances or possible consequences
NEW_VALUE: (rare use) doubt, hesitation, caused by consideration of circumstances or possible consequences
FULL_ENTRY: guca [guuca], ~, n. (1), I. (rare use) doubt, hesitation, caused by consideration of circumstances or possible consequences; ũcio ndari ~, that man never wavers (when once embarked on a line of action). Gũtirĩ kĩndũ kĩagaga ~ hana, there is nothing completely flawless. 2. (as conj.) were it not that, but for the fact that, unless, except; ~ woka, had you not come; cf. anga, korũo.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. shrub. 2. timber-tree growing at high altitudes (Faurea saligna)
FULL_ENTRY: mũgucai, mĩ-, n, (v1), 1. shrub. 2. timber-tree growing at high altitudes (Faurea saligna); cf.

gucia [guucia], v.t. (1), 1. pull, draw, draw (wire); ~ thigara, take a puff at a cigarette. 2. entice, attract. gucio, i pass. ; ~ nĩ, be impressed by, interested in. refl. slow down when doing sth., be half-hearted; cf. ruria. gucagucia, dim. pull a little more, draw tighter. gucangia, diff. gucania, rec. I1. pull others, each other; join hands with others in an enterprise (usu. undesirable), fall in with bad company. 2. attract people. gucanĩria, caus. app. pull (things) together, pull sth. together with sth. else; tussle with sb. for; have a tug of war at. gucanirira, inten. rec. shrink up; draw, shrink, together; shrivel. gucanĩriria, caus. inten. rec, interest, encourage each other to do sth., make people interested in sth.; maya no maũndũ ma kũ~, these are merely attractions. gucithia, d. caus, assist sb. to pull, make sb., sth., pull. gucika, st. pot. gucĩria, app. pull, draw, at, for. guciriria, inten. r. draw tight, tighten, draw hard. 2, entice, attract. gucũra, conv. stretch out; make expansive. gucũrũria, caus, inten. conv. draw out, pull, forcibly; drag along,, tug. gucũrũrũka, neut. inten. conv. I. be, become, stretched out; become elongated (by pulling). 2. come uncoiled. gucũarũrũkia, caus. neut. inten. conv. pull, stretch, out; tug and draw out forcibly. gucũka, neut. be, become, stretched out, expand; be spread out, expansive.
rũgucio [rũguucio], ngucio, n. (1), I. wire (esp. very fine wire). 2. (cl. 10 only) wire netting. [gucia]
igucu, ma- ; ngucu, ~; n. (IV), 1. kite, raven, crow; cf. ihuru, ĩgogo. 2. gluttonous person. 3. elder’s skin bag for carrying meat.
mũgucu, see mŨgfoco.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sth. long like a stick, that makes a whirring noise as it hurtles through the air
NEW_VALUE: long like a stick, that makes a whirring noise as it hurtles through the air
FULL_ENTRY: rũgucugucu, ngucugucu, n. (v), sth. long like a stick, that makes a whirring noise as it hurtles through the air; ikia ngucugucu, fling sth. (like a club) that turns over and over, hurl; cf. gucugucu; see [see cugucia]
gucugucu?, ideo. (1), descr. of hurtling (e.g. a club turning over and over).
guguha (N.K.), see huhuga (hubuya).
gugui / guguiya, see gui.
guguma, v.i. (11), (of cat, motor-car engine) purr.
iguguma, see
(ka-) (kĩ) gui, see under ngui,
(ka-) (ki-) guiya, see under ngui.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. dress, clothe, cover over with a cloak or garment
NEW_VALUE: dress, clothe, cover over with a cloak or garment
FULL_ENTRY: guika, v.t. (1), 1. dress, clothe, cover over with a cloak or garment; cf. humba. 2. (of a man) have simulated sexual intercourse with a woman; see nguiko; cf. thiga. guika, refi, dress; Oaa mm ST -ro E dim. guĩkanga, diff. guĩkana, rec. guĩkania, caus. rec., clothe haphazardly. guĩkanĩra, app. rec. guikanĩria, caus. app. rec. clothe, cover, (things) at the same time. guikithia, d. caus. guikika, st. pot. guikira, app. ,
guĩma, v.t. (11), hunt wild game.
mũguimi, a-, n. (111), hunter. [guĩma]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (used in sarcastic or ironical expressions) so, so then
FULL_ENTRY: guku [guuku] / kuku [kuuku], interj. (U), (used in sarcastic or ironical expressions) so, so then; atĩ ~ iri mwanake ma, so you are really a young man, are you?; ~ n? mũgũnda ũrĩmĩte mĩtĩ-inĩ!, so you have made a garden near the trees (what a stupid thing to do)!; cf. kukuiya. gukuguku / kuku-kuku, inten. of above; ~ don’t go imagining you are a young lady yet!; cf. cucu-cucu.
kaguku?, see wakaguku,

mũguku [mũguuku], mĩ-, n. (1), 1. anything abnormally large; a strong shoot of sugar-cane fit for replanting; cf. muonjoro. 2. (N.K.) long yam root; any long root or runner; cf. muun gunũ. [see gukuma]
gukuirĩra, see gukumirira,
Kariũki; cf. tonyania. gukumanira, w Ren Mpa ep i m pad jodi pd O a

oa pae H A Do O 2 Aa. de ULICI, Pul in under (an archway, bar, &c.). 2. bypass a previous decision. gukumithia, d. caus. gukumika, st. pot. gukumira, app. crawl under, at, for. gukumiria, caus. app. gukumirira j gukuirĩra, inten. bend low in order to go through sth. gukumiriria, app. inten. gukumũra, conv. straighten up. gukumũka, neut. come out from a hiding-place, straighten up, come out into the open.
igukumiro [iguukumiro], ma-, n. (G1), entrance. [gukuma]
kĩgukumo [kĩguukumo], i-, n. (1), a low entrance, tunnel. [gukuma]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: knot, flaw (in a piece of wood, &c.).
NEW_VALUE: knot, flaw (in a piece of wood, &c
FULL_ENTRY: kĩgukunyu, i-; igukunyu, ma-; n. (ix), knot, flaw (in a piece of wood, &c.). Thaka ndiagaga ~, there’s no fop without some flaw.
mũgumucano, mi-, n. (1), large timber-tree (Mimusops usambarensis).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. race of diminutive people believed to have been former inhabitants of Kikuyu country, dwelling in holes in the ground covered by a rough shelter
NEW_VALUE: race of diminutive people believed to have been former inhabitants of Kikuyu country, dwelling in holes in the ground covered by a rough shelter
FULL_ENTRY: Gumba, n. cl. 14 only (1), 1. race of diminutive people believed to have been former inhabitants of Kikuyu country, dwelling in holes in the ground covered by a rough shelter; cf. maitha-a-ciana. 2. name of an early major age-set, belonging to the time when the Agikũyũ met these people. Mũgumba, A-, n. one of the Gumba people.
kĩgumba, i-, n. (1) mean, stingy, person.

guna, v.t. (1), benefit, profit; promote the well-being of; succour, save, deliver from harm or in need. Gĩti~xga it does not benefit one (who does not understand) to have everything translated for him. gunwo, pass.; twa~ ni ihenya, we were saved by running. refl. benefit, profit, oneself; do to one’s own advantage; save oneself. gunaguna, dim. gunanga, diff. gunana, rec. gunania, caus. rec. gunanira, app. rec. gunaniria, caus. app. rec. gunia, caus, make benefit from, benefit with. gunithia, d. caus. gunika, st. pot. be able to benefit; obtain benefit; gain; tũtingĩ~ nĩ Ũmenyo Ũtari na ciko, we cannot be benefited by sheer knowledge without actions. gunikira, app. st. pot. benefit at, for. gunira, app. benefit because of; be of benefit to sth. at; save, at, for. mi-, n. (1), 1. tree; cf. 2. nickname for European potatoes; cf. waru. [guna] mi-, n. (1-1), a tree. [guna]
ũgunani, mogunani [moogunani], n. (1), mutual profit, advantage. [guna]
-guni, a. (1), profitable. [guna]
mũguno, see under kiguni.

gunda, v.i. (1), be discoloured, stained; (of calabashes} rot; (of metals, &c.) be mildewed, blackened, dulled; (of people) be cast down, depressed ; cf. gaca, rota?. gundagunda, dim. gundanga, diff. gundana, rec. become stained entirely. gundanira, app. rec. gundaniria, caus. app. rec. gundia, caus. cause to be discoloured. gundithia, d. caus. gundika, st. pot. gundira, app. gundiria, caus. app. gundũra, conv. gundũria, caus. conv. I1. freshen, cheer up. 2. get sth. out of a place where it is hidden; unearth, expose; cf. rindũria, umbũrũria. gundũrĩria, d. caus. conv. gundũka, neut. gundara, stc. become dull.
mũgunda, a-, n. (11), churlish person. [gunda]
gungura, see ng’ ung’ura.

gunya?, v.t. (11), I1. nibble, gnaw; (of a worm, bug) bore into sth. 2. throb, nag, irritate. 3. (of a small creature under one’s foot) stir, wriggle (when suppressed); cf. genya. gunyagunya, dim. (of small insects, lice) keep on nibbling; stir, make a suppressed movement under sth.; (of a rat in a dark corner) make continuous nibbling sounds; pilfer; (of an old woman trying to cultivate her field) pick at feebly. gunyagunyana, rec. dim. throb, nag, irritate. gunyagunyania, caus. rec. dim. stir, fidget; cause a suppressed sound ; fidget with ; cf. barabaria. Tgunyagunyania, refi. rec. dim. gunyagunyanira, app. rec. dim. gunyagunyaniria, caus. app. rec. dim. gunyagunyia, caus. dim. ĩgunyagunyia, refl. caus. dim. gunyagunyithia, d. caus. dim. gunyagunyika, st. pot. dim. be restless, fidgety. gunyagunyikia, caus. st. pot. dim. gunyanga, diff. gunyana, refi. gunyania, caus. rec. gunyanira, app. rec. gunyaniria, caus. app. rec. gunyia, caus. gunyithia, d. caus. gunyĩra, app. gunyĩria, caus. app. gunyũra, conv. gunyũria, caus. Conv.
mũgunyagunu, mĩ-, n. (v), shrub (Capparis lilacina).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sp. of black, hairy, stinging caterpillar
FULL_ENTRY: kigunyarĩrĩ [kĩgunyaarĩrĩ], i-, n. (1), sp. of black, hairy, stinging caterpillar; cf. kĩgunyũ.

gunyeka [gunyeeka] / gumeka [gumeeka], v.i. (1) become soft, pappy, through soaking; become musty, stale, damp; ferment; cf. guma, gunya. gunyagunyeka, dim. gunyekanga, diff. gunyekanira, assoc. gunyekanĩria, caus. assoc., make (things) soft, cause to ferment, at the same time. gunyekia, caus. cause to ferment, soften; make soft and moist. gunyekithia, d. caus. gunyekeka, st. pot. gunyekera, app. soft, moist, for, at. gunyekeria, caus. app. soak, soften, for, at.
maũgunyio, mĩ-, n. (1), sth. earmarked, reserved; iga ~, earmark (as beast for slaughter, boy for punishment, &c.); cf. mahohio. [gunyia]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of suppressed wriggling, nibbling movements or sounds, &c.
NEW_VALUE: of suppressed wriggling, nibbling movements or sounds, &c
FULL_ENTRY: gunyu / gunyũ, ideo. NEW_VALUE: get dry after being drenched to the skin, get warm after feeling bitterly cold, cease being tough and hard
FULL_ENTRY: gũma, v.t. & i. (1), I. dominate, be hard on, have overbearing power over sth. or sb. 2. become hard, shrunk, stunted; be hardy, wellknit; be stiff with cold. refl. apply oneself over-confidently to, tackle sth. too big, presume to deal with sth.; ~ na ũndũ, attempt what is beyond one’s capacity. gũmagũma, dim. gũmanga, diff. gũmanira, app. rec. & assoc. I. join forces to dominate sth., dominate each other in turn, be too much for others. 2. become stunted, &c., together. gũmaniria, caus. app. rec. (assoc.) I. dominate (others) at the same time. 2. make stunted, &c., at the same time. ĩgũmanĩria, refi. app. rec. set oneself to overcome a difficult situation. gũmia, caus. spoil, ravage; cf. gũmbia. gũmithia, d. caus. cause subjugation of. refl. d. caus. make as if a situation was too much for one; cause oneself to be dominated, subjugated. gũnũmĩka, st. pot. gũmira, app. I. be too much for sb. for, at. 2. be stunted, &c., at, for. ĩgũmira, refl. app. set oneself to overcome sth., strain oneself, make intense effort, tax one’s strength, attempt what is beyond one’s power. Mũndũ wĩ na ithe cira, a man with a father does not have to strain himself in a court case. Cf. ĩrutaniria. gũmũra, conv. make grow after being retarded, straighten after being shrunk, make warm and dry after being cold and wet; give a beating to (oxhide, carpet, blanket, &c.) to remove creases and dust; beat up (a person, animal). pass. conv.; hĩndi ya heho no njoka igacoka nĩ rina, during the cold season snakes hibernate until the sun puts life into them again. gũmũka, neut. 1. get dry after being drenched to the skin, get warm after feeling bitterly cold, cease being tough and hard; (of a retarded crop) begin to grow again. 2. cease to be petrified (with cold, fear, constraint, self-consciousness, diffidence); speak freely, without restraint; be bold, forward (as women taking part in a men’s discussion). gũmara, stc. be dried up, stiff, hard; be stiff with cold, fright, fear.

mũhaci, mi-, n. (1), (N.K.) twisted stick; mĩrĩnga ya ~, hoops of twisted mũkenia sticks (worn round the neck by neophytes at a certain stage before initiation, also thrown by girl neophytes into a hollow in the ceremonial mũgumo tree during the rites on the day before circumcision). [haca]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: curved ornament of rhino or buffalo hide, horn or ivory, shaped like a double wish-bone, worn by men on the upper left arm.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (a vigorous expr. of protest, consternation, depreciation) really, is that so?, fancy that! 2. ~? ~/, (N.K.) stop!, hold!

haga, v.t. & i. hagaga, see agaga.

NEW_VALUE: be whetted, become sharpened by rubbing on or with a stone
FULL_ENTRY: hagara, v.i. (11), be whetted, become sharpened by rubbing on or with a stone. hagahagara, dim. hagahagaria, caus. dim. whet, sharpen, a little more. hagaranga, diff. hagarania, caus. rec. (as in sharpening) rub (two or more knives) together, one on the other; ~ magego, gnash one’s teeth. hagaranira, assoc. hagaranĩria, caus. assoc. hagaria, caus. whet, sharpen, (a knife, &c.) by rubbing on stone, or with another knife; cf. noora.

hagaya, v.i. (11), haye a craving for sth.; be ill at ease, be apprehensive. hagahagaya, dim. hagayanga, diff. hagayanĩra, assoc. hagayaniria, caus, assoc. hagayia, caus. hagayithia, d. caus. hagayĩka, st. pot. hagayira, app.
mũhagayo, mĩ-, n. (11). greed, craving, inordinate desire; cf. thuti, mũhaha, njeme. [hagaya]

(N.K.) as haha. 2. snatch at other people’s things; clasp in arms, seize and hold fast; be grasping, avaricious.
haha, see under ha-.

FULL_ENTRY: hahaata, v.i. (11), feel, feel about with hands or feet, search blindly, grope. hahahahaata, dim. hahaatanga, dif. hahaatanira, assoc. hahaataniria, caus. assoc. hahaatithia, d. caus. hahaatika, st. pot. hahaatira, app. hahaatĩria, caus. app. 1. grope about after, search for, sth. in the dark by feeling with hands; ~ njira, feel way with feet and hands at night; cf. hambata, caria, hutia. 2. grope for (in the mind), be vague about, surmise.

mũhahami, a-, n. (111), acquisitive person; one who is grasping, avaricious. Ũhahami, n. cl. 14, avarice, oppression, rapacity. Ndũngitongio ni ~, you cannot really get rich by encroaching on other people’s property. Cf. ahahanyi. [hahama]
-hahami, a. (1), grabbing, encroaching. [hahama]

hahanya, v.t. (111), grasping, extortionate person; robber, cheat; cf. kĩhahanyi, i-, n. aug. of above. ũhahanyi, n. cl. 14, extortion; rapacity ; cf. [hahanya]

FULL_ENTRY: hahata, v.t. mũhahi [mũhaahi], mĩ-, n. (11), gossip, scandal; wa ~, gossipmonger; cf. mũceneneko, ũhahi, n. cl. 14 only, inquisitiveness; kara ~, be inquisitive, be a ‘nosy parker’. [hahika]
hahũ, ~, n. (11), nervous apprehension, panic, trepidation, funk. [hahũka]
mahahũra-igũta, n. cl. 6 only (1-11), short, light shower of rain. [hahũra]

NEW_VALUE: a slope up, upgrade, hill (on a road, &c

haiya!, interj. (U), expr. surprise, satisfaction, agreement.
haiya?, see haya?.
hai, ~, n. (1) High School, senior Secondary [E.]

haka!, v.t. ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. boundary. 2. used as conj. foll. by subj. (1) preceded by no
FULL_ENTRY: mũhaka, mĩ-, n. (11), 1. boundary. 2. used as conj. foll. by subj. (1) preceded by no; no ~ there is nothing for it but for you to go, you should go (2) until; ~ riii, until you reach the river. [haka?]

NEW_VALUE: blood left on the trail of a wounded animal
FULL_ENTRY: ihaki, ma-, n. (1v), offering, bribe, sacrifice; ya ~, goat paid by recently initiated youth to youths of earlier age-sets prior to admission to dances; ~ rĩa njino, burnt-offering or sacrifice; ~ rĩa gũthambania, fee payable to female officials at the ceremonial washing in the Masai ritual. ũhaki, n. cl. 14, blood left on the trail of a wounded animal. FULL_ENTRY: nũhaki, a-, n. (11); ~ wa rangi, a painter. kĩhaki, i-, n. paint brush; cf. kahaki, tũ-, n. dim. of above.

(1), 1. present for appeasing. 2. besmearing; nũki wa ~, wild honey used to besmear those taking part in ceremonial transactions with the Ndorobo; mbũri cia ~, goats levied from newly initiated set by a senior set for concession of certain privileges. [haka!, 2] v.t, (11), scoop up (earth, dirt, &c. with a shovel or the hands). hakahakũra, dim. hakũranga, diff. hakũrania, caus. rec. hakũranira, app. rec. hbakũraniria, caus. rec. app. hakũrithia, d. caus. hakũrika, st. pot. hakũrĩra, app. hakũririra, inten. hakũka, neut.
-bakũre, a. (Iv), castrated, gelded. [hakũra?]

ihakwe / ihokwe, ma-, n. (v11), high forest, dense wood, thicket.
hama, see hema.

hanini, see hanini.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: see kĩbococo. 2. insert in the ground, set up, stand on end
NEW_VALUE: stand firm, establish oneself, take one’s stand
FULL_ENTRY: handa, v.t. (1) x. plant (seeds); ~ kĩbococo (gĩthomboco), see kĩbococo. 2. insert in the ground, set up, stand on end; ~ itimũ, stick a spear in the ground; ~ gĩkĩngĩ, put in a peg; ~ njoya, stick feathers (in the hair, &c.); e ikũmbĩ, set up a grain-store; ~ nyĩrĩ, stand on one’s head; ~ put down a marking string; ~ njohi, set sugar-cane or honey-beer to ferment; ~ ndĩrĩ, set up a mortar ready for pounding (maize, millet, &c.); ~ njacĩ, nguthi, (esp. of women and children) behave cheekily (towards their elders and betters); ~ mbatha, see mbatha. refl. 1. stand firm, establish oneself, take one’s stand. 2. stand arrogantly, aimlessly, hopelessly. 3. rina rĩehanda, when the sun is well overhead, towards midday ; see mĩaraho. handahanda, dim. ĩhandahanda, refl. dim. handanga, diff. handana, rec. handania, caus. rec. handanira, app. rec. handanira, refl. app. rec. stand up to each other (in opposition or fight). handanirira, inten. rec. fhandanirira, refi. inten. rec. ; tiga kw~ na nyũkwa, stop quarrelling with your brother. handaniria, caus. app. rec. handithia, d. caus. handika, st. pot. app.; ~ gĩtatĩ, see gĩtatĩ?. handirira, inten. r. stick (flowers, &c.) in (a vase). 2. plant, alongside of, among. ĩfhandirira, refl. inten. set oneself against, take up one’s stand for a fight; stand close up against sb. else. handũra, conv. uproot, pull up, out of the ground; ~ mũtokaa, get a car moving. handũka, neut.
handi, ~, n. (1), (N.K.) small grasshopper; cf. ndaahi.
mũhandano, (mi-), n. (XI), -a ~, newly planted, recently established. [handa]

mũhbando, mi-, n. (1), 1. planting season; ngwaci cia ~, fruits of a recent planting of potatoes, new potatoes; airĩtu a ~, recently initiated girls; cf. kĩmera. 2. stack; ara ngũ ~, stack up firewood ; cf. kĩhayo, kĩbaca. 3. tetherpost to which animal is tethered, post with forks on which bundles of green fodder are hung for the livestock to eat. 4. ~ wa ikũmbĩ, corner pole of grain-store; cf. mũgamba, mwaũro. [handa]
hangaika, see angaika.

hangereta, see hangarata,

(with ref. to lance thrown in ceremony at mũgumo tree before initiation) lodge in branches. hangahangira, dim. hangĩranga, diff. hangĩrana, rec. hangĩrania, caus. rec. hangiranira, app. rec. hangiraniria, caus. rec, app. hangĩrithia, d. caus. hangirika, st. pot. hangirĩra, app. hangiririra, inten. hangũra, conv. hangũka, neut. r. (of handle of machine, tool, &c.) become loose. 2. (of animals) be gluttonous, wander away from the herd; cf. hũngũtũka.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. small wedge driven in to secure an axe-head or hoe
NEW_VALUE: small wedge driven in to secure an axe-head or hoe
FULL_ENTRY: kĩhangiro, i-, n. (11), 1. small wedge driven in to secure an axe-head or hoe; cf. gĩkwĩro. 2. end of sweet potato cutting used for propagation (which is buried in soil). [hangĩra]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: r. haft, handle. 2. wooden club used to strike branches from the wild fig-tree at the time of the initiation ceremony
FULL_ENTRY: kĩhango, i-, n. (11), r. haft, handle. 2. wooden club used to strike branches from the wild fig-tree at the time of the initiation ceremony; cf. kĩbeco. 3any bat used in a game, tennis racket, cricket bat. [see hangira]
(i-) (kĩ-) hanguru (N.x.), see kĩhunguru.

NEW_VALUE: be busy with many things to do or attend to, bustle about
FULL_ENTRY: hang’a, v.i. (1), 1. be busy with many things to do or attend to, bustle about; cf. hũga. 2. be raised, dangle, hang in the air, be cocked up and hang unsupported; cf. ambarara. hang’ahang’a, dim. hang’ahang’ania, caus. rec. dim. set pace for each other; cf. enyerania, teng’erania, hanyũkania. hang’ahang’ia, caus. dim. chase, chase about, make run to and fro. hang'anga, diff. hang’ana, rec, hang’ania, caus. rec.; ~ na, busy oneself with. hang’anĩra, app. rec. 1. bustle about together with. 2. be cocked up, hang unsupported, at the same time as sth, else. hang’anĩria, caus. app. rec. hang’ia, caus. I. lift up, raise, tilt, cock; ~ njora, wear a sword tilted at an angle. 2. harass, hustle, make (goats, &c.) run aimlessly. hang’ithia, d. caus. hbang’ika, st. pot. hang’'ira, app. be busied with, busy oneself with, for, at. ihang’ira, refl. app. be busy with one’s own affairs, be about one’s own business. caus. app. hang’ũra, conv, 1. release, set free; let, pull, down sth. cocked up; lift a thing up by leverage and let it down again; lift up a person’s arm and hold it thus. 2, harass, worry, give a matter no rest. hang’ũrania, caus. rec. conv. overturn completely. hang’ũrria, caus, inten. hang’ũrũrania, caus. rec. inten. hang’ũka, neut. be dislodged, knocked, off, down. hang’ũkana, rec. neut. hang’arara, inten. stc. be raised up in the air. hang’araria, caus. inten. stc. raise and suspend in the air (as a mole, &c., caught in trap); ~ ngingo, crane the neck; cf. gucia rũkingo; ~ ciara, raise the toes (or fingers). ĩhang’araria, refl. caus. inten. stc. stand on tiptoe; rear up; cf.

NEW_VALUE: of being turned upside down, e

hanya, v.t. (11), scratch, lacerate with nails, claws, &c.; cf. hara, tarũra, tambura, kunya, hanyahanya, dim. hanyanga, diff. hanyana, rec. claw or scratch one another with fingernails, fight with tooth and claw. hanyania, caus. rec. hanyanira, app. rec. hanyaniria, caus. app. rec. hanyithia, d. caus. st. pot. hanyira, app. hanyũra, conv. scratch off, tear off. hanyũrũra, inten. conv. thanyũrũra, refl. inten. conv. hanyũka!, neut. become scratched or torn off.

FULL_ENTRY: hanya, ~, n. (1v), fork on branch or in a tree; cf. hwang’a. kihanya, i-, n. 1. multi-forked stick on which sweet potato vines are hung for stock; any similar forked pole or stick; ~ kĩa family altar (consecrated by. burying an earthen pot containing honey, oil, fat, and beer at its foot); see kĩgongona; ~ kĩa Ũrĩrĩ, post on which a woman hangs her cloak and certain persona] ornaments when retiring for the night. 2. pole with projections for climbing over fence, stile; rung of ladder; cf. kragararo. 3. forked upright stick for supporting one of four corners of grill for roasting meat over fre; cf. ndara. &-, n. (1), an impulsive, rash person (in act or speech). [hanya]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a chase, chasing of one another, pursuit.
NEW_VALUE: a chase, chasing of one another, pursuit
FULL_ENTRY: mũhanyũkanio, mĩ-, n. (1), a chase, chasing of one another, pursuit. [hanyũka]

NEW_VALUE: of scratching or being scratched, lacerated by claws or fingernails
FULL_ENTRY: hanyũrũru, ideo. (1), descr. of scratching or being scratched, lacerated by claws or fingernails. [hanya]

haral, v.t. (11), scratch, abrade, scrape; remove (the charred portion of roast food, &c.) by scraping with a knife or rubbing with a rough object; ~ rũga, remove hair from a skin; cf. kũra!, kũrũta. hara, refl. harahara, dim. 1. scrape (carrots, arrowroot, &c.) before washing and cooking. 2. ~ mbiro ya kĩenji, scrape off soot left by initiation knife (ref. to performance of ceremonial intercourse by newly initiated girls to remove miasma associated with sex act in marriage); cf. haka mũnyũ, te ngata, huura mbiro, haranga, diff. harana, rec. harania, caus. rec. haranĩra, app. rec. haranĩria, caus. app. rec. harithia, d. caus. refi. d. caus. scrape pretentiously, halfheartedly. harĩka, st. pot. harira, app. harĩrĩra, inten. scrape (some more) off into. harũra, conv. 1. lacerate, scratch, tear off, remove violently (the surface layer of sth.). 2. (of a torrent) tear up (ground). 3. (of wind) tear down, devastate, beat down crops. 4. (of fire) ravage. 5. make a loose bundle roll down; cf. garagaria, harũrũkia. harũrĩra, app. cony. I. empty into, pour solid contents from one container into another ; ~ trio kanua, pour food into the mouth; cf. onoreria. 2. overthrow. harũrũra, inten. conv. cause (a large mass of things, earth on a hillside, &c.)} to fall, slide, down, bring down in a mass. harũrũka, neut. conv. go, come, slide, fall, down from a high point, descend;
