kĩherithio / kĩherithanio, i-, n. (111), punishment, chastening. [hera]

heta, v.t. (1), shorten, tuck up (clothes); cut trim, set back (on the side); fold back; ~ nguo, raise dress or clothes (so as to be partly uncovered); ~ rũtere, clear bush round the boundary of a garden; ~ ũthiũ, frown, set one’s face against, scowl; cf. thithinaria ũthiũũ; ~ ngũ, set firewood aside; ~ mũndũ (kĩmba), bend legs and arms at burial; cf. kũnja, thuna. hetwo, pass. ndaregaga akĩ~, he who ignores advice does not resist when being prepared for burial. ĩheta, refl. double, fold, oneself up; shrink, back, away; avoid, dodge (by drawing back), withdraw, retreat, draw back (as a snail when touched); cf. ĩthuna, rkũnja, rhaga, ĩgita. hetaheta, dim. hetahetithia, dim. d. caus. hetanga, diff. hetana, rec. hetania, caus. rec. hetanĩra, app. rec., hetithia, d. caus. heteka, st. pot. hetera, app. heterera, inten. hetereria, caus. inten.; ~ mahuti rũtere-inĩ, heap up rubbish at edge of garden prior to burning. rhetereria, refl. caus. inten. hetũra!, conv. straighten out. hetũka, neut. theta, i-, n. (1), (N.K.) homestead in which there has been a death; cf. kĩhena-inĩ. kaheta, tũ-, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. ~inĩ, space against inside
Fo of hut wall; cf. kamwena-ini, gatura-ini. [heta]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: fat attached to sheep or goat’s skin next to the tail.
NEW_VALUE: fat attached to sheep or goat’s skin next to the tail
FULL_ENTRY: hetero, ~, n. (1), fat attached to sheep or goat’s skin next to the tail. [heta]
kĩheti'!, i-, n. (1) woman past childbearing; cf. mwongia. [heta]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: elephant spear (with weighted head, used by the early hunters, Aathi).
NEW_VALUE: elephant spear (with weighted head, used by the early hunters, Aathi)
FULL_ENTRY: iheti, ma-, n. (1), elephant spear (with weighted head, used by the early hunters, Aathi). kĩheti?, i-, n. Ndorobo dart.
kĩheto, i-, n. (1), doubled-up swordor spear-blade. [heta]

hetũra?, v.t. (11), cut down original stem (of sugar-cane, &c.). hetũranga, diff. hetũrania, caus. rec. hetũranira, app. rec. hetũraniria, caus. rec. app. hetũrithia, d. caus. hetũrika, st. pot. hetũrĩra, app.
hethũka (N.K.), see thethũka.
mahethũka (N.K.), see
hiana, see haana.
mũhianano, mi-, n. (11), likeness, image, replica. [hiana]
mũhiano | mũhiana / mũhianire, mi-, n. (111), appearance, resemblance. [hiana]
hianyũra, v.t. (1), break up new land. ideo. (1/11), descr. of digging, turning, up, the soil. [hianyũra]
kahiato | kaihato, n. (1); nyita ~, hold sb. tightly so as to render him helpless (unable to move).

NEW_VALUE: solid bone without a hole through it
FULL_ENTRY: hica / (N.K.) hita, ~, n. (v11), 1. solid bone without a hole through it. 2. lean, well-knit, sturdy, sinewy, person. 3. woman who miscarries or cannot bear child because of malformation of bones.

ihiga, ma-, n. (111), 1. rock, stone, piece of stone; lump of hard earth, dry mud; ~ gũkia, lower large grinding stone; ~ rra riiko, stone for cooking on, curbstone in men’s hut; ~ rĩa anvil; ~ rĩa thĩo, ũthĩro, upper, lower, grinding-stone; kanda ~, set up an altar; morimũ wa ~, tumour; cf. ihagĩ, ihai. 2. mbura ya mahiga, hail storm. 3. ~ rĩa nyaigi, flint; cf. ngũikũni, nyuranyura, ngomongo, thũi. higa, n. stretch of rocky or stony ground ; expanse of rock. kahiga, tũ-, n. small stone, piece of stone, pebble. kĩhiga, i-, n. 1. large, bulky stone, rock. 2. swollen gland; cf. bubo.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. peg, tent peg, bent stick used as peg
NEW_VALUE: peg, tent peg, bent stick used as peg
FULL_ENTRY: higi, ~, n. (1v), I1. peg, tent peg, bent stick used as peg; mũndũ wĩ na ~, a bow-legged person; cf. rambo, rũhwang’a.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of a trap) be set. higira, v.t. support, wedge up, make tight by inserting a peg or wedge. ĩhigira, refl. refuse to give way, be uncompromising, dig one’s heels in. higũra, conv. upset the balance (of a supported object), tilt, let down (with a jerk). fhigũra, refl. conv. (of a pot, &c.) tilt by itself. higahigũra, dim. conv. higũranga, diff. higũrana, rec. conv. higũrania, caus. rec. conv. higũranĩra, app. rec. conv. higũranĩria, caus. app. rec. conv. higũrithia, d. caus. conv. higũrika, st. pot. conv. higũrira, app. conv. higũka, neut. a pot or other supported object} lose support and jerk, tilt, or slip, tilt intentionally, become dislodged. 2. (of persons, animals, &c.) lose footing or balance and stagger hobble. 3. (of. banana-tree, &c.) lean, tilt, bend with a jerk when the prop is removed
FULL_ENTRY: higika, v.i. (11), (of a trap) be set. higira, v.t. support, wedge up, make tight by inserting a peg or wedge. ĩhigira, refl. refuse to give way, be uncompromising, dig one’s heels in. higũra, conv. upset the balance (of a supported object), tilt, let down (with a jerk). fhigũra, refl. conv. (of a pot, &c.) tilt by itself. higahigũra, dim. conv. higũranga, diff. higũrana, rec. conv. higũrania, caus. rec. conv. higũranĩra, app. rec. conv. higũranĩria, caus. app. rec. conv. higũrithia, d. caus. conv. higũrika, st. pot. conv. higũrira, app. conv. higũka, neut. a pot or other supported object} lose support and jerk, tilt, or slip, tilt intentionally, become dislodged. 2. (of persons, animals, &c.) lose footing or balance and stagger hobble. 3. (of. banana-tree, &c.) lean, tilt, bend with a jerk when the prop is removed; cf. inama, tirũka, tũgũga. higahigũka, dim. neut. higũkanga, diff. neut. higũkana, rec. neut. higũkania, caus. rec, neut. higũkanira, app. rec. neut. higũkaniria, caus. app. rec. neut. higũkia, caus. neut. higũkithia, d, caus. neut. higũkika, st. pot. neut.
kihigoigũ / krĩugũigũ, i-, n. (vui), nightjar.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of jerky movement. higũhigũ, ideo. descr. of
FULL_ENTRY: higũ, ideo. (11/1), descr. of jerky movement. higũhigũ, ideo. descr. of: 1. hobbling as sb. with lame leg. 2. mumbling efforts at chewing of one who has no teeth. [cf. higũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: squeeze liquid from sth. by twisting or pressing
NEW_VALUE: squeeze liquid from by twisting or pressing
FULL_ENTRY: hiha, v.t. (11), squeeze liquid from sth. by twisting or pressing; ~ nguo, wring clothes; ~ ndimũ, squeeze lemon (to obtain juice); ~ njohi, squeeze juice from soaked sugar-cane fibre or yeast sponge (in preparing liquor); ~ mahũri, (of a witch doctor) squeeze fear or cowardice out of sb.’s lungs; cf. hihinya, thiora, mimira. hihahiha, dim. hihanga, diff. hihana, rec. hihania, caus. rec. hihanira, app. rec. hihanĩria, caus. app. rec. hihithia, d. caus. hihĩka, st. pot. hihira, app. hihirira, inten. hihũra, conv. hihũka, neut.

NEW_VALUE: wringing, squeezing-out of crushed cane for beer-making
FULL_ENTRY: bihi, ~, n. (111), 1. cupping horn. 2. cut in skin to which a cup has been applied. ũhihi, n. cl. x4 only, wringing, squeezing-out of crushed cane for beer-making. [hiha]

hihinya, v.t. (11), press, squeeze; cf. totia, monda, hatĩka, tindĩka; see hinya. hihahihinya, dim. hihinyanga, diff. hihinyana, rec. press, squeeze, one another; be squeezed together, packed tight. hihinyania, caus. rec.; ~ magego, press the teeth together. rfhihinyania, refl. caus. rec. pull oneself together (for an effort), exert all one’s strength; cf. ũmĩriria, ĩrutaniria. hihinyanira, app. rec. press together, for, at the same time. hihinyanĩria, caus. app. rec. hihinyithia, d. caus.; ~ kũgũrũ thi, press the foot on the ground. hibinyika, st. pot. hihinyira, app. press in (a pin, &c.) for, at; ~ mGti, (in discussion) press, stress, emphasize a point; cf. hinyiria. hihinyiria, caus., app. hihinyũra, conv. 1. reduce pressure, let go; cf. regeria, ũkĩrĩria. 2. exert pressure on sth. over a wider area. hihinyũka, neut.
mũhihinyano, mĩ-, n. (111), overcrowding, congestion. [hihinya]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of a bride) get married, move to the husband’s home
FULL_ENTRY: hika, v.i. (1), (of a bride) get married, move to the husband’s home; ~ gĩkururio, be escorted to the husband’s home by his friends and relatives; ~ kwert, (of a twin sister on the marriage of the elder of the two) be ritually placed on the left-hand side of the mother’s hut to sleep until the end of the ceremonial visit of the sister on the eighth day after marriage; ~ kĩrĩu, get married in modern style (in church, registry office, &c.). hikania, caus, rec. (of a couple) get married; (of a man) marry. hikanira, assoc. I1. come to grips. 2. (of girls) get married at the same time. hikaniria, caus., assoc, ; ~ caci, marry in church. hikia, caus, (of bridegroom) marry, take a wife. hikio, pass. caus.; ~ nĩ, (of bride) be married to. hikithia, d. caus. hikithania, rec. d. caus. officiate at a wedding. hikika, st. pot. hikĩra, app. 1. join up with, accompany; cf. rũmirĩra, uma thutha. 2. (of a girl) cohabit with a man, become the wife of, get married to, at, into; cf. kĩrĩra. hikĩria, caus. app. hikũka, neut. cease to be a bride,
mũhikania, a-, n. (11), bridegroom. ũhikania, n. cl. 14 only, marriage, taking home of bride. [hika]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of man’s side and combined procedure) wedding, marriage.
NEW_VALUE: (of man’s side and combined procedure) wedding, marriage
FULL_ENTRY: kĩhikanio, i-, n. (1), (of man’s side and combined procedure) wedding, marriage. [hika]

mũhiki, a-, n. (1), 1. bride; name given to married woman for some time after marriage; cf. nyakĩnyua, maotumia. 2. player captured in hoop game. mohiki [moohiki), n. (of woman) wedding, marriage. [hika]
himbita [himbiita], v.t. (11), suck juice from (sugar-cane, &c.). himbitanga, diff. himbitanĩria, caus. rec. app. himbitithia, d. caus. himbitĩka, st. pot. himbitĩra app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: pause with a blank face, sit in sullen silence, hold the breath, hesitate (in speaking), be at a loss (to know what to do or say), come to a halt in speech
FULL_ENTRY: hinda, v.i. (1), pause with a blank face, sit in sullen silence, hold the breath, hesitate (in speaking), be at a loss (to know what to do or say), come to a halt in speech; cf. thindima, kinda, riga. hindahbinda, dim. hindanga, diff. hindana, rec. be mentally handicapped. hindania, caus. rec. drown sb. (with words). hindanira, assoc, hindanĩria, caus. assoc. hindika, st. pot. hindĩkana, rec. st. pot. I. become altogether silent, be silenced completely. 2. ~ iria-inĩ, sink in the water, be drowned; cf. cibĩkana. hindĩkania, caus. rec. st. pot. 1. confuse with words. 2. cause sb. to drown, drown. hindĩra, app. hindũra, conv. rouse, wake up, remind. hindũka, neut. wake up suddenly from sleep, become awake mentally, recall after an effort to remember; pause, hesitate. hindara, stc. 1. be speechless with anger; cf. gindaya. 2. be belated, be overdue. hindaria, caus. stc. hindarĩka, st. pot. stc. hindarĩra, app. stc.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: peg stuck in to prevent the end of a hive from falling out.
NEW_VALUE: peg stuck in to prevent the end of a hive from falling out
FULL_ENTRY: rũhindigo, hindigo, n. (x1), peg stuck in to prevent the end of a hive from falling out.
hindo, see hindi.

hinga, v.t. & i. (11), 1. shut, close; ~ mũrango, shut, fasten, a door; ~ nduka, close down a shop; ~ njira, close a path (physically or ritually); ~ horo, bring a matter to an end; ~ matũ, block the ears, refuse to listen; ~ nda, be the last born, see rigithatha, kĩhinga nda; ~ ng’i, fasten firmly, bolt (a window, &c.); ~ ngi, be absolutely silent, pretend not to know or understand what sb. else is saying; cf. tiiria, cokia mũrango, kunika, kuburia. 2. excommunicate, put out of fellowship, prohibit, put a ban on, outlaw. 3. be complete, fulfilled; come to an end; nĩmaa~, they have reached the expected number; cf. igana?. fhinga, refl. refrain, withhold oneself from doing sth.; put oneself under a ban, fast; ~ kũrĩa (irio), go fasting; cf. dim. hingahingika, st. pot. dim. hingahingira, app. dim. hingahingũra, conv. dim. open a little more. hingahingũka, neut. dim. hinganga, diff. hingana, rec. hingania, caus. rec. hinganira, app. rec. hinganĩria, caus. app. rec. hinganirĩria, czus. inten. rec. shut each other out or in. hingia, caus. complete; ~ ciringi, make up a shilling. hingithia, d. caus. 1. cause to be closed, shut; ~ ngi, fool sb. by pretending not to know sth. 2. make complete, bring up to the expected number; help to complete; ~ mĩrongo ya complete the tens of goats (the standard bridewealth payment ofninety goats). hingithĩria, app. d. caus. hingika, st. pot. hingira, app. shut, for, at, in, out, up; imprison. ĩhingira, refl. app. shut oneself in. hingiria, caus. app. complete payment for; ~ mohiki mbũri, complete the payment of bride-wealth. hingirira, inten. caus. inten. I1. keep back, block, bar; pen up, completely enclose, close in completely ; dam, stop the flow (of running water); ~ gĩkũ, perform ritual to prevent death. 2. -a last in a line or batch; cf. (-a}) mũthia, rigĩrĩria. rhingĩrĩria, refl. caus. inten. lock oneself in, out. hingũra, conv. open, unlock; ~ nyũmba, open a house ceremonially or otherwise; ~ cukuru, start a school; ~ gĩrthomo, begin a term; ~ mnhingo, lift a ban; cf. tiirũria, kunũra, tumũra. rhingũra, refl. conv. hingũranga, diff. conv. hingũrana, rec. conv. hingũrania, caus. rec. conv. hingũranira, app. rec. conv. hingũraniria, caus. app. rec. conv. hingũria, caus, conv. enter a woman’s residence peacefully or by force with the intention of making love. hingũrithia, d. caus. conv. fhingũrithia, refl. d. caus. conv, open pretentiously, reluctantly. hingũrithĩria, app. d. caus. conv. hingũrika, st. pot. conv. hingũrira, app. conv. open for, let loose. fhingũrira, ref. app. conv. hingũriranga / (hingũrangira), diff. app. conv. hinggka, neut. open, become open. hingũkana, rec, neut. hingũkanira, assoc. neut. hingakika, st. pot. neut. hingũkĩra, app. neut. hingara, stc. be completely shut, blocked (leaving no gap at all); (of a knife) be blunt; (of a person) be stupid, obtuse; ~ ngoro, be crabbed, taciturn, unhappy; cf. kingitara. hingaria, caus. stc. make complete. hingarĩka, st. pot. stc. hingarira, app. stc. hingarĩria, caus. app. stc.

NEW_VALUE: of bee (which arranges its cells transversely across the mouth of the hive)
FULL_ENTRY: hingal, ~, n. (1v), sp. of bee (which arranges its cells transversely across the mouth of the hive).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. hypocrite, dissembler, crafty person. 2. (with cl. 1/2 concords) a common personal name
FULL_ENTRY: hinga?, ~; mũhinga, &-; n. (1V), I. hypocrite, dissembler, crafty person. 2. (with cl. 1/2 concords) a common personal name; ~ wa an incorrigible liar. (mobinga) [moohinga], n. guile, hypocrisy, duplicity, cunning, craftiness, treachery. [hinga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a boy who is ostracized by his fellows.
NEW_VALUE: a boy who is ostracized by his fellows
FULL_ENTRY: kĩhinga?, i-, n. (1v), a boy who is ostracized by his fellows. [hinga]
bingirita, see hangarata.
mũhingiritia, a-, n. (1V), grasping, guileful, person; cf. moria-Ũhingiritia. Ũhingiritia, n. cl. 14 only, guile, rapacity.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. bar for closing a door or gateway. 2. closed period for circumcisions. 3. prohibition, ban, ostracization
NEW_VALUE: bar for closing a door or gateway
FULL_ENTRY: mũhingo, mi-, n. (11), 1. bar for closing a door or gateway. 2. closed period for circumcisions. 3. prohibition, ban, ostracization; cf. mũgiro. kĩhingo, i-, n. 1. short tunnel of strong tree-branches barricaded at night with long branches drawn, bushy ends facing outwards. 2. wooden bar for closing a hut doorway, gate, closable entrance way, enclosure ; cf. mũrango, moromo. 3. button; cf. gĩthungo, kĩbungo. [hinga]
-hingu, a. (1v), (primarily of numbers) complete. [hinga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of maize, millet, &c., in preparation of gruel) be finely ground
FULL_ENTRY: hinya, v.i. (11), 1. (of maize, millet, &c., in preparation of gruel) be finely ground; cf. thĩka, tanukĩka. 2. be weak; be reduced by illness. 3. (of a matter) be fully understood; cf. ithira, fhinya, refl. be self-sufficing; cf. rhota. hinyahinya, dim. hinyahinyia, caus. dim. grind a little more. hinyanga, diff. hinyania, caus. rec. bring pressure on, deal scrupulously with, one another. hinyanira, app. rec., hinyanĩria, caus. app. rec. refl. caus. inten. rec. pull oneself together; (of sb. having a tooth out, a woman in childbirth, &c.) brace oneself. hinyia, caus. 1. grind finely, pulverize; ~ irio, chew food thoroughly. 2. investigate thoroughly, thrash out, deal thoroughly with (a matter); ~ mũgũnda, weed, cultivate, patches left over in a garden; cf. ithĩria. 3. exert force on, press out. hinyangia, diff. caus. hinyithia, d. caus. hinyika, st. pot. hinyira, app. hinyiria, caus. app. hinyirira, inten. 1. lose strength, weaken progressively. 2. be obdurate, harden up. hinyirĩria, caus. inten. press bard against, into; oppress, suppress. hinyara, stc. be weak, thin, overburdened, worn down, reduced. hinyaria, caus., stc. weaken, wear down, reduce, hinyarithia, d. caus. stc.

hinya, ma-, n. cl. 14/6 (m1) 1. strength, power; ~ wa mũndũ semen, male powers of procreation; see mahira; mwanake wĩ ~, a strong youth; korĩ-~, a strong, forceful, arbitrary, pushing, violent person (robber or champion); cf. kĩhahinya. 2. difficulty; mũnda wa kĩ-~ [kĩ-~], a difficult, tough, violent person; Ũhoro ũcio wi ~, that matter is a difficult one; see iriga. kĩhinya [kĩhinyaj, n. cl. 7, great difficulty. ũhinya, n. cl. 14, force, violence, unyieldingness, stubbornness; mũndũ wa ~, dogmatic, obstinate person, one impervious to argument. wa ~ nĩ mwĩtigĩri kĩhooto, a person who relies on might is afraid of right. kahinya, n. cl. 12, a moderate degree of force, difficulty.
-hinyu, a. (1v), 1. fine, finely ground, powdery; mũtu mũ~, finely ground flour; cf. -ceke. 2. emaciated; mondũ mi~, an emaciated person; see hĩnja. [hinya]
mahira, n. cl. 6 (1v), 1. pus, matter from sore; mucus in stools; cf. kĩga, ngũci. 2. waters breaking in parturition; see hihira.
hirigita, see hangarata.

NEW_VALUE: of the sound of rustling, quivering, made by wind among dry maize-plants or among palm-fronds, &c
FULL_ENTRY: hiririri, ideo. (1), descr. of the sound of rustling, quivering, made by wind among dry maize-plants or among palm-fronds, &c.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: r1. hyena (mock hero of many tales, renowned for greed and stupidity
NEW_VALUE: hyena (mock hero of many tales, renowned for greed and stupidity
FULL_ENTRY: hiti / (N.xK.) mbiti, ~, n. (1v), r1. hyena (mock hero of many tales, renowned for greed and stupidity; a first-class scavenger and disposer of unburied dead; ceremonially unclean to kill); see kiuga mbu. 2. any unspecified carnivorous animal. hiti-u / hiti-uu, ~, n. the common spotted hyena.

hitha / (N.k.) thitha, v.t. (1), hide (from), conceal; ~ makinya, cover one’s tracks; cf. thiika, iga, rinda, umbĩka. fhitha, refl. hide oneself; nĩegũiw~, he is going to hide from us. hithahitha, dim. ĩhithahitha, refi. dim. hithahithĩra, app. dim. hithanga, diff. hithana, rec. hithania, caus. rec. fhithania, refl. caus. rec. hide oneself behind, between, in the midst of; be unobtrusive. hithanĩra, app. rec. hithaniria, caus. app. rec. hithia, caus. cause sb. to conceal; ~ mbũri, place goats in another person’s keeping (to ensure against raids, disease, evil eye, &c.); ~ mwana, have a child initiated in another homestead (when one cannot afford the ceremonies, or is not entitled to hold them; children thus initiated become initiatory brothers and sisters; cf. naũmba mwe). hithĩka, st. pot. hithĩkana, rec. st. pot. hithĩra, app. fhithĩra, refl. app. lie in wait for, ambush; cf. ohia. hithĩria, caus. app. hithĩrĩra, inten, hithĩrĩria, caus. inten. fhithĩrĩria, refi. caus. inten. keep a secret, to oneself, among selves.) hithũra, conv. hithũria, caus. conv. fetch (goats, &c.) from sb. who has been looking after them. hithũka, neut., come out of concealment, make attack; cf. tharĩkĩra. hithũkira, app. neut. make an attack on. hithara, stc. be concealed, remain completely concealed.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: dandelion (used as spinach). n. cl. 14 (11), heat
FULL_ENTRY: kihiũ, i-, n. (1), dandelion (used as spinach). n. cl. 14 (11), heat; fieryness of nature.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. livestock, possessions in form of ‘stock. 2. (cl. 6) flocks and herds
FULL_ENTRY: ũhiũg?, ma-, cl. n. 14/6 (111), 1. livestock, possessions in form of ‘stock. 2. (cl. 6) flocks and herds; mahi ma nyeki-ini, thistles; cf. gĩtemani.

-hiũ, a. (11), 1. hot; maĩ ma~, hot water; horo mi~, an urgent matter. 2. quick-tempered, hasty, impulsive; mũndũ a tempersome man. 3. fleet, quick; thiya ~, a fleet-footed buck; matu ma~, fleeting clouds. [see hiũha]

hiũha, v.i. (11), 1. become hot, warm; riũa rĩa~ wega, when the sun is pleasantly hot (a point of time depending on the weather), see 2. hurry, go fast; (of stock) be eager to go to the grass; cf. teng’era, hanyũka, marita. hiũhahiũha, dim. hiũhahiũhia, caus. dim. hiũhanga, diff. hiũbangia, caus. diff. hiũhania, caus. rec. hiũhanira, assoc, hiũhanĩria, caus. assoc. warm, heat, together. hiũhia, caus. 1. warm up, make hot; rita rĩa~ ciande, when the sun warms the shoulders (some time in mid-morning according to the weather), see mũthenya. 2. bring, take, quickly. hiũhithia, d, caus. hiũhika, st. pot. hiũhĩra, app. ; ~ horo, rush at a matter (with comments or action). hiũhĩria, caus. app. warm, heat, for; take care of a sick, weak, person; ~ look after (nurse, feed, cook for) a woman with a new baby. rhiũhĩria, refl. caus. (of an old, sick, person) look after oneself. hiũhirira, inten. hiũhũra, conv.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of 1. the swinging, switching to and fro of a cow’s tail to keep off flies
NEW_VALUE: the swinging, switching to and fro of a cow’s tail to keep off flies
FULL_ENTRY: hiũhiũ, ideo. (1), descr. of 1. the swinging, switching to and fro of a cow’s tail to keep off flies; swishswish. no ~? Icuthi cia ng'ombe, (riddle) in the desert just a swishswish?; (ans.) tails of cows. 2. the brandishing of a weapon. [see hiũria]

ũhiũhu, n, cl. 14, (iv), 1. heat, irritation, indignation, choler, vexation ; cf. mũhĩahio, mwagatha. 2. urgency, mischance; ma ~, distressful circumstances, things requiring to be put right. 3. ceremonial pollution. 4. ardour, warmth. [hiũha]
mũhiũhũko, mi-, n. (111), 1. hastiness, impetuosity, precipitancy; wa ~, a hasty person. 2. rush, dash, scamper (of children, goats, &c., to get their share of food, &c.). [hiũha]

hiũka, v.i. (11), 1. spring, shoot, out; (of a trap) spring back; cf. tegũka. 2. (of cloth, &c.) shrink; cf. nyiha. 3. (of sb., sth., passing) flash by. 4. (of rain) stop for a moment; cf. cenia. hiũkahiũka, dim. hiũkanga, diff. kana, rec. hiũkania, caus. rec. box one another, others. hiũkanira, assoc. hiũkanĩria, caus. app. rec. make cutting remarks to one another. hiũkia, caus. 1. fick: cf. camũra. 2. mention briefly in passing. 3. (of cow during milking period) stop yielding milk. hiũkithia, d. caus, hiũkĩka, st. pot. hiũkĩra, app. (of a man) spring at. hiũkĩria, caus. app. I. give sb. the gist of sth., throw a hint, suggest, to; interrupt. 2. say sth. derogatory, insult, provoke. higũkũka, neut. dash, leap.
hiũki, ~, n. (1m1), 1. quick, darting, movement; suspicious movement. 2. thing, person, making a suspicious movement. [hiũka]

NEW_VALUE: (of arrow or other missile passing or dove in swift flight) short sharp whizzing sound
FULL_ENTRY: mũhiũko, mi-; rũhiũko, hiũko; n. (111), 1. (of arrow or other missile passing or dove in swift flight) short sharp whizzing sound; whizz, rush. 2. flash, passing glimpse; pang, dart of pain. [hiũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: wave, whirl, swing sth. round and round in the air
NEW_VALUE: wave, whirl, swing round and round in the air
FULL_ENTRY: hiũria, v.t. (11), wave, whirl, swing sth. round and round in the air; shove about; ~ mũcuthĩ, wag the tail; ~ gũtũ, turn away the ear; cf. thiũrũrũkia, garũra, fhiũria, refl. swing oneself round, turn round quickly. hiũrahiũria, dim. hiũrangia, diff. hiũnrania, rec. fhiũrania, refl. rec. throw oneself about, struggle, wrestle with; cf. ĩgia, ĩtugutania. hiũranĩria, app. rec. hiũrithia, d. caus. hiũrĩka, st. pot. hiũrĩria, app.
hiũrio, ~, n. (11), whirling, swinging sth. round and round. [hiũria]
hĩ, interj. (uU), expr. disapproval, pained surprise, expostulation; cf. he.

hia, v.i. & t. (11), 1. (of food) be burnt, cooked, scalded; (of houses, &c.) be on fire; cf. cinwo, rugwo, hĩhio. 2. be affected by heat, cold, &c.; ~ mbara, be scorched (by sitting too close to a fire); ~ nĩ mbaa, be blasted by frost, ~ nĩ thabai, be stung by nettles. hĩahia, dim. become impatient, anxious; begin to bubble with joy. hĩahĩo, pass. dim.; ~ nĩ be boiling with sth. to say. hianga, diff. hĩanĩra, assoc. hĩaniria, caus. assoc, hiithia, d. caus. hĩka, st. pot. hĩra!, app. hiirira, d. app. inten. ma-!; kĩhia,! i-2; n. (ur), 1. burn, scald. 2. soreness in groin and buttocks after a long walk, due to perspiration and rubbing.

FULL_ENTRY: nũhia, mi-!, n. (coll.) a five-shilling note.,
mihia?, see mĩhiiro.

hiba?, see hũba.
hibibi, ideo. (1/11), descr. of fullness; iyũra ~, be brimful, chock-full; cf. bibi, kebe, bĩbĩa, kebebe.

mahigahigũ, see mahigahigũ.

FULL_ENTRY: higiriti, ~, n. (1v), verge, margin of stream, edge of field, table, &c.; ~-ini cia itũra, on the outskirts of the village; cf. hari?.

FULL_ENTRY: kĩhĩha, i-, n. (v11), 1. heap, bundle (as of sticks, &c.). 2. bunch (of goats, cattle); cf. gĩkundi.

kĩhĩ / (in certain contexts) kĩhi, i-, n. (iv), 1. big, uncircumcised boy; ~ kĩa mũhĩro boy painted white in preparation for the preinitiation dance; see also hĩra?; ~ kĩa contemptuous term for Luo who do not use circumcision as an initiation rite; cf. mwanake. 2. ~ kĩa rũi, tadpole; cf. kĩrnra. kahĩ / kahi, tũ-, n. small boy (up to age of twelve); term of endearment for a son even when grown up. Ũhii, n. cl. 14 only, childhood; boyhood, state of male uncircumcision.

hĩra?, v.i. (1) 1. (of maize, &c.) be ready to burst into flower; cf. thuka. 2. FULL_ENTRY: ihiira / ihĩro, ma-, n, (vin), the burning of a hut, homestead, or house; embers, ashes, charred ruins of a burnt hut; mbũri ya kũhoria ~, sacrifice performed by village elders while a new hut is being built (a weaverbird nest is burnt as part of the ceremony). hĩra, ~, n. charred end of grass after a grass-burning; nyeki ya ~, fresh growth of grass after a fire or drought; mbura ya kũmeria (kũhoria) ~, the first light rains after the dry season; cf. magũna-ng’ombe. (N.K.) 1. a bundle of long things, rods, poles, reeds, grass, &c. 2. several, a number, a quantity of similar things; cf. mũngĩkĩ, kahĩri, tũ-, n. a bundle of firewood, sugar-cane, or small things tied together. hiki, ~, n. (1), impatience, premature haste. [hĩka]

Mũhindi [mũhindi], A- ; Mũhindi, Ohindi [oohindĩ]; n. (1v), an Indian. kĩhindi [kĩhĩndĩ], n. cl. 7 only, Indian language, Indian ways. Uhindi, n. cl. r14 only, India, Indian manners and customs, Indian culture.

FULL_ENTRY: ihĩndu, ma-, n. (11), 1. inedible ‚wild banana (Ensete edule) (also known as ‘male banana’). 2. goat, cow, sheep, which has borne but one offspring.

African Box (Sida schimperiana) (useful as a hedgingplant, low-growing, people trip over it; a looped twig of it taken on a journey to ward off evil). 2. stumbling-block, stump, stone, root, anything which causes stumbling. kahinga, tũ-, n. r. (of a conflagration) abate; (of a swelling, boil, &c.) burst, be relieved; komanga ũhubũke, sleep more so that you may relax. hubũkanĩra, app. rec. nyau cieri ikĩ~ nyoni, both cats pounced on the bird. hubũkaniria, caus. app. rec. hubũkira, app. neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of stopping sb. from speaking by covering his mouth or by quick reproof, catching a bird by sudden envelopment with the hands or some sort of covering. [huba] n. cl. 6 (11), 1. coverings, muffings. Nguyo ũmbagio nĩ ~, the colobus is swelled up by its heavy enveloping fur. 2. concealments, dark speech, hidden meanings, disingenuousness
FULL_ENTRY: huba / hubi, ideo. (1), descr. of stopping sb. from speaking by covering his mouth or by quick reproof, catching a bird by sudden envelopment with the hands or some sort of covering. [huba] n. cl. 6 (11), 1. coverings, muffings. Nguyo ũmbagio nĩ ~, the colobus is swelled up by its heavy enveloping fur. 2. concealments, dark speech, hidden meanings, disingenuousness; ng'ania ndarĩ ~, soand-so has no secrets, is frank, open and above-board; cf. hitho; see huba.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of dull resonance, also of sudden action, pouncing on, enveloping, as animals and birds as well as men springing on their prey
NEW_VALUE: of dull resonance, also of sudden action, pouncing on, enveloping, as animals and birds as well as men springing on their prey
FULL_ENTRY: hubu, ideo. (1/11), descr. of dull resonance, also of sudden action, pouncing on, enveloping, as animals and birds as well as men springing on their prey; ~ na ~, suddenly. hububu / hubububu, ideo. descr. of being taken by surprise.
hubuniria, see under huba.

huha [huuha], v.t. (1), 1. blow, blow upon; winnow; ~ mwaki, blow up the fire; ~ irio, blow upon (hot) food (to cool it); ~ mwere, winnow millet (by tossing it in a tray and occasionally blowing on the threshed grain); ~ mĩrũri, whistle (with the mouth); ~ karumbeta, play a flute, trumpet, &c., (coll.) exult oneself; ~ (ngũri) biringi, blow a whistle (e.g. as a referee in a match). 2. treat with care; ndikũmũ~, Pm not sparing him (in words or deeds). huhahuha, dim. huhanga, diff. huhana, rec. huhania, caus. rec. huhanira, app. rec. huhaniria, caus. rec. app. huhia, caus. blow air out, breathe out heavily, sniff up; (of wood covered with soil burning up slowly into charcoal) blow, puff, smoke out; cf. ruta mĩrukĩ, ndogo. huhithia, d. caus. have sth. winnowed, assist sb. in winnowing or blowing. huhika, st. pot. huhira, app. puff upon; ~ mũbira, inflate ball, tyre, &c. buhiria, caus. app. huhirira, inten. huhirĩria, caus. inten., ~ mwaki, blow a dying fire continuously (until it revives); ~ ũhoro, explain sth., try to persuade sb., in a delicate, slow manner. huhũra, conv. puff at, blow away; deflate. fhuhũra, refl. conv. kuhũka, neut. be deflated; blow in gusts; blow away with the wind. caus. rec. neut.; rũkĩ~, and the wind blew violently. caus. neut. let air out; ~ rũkũngũ, blow off dust. huhũkira, app. neut. 1. (of an air tube, tyre, &c.) burst, lose air, at; (of a person) get hungry, at: 2. rush through a place, along a path; rush like the wind. huhũkiria, caus. app. 1. blow dust with the mouth on to, for. 2. let the air out of a tyre, football, &c., for, at. 3. winnow grain at, for. 4. blow the fire for. huhũrũrũkia, caus. inten. neut. blow in a long-drawn manner.

mũhuhi [mũhuuhi], a-, n. (1), I. a blower; one who treats ailments by ‘blowing’, stroking with an amulet, &c.; ~ (wa) mbura, one who checks or tries to check rain. 2. ~-mũimbia, ~-muuria, one whose good intentions make matters worse; cf. mũthwagimuohi, krama-ita. [huha] [mũhuuhio], mi-, n. (1), a sniff. [huha]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: an pi ph A inflator, pump for inflating a tyre or football.
NEW_VALUE: an pi ph A inflator, pump for inflating a tyre or football
FULL_ENTRY: kĩhuhĩri [kĩhuuhĩri}], i-, n. (x1), an pi ph A inflator, pump for inflating a tyre or football. [huha]

mũhuhirĩria [mũhuuhiriria], a-, n. (iv), spokesman, advocate; ~ kĩrimũ ndaigaga the man who speaks on behalf of a fool can never put his staff down; cf. mwaririria, mĩcirĩri. ũhuhiriria, n. cl. 14 only, agitation, advocacy; maũndũ ma ~, political activity. [huha]
ihuhiro [ihuuhiro], ma-; kĩhuhiro, i-; n. (x1), threshing-flcor, winnowing-place. [huha]

NEW_VALUE: just headgear and breeze, nothing unusual
FULL_ENTRY: rũhuho [rũhuuho], hubo, n.\ 1), wind, air; igua na ~, get information through secret sources; itumbi ria ~, sterile egg laid where there is no cock; no na ~, (answer to greeting) just headgear and breeze, nothing unusual; gwatia ~, bring sickness upon sb. by talking about him, cf. themenga. mũhuhbo, mi-, n. (rare) whispered talk, gossip. kahuho, tũ-, n. dim.; ~ gaceke, a light breeze, movement in air. karũhuhbo, tũ-, n 2nd dim. a gentle breeze; cf. kahurutano, kariera.
mũhuhu? [mũhuuhu] / mũhuha [mũhuuha], mi-, n. (1) Guatemala cypress, box.
rũhuhu hubu, n. (1), bat.
buhuga (N.K.), see huhuya.

NEW_VALUE: of whispered rumours, gossip, calumny, irresponsible chatter
FULL_ENTRY: hu-hu-hu / huhu-huhu / huhuhuhu, ideo. (1), descr. of whispered rumours, gossip, calumny, irresponsible chatter.
huhuma, see hohoma.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. move about in search of food, &c. 2. be inquisitive
NEW_VALUE: move about in search of food, &c
FULL_ENTRY: hubhunga, v.t. (1), I1. move about in search of food, &c. 2. be inquisitive; be a busybody. 3. (of police, &c.) search closely. hubungania, caus. rec. huhunganira, app. rec, huhucaus. app. rec. huhungithia, d. caus. huhungika, st. pot. huhungira, app. huhungiria, caus. app.

NEW_VALUE: strong wind, tempest, squall, tumult (of people)
FULL_ENTRY: kĩhuhũkanio [kĩhuuhũkanio], i-, n. (1) strong wind, tempest, squall, tumult (of people); ~ kĩa rũhuho, storm or gale of wind, cf. ngoma cia aka. [huha]

NEW_VALUE: reaction to sudden pain, pinch, burn, scald
FULL_ENTRY: hui, interj. (U), expr. reaction to sudden pain, pinch, burn, scald.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. heap of wood (usu. black wattle) covered with soil and lit from one end to burn into charcoal
FULL_ENTRY: mũhuko!, mi-, n. (1), 1. heap of wood (usu. black wattle) covered with soil and lit from one end to burn into charcoal; ara ~, arrange wood in a pile of this sort; thika ~, cover wood in a pile with a layer of green leaves and soil; gwatia ~, set the charcoal pile alight; kuria ~, uncover the pile; cf. makara, huhirio. 2. (of bananas) a pocket put to ripen; thatanĩria mĩhuko, spoil one another’s bananas by uncovering them, upset plans by premature disclosure. mĩ-, n. (1), bag made of cloth (not of leather or skin); pocket in a garment; cf. mondo, kĩondo.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: stalk of millet left in the ground after reaping by cutting off the ears.
NEW_VALUE: stalk of millet left in the ground after reaping by cutting off the ears
FULL_ENTRY: huku [buuku], ~, n. (u1), stalk of millet left in the ground after reaping by cutting off the ears.

NEW_VALUE: of the scraping away of earth from rootcrops, &c
FULL_ENTRY: hukuhuku, ideo. (1), descr. of the scraping away of earth from rootcrops, &c., by porcupine, domestic fowl, people. [huka]

NEW_VALUE: loose, dusty, top-soil lacking in humus, good for yams
FULL_ENTRY: kihukuhuku, n. cl. 7 only (v); tĩri wa ~, loose, dusty, top-soil lacking in humus, good for yams. [huka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: break clods of recently dug new garden
NEW_VALUE: one who performs the purification ceremony after the death of the head of the household
FULL_ENTRY: hukũra?, v.t. (1), break clods of recently dug new garden ; harrow; cf. hianyũra, cimba, ũnga. hukahukũra, dim. dig up throwing the earth out, hukũranga, diff. hukũrania, caus. rec. hukũranira, app. rec. hukũranĩria, caus. app. rec. hukũria, caus. beat earth from sods, clear soil from grass, roots, weeds, &c., in preparation for planting. hukũrithia, d. caus. hukũrĩka, st. pot. hukũcaus. app. a-, n. (11), one who performs the purification ceremony after the death of the head of the household. [huka]

NEW_VALUE: the mole’s way of digging by scraping
FULL_ENTRY: mũhukũririe, mi-, n. (G1); ~ wa huko, the mole’s way of digging by scraping. [hukũra?]
hukũro, ~, n. (11), death-cleansing ceremony. [huka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. draw blank, be abortive, return empty-handed from a foray
NEW_VALUE: draw blank, be abortive, return empty-handed from a foray
FULL_ENTRY: huma [huuma], v.i. (11), 1. draw blank, be abortive, return empty-handed from a foray; ngigĩ itingĩ~, the locusts are so many that they will never cease; cf. homa. 2. take origin.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: childless man, woman who has never had a child. kĩhuma, i-, n. 1. that which is abortive, purposeless, abandoned
NEW_VALUE: that which is abortive, purposeless, abandoned
FULL_ENTRY: huma [huuma], ~, n. (viu), childless man, woman who has never had a child. kĩhuma, i-, n. 1. that which is abortive, purposeless, abandoned; an empty quiver or sword-sheath ; a missfire, a discharge of blank cartridge; ikia mgu ~, shoot and miss the mark. 2. man; cf. mũkirũ. [huma]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: pile of earth cast up from a burrow.
NEW_VALUE: pile of earth cast up from a burrow
FULL_ENTRY: ihumbia, ma-, n. (1), pile of earth cast up from a burrow. [humba]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: heap (of maize plants beaten down by rain, &c.).
NEW_VALUE: heap (of maize plants beaten down by rain, &c
FULL_ENTRY: kĩhumbira, i-, n. (x1), heap (of maize plants beaten down by rain, &c.). [humba]
kihumbiri, i-, n. (x1), a covering, veil. [humba]
kihumbu, t-, n. (111), heap, mound.
mahumbuto, n. cl. 6 (111), 1. lobes of lungs; cf. mathagu, mahũri, mahomboirũ. 2. inferior quality of grain left after grading.

huna, v.i. (1) x1. (of animals and humans) abort; give premature birth ; cf. thata. 2. (of bast which fails to come off clean, a plant or log being sawn) tail, taper, off prematurely. hunanga, diff. hunania, caus. rec. hunanĩra, assoc. hunaniria, caus. assoc, hunia, caus. hbunithia, d. caus. taper off. hunĩka, st. pot. hunira, app. huniria, caus. app.
kĩhuna, i-, n. (1), contagious abortion. [huna]

kĩhuno, i-, n. (1), discarded foetus, still-born child; any barren product, e.g. an infertile, empty, maize-cob. kahuno, tũ-, n. dead-born offspring, premature birth, not surviving; plank which breaks off short in sawing or splitting. huno, n. an abortion. n. ci. x4 only, 1. abortion (in general). 2. ceremonial pollution resulting from abortion.

hungurucũka, v.i. (11), pass through, shoot through, penetrate. hungurucakanga, diff. hungurucũkana, rec. hungurucũkania, caus. rec. hungurucũkanira, app. rec. hungurucũkithia, d. caus. hungurucũkira, app. hungurucũkiria, caus. app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be spread, spread evenly, become disseminated
FULL_ENTRY: hunja, v.i. (11), be spread, spread evenly, become disseminated; soak, seep; irio itina~ mboco, the food lacks a fair proportion of beans, see cuca; rangi nĩwa~ wega (of cloth, &c.) the dye is very evenly distributed. hunjana, rec. 1. be completely covered, spread. 2. mix, blend, into each other; (of people) intermingle, fraternize; cf. cocorana. hunjia, caus. proclaim, spread news; preach; cf. heana horo. hunjithia, d. caus. hbunjira, app. hunjĩria, caus. app.; ~ andũ horo wa Ngai, preach the word of God to the people.
kĩhunjio, i-, n. (11), preaching, preaching service, evangelistic service. [hunjia]

NEW_VALUE: wind thin wire round another wire
FULL_ENTRY: hunya', v.t. (11), 1. wind thin wire round another wire. 2. cover up smoothly. 3. adorn a daughter by giving her ornaments when she leaves to be married. hunyahunya, dim. hunyanga, dif. hunyana, rec. hunyanira, app. rec. hunyaniria, caus. app. rec. hunyithia, d. caus. hunyĩka, st. pot. hunyira, app. hunyirira, inten. hunyũra, conv.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: weed haphazardly, pull out the big weeds (leaving the small ones) hunyahunya, dim. hunyanga, diff. hunyanĩra, app. rec, hunyaniria, caus. hunyithia, d. caus. hunyika, st. pot. hunyira, app. weed carelessly, for, at. rira, inten. weed here and there (leaving patches of weeds in between). hunyũra, conv. 1. make the skin dry and rough
NEW_VALUE: make the skin dry and rough
FULL_ENTRY: hunya?, v.t. (11), weed haphazardly, pull out the big weeds (leaving the small ones) hunyahunya, dim. hunyanga, diff. hunyanĩra, app. rec, hunyaniria, caus. hunyithia, d. caus. hunyika, st. pot. hunyira, app. weed carelessly, for, at. rira, inten. weed here and there (leaving patches of weeds in between). hunyũra, conv. 1. make the skin dry and rough; scrape free of hair; peel off (the layer of dry lamb-skin on which the hair grows); make dull, dry, rough; cf. munyũra. 2. (of children playing in a garden, &c.) raise dust; make (a garden) unfertile (by trampling on it, &c.); ~ thĩ, raise dust, gallop away. hunyũrana, rec. conv. make each other’s skin rough. rania, caus. rec., conv. hunyũria, caus. conv. take a little away (from what is left); ~ ndeto, ũhoro, hint at, speak obscurely, disjointedly, give only an inkling, be inexplicit. hunyũriria, caus. app. conv. (as hunyũria). rhunyũrũria, refl. caus. app. conv. tear oneself to pieces in rage. hunyũrũrania, rec. caus. inten. conv. (of cattle, &c.) eat long grass carelessly; cf, munyũrũrania. bunyũrũrũkia, neut. caus. inten. conv. I1. weed scrappily in a place where there are crops; act in a slipshod manner, perfunctorily; scamp, give a lick and a promise. 2. trifle with, make half-hearted attempts. hunyũka, neut. conv. I. (of gardens in drought) be patchy and bare. 2. (of skin) be rough, be lacking gloss, be dry and dirty. hunyũkaniria, caus. assoc. neut. I. make (things) dry, rough, at the same time. 2. speak to one another in angry words. hunyũkia, caus. neut. 1. make soil dry, poor. 2. make rough; speak without regard for rules of decorum. hunyũkithia, d. caus. neut. hunyũkira, app. neut. be rough skinned, at, for.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1I. patch, dab. 2. (used as adv.) patchily, incompletely
FULL_ENTRY: hunye, ~, n. (11), 1I. patch, dab. 2. (used as adv.) patchily, incompletely; (with ref., to oil paint) lunevenly; carelessly, in a slapdash way; cf. maboyaboya; see hunyirira.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: wire ornament made by spinning fine wire round a thicker wire
FULL_ENTRY: mũhunyo [mũhuunyo], mi-; hunyo, ~~; (11), wire ornament made by spinning fine wire round a thicker wire; cf. (N.K.) morara, kĩkunyo, i-, n. wristlet decorated with fine wire.
hunyũrio, ~, n. (x1), small sample. [hunya]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. strip of hair torn off dry goat or sheep-skin in process of removing rough hair. 2. mbũri ya ~, goat given to mother by daughter -on marriage in recognition of her years of toil looking after her
NEW_VALUE: strip of hair torn off dry goat or sheep-skin in process of removing rough hair. 2
FULL_ENTRY: kĩhunyũro, i-, n. (x1), 1. strip of hair torn off dry goat or sheep-skin in process of removing rough hair. 2. mbũri ya ~, goat given to mother by daughter -on marriage in recognition of her years of toil looking after her; goat from which the first skin garment of a bride was made, given to her by her father in recompense for her help in herding. (i-), mahunyũro, n. small hairy bits of skin scraped off a hide in its preparation. [hunya]

hura!, v.t. (1) broadcast, scatter, throw; ~ mbeũ, sow seeds, broadcast; cf. handa, hurunja, nyagania. hurahura, dim. hurahuria, caus. dim. huranga, diff. hurania, caus. rec. huranira, app. rec. huraniria, caus. app. rec, huria, caus. 1r. snatch, snatch away, whistle away; pluck, seize violently. 2. scratch, scatter (earth by scratching, &c.}); ~ (of people and animals) take off fast; ~ rũkũngũ, raise dust; ~ mũhuko, unearth ~ mũndũ, pick a quarrel with sb.; ~ ndaka, paw the earth, defy, challenge. hurithia, d. caus. hurika, st. pot. hurira, app. hurĩria, caus. app.; ~ rũkũngũ, throw dust at. hurirĩra, inten.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of pus, blood) exude. 2. (of pregnant woman) have haemorrhage
FULL_ENTRY: hur2a?, v.i. (1), 1. (of pus, blood) exude. 2. (of pregnant woman) have haemorrhage; cf. ura. 3. (of animals) miscarry; cf. huna.
mũhurahuro, see under mũhuro.

NEW_VALUE: ox or bull of this colour. [see huria]
FULL_ENTRY: huria, ~,n. (11), 1. rhinoceros; cf. munyi. 2. cow of dirty brown colour like the dust in which a rhinoceros rolls. kĩhuria, -i, n. usu. with cl. 9/10 concords, ox or bull of this colour. [see huria]
rũhurio, hurio, n. (1), 1. fierce anger, fiery temper, impulsiveness; aggressiveness, pugnacity, violence, 2. place where rhinoceros has defaecated and scattered dung, scrape. [huria]

mũhuro, mĩ-, n. (1), place, part, below; place lower down; ~ wa igwa, low-lying ground planted with sugarcane; ~ wa, below; cf. magũri-ini, nguruĩ, ruungu. mũhurahuro, n. cl. 3 only, (used as adv.) a little below, beneath. kĩhuro, i-, n. a shallow valley, depression, dip in the land; {h lower level; cf. gĩtuamba, kĩrũahũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: tall forest tree (Vitex keniensis) (provides good, easily worked timber, used for making birdscaring platforms).
NEW_VALUE: tall forest tree (Vitex keniensis) (provides good, easily worked timber, used for making birdscaring platforms)
FULL_ENTRY: mũhuru', mi-, n. (v11), tall forest tree (Vitex keniensis) (provides good, easily worked timber, used for making birdscaring platforms).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. patch of white fur, ruff, round birdd’s or monkey’s face, neck
NEW_VALUE: ox or bull with white patch on neck
FULL_ENTRY: rũhuru!, huru, n. (vz), I. patch of white fur, ruff, round birdd’s or monkey’s face, neck; mũndũ ~, a marked man. 2. she-goat or cow with broad white patch on back of neck like raven. ihuru, ma-, n. 1. crow with white patch on neck; wa-~, greedy, gluttonous, person, cf. igucu; see igogo. 2, stick carried by youths at pre-initiation ceremonies, triangular in section, with one cutting edge. kfhuru, f-, n. usu. with cl. o/r0 concords, ox or bull with white patch on neck. kahuru, tũ-, n. also ~ ka hari, sp. of crow, raven; cf. kagogo.

NEW_VALUE: of a sudden rush of beast, bird, or man, in a flash
FULL_ENTRY: nuru, ideo. (1), descr. of a sudden rush of beast, bird, or man, in a flash.

huruma, v.i. (11) dig underneath, tunnel, excavate. hurumahuruma, dim. hurumanga, diff. hurumana, rec. (of a tunnel being dug from both ends) come through. hurumania, caus. rec, make a tunnel come through. hurumanira, assoc. hurumaniria, caus. assoc. hurumia, caus. hurumithia, d. caus. hurumika, st. pot. hurumira, app. (of mole, porcupine, man) dig at, tunnel in, a place.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. hasty, precipitate, quick-tempered, or violent person. 2. one who is smart and quick. [hurumũka] ideo. (1/11), descr. of bolting, breaking cover, charging out
NEW_VALUE: hasty, precipitate, quick-tempered, or violent person
FULL_ENTRY: hurumũka, v.i. (1) dash, rush off, jump out (from a hiding-place); cf. gurumũka, umbuthũka. kanga, diff. hurumũkania, caus. rec. hurumũkanira, assoc. hurucaus. assoc. hurumũkia, caus. hurumũkithia, d. caus. app. dash, rush, jump, at. hurumnũra, v.t. arouse; stir from rest, hiding; cause to jump out, rush off. ~, n. (v1), 1. hasty, precipitate, quick-tempered, or violent person. 2. one who is smart and quick. [hurumũka] ideo. (1/11), descr. of bolting, breaking cover, charging out; cf. guthũkũ. [see hurumũka] i-, n. (1), that which causes a stampede. [hurumũra]
urunda (N.K.), see huruta,

wurunga, v.t. & v.i. (1) scour for, hunt about in (e.g. a wood for fuel or lost goat); search for yams among yam-plants or for sweet potatoes in an exhausted sweet-potato patch; (of ant-bear, porcupine, &c.) dig all over the place in search of food.

hurunja, v.t. (1) scatter (people, things); throw away, scatter in a rage; cf. haragania, aragania, nyagania, ikia. hurahurunja, dim. hurunjanga, diff. hurunjana, rec. hurunjania, caus. rec. hurunjanira, app. rec. hurunjanĩria, caus. app. rec. hurunjithia, d. caus. hurunjika, st. pot, hurunjĩra, app.; ~ ng'ombe mabebe, spread out maizestalks for cattle. hurunjũra, conv. (used inten.). hurunjũka, neut.; kĩrĩndĩ nĩkĩa~, the crowd has dispersed,
hururu, ~, n. (x), 1. chasm, deep gorge, precipice; ~ ya Cania, the Chania gorge. 2. deep pit for trapping game. Nyahururu, n. with loc. concords, Thomson’s Falls.
hurururu, ideo. (1), descr. of falling, dropping sheer down.

huruta [huruuta] / (N.K.) hurunda / ruguta, v.t. (1) I. blow, fan, with smith’s bellows; ~ mwaki, blow up a fire with bellows. 2. hurl, swing round, peddle. hurahuruta, dim. hurutanga, diff. hurutana, rec. (of wind) blow violently. hurutania, caus. rec. hurutanĩra, app. rec, hurutanĩria, caus. app. rec. hurutithia, d. caus. (as in smelting) assist in working bellows. hurutĩka, st. pot. hurutira, app. 1. blow, fan, (fire) at. 2. ~ mũithikiri, peddle a bicycle; ~ kĩgera mwaki, make iron red-hot by blowing up the fire. hurutũka, neut. 1. (of wind) cease to blow. 2. dart quickly.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: light breeze, a stir of wind.
NEW_VALUE: light breeze, a stir of wind
FULL_ENTRY: kahħhurutano [kahuruutano] (N.K.) kahurundano, tũ-, n. (1), light breeze, a stir of wind. [huruta]

NEW_VALUE: smithb’s assistant, bellows-blower. kihuruti [kĩhuruuti], i-, n. fan, electric fan, propeller. [huruta]
FULL_ENTRY: mũhuruti [mũhuruuti] / (N.K.) mũruguti [mũruguuti], a-, n. (1), smithb’s assistant, bellows-blower. kihuruti [kĩhuruuti], i-, n. fan, electric fan, propeller. [huruta]
kihurũko [kihuurũkoj, n. (11), resting-place (e.g. on a journey). ũhurũko, n. cl. 14, rest. kahurũko, tũ-, n. junction of paths where people sit and rest. [hurũka]

hurũra, v.t. (1), 1. make slide, down, out; cf. tirũra, ikũrũkia. 2. clear out, drive out, sweep away; cf. rutũrũra. huraburũra, dim. diff. hurũrana, rec. hurũrania, caus. rec. hurũranira, app. rec. hurũraniria, caus. rec. app. hurũria, caus. ; ~ ũcũrũ, gulp porridge; cf. hurũrithia, d. caus. hurũrika, st. pot. hurũrira, app.; ~ irio, gobble food. huraũrũra, conv. (used inten.) bring down, make fall, make gush, out, down. hurũrũka, neut. 1. rush, slide, down, skid, slip on mud. 2. descend, fall sheer down. hurũrũkira, app. neut. hurũrũkiria, caus. app. neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: x. beat, hit, strike, pound, tap
FULL_ENTRY: hũra [bũra], v.t. (11), x. beat, hit, strike, pound, tap; ~ hĩ, clap hands; ~ hũti, knock at a door and ask to be allowed in; ~ itumbĩ, beat up an egg; ~ kagoto (kogoto), break up stone for mixing with cement; ~ kobiritĩ, strike a match; ~ magitaimu, mark time; ~ matiira, (esp. of animals) stamp the feet in discomfort or anger; ~ mũhũri, mark with a rubber stamp; ~ mũtu, beat flour mixture in a container; ~ mũtwe, break up the bones of a slaughtered animals head by knocking them (prior to scorching and cooking), cf. atia; ~ mbembe, crush maize in a mortar; ~ mwere, thresh millet with a flail; ~ nguo, wash clothes, cf. thambia; ~ njohi, make beer (esp. from sugar-cane); ~ rũna, soften a hide by beating it; cf. ringa. 2. fight, defeat; cf. rũnũa. 3. slap; ~ mũndũ rũhĩ, kobi, slap sb. hard; cf. gũtha, hiũkia, camũra. 4. drive (goats, cattle). 5. play (games, musical instruments); ~ (mũndũ) cuti, tell (sb.) off; ~ (horo) cuti, toss a matter about with a view to discarding it; ~ (mũbira) cuti, kick (a ball) up in the air; ~ koni, hoot, blow a horn; ~ kĩnanda, play a musical instrument, organ; ~ mũbira, play football; ~ ndarama, play a drum; ~ ngengere, ring a bell; ~ thani cia kinanda, play gramophone records; cf. inia. 6. used as an operative word with vv. nouns: ~(nguo) bathi, iron (clothes); ~ birigiceni, inspect, search, a place or person; ~ (mũtokaa) cethi, step on accelerator in the midst of changing gear; ~ kanua (inegene, kerere), make a lot of noise (gossip, chatter); ~ kona, swing round a corner; cf, ~ magendambia, squeeze one’s way out of a tight corner (between the rafters); ~ maita, multiply (in arithmetic), calculate, weigh up pros and cons; ~ maraya, practise prostitution; ~ mĩreko, push one’s way through bush; ~ mand na ũhoro, confuse, quieten, subdue sb. by telling him a lot; ~ mbica, take a photo; ~ mburĩki, clap on brakes; ~ randa, plane (wood); ~ riboti, report Ein person), give out a notice; ~ thimũ, make a telephone call, send a telegram; ~ thurutia, salute. ĩhũra, refl. hũrahũra, dim. hũranga, diff. hũrana, rec. hũranira, rec. app. hũraniria, caus. rec. app. hũranirĩra, jinten. rec. add to; ~ hamwe, heap, pile, together, concentrate; cf. turanĩra, gũthanirira, iganĩrĩra. hũrithia, d. caus. rithia, refi. d. caus. hũrithania, rec. d. caus. cause to knock, beat, against one another; make (people, animals) fight. fhũrithania, refil. rec. d. caus. I. writhe in pain; fling oneself about (as in a fit). 2. exert oneself. hOũrika, st. pot. hũrikira, app. st. pot. hũrira, app. I. beat, strike, for. 2. drive, knock, in (a nail, peg, &c.) hũrĩrira, inten.; ~ mũĩcumarĩ, nail sth. on to sth. else. hũririria, caus. inten. force on, into; ~ ngombe gĩthaka-inĩ, force cattle into the bush.

ibũri, ma-, n. (111), 1. lung; (of cattle) pleuro-pneumonia ; (of human beings) tuberculosis, T.B. 2. (cl. 6) mũrimũ wa ~, pleuro-pneumonia.

FULL_ENTRY: ũhũri n. cl. 14 (111), beating, pounding (esp. of sugar-cane for beermaking). [hũra]

FULL_ENTRY: mũhũri mĩ-, n. (1) 1. depression, hollow, land at a lower level; cf. moriko. 2. channel, runnel, furrow; run, runway, track (as made by rats, &c.); alternating groove and ridge, corrugation in iron, milling on rim of a coin; ridge between two furrows, a EA bed as for planting sweet potatoes or ridging European potatoes; mĩhũrĩ ya mbura, furrows made by rain in loose soil, kĩhũri, i-, n. a depression, land at a lower level; level land by the side of a river; lowlying flat in a river valley.
ihũrĩro [ihũrĩro]}, ma-, n. (11); ~ rĩa mwere, threshing-floor. [hũra]
hũro [hũro], ~, n. (111), beating.

mahũro [mahũro], n. cl, 6 (111); iria rĩa ~, separated milk, butter-milk; cf. mathũco. [hũra]

NEW_VALUE: bindweed or other creeper gathered for handfeeding goats in the homestead
FULL_ENTRY: hũrũra, v.t. (1), strip, whittle, chip (leaves, branches from a tree) with a bush knife ; cf. bura, thambura, ceha. hũrahũrgũra, dim. hũrũranga, diff. hũrũrana, rec. hũrũrania, caus. rec. rec. app. hũrũraniria, caus. rec. app. hũrũria, caus. gulp, drink noisily with gusto, audible relish; cf. hororia. hũrgrithia, d. caus. st. pot. hũrũrira, app.; ~ mũndũ thĩ, despise sb. neut. become emaciated; become unstrung; (of fruits, leaves, &c.)} be shed from the tree. hũrũrũkia, caus. neut. strip off (branches, leaves) completely. ma-, n. (1), bindweed or other creeper gathered for handfeeding goats in the homestead.
hũta [bũta], v.i. (11), be, become, hungry, empty, hollow. hũtahũta, dim. hũtanga, diff. hũtia, caus. hũtithia, d. caus. hũtika, st. pot. hũtĩra, app.
hũtĩri / hoteri, ~, n. (vi), hotel, boarding-house. [E.]

hũtha, v.i. (11), 1. be light in weight; be thin, light, in texture. 2. be easy to perform; (of a problem) be easy to solve; ~ mũtwe, be easily taught. 3. be light on one’s feet, be swift of foot; ni tũhũthe, let’s go, steal away. 4. be weak in body; be poor, penurious. hũthahũtha, dim. hũthanga, diff. hũthangira, app. diff., spend a little more. hũthangirũo, pass. app. diff.; nĩnda~ nĩ mũrigo, the load is sitting a bit lighter on me. hũthania, caus. rec. hũthanĩra, assoc. hũthaniria, caus. assoc. hũthia, caus. make light, easy, thin; make little of, belittle (a person). ihũthia, refl. caus. 1. make oneself light. 2. work unwillingly, steal away. hũthithia, d. caus. hũthika, st. pot. hũthira, app. 1. make use of, use up, spend; cf. tũmĩra. 2. be accustomed to, be in the habit of. hũthirũo, pass. app.; ~ nĩ, be prone to, find it easy to, incline to, hũthiranira, app. rec. app. hũthĩrĩria, caus. inten. make light of, belittle; cf. maniriria, raririria, nyikirĩria.
kahũtha, (tũ-), n. (vIr), indolence, supineness; wa ~, easy-going, dilatory, lackadaisical person; cf. mũhũthia. [hũtha]

NEW_VALUE: he does not find it easy, is not inclined to do it

mahũthiro, n. cl. 6 only (11), (coll.) expenses, living costs, livelihood. [bhũtha]

ũhũthũ, n. cl. 14 (111), 1. (of weight) lightness; (of texture) thinness ; swiftness of foot. 2. weakness, debility. 3. (coll.}) nĩ ~, it is easy.
mũhũri, mi-, n. (111), seal, signet, rubber-stamp. [cf. Sw. MUHURI]
mũhũyũ [mũhũyũ], mi-; kihũyũ, i-; n. (1) foam, froth. hũyũ, n. much froth. [see hũyũka]

hwa, ideo. (U), descr. of: 1. smooth, swift movement; athiaga ~ ~, he walks with long, swinging strides. 2. a flying leap; ndarnũnũga o ~ na ndihutie kagiri, I jumped clean over the hedge without touching it at all. 3. cutting through with a sharp knife.

hwaa, ideo. (u), descr. of: 1. surprise or effect of pleasure. 2. smooth motion, e.g. a gush of diarrhoea, of pus from a boil, the quiet easy movement of a motor-car, people moving along at a smooth even pace; nyua o ~, drink it down in one unbroken draught.

NEW_VALUE: cutting with a sharp knife, fast walking, quick motion
FULL_ENTRY: hwe, ideo. (U), descr. of: 1. cutting with a sharp knife, fast walking, quick motion. 2. silent, swift flow of stream or river.

FULL_ENTRY: hwee, ideo. (U); thit ~, get worse and worse, go completely to the dogs; see hwerereka.

hwerereka, v.i. (1), 1. tilt, lean; lean slowly, gradually; cf. erereka, inama. 2. (of fire, light, &c.) dwindle, fade away; cf. hora. 3. (of a person) get weak slowly, be in the last stages of illness. hwererekanga, diff. hwererekangia, caus. diff. hwerere-kania, caus. rec. cause one another to tilt, lean, bend, &c. hwererekanira, assoc. hwererekaniria, caus, assoc. hwererekia, caus. hwererekithia, d. caus. hwererekeka, st. pot. hwererekera, app.; mwaki nĩũra~, the fire is dying out. hwererekerera, inten. I
i-, conc. prefix (U), used before a consonant: 1. with n. cl. 5 and cl. 8. 2. as a.c. cl. 5. 3. as p.c. cl. 8 and ch 10.

NEW_VALUE: screen, hedge, anything screening or partly concealing an object
FULL_ENTRY: rũibi / rũibĩ, njibi / njibii, n. (11), screen, hedge, anything screening or partly concealing an object.

Gũtiri ~ na mũcũthirĩria, there is no real difference between a thief and an onlooker. ~ wa ngwaci na wa mĩrĩyo, when it comes to theft there’s not much difference between sweet potatoes and their vines. ~ tari mũnyite nĩ gake, a thief who is not caught eats what he steals as if it were his own. See under nda
FULL_ENTRY: mũici, a-, n. (111), thief. ~ ti mwendwa, no one loves a thief. Gũtiri ~ na mũcũthirĩria, there is no real difference between a thief and an onlooker. ~ wa ngwaci na wa mĩrĩyo, when it comes to theft there’s not much difference between sweet potatoes and their vines. ~ tari mũnyite nĩ gake, a thief who is not caught eats what he steals as if it were his own. See under nda; cf. mũtunyani. gĩci, ci-, n. a confirmed thief; ny& nĩ ~ kihoro, he is a harmless-looking or plausible thief. &ici, maũici, n. theft, thieving, stealth. [iya]

(1), put, place, keep; put on one side, set down, put away, establish, place securely; ~ thĩ, put down (on the floor); ~ mwena, put aside; ~ mũtumia, cohabit with a woman; ~ gĩathĩ, make an appointment; ~ kĩrĩkanĩro, make a promise; ~ watho, make a law; ~ rũaru, lie down, rest; ~ ithaga, make a bet; Ngai arokũ~ wega!, may God keep you!; ~ nduka, start a shop; ~ maĩ, (of a pot) hold water. figa, refl. place oneself (at), entrust oneself (to), surrender (to God, &c.). igaiga, dim. iganga, diff. igana!, rec. keep one another. igania, caus. rec, place, keep, haphazardly. iganira, rec. app. iganiria, caus. app. rec. iganirira, inten. rec. pile on top of one another; ~ kĩgenyo, testify falsely (about people). igithia, d. caus, igika, st. pot. igĩra, app. igirira, inten. place on, put on top; ~ kĩgenyo, accuse sb. falsely; ~ moko, lay on hands (e.g. in the Christian sacrament of confirmation). figirira, refl. inten. I1. hoist (a load) on to the back or head. 2. take on, assume, responsibility without being asked; pretend, lay baseless claim to; ~ gĩcacĩ, expect more attention, respect, than one deserves; ~ kĩgenyo, incriminate oneself; cf.

-igana, p.a. (U), (interrog.) how much ?, how many?; nĩ thaa ci~?, what time is it?; ka~, (enumerative prefix, used adv.) how often?, how many times?; see kaingi.

igana?, v.i. (1), 1. be enough, sufficient; get, come up, to a point, mark, stage; irio ciitũ cia gnũt~, our daily bread; irio nĩcia~, the food is plentiful; -~ atĩa?, (of numbers, size, amount, &c.) how many, how much, how big?; nguo the dress fits her; mũndũ Ũcio ndakũ~, that person is not your equal. 2. in the neg. as aux. v. with infin.; nda~ gũthĩ, he did not get to the point of going, he did not actually go. tigana, refi. igaigana, dim. igananga, diff. iganana, rec. be equal, be of the same size, weight, quantity, &c.; ~ na, be equal to. iganania, caus. rec. make equal. refl. caus. rec.; wrigananie na gĩtugĩ kĩrĩa, size yourself against that post. igananira, assoc. igananiria, caus. assoc. I. coincide. 2. (of people, horses, getting set for a race) stand in a straight line. 3. be half-way, equidistant, reach the middle, be of medium size; see maigananĩrio. iania, caus. make sufficient, have plenty of sth.; cf. hũna. figania, refl. be self-sufficient, self-supporting, independent. iganithia, d. caus. add, bring to desired number, complete. iganika, st. pot. iganĩra, app. be adequate, sufficient, fitting, for; karumu gaka gatingĩ~ arutwo, this room is not big enough for the students; thuruarĩ ĩo ndĩkũn~, these trousers will not fit you; ũkũ~ mahũthiro, a salary sufficient to cover expenses. iganiria, caus. app. make complete for; ~ mũhiki mbũri, complete bridewealth payment. iganĩrira, inten.; ~ matkiirĩ, accumulate debts. iganiriria, caus. inten. make up an amount, balance a deficit.
maigananiro, n. cl. 6 only (1) halfway. [igana?]
maigananu, n. cl 6 (11), equal quantities (e.g. in bartering). [igana?]
maigananirio, n. cl. 6 (1), centre, middle, half-way point. [igana?]
mũiganiriro, mi-, n. (1), a putting on sb., allegation (esp. one that is unfounded), false charge, false witness. [iga]
mũigi, a-, n. (1) one who keeps or puts away ; ~ wa kĩgĩna, see kĩgĩna. [iga]

igiri, see -piri.
e ci-, n. (1), guess, supposition, iga

igua, v.t. (1), 1. hear, heed, listen, obey; wa~ atĩra?, what have you heard, what is the news? 2. be sensitive, feel, feel by touching, smell; nĩwa~ ta me matotu?, did the bananas feel soft to the touch?; ~ kĩeha, feel sad; ~ guoya, be afraid, feel frightened; ~ thoni, feel shy, have a sense of shame, cf. conoka; ~ ngoro ya, have a strong desire for, feel inclined to, cf. ~ thuti, lust after, itch to get at, have a burning desire to lay hands on, cf. cumikira; ~ mũrĩo, have a nice taste in the mouth; ~ thithi, be ticklish, experience a sensation (of the teeth, ears, &c.), feel irritated as a result of a grating movement or sound, haye creepy feelings of fear; ~ gĩkeno, feel glad, happy; ~ wega, feel well, pleased; ~ ũru, feel unwell, bad, grieved; ~ (o) tii, have a splitting headache, buzzing ears; ~ ruo, feel pain; ~ tha, have pity, mercy. figua, refl. come to oneself, become conscious after a stupor; wake up from sleep, come round after fainting; feel better. iguaigua / igaigua, dim. pay a certain amount of attention, give some hearing to. iguanga, diff. iguana, rec. agree with one another, be reconciled, have one mind, be friends. iguanira, app. rec.; nĩtwa~ ũndũ ũy ũmwe, we are agreed on this one point. iguithia, d. caus. cause to feel; help, make, understand, explain. riguithia, refi. d. caus. iguithania, rec. d. caus. iguithanĩria, app. rec. d. caus. be agreed on. iguithanika, st. pot. rec. d. caus. iguika, st. pot. iguĩkana, rec. st. pot.; nĩ inegene, no one can hear the other because of the noise. iguira, app.; ~ nũru, be angry, disappointed with sb.; ~ tha, feel pity for. riguira, refl. app.; ~ tka, have pity on oneself.

mũigua?, a-, n. (1v) one who is obedient; ~-tka, one who is compassionate. n. cl. r4 only, heed, obedience. [igua]
igũrũ (wa), see

(11), feel dissatisfaction about sth., hesitate. ikaika, dim. ikanga, diff. ikania, caus. rec. ikanira, assoc, ikithia, d. caus. fikithia, refl. d. caus. ikĩka, st. pot. ikĩra, app.; cf. ira?, 1 ika / -ike, see -iki. iika, see rika [riika]. kamba, n. with cl. 15 b concords (11), the Kamba country, Ukamba; Mũikamba, A-, n. one of the Kamba tribe; Ikamba, n. cl. 5 only, the Kamba people; ~ rĩrĩ rĩrutaga wĩra Nairobi, these Kamba are working in N. gĩkamba, n. cl. 7 oniy, the Kamba language. nũikania, a-, n. FULL_ENTRY: mũikarirĩria, a-, n. (11), one who looks after sth. for sb. else, guardian, agent. Dikara]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: place where sth. remains, lives, stays
FULL_ENTRY: gĩkaro, ci-, n. (1), place where sth. remains, lives, stays; resting-place, quarters, abiding-place, seat; station, post, ~ kĩmwe kĩ ndaa, staying in the same place begets lice. ũikaro, maũikaro, n. abiding-place, room to stay or live in or at; agia ~, harass sth. ; deny rest, make unsettled; cf. kĩraaro. [ikara]

ikia, v.t. (1) 1. throw; ~ mũrengeti, throw a blanket round the shoulders; ~ be well-stricken in years; ~ mũndũ ciugo, throw hints at sb., say things that touch deep; ~ mĩcobio, interrupt, be offensive in conversation; ~ rtiitho, look intensely, wink, at. 2. shoot; ~ shoot an arrow at sb.; ~ na kano, see under kĩano; ~ na kĩbũikũ, see under kibũnũikn; nĩ wana it is the hot-blocdedness of youth that is throbbing in him. 3. twist (hair into ringlets using two strands only); cf. thogota. 4. in a variety of meanings; rĩa rĩa~ huho, when the sun raises breezes (in the cool of the afternoon); ~ mũndũ moko, njara, ngono, arrest sb.; ~ hĩra, see hĩra; ~ hiriri, see hiriri; ~ matikĩrto, see maikĩrio; ~ matatha, see itatha; ~ eat. 5. shove (with hands), charge (with body as in football); ~ rũhati, iteke, kick sth., sb., ~ thari, kick about when on the point of death. ikio, pass.; ~ nĩ ngoro, see under ngoro. fikia, refl. ikaikia, dim, ikangia, diff. ikania, rec.; ~ moko, fight with the hands. fikania, refl. rec. ikanĩria, app. rec. ikaniriria, inten. rec., set (two or more opponents) against each other. ikithia, d. caus. ikĩka, st. pot. ikĩria, app. 1. throw at, to. 2. (of people at work) take a rest; rest after hard work; ~ hitch up a load on the back into a more comfortable position. ikĩrĩria, inten. 1. throw on to, pile on to. 2. stir up, incite against. 3. add to in order to make up a desired number or quantity. ikũria, conv. harrow (a new garden). ikũrania, rec. conv. 1. unplait, untwist, unbraid (the hair). 2. fight, struggle.

NEW_VALUE: thrower, shover. ~ ndoĩt mwehereri, the flinger does not know what the action of his intended victim will be (whether he will be quick enough to step aside)
FULL_ENTRY: mũikia, a-, n. (1), thrower, shover. ~ ndoĩt mwehereri, the flinger does not know what the action of his intended victim will be (whether he will be quick enough to step aside). [ikia]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: Red-chested cuckoo or Pietmy-vrou (known as ‘rainbird’ because of three-syllabled cry repeated incessantly during wet seasons).

FULL_ENTRY: gaikia-ngũ, tũ-; njikia-ngũ, ~; n. (-u1), Red-chested cuckoo or Pietmy-vrou (known as ‘rainbird’ because of three-syllabled cry repeated incessantly during wet seasons). [ikia]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. little ringlets of hair. 2. suggestions, promptings, impulses. [ikia] mi-, n. (1), one of the long rods attached to the heads of uprights inside a round hut forming low arches which support the rafters half-way up, tie stick, wall plate
FULL_ENTRY: maikio, n. cl. 6 (1), 1. little ringlets of hair. 2. suggestions, promptings, impulses. [ikia] mi-, n. (1), one of the long rods attached to the heads of uprights inside a round hut forming low arches which support the rafters half-way up, tie stick, wall plate; cf. muoho, mũitiriro, (N.K.) mũratho. [ikia]
mũiking’a, a-, n. (u?), alien, foreigner.

NEW_VALUE: pad, usually of leaves, put under a load carried on the back
FULL_ENTRY: gĩkirio, ci-, n. (x1), resting-place, place of respite; halt for rest. maikirio, n. cl. 6 only, pad, usually of leaves, put under a load carried on the back; tkta ~, jerk a carrying-pad into place to ease a load. [ikia]
mũiko, mi-, n. (111), 1. broad-ended mashing-stick. 2. paddle, oar. [Sw. MWIKO]
riiko, see riko. see Mũkũnũmbania.

ikũrũka, v.i. (1) descend, go down, come down; (of river, &c.) flow; cf. harũrũka. ikaikũrũka, dim. ikaikũrũkia, caus. dim. lower a little more; let down further. ikaikũrũkanga, diff. caus. dim. diff. caus. diff. ikũrũkania, caus. rec.; ~ na, go down with sb., go down along (a river, path, &c.). ikũrũkanira, rec. app. ikũrũkaniria, caus. rec. app. ikũrũkia, caus. let down lower. ikũrũkithia, d. caus. ikũrũkika, st. pot. ikũrũkira, app.; ~ kaha, go down here., ikũrũkiria, caus. app. inten. descend towards,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. term for poor gruel or beer, watery stuff. 2. (cl. 6) water
NEW_VALUE: term for poor gruel or beer, watery stuff
FULL_ENTRY: rũikũrũki, ma-, n. cl. 11/6 (1), 1. term for poor gruel or beer, watery stuff. 2. (cl. 6) water; cf. magũ, maĩ. [ikũrũka]
riikũrũkia, ma-, n. (1), 1. marriage feast; cf. ngurario. 2. flow of saliva. [ikũrũka]
gĩkũrũkio, ci-, n. (1), (coll.) a copious drink; cf. kĩhororokio, [ikũrũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: refuse to give sth. to, deny what has been asked for, withhold from
NEW_VALUE: refuse to give to, deny what has been asked for, withhold from
FULL_ENTRY: ima, v.t. (11), refuse to give sth. to, deny what has been asked for, withhold from; cf. karĩha. fima, refi.; ~ deny oneself pleasures. imaima, dim. imanga, diff. imana, rec. imanira, app. rec. imaniria, caus. app. rec. imithia, d. caus. fimithia, refl. d. caus. imika, st. pot. imira, app.
kaimana, n. (1v), (as adv.) completely ; thira ~, be entirely finished.
-imani, a, (111), close, stingy, mean. [ima]

mũimwo, a-, n.(1v), one who is deprived; ~-nĩ-irĩ, one destitute of property (including possessions and descendants), a poor wretch, a goodfor-nothing, scoundrel. mmaimwo, n. cl. 6, times of being deprived. Gũtirĩ ũtaraga maheo no ~, nobody counts his blessings, only the times he has been denied things. ũimwo, n. cl. 14; wickedness, crime. [ima]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. swell. 2. work up for rain. imbaimba, dim. imbanga, diff. imbania, caus. rec, imbanira, rec. app. imbaniria, caus. rec. app. imbia, caus. make swell
FULL_ENTRY: imba!, v.i. (11), 1. swell. 2. work up for rain. imbaimba, dim. imbanga, diff. imbania, caus. rec, imbanira, rec. app. imbaniria, caus. rec. app. imbia, caus. make swell; ndawa ya kũ~ mũgate, yeast, leaven, imbithia, d. caus, imbikz2, st. pot. imbira, app. imbiria, caus. app. imbirĩra, inten. (coll.) get set to; aka~ aguthũke, he was getting ready to dash off. imbũra, conv. take swelling out of sth. imbũka, neut. (of swelling) subside, get cured. imbatara, stc. ten .swell; (of river in flood) rise; cf. hũhita.

imba, v.t. (1), 1. cover over with soil; ~ makara, cover logs with soil for charcoal making; cf. thika, emba. 2. camouflage pitfall or game pit. 3. dig holes for draughts game; see grnthi. imbanga, diff. imbana, rec. 1. incite one another to emulation. 2. play a game of draughts with, imbanĩra, app. rec. imbaniria, caus. app. rec. imbithia, d. caus. imbĩka, st. pot.; cf. umbika. imbira, app.; cf. thikira. imbũria, caus. conv. take charcoal from a pit; see umbũria. imbũka, cf. umbũka.

maimba, n. cl. 6 (1), 1. (N.K.) old-time warriors cape of feathers; cf. riba. 2. mop of split feathers each tied to the lock of a young man’s short hair covering the head completely; cf. njoya. 3. wreath of leaves worm by boys in the ngũcũ dance. [imba?]

maimbira [maimbiira], n. cl. 6 (vini), tree creepers. n. cl. 11 (N.K.) cobwebs, dirt, hanging down in strings from the roof of a disused house. rũimbirira [rnimbiriira], n. cl. 11, mass of creepers enveloping bush or tree.
-imbiriru, a. (x), bulging. [imba]
mũimbo, mi-; (rĩ-), maimbo; kiimbo, ci-; ũimbo, nyimbo; n. (1), a swelling, blain, bleb; cf. mathuguku, nyimba. [imba:] n. cl. 14 only (1), swelling. [imba:]
rĩnabu, n. (111), pineapple. [E.]
kiinaino, ci-, n. (1v), 1. trembling, fright; tiga ~, do not look so frightened. 2. eagerness, eager expectation. Gĩeterero ti ~, patient waiting does not mean fussing. [ina]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: used with v. roots ending in -na, including the rec, extension, as alternative to the -ite Perfect suffix
NEW_VALUE: used with roots ending in -na, including the rec, extension, as alternative to the -ite Perfect suffix
FULL_ENTRY: -ine, v. suffx (U), used with v. roots ending in -na, including the rec, extension, as alternative to the -ite Perfect suffix; nĩtimena~ nao, we are at loggerheads with them; with v. ona the contraction goes further, nĩndamũ~, I had seen him.
ini, (rare) alt. form of imp.; see one under ona.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: expr. proximity, at, near to, by, among, into
NEW_VALUE: proximity, at, near to, by, among, into
FULL_ENTRY: -ini, loc. suffix (U), expr. proximity, at, near to, by, among, into; nyũmba-~, at the house; nyũmba-~ thiini, see under thĩni; tiirania rũthingo-~, prop it against the wall; atĩ njige what am I to put it by?

ORIGINAL_VALUE: drain liquid from a container by drinking, pouring. inainĩka, dim. inĩkanga, diff. inikanira, rec. app. inĩkaniria, caus. rec. app. inĩkia, caus. toss off a draught (inverting the cup). inikithia, d. caus. inĩkĩka, st. pot. inikira, app. inĩkĩrĩra, inten. drain the dregs from sth., drink to the last drop. caus. inten.
