FULL_ENTRY: inĩka, v.t. & i. (1), drain liquid from a container by drinking, pouring. inainĩka, dim. inĩkanga, diff. inikanira, rec. app. inĩkaniria, caus. rec. app. inĩkia, caus. toss off a draught (inverting the cup). inikithia, d. caus. inĩkĩka, st. pot. inikira, app. inĩkĩrĩra, inten. drain the dregs from sth., drink to the last drop. caus. inten.; cf.
mũinikani, a-, n. (1), emptier, person who empties. [inĩka]

NEW_VALUE: when one has stumbled and recovered
FULL_ENTRY: ing, interj. (u), used (mostly by old people): r1. when one has stumbled and recovered. 2. when a tiresome task has been accomplished; ~, reke ndiire mahindi, there we are, now Ĩ can rest my bones.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: go home. inũkanga, diff. go home quickly. inũkania, caus., rec. inũkanira, assoc. inũkaniria, caus. assoc. inũkia, caus.
FULL_ENTRY: inũka', v.i. (1), go home. inũkanga, diff. go home quickly. inũkania, caus., rec. inũkanira, assoc. inũkaniria, caus. assoc. inũkia, caus.; ~ mbũri, drive goats home, from pasture, &c.; cf. umagaria. refl. caus. inũkithia, d. caus, rinũkithia, refl. d. caus. inũkĩka, st. pot. app.; ~ njira go home this way. inũkĩria. caus. app.

NEW_VALUE: place to go home to, place of residence
FULL_ENTRY: mũinũko, mi-, n. (1) going home; mĩnũko ya mbũri, late afternoon, about sunset, when the goats, &c., are taken home; arorete I met him on his way home. ũinũko, n. cl. 14 only, place to go home to, place of residence.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. lift up, tilt, move sth. heavy
FULL_ENTRY: inũra, v.t. (1), 1. lift up, tilt, move sth. heavy; put upright. 2. knock down a supposedly strong man. inainũra, dim. inũranga, diff. inũrana, rec. (of wrestlers in a match) put one another down. inũrania, caus. rec. inũranira, app. rec. lift up together. inũraniria, caus. app. rec. inũrithia, d. caus. inũrika, st. pot. be liftable. inũrira, app. lift up for, at. inũka?, neut. be lifted; be put upright.

indiba, v.i. (1), (N.K.) r. be brim-full; (of receptacle, space, &c.) be crammed full. 2. be satisfactorily completed, settled; be perfectly adjusted; be screwed on right. indibana, rec. inten. of above.
(tũ-) (ũ-) indo, see under

ingata, v.t. (1) drive, chase, away; expel (a student, &c.)}; cf. ruta, rutũrũra, teng’eria. ingatwo, pass. be overwhelmed; see mũingatwo. ingaingata, dim. ingatanga, diff. ingatana, rec. ingatania, caus. rec. ingatanira, app. rec. ingataniria, caus. app. rec. ingatithia, d. caus. tingatithia, refi. d. caus. ingatika, st. pot. ingatira, app.

mũingatwo, a-, n. (X1), one who is driven away. ~ na njũgnma nĩacokaga, no ~ na kĩhooto ndacokaga, he who is driven away by a club returns, but he who is driven away by justice does not return. [ingata]
Mũingeretha, see Mũngeretha.
wingi, mawingi, n. (1), ink. [E.]
-ingi, a. (1v) many, much, more; ka~, often, frequently, many times; see kaigana. -ingaingĩ, a. a fair number of.
ũingihu, mmaũingihu, n. (1v), increase, abundance. [ingiha}]

maingirano, n. cl. 6 (111), joining-up, confluence; ~ ma njira, a road or path junction; cf. macemanio, magomano. [ingira] mi-, n. (11), 1. entrance. 2. junction of small intestine with the large intestine (of special importance in certain ceremonies). [ingira]
rĩngiriro, ma-, n. (111), enteringplace, harbour. [ingira]
inji [iinji], ~, n. (1), inch. [E.]
injini [injini], ~, n. (XVI), engine. [E.] injinia liinjiniya], ~, n. cl. 1/2 (xiv), engineer. [E.]
mũinyabara, see mũnyabara,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 2nd person pl., you, inyu-, form used in conjunction with -eri, -othe, -iki, -ene.
FULL_ENTRY: inyui, indep. p. (U), 2nd person pl., you, inyu-, form used in conjunction with -eri, -othe, -iki, -ene.

iraj, v.i. (11), 1. be black; (of evening) get gradually dark; (of skin) be darkcoloured; ~ riitho, be dull-eyed, have difniculty in seeing; ~ maitho, be dazzled, blinded, by glare, long looking or search, weeping; ~ ngoro, feel bilious, have sick feeling; ~ curu, ciri, biri, be absolutely black, dark. 2. menstruate; cf. ona ĩnenũra. iraira, dim. iranga, diff. irania, caus, rec. iranira, assoc. iranĩria, caus. assoc. iria, caus., make dark, nauseate. firia, refl. caus. irithia, d. caus. stain black (by steeping in water and treating with mud or juice). irika, st. pot. irĩra, app. irĩria, caus. app. disappear, get out of sight at a distance. irĩrĩra, inten.; see also cina. irũgrũka, neut. conv. belch. irũka, neut. have hiccups.
mũira!, &-, n. (1), 1. eye-witness; one who gives evidence. 2. (usu. ref. to sb. in disfavour or known to have committed an offence) the person in mind. Gira, maũira, n. testimony, evidence, witness.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (freq. used as conj. foll. by v. in subj. or fut., with or without the -a of relationship preceding) while, until
FULL_ENTRY: mũira?, n. cl. 3 only (1), 1. (freq. used as conj. foll. by v. in subj. or fut., with or without the -a of relationship preceding) while, until; ~ wa kiu gĩkaneneha, until the lad grows up; ikara haha ~ (wa) ndĩe, stay here while I eat. 2. kaara ka ~, the little finger. n. cl. 13 only, used as in 1. above.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: aloofness, belittling of others. Ũirano, n. cl. 14 only, looking down on, snobbishness
FULL_ENTRY: kĩrano, ci-, n. (111), aloofness, belittling of others. Ũirano, n. cl. 14 only, looking down on, snobbishness; mutual contempt. [ira?]
-iri, a. (111), tending to be dissatisfied. [ira?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: Red stinkwood (Pygeum africanum) (a tall timber-tree used for making digging sticks, beer mortars, bridge poles, furniture, wagons, &c.).
NEW_VALUE: Red stinkwood (Pygeum africanum) (a tall timber-tree used for making digging sticks, beer mortars, bridge poles, furniture, wagons, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũiri! [mũiiri] / (N.k.) mũiria, mi-, n. (11), Red stinkwood (Pygeum africanum) (a tall timber-tree used for making digging sticks, beer mortars, bridge poles, furniture, wagons, &c.). riiri, ma-, n. leaf of this tree. [ira"]
mũiri?, see mũiru.

NEW_VALUE: dwindling half light, twilight, deep shade, gloom
FULL_ENTRY: kĩria, ci-, n. (v), a dark spot, dark shadow; ~ kĩa mũndũ, a mere shadow of a man; ~ kĩa ngoro, nausea, feeling of disgust or loathing. kairia, tũ-, n. dark spot, dot, jot; dark shadow or shape (as of an object seen in the dark, a thorn under the skin, &c.). mairia, n. cl. 6, dwindling half light, twilight, deep shade, gloom; ona na ~, see indistinctly; ~-inĩ, kwagĩa ~, twilight time, period just before dawn or just after sunset; cf. ithukĩ ciatuĩka andũ, that. [ira"]

iriga, v.t. (I1) fence, plant a hedge; construct a fence, railing, &c. (to partition a house or field, or to keep out intruders). iriganga, diff. irigana, rec. irigania, caus. rec. iriganira, app. rec. iriganiria, caus. app. rec. irigika, st. pot. irigira, app. encircle. irigirira, inten. 1. stand round, mob (sb.). 2. fence round. irigũra, conv. remove a fence round sth. irigũka, neut. become unfenced (through the breaking down or removal of a fence).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: stockade, fence. ci-, n. fenced place, stockade, stronghold
FULL_ENTRY: rũirigo, njirigo, n. (11), stockade, fence. ci-, n. fenced place, stockade, stronghold ; cf. rũgiri, [iriga]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (used adv.) out of sight, far and fast
NEW_VALUE: out of sight, far and fast
FULL_ENTRY: rũirii, n. cl. 11 (VIII), (used adv.) out of sight, far and fast; see irĩria.
tũirio, see under irio.
irithi, ~, n. (11), modern antidote against magic. [Sw. HIRIZI]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a skin complaint of human beings.
NEW_VALUE: a skin complaint of human beings
FULL_ENTRY: kiiri, ci-, n. (1), a skin complaint of human beings.

NEW_VALUE: girlhood, state of being a young unmarried woman
FULL_ENTRY: mũiritu [mũiritu], &-, n. (1v), fullgrown girl, not yet married; initiated girl; ~ wa mhando, newly initiated girl. kiiritu, ci-, n. big, plain-looking or otherwise unattractive girl. kĩmũiritu, i-, n. large ungainly girl. rũiritu, n. company of initiated girls, bevy of maidens (used in slightly derogatory way). kairĩtu, tũ-, n. young, small, initiated girl; young girl, cf. kĩrigũ, karĩgũ. Ũirĩtu, n. cl. 14 only, 1. girlhood, state of being a young unmarried woman. 2. things belonging to a girl, ornaments, finery, equipment.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: remedy, cure for sth., strengthening food helpful to a sick person or animal, fresh blood and honey given to pregnant women for pains of pregnancy
NEW_VALUE: remedy, cure for , strengthening food helpful to a sick person or animal, fresh blood and honey given to pregnant women for pains of pregnancy
FULL_ENTRY: mairo!, n. cl. 6 (111), remedy, cure for sth., strengthening food helpful to a sick person or animal, fresh blood and honey given to pregnant women for pains of pregnancy; see Qũaki, nyama, ngaita, thakame, mĩti.
mairo?, n. cl. 6 (111), disdain, contempt. Mũtino ndri ~, misfortune cannot be rejected with contempt. [ira?]
mairo? | mairũ, see mairo,
mũirong’e, mi-, n. (11), shilling.

mũiru /(N.K.) mũiri?, a-, n. (11), 1. companion, partner, fellow; ~ witũ wa itũra, our neighbour. 2. any concomitant; rũgi ~, a river without a tributary. 3. co-wife; ~we, ~guo, ~wa, her, your, my, co-wife. kairu, tũ-, n. (term of address, also used by woman to husband’s classificatory wives) dear co-wife.
ũiru, n. cl. 14 only (11), jealousy, envy. [ira?]
iruga, see uruga,
mairutĩ, n. cl. 6 only (111), (N.K.) warrior’s feather-cape; cf. riba, maimba. [cf. Ma. OL-MAIRUTYE]
irũ / urũ, interj. (U), 1. used to announce one’s arrival; cf. i!, ii! 2. used when one is thrown off balance and narrowly escapes falling.

mũirũ!, mĩ-, n. (11), dark colour; menstrual period, cf. mweri; e na ~, she is menstruating ; ciuma cia ~, dark blue beads; maguta ma ~, pure oil. ũirũ, n. cl. 14 only, darkness of colour, blackness, duskiness. [ira!]
mũirũ?, mĩ-, n. (111), tall timber-tree (Psychotria sp.).
ũirũkiri, n. cl. 14 only (111), hopelessness, [ira?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. loss of hope, despair, loss of patience
NEW_VALUE: loss of hope, despair, loss of patience
FULL_ENTRY: kĩrũkiro, ci-, n. (111), 1. loss of hope, despair, loss of patience; cf. 2. sb., sth., repulsive, displeasing; umai haha, ciirũkĩro ii, go away, you nuisances. [ira?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. tall timbertree with buttressed trunk (Casearia battiscombei.). 2. small tree (Catha edulis). ~, n. fruit of C. edulis, inedible. rĩrũngi, ma-, n. leaf of C. edulis in particular (known to Somalis as qat, contains habitforming drug used by Masai as well as Somalis as an aphrodisiac and to prevent sleep while escorting sheep). n. (v), the name of the generation succeeding Mwangi, sometimes spoken of as Maina Kanyi
NEW_VALUE: tall timbertree with buttressed trunk (Casearia battiscombei
FULL_ENTRY: mũirũngi, i-, n. (111), 1. tall timbertree with buttressed trunk (Casearia battiscombei.). 2. small tree (Catha edulis). ~, n. fruit of C. edulis, inedible. rĩrũngi, ma-, n. leaf of C. edulis in particular (known to Somalis as qat, contains habitforming drug used by Masai as well as Somalis as an aphrodisiac and to prevent sleep while escorting sheep). n. (v), the name of the generation succeeding Mwangi, sometimes spoken of as Maina Kanyi; it is the generation which would have been in power in the middle years of the twentieth century if the Ituĩka system had still been operating; see Ituika, Maina. mũirũngũ, a-, n. a man belonging to the Irangũ generation set.

irũra, v.t. (11), 1. knock down (maize); lay sb. low (with a weapon); fell (an ox, goat). 2. kill sb. by witchcraft; destroy utterly. irairũra, dim. irũranga, diff. irũrana, rec. irũrania, caus. rec. irũranira, app. rec, irũraniria, caus. app. rec. irũria, caus. irũrithia, d. caus. irũrĩka, st. pot. irũrĩra, app. irũrĩria, caus. app. irũrũra, conv. neut. irũcaus. neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a timber-tree with black bark (Diospyros and Strombosia grandifolia).
NEW_VALUE: a timber-tree with black bark (Diospyros and Strombosia grandifolia)
FULL_ENTRY: mũirũthi, mĩ-, n. (11), a timber-tree with black bark (Diospyros and Strombosia grandifolia).

itat, v.t. & i. (1), I. pour out, spill (liquids); shed tears, hair, leaves, fruit, &c.); throw, let, out; ~ njoya, drip with perspiration; ~ dribble, slobber. 2. leak; the pot has a leak. itaita, dim. itaitithia, d. caus. dim, itaitika, st. pot. dim. itaitĩkĩra, app. st. pot. dim. itaitĩkĩria, caus. app. st. pot. dim. itaitira, app. dim. itaitĩrĩra, inten. dim. itaitĩrĩria, caus. inten. dim. neut. dim, subside a little. itanga, diff. itana, rec. itania, caus. rec. itanira, app. rec. itaniria, caus. app. rec. itithia, d. caus. itĩka, st. pot. I1. be spilt, poured out; (of fluids, flour, anything packed) run out, overflow; (of hair) fall out; mbura ĩtĩkite wega, it is really pouring with rain. 2. (as aux. v. foll. by infin.) begin to; agĩ~ kwaria, all at once he started to speak. itikia, caus. st. pot. itikana, rec. st. pot.; ~ na, fall to, get to work on, get down to, start. eating or doing sth.; cf. rika, arũka. itikania, caus. rec. st. pot. itikanira, app. rec. st. pot. itikanĩria, caus. app. rec. st, pot. itĩkĩra, app. st. pot. spill out on; fall, drop, drip on, upon. itĩkĩria, caus. app. st. pot. itikĩrira, inten. st. pot. go down towards, descend upon. app. pour, spill, throw, for, at. nitira, refl. app. itiranga, diff. app. itirana, rec. app. itĩranĩra, app. assoc. itĩraniria, caus. app. assoc. itirira, inten. 1. pour out into; ria Cania rũitĩriire ria Thika, the C. flows into the T. river. 2. begin; nyoni cia~, see under nyoni. 3. add, put on; ~ nyũmba, fit rafters on a house, titirira, refl. inten. (of cattle, &c.) flock into (a garden, &c.). itĩriranga, diff. inten. itirĩrana, rec. inten. itirirania, caus. rec. inten. itirĩranira, rec. app. inten. itĩriraniria, caus. rec. app. inten. itirĩria, caus. inten. pour out on, spill over, refl. caus. inten. itĩrĩrithia, d. caus. inten. itũrũra, inten. (conv.). itnrũria, caus. inten. 1. splash about wastefully. 2. perform a cleansing ceremony. Ĩtũrũria, refl. caus. inten. itũrũrũka, neut, inten. gush out copiously ; (of ants, people, &c.) pour out; cf. thothoka, umũrũrũka., neut, 1. have an emission (of semen). 2. (of a river, &c.) subside.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1st pers. pl., we. ithu-, form used in conjunction with -eri, -othe, -iki, -ene. mĩ-, n. (11), 1. girts walking-stick. 2. FULL_ENTRY: ithui, indep. p. (U), 1st pers. pl., we. ithu-, form used in conjunction with -eri, -othe, -iki, -ene. mĩ-, n. (11), 1. girts walking-stick. 2. (N.K.) a bride’s stick (nicely carved and preserved in her enclosed bed, produced at her daughters circumcision for use until her initiation is completed). 3. stick carried by women and brandished in anger; back scratcher; see thũiya.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. bring about a customary reciprocal process
FULL_ENTRY: itbũra, v.t. (11), I. bring about a customary reciprocal process; yield, produce, part with, sth. valuable (thereby causing the recipient to be under an obligation). 2. (of one who tends a dying person expecting to have a share of his possessions, &c.) do sth. in expectation of getting sth. in return. 3. buy (beer to be used for traditional ceremonies, &c.); measure out (beer, lablat beans) in small quantities when selling in time of scarcity; (of a cow) yield milk grudgingly; I am going to relieve myself. ithũrũo, pass. 1. be |; made to yield sth. valuable; (of sth. |. scarce or valuable) be obtained, secured, drawn out. 2. be made to enter into a customary reciprocal process (e.g. when one is given sth. valuable and is obliged to return the compliment. 3. (of a cow which has just given birth) have the second calabash of colostrum drunk so that anyone can drink its milk. ithaithũra, dim. part |1 with sth. valuable more easily; measure out in more generous quantities. | i ithũranga, diff. ithũranira, app. rec. ithũria, caus. sell (beer) to. itkũrithia, d. caus. 1. cause sth. to be measured out; make share out, make part with sth. valuable. 2. cause sth. to come about (esp. a customary reciprocal process). ithũrika, st. pot. ithũrĩra, app. put in debt to.
iũhia, see icũhia.

NEW_VALUE: of rattling of branches as sb
FULL_ENTRY: karagaca / keregece, ideo. (1), descr. of rattling of branches as sb. forces way through bush, clatter of wooden door-bars, falling calabashes, plates, or spoons, rattle of swords of young men fencing; gamba, hanyũka, (0) ~, ũthĩ o kwa N., get cracking to N.s place.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: walk on a gravel road, scamper, trot.

FULL_ENTRY: makaragatha / ngaragatha, n. cl. 6 or 10 (v), bits of straw, &c., in gruel, husks in flour, dry stems among plucked sweet potato vines; toro wa ~, nap, short sleep.

mũkarakaba, n. (xiv), tree (Cupressus macrocarpa) (introduced in recent times).

NEW_VALUE: number equality, counting into two or more lots
FULL_ENTRY: karakara, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1., number equality, counting into two or more lots; ¿ganana ~, be equal in number. 2. opening of the gullet; nĩ ngi ~, flies were swarming round him.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: small tree of light brown hue (Rhamnus prunoides) providing 1.
NEW_VALUE: withies used in construction of grain-stores and for necklets worn by males in initiation ceremony
FULL_ENTRY: mũkarakinga, mĩ-, n. (X11), small tree of light brown hue (Rhamnus prunoides) providing 1. withies used in construction of grain-stores and for necklets worn by males in initiation ceremony. 2. bitter medicine brewed from leaves or twigs as remedy for rheumatism and bile troubles.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a bush plant from whose roots a crimson dye is extracted.
NEW_VALUE: a bush plant from whose roots a crimson dye is extracted
FULL_ENTRY: gakarakũ, tũ-, n. (v), a bush plant from whose roots a crimson dye is extracted.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. start, jump, in angry surprise or alarm as when sth. falls on one from above. 2. be aggressive, truculent, fearless, outspoken. karambira, app. be truculent towards, outspoken to
NEW_VALUE: start, jump, in angry surprise or alarm as when falls on one from above
FULL_ENTRY: karamba, v.i. (11), I. start, jump, in angry surprise or alarm as when sth. falls on one from above. 2. be aggressive, truculent, fearless, outspoken. karambira, app. be truculent towards, outspoken to; thenge ĩgĩ~ iria ingĩ, the he-goat was aggressive towards the others.
karambaa [karambaa], ~, n. (xiv), colour-bar. [E.]
karambi, see under karambi.
karanga, see under njũgũ.
rũkaranyũ, ngaranyũ, n. (v), scratch, abrasion drawing blood. [karanyũka]

FULL_ENTRY: karara, v.t. (1) scratch with a point, cut lightly with the tip of sth.; make an incision; cut, in, on, across, round ; slit, score; ~ make an incision in a raw banana skin (before peeling it); cf. kurura, hara. ĩkarara, refl. kararanga, diff. kararana, rec. kararanira, app. rec. kararaniria, caus. app. rec. kararithia, d. caus. kararĩka, st. pot. kararĩra, app. kararũka, neut. kararũkia, caus. neut. kararũkika, st. pot. neut. kararũkira, app. neut.
mũkarara!, mĩ-, n. (1), a tall deciduous tree (Phyllanthus discoideus).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. a long scratch, a line made by scratching
NEW_VALUE: a long scratch, a line made by scratching
FULL_ENTRY: mũkarara? | mũkararo, mi-, n. (1), I1. a long scratch, a line made by scratching; ~ wa mũbira, bicycle tread made on soft ground; cf. mũkaro, mũkururo. 2. stripe, band of colour; nguo ya ~, striped cloth. rũkarara / ngarara, ngarara, n. 1. light cut, incision, score, scratch. 2. striation, stripe on animal’s skin; cf. mrnkia. [karara]
mũkararai, mĩ-, n. (1), quill of a feather.
karati [kaarati], ~, n. (1v), carrot. [E.]
ũkaratha, see Ũkuratha.
maũkarathing’a, see karathing’a.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: mighty man of valour, strong man
FULL_ENTRY: karau, ma-, n. cl. 14/6, usu. with cl. 1/2 concords. (1), mighty man of valour, strong man; cf. njamba, mũti wa Kenya, ikandaũ, tkindũi, tkinũ, ũkarau, maũkarau / mokarau [mookarau], n. valour, prowess; act of valour., [see kara?]
rũkari n. cl. 1x, (1x?), smell of frying; see karanga.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: crunch, munch, chew (hard things, raw potatoes, &c.)
NEW_VALUE: crunch, munch, chew (hard things, raw potatoes, &c
FULL_ENTRY: Karia, v.t. (11), crunch, munch, chew (hard things, raw potatoes, &c.); cf. kaburia, thathura, kanda. karakaria, dim. karangia, diff. karania, rec. karaniria, app. rec. karithia, d. caus. karika, st. pot. karĩria, app. karĩriria, inten.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sound of the piece of resonant metal used for tapping in present-day dances (like a triangle).
NEW_VALUE: sound of the piece of resonant metal used for tapping in present-day dances (like a triangle)
FULL_ENTRY: karikari-ka, ideo. (1), sound of the piece of resonant metal used for tapping in present-day dances (like a triangle).
ikariũngũ, see kĩariũngũ.
kari (N.K.), see kai.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: mean, niggardly, avaricious, ungenerous person. Ũkari, (moekari) [mookarĩ], n. stinginess, meanness, miserliness
FULL_ENTRY: mũkari, a-, n. (111), mean, niggardly, avaricious, ungenerous person. Ũkari, (moekari) [mookarĩ], n. stinginess, meanness, miserliness; [see karĩha]
karinga, see karinga.
mũkaro, mi-, n. (1), 1. ditch, furrow, trench; lead for a water-mill; cf. mũtaro. 2. name used in former days for Nyeri government station. [kara"]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: name given to a strong bull that likes fighting.
NEW_VALUE: name given to a strong bull that likes fighting
FULL_ENTRY: gikaru, i-, n. (11), name given to a strong bull that likes fighting. (mokaru [mookaru]), n. majesty, splendour, greatness, importance. [kara?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: name of a river in South Kikuyu, used by people outside the area to refer to the whole of S.K.
NEW_VALUE: name of a river in South Kikuyu, used by people outside the area to refer to the whole of S
FULL_ENTRY: Karura, n. with locative concords, (vi), name of a river in South Kikuyu, used by people outside the area to refer to the whole of S.K.
gakaruri, see under ngaruri,
gĩkaruthi, see under ngaruthi.,
karũ [kaarũ], ~, n. (1), 1. war, fighting; mbogo ĩrĩ ~ mũno, the buffalo is very fierce. 2. (as a.) strong ; andũ arĩa nĩ ~, those men are powerful.
mũkati, see mũrukwati.

NEW_VALUE: mi-, n Gu), long drawn-out cry, scream
FULL_ENTRY: mũkaũrũrũko [mũkayũrũrũko] / mũkaũrũko [mũkayũrũko], mi-, n Gu), long drawn-out cry, scream. [kaya"]
mũkawa! [mukaawa], mi-, n. (V), coffee shop, eating-house, hotel. [Sw. MKAHAWA]

NEW_VALUE: thorny shrub (Carissa edulis) (has clusters of white or pinky-white flowers)
FULL_ENTRY: mũkawa? mi-, n. (v), 1. thorny shrub (Carissa edulis) (has clusters of white or pinky-white flowers). 2. small spreading tree (Elaeodendron aethiopicum | Cassine aethiopica). ngawa, ~, n. bright red edible fruit of 2. above.

kaya!, v.i. (11), scream, yell, howl; cry out, bewail, moan, protest noisily; cf. cakaya, caaya?, ania, uga mbu. kayakaya, dim. kayakaiya, caus, dim, 1. howl down, contradict sb. noisily, protest against a speaker noisily, heckle sb., keep on interrupting sb. vociferously. 2, make go on screaming, &c. kayakaika, st. pot. dim. ; nda~ga rĩrĩa e kwaria, you cannot shout him down when he is speaking. kayakaira, app. dim. beseech sb. for help again and again. kayanga, diff. kayania, caus. rec. kayanira, assoc. I. scream, cry out, together, 2. cry for help from others, seek help from one another. kayanĩria, caus, assoc. kaiya [kayia], caus. make cry out; make (birds, dogs) utter cries of alarm; cf. anithia. kaithia, d, caus. st. pot. kaĩkĩra, app. st. pot. ; ndangĩ~, it is not possible to appeal to him for help. kaĩra, app. appeal for help from; ~ Ngai, ask for grace from God. kaĩrĩria, caus. inten. shout, call loudly, to, at; (of a sentry) challenge. / (N.K.) kaneut. conv. inten. scream, yell, call out at the top of one’s voice; shout loudly, cheer wildly; cf. ugĩriria, anũka. kayũrũrũkia, caus. neut. conv. inten. make yell out, scream, shout excitedly. (kayũka, neut. scream, shout loudly.)
ikaya, ma-, n. (111), cry, yell, of pain, &c. [kaya!]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cry for help, cry of pain.
NEW_VALUE: cry for help, cry of pain
FULL_ENTRY: mũkayo!, mĩ-, n. (111), cry for help, cry of pain.

FULL_ENTRY: mũkayo, mi-, n. (1), 1. scourge made from brambles, thongs, &c. 2. (N.K.) strap decorated with beads worn by girls over the shoulder or round the waist. ikayo, ma-, n. 1. scourge, whip of one or more thongs used in boys’ pre-circumcision dance. 2. piece of twisted hide fastened on a stick; ~ rĩa mbogo, piece of twisted hide. [kaya?]
ke! / (N.K.) ka, v. (u), (only in imper.) take; ta ~ itimũ rĩaku, here you are, take your spear.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: alt. form of 3rd pers. s. (-i, verb ‘to be’ with cl. 12 prefix)
NEW_VALUE: the child is in the house
FULL_ENTRY: ke?, v. (U), alt. form of 3rd pers. s. (-i, verb ‘to be’ with cl. 12 prefix); kaana ~ nyũmba, the child is in the house; see -ri.

NEW_VALUE: of the warning crack of a tree about to break or fall
FULL_ENTRY: ke, ideo. (U), descr. of the warning crack of a tree about to break or fall. keke, ideo. the preliminary creaking of a tree partially cut through or getting near the breaking-point.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 3rd pers. s., only used when suffixed to the part. na and ta
FULL_ENTRY: -ke, indep. p. (U), 3rd pers. s., only used when suffixed to the part. na and ta; see also we. kea!, see under kiea.
kea2?, see under kiia.
kebe / kebebe, ideo. (1), 1. brimful, chock-full; cf. hĩbĩbĩ, bĩbĩ, kibi, mũngang’a. 2. completely; unwo guoko ~, have one’s arm shattered to pieces.
gakee, see under ngee,
ikeera, ma-,n.(I111), a straining. [keera]
mũkeera, n. (111); mweri wa ~, clear, brilliant moon. [keera]
gĩkeero | gikeeri, i-, n. (111), x. filter, strainer; cf. gĩcungi. 2. loud-speaker horn, megaphone; cf. kĩrhehe. [keera]
kega-ta-kĩ, see under -eega.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: place apart, where sth. is out of the way
NEW_VALUE: place apart, where is out of the way
FULL_ENTRY: keheri-inĩ [keeheri-inĩ], tweheri-ini, n. cl. r2/13 (11), place apart, where sth. is out of the way; zga ~, keep apart; cf. mwanya, kĩeheri-ini, ci-, n. aug. of above, aside, apart out of sight. [ehera?]
mũkehũ, see mũheehũ.
kei, see under -ĩ.

FULL_ENTRY: keki [keeki] / geki [geeki], ~, n. (1), 1. cake, 2. marsh cake, &c., used for feeding stock and farm animals. 3. remains of food esp. mealie mea! stodge left to bake or burn brown on the pot after cooking; see ngima. [E.]
rũkemi, see under ngemi.

kemba, v.t. & i. (11), 1. strain (liquids); push back slime on stagnant water and draw in a dipper ; make clear, use one’s teeth to stop bits getting into the mouth when drinking; ~ drink gruel heartily; cf. keera, taha, tagia, kundũkia, cenga. 2. (of fluids, fat, &c.) be clear, limpid, pure; ~ (of animals and humans) be in good condition, be healthy-looking, clearskinned, clean, plump, shiny. kembakemba, dim. kembanga, diff, kembania, caus. rec. kembanira, app. rec. & assoc. kembaniria, caus. app. rec. kembithia, d. caus. kembeka, st. pot. kembera, app. kembũra, conv. kembũka, neut.
kemba, see under rũemba.
kembo, n. (111), (N.K.); ~, smallleaf-shaped spear; cf. krando, kĩberethi.
-kembu, a (1v), clear, limpid, pure. [kemba]

NEW_VALUE: name given to a cheerful person
FULL_ENTRY: ikena, ma-, n. (H1), I. rejoicing. 2. (cl. 6) name given to a cheerful person. [kena]
gĩkeni, i-; keni, cl. 14; n. (111), joy, happiness, pleasure. [kena]
keni, see under kĩeni.
-keni, a. (111), happy, joyous. [kena]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. cl, 1/2 (v11), 1. one who pleases, gladdens
FULL_ENTRY: gakenia, (tũ-), n. cl, 1/2 (v11), 1. one who pleases, gladdens; woman or girl of pleasing, happy disposition; cf. kĩgotho. 2. playful nickname for mother (freq. in songs); ~ maitũ wanjiarire, darling mother who brought me into the world. [see kena]
kenda? / getha, n. (1); nĩ ~, in order that (foll. by subj.).
mũkenga:!, a-, n. (1), one who hoodwinks. ~ ago oigaga mĩrimũ nĩyathirire, he that would hoodwink the medicine men says that all illnesses have come to an end. Cf. moheenia. [kenga?]
mũkenga?, n. cl. 3 (1), clear green glass beads. [cf. kenga!]
kenga (N.K.), see gacenga.
mũkenga-mbura, see mũkũngambura,
makengemara, n. cl. 6 (111), astonishment, amazement. [kengema]

NEW_VALUE: weed (Commeline ) (has jointed fleshy stems which creep over the ground and small flowers like little faces with large distended ears, hence name Micky Mouse or Listening Willie
FULL_ENTRY: kengere, see under kiengere, mĩ-, n. (vii), weed (Commeline sp.) (has jointed fleshy stems which creep over the ground and small flowers like little faces with large distended ears, hence name Micky Mouse or Listening Willie; used for treating pendent foreskin after circumcision, see ngwati); cf. gatumia.

kengeũka, v.i. (11), r. (of sun) shine out; cf. kenga!. 2. rejoice greatly; be happy, glad; cf. kena. 3. laugh hilariously, give vent to cries of joy; cf. kenũka, canjamũũka. kengeũra, v.t. cause to be jubilant, elated, mirthful; ni kĩ?, why this mirth (when there is death in the village)?
mũkengeũko, mi-; ngengeũko, ~; n., (111), unrestrained merriment, boisterous laughter.
ũkengi, n. cl. 14 brilliance, scintillation ; glint, coruscation, splendour, lustre. [kenga']
kengo, see under kiengo.
rũkengo, (ngengo), n. (11), glare. [kenga!]
-kengu, a. (1v), bright, shining, gleaming.

kenya, v.i. (11), 1. (of small things, precious metals) glitter; cf. kenga'!. 2. dawn, get light; cf. kĩa, ratha, thũa. kenyakenya, dim. (of tears in eyes, blood from wound, water in damp spot) begin to well up; (of weeds) begin to appear and give the ground a green tinge. kenyakenyũka, neut. dim. recover strength. kenyanga, diff. kenyũka, neut. 1. cheer up, be glad, look gay. 2. (of sky, mountains, &c.) be, become, clear of clouds, mist. kenyũkanga, diff. kenyũkana, rec. kenyũkania, caus. rec, kenyũkanira, assc. kenyũkanĩria, caus. assoc. kenyũkia, caus. kenyũkithia, d. caus. kenyũkika, st. pot. kenyũkira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of food) begin to cook, be roasted.
NEW_VALUE: (of food) begin to cook, be roasted
FULL_ENTRY: kenyat, v.i. (1), (of food) begin to cook, be roasted.

NEW_VALUE: necklace made of small discoidal fragments of ostrich shells, worn by uninitiated lads at the ngũcũ dance, 3
FULL_ENTRY: ikenye, ma-, n. (1v) 1. ornament made of ostrich egg-shell. 2. (cl. 6) necklace made of small discoidal fragments of ostrich shells, worn by uninitiated lads at the ngũcũ dance, 3. speck, spot; nguo ĩ na makenye, a spotted dress. ngenye, ~, n. I. small piece of perforated ostrich egg; ~, there is absolutely nothing; cf. ngenyakenyi, nyuri. 2. name given to a cow or ewe having a white spot on the forehead. gĩkenye, i-, n. aug. of above. nyagikenye, ~, n. variety of sweet potato. mũkenye, mi-, n. a beaded headstrap ; cf. mũnyiro.
kenyũ, see under kienyũ.

NEW_VALUE: a kind of) bead (small and valuable)
FULL_ENTRY: ikenyũa, (ma-), n. (vir), a kind of) bead (small and valuable). Thi ndĩoyagwo na ~, possession of land is not obtained without an effort. Cf. iruku.

kera, v.t. (1) chop, cut up (spinach, onions, &c.) for cooking; cf. renga, tinia. kerũo, pass.; ~ nĩ nda, have recurrent sharp pains in the stomach. kerakera, dim. kerakerithia, d. caus. dim. keranga, diff. kerana, rec. kerania, caus, rec. keranira, app. rec. keranĩria, caus. app. rec. kerithia, d. caus. kereka, st. pot. I1. begin to split, snap; become cracked. 2. (of a wasp’s nest) become narrowed. 3. (of the skin, &c.) have a mark left as the result of a strong ligature. kerera, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of cutting across country (as a light-footed person for the sake of speed). kerekere, ideo. descr. of
FULL_ENTRY: kere, ideo. (1/11), descr. of cutting across country (as a light-footed person for the sake of speed). kerekere, ideo. descr. of: 1. light-footed running like a dog. 2. the twitter of the ngerekeri bird which tells of the coming of a stranger or visitor who will bring good or bad luck; cf. kekekeke.
makereere, see ngereere,
keregece, see karagaca.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: famine food, gruel made from unripe millet.
NEW_VALUE: famine food, gruel made from unripe millet
FULL_ENTRY: ũkereke, n. cl. 14 only (111), famine food, gruel made from unripe millet.
kerenga, v.t. (1) set a trap; snare (birds); cf. tega, gwata, kerenganira, app. rec. kerenganiria, caus. app. rec. kerengithia, d. caus. kerengeka, st. pot. kerengera, app.
keri, see under ka-2.
kerigi, see under kĩrigi.
kero [keero], n. cl. o (1), disinfectant, Jeyes fluid. [E. KEROL]
gĩkero, i-, n. (1), 1. gnawing, cutting, sensation; ~ kĩa nda, anxiety, grief, painful feeling caused by anxiety. 2. pain in woman’s abdomen after childbirth. [kera]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: empty out, drain water from cooked food, &c.
NEW_VALUE: empty out, drain water from cooked food, &c
FULL_ENTRY: kerũra?, v.t. (11), empty out, drain water from cooked food, &c.; ~ njohi, transfer beer from big calabashes to smaller gourds for distribution or serving; cf. hiha. kerakerũra / kerũrakerũra, dim. kerũranga, dif. kerũrania, caus. rec. kerũranira, app. rec. caus. app. rec. kerũrithia, d. caus. kerũrika, st. pot. kerũrĩra, app. kerũririra, inten.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: man called to strain beer from the fermenting calabash.
NEW_VALUE: man called to strain beer from the fermenting calabash
FULL_ENTRY: mũkerũri, a-, n. (111), man called to strain beer from the fermenting calabash. [kerũra?]
gakerũro, see under ngerũro.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. clear castoroil at first stage of extraction
NEW_VALUE: clear castoroil at first stage of extraction
FULL_ENTRY: makerũro, n. cl. 6 (111), 1. clear castoroil at first stage of extraction; cf. manjaguca. 2. water poured off from gruel which has stood and become cold; water or other fluid decanted from anything. [kerũra?]

keũka, v.i. (1) x1. (of skin, leather, metal, &c.) develop cracks. 2. be splayed. keũnkakeũka, dim, keũkanga, diff. keũkania, caus. rec. keũnkanĩra, assoc. keũkia, caus. keũkithia, d. caus. keũkika, st. pot. keũra, v.t. splay out, open out. keũnrakeũra, dim. keũranga, diff. keũrana, rec, keũrania, caus. rec. keũranĩria, caus. app. rec. keũrithia, d. caus. keũrĩka, st. pot. keũrira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: expr. the idea of exactness, precision
FULL_ENTRY: ki / kii, ideo. (U), expr. the idea of exactness, precision; kira ~, keep absolutely quiet.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. make, prepare, gruel (by soaking grain, pounding it in mortar, grinding it between two stones, soaking paste again, straining it and cooking it into thin, smooth, gruel)
NEW_VALUE: make, prepare, gruel (by soaking grain, pounding it in mortar, grinding it between two stones, soaking paste again, straining it and cooking it into thin, smooth, gruel)
FULL_ENTRY: kia [kiya], v.t. (11), 1. make, prepare, gruel (by soaking grain, pounding it in mortar, grinding it between two stones, soaking paste again, straining it and cooking it into thin, smooth, gruel); ~ prepare gruel by mixing grain with water, pounding it, &c. 2. make, mix, clay for plastering; ~ ndoro, prepare daub for plastering walis. kiakia, dim. kianga, diff. kiana, rec. kiania, caus. rec. 1. mix soil and tread on it haphazardly. 2. be messy. kianĩra, app. rec. kianiria, caus. app. rec. kiithia, d. caus. kĩra, app. 1. make gruel for sb. (a mark of respect). 2. make daub for sb. kĩka, st. pot. kiũka, neut. 1. become clear; cf. enjũaka?. 2. understand; cf. hubũka. kiũkanga, diff. neut. kiũkana, rec. neut. 1. become completely clear. 2. understand completely, have vivid recollection of event. kiũkania, caus. rec, neut. kiũkanĩra, assoc. neut, kiũkaniria, caus. assoc. neut. kiũkia, caus. neut. kiũkithia, d. caus. kiũkĩka, st. pot. kiũkĩra, app. neut. kiũkiria, caus. app. neut.
ikia, ma-, n, (111), a preparation of gruel. [kia]
kiara, see kĩara,
gĩkiatho, see under mairĩti.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of a space, vessel, &c.) be, become, filled up
FULL_ENTRY: kibana, v.i. (1), 1. (of a space, vessel, &c.) be, become, filled up; be, become, entirely enclosed; cf. kibibana, cingimana, 2. (rare) knock together, collide, fight. 3. be stupid, senseless; lose one’s wits, be struck dumb. kibania, caus. kibanithia, d. caus. kibanĩra, app. kibaniria, caus. app.

kibi!, ideo. (1), descr. of: r. fullness; iyũria ~, fill to brim; cf. bibĩbĩbi. 2. heavy tread. kibibi, ideo. descr. of entire closing up, blocking; see kibibania. kibikibi, ideo. descr. of light running feet, of goats, women, children; gamba ~, trot along; cf. kiririri.

~ thĩ, put your foot down (on); ~ cira, stick to the facts (of a case); ~ na run down sb., cause sb. to be run down (by a car). kinyithangia, diff. d. caus.; ~ njingiri, make a few more steps to rattle the rattles on your feet. kinyĩka, st. pot. kinyira, app. tread down, step on, tread on at; ~ cira, stick to the point; ~ njĩra gatagatĩ, keep to the middle of the road (out of danger). refl. app. 1. take up a firm stand in defence; be firm on one’s feet, find a foothold. 2. stick to one’s guns, stand firm by one’s decision, refuse to compromise; be resolute; be healthy, sturdy; cf. ĩhanda. kinyiria, caus. app.; ~ njingiri haha, walk this way with your (leg) rattles. refl. caus. app. reach a ripe old age. kinyirira, inten. put one’s foot down hard, tread on firmly. kinyĩrĩria, caus. inten. 1. trample, plant foot down firmly; ~ maguta, step on the ‘gas’, accelerate (car); cf. rangĩrĩria, hinyĩrĩria. 2. repress feelings. kinyũka, neut. walk smartly. kinyũkanga, diff. neut. kinyũkangia, caus. diff. neut. kinyũkania, caus. rec. neut.; ~ na, walk, with, smartly after. kinyũkanira, app. rec. neut. kinyũkaniria, caus. app. rec. neut. keep, walk, in step. kinyũkia, caus. neut. 1. lift foot off; ~ maguta, take foot off accelerator (of car), slow down. 2. set out smartly ; (of baby, adult with infantile paralysis, &c.})} take steps on one’s own. kinyũkithia, d. caus. neut. cause, assist, sb. to walk smartly ; ~ njingiri, walk smartly with rattles on your feet. kinyũkĩka, st. pot. neut. kinyũkĩra, app. neut. walk smartly at, for. kinyũkiria, caus. app. neut. 1. walk smartly through. 2. move sth. that is standing on sth. else away for sb.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. arrive, arrive at, reach, get to. 2. come to pass, happen
NEW_VALUE: get oneself to, reach by one’s own effort
FULL_ENTRY: kinya?, v.i. (1), I. arrive, arrive at, reach, get to. 2. come to pass, happen; na gũgĩ~ atĩrĩ, and it came to pass that. kinyanga, diff. walk about here and there. kinyania, caus. rec. arrive at destination together with the people you are sending there; ~ na, arrive at same time as, kinyanĩra, assoc. I. arrive at same time as. 2. go after sb. lost or running away. 3. be compact, well proportioned, symmetrical, plump; be prosperous, complete, whole, perfect. kinyaniria, caus. assoc. I. take to destination more than one thing at the same time. 2. pass word from sb. to another, spread the word about sth. 3. send sth. after sb. who has forgotten it or left it behind. rkinyanĩria, refl. caus. assoc. be, become, prosperous, welloff, complete. kinyia, caus. I. get sth., sb., to destination; take sb., sth., as E as, convey to, deliver (a letter, &c.). 2. come to a stop at; karaare gakinyie rũcinĩ, (let) it remain until the morning; akinyĩtie mĩaka 6o, he is 6o years old. rfkinyia, refl. caus. 1. get oneself to, reach by one’s own effort. 2. attain to (a higher status, &c.). kinyithia, d. caus. 1. help to reach, arrive. 2. cause to happen, bring about, bring to pass. kinyika, st. pot. kinyĩra, app. I1. catch up with; overtake, follow in order to overtake. 2. reach with arm, hand, foot, stick. kinyiria, caus. app. 1. take sth. to sb.; ~ horo, pass on news to, fkinyiria, refl. caus. app. elevate oneself to higher status by words or undesirable means. kinyirira, inten. 1. arrive fully; hindi ĩrĩa yerekereire ya~, in due course, 2. overtake on the way, catch up with; follow after sth. or sb. for; nĩnga~ baba ithaka ciake, I shall resolutely fight for the land rights on my father’s behalf. kinyĩrĩka, st. pot. app. 1. be attainable, within reach; be overtaken by sb. following. 2. (of woman in childbirth) be overtaken by pains of labour, have one’s time come.

kira!, v.i. (11), be quiet, stop making noise; ~ ngiria, ki, githi, gici, be absolutely silent; ~ ihooru, be desolate, lonely, deserted; macoya maa~, when the leaves stop rustling, late evening time; cf. ĩkiria-nyoni; see mũthenya. kirakira, dim. be silent for a short time; ngirakirĩte ngĩona aacoka, after a while I saw him. come back. kiranga, diff. kirangia, caus. diff., soothe, keep quiet, for a while. kirana, rec,; andũ makiranĩte kuo ki, people are tightly packed in there, the room is chock-full of people. kirania, caus. rec, make (people) quiet. kiranira, app. rec. 1. be forgiving, forgive. 2. be silent, stop speaking, to one another. 3. stop, talking at same time. kiranĩria, caus, app. rec. cause (people) to be quiet at same time, cause to stop making noise for. kiria, caus. 1. quieten, console, comfort. 2. cause to stop making noise. rkiria, v. refi. kirithia, d. caus. v. refi. keep quite still and quiet. kirika, st. pot. kirĩkana, rec. st. pot. used inten. be chock-full; cf. (N.k.) kurũkana. kirira, app. 1. keep silent about an offence, forgive; cf. ohera. 2, refuse to talk to. ĩkirira, refl. app. hold one’s peace. kirĩria, caus. app. stop sth., or sb. making a noise, for sb. else, at a place. kirĩrĩria, caus. inten. be patient (about); keep quiet, settle down; tolerate, endure. (kirama), stc. be silent waiting to do sth.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. exceed, be more e.d FA d mb ko o o n than
FULL_ENTRY: kira, v.t. & i. (11), 1. exceed, be more e.d FA d mb ko o o n than; (used adv. in e hinya Kamau, he is stronger than K. Kwaria kwega gwa~ kũhe, kind speech is better than a gift. 2. g0 beyond, pass, cross (a road, boundary, &c.); cf. agarara, rũga, hĩtũka. 3. (of a girl) elope, go off with a man without the formalities of marriage. kĩrakira, dim., move back and forth several times. kĩrakĩria, caus. dim. excel, surpass, outdo, a little more. kirakĩrũka, neut; dim. kĩrakirũkia, caus. neut. dim. kiranga, diff. cross over (a road, &c.) quickly. kĩrangia, caus. diff. kĩrana, rec., I1. cross Over one another. 2. be different in size, weight, stature, &c. 3. contend, compete, vie with one another. kĩrania, caus. rec. I. place across one another. 2. have more of sth. than sb. else. 3. (of man) contract a marriage without the consent of the girls parents; elope with a girl. 4. assist sb. across a road. kĩranĩra, assoc. I. Cross a road at the same time as. 2. be more than (at the same time). 3. (of girl) elope with a man. kĩraniria, caus. assoc. I. stand, lie across from one side to the other of a path, stream, &c. 2. place across a space; cross from one side (of open space, sky, &c.) to the other. 3. take (livestock, &c.) across (a path, road, planted garden, for sb.). kiria, caus. 1. take beyond, across, over (other than a river); put across. 2. cause to exceed; endetwo nĩ nyina aga~ njano, he is very much beloved by bis mother. 3. (of a man) elope with, marry (a girl) without the consent of her parents. 4. surpass in; ~ hinya, be stronger than; ~ mayi, overcome in contention, (of knowledge, &c.), be too strong for. ikiria, refl. caus. 1. (of girl) go off to the house of a man whom she desires to marry. 2. (of livestock) cross to the other side of the road by themselves. kĩrithia, d. caus. assist in taking across (over a path, garden, &c.). refi. d. caus. pretend to be crossing the road (to avoid sb. or a situation). kirika, st. pot. kirĩra, app. 1. pass over, cross at. 2. elope with sb., to sb.s home; marry a man without going through the usual formalities. kĩrĩria, caus. app. take across, at, for; putin excess for, at. kĩrĩrĩria, caus. inten. I. increase, redouble effort; do still more; do to excess; give, add, more to; cf. ongerera, 2. go on with increased insistence, persist; cf. kindirĩria. neut. 1. be in addition to, be more than, be in excess. 2. overstep the limit, go beyond a stated number, limit, quantity, distance; gũtingĩ~ mairo inyanya, it is not more than eight miles. kirũkana, rec, neut. go beyond, disobey; nĩ gũ~ na watho, that is beyond (against) the law. kĩrũkia, caus. neut. cause to go beyond, a limit, a stated amount; arakarĩte aga~, he is exceedingly angry. kĩrũkithia, d. caus. neut. kĩrũkira, app. neut. kĩrũkiria, caus. app. neut.

(1/11/111) descr. of: 1. pulling out, the coming out of things which are contained in sockets or sheaths, such as horns, tusks, the skin of an animal’s tail, a stalk of grass from its sheath, a jigger from its hole, a kid or other offspring from the uterus, kidneys from their outer skin; ndagambia ngwacĩ ~, I swished the potatoes out of the gourd; cf. cono. 2. the protruding of eyes.
gĩkono, i-, n. (1v), anything contained
bd KDa. S m a aa a ETS a in a tube or object of tubular type capable of being drawn out; esp. the nerve in the conical hollow of the base of an elephant’s tusk. [see konoka]
mũkono, mĩ-, n. (Iv), a cubit, length of forearm. [Sw. MKONO]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of bird’s feather, stalk of grass, when pulled) come out
NEW_VALUE: (of bird’s feather, stalk of grass, when pulled) come out
FULL_ENTRY: konoka, v.i. & t. (11), 1. (of bird’s feather, stalk of grass, when pulled) come out; (of sb. coming out of hut) emerge suddenly; (of eyes full of suspicion, terror, &c.) pop out, protrude; (of seedlings) shoot up; (of a tree that has broken surface) stick up out of the ground; (of a horn drawn off its core) come, slip, off. 2. confront sb.; akĩngonoka, I was suddenly confronted with him; cf. comoka. konokwo, pass.; ~ nĩ mũndũ, be suddenly confronted by sb. konora, v.t. pull, draw, out (telescope, feather, stalk of grass, &c.); ~ unearth, harvest a rich yield of, potatoes; ~ maitho, stare, glare, at. konakonora, dim. konoranga, diff. konorana, rec. konoranira, app. rec. konoraniria, caus. app. rec. konoria, caus. pull (potatoes) out of the ground; protrude (eyes). konorithia, d. caus. konorera, app.; ~ maitho, look at sternly; ~ ngwacĩ, unearth good potatoes for sb. konoreria, caus. app. konororia, caus. conv. pull right out, tear out; pull arm, leg, finger, ear, to make it crack; ~ mĩhũextract midribs of banana leaves; cf. kondora.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of sth. popping out, protruding
NEW_VALUE: piece of dry banana bark used for tying a bundle of sweet-potato vines, &c
FULL_ENTRY: conoko, ideo. (r1), descr. of sth. popping out, protruding; wona (indo) ciene, riitho ~! wona ciaku riitho ciciri, when you see sb. else’s things your eyes pop out, but when you see your own your eyes keep quiet. [konoka] nũkonokoni, mi-, n. (v), piece of dry banana bark used for tying a bundle of sweet-potato vines, &c.; cf. igoto. nũkonokono, mi-, n. (v), a long length of ~ wa kiugo, long sounding word. konoku, a. (1), protruding out of normal place; e na maitho ma~, he has protuberant eyes. [konoka] nũkonori, mi-, n. (111), a disease, onororo, ideo, (1), descr. of: x. the stripping of mid-ribs of banana leaves for mat-making. 2. the cracking of a personn’s arm, leg, or ear when stretched; cf. kondora. [konora]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sit hunched up, bowed, in silence
NEW_VALUE: (11), sit hunched up, bowed, in silence
FULL_ENTRY: ikonota, v.i. (refl.) (11), sit hunched up, bowed, in silence; sit back and do nothing; sit empty-handed with arms folded on the chest. wĩ na ihĩndi akĩrite wĩkonotete, (of men eating meat) a man holding a bone is better than one empty-handed. rkonotanga, diff. assoc. ĩrkonotanĩriaa, caus. assoc. fkonotera, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: stalk of a sp. of grass (tall, with fluffy pink bloom and seeds that stick to things passing)
NEW_VALUE: stalk of a of grass (tall, with fluffy pink bloom and seeds that stick to things passing)
FULL_ENTRY: rũkonda, ngonda, n. (vinr), stalk of a sp. of grass (tall, with fluffy pink bloom and seeds that stick to things passing); cf. gaita-ime, kaiga-ime. gakonda, tũ-, n. dim. of above.
kondiani, ~, n. (vii), accordion, concertina. [E.]

NEW_VALUE: clear honey, honey chipped from the comb
FULL_ENTRY: mũkondigũ, mi-, n. (X1) 1. clear honey, honey chipped from the comb; cf. mũruru. 2. njohi ya ~, beer brewed from honey.
kondo, see under kiondo.

kondora, v.t. (11), I. strip, peel; ~ mũtũrirũ, make a flute by slipping the bark off the inner wood ; see mũgio; ~ ndigi, strip bark from twigs; cf. unũra. 2. crack finger or knee joints. 3. beat; take a kink out of a chain. kondakondora, dim. kondoranga, diff. kondorana, rec. beat one another, others; cf. gondorana. Kondorania, caus. rec. kondoranira, app. rec. kondoraniria, caus. app. rec. kondoria, caus. kondorithia, d. caus. kondoreka, st. pot. kondorera, app. kondoreria, caus. app. kondoka, v.i. 1. be, become, peeled off, cracked; cf. cũnũka. 2. make a cracking sound (of fingers or knee joints). kondokanga, diff. kondokana, rec. be, become, peeled off completely, come off. kondokania, caus. rec. kondokanira, assoc. kondokaniria, caus. assoc, kondokia, caus. kondokithia, d. caus. kondokeka, st. pot. kondokera, app. kondokeria, caus. app.
kondoro, ~; gĩkondoro, i-; n. (111), European type of mattress. [Sw. GODORO]
-kondu, a. (1v), emaciated, thin.

mũkondwe, mĩ-, n. (viin), 1. shrub (Antidesma venosum); cf. mũkoigwa. 2. shrub (Grewia sp.). 3. small tree (Rytigynia battiscombei). mũkonge | mũkongwe | mũkongo, mi- ;ikongwe / ikongo, n. (1), sisal, hemp plant (sansevieria).
kongero, see under kiongero.
kongo, see under kĩongo.
makongo, see under mũkonge.
kongondiri, see under
mũkongoro, mĩ-, n. (XI), I. very large timber-tree (Lovoa swynnertonii). 2. beer mortar. gĩkongoro, i-, n. large beer mortar.
kongũ, see under nyũngũ.

kong’akong’a, v.t. (11), rap repeatedly. kong’akong’anga, diff. kong’zkong’ana, rec. kong’akong’anira, app. rec. kong’akong’aniria, caus. app. rec. kong’akong’ithia, d. caus. kong’akong’eka, st. pot. kong’akong’era, app. kong’akong'’eria, caus. app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of a woodboring bird rapping on a treetrunk
NEW_VALUE: of a woodboring bird rapping on a treetrunk
FULL_ENTRY: kong’okong’o, ideo. (1), descr. of a woodboring bird rapping on a treetrunk; see ngong'okong’i. [kong’akong’a]
(gi-) (ũ-) kong’u, see under ngong’'u.
ikongwe, see ikongo.

NEW_VALUE: wicker-work, hurdle made of saplings, built round a girls’ sleeping place
FULL_ENTRY: gĩkonjo, in. (1), basket-work grainstore; cf. ikũmbi. rũkonjo, ngonjo, n. wicker-work, hurdle made of saplings, built round a girls’ sleeping place; lattice-work; see mũcatha, kĩrĩrĩ. [konja]

NEW_VALUE: give oneself a good bath, scrub oneself thoroughly
FULL_ENTRY: konya’, v.t. (11), pull up, pull out, uproot; pluck. konya, refl. 1. give oneself a good bath, scrub oneself thoroughly; cf. 2. be easily uprooted. konyakonya, dim. konyanga, diff. konyanira, app. rec. konyanĩria, caus. app. rec. Konyithia, d. caus. konyeka, st. pot. konyera, app. konyerera, inten. konyora, conv. dehusk; beat thoroughly; ~ mwere, beat husks off millet; ngũgũ~ ta mwere, I will pound you like millet. rkonyora, refl. conv. give oneself a thorough bath, a scrubbing. 2. mwere nyũ this millet is easy to dehusk. konyorana, rec, conv. beat sb., one another, badiy. konyorithia, d. caus. conv. assist to dehusk, have sth. dehusked. konyoka, neut. be clear (as dehusked and winnowed millet), be well-flavoured, handsome, fine, comely, beautiful.

NEW_VALUE: name given to an ox or bull with a long navel
FULL_ENTRY: konya?, v.t. (11), twist, bend; cf. thiora. konya, refl. 1. be twisted, crooked. 2. change one’s mind; run away. konyakonya, dim. konyanga, diff. konyana, rec. konyania, caus. rec. konyanĩra, app. rec. konyanĩria, caus. app. rec. konyithia, d. caus. konyeka, st. pot. konyera, app. konyerera, inten. twist some more, ĩkonyerera, refl. inten. (of a twining plant) twine upon another plant, tree, &c. mĩ-, n. (11), navel; kanyam ga gũikia ~, a trifle to cast into one’s stomach, a snack; mũndi ũcoketie ~ nda, a person who is famished; ĩthiora ~, get angry; cf. rũrira. ngonyo, ~, n. name given to a cow with a long navel. gĩkonyo, i-, n. I1. protruding navel seen esp. in small children. 2. protuberance, knob, boss, excrescence; cf. 3. sun-shaped face tattoo mark; cf. gĩca., 4. (usu. with cl. o/10 concords) name given to an ox or bull with a long navel.
ũkonyoku, mokonyoku [mookonyoku], n. (11), handsome appearance. [konya]
-konyoku, a. (11), well-favoured, handsome, beautiful (as clean milet).
gakonyonggo, see under ngonyongo.

NEW_VALUE: of stripping, screwing up, in the hands (as in wringing out a wet garment or a roll of pounded sugar-cane to secure the juice)
FULL_ENTRY: konyororo, ideo. (1), descr. of stripping, screwing up, in the hands (as in wringing out a wet garment or a roll of pounded sugar-cane to secure the juice). [konya]

NEW_VALUE: of the crack of a branch torn off a tree by the wind, of a maize cob when broken from the plant, of sugar-cane broken off in the field (by thieves, &c
FULL_ENTRY: koo, ideo. (U), descr. of the crack of a branch torn off a tree by the wind, of a maize cob when broken from the plant, of sugar-cane broken off in the field (by thieves, &c.).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: come across, come upon, light upon
FULL_ENTRY: kora, v.t. (11), come across, come upon, light upon; find, discover, realize, korũo, pass, 1. (as aux. v.) be; nake agĩ~ athite, he was already gone; nĩaga~ atarĩ ho, he will not be there. 2. (as conj. introducing a conditional sentence) if; without prefix, expr. doubt about the actuality of the condition; ~ ndingĩthii, if they were not coming (but they will come) I would not go; with prefixes, expr. the probability of the condition; a~ [aakorũo], gwa~, o~ (also ongorno), angĩ~, &c., foll. by some tense of the, indic. 3.with naarĩ [na arĩ}; naarĩ ~| nĩ gnũkua, would that I had died. 4. nĩ! gũ~, because, it being the case that; cf. nĩ gũtuika, nĩ gũthirno, nĩ gũthũrũo. 5. no ga~, it may be so, perhaps; cf. hihi. koranga, diff. korana, rec. korania, caus. rec. take, bring, sth. to people. koranira, app. rec, koranĩria, caus. app. rec. koria, caus. send sth. to sb., take sth. to sb. unexpectedly; light upon sb. with sth. korithia, d. caus. korera, app. come suddenly upon. korerera, inten. come suddenly upon (before sth. happens); ngĩ~ matananina wĩra, I arrived just before they finished work.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. sb. well-off, a magnate. 2. a town-loafer, one who lives by his wits, who affects to be well-off and above work
FULL_ENTRY: mũkora, mi-, n. (11), 1. sb. well-off, a magnate. 2. a town-loafer, one who lives by his wits, who affects to be well-off and above work; hooligan, corner-boy; cf. mũhũni, gĩkore, ũkora, n. cl. 14, hooliganism; condition of loafing, spivvery. see mũkoro.
korambu, see under mũrambu.

NEW_VALUE: gathering from many parts of the country
FULL_ENTRY: ikorani', ma-; ngorani, ~; n. (111), gathering from many parts of the country. [kora]

NEW_VALUE: strip of skin from chin to scrotum of animal removed when it is skinned
FULL_ENTRY: mũkore, mi-, n. (1), I. strip of skin from chin to scrotum of animal removed when it is skinned. 2. dewlap of cow or ox. ikore, ma-, n. outer skin or husk of maize or millet (removed by pounding when making gruel); cf. tkorogoca.
rũkore, ngore, n. (1), xI. hair of mane; cf. mũrera. 2. hair at end of tail of cow, horse, elephant, gnu.
makore, n. cl. 6 (111), 1. offal of grain; cf. macungo, makorogoca ma mbembe. 2. floating refuse; cf. mataaho ma ũcũrũ,

korenji [koorenji / kooreenji], ~, n. (xv1), college. [E.]
kori, see under

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. stem of sweet potato vine stripped of leaves
FULL_ENTRY: mũkori, mi-, n, (1), 1. stem of sweet potato vine stripped of leaves; rĩoa mĩkori, act stupidly; cf. rĩa rũrua. 2. tops of European potatoes. 3. strip of green banana bark as left by cattle after they eat the trunk; cf. igoto. tũmikori, n. sth. v. small and thin ; no tamĩkori ndongania, I have only got some small (potatoes).
koriburaũ [kooriburaũ], ~, n. (V11), cauliflower. [E.]

mũkoro | mũkorakori, mi-, n. (1), stem of buxvch of bananas; cf. irigũ. ikoro, ma-, n. 1. skin of banana, potato, &c.; cf. rrbnwa. 2. (cl. 6) peelings, rũkoro, n. cl. 1r only, pile of peelings. nyagikoro, ~, n. sp. of cow pea or field bean; cf. thoroko.
gakoro, see under ngoro.

NEW_VALUE: jumbled, garbled talk (passing without pause from subject to subject)
FULL_ENTRY: koro, ideo. (1/11), descr. of sound made by a branch striking against a shield or against a woman’s leather garment in the bush. korokoro, ideo. descr. of: 1. jumbled, garbled talk (passing without pause from subject to subject). 2. rattling. [korakora]

NEW_VALUE: drop, stir, mix things up with banging, clashing, crashing, clattering sounds
FULL_ENTRY: korogoca, v.t. (11), drop, stir, mix things up with banging, clashing, crashing, clattering sounds. Korogocanga, diff. korogocana, rec. clash, crash; (of things falling or banging together) clatter; get tumbled noisily together. korogocania, caus. rec.; cf. kurugucania. korogocanira, app. rec. korogocaniria, caus. app. rec. korogocithia, d. caus. korogoceka, st. pot. korogocera, app. korogoceria, caus. app. korogocoka, neut. crack; be very emaciated (a ‘barebones’). korogocokana, rec. neut. korogocokania, caus., rec. neut.

FULL_ENTRY: korogoco, ideo. (1) descr. of the noise made by things falling and clashing against each other, of crashing, &c.; ikombe iragamba ~, the cups are rattling; mũndi wa ~, a fussy, interfering busybody.

FULL_ENTRY: koroka, v.i. (1), be greedy, gluttonous. korokanga, diff. korokangira, app. diff. be a little greedy for. korokanira, assoc. korokaniria, caus. assoc, korokia, caus. ĩkorokia, refl. caus. korokithia, d. caus. korokera, app. be inordinately greedy for sth., be extremely fond of a particular kind of food; tiga indo ciene, do not lust after other people’s property. mi-, n. (1), greed (for food). [koroka]
-koroku, a. (11), greedy, gluttonous. Uhũniũ etaga mũ~, the well-fed calls the hungry glutton. [koroka]
ũkoroku, n. cl. 14 only (11), greed, gluttony. [koroka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: phlegm. rũko- Tora, n. cl, 11 only, cough, coughing

NEW_VALUE: of the sound of a dry ox-hide or shield when struck
FULL_ENTRY: Kororo, ideo. (1), descr. of the sound of a dry ox-hide or shield when struck.
mũkororo, mi-, n. (v), a long thing.

NEW_VALUE: top soft part of sugar-cane, maize plant &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũkorũe [mũkoorũe], mi-, n, (vui), immature maize-cob in its leafy sheath with the grains of maize still at the rudimentary stage; cf, mboirũ. gĩkorũe [gikoorũe], i-, n. top soft part of sugar-cane, maize plant &c.
gũkorũo, see under kora!,

koune, see koine,

NEW_VALUE: choose, pick out, select (a number of things)
FULL_ENTRY: koya, v.t. (1), choose, pick out, select (a number of things). refi. withdraw oneself.
koya, see under kioya.
koyo, see under muoyo.
ku, ideo. (U), 1. ĩkuĩte o ~, it just died (was not killed). 2. descr. of sound of thud, stroke of wooden mallet, passing footsteps, &c.
kuarara, v.i. (11?), 1. alternate from shadow to brightness; see makũkũ. 2. be in a state of suspense, uncertainty.

FULL_ENTRY: mũkua-tũhũ, mi-, n. (1-1v), joint consisting of first four dorsal vertebrae, cut back to remove heart and lungs.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. be below the surface or level
NEW_VALUE: be below the surface or level
FULL_ENTRY: kuba, v.i. (1), 1. be below the surface or level; be immersed, covered by water. 2. be short of standard, below par. kubakuba, dim. kubanga, diff. kubania, caus. rec. kubanira assoc. kubanĩria, caus. assoc. kubithia, d. caus, kubika, st. pot. I. be capable of being immersed, covered. 2. put a lid on a container; cover with a cloak, blanket, tentcloth kubĩkanga, diff. st. pot. kubĩkana, rec. st. pot. I1. cover sb. over entirely with a blanket, &c. 2. (of a floating object) be submerged by surging water., kubikania, caus. rec. st. pot. (of surges of water, &c.) force sth. to submerge, drown. kubikanira, assoc. st. pot. kubikaniria, caus. assoc. st. pot. (of swirling waters) submerge several things together at; overturn (on to). kubĩkia, caus. st. pot. (of water swirls and eddies) swallow objects violently. kubĩkithia, d. caus. st. pot. kubikira, app. st. pot. kubikiria, caus. app. st. pot. kubira, app. kubiria, caus. app. kubũra, conv. cause to well up, cause water to surge up in excessive quantities. kubũka, neut. well up (like a fountain). kubũkanga, diff. neut. kubũcaus. diff. neut. kubũkana, rec. neut. kubũkania, caus. rec. neut. (of surges) well up violently. kubũkanĩra, assoc. neut. kubũ-kaniria, caus. assoc. neut. kubũkia, caus. neut. ĩkubũkia, refl. caus. neut. kubũkithia, d. caus. neut. kubũkĩra, app. neut. kubũkiria, caus. app. neut. kubũkirĩra, inten. neut. kubũkirĩria, caus. inten. neut,

kubia / kibia, v.i. (1), 1. run, trot; trip, patter, along; cf. cũacũa, hiũka, teng’era. 2. pass by (esp. at night) so that footsteps are audible. kubakubia / kibakibia, dim. (as encouragement to young children, &c.) come along, try and run. kubakubania, rec. dim. kubangia, diff. kubania, rec. kubaniria, app. rec. kubithia, d. caus. kubiria, app. [see kubukubu, kibikibi].

ikubu, ma-, n. (111), 1. husk of castoroil bean; outer husk of any similar fruit or nut. 2. firm resilient breast of pubescent girl; cf. ikindi, nyondo'. gikubu, i-, n. aug. of above. nyagikubu, n. large round variety of castoroil bean.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of the noise of running
FULL_ENTRY: kubukubu, ideo. (1), descr. of the noise of running; cf. kabakaba, kibikibi.
kuburi, ~ / ma-, n. (111), padlock. gĩkuburi, i-, n. aug. of above. gakuburi, tũ-, n. dim. of above. [Sw. KUFULI]
-kue, a. (11); gokũ nĩ gnũ~, death has taken place here. [kua]
(gĩ-) (ma-) kuguarũ, see under nguguarũ,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. rub, scrape, smooth, polish (with knife, sandpaper, pumice-stone, &c.)
