FULL_ENTRY: kuha [kuuha], v.t. (1), 1. rub, scrape, smooth, polish (with knife, sandpaper, pumice-stone, &c.); cf. guta, nyorokia. 2. scuffle, shuffle feet about. ikuha, refl. polish one’s skin by hard rubbing; rub down hardness of soles of feet with a stone. kuhakuha, dim. kuhakuhanga, diff. dim. kuhakuhana, rec. dim. kuhakuhania, caus. rec. dim. kuhakuhanira, app. rec. dim. kuhakuhithia, d. caus. dim. kuhakuhithĩria, app. d. caus. dim. kuhakuhĩka, st. pot. dim. kuhakuhikĩra, app. st. pot. dim. kuhanga, diff. kuhana, rec. kuhania, caus. rec. kuhanĩra, app. rec, kuhaniria, caus. app. rec. kuhithia, d. caus. kuhĩka, st. pot. kuhĩra, app. kuhirĩra, inten. kuhũra, conv. kuhũka, neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. awl used for work involving the sewing of leather, skin garments, &c.
NEW_VALUE: awl used for work involving the sewing of leather, skin garments, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũkuha, mi-, n. (111), 1. awl used for work involving the sewing of leather, skin garments, &c.; any big needle. 2. cotyledon, sprout. nguha, ~, n. 1. first shoot of cotyledon; sprouting grain. 2. (of potato) eye; cf. gũtũ. [kuha]
ũkuhe [ũkuuhe], n. cl. 14 (m), polished appearance, smoothness, polish. [kuha]
ũkuhi, n. cl. 14 only (1v), shortness, nearness.

kuhĩha, v.i. (11), (of things, people, story, &c.) be short; (of places) be close, near. kuhakuhĩha, dim. kuhakuhihanga, diff. dim. kuhakuhihangia, caus. diff. dim. shorten a little bit, quickly; move a bit farther on to shorten the distance. kuhakuhihaniria, caus. app. rec. dim. make (things) short for sb. kuhakuhĩhia, caus. dim. kuhakuhihithia, d. caus. dim. kuhakuhihika, st. pot. dim. kuhakuhĩhira, app. dim. be a little too short for, be a little bit shorter than expected. kuhĩhanga, diff. kuhihangia, caus. diff. kuhĩhania, caus. rec, kuhihanira, assoc. be short at same time as. kuhĩhaniria, caus. assoc, kuhĩhia, caus. kuhĩhithia, d. caus. ; ~ nguo, have a dress shortened. kuhĩhĩka, st. pot. kuhĩhĩra, app. kuhĩhiria, caus. app. 1. make short for, at. 2. come close, be a short distance away; cf. kuhirĩria.
mũkui!, mi-, n. (1v), 1. (of zebra, &c.) stripe, broad line; cf. 2. mark; (of blood in pus, phlegm, excreta) streak,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: any person who has died, deceased.
NEW_VALUE: any person who has died, deceased
FULL_ENTRY: mũkui?, a-, n. (11), any person who has died, deceased. [kua]
gakuiya, see under nguiya!,
gĩkuĩko, i-, n. (11), 1. crown of thatch; mĩtĩ wa ~, spike at apex. 2. semicylindrical wicker-work snare; cf. gĩkerenge.
kuku, see guku,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: expr. mockery or sarcasm when sb. is boasting or drawing a long bow
FULL_ENTRY: kukvuiya, interj. (U), expr. mockery or sarcasm when sb. is boasting or drawing a long bow; cf. guku.

NEW_VALUE: extolling one another, praising people to others
FULL_ENTRY: kukukuku, ideo. (1), descr. of the noise of stampeding animals, a rush of people, esp. women and children. mi-, n. (111), extolling one another, praising people to others. [kumia]

kumia, v.t. (11), praise, extol sb. to others ; cf. gaatha, ikĩrĩra, tia. refl. sing one’s own praises. kumakumia, dim. kumanggia, diff. kumania, rec., kumaniria, app. rec. kumithia, d. caus, kumika, st. pot. kumiria, app. kumiriria, inten. belaud, cry up, magnify, make a song about.
ũkumio, n. cl. 14 only (11), praise, adulation. [kumia]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: hollow, e.g. one left by an old ulcer
FULL_ENTRY: gi-)(ga-) kumba, see under ngumba. mi-, n. (iv), hollow, e.g. one left by an old ulcer; dent as in a hat; concavity in side of hill; neck or waist in a gourd bottle or in a handle; cf. [kumbata] / kumbacũria, see kumbatĩria / kumbatũria,
kumbatũ / kumbacaũ, ideo. (11), expr. of gripping. [see kumbatĩria]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. bulge caused by constriction, buige in a ridge of land
NEW_VALUE: bulge caused by constriction, buige in a ridge of land
FULL_ENTRY: gĩkumbo, i-, n. (11), 1. bulge caused by constriction, buige in a ridge of land; cf. ngumba, mũkumba. 2. land partially surrounded by a bend in a river; a deep pool, dam; cf. ndia.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: bulge (as on back of cricket bat, &c.).
NEW_VALUE: bulge (as on back of cricket bat, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũkumbu, mĩ-, n. (1v), bulge (as on back of cricket bat, &c.).
gikumbũkũ, n. (x1?), empty space encircled by sth.; ndia ya ~ igana? Gĩtarnrũ, (riddle) pool with empty hollow inside it and a hundred eyes; (ans.) a woven tray (of concave shape).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of sb. asleep or kept silent by force) break silence, utter a sound
FULL_ENTRY: kunia, v.i. (1), (of sb. asleep or kept silent by force) break silence, utter a sound; (of a bird) make a single chirp. kunakunia, dim. kunangia, diff. kunaniria, assoc. kunithia, d. caus. kuniria, app. utter a sound at, slip a word to.
mũkunio, mĩ-, n. (1), sniff, sound of disapproval. [kunia]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cover, put lid on. kunĩkanga, diff. kunĩkania, caus. rec. refl.
NEW_VALUE: become opened, have the lid off
FULL_ENTRY: kunĩka, v.t. (1), cover, put lid on. kunĩkanga, diff. kunĩkania, caus. rec. refl.; ~ ũthiũ, veil one’s face (leaving only eyes visible). kunikanĩra, app. rec. kunikaniria, caus. app. rec. kunĩkithia, d. caus. kunikĩka, st. pot. kunikira, app. cover up sth.; cover, for, at. kunikiria, caus. app.; ~ tawa, invert sth. over a lamp, kunikirira, inten. kunũra, conv. remove lid, uncover; ~ njohi, (of a guest of honour) take the first sip of beer; ~ mũndũ, initiate a person into the craft of a medicineman. kunakungũra, dim. conv. kungũranga, diff. conv. kunũrana, rec. conv. kunũrania, caus. rec. conv. kunũranĩra, app. rec. conv. caus. app. rec. conv. kunũrithia, d. caus. conv. kunũrika, st. pot. conv. kunũrira, app. conv. ĩkunũrĩra, refl. app. conv. kunũka, neut. 1. become opened, have the lid off. 2. (of seeds, plants, germinating) break through the soil, shoot up. kunakunũka, dim. neut. I1. (of a calabash lid) come off slightly. 2. (of sprouting seeds) have a splendid germination. kunakunũkia, caus. dim neut. kunakunũkĩra, app. dim. neut. kunũkanga, diff. neut. kunũkana, rec. neut. (of a lid that is difficult to move} come off with a pop. kunũkania, caus, rec. neut. (of germinating seeds) germinate healthily together because of a right amount of rain, temperature, and soil fertility. assoc. neut., 1. (of bottles, &c.) lose stoppers, &c., together. 2. (of seeds) germinate at the same time. kunũkaniria, caus. assoc. neut. kunũkia, caus. neut. (of a farmer) have a healthy germination of seeds. kunũkithia, d. caus. neut. kunũkika, st. pot. neut. kunũkira, app. neut. x. (of bottles, &c.) lose stoppers, corks, at, for, upon. 2. (of a stream) have its source at.
(i-) (ma-) (gi-) (ga-) kuniko, see under nguniko.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (1), circlet of pleated creepers worn by some young men
NEW_VALUE: circlet of pleated creepers worn by some young men
FULL_ENTRY: ikuno, (ma-), n. (1), circlet of pleated creepers worn by some young men; see mũriira; cf. ithuno.
kunu!, ~, n. (1), speck, sth. very small; ndũkoige ~ kana cere, do not say a word. ngunu, ~, n. colloquial name for a one-cent piece. gakunu, tũ-, n. a one-cent piece; cf. thendi.

NEW_VALUE: of: sound of sudden yielding of a stuck or stiff wooden lid, shutter, end of bee-barrel, &c
FULL_ENTRY: kunu? / kunũ, ideo. (1), descr. of: sound of sudden yielding of a stuck or stiff wooden lid, shutter, end of bee-barrel, &c. 2. sharp sound of sth. coming open with a pop or click (e.g. a drawn cork, stiff lid). 3. a knock on wood. 4. fast staccato talking. 5. quick growth esp, germination. [see kunia]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. mushroom, toadstool, 2. sprout of English potato, &c.
FULL_ENTRY: ikunũ, ma-, n. (iv), 1. mushroom, toadstool, 2. sprout of English potato, &c.; mĩrĩyo ya makunũ, see under mĩriyo; ngwacĩ cia makunũ, self-sown potatoes; cf. maitika. [kunũka]

NEW_VALUE: the uncorking of a bottle or gourd
FULL_ENTRY: kunũkũ, ideo. (11), descr. of: 1. the uncorking of a bottle or gourd. 2. the uncovering of lidded things. [kunũka]
ũkunderu [ũkundeeru], n. cl. 14 (1), 1. narrowness, constriction. 2. poverty. [kundera]

gĩkundi, n. (11), 1. company, assembly, group of people; collection of people or things. 2. (with n. in pl.) several. rũkundi, ngundi!', n. a large number of things. gakundi, n. 1. a small group. 2. a revival group in the Christian church. 3. (with n. in pl.) a few.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: tie a knot. ĩkundika, refl. (of thread, rope, &c.) get tangled
FULL_ENTRY: kundika, v.t. (11), tie a knot. ĩkundika, refl. (of thread, rope, &c.) get tangled; (of a muscle) get cramp; ~ mara, be angry, take offence. kundakundĩka, dim. kundĩkanga, diff. kundikana, rec. kundikania, caus. rec. join two pieces of rope by tying. ikundĩkania, refl. caus. rec. kundikanĩra, app. rec. kundikaniria, caus. app. rec. kundikithia, d. caus. kundikika, st. pot. kundikira, app. kundikirira, inten. tie on more (to get a long string, &c.). kundũra?, conv. untie (a knot). ĩkundũra, refl. conv. kundakundũra, dim. conv. kundũranga, diff. conv. untie (knots) quickly. kundũrana, rec. conv.; see ohorana. caus. rec. conv. kundũranira, app. rec. conv. kundũraniria, caus. app. rec. conv. kundũrithia, d. caus. conv. kundũrika, st. pot. conv. kundũrira, app. conv. kundũririra, inten. conv. kundũka, neut, kundakundũka, dim. neut. kundũkanga, diff. neut. kundũkana, rec. neut. (of a knot) become entirely untied by itself. kundũkania, caus. rec. neut. kundũkanira, assoc. neut. kundũkaniria, caus. assoc. neut. kundũkia, caus. neut. kundũkithia, d. caus. neut. kundũkĩka, st. pot. neut. kundũkira, app. neut. become untied, at, to, for. kundũkiria, caus. app. neut. untie a knot entirely for.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: knot in fibre string, made to count days on a joumey or before termination of agreement or fulfilment of appointment (one being untied daily), cf. njano
NEW_VALUE: knot in fibre string, made to count days on a joumey or before termination of agreement or fulfilment of appointment (one being untied daily), njano
FULL_ENTRY: ikundo, ma-, n. (11), knot in fibre string, made to count days on a joumey or before termination of agreement or fulfilment of appointment (one being untied daily), cf. njano; ~ rĩake nĩrĩathenga, his appointed days are over; cf. gĩthiari, kĩana kĩa rũrigi, kĩgucĩrĩrio, kĩririmithario, kĩaramiririo. gĩkundo, i-, n. (of ribbon, &c.) bow; cf. krana. [see kundika]
mũkundũro, mi-, n. (11), an untying. [see kundika]
mũkundwa, mĩ-, n. (11), a widespreading climber (Helinus mystacinus).

kunga, v.i. & t. (1) crouch, bend, stoop; move in a crouching position (to escape notice); go through (a fence); cf. inama. kungakunga, dim. kunganga, diff. kungania, caus. rec. kunganira, assoc. kunganiria, caus. assoc.; ~ engage in repartee. kungia, caus.; ~ ũgi, use guile against, act deceitfully towards. kungithia, d. caus. kungira, app. kungiria, caus. app. kungirira, inten.; cf. kunguirĩra. kungara, stc. be bent down, double. kungarara, inten. stc. kungararia, caus. inten. stc.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sp. of wild spinach used in time of scarceness
NEW_VALUE: of wild spinach used in time of scarceness
FULL_ENTRY: gikungui [gĩkunguwi], ĩ-, n. (x1), sp. of wild spinach used in time of scarceness; see ngungui.
kunguta, v.t. (11), cut with a blunt tool. kungutanga, diff, kungutana, rec. kungutanira, app. rec. kungutithia, d. caus. kungutika, st. pot. kungutira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. draw near in curiosity, crowd around to watch sth. uninvited. 2. (of rain) be just about to fall heavily
NEW_VALUE: iron ring made for initiates out of stout wire, in shape resembling a coat button, worn in the ear from the third to the eighth day
FULL_ENTRY: kungutiria, v.i. (11), 1. draw near in curiosity, crowd around to watch sth. uninvited. 2. (of rain) be just about to fall heavily; cf. tutungiria. kungakungutĩria, dim. kungutangiria, diff. kungutaniria, rec. kungutirĩka, st. pot. kungutirĩra, app. kungutirĩria, caus. app. mĩ-, n. (111), iron wire collar; cf. morumbo. rũkunju, ngunju, n. 1. iron ring made for initiates out of stout wire, in shape resembling a coat button, worn in the ear from the third to the eighth day. 2. gold or other stud ornament worn in the side of the nose, esp. by Indian and Swahili women. gakunju, tũ-, n. dim. of above.

kunya, v.t. (1), 1. pinch; claw, scratch ; ~ kĩondo, weave a string bag diligently ; ~ riya, pull up weeds one by one. 2. (of boys and girls in a dance) give a sign or hint to an admired party. ikunya, refl.; ~ gatũ, take warning, take heed. kunyakunya, dim. kunyanga, diff. kunyana, rec. kunyania, caus. rec. kunyanira, app. rec.; ~ mũndũ, betray sb. kunyaniria, caus. app. rec. kunyithia, d. caus. kunyĩka, st. pot. kunyĩra, app. pinch, for, at; ~ kaha, (with ref. to shortening a dress) tack and sew here; ~ mũndũ ũhoro, give a hint, a small bit of information, to sb. kunyirĩra, inten. kunyũra, conv. kunyũrũra, inten. scratch heavily, mangle, wound badly with nails or claws. diff. inten. kunyũrũrana, rec., inten. kunyũrũrania, caus. rec. inten. kunyũrũranira, app. rec. inten. kunyũrũraniria, caus. app. rec. inten. kunyũrgria, caus. inten. kunyũrũrithia, d. caus. inten. kunyũrũrika, st. pot. inten. kunyũrũrira, app. inten. kunyũrũriria, caus. app. inten. kunyũka, neut. (of claws, nails, stuck in the body) get released. kunyũkia, caus. neut. release sth., sb., held by claws or nails. kunyũkithia, d. caus. neut. frighten (a bird of prey, animal) away causing it to release its victim. kunyũkiria, caus. app. neut, release sth. clawed at.
mũkunya | mũkunyi, see mũkũnyi.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: tree, plant, used in initiation of medicine-men.
NEW_VALUE: tree, plant, used in initiation of medicine-men
FULL_ENTRY: mũkunya-ngunũ, mi-, n. (1-111), tree, plant, used in initiation of medicine-men.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of a griping, throbbing, or twitching sensation, as in stomach-ache, headache, toothache, grit in the eye
NEW_VALUE: of a griping, throbbing, or twitching sensation, as in stomach-ache, headache, toothache, grit in the eye
FULL_ENTRY: kunyukunyu, ideo. (1), descr. of a griping, throbbing, or twitching sensation, as in stomach-ache, headache, toothache, grit in the eye; the biting of bugs, fleas, &c.; nda ĩgambaga ~ ĩkĩrĩa mũndũ, one’s stomach goes pinch-pinch as it aches. [kunya]

rũkunyũ, ngunyũ, n. (11), 1. scratch, laceration, claw-mark, pinch. 2. fingernail, toe-nail. 3. secret sign, nip, cue, warning, hint. karũkunyũ, tũn. undercurrent of thought, doubt, idea, notion, [kunya]
kuo, indep. pron. (U), r. cl. 15%, there; e (ndarĩ) ~, he is (is not) there, at home, &c.; thĩ ~, go there (to the area specified); cf. ho; see also -o!. 2. also cl. 15 and 15a.
gakuo, see under nguo.

NEW_VALUE: display ability or agility (esp. in the dance)
FULL_ENTRY: kuoga, v.i. (1) display ability or agility (esp. in the dance); dance (and enjoy it) excessively. kuoganga, diff. kuoganira, assoc. kuoganiria, caus. assoc. kuogithia, d. caus. kuogeka, st. pot. kuogera, app.

NEW_VALUE: carry out postmarital ceremonies symbolizing the union of two families
FULL_ENTRY: kura!, v.t. & i. (1) carry out postmarital ceremonies symbolizing the union of two families; ~ ngc, hold a young people’s dance, kuranira, assoc. I. carry out the customary ritual meat feast; see wakiri. 2. carry out the meat-eating ceremony in the circumcision lodge when the neophytes begin to greet each other with the salutation kurira, app. perform customary rites two or three months after a marriage when the bride has been installed in a permanent hut of her own; see nguro.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of razor) cut with difficulty, causing pain
NEW_VALUE: (of razor) cut with difficulty, causing pain
FULL_ENTRY: kura?, v.t. (1), 1. (of razor) cut with difficulty, causing pain; (of a barber) apply pressure, causing discomfort or pain, cut hair with a blunt razor; be inefficient, nyenja na ndũkangure, give me a haircut but not too short. 2. dig up (potatoes) in a recently abandoned garden, 3. (with ref. to urination) take short (with violent need to pass water). refl. (of sb, with a skin disease, &c.) scratch oneself violently. kurakura, dim. scrape hard on the surface (not deep); cause sharp pain; scrape rapidly. kurakuria, caus. dim. run with a light scuffling gait; see kurukuru. kuranga, diff. kurana, rec. give each other, others, an uncomfortable haircut. kuranĩra, app. rec. kuranĩria, caus. app. rec. kuria, caus. journey, walk briskly, along a road. kurithia, d. caus. 1. have sb. scraped with a blunt razor when he gets a haircut. 2, help to dig up potatoes in an abandoned garden. kurika, st. pot. kurĩra, app. ĩkurira, refl. app. I. scrape and keep every bit possible for oneself. 2. scratch oneself hard at. kuriria, caus. app. go on a journey along a certain road. kurara, stc. x. chafe, be worked up, impatient with anger, expectancy, &c. 2. (of maize) be on the point of bursting through the ground. kurarara, inten. stc. as kurara.
kura, ~, n. vote. [Sw. KURA]
-kure!, a. (11). Uthoni nĩũkuaga wĩ a marriage arrangement can break down even when the ceremonial goat has been sacrificed. fkura!]

-kure?, a. (11) 1I. scraped; ndacanjarĩka ngure nja. Ikoro rĩa ndũma, (riddle) I have spread out scrapings in the courtyard; (ans.) peel of edible arum, 2. bursting; ndi I badly need to go to the toilet. [kura?]
makuro, n. cl. 6 (1), (N.K.) scrapings from the pot. [kura?]
mũkuru! | mũkuri?, mĩ-, n. (1x), 1. deep gorge, ravine, nullah, gulley; ~wa-nda, pangs of hunger; cf. mũhũhi. 2. doubt, lack of knowledge; perpetual memory of some evil that has befallen.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sword or large knife worn narrow by long use and whetting.
NEW_VALUE: sword or large knife worn narrow by long use and whetting
FULL_ENTRY: mũkuru?, mi-, n. (1x), sword or large knife worn narrow by long use and whetting.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: widespreading climber (Achyrothalamus marginatus) (with small strawcoloured flowers and small black fruit). ngurua, ~, n. 1. strong flexible stem of above (much used in wicker-work). 2. stiff ring of these stems worn round neck by old man conducting the deathpurification ceremony
NEW_VALUE: strong flexible stem of above (much used in wicker-work)
FULL_ENTRY: mũkurua [mũkuurua], mi-, n. (11), widespreading climber (Achyrothalamus marginatus) (with small strawcoloured flowers and small black fruit). ngurua, ~, n. 1. strong flexible stem of above (much used in wicker-work). 2. stiff ring of these stems worn round neck by old man conducting the deathpurification ceremony; see hingĩrĩria gĩkũ. 3. a similar ring worn by candidates for circumcision ; see irig.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. make a scuffing rustling sound (as of a body scraping along the side of a store or an animal dashing past). 2. (of a thief) disturb, upset (things). kurugucania, rec, (of people in the datk, cats, rats, &c.) make utensils, &c., rattle (by disturbing them)
NEW_VALUE: make a scuffing rustling sound (as of a body scraping along the side of a store or an animal dashing past)
FULL_ENTRY: kurugucia, v.t. & i. (11), 1. make a scuffing rustling sound (as of a body scraping along the side of a store or an animal dashing past). 2. (of a thief) disturb, upset (things). kurugucania, rec, (of people in the datk, cats, rats, &c.) make utensils, &c., rattle (by disturbing them); cf. korogocania. kurugucanĩria, app. rec. make (utensils, &c.) rattle at the same time as sb, else. kurugucithia, d, caus. kuruguciria, app. kurugumake a long sustained rattling noise of utensils (in the dark, when groping for sth.). kurugucũrania, caus, rec. cony. kurugucũka, neut, rush with rustling sound, go headlong through the bushes, &c. (like an angry rhino). kurugucũkia, caus. neut, kurugucũkithia, d. caus. neut. kurugucũkira, app. neut. rush with rustling sounds across, at, through. kurugucũkiria, caus. app. neut. start, scare (an animal, person), for, through.

NEW_VALUE: of the startled dash of a disturbed hare, small buck, cat, small dog, Masai sheep, quail, &c
FULL_ENTRY: kurugucu, ideo. (1), descr. of the startled dash of a disturbed hare, small buck, cat, small dog, Masai sheep, quail, &c., when taken by surprise.
gakurugutia / gakuruguta, see under ngurugutia,

kuruha, v.i. (1) go, come, close to; be near to; be related to; cf. kuhĩhĩria. kuruhana, rec. be near to one another, be boon companions, be in one another’s confidence, be related to; ~ na, resemble, be akin to. kuruhania, caus. rec. kuruhanira, assoc. kuruhanĩria, caus. assoc. kuruhia, caus. kuruhithia, d. caus. kuruhika, st. pot. kuruhira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of the sound of scurrying, children or young people running after one another, ləmbs frolicing, &c. ~ ndũma-inĩ! Gĩcogoru, (riddle) a rustle in the arums!
NEW_VALUE: of the sound of scurrying, children or young people running after one another, ləmbs frolicing, &c
FULL_ENTRY: kurukuru, ideo. (1), descr. of the sound of scurrying, children or young people running after one another, ləmbs frolicing, &c. ~ ndũma-inĩ! Gĩcogoru, (riddle) a rustle in the arums!; (ans.) a bulbul.
gĩkuruma, see nguruma.,

FULL_ENTRY: kurumutha, v.t. (1), abrade (skin, &c.) in rubbing past, graze, cause abrasion on; tear, scratch sb.’s skin, lacerate; cf. kumutha, hararia, tararia. kurumuthanga, diff. kurumuthana, rec.; see kumuthana. kurumuthania, caus. rec. kurumuthanira, app. rec. kurumuthaniria, caus. app. rec. kurumuthia, caus. kurumuthithia, d. caus. kurumuthika, st. pot. kurumuthĩra, app. kurumuthũra, conv.; see kurumutha. kurumuthũranga, diff. conv. kurumuthũrana, rec. conv. graze, abrade, one another’s skin. kurumuthũrania, caus. rec. conv. abrade, lacerate entirely, carelessly. kurumuthgranira, app. rec. cony. kurumuthũranĩria, caus. app. rec. conv. abrade, graze entirely, for, at, at the same time. kurumuthũria, caus. cony. kurumuthũrithia, d. caus. conv. cause sb., sth., to be abraded, grazed, skinned. kurumuthũrika, st. pot. conv. kurumuthũrira, app. conv. kurumuthũriria, caus. app. conv. kurumuthũrũria, inten. conv. cause abrasions on intensively. kurumuthũka, neut. be, become, abraded, grazed, torn, lacerated. kurumuthũkanga, diff. neut. kurumuthũkana, rec. neut. become abraded, badly, entirely. kurumuthoũkania, caus. rec. neut. kurumuthũkanira, assoc. neut. kurumuthũkaniria, caus. assoc. neut. kurumuthũkia, caus. neut. abrade sth, sb., by dragging along; see kururũkia. kurumuthũkithia, d. caus. neut. kurumuthgkira, app. neut. be abraded at. kurumuthũkiria, caus. app. neut. kurumuthara, stc. / mũkuruguthu, mi-, n. (1v), 1. hollow of back, spinal furrow; cf. kurumbara, mũngũng ũtũ. 2. centre rib, ridge, of swordblade, spear, knife; cf.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: backbone, spine (upper part where it forms a ridge or hump).
NEW_VALUE: backbone, spine (upper part where it forms a ridge or hump)
FULL_ENTRY: kurumbara, ma-, n. cl. 14/6 (1), backbone, spine (upper part where it forms a ridge or hump).

kurururu, ideo. (1), descr. of sth. being dragged along. [see kurura]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of sth. stable) fall, fall in, collapse
FULL_ENTRY: kurũka:, v.i. (11), 1. (of sth. stable) fall, fall in, collapse; (of a slumbering person) drop the head; pop up and down in water. 2. (of sb. walking) go up one ridge and down the other side; cf. turũka. kurakurũka, dim. kurũkanga, diff. kurũkana, rec. 1. collapse as a burning house, fall in. 2. swarm upon, be thick all over (a person, &c.) kurũkania, caus. rec. kurũkanira, assoc. kurũkaniria, caus. assoc. ’'kurũkia, caus. kurũkithia, d. caus. kurũkika, st. pot. kurũkĩra, app. kurũkĩria, caus. app. kurũkirira, inten.
kuua, v.t. & i. (1), 1. carry (away), transport, take away. 2. mbembe nĩra~, 834403 F

R the maize is beginning to bear (cobs). ikuua, refl. 1. (of a load) be light. 2o drive oneself (in a car). kuuakuua, dim. kuuakuuanga, diff. dim. kuuakuuanira, app. rec. dim. (of more than one person) keep on carrying (by combined effort) for a while. kuuakuuithia, d. caus. dim. supervise sth. that is being carried for a longer period, keep on assisting in carrying for a while. kuuakuuira, app. dim. kuuanga, diff. kuuangithia, d. caus. diff. kuuangithiria, app. d. caus. diff. kuuana, rec. I. carry people, one another. 2, act as a substitute (in an oath-taking). 3. (of words, matters, &c.) be related, associated. kuuania, caus. rec. carry too heavy a load, carry bundles anyhow. kuuanira, app. rec. carry together (by combined effort); carry sth., for sb., for one another. kuuaniria, caus. app. rec. carry (things) together. kuuithia, d. caus. have carried, assist in carrying. kuuithangia, diff. d. caus, kuuithangĩria, app. diff. d. caus. kuuithania, rec, d. caus. assist sb. to put a load on his back, &c., put a load on sb.’s back. kuuithaniria, app. rec. d. caus. kuuika, st. pot. be portable, be able to be carried. kuuikira, app. st. pot. kuuira, app. carry, for, at. kuuirira, inten. carry sth. in addition to. kũkia, caus. neut. let the strap or string tying a load be loose on the head.
kuuma, see under uma,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1 pad placed between load and back or head. 2. (N.K.) carrying-strap
NEW_VALUE: 1 pad placed between load and back or head
FULL_ENTRY: moũkuuo [mũkuuwo], mi-, n. (1), 1 pad placed between load and back or head. 2. (N.K.) carrying-strap; ~ wa ngũi, strap which secures a baby pouch of leather or cloth. Mũremwo nĩ ngũ egwatagia ~, the one who cannot carry a load of firewood blames the strap by which it is carried. [kuua]
Ũkuurũ, see under nguurũ.

kuurũka, v.t. & i. (1), 1. tell the difference, distinguish, explain; cf. thutũkania, thuura. 2. be full. kuurakuurgka, dim. kuurũkanga, diff. kuurũkana, rec. kuurũkania, caus, rec. kuurũgkanira, app. rec. & assoc. kuurũkaniria, caus. app. rec. kuurũkia, caus. kuurũkithia, d. caus. kuurũkika, st. pot. kuurũkira, app. kuurũkiria, caus. app.
kuuuu, ideo. (U), descr. of: 1. roaring in the ears. 2. deafening effect of grief, consternation, noise, or hubbub.

FULL_ENTRY: mũkũ [mũkũ], mi-, n. (11), small spreading tree, tree (Cordia ovalis). ikũ, ma-, n. leaf of above (used for polishing wooden articles); kaka ~, polish sth. with sand-paper leaves.

NEW_VALUE: death, visitation of death in a family
FULL_ENTRY: gikũ, i-, n, (11), 1. death; ~ gia itimũ, death by violence. ~ gĩtithemagwo ta njira, death cannot be bypassed as if it were a path. 2. a crack. rũkũ, n. cl. 1x1 only, the uncleanness of death, ũnkũ, n. ci. 14 only, 1. death, visitation of death in a family. 2. a slight crack; a split (in a calabash, plank, &c.). [kua]
-kug, a. (111), 1. dead, deceased, the late; kaana gaka nĩ ga~ ke muoyo, this child has one foot in the grave. 2. mũ~, dead person; mũtũgũ, see mũtũgũ; cf. krimba. [kua]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: comprehend, recollect, remember after some difficulty. kuakũka, dim. kũkanga, diff. kũkana, rec. r. be entirely enlightened
NEW_VALUE: comprehend, recollect, remember after some difficulty
FULL_ENTRY: kũka [kũka], v.i. (11), comprehend, recollect, remember after some difficulty. kuakũka, dim. kũkanga, diff. kũkana, rec. r. be entirely enlightened; call to mind sth. previously forgotten; cf. ririkana. 2. understand fully as by revelation. kũkania, caus. rec. kũkanĩra, assoc. understand at the same time as. kũkaniria, caus. assoc. kũkia, caus. kũkithia, d, caus. kuukĩka, st. pot. kũkira, app. kũkiria, caus. app. kũra, v.t. make sb. understand sth. diffficult.
mũkũko [mũkũko], mi-, n. (111), (of water) upsurge, swelling, wellingup. [kũka]
-kũku [-kũku], a. (r1), alert, intelligent. [kũka]

kũ, loc. part. (U), 1. denoting a relatively wide area; njũi nĩ ~?, how do I know where the place is? ; ndinamenya nĩ na ~ oimĩre, I did not find out from which direction he came; o ~ kana ~, wherever, whithersoever. 2. as an interrog., where?; wathĩ ~?, where are you off to?; uuma ~?, where have you been?; wetwara ~?, where are you bound for?; nĩ ~?, where is the place?, what is going on there?, where do you think you are going?, whom are you pushing?; na ~?, in what o ~?, just -a ~?, belonging to where?; gũkũ ni ~?, what place is this? ; ~ na ~?, to, from, which places?

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n., a., & p.c., also subj. prefix, cl. 15, 154, and 15b
NEW_VALUE: the 15b prefix, locative, denotes a relatively wide area
FULL_ENTRY: kũ-, conc. prefix (U), n., a., & p.c., also subj. prefix, cl. 15, 154, and 15b: 1. the 15b prefix, locative, denotes a relatively wide area; Nairobi nĩ gnũkuhĩ, N. is close; see also kwa under -a, kuo under -o. 2. duplicated this prefix gives the ‘near’ dem. a. & p. of cl. 15, 15a and 15b; gũkũ nĩ kwega, this area is all right, everything is all right here, in these parts; see kiu under -u, kũria under -ria.
-kũ-, obj. infix (U), cl. 15, 15a, and 15b.

rũkũ, ngũ, n. (111), piece of firewood; salutation between members of the same age-group; mũkũna- ~, see mũkũna-rakũ; ma ta ngũ, be very hard, be hard as a rock; ara ngũ, stack firewood; cf. mohando, kĩbaca. gakũ, tũ-, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. (cl. 13) a little firewood. karũkũ, tũ-, n. znd dim. of above, a small piece of firewood.
kũa [ikũa], v.t. (1), suspect. Tknana, rec, app.
kũana [kũana], v.i. (1) come together, close up; (of space, crack, aperture) narrow; cf. kundera.

mũkũbg?, mĩ-, n. (v1), flowering tree (Craibia elliottii).

FULL_ENTRY: kũga, v.i. (11), 1. (of dogs) bark. 2. (of cocks) cluck, crow. 3. (N.K.) (coll., in derogatory sense, of human beings) bark, bleat, bray. kũgakũga, dim. kũganga, diff. kũganira, app. rec. kũgia, caus. kũgithia, d. caus, kũgira, app. bark, yelp, cackle, quack, at; crow over.

gikũmba, see ngũmba.

ũkũmbarũ, see under ma-, n. (1), 1. grain-store; ~ rĩa githiũrũri, domed wicker-work store with a mouth at the side near the top; ~ rĩa gĩkonjo, store made of hurdles subsequently floored and roofed; ~ rra store made in either way, without a domed roof; ~ rĩa mũthũnũ, store built in situ, walled with grass and plastered inside with dung; ~ rĩa gũita irio cia kĩmera, store built by district elders to receive the ceremonial offering of first fruits. 2. (of railway train) coach.

FULL_ENTRY: ikũmbi?, ma-, n. (1), wave (on a body of water). [kũmba] mi-, n. (11), ridge raised on flesh by beating, wale; anything lumpy resembling this. makũmbũn. cl. 6 only, lumps in gruel; cf. makindi, [kũmba}] see ngũmbũrũ.

kũna, v.t. (1), 1. strike a blow, knock, rap; cf. ringa, gũtha, komoria, komonga. 2. decorticate (sisal); ~ makongo, prepare sisal fibre. kũnakũna, dim. chatter, prattle, wag the tongue, blether. kũnakũnanga, diff. dim. kũnakũnana, rec. dim. kũnakũnanira, app. rec. dim. kũnakũnithia, d. caus. diff. kũnakũnira, app. dim. kũnakũnika, st. pot. dim. kũnanga, diff. kũnangithia, d. caus. diff. kũnangika, st. pot. diff. kũnangira, app. diff. kũnithia, d. caus. kũnithiria, app. d. caus. kũnika, st. pot. konikira, app. st. pot. kũnĩra, app. kũnirira, inten.

NEW_VALUE: one who gives signals by means of a gong (a legendary figure, probably an Arab ivory trader from Mombasa)
FULL_ENTRY: kũna, adv. (u), certainly, absolutely, really; cio ti mũndũ mũgo wa ~, he is not a real medicine-man; yũ ni ~, this man is a true Kikuyu. mi-, n. (1-111), 1. one who gives signals by means of a gong (a legendary figure, probably an Arab ivory trader from Mombasa). 2. a mythological figure, with body all eyes, giving light to the sun. 3. a person of outstanding authority or personality; tough hard-boiled person; nickname for Arab or European; cf. koigi. [kũna]

kũnũkũnũ, ideo. (1), descr. of chatter, tongue-waggiing.
mũkũnũngũ, mi-, n. (vi), string of dark blue beads. ngũnũngũ, ~, n. single bead of this type.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. be caught out, lose (in a children’s guessing game)
NEW_VALUE: be caught out, lose (in a children’s guessing game)
FULL_ENTRY: kũnda, v.i. & t. (11), 1. be caught out, lose (in a children’s guessing game); lve caught you out (beaten you in a game). kũndana, rec. kũndanira, app. rec. kũndaniria, caus. app. rec. kũndithia, d. caus. kũndĩka, st. pot. kũndĩra, app. kũndũra, conv. corrupt. ĩkũndũra, refi. conv. r1. become corrupt, change for the worse; retaliate. 2. redeem, free, oneself; change for the better; (of a children’s game) regain one’s status by winning, win (in any competitive or guessing game). kũndũrithia, d. caus. conv. reform. rika, st. pot. conv. kũndũrira, app. conv. kũndũka, neut. 1. be evil; be, become, corrupt; change, deteriorate; 2. change for the better. 3. be, do, worse than. kũndũkia, caus. neut. corrupt; make bad or good. v.t. (1) deck oneself out nicely, attend to one’s personal appearance.

mũkũndũ?!, mi-, n. (1v), 1. muddy current of water; cf. muunju. 2. evil, corrupt, person. Mũceera na ~ akũndũkaga taguo, see under mũceera. 3. (of one defeated in a children’s game) prisoner, serf. [kũnda]

rũkũngũ, n. (11), dust, dust that rises. [kũngũra]

NEW_VALUE: pull out people’s teeth (in general sense)
FULL_ENTRY: kũra, v.t. & i. (11), ri. pull out by roots, pull out of ground, uproot; ~ igego, pull out a tooth; ~ njoya, pluck feathers off a bird; ~ maitho, open eyes wide, stare at, pull eyes of slaughtered animals out of sockets; cf. munya. 2. move, remove; depart, move faster, hurry; ~ magũrũ, lift your feet off the ground, hurry up; ngaari nĩya~, the train (lorry) has started, 3. put down bail for sb., buy back, redeem; hire (head-dress); ~ kĩgenyo, present evidence to get sb. acquitted of a false charge; ~ bũrũri, (of ituĩka ceremony) redeem country from outgoing generation set. refl. redeem oneself; convince authority, court, that one is innocent. kũrakũra, dim. kũgrakũrũkia, caus. neut. dim. (of tired travellers) move faster as it gets late in evening. kũranga, diff. kũrana, rec. 1. ~ tgego, pull out people’s teeth (in general sense). 2. put down bail so that people may be released. 3. distinguish between; esteem, respect; discern, differentiate; know, recognize; ~ ndeto, elucidate the matter; ndicikũratine, I cannot tell the difference between them. refl. rec. be different, distinct from; be recognizable among others. kũrania, caus. rec. exchange, barter, interchange. kũranika, st. pot. rec. be able to be interchanged. kũranira, app. rec. 1. explain to, expound for. 2. make distinction between sth. and sth. clear to sb. kũraniria, caus. app. rec, exchange one thing for another for sb.; barter for sb. kũrithia, d, caus. ~ drive a car away. kũrĩka, st. pot. 1. be pullable. 2. be hireable, redeemable. kũrĩkana, rec. st. pot. be distinguishable one from another. rira, app. 1. pull out of ground for, at; ~ maitho, stare at. 2. pay money so that sb. else may get back his property. kũririra, inten. r. shift (a boundary mark). 2. move farther on, go on step by step, by successive stages; ~ magũrũ, move faster, accelerate, hurry up (before it gets dark, &c.). kũrũka, neut. 1. be uprooted, come out by force; cf. munyũka, gũka. 2. get up and depart. kũrũkangia, caus. diff. neut. move a bit faster. rec. neut. ~ na, be different, differentiated from. kũrũkia, caus. neut. Ĩ. uproot, pluck out by roots. 2. ~ magũri, move faster. d. caus. neut. (of fighting bulls) defeat and cause to run away. kũrũkithania, d. caus. rec. neut. (of victorious bull, &c.) chase the defeated one. niria, d. caus. rec. app. neut. (of fighting bulls, &c.) chase one another across, through, at, a place. kũrũkithiria, d. caus. app. neut. (of victorious bull, &c.) chase across, through, at.

I1. ~ mwaki, set fire to; ~ rũtungu, stick a log into the fire. 2. ~ trio, eat with gusto, fill the mouth. 3. mbũtũ ĩkĩ~, the company started to move (in ranks or single file). mundithia, d. caus. 1. ~ gũthiũ, begin to go. 2. assist in piercing, inoculating ; have pierced, inoculated. 3. have logs put on fire in house, light fire on pile of logs in wood-distillation. 4. ~ nyondo, (of boy and girl, man and woman) embrace intimately. 5. assist, encourage, sb. to eat food. mundithangia, diff. d. caus. mundithania, rec. d. caus. I1. put end to end; ~ ndungu, arrange logs in such a way that burning ends meet; cf. turumithania. 2. foment enmity between, set against one another; ~ ndeto, pass word from one to another, from mouth to mouth; cf. ringaniriria. mundithaniria, app. d. caus. mundika, st. pot. mundira, app. pierce at, for. refl. app. mundirira, inten.; cf. thecerera. ĩmundirira, refl. inten. I. pierce and cause trouble. 2. fall to; ~ kũrĩra, burst into tears. 3. (of a sharp object) lodge itself in. rmundirĩria, refl. caus. app. (of sth. sharp) lodge itself in sth. tough, hard.

(b) at one; me rũ~, they are in company, together, in sth.; matirĩ rũ~, they are not united, do not belong to the same ground, have no connexion; tuma ri~ na, be in league with; ¿kara o rũ~, live in unity; gĩa rũ~ na, be united with. (c) ka~, once (ordinal number). (d) ũ~ na, the same thing as. (e) ka~, together. (f) ko~, the same place; see also mwemwe.

(U), indicating affirmation, used as follows: r. as simple copula; (he, she, it) is, (they) are (for ref. to place see -rĩ). 2. as a verbal prefix normally written conjunctively, with all Indicative tenses, when initiating statements or asking questions of fact; ~aathiire? Ni, ~aathiire, is it a fact that he went, did he actually go? yes, he did. 3. foll. by a v. in the subj. indicating a strong exhortation (written conjunctively) ~tahoe, let us pray. 4. joined to the indep. pron. forms of all classes, it is (it) (they), by (it) (them); see -we, also nĩkĩo, nĩrĩo, nĩguo under -o. 5. with getha, kenda, gũkorũo, gũthurũo, see getha, kenda, kora, thura. 6. after certain intransitive vv. and the passive; batara ~, be in need of; ~, be beaten by. 7. as equivalent to nĩ ũndũ wa; nĩndakarĩte ~we, I am angry because of his behaviour; mbembe ~ riũa, the maize is withering because of the drought. 8. with certain interrog. part.; ~ kĩ?, see under ki; ~ see kĩ; ~ rĩ?, see rĩ, ~ ha?, see ha; ~ kũ?, see kũ. 9. with other part. and v. pieces; ~rĩ, ~ngĩrĩ, see under -ri; ~ wa, see under wa; ~ngari, see under anga.

no [noo], part. (U), emphatic form of ni: 1. (it is) just, only, certainly; ~ za, (itis) just like, about; ~ ici, only these; ~ Ũmwe, just one; ~ gĩkĩ kĩega, this is the only good one, the better, the best, one; ~wa, see under -Wa. 2. used also in the following ways: (i). i combination with the independent pronouns, all classes; ~we, it is you, it is he (she); cf. niwe; ~guo, it is just like that, that is it; gĩkĩ ~kĩo, that is right; cf. nĩguo; ~kĩo gĩkĩ, this is certainly it; ~kco, that is why; cf. nĩkĩo; ~rĩo, that is when; cf. nirio. (ii). yet, still; ~ Akũmuona?, do you still see him?’ (iii). only by, only because of; agiririo ~ hinya, he was prevented only by force; ekire ~ kũnana, he did it just out of mischief. (iv). foll. by v. in subj. expr. necessity; ~ wohwo, you will have to be locked up. (v). foll. by getha; ~ getha, only in order that. (vi). but; nĩendaga gũthĩ ~ ndari etikĩrio, he was longing to go but he has not been given permission; ~ rĩrĩ, but; ~ kaĩ, but for all that, nevertheless. (vii). in combination with anga, ~nga, ~ngana (~ anga (na)), see under anga. (viii). with gũkorũo, ~ gũkorũo, &c.; see under kora.

NEW_VALUE: make smell at the same time as
FULL_ENTRY: nunga, v.i. (1), smell (pleasantly or otherwise); ~ smell bad, stink; ~ mbaara, scent battle, be in a mood for a fight or for beating sb. (esp. a child); ~ mbura, (of the atmosphere) show every sign of rain. Wa mũru ũ~ga Ũri thiaka, the wicked man’s (arrow) emits its unpleasant smell even if hidden in a quiver. nunganunga, dim. smell a little bit. nunganungira, app. dim. nunganungũra, conv. dim. nunganungũka, neut. conv, nunganga, diff. nungangira, app. diff. nunganĩra, assoc. I. (of animals when they meet) sniff at each other; sniff at sb. 2. (of two or more things) smell at the same time. nunganiria, caus. app. rec. 1. make smell at the same time as. 2. speak evil of sb. as well as sb. else. nunegia, caus. I. make smell, scent; impart a smell to. 2. speak evil of. Mũndũ ndanungagia tũnya tuothe, a person does not scent all his calabashes at the same time (does not quarrel with all his friends at once). refi. caus. cause oneself to smell; get oneself disliked. nungithia, d. caus. make smell; cause sth. to be spoken evil of. nungithiria, app. d. caus. nungika, st. pot. nungira, app. nungiria, caus. app. cause to smell, at, for. nungirĩra, inten. smell at sth. at. nungirithia, d. caus. app. take a smell at, hold sth. to sb.’s nose. nungũra, conv. take away a smell, purify; ~ mwata, kill a ram to purify a beehive whose honey has been stolen by a man or a ratel; ~ mũciũ, purify a home by killing a ram after the owner has died. nungũranga, diff. nungũranganira, app. rec. diff. nungũrangithia, d. caus. diff. nungũrangira, app. dif. nungũrana, rec, deodorize, purify, sb., each other. nungũranira, app. rec. nungũraniria, caus. app. rec, nunguria, caus. nungũrithia, d. caus. assist in deodorizing; have sb. (a beehive, a house, &c.) purified, deodorized. nungũrithania, rec. d. caus. have sb. purified; have people, things, deodorize each other. nungũrithaniria, app. rec. d. caus. have people, things, deodorize each other at a place simultaneously; have people, things, purified, deodorized at the same time. nungũrithĩria, app. d. caus. have sth. sb. deodorized, purified, at, for. nungũrĩka, st. pot. nungũrira, app. nungũrĩria, caus. app. nungũririra, inten. nungũka, neut. (of a smelly rotting thing washed and put out in the sun, &c.) cease to smell, nungũkanga, diff. neut. nungũkaneithia, d. caus. diff. neut. nungũkangira, app. diff. neut. nungũkanira, assoc. neut. nungũkaniria, caus. app. rec. nungũkia, caus. neut. cause to cease to smell. nungũkithia, d. caus. neut. nungũkithania, rec. d. caus. neut. nungũkithaniria, app. rec. d. caus. neut. make people cease to have a smell, purify sb., at, for. nungũkika, st., pot. nungũkira, app.

FULL_ENTRY: ng’ata!, v.t. (11), cut, chop down (hard wood) with a blunt (knife, axe, &c.); wound badly. ĩng’ata, refl. wound oneself seriously. ng’atang’ata, dim. ng’atanga, diff. ng’atithia, d. caus, ng’atika, st. pot. 1. die of an epidemic. 2. be cuttable. ng’atĩra, app

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. push right on, walk straight on without halting. 2. travel along (a long hot road)
NEW_VALUE: push right on, walk straight on without halting
FULL_ENTRY: ng’ata? | ng’aata, v.i. (11), 1. push right on, walk straight on without halting. 2. travel along (a long hot road); climb up a steep slope, ng’ang’ata, dim. I1. tramp heavily. 2. climb, a mountain, a long steep slope. ng’atanga, diff. ng’atanira, assoc. ng’atira, app. v.i. (11), dry up, die, go stiff; cf. ng’ara!, ng’atika, st. pot. come to an end, be finished; (of people, animals) die off, diminish, shrink and come to an end, be decimated, wiped out, annihilated by an epidemic; (of a sick child) waste away; (of an ulcer) flake away; (of a river) dwindle, dry up. ng’atĩkia, caus. st. pot. cause to diminish, dwindle, die off. ng’ati- BT app. st. pot. ng’atikĩria, caus. app. st. pot.
ng’ Mo f ng’ati, ~, n. (111), dry place.

NEW_VALUE: irio cia ~, plain mashed food, boiled potato or banana without intermixture of beans, &c
FULL_ENTRY: king’atũ, n. (vu); irio cia ~, plain mashed food, boiled potato or banana without intermixture of beans, &c.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: shake by the hand. ng’athana, rec. shake one another’s hand. ng’athanira, app. rec, ng’athaniria, caus. app. rec. (of persons) stare at each other in confused anger, be hard on one another. ng’athia, be obstinate (in wonder, anger, or fright), be surprised (at abnormality). ng’athithia, d. caus.
FULL_ENTRY: ng’atha, v.t. (1), shake by the hand. ng’athana, rec. shake one another’s hand. ng’athanira, app. rec, ng’athaniria, caus. app. rec. (of persons) stare at each other in confused anger, be hard on one another. ng’athia, be obstinate (in wonder, anger, or fright), be surprised (at abnormality). ng’athithia, d. caus.; ndeto icio nĩcia~, I am astounded by the matter. ng’athĩka, st. pot. ng’athira, app. ng’athiria, caus. app. be obstinate to; tiga k~, do not stand in my way, do not stare at me (in obstinacy). ng’athũra, conv. let off the grip suddenly. ng’athũka, neut. (of hand grasp) be broken, slip off. [cf. Ma. NG’ASA]

ng’athia, v.i. (11), 1. show resentment, anger; be defiant, defy, adopt an insolent attitude. 2. be hard, unyielding (as a piece of dried up hide, or unripe banana); be barren. ng’athang’athia, dim. ng’athangia, diff. ng’athangiria, app. diff. ng’athithania, caus. rec. ng’athithia, d. caus. ng’athiria, app. look defiantly, defy, behave insolently towards a bully, show insubordination: to. ng’athanĩria, app. rec. face each other defiantly. ng’'’athũka, neut. unbend, relax toward, take notice of sb. ng’'athũkanga, neut. diff. ng’athara, stc. 1. feel very resentful, be stubbornly defiant. 2. be dry. [cf. Ma, ING’ASYA]
kĩng’athia, i-, n., (111), defiant person. [ngathia]
rũng’athio, ng’athio, n. (111), defiance, insolence, arrogance. [ng athia]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of biting hard (as an ant, dog, child biting another in anger)
NEW_VALUE: of biting hard (as an ant, dog, child biting another in anger)
FULL_ENTRY: ng’au, ideo. (1/11), descr. of biting hard (as an ant, dog, child biting another in anger); aria ~, mumble.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of cows cropping grass, lambs champing at sweet-potato vines from a bunch, children eating with gusto
NEW_VALUE: of cows cropping grass, lambs champing at sweet-potato vines from a bunch, children eating with gusto
FULL_ENTRY: ng’au-ng’au, ideo. (1), descr. of cows cropping grass, lambs champing at sweet-potato vines from a bunch, children eating with gusto; cf. maumau, ng'auria.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: take greedy voracious bites at sth.
FULL_ENTRY: ng’auria, v.t. (11), take greedy voracious bites at sth.; cf. mauria, kenyũria. ng’aurangia, diff. ng’'aurania, caus, rec. bite off, bite one another. ng’auraniria, caus. app. rec. ng’aurithia, d. caus. ng’aurika, st. pot. ng’aurira, app. ng’auriria, caus. app. ,
kang'’aurũ, tũ-, n. (1x), scorpion, kĩng’aurũ, ĩ-, n. 1. aug. of above. 2. any biting creature equipped with mandibles (e.g. brown ant, &c.). [ng auria]
mũng’'aũ!, mi-, n. (11), sp. of chameleon; see
mũng’'aũ? | mũng’'au, mi-, n, (11), (arch.) cat; cf. mbaka, nyau.
ng’aũko, see ngarũko.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. topple over, knock down from the position of stability, overturn, push off, 2. shave (the whole head) clean. fng’aũra, refl. ng’'aũranga, diff. ng’'aũrana, rec. knock sb., each other, down. ng’aũrania, caus. rec. turn right over. ng'’aũrangania, diff. caus. rec. ng’aũranira, app. rec. ng’aũrithia, d. caus. ng’aũrika, st. pot. ng’aũrira, app. ĩng’aũrira, refl. app. laugh uproariously (until one falls backwards). ng’aũka, v.i. 1. topple. over
FULL_ENTRY: ng’aũra, v.t. (11), I. topple over, knock down from the position of stability, overturn, push off, 2. shave (the whole head) clean. fng’aũra, refl. ng’'aũranga, diff. ng’'aũrana, rec. knock sb., each other, down. ng’aũrania, caus. rec. turn right over. ng'’aũrangania, diff. caus. rec. ng’aũranira, app. rec. ng’aũrithia, d. caus. ng’aũrika, st. pot. ng’aũrira, app. ĩng’aũrira, refl. app. laugh uproariously (until one falls backwards). ng’aũka, v.i. 1. topple. over; die. 2. descend the leeward, other, side of a hill, ridge. ng’aũ-kana, rec. neut. topple over. ng’'aũkia, caus. neut. cause to topple over; ~ mbũri ngarũko ĩrĩa, send the goats down the other side (of the hill). ĩng’aũkithia, refl. d. caus. pretend to topple over. ng’aũkira, app. 1. (of a jug of water, &c.) topple over (upon sb.), fall down (at a place). 2. descend the other side of a ridge (at a certain point). ng’aũkiria, caus. app.
mũng’'aũranio, mi-, n. (111), overthrow, overturning. [ng aũra]

ng’ea, v.t. (11), cut throat, chop head off; kill, butcher, slaughter. ng’eang’ea, dim. (in a dance) walk with sore feet. ng'’eanga, diff. cut more of the neck of the animal; keep on butchering. ng’'eana, rec. slaughter others, each other. ng’eania, caus. rec, ng’eanira, app. rec. ng’eaniria, caus. app. rec. ng’eithia, d. caus. rng’eithia, refl. d. caus. (used impolitely) have one’s head cut off (by thugs). ng’eeka, st. pot. ng’'eera', app. cut for, at. a-, n. (III), professional cut-throat; cf. [ng ea]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. trim, clip, cut short or level. 2. (coll.) cheat, cheat over a bargain. ng’'eerang’'eera, dim. ng'eeranga, diff. ng’eerana, rec. deceive, cheat
NEW_VALUE: trim, clip, cut short or level
FULL_ENTRY: ng’eera?, v.t. (11), I. trim, clip, cut short or level. 2. (coll.) cheat, cheat over a bargain. ng’'eerang’'eera, dim. ng'eeranga, diff. ng’eerana, rec. deceive, cheat; be fraudulent; act deceptively, fraudulently. ng’eerania, caus. rec. ng’'eeranira, app. rec. ng’'eeraniria, caus. app. rec. ng’eerithia, d. caus. ng’eereka, st. pot. ng’'eerera, app. ng’'eererera, inten.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sharp-fingered person, expert thief of goats.
NEW_VALUE: sharp-fingered person, expert thief of goats
FULL_ENTRY: kĩng’eeri, i-, n. (111), sharp-fingered person, expert thief of goats. [ngea]

ng’eeta, v.i. (1), 1. be arrogant, presumptuous, rebellious; be confident in one’s ability, strength; mũng eetete ta ihũĩ cia mũturi, you are as arrogant as the sons of a blacksmith. 2. be tight, taut. ng’eetang’eeta, dim. ng’eetanga, diff. ng’eetangia, caus. diff. ng’eetania, caus. rec. ng’'eetanira, assoc. behave aggressively together. ng’eetaniria, caus. assoc. ng’eetia', caus. ng’eetera, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. white patch, ruff, ring on neck, face, of monkey
NEW_VALUE: white patch, ruff, ring on neck, face, of monkey
FULL_ENTRY: kĩng’eeti, i-, n. (1), 1. white patch, ruff, ring on neck, face, of monkey; cf. 2. smear, smudge, of white or black; cf. kĩmeni. 3. long eyebrows of monkey; beaver of cap or helmet.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: pay a call on, see if sb. is there, has come, &c.
NEW_VALUE: pay a call on, see if sb
FULL_ENTRY: ng’eetia?, v.t. (1), pay a call on, see if sb. is there, has come, &c.; see if sth. has happened or not; keep an eye on, peep, peer at; ~ kana kaana nĩ kararĩra, see if the baby is crying. ng’eetang’eetia, dim. ng’eetang’'eetangia, diff. dim. ng’eetang’eetangithia, d. caus. diff. dim, ng’'eetang’eetangiria, app. diff. dim. ng’eetangia, diff. ng’'eetaniria, app. rec. ng’eetithia, d. caus. ng’eeteria, app. regard, watch, look at sth. for sb., at a place., ĩng’eeteria, refi. peep at sth. for one’s own satisfaction.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of anything strung, a rope, stringed instrument, &c.) tightness, tautness, state of being taut.
NEW_VALUE: (of anything strung, a rope, stringed instrument, &c
FULL_ENTRY: ũng’eetu, n. cl. 14 (1v), (of anything strung, a rope, stringed instrument, &c.) tightness, tautness, state of being taut. [ng eeta]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. big sharp knife, axe, guillotine. 2. thief of violent type. (mi-)
NEW_VALUE: term of address to married woman with children not yet circumcised
FULL_ENTRY: kĩng’ei, i-, n. (111), 1. big sharp knife, axe, guillotine. 2. thief of violent type. (mi-); kang’ei!, (tũ-); n. married woman belonging to the grade whose children are not yet circumcised; cf. wa kang’ei. nyakang’ei, n. with cl. 1/2 concords, term of address to married woman with children not yet circumcised; cf. nyakĩnyua.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: any creature that walks or moves on the ground.
NEW_VALUE: any creature that walks or moves on the ground
FULL_ENTRY: ng’enda-thi, ~, n. (1-11), any creature that walks or moves on the ground. ~ ndĩagaga mũtegi, every animal has someone ready to trap it. [genda]
ng’'endo, see rũgendo.

ng’'eng’a, v.t. (1), I1. put to the test; try one’s strength (against sb.). 2. carry a heavy load, try to lift a heavy object. ĩng’'eng’a, refl. 1. summon up one’s strength; brace oneself; be daring, be a dare-devil. 2. ~ na wĩra, tackle a hard job with determination, ng’ eng’ang’eng’a, dim. ng’eng’anga, diff. ng 'eng’ana, rec. pit strength against one another, come to grips with each other; ~ na, wrestle, struggle with. ng’ eng’ anĩra, app. rec. ng’ eng’aniria, caus. app. rec. ng’'eng’anithia, d. caus. rec. ng’eng’ithia, d. caus. ng’eng’'eka, st. pot. ng'’eng’era, app. ng’eng’eria, caus. app.
mũng’'eng’ereko [mũng eeng eereko], mĩ-, n. (11), gadding about. [ng eng

ORIGINAL_VALUE: argue, bicker, try to do sth. that is hard
NEW_VALUE: argue, bicker, try to do that is hard
FULL_ENTRY: ng’enya, v.i. (11), argue, bicker, try to do sth. that is hard; cf. gekenya. ng’'enyang’enya, dim. ng’'enyanga, diff. ng’enyana, rec. ng'enyanira, assoc, ng’'enyaniria, caus. assoc, ng’enyithia, d. caus. ng'enyeka, st. pot. ng’'enyera, app.
ne'enyano, ~, n, (I1), argument, altercation, bickering, strife. [ng enya]

ng'enyũ, ~, n. (vi), unruly, wilful, rebellious, person; naughty, wilful, child; cf. ndũrĩka, kĩnari. Ũgenyũ, n. cl, 14 only, 1. naughtiness, selfwill, conceit. 2. lameness; cf. &ũgũ, Ũrema; see genyũka.
mang’'ereng’ere, n. cl. 6 (v), roughness of surface, ruggedness.

ng’eta, v.i. (1), 1. be dangerous, difficult; be too big or heavy to deal with. 2. (of swollen river) be difncult to cross. ng’'etang’'eta, dim. ng'etanga, diff. ng’'etanĩra, assoc. ng’etaniria, caus. assoc. ng'’etia, caus. cause to be daring. ng'etithia, d. caus. ngg’etera, app. ng’eteria, caus. app.
-n’eti, a. (1), obstinate. [ng eta]
rũng’eto, n. cl. 11 (1), arrogance, presumptuousness. [ng eta]
ũng’etu, n. cl, 14 (1), (of a river, &c.) formidableness; (of people) dumb defiance. [ng eta]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of teeth, &c.) protrude. 2. stand ajar, gape, show (teeth), stare with open mouth. ng’ethanga, dif. ng’ethangia, caus. diff. ng’ethangĩra, app. diff. caus. app. dim. ng’ethanira, assoc. I1. sit opposite one another
FULL_ENTRY: ng’etha, v.i. (11), 1. (of teeth, &c.) protrude. 2. stand ajar, gape, show (teeth), stare with open mouth. ng’ethanga, dif. ng’ethangia, caus. diff. ng’ethangĩra, app. diff. caus. app. dim. ng’ethanira, assoc. I1. sit opposite one another; ~ na, be face to face with. 2. face one another (in a lawsuit). 3. be protruding, cropping out, at the same time as. ng'ethanĩria, caus. assoc. cause people, things, to face each other; build homes, villages, on opposite sides of a valley so that they face each other. ng’ethia, caus. I1. ~ magego, bare teeth, have long teeth that show. 2. gape, be ajar. ng’'ethera, app. I1. protrude, crop out, at, for. 2. be facing a certain direction; ki~ gwa Kamau opposite Kamau’s home. 3. watch for sb., go and see, visit; be in full view; cf. ng’eetia?. ng’etheria, caus. app. ng’'ethũra, conv. break off sth. hard (e.g. stone); chip off the edge (of sth. brittle). ng'ethang’ethũra, dim. conv. ng’'ethũranga, diff. conv. ng’ethũrana, rec. ng'ethũrania, caus. rec. ng’ethũranira, app. rec. ng’ethũraniria, caus. app. rec. ng'’ethũria, caus. ng’'ethũrithia, d. caus. ng’ethũrika, st. pot. ng’ethũrĩra, app. ng’'ethũrĩria, caus, app. ng'ethũrĩrira, inten: ng’ethũka, neut. get broken off; be, become, chipped. ng’ethang’ethũka, dim. ng’'ethgokanga, diff. ng’ethũkangia, caus. diff. ng’ethũkangira, app. dif. ng'ethũkana, rec. be broken in two. ng’ethũkania, caus. rec. ng’'ethũkanira, app. rec. ng’'ethũkaniria, caus. app. rec. ng’ethũkithia, d. caus. ng’ethũkĩka, st. pot. ng’ethũkira, app.
mang’'etheng’ethe, n. cl. 6 (v), ragged edges, raggedness. [ng etha]
kang’ethera, (tũ-), n. (111), position of being opposite, facing. [ng etha]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who looks after, keeps a watch over, another.
NEW_VALUE: one who looks after, keeps a watch over, another. [ng etha]
FULL_ENTRY: mũng’etheri, a-, n. (111), one who looks after, keeps a watch over, another. [ng etha]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: open place opposite the homestead (used for meetings, &c.).
NEW_VALUE: open place opposite the homestead (used for meetings, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũng’etho, mi-, n. (11), open place opposite the homestead (used for meetings, &c.). [ng etha]
ng’ethũ, ideo. (11/1/11), descr. of sth. hard breaking off. [cf. ng ethũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. space, gap left in the mouth when a tooth is removed. 2. notch left in a straight sharp object (e.g. the rim of a calabash) when chipped
NEW_VALUE: space, gap left in the mouth when a tooth is removed
FULL_ENTRY: mũng’'ethũ, mi-; i-; n. (11), 1. space, gap left in the mouth when a tooth is removed. 2. notch left in a straight sharp object (e.g. the rim of a calabash) when chipped; cf. king'uru. kang’ethũ, tũ-, n. dim. of above. [ng etha]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of a house) state of being bare, stripped of thatch
NEW_VALUE: (of a house) state of being bare, stripped of thatch
FULL_ENTRY: kĩng’'eũ, i-, n. (vi1), 1. (of a house) state of being bare, stripped of thatch; ~, hut under construction, unthatched and unplastered. 2. (of clearing in forest, grain-store, door of house) state of being open; tiga mũrango ~, leave the door wide open.

NEW_VALUE: noise made by iron on iron
FULL_ENTRY: ng’i, ideo. (u), descr. of: 1. noise made by iron on iron; gamba ~, 3 clang; cf. cangarara. 2. completeness; iyũria ~, fill to the brim.

mũng’i/ mũngi-mũng’i/ mũng’ũ / mũng’'ũ-mũng’'ũ, n. cl. 3 only (1), (used adv.) 1. at a bound; ugio ~, be pounced upon. 2. in the act; nyita mici ~, grab a thief on the spot; ~ na ~ ĩri kũ?, (N.K.) what is the direct evidence?
neg’'ima, see -gima,

ng’irang’ira!, v.i. (11), faint, be unconscious. assoc. (of two or more living things) become unconscious simultaneously. ng’'irang’'iraniria, caus. assoc. n'irang'iria, caus. cause sb., animal, to faint, become unconscious from a hard blow. ng'irang'irithia, d. caus. ng’irang’irika, st. pot. ng’irira, app. ng’'irang’iriria, caus. app.

NEW_VALUE: drawing a knife across or cutting
FULL_ENTRY: ng’iri, ideo. (1/11), descr. of: 1. drawing a knife across sth. or cutting. 2. the sound of such actions (which gives one a shrinking sensation).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: metal bangle. kang’iri, tũ-, n. cent of a shilling
FULL_ENTRY: mũng’iri, mi-, n. (v11), metal bangle. kang’iri, tũ-, n. cent of a shilling; cf. gathendi, gacirikn; cf. ngiriri-ri-ri.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of the sound made by a small bell, bicycle bell, electric bell, hand bell, or small metallic objects such as coins
NEW_VALUE: of the sound made by a small bell, bicycle bell, electric bell, hand bell, or small metallic objects such as coins
FULL_ENTRY: ng’iri-ri-ri-ri, ideo. (1), descr. of the sound made by a small bell, bicycle bell, electric bell, hand bell, or small metallic objects such as coins; cf, ciriciri.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. cut (tail of animal, finger, &c.). 2. saw through, cut crosswise, sever
FULL_ENTRY: ng’irita / ng’uruta, v.t. (11), I. cut (tail of animal, finger, &c.). 2. saw through, cut crosswise, sever; chop, slash, snick off. ĩng'irita, ref. ngg’itanga, diff. ng’iritana, rec. ng’iritanĩra, app. rec. ng'iritaniria, caus., app. rec. ng'iritithia, d. caus ĩng’iritithia, refi. d. caus. ng'iritika, st. pot. ng'iritĩra, app.

NEW_VALUE: of the sound of cutting through with a sawing moyement, [ng irita]
FULL_ENTRY: ng’'iriti, ideo. (1), descr. of the sound of cutting through sth. with a sawing moyement, [ng irita]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. dark spot, stain, black patch on sword or spear due to imperfection of workmanship or of burnishing
NEW_VALUE: dark spot, stain, black patch on sword or spear due to imperfection of workmanship or of burnishing
FULL_ENTRY: ng’'irũ [ngiirũ], ~ | ma-; king’irũ, i-; n, (vin), 1. dark spot, stain, black patch on sword or spear due to imperfection of workmanship or of burnishing; unfinished patch, unweeded portion of a garden. 2. blemish, dark place, in the heart.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be sullen, cf. ng’athia. ng’'itang’ita, dim. ng’'itanga, diff. ng’'itanira, assoc. ng’itaniria, caus. assoc. ng’itia, caus. ngg'itithia, d. caus. ng’'itika, st, pot. ng'itira, app. be sullen towards sb., for, at (a place, a certain time). ng’'itũra, conv. make sb. change from sullenness to happiness
FULL_ENTRY: ng'ita, v.i. (1), be sullen, cf. ng’athia. ng’'itang’ita, dim. ng’'itanga, diff. ng’'itanira, assoc. ng’itaniria, caus. assoc. ng’itia, caus. ngg'itithia, d. caus. ng’'itika, st, pot. ng'itira, app. be sullen towards sb., for, at (a place, a certain time). ng’'itũra, conv. make sb. change from sullenness to happiness; hit an obstinate person in the hope of changing his attitude. ng’itũka, neut. be, become, happy after one has been sullen. ng’'itũkania, caus. rec. neut, ng’'itũkanira, assoc., neut, ng'itũkaniria, caus. assoc, neut. ng’'itũkia, caus. neut. ng’'itũkithia, d. caus. app. ng'itũkiria, caus. app. neut.
king’ita, see kinyita.
n’ia!, see ng’ea.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: jog along (as a tired person). ng’iang’ĩna, dim. trot
FULL_ENTRY: ng’ia?, v.i. (11), jog along (as a tired person). ng’iang’ĩna, dim. trot; (of a dog, mongoose, old man, child) toddle along; cf. ranjaranja, nĩania, bĩabia, cĩng’acĩng’a.
kĩng’iũ, see king’aũ.

NEW_VALUE: a rap on the head or on a gourd, &c
FULL_ENTRY: ng’o, ideo. (U), descr. of: 1. a rap on the head or on a gourd, &c. 2. a sharp tap, whack or flick with a whip, flexible wand, or rod.

ng'ombe, ~, n. (1), cattle, cow; ~ ya mũgoma, heifer; ~ cia atumia, (ref. to legend about women being punished for keeping cattle by having their cows turned into buffalo) see mbogo; cf. mbũri cta atumia; ~!, (of a person) foolish one who lets others take his property. kĩgombe, i-, n. 1. sp. of grass fed to cattle; cf. witima. 2. njuĩrĩ cia ~, soft pliable hair of European, Somali, or Indian type. kagombe, tũ-, n. dim.
ng’ongo, see rũgongo.

NEW_VALUE: drink copiously, be a copious drinker by habit
FULL_ENTRY: ne'’ong’a, v.t. (1) drink copiously, be a copious drinker by habit.
ng’'ong’oa, see ng'ong’ora.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of child, youth, woman, who has been silenced or made angry) go on muttering in dissatisfaction.
NEW_VALUE: (of child, youth, woman, who has been silenced or made angry) go on muttering in dissatisfaction
FULL_ENTRY: ng’ong’oira, v.i. (11), (of child, youth, woman, who has been silenced or made angry) go on muttering in dissatisfaction. ng’'ong’ang’'ong’oira, dim, ng’'ong’oiranga, diff. ng'ong’oiria, caus. ng'’ong’oirithia, d. caus. ng’'ong’oirika, st. pot. ng’'ong’oirira, app. ng’'ong’oiriria, app. caus.
ng’ong’o-ng'ong’o, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1. repressed murmuring, dissatished muttering. 2. mocking imitation of a sick person’s nasal way of speaking. [see ngongora]
kĩng’ong’ora [king oong oora], i-, n. (ur), grumbler, mumbler. [ngongora]
-ng'ong’ori a. (111), given to mumbling, complaining. [ng ong ora]

NEW_VALUE: the subdued sound of people’s voices (as people conversing in a hut)
FULL_ENTRY: ng’oo, ideo. (U), descr. of: 1. the subdued sound of people’s voices (as people conversing in a hut); the hum, of conversation, of a car-engine; the sound of a horn or hooter; deep snoring. 2. the groan of a falling tree or cut banana-palm before crashing.

NEW_VALUE: curved attachment of sweet-potato to its root
FULL_ENTRY: kĩng’oothi, i-, n. (1), 1. curved attachment of sweet-potato to its root. 2. * (from analogy of shape of above with clitoris) term of abuse used in scolding children. 3. ceremony for removing sterility in women; see ng'’ootha. 4. stiff, dry, rucked-up leather shirt or apron. [ngootha]

(of goats) snuffle up, scour about for, nose around for; cf. haara. 3. drink a little, take a few sips of. ng’orang’ora, dim. ng’orang’oria, caus. dim. ~ gacohi (gacũarũ), make a little beer (gruel). ng'’oranga, diff. ng’orithia, d. caus.

ng’oro-ng’oro | ng’ororo, ideo. (1), descr. of sound of snoring; cf. ng'oo.

ng’ũri, ideo. (1); gamba ~, (of a goatbell) ring, jingle.

njaguara / njiguara, ~, n. (111), 1. well-shaped ox-horn; maĩ ma ~, Kikuyu beer. 2. she-goat with long horns.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) season of daily light rain by day
NEW_VALUE: () season of daily light rain by day
FULL_ENTRY: njakoira, n. (v), (N.K.) season of daily light rain by day; cf. mbura ya mwere, themithũ; see mwaka.

njama [njaama], ~, n. (1), manpower, organized or unorganized; potential people for a task, those waiting to serve an individual group or country; ~ ya ita, young men of warrior grade who served as military and police force; ~ cia ita, organized regiments of warriors; ~ ya aanake, council of warriors parallel to the elders’ council with military and civil functions; ~ Ja aanake a muumo, council of junior warriors; ~ ya ihĩ, boys’ council, for matters concerning the communal life of uninitiated boys; cf. ngũtũ; ~ ya atumia, aka, gathering of married women to make representations regarding their interests to neglectful husbands, to exact fines for maltreatment, to impose penalties on women who brought their sex into disrepute, &c.; ~ ya airĩtu, gathering of initiated girls for disciplining offenders among themselves; ~ ya nyũmba, gathering of male aviks of extended family; ~ ya mũhĩrĩga, wandnũgũ, touring deputation of representatives of a clan to revive contacts between its sections and deal with matters of common concern; ~ ya ituĩka, body of representative elders chosen to operate customs connected with the periodic transfer of governmental responsibility; see ituika; kwarahũka kwa ~, a ‘rising’ of young warriors to exact fines from habitual offenders against social custom and enforce decisions of elders; ~ ya bũrũri, manpower available in a country; thi, ~, visitation by one of the groups referred to above in the exercise of its powers; iga mũka ~, place one’s wife at service of visiting delegate(s); ~ yake, his companions. mũnjama [mũnjaama], njama, n. cl. 1/9, chief’s retainer, attendant; messenger of Local Tribunal, member of tribal police. gacama [gacaama], tũ-, n. a small group meeting together for a short period.

(b) ~ ya thĩ, general term for snake; cf. nyoka. (c) ~ ya rĩera-inĩ, bird of the air, cf. nyoni; ~ ya igũrũ, any large highflying bird, bird of prey. (d) ~-yaheho, (N.K.) colobus monkey. (e) ~-yambũri, (N.K.) leopard. (f) ~-cia-ũtukũ, safari ants (used at night to avoid referring to thuraku q.v.). 3. thing, object, anything not readily nameable or which one does not wish to name (esp. ref. to an object in cl. 9/10.) 4. a man’s name (given to a new-born child after a number of children have died in the family, in order to change the run of bad luck and in the hope that the angry departed spirit would ignore this one). mũnyamg, mi-, n stuff, material, substance.
