ORIGINAL_VALUE: r. be little, small, in size, quantity, status
FULL_ENTRY: nyiha [nyiiha], v.i. (11), r. be little, small, in size, quantity, status; be few, decrease, diminish; ana, not less than four people. 2. (coll.) feel ashamed; cf. ingiha. nyihanyiha, dim. get smaller, less; diminish. nyihanyihia, caus. dim. reduce in size, quantity. nyihanyihiria, caus, app. dim. reduce, make smaller, for sb. at a place. nyihanga, diff. nyihangia, caus. diff.; ~ khwaĩ, spend a little bit more of the evening. nyihania, caus. rec. make sb., others, feel small. nyihanira, assoc. nyihaniria, caus. assoc. ĩnyihaniria, refl. caus. app. rec. be humble towards one another, to people. nyihia, caus. 1. reduce, make small, decrease. 2. be little; ciringi igana not less than Shs. r0o/-. inyihia, refl. caus. make oneself small, humble; show respect to superiors, elders, seniors. nyihithia, d. caus. nyihĩka, st. pot. be reduceable, diminishable. nyibira, app. be too small, for, at. nyihiria, caus. app. ĩnyihiria, refl. caus. app. be humble, respectful, to. nyihirĩra, inten. become reduced, shrink. nyihiriria, caus. inten. belittle in speech, run down, speak slightingly of, make appear to be too small or very unimportant; diminish unduly. rĩria, refl. caus. inten. make oneself too small, humble, servile; belittle, abase, oneself.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of squeaking as of trapped bird, mole, or rat, high-pitched rasping as of tree branches in a wind
NEW_VALUE: of squeaking as of trapped bird, mole, or rat, high-pitched rasping as of tree branches in a wind
FULL_ENTRY: nyii, nyii-nyii, ideo. (uU), descr. of squeaking as of trapped bird, mole, or rat, high-pitched rasping as of tree branches in a wind; cf. nyinyira, nyinyinga.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cry in a low voice (as a child).
NEW_VALUE: cry in a low voice (as a child)
FULL_ENTRY: nyiihĩka, v.i. (1), cry in a low voice (as a child). nyiihanyiihika, dim. nyiihikanga, diff. nyiihĩkanĩra, assoc. nyiihĩkanĩria, caus. assoc. nyiihĩkia, caus. nyiihĩkĩra, app. nyiihĩkĩria, caus. app.

NEW_VALUE: stare at sternly, regard with contempt
FULL_ENTRY: nyiira, v.t. (1) stare at sternly, regard with contempt; scowl, frown, at; look at contemptuously, quizzically, superciliously; show dislike or nausea by facial expression (often accompanied by some noise); cf. heta nyiiranyiira, dim. nyiiranga, diff. nyiirana, rec. nyiiranira, app. rec. nyiiravĩria, caus. app. rec. nyiirithia, d. caus. nyiirĩra, app.; ~ mwana, frown at sb. else’s child.
nyimba, see nyimbo under rũimbo.
nyimbo, see rũimbo.

nyina, ma- [|maanyina], n. with cl. 1/2 concords (v1), 1. his, her, its, their mother; ~ mũkũrg, mũnyinyi, senior, junior, mother (in a połygamous family); ~-wa-ndune, nickname for fire, cf. mwari-wa-ndune, Mwana wi ~ ndarĩaga mai, the child who has a mother does not have to eat dung. Cf. nana, yũyũ. 2. ~ wita, our mother, see maitũa, nyũkwa. 3. term of respect for girl or young woman; wa~, affectionate term of address used by siblings; kĩ~, (affectionate term of address, to a young woman) ‘my dear’.

nyingira [nyingiira], v.i. (11), squea!, grizzle, whimper, whine; (of water almost boiled away in a container) sizzle; squeak like a rat; speak in a thin voice; give expression to doubt or disappointment ; cf. cirira, nyinyira, nyinyinga, nikinga. nyingiranga, diff. nyingiranĩra, assoc. nyingiraniria, caus. assoc. nyingiria, caus. fnyingiria, refl. caus. nyingirĩra, app. nyingiriria, caus. app. [mũnyingiiri], n. cl. 3 only (1v), expressions of discontent; mũndaũ wa ~, discontented person, grumbler. [nyingira]
nyinya!, v.t. (11), 1. truss up, tie up thoroughly. 2. hold tightly, snatch away from sb.
nyinya?, (rare) var. of niina.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) twist, make a wry, face (as result of sour taste).
NEW_VALUE: () twist, make a wry, face (as result of sour taste)
FULL_ENTRY: nyinyikara, v.i. (11), (N.K.) twist, make a wry, face (as result of sour taste). 2. be sour-faced, bad-tempered looking.

nyinyira [nyinyiira], v.i. (11), 1. squeal, squeak; grizzle, whimper; (of a hawk, pig, trapped rat) give a thin squeak, squawk. 2. sizzle and splutter. nyinyanyinyira, dim. nyinyiranga, diff. nyinyiranĩra, assoc. nyinyiria, caus. I. make squeal, whimper. 2. make hiss, sizzle; make hissing sound (like white ants). nyinyirithia, d. caus. nyinyirika, st. pot. be squealing, whimpering. nyinyirira, app.

NEW_VALUE: there is nothing at all, no sound, no movement, nobody
FULL_ENTRY: kanyinyo, n. cl. 12 only (1); ~, there is nothing at all, no sound, no movement, nobody; cf. kanyuri.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. be satiated, have so much food that the sight of it makes you feel ill. 2. detest, hate, despise, loathe, regard with contempt
NEW_VALUE: be satiated, have so much food that the sight of it makes you feel ill
FULL_ENTRY: nyira, v.t. (1), 1. be satiated, have so much food that the sight of it makes you feel ill. 2. detest, hate, despise, loathe, regard with contempt; have a dislike for, express dislike in words. inyira, refl. hate oneself, be fearless; be, become, reckless, foolhardy, desperate; have no regard for one’s own safety; cf. thnũra, matha, ĩrega. nyiranga, diff. nyiranira, app. rec, nyiria, caus, nyirithia, d. caus. nyirira, app. nyirĩria, caus. app.

kĩnyiria, i-, n. (11), 1. surfeit; excess of fatty food causing discomfort; ndĩraigua ~, I am feeling sick; trio ici irĩ na ~, this food is too oily. 2. sourness of disposition, unfriendliness; wĩ ~, an unsociable person.

rũnyiriria [rũnyiriiria], nyiriria, n. (vir), 1. (of door, drawer, &c.) squeak; unpleasant, small sound, singing in ears, sensation of teeth on edge. 2. effect of sourness, teeth being on edge. [mũnyiiro], mi-, n. (1), any strip overlaying sth. of lighter colour; string of small beads, with small chains depending at intervals, connecting the front peaks of a warrior’s coiffure; any fillet of beads round the forehead of a young man; cf. mũkenye.

NEW_VALUE: hold, take hold, grasp, catch, acquire, receive
FULL_ENTRY: nyita [nyiita], v.t. & i. (1) 1. hold, take hold, grasp, catch, acquire, receive; arrest, capture; ~ mũcara, receive wages; ~ njĩra, set out, go away. 2. be tight, fast, firm, ĩnyita, refl. becalm, trap, oneself; ~ kanua, be surprised, say little or nothing. nyitanyita, dim. 1. touch lightly with the hands (as when groping for sth. in the dark. 2. hold (sth., a baby, &c.) a little longer. nyitanyitia, caus. dim. tighten a little more. nyitanyitithia, d. caus. dim. nyitanyitika, st. pot. dim. nyitanyitĩra, app. dim. nyitanyitũra, conv. dim. loosen, relax a little more. nyitanyitũka, neut. dim. be a little bit looser. nyitanyitũkia, caus. neut. dim. x1. let out a little, relax a little more. 2. have a headache, stomachache, which feels as if you had insects eating you away. nyitanga, diff. continue holding; take hold in several places; ~ indo ciene, steal other people’s property. nyitangia, caus. diff. nyitana, rec. 1. hold one another, come to grips with one another, fight. 2. capture, arrest, others. 3. coagulate, congeal; (of things that are glued, &c.) stick together; (of sides of wound, &c.) join together, heal up. nyitania, caus. rec. make hold one another, make fast, fix together, make thick, join together. nyitanira, app. rec. I. hold, seize, together. 2. congeal at. 3. be in agreement with, 4. arrest for, at, nyitanĩria, caus. app. rec. 1. hold (things) together; make hold fast at; combine. 2. arrest (people) at the same time. refi. caus. app. rec. stand with arms akimbo (doing nothing). nyitia, caus. tighten, make fast; ~ mũthiori, tighten waist-band, endure suffering and work harder; ~ rũrĩmĩ, bridle the tongue. nyitithia, d. caus. I. assist sb. to hold, seize; cause to be caught, taken hold of, arrested. 2. betray. inyitithia, refl. d. caus. 1. get oneself caught, arrested. 2., pretend to be catching sth. (in defiance of some other order). nyitĩka, st. pot. r. be holdable, catchable. 2. squirm, writhe with pain, have a seizure. nyitikana, rec, st. pot. 1. be fully grasped, be found. 2. be firmly seized, captured intact. nyitĩkia, caus. st, pot. (of pain, headache, &c.) be intense. nyitira, app. hold, catch, seize, by, at, for; ~ (mwari wa nynkwa) (hold the ear (of your sister) for me) introduce me to (your sister). nyitiria, caus. app. fasten, tighten, for sb. nyitirira, inten. 1. support, hold up, uphold, hold steady. 2. hold on to, support for sb. 3. deputize for sb. inyitĩrira, refl. inten. support oneself from falling, cling on to, fasten oneself on to; take a firm hold of; cf. ĩgwatĩrĩra. nyitĩrĩria, caus. inten. conv. loosen, remove from a grasp; cf. regerũra. nyitũrana, rec. conv. nyitũranĩra, app. rec. conv. nyitũria, caus. conv. nyitũrithia, d. caus. cony. nyitũrira, app. conv. nyitũka, neut. become loose. nyitũkia, caus. neut. r. let out (belt), relax, let go of. 2. cause to let go. 3. (of a spasm, &c.) seize suddenly. nyitũkĩria, caus. app. neut. have a seizure of pain at (a place).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. sediment, lees. 2. soaked paste of crushed corn used to make gruel
FULL_ENTRY: kĩnyita, i-, n. (1x), 1. sediment, lees. 2. soaked paste of crushed corn used to make gruel; see kia; cf. kĩmere. 3. gruel or any sticky substance adhering to pot, scrapings of pot. kanyita / kang’ita, ta-, n. sth. kept back; a lingering doubt, misgiving; ndiri na ~ na I owe nothing to anyone; cf. kamũkiha.
nyitano [nyiitano], ~, n. (xI), an arresting, capture. [nyita]
-nyite [-nyiite], a. (1v), held, caught, arrested. [nyita]

NEW_VALUE: meat given to those who help in the slaughtering of a beast
FULL_ENTRY: kĩnyiti [kĩnyiiti], i-, n. (1), a fastener, door-bolt; cf. kĩrhingi. ũnyiti, n. cl. t4 only, 1. meat given to those who help in the slaughtering of a beast. 2. a catching, capturing. [nyita]
kinyitio [kĩnyiitio] / kĩnyitirĩrio [kĩnyiitirĩrio], i-, n. (1), reef-knot, tight knot. [nyita]

NEW_VALUE: one who holds on behalf of another person
FULL_ENTRY: -nyitĩkanu [nyiitĩkanu], a. (1v), obtained, comprehended fully; cio ti wega, that information has not been fully grasped. [nyita] [mũnyiitĩrĩri], a&-, n. (x1), one who holds on behalf of another person; representative, deputy ; cf. mũrngamirĩri, mwarĩriria, mũciririri. [nyita]
mũnyito [mũnyiito], mi-, n. (1) seizure; kĩaro kĩa ~, forced labour, conscription. [nyita]
-nyitu [-nyiitu], a. (1v), tight, fast firm (gripping). [nyita]
nyitha, see nitha.

nyirĩ?, ideo. (1), descr. of spurting of liquid; gamba ~, spurt out; cf. mũnyĩri. nyiri-nyĩrĩ, ideo. descr. of intermittent trickling, little gushes; cf. nyirirĩrĩ, ideo. descr. of continuous flow of jet or thin stream of water, sound of water trickling in a furrow or ditch, flow of blood from the tapped vein of a cow or goat; see nyiririka.
Nyiri, n. with loc. concords, (vii), Nyeri Township, Nyeri Administrative District.

mũnyiri?, mi-, 1. long-flowing tail feather of bird. 2. small bird with long-flowing tail (Paradise Fly-catcher or Whydah). 3. mũka wa ~, Pintailed Widow Bird, Whydah; cf. mrũgũ, ndĩthi. kĩnyĩrĩ, i-, n. male bird of above spp. kanyiri, tũ-, n. I1. dim. of above. 2. long tail-feather of male bird. ~ kainagĩo ni mwene, a tail-feather can only be waved by its owner.

NEW_VALUE: flow, spurt smoothly in a thin trickle
FULL_ENTRY: nyĩrĩka, v.i. (11) spurt out, trickle, proceed in spurts. dim. spurt out intermittently. nyirikanga, diff. nyĩrĩkanĩra, assoc. caus. assoc. nyirikia, caus. I. cause to spurt; ~ thĩ, pour in a thin stream, sprinkle on the ground., 2. (of a cow) have diarrhoea. rikia, refl, caus. (of a liquid) cause itself to spurt, out, up. nyirikira, app. (of liquid) spurt, trickle on to sb., sth.; be sprinkled on to. nyirikĩria, caus. app. spurt, on, at, for. nyiririka, inten. 1. flow, spurt smoothly in a thin trickle; cf. cĩrĩrika. 2. proceed in same direction (usu. descending); (of arrow) fly; cf. thĩririka, nyĩrĩrĩkanga, inten. continue spurting for some time. nyiririkania, caus. rec. inten. go down to a place with, sb., others. nyirirĩkanira, assoc. inten. nyiririkaniria, caus. assoc. inten. nyirĩrĩkia, caus. inten. nyĩrĩrĩkithia, d. caus. inten. nyiririkĩra, app. inten. nyirĩrĩkĩria, caus. app. inten. nyĩrĩririka, d. inten. flow smoothly for a long time.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of water, &c.) continuous flow (from pipe, trickle, stream). 2. (of people) long line, string
FULL_ENTRY: kanyirĩnyirĩ, tũ-, n. (v) small whydahb-like bird with long waving tail (Paradise Fly-catcher); see mũnyũrũrũ. [see nyirirĩka] mi-, n. (1v), 1. (of water, &c.) continuous flow (from pipe, trickle, stream). 2. (of people) long line, string; thit ~, go in single file, move smoothly and swiftly; twara ndeto ~, speak fluently ; cf. kanyirĩrĩ, tũ-, n. strong spurt, spout, of water, &c. [nyiririka]
nyoge | (N.K.) nyogo, ~, n. (III), 1. tapeworm; segment of tapeworm; cf. ndangũrũ, njoka, nyamũ-ya-nda. 2. a term of abuse.
mũnyopi (N.K.), see mũnyugi.

ryogonda, v.t. (1), 1. bring confusion to, amongst; complicate, muddle, trouble sb.; cf. gaagaiya. 2. bring suffering to, cause severe pain by beating. 3. cheat, defraud by devious means, ĩnyogonda, refl. twist, roll, about; writhe (as one in agony or suffering from severe pain, eg. stomach-ache}; sit huddled up; cf. ĩthĩnia, hata, ĩcthunanĩra. nyogondanga, diff. nyogondana, rec. trouble sb., trouble each other. nyogondania, caus. rec. 1. bring in complications regarding sb., create a confused issue over sth. in order to cheat sb. out of it. 2. mix up things, unknowingly, in the dark. nyogondia, caus. 1. harass, perplex, bewilder. 2. starve. nyogondithia, d. caus. nyogondeka, st. pot. I. become confused, dget. 2. be victim of a fraud. nyogondera, app.
mũnyogonde (N.K.), see mwinyogondi.
mũnyogondo, mĩ-, n. (VII), (N.K.) tree (Brayera anthelmintica); cf. mũthithikũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. sth. long and narrow, not flexible (such as a bamboo, sugar-cane, prop for banana-palm, a long narrow gourd, the rafter of a round hut). 2. silly, irresponsible person
NEW_VALUE: long and narrow, not flexible (such as a bamboo, sugar-cane, prop for banana-palm, a long narrow gourd, the rafter of a round hut)
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyogonyogo | mũnyonyo!, mi-, n. (11), 1. sth. long and narrow, not flexible (such as a bamboo, sugar-cane, prop for banana-palm, a long narrow gourd, the rafter of a round hut). 2. silly, irresponsible person; a little fool.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: give a severe beating (with a green stick), make writhe in pain, ĩnyogora, refl. 1. evoke sympathy for oneself, writhe with pain. 2. be conceited, show off. nyogoranga, diff. nyogorana, rec. beat severely, whip, sb. nyogoranira, app. rec. nyogoraniria, caus. app. rec. nyogoria, caus. nyogorithia, d. caus, nyogorera, app. nyogoreria, caus. app. nyogoka, v.i. (coll.) (of a big, fast car in traffic) snake along
NEW_VALUE: evoke sympathy for oneself, writhe with pain
FULL_ENTRY: nyogora, v.t. (11), give a severe beating (with a green stick), make writhe in pain, ĩnyogora, refl. 1. evoke sympathy for oneself, writhe with pain. 2. be conceited, show off. nyogoranga, diff. nyogorana, rec. beat severely, whip, sb. nyogoranira, app. rec. nyogoraniria, caus. app. rec. nyogoria, caus. nyogorithia, d. caus, nyogorera, app. nyogoreria, caus. app. nyogoka, v.i. (coll.) (of a big, fast car in traffic) snake along; cf. nyogonyoka.

nyogota, v.t. (11), stike with a cane, whip. nyogotanga, diff. nyogotana, rec. 1. strike sb., strike each other, fight. 2. (of rain) pelt, storm. pyogotanira, app. rec. fight, for, at. nyogotanĩria, caus. app. rec, strike, whip (two or more people) simultaneously. nyogotithia, d. caus. nyogotera, app. nyogotora, conv. whip hard. nyogotoranga, diff. cony. nyogotorana, rec. Conv. I. strike, whip, sb., each other, very hard. 2. (of rain) fall very heavily (as a hail storm). nyogotoranĩra, app. rec. nyogotoraniria, caus. app. rec. nyogotoria, caus. nyogotorithia, d. caus. nyogotoreka, st. pot. nyogotorera, app. nyogotoreria, caus. app.
nyoha [nyooha], v.t. (11), have unregulated sexual intercourse with. nyohana, rec.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of sb. swinging from beam or branch) swing smoothly, let oneself go and drop. 2. (of man in bed, lion in bush or den) come out unexpectedly. 3. (of rain) fall down. nyohanyohoka, dim. nyohokanga, dif. nyohokanĩra, assoc. nyohokaniria, caus. assoc, nyohokia, caus. nyohokera, app. nyohokeria, caus. app. nyohora, v.t. 1. draw out, prolong. 2. eat with avidity. ĩnyohora, refi. 1. (of a lion, &c.) come out and go off unconcernedly
FULL_ENTRY: nyohoka [nyoohoka], v.i. (1), 1. (of sb. swinging from beam or branch) swing smoothly, let oneself go and drop. 2. (of man in bed, lion in bush or den) come out unexpectedly. 3. (of rain) fall down. nyohanyohoka, dim. nyohokanga, dif. nyohokanĩra, assoc. nyohokaniria, caus. assoc, nyohokia, caus. nyohokera, app. nyohokeria, caus. app. nyohora, v.t. 1. draw out, prolong. 2. eat with avidity. ĩnyohora, refi. 1. (of a lion, &c.) come out and go off unconcernedly; take oneself off; cf. ĩthiira. 2. show off, present oneself to view ; (of dancers) step out, show one’s paces. 3. (of child) be precocious. ĩnyohoranga, refl. diff. ĩnyohoranira. refl. assoc. ĩnyohoraniria, refl. caus. assoc. rnyohorera, refil. app.
nyokabi, see under mũkabi.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. handsome finelooking person of either sex
NEW_VALUE: handsome finelooking person of either sex
FULL_ENTRY: nyoko, ~, n. (1), 1. handsome finelooking person of either sex; miiritu ~, a beautiful girl; cf. -thaka, kĩirorerũa, nyarari, konyori. 2. fine, beautiful-looking, thing; mbembe ici nĩ ~, this maize looks fine.
nyoneka-narua, ~, n. (11-11), sb. or sth. that appears suddenly. ~ yũraga narua, quickly come, quickly go. Cf. njũnũka-narũa.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: bird of the air, any song-bird, grain-, or insect-eater
FULL_ENTRY: nyoni, ~, n. (1), bird of the air, any song-bird, grain-, or insect-eater; ~-n, when the birds begin to twitter, very early in the morning, about 5 am., cf. rũcinĩ; ~ ciaitĩrĩra, see under ita; theria ~, see under thera!; tua ~, break one’s fast early in the morning, have breakfast; ~ ya mũndũ, a person’s harbinger of good or bad omen; tũnga ~, (on setting out in the morning) encounter sb. or sth. of good or bad omen; cf. nyamũ cia rĩera-inĩ. kanyoni, tũ-, n. 1. dim. of above; ni mũguĩ na ~, lose both arrow and bird (ref. to an abortive enterprise), 2. ~-ka-nja, village sparrow (Ruddy Waxbill) (blue with red chest); cf. kamiri.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a cow or heifer that was bought with goats and/or sheep.
NEW_VALUE: a cow or heifer that was bought with goats and/or sheep
FULL_ENTRY: nyondari, ~, n. (11), a cow or heifer that was bought with goats and/or sheep.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cow or heifer handed over as part of the brideprice.
NEW_VALUE: cow or heifer handed over as part of the brideprice
FULL_ENTRY: nyondũyi, ~, n. (xi), cow or heifer handed over as part of the brideprice. [cf. Ma. IN-TOYIE]

NEW_VALUE: a former custom whereby a fugitive warrior escaped death by taking hold of the breast of a woman of the enemy, or a warrior worsted in fight grasped his opponent by the private member. 2
FULL_ENTRY: nyongano, ~, n. (111), 1. a former custom whereby a fugitive warrior escaped death by taking hold of the breast of a woman of the enemy, or a warrior worsted in fight grasped his opponent by the private member. 2. act between men or man and woman or girl while sleeping together, involving ceremonial cleansing. [onga]

NEW_VALUE: extra over and above what is due for the price
FULL_ENTRY: nyongeri / nyongerera / nyongereri, ~; kĩongereri, ci-; n. (x1), 1. extra over and above what is due for the price. Thogora nĩ ~, a bargain breaks down over the extra demanded. 2. interest on money. [ongerera]

nyongo, ~, n. (11), x. gall; gall bladder; tahĩka ~, vomit excessively ; maĩ-ma-~, bile, gall-fluid. 2. (of goats and sheep) disease in which the gall is affected; (of humans) biliousness. 3. ill-feeling, fear; ũtari ~, a fearless person; gĩa ~, be cowardly. kĩnyongo, i-, n. 1. bitterness, resentment, grudge; ndari na ~ (na), he has not any ill-feeling (towards). 2. (in abuse or joking) cowardly person; ~ gĩkĩ, you coward!
mũnyongoro, mi-, n. (VII), 1. millipede, wireworm, earthworm. 2. a long line of people.
mũnyonyo', see mũnyogonyogo.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: act of standing and walking on the hands (with legs in the air).
NEW_VALUE: act of standing and walking on the hands (with legs in the air)
FULL_ENTRY: mmũnyonyo?, mi-, n. (I1), act of standing and walking on the hands (with legs in the air).

nyonyoa, v.i. (11), (N.K.) (of children, &c.) be thin, shrivelled, underdeveloped; cf. ng’ong’oeka. nyonyoanga, diff. nyonyoana, rec. be stunted, dwarfed entirely. nyonyoania, caus. rec. nyonyoanira, assoc. nyonyoaniria, caus. assoc., nyonyoia, caus. nyonyoithia, d. caus. nyonyoeka, st. pot. nyonyoera, app.
mũnyonyonga, see mŨnyenyenga.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. long string, column of animals, &c. 2. long straight gourd
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyonyori [mũnyonyoori], mMi-, n. (11), 1. long string, column of animals, &c. 2. long straight gourd; cf. mũnyogonyogo.
nyonyoria, v.t. (11), (used by children) eat. nyonyanyonyoria, dim. nyonyorangia, diff. nyonyoraniria, app. rec. eat two or more things at the same time. nyonyoreria, app.

nyonyoteka, v.i. (11), (of child, &c.) be thin, shrivelled, underdeveloped; see nyonyoa, nyonyotekanga, diff. nyonyotekanira, assoc. nyonyotekaniria, caus. assoc. nyonyotekia, caus. cause to be thin, shrivel up. nyonyotekithia, d. caus. nyonyotekera, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. eat (soft food such as bananas, beans, &c.) with relish
FULL_ENTRY: nyora [nyoora], v.t. & i. (11), I. eat (soft food such as bananas, beans, &c.) with relish; finish off (remnant of food) at a gulp. 2. beat sth. soft, give (child) a stroke, beat on behind with soft stick. 3. whittle down, shape (clay) into a pot meticulously, draw out to form neck and mouth. 4. (of hunger) be intense, severe. 5. strike to kill; overcome, subdue; kiugo gĩkĩ nĩkiramũnyorete, this word had stumped him. nyora, refl.; a tall, thin, tapering tree. nyoranga, diff. nyorana, rec. 1. beat sb. with a green stick. 2. fight. nyoranira, app. rec. nyoraniria, caus. app. rec. nyoria, caus, nyorithia, d. caus. nyoreka, st. pot. I. be pliable as clay. 2. be weak from lack of food. nyorera, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: long leather string strung with cowrie shells tied round buttocks
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyora! [mũnyoora], mi- (1/Vin), long leather string strung with cowrie shells tied round buttocks; see ngũgũtũ. kanyora, tũ-, n. (of old coinage strung on a string) cent; cf. gathendi; see nyohora. [nyora!]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sth. straight and thin, sth. that inflicts pain
NEW_VALUE: straight and thin, that inflicts pain
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyora? [mũnyoora], mi-, n. (I1), sth. straight and thin, sth. that inflicts pain; switch, whip. rũnyora', n. cl. r11; kamandũ ka ~, greedy, rapacious person, one capable of wheedling money from people. [nyora]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: seam of under side of penis.
NEW_VALUE: seam of under side of penis
FULL_ENTRY: rũnyora? j rũnyoria, nyora | nyoria, n. (111?), seam of under side of penis.
-nyora, a. (11 ?), (N.K.) straight, sharp, smooth.
nyoro, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1. smooth flowing, straight, free motion. 2. gliding, gliding gait, swallowing; gamba, thĩ, ~, go quietly and quickly.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. a man’s waist-belt of fine chains closely hung on a narrow strip of leather decorated with beads
NEW_VALUE: a man’s waist-belt of fine chains closely hung on a narrow strip of leather decorated with beads
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyoro, mi-, n. (1x), 1. a man’s waist-belt of fine chains closely hung on a narrow strip of leather decorated with beads; ara ~, make such a waistbelt; ~ wa itina, a man’s waist-belt; cf. (N.K.) ndũrĩ, gĩthaitha. 2. ~ wa mũtwe, a girls browband of beads with a fringe of fine chains and strings of beads; cf. gĩtihio, (N.K.) kiũrĩ. 3. truce, peace-treaty, alliance, pact; blood-brotherhood; ara, tama, ~, enter into an alliance, make a truce; wa ~, party to a pact, bloodbrother; wanjokia ~-inĩ waku, you have become reconciled with me; ~/ wa-~!, salutation between bloodbrothers; cf. njiarano.

mũnyororo, mi-; nyororo, w; n. (111), 1. thick iron chain; cf. kĩrengeri. 2. imprisonment; a chain-gang of gaol prisoners. 3. main road made by prison labour; (N.K.) ya ~, an engineered highway (made by prisoners); cf. barabara. [cf. Sw. MNYO- RORO]
mũnyororoa, mi-, n. (v), a small tree (Heteromorpha trifoliata).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of gliding of snake, snail, &c., gliding gait of humans, animals, free running of train down incline, water flowing
FULL_ENTRY: nyorororo, ideo. (1), descr. of gliding of snake, snail, &c., gliding gait of humans, animals, free running of train down incline, water flowing; see nyororoka.

nyota [nyoota], v.i. (1), 1. be thirsty, dry, dried out; cf. ng’ara. 2. be angry, indignant. nyotanyota, dim. nyotanga, diff. nyotania, caus. rec. make sb., each other, thirsty, angry. nyotanĩra, assoc., nyotaniria, caus. assoc. nyotia, caus. I1. cause to be thirsty, dry. 2. make angry, indignant; ũkũ~ nĩkĩ?, why do you make me angry? nyotithia, d. caus. nyoteka, st. pot. become angry. nyotera, app. I. be thirsty for, at; thirst after. 2. be angry, about, with. nyoterera, inten. be stunted, shrunken. 2. (of water) sink into the ground. nyotoka, neut, become quenched of thirst. nyotokwo, neut. pass. Nyota wa gĩkũ ndũnyotokagwo, death’s thirst is never quenched. nyotokia, caus. neut. cause to be quenched of thirst, have enough of a drink. nyotokera, app. nyotokeria, caus. app. nyotora, cony. r. quench thirst. 2. strike very hard. nyotoria, caus. conv. 1. quench thirst. 2. strike with a stick. nyotorera, app. cony. nyotoreria, caus. app. conv.
nyota! [nyoota], n. cl. 14 only (11), thirst. inyota, ma-, n. time of thirst. [nyota]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: shoulder badge, badge of military rank, pip.
NEW_VALUE: shoulder badge, badge of military rank, pip
FULL_ENTRY: nyota? [nyoota], ~, n. (11), shoulder badge, badge of military rank, pip. [Sw. NYOTA]
kĩnyothi, see kĩnyũthi.

NEW_VALUE: one allowed to drink beer in public
FULL_ENTRY: nyua, v.t. (11), drink, imbibe, absorb, soak up; ~ ruoga, see ruoga; ~ muuma, see muuma; ~ ime, kagera, disappear, get lost. ~ heho, take the air; ~ thigara, smoke cigarettes; cf, kunda. ĩnyua, refl. gird oneself, brace oneself for a fight; ~ mũthiori, gird oneself; ~ njomo, gird on a sword; menyuĩte na macinota mao, they had donned their military equipment. nyuanyua, dim. nyuanga, diff. nyuana, rec.; ~ thakame, exhaust each other, draw vitality from each other. nyuanĩra, app. rec. drink together; drink (beer) from the same container as a mark of friendship or understanding. nyuaniria, caus. app. rec. drink sth. at the same time as sth. else. nyuithia, d. caus. 1. make drink, give sb. to drink; water stock. 2. * wish sb. to drink menstruation of mother, curse, utter imprecation upon; cf. ruma, girithia. nyuithania, rec. d. caus. nyuithaniria, app. rec, d. caus. nyuĩka, st. pot. be drinkable. nyuira, app. drink from, for, at. nyuĩririra, inten. drink sth. from (the top of) an overflowing container, a-, n. (11), one who drinks. kanyua!, n. with cl. r concords, name given to sb. who imbibes a good deal of beer also to child fond of drinking milk and other fluids. nyakinyvua, ~, n. with cl. 1/2 concords, I1. one allowed to drink beer in public. 2. married woman whose children have reached married status; cf. nyakang’ei, mũka, mũtumia. 3. (mod.) lady, consort. [nyua]
(mũ-) (ka-) nyua? (N.K.), see mũnua,

NEW_VALUE: of swift, smooth, motion (as of car, train, cycle downhill, river in flood, rhythmic progress of young warriors in a dance, silent marching of troops, &c
FULL_ENTRY: nyuaa, ideo. (u), descr. of swift, smooth, motion (as of car, train, cycle downhill, river in flood, rhythmic progress of young warriors in a dance, silent marching of troops, &c.).
kanyua-njii / kanyua-njui, tũ-, n. (11-111), sp. of sun-bird; dragon-fiy; see njii. [nyua]
mũnyuati, see mũnyati.
kanyua-ũki, tũ-, n. (1-111), sp. of sun-bird; see [nyua]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of sound of razor shaving, cutting of soft things
NEW_VALUE: of sound of razor shaving, cutting of soft things
FULL_ENTRY: nyue, nyue-nyue, ideo. (U), descr. of sound of razor shaving, cutting of soft things; cf. ng’we-ng'we.
nyugat, see nyoga.

NEW_VALUE: chant a solo (the recitative part in a song), lead in a dance
FULL_ENTRY: nyuga?, v.t. (1) chant a solo (the recitative part in a song), lead in a dance; ~ lead off in the mũgoiyo dance; cf. kũya. nyuganyuga, dim. nyuganga, diff. nyuganĩra, app. rec. nyugithia, d. caus. nyugika, st. pot. nyugira, app. lead a dance for, at; sing in praise of an individual dancer or well-known person.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: lower intestine, rectum of cow, ox
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyuga!, mĩ-, n. (111), lower intestine, rectum of cow, ox; (cl. 4) intestines; cf. mũneri, mũtura, gĩtungati, njoka-cia-nda. |

ORIGINAL_VALUE: gurgling sound (like that of African frogs in water).
NEW_VALUE: gurgling sound (like that of African frogs in water)
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyuga?, mĩ-, n. (vir), gurgling sound (like that of African frogs in water). [nyuga?]
ũnyugi, n. cl. 14 (111), jeering, jibes. [nyuga'"]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. plume of tail feathers of bird worn on crown of head, or over head-dress other than of ostrich feathers, for dances where head movement is necessary
NEW_VALUE: plume of tail feathers of bird worn on crown of head, or over head-dress other than of ostrich feathers, for dances where head movement is necessary
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyugi | (N.K.) mũnyogi [mũnyoogi], mĩ-, n. (vir), I1. plume of tail feathers of bird worn on crown of head, or over head-dress other than of ostrich feathers, for dances where head movement is necessary; ~ wa ngũkũ, rooster’s tail, single plume; menya ũkanyinia ta ~, see that you do not play fast and loose with me; cf. mũnyiri, muongoyo.
nyugu-nyugu, ideo. (1), descr. of grumbling, muttering; cf. nugunugu.
nyuguto, ~, n. (1), act of throwing sth.; ikta ~, whirl sth. in the air, throw hard, toss away. [nyuguta]
nyugutha, see nyagutha.,
nyui-nyui, ideo. (1), descr. of chirping, chattering of birds.
nyuĩria, v.i. (11), 1. feel insignificant, small. 2. (of penis) shrink into a small size. nyuiranyuĩria, dim. nyuirangia, diff. nyuĩranĩria, assoc. nyuirithia, caus. nyuĩrĩria, app.
kĩnyuĩro, i-, inyuĩro, ma-; n. (111), 1. drinking-place; mũndũ nĩ wa ~ gutũ, this man belongs to our community. 2. petrol-filling station, pump. [nyua]

NEW_VALUE: beast in poor condition, thin and bony
FULL_ENTRY: ĩnyuka, v.i. (refl.) (11), run, hurry, away; shake oneself and run, bolt away from pursuers; cf. teng’era, marita, hanyũka, enyera. fnyukanyuka, dim. kanga, diff. rnyukanĩra, assoc. inyukaniria, caus. assoc. fĩnyukithia, d. caus. rnyukira, app. hurry along, run away from sth., at. tmĩ-, n. (1), beast in poor condition, thin and bony; a weakling; mbũri (ya) ~, ~ wa mbũri, a thin, emaciated goat; hiha ta thathi wa ~, be extremely hot; cf. mũgoco, mũguyu, mũguna, nyamena.
mũnyuku, mi-, n. (vIr), conceit,
nyuma-andg, see muuma-andũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n, (v/x), 1. exit, opening, point of departure (of boundaryline, &c.)
NEW_VALUE: exit, opening, point of departure (of boundaryline, &c
FULL_ENTRY: nyumirĩra, (~), n, (v/x), 1. exit, opening, point of departure (of boundaryline, &c.); ~ yao, in their path. 2. outcome, issue, result; cf. moimirira, marikirĩro. [uma]
nyuno, see ruuno, nyundo, see nyondo. nyungu-nyungu, see gunyu-gunyu. mũnyunguro, min. (111). grumbling; cf. moguguro.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: behind, back, of home or village.
NEW_VALUE: behind, back, of home or village
FULL_ENTRY: nyunjuri, (~) / ma-, n. cl. or 14/6 (x1), behind, back, of home or village.

NEW_VALUE: of nagging, irritating chatter/chirping of birds
FULL_ENTRY: nyunyu-nyunyu, ideo. (1), descr. of nagging, irritating chatter/chirping of birds.
nyunyura, see ng'ung’'ura,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: little chips of obsidian (vulcanic glass) (remains of heavenly bodies, meteors, &c.)
NEW_VALUE: little chips of obsidian (vulcanic glass) (remains of heavenly bodies, meteors, &c
FULL_ENTRY: nyuranyura!, n. cl. 10 (v), little chips of obsidian (vulcanic glass) (remains of heavenly bodies, meteors, &c.); cf. nyenji cia-ngoma.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: small lip which governs micturition in female, labia minora.
NEW_VALUE: small lip which governs micturition in female, labia minora
FULL_ENTRY: nyuranyura?, ~, n. (v), small lip which governs micturition in female, labia minora.
mũnyurio, mi-, n. (1), a slight sound, a whisper. [nyuria]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of sth., small, esp. sound
FULL_ENTRY: nyuru, ideo. (1), descr. of sth., small, esp. sound; ndikaigue ~, may Ĩ not hear a sound from you; cf. nyuri. [see nyura]

nyutha, v.t. (1) brush, rub off; cf. gutha. refl. r1. shake oneself and run; cf. ĩnyuka. 2. (N.K.) rub dry mud off one’s body after a mud bath; look after oneself by eating good food, be concerned about one’s diet. nyuthanyutha, dim. nyuthanga, diff. nyuthana, rec. brush, rub dry mud, off one another. nyuthanĩra, app. rec, nyuthaniria, caus. app. rec. nyuthithia, d. caus. nyuthĩka, st. pot. nyuthira, app. nyuthũra, conv. rub off; cf. buthũra. nyuthũka, neut. 1. (of mud on the body) come off when rubbed (with soft, dry fibre or woolly material. 2. (of clothes, &c.) begin to be tattered, threadbare. nyutbũkia, caus. neut.; cf. buthũkia. nyuthũkĩra, app. nyuthũkiria, caus. app. neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: use a razorblade in shaving, shave. nyũanygũa, dim. I1. shave with quick motions of the razor. 2. (chiefly by women) backbite, mock at, deride, make fun of
NEW_VALUE: shave with quick motions of the razor. 2
FULL_ENTRY: nyũa [nyũwa], v.t. (11), use a razorblade in shaving, shave. nyũanygũa, dim. I1. shave with quick motions of the razor. 2. (chiefly by women) backbite, mock at, deride, make fun of; cf. nyũrũria. nyũanyũanga, diff. dim. nyũanga, diff. continue shaving for some time; shave rapidiy. nyũana, rec. shave people, each other. nyũanira, app. rec. nyũaniria, caus. app. rec. nyũithia, d. caus. nyũika, st, pot. nyũira, app.
nyũa-nyũa, ideo. (1) descr. of trotting of humans; cf. cũa-cũa, rigi-rĩgi.
kanyũgũ, (tũ-), n. (111), dysentery; cf. gatema.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of people, links of chain, back-bone, train, &c.) string of things following one another or joined together.
NEW_VALUE: (of people, links of chain, back-bone, train, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyũgũnyũgũ, n. (v), (of people, links of chain, back-bone, train, &c.) string of things following one another or joined together. ~ ndũtiganaga, people of two agegroups close to one another do things together.

nyũgũta, v.i. (I) I. (N.K.) have diarrhoea. 2. be very angry. 3. drink hurriedly; cf. thũgũta. nyũganyũgũta, dim. nyũgũtanga, diff. nyũgũtana, rec. nyũgũtania, caus, rec. nyũgũtanira, assoc. nyũgũtaniria, caus. assoc. caus. defecate (as one with diarrhoea). nyũgũtũra, conv. (used to describe what one has relished in the past) nda~ rũhia, I drank a horn of beer. nyũgũtũka, neut.; aharĩtwo aka~, his diarrhoea has been so bad that he is unconscious, he is entirely empty and helpless.
nyũkwa, ~, n. with cl. 1/2 concords (vI), 1. your (s. and pl.) mother; ~
a mũkũrũ, mũnyinyi, your elder, younger (classificatory) mother. 2. sometimes used as expletive or in oaths (therefore some prefer to use maitũ(guo) q.v. instead).

nygmba!, ~, n. (11), 1. hut, house; woman’s hut. 2. room; ~ ya toro, bedroom; ~ ya metha, dining-room; ~ ya ngoro, caul of the heart; cf. kĩũmba. 3. building, hall; ~ ya gwĩkeneria, social hall. 4. family group, lineage; ~ na riika itiumagwo, you cannot contract out of family and ageset. n. cl. 6, women’s huts in a homestead; see thingira. kinyũmba, i-, n. large house, large building, palace. kanyũmba, tũ-, n. dim. of above; cf. krũnũmba, komba. |
nyũmba? | see rũmba! j. rũmbo.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. refusal to comply, agree, co-operate, until conciliated, cajoled. 2. conciliatory gift
NEW_VALUE: refusal to comply, agree, co-operate, until conciliated, cajoled
FULL_ENTRY: nyũgmbio, ~, n. (111), I1. refusal to comply, agree, co-operate, until conciliated, cajoled. 2. conciliatory gift; courting, inducing admiration, favour, | desire in sb. else. Cf. mbatha. [ũmbia]
nyũmbũ, ~, n. (1n), mule. [Sw. NYUMBU]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. earthen pottery in general. 2. cooking pot
FULL_ENTRY: nyũngũ!, ~ / ma-, n. cl. 9/10 or 6 (11), 1. earthen pottery in general. 2. cooking pot; ~ ya gĩteng’ ũ, very large pot; ~ ya smelting furnace; ~ ya pot in which gruel is made, also used for boiling meat (food at man’s disposal); cf. ndeng’ũ, njuuri, ndigithũ. 3. pothole in rock or river-bed. 4. ~ ya mwana, foetal membrane, caul. kanyũngũ, tũ-, n. dim. of above. kongũ, tũngũ, n. znd dim. small cooking-pot; pot that has been in long use.
nyũngũ??, see under

NEW_VALUE: bloom of smelted iron-sand which has to be re-smelted to win purer iron
FULL_ENTRY: nyũngũt, ~, n. (11); ~ ya korugutĩro, bloom of smelted iron-sand which has to be re-smelted to win purer iron; see gĩkama; cf.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: have violent diarrhoea. nyũranyũria, dim. nyũrangia, diff. nyũraniria, assoc. nyũriria, app. have diarrhoea at a place. (mi-), n. rinderpest
FULL_ENTRY: nyũria, v.i. (11), have violent diarrhoea. nyũranyũria, dim. nyũrangia, diff. nyũraniria, assoc. nyũriria, app. have diarrhoea at a place. (mi-), n. rinderpest; cf. minamũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a tree or shrub used for witchcraft and much feared (when it is being cut it is first secured with pegs lest it should spring and touch sb.
NEW_VALUE: a tree or shrub used for witchcraft and much feared (when it is being cut it is first secured with pegs lest it should spring and touch sb
FULL_ENTRY: mũnyũria?, mMĩ-, n. (v1), a tree or shrub used for witchcraft and much feared (when it is being cut it is first secured with pegs lest it should spring and touch sb.; when cut into pieces by sorcerers it writhes and twists as though alive).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: slip down on slippery ground. nyũrũkanga, diff. nyũrũkania, caus. rec. nyũrũkanira, assoc, nyũrũkanĩria, caus. assoc, nyũrũkia, caus. cause to slip
FULL_ENTRY: nyũrũka, v.i. (1), slip down on slippery ground. nyũrũkanga, diff. nyũrũkania, caus. rec. nyũrũkanira, assoc, nyũrũkanĩria, caus. assoc, nyũrũkia, caus. cause to slip; grind wet grain on grindstone to make a little gruel. nyũrũkira, app. nyũrũkiria, caus. app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of running down of water from a leaking vessel, blood from a cut. nyũrũnyũrũ, ideo. descr. of oozing of liquids such as sap, milky latex, &c.
NEW_VALUE: of running down of water from a leaking vessel, blood from a cut
FULL_ENTRY: nyũrũ-nyũrũ, ideo. (1), descr. of running down of water from a leaking vessel, blood from a cut. nyũrũnyũrũ, ideo. descr. of oozing of liquids such as sap, milky latex, &c.; aakua maithori make maagambaga ~ ũtukũ na mũthenya, when her husband died she wept day and night. ideo. as above but descr. continuity and duration. [see nyũrũrũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: make fun of, ridicule, make mock of, burlesque, flatter falsely, travesty
FULL_ENTRY: kĩnyũrũri, i-, n. (xiv)}, mocking, gibing, sarcasm; cf. kĩnyugo, kĩnyenya, kĩnyarari, kĩnyaka. rũnyũrũri, nyũrũri, n. habit of mocking. v.t. (11), make fun of, ridicule, make mock of, burlesque, flatter falsely, travesty; cf. nyenyekia. nyũranyũrũria, dim. nyũrũrangia, dif. nyũrũrania, rec. nyũrũraniria, caus. app. nyũrũrithia, d. caus. nyũrũrĩka, st. pot. nyũrũriria, app. mock, because of, for, at. nyũrũrũkia, caus. inten. be caustically sarcastic, divulge sb.’s faults. nyũrũrũkiria, caus. app. inten. be severely sarcastic to sb., when speaking about sb., at, because of. | mũnyũrũra | mũnyũrũrũko, mi-, n. (1), thin stream, trickle, rivulet; cf. mũnyirĩri. [nyũrũrũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of liquids, rivers, &c.) run down, flow down. 2. (of men, cars) slide, slip downwards, go down a hill or steep slope
FULL_ENTRY: nyũrũrũka, v.i. (1), 1. (of liquids, rivers, &c.) run down, flow down. 2. (of men, cars) slide, slip downwards, go down a hill or steep slope; cf. nyirĩrika, hurũrũka, teretha. nyũranyũrũrũka, dim. diff. nyũrũrũkangia, caus. diff. peel a little more off potatoes, shave quickly. nyũrũrũkania, caus. rec. go down (with sb.). nyarũrũkanira, assoc. nyũrũrũkaniria, caus. assoc. nyũrũrũkia, caus. 1. cause to slide down, run down, slide downwards. 2. draw fingers down strands of bast when twisting it into string. 3. weep (tears) nyũrũrũkithia, d. caus. nyũrũrũkika, st. pot. nyũrũrũkira, app. (of liquids) run down over sth., at; slide down along sth., at. nyũrũrũkiria, caus. app. 1. take down sth., for, to, sb. 2. weep, for, at.

NEW_VALUE: large of jackal (with a reputation for fierceness)
FULL_ENTRY: nyũtũ, ~, n. (111), large sp. of jackal (with a reputation for fierceness). kanyũtũ, tũ-, n, meaning as above.
kinyũthi / kĩnyothi, i-, n. (u1), barber. [Sw. KINYOZI] O

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 3rd pers. pl. (cl. 2) they, them (written conjunctively with the part. nĩ, ti, na, ta)
NEW_VALUE: they, them (written conjunctively with the nĩ, ti, na, ta)
FULL_ENTRY: o | (N.K.) mo, indep. pron. (U), 3rd pers. pl. (cl. 2) they, them (written conjunctively with the part. nĩ, ti, na, ta); andũ ~ ene, the people themselves; na~, and they, they also, with them; see also no, to.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. used as intensifier, exactly, just, even, only
NEW_VALUE: used as intensifier, exactly, just, even, only
FULL_ENTRY: o [o0], part. ORIGINAL_VALUE: preceded by the regular p.c. of all classes 4—13 and 15-16 (guwith cl. 3 and 14) forming the indep. or emphatic p. of reference
NEW_VALUE: in conjunction with the ni, no, ti, to, na, ta
FULL_ENTRY: =01, part. (U), preceded by the regular p.c. of all classes 4—13 and 15-16 (guwith cl. 3 and 14) forming the indep. or emphatic p. of reference; for guo, ho, kuo, see also individual entries. Indep. p. are used in the foll. ways: 1. in conjunction with the part. ni, no, ti, to, na, ta; nĩkĩ~ kĩega, this is the better chair; nĩkĩ~, that’s why; ithanwa rĩrĩ tirĩ~ rĩega, this is not the good axe; nĩrĩ~, that’s when. 2. in conjunction with the -a of relationship, preceded by the p.c. all cl., forms the possessive a. for cl. other than 1/2; mũtĩ na honge ciagu~, the tree and its branches; and aku~, its (the country’s) people. 3. expr. the second of two obj. of the v. or the retained obj. of the v. in the pass.; akĩmũhe m~, he gave him them (the books). 4. in an interrog. sentence for emphasis; nĩgkamũhe mabuku macio. M~?, you will give him those books. Those ones do you mean? 5., together with -ene, (-self) q.v.; mĩtĩ y~ miene, the trees themselves; indo ci~ nyene, the things themselves,

(11), twine (string), twist two strands together in making string; spin, make yarn, twist a rope. ĩrogotha, refl. ogogotha, dim. ogothanga, diff. ogothana, rec. ogothania, caus. rec. 1. twist together, twine two or more strings. 2. ~ Nhoro, complicate a matter by bringing in confusing issues. ogothanira, app. rec. ogothanĩria, caus. app. rec. ogothithia, d. caus. ogotheka, st. pot. ogothera, app. ogotherera, inten. twine some more strings, for, at; add to a string, rope, by splicing in more material. ogothora, conv. untwine, untwist. ogothorithia, d. caus. cony. ogothorera, app. conv. ogothoka, neut. come untwisted.

ogũgi, see

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. tie (up), fasten by tying, button up, enfold, wrap up
NEW_VALUE: tie (up), fasten by tying, button up, enfold, wrap up
FULL_ENTRY: oha, v.t. (11), 1. tie (up), fasten by tying, button up, enfold, wrap up; ~ nda, become pregnant. 2. ~ nyeki! nĩnd~, (question by youth accosting a girl and her reply if willing to consent) tie grass! I tie it; cf. uha, ũmba, ĩcucura, ohera. 3. ~ irĩgũ, postpone the period of circumcision (for illicit purposes). 4. use magic, put a spell on sb. 5. ambush, lie in wait for. foha, refl. 1. gird oneself to attack, put on belt or sword. 2. dress up smartly, button oneself up, tie a loin-cloth round oneself. ohoha, dim. tie up quickly, loosely. ohohana, rec, dim. give first aid to. ohohora, conv. dim, loosen a bit more. ohohoka, neut. dim. ohanga, diff. ohangia, caus. diff. ambush for some time. ohana, rec. r. tie up, imprison, each other, others. 2. put a magic spell on sb. ohania, caus. rec. tie (things) together. ohanĩra, app. rec, tie up at the same time as sb. else does; tie up for sb. ohanĩria, caus. app. rec. tie (things) together, include sth. in a bundle. ohia, caus. ambush. ĩohia, refl. caus. stand in a safe corner, be ready in expectation of a fight. ohithia, d. caus. 1. assist sb. to tie up sth. ; have (things) tied up. 2. have sb. bewitched, have a madman tamed magically. ohithania, rec. d. caus.; ~ nyeki, accost (a girl). ohithĩria, app. d. caus. have sth. tied, wrapped up, for sb., assist sb. to, tie up sth. at. oheka, st. pot. ohera:!, app. I. tie, wrap, up for; w~ &?, (said to a girl approached by many suitors) whom do you tie grass for (who is your choice)? 2. imprison because of. oheria, caus. app. ambush, at, for. oherera, inten. tie on to. foherera, refl. inten. tie, on to, round, oneself. ref. caus. inten. ohora, conv. 1. unfasten, release from prison; ~ nga, release, pardon. 2. free from a magic spell; ~ aka, perform a ceremony on women before they plant (when the previous rains have been poor and the crop has suffered); cf. kura. 3. borrow money from sb. fohora, refil. conv. release oneself from a situation. ohoranga, diff. conv. ohorana, rec. conv. I. untie, unfasten, one another, others. 2. bail sb. out. ohorania, caus. rec. conv. disentangle, untie, things tied together; open up a parcel. ohoranira, app. rec, conv. Ohoraniria, caus. app. rec. cony, Ohorithia, d, caus. conv. I. cause to be untied; liberate by intervention. 2. get a loan; pay ransom to get a captive released; cf. regerũrithia. 3. get sb. released from a magic spell or misfortune. ohorera, app. conv. 1. untie, for, at. 2. lend money to. ohorerera, inten. conv. unfasten some more. ohoka, neut. I. come unwrapped, become unbuttoned, get untied. 2. be delivered of a child; ~ ithe, be delivered of a male child named after one or other of its grandfathers; cf. ciara, ohokanga, diff. neut. ohokana, rec. neut. ohokera, app. neut.

omoka, v.i. (11), I1. break down, collapse; fall, crash, down (like an old house); cave in. 2. (of a clay pot) disintegrate before firing. 3. (of ants, bees, people when disturbed) scatter. 4. (of plants before a gust of wind or storm) bend, fall. 5. (of people) slip down, die. omomoka, dim.; cf. mornoka. omokanga, diff. omokana, rec. crash and collapse entirely. omokania, caus. rec. omokanira, assoc. omokeka, st. pot. omokera, app. I1. crash down on to, collapse, spill over sth. 2. (of a crowd) rush with one accord upon; (of wolves, &c., attacking sheep) descend upon. omokeria, caus. app. omora, v.t. cause | to crash, scatter, collapse. omorania, caus, rec. 1. crush in, cause to cave in. 2. scatter (people) in all directions; cf. hurunjania. 3. make havoc (of things), overturn. omorera, app.
muomoko, mĩ-, n. (111), tangled confusion of fallen things (e.g. a banana grove, a field of millet blown down by the wind). [omoka]

(of the body) break out suddenly in spots; ~ mwiri, have skin disease. 4. go fast; tear, rip, along. ombomboka, dim. ombokanga, diff. ombokana, rec. x1. (of the skin over a wound) peel, come off. 2. (of pegs) come out of the ground. ombokanira, assoc. ombokaniria, caus. assoc. ombokithia, d. caus. ombora, v.t. pull out (roots and all); uproot; ~ mĩrĩyo, tear and root up sweet-potato vines; ~ mũgũnda, dig, plough up, a garden; ~ njira, (of a motor-car) tear along a road. ombombora, dim. omboranga, diff. omborana, rec., omborania, caus. rec. dig up (a garden) carelessly. omboranira, app. rec, omboraniria, caus. app. rec. omboria, caus. omborithia, d. caus. omborera, app. ombororia, caus. inten.

ona, v.t. (Ii), I. see, perceive, understand, comprehend; nĩw~?, do you understand?; ku~ atĩ, seeing that; ~ mweri, menstruate. 2. find, get, acquire; konokia mbeca iria w~, save the money you have got; ~ kahinda, find time (to do sth.); ~ njira, find the way, method, (of doing sth.); nĩngũthii o ng~, I shall go whatever the consequences. The perf. aspect has a contr. form -ine. The Imper. has an alt. form; one hũ, look now (all you have been asking for is already agreed). rona, refl. 1. be self-important, proud, conceited. 2. be set up, attain a sound position economically. 3. find oneself (in an unusual situation). onanga, dif. onana, rec. I1. see, meet, each other; ~ na, meet with, cohabit. 2. clash, come to blows. onania, caus. rec. display, show, demonstrate, exhibit sth. ronania, refi. caus. rec. show off, make oneself appear to be, display oneself, brag, boast. onanira, app. rec. onaniria, caus. app. rec. display, teach, sth. in, at, for. onaniriria, caus. inten. rec, set an example, show people how, show the way; point out sth. to sb. onia, caus. show, direct, teach; ~ rũkũre, see rĩakũre. onio, caus. pass, be shown; ~ tiriri, be threatened with strife. fonia, refi, caus. teach oneself. onitkia, d. caus. make see, cause sb., sth., to be seen. ronithia, refi. d. caus. cause oneself to be seen, reveal oneself to (the police, &c.) onithania, rec. d. caus. introduce people to each other. oneka, st. pot. be seen, found, forthcoming, procurable; be capable of being seen. onekana, rec. st. pot. be clearly visible. onekera, app. st. pot. be able to be seen, at, through. onera, app. I1. see sth., at, for. 2. find, obtain, produce, at, for. fonera, refl. app. see for oneself; find sth. on one’s own. oneria, caus. app. Onereria, caus. inten. point out to, indicate, show by example, instigate, teach, illustrate; ~ ngui make a dog chase an animal; ~ mOcinga, point a gun at sb. ; ny~ show me the way. ronereria, refl. caus. inten. direct, teach, oneself.

(11), 1. suck, feed at breast; ~ kinya wa, sap the strength of; thit ũkonge, (said to a person who refuses to face a task or situation with fortitude) go home and suck. 2. implore vehemently, beseech sb. who is about to do you harm; ndaku~, I beseech you most sincerely. ~ mũndũ, be servile to a person. ongonga, dim. ongongithia, d. caus. dim. breast feed a little more. ongongera, app. dim. onganga, diff. ongana, rec. I. suck sb., derive sustenance from one another. 2. implore each other, others. onganĩra, app. rec. ongithia, d. caus. 1. breast feed, suckle; ~ kĩreru, give sb. one’s chin to be sucked. 2. cause sb. to implore sb. else. ongithaniria, app. rec, d. caus. breast feed more than one baby, &c. ongithiria, app. d. caus. suckle, breast feed, for, at. ongeka, st. pot. ongera, app. suck, at, for.

ongerera / (N.K.) ongera, v.t. (1), add, add sth. to; increase; cf. congera. ĩongerera, refl. increase (in number or quantity), accumulate; aya nĩmareongerera, this crowd is increasing in size. ongongerera, dim. add a bit more, increase further. onganĩrĩra, app. add sth. more for others, increase sth. for each other; mix one quantity with another; cf. turanira. onganiriria, caus. app. rec, add up together; mathabu ma ku~, addition sums. ongereria, caus. cause to be added to. ongererithia, have sth. increased for; ny~ mũcara, please have my salary increased. ongererithĩria, app. d. caus. have sth. increased, for, at. ongerereka, st. pot. be increased, added to; increase; mar maya nĩmar~, this water is increasing; nũgi wa mnd w~ga o arakũra, man’s knowledge increases as he grows older. ongererekera, app. st. pot. increase, be increased, be increaseable, at.

(of neophytes) visit relatives who give them gifts. rathithia, d. caus. rathĩka, st. pot. rathikania, caus. rec. st. pot. join, fit, weld, together; fit into each other; cf. gathĩkania. rathĩra, app. give gifts to neophytes; rina rĩa~ Gacarage, we were at Gacarage at sunrise. rathĩrira, inten. probe for, rathũra, conv. 1. make shoot out, burst forth. 2. disjoint; cf. gathũra. rathũka, neut. 1. (of a gun, arrow) go off, be discharged. 2. (of sth. taut) shoot out, spring loose. 3. (of fire) spark, shoot out with sparks. 4. (of seeds from dry pods) shoot out with a pop, explode, jump away; cf. carika. rathũkana, neut. rec. be pierced through. rathũkia, caus. neut. spray (hot liquids) on. rathũkĩra, app. 1. (of boiling liquids) spray on 2. (of mousetrap) spring loose (and hit the setter or the mouse), rathũkĩria, caus. app. neut. make sth. shoot; pop, out, at, because of.
kĩrathi, i-, n. (111), 1. blacksmith’s cold chisel. 2. wooden wedge for splitting timber.
mũrathi!, a-, n. (111), shooter, (N.K.) stabber, piercer. [ratha']
mũrathi’, mi-, n., (111), (N.K.) Kenya Pencil Cedar; cf. mctarakwa.

FULL_ENTRY: rathima [raathima], v.t. rathima [raathima], n. (v1); wĩra wa ~, compulsory labour, [Sw. LAZIMA]
kirathimo f[kĩraathimo], i-, n. (X1), blessing, benediction. [rathima]

mũrathũko, mi-, n, (11), 1. a flying out of sparks. 2. sound of a shot. [ratha"]
rauta, v.i. (1), make baseless statements.

mũraũ? [mũraaũ], mi-, n. (11), pot, tankard (esp. of beer).

FULL_ENTRY: mũraũni! | mũraũri, mĩ-, n. (111), metal tumbler for drinking purposes. [Sw. BILAURI] mũraũni?, a-, n. (1x?), (N.K.) one who goes about, pays visits; cf. mũceeri, mũcangi. [raũna]
kĩraũni, i-, n. (111), crown. [E.]
raũra (N.K.), see roora,
maraya, see under maraya.

mũrebi [mũreebi], a-, n. (11), drunkard. [Sw. MLEVI]
recareca, see racaraca.
karecũ, see

rega, v.t. & i. (11), 1. refuse, reject; ~ watho, disobey the law. Mũ~ nda~ga see under heta. 2. fail to, happen not to be; mangĩ~ ho thaa inya matige, if they are not there by 10 o'clock, leave them behind. frega, refl. r. beabstinent, deny oneself, behave stoically ; exert oneself unflinchingly. 2. cease to care about one’s food, appearance, &c.; lose one’s self-respect; cf. reria, ĩthũra. regarega, dim. reganga, diff. regana, rec. I. refuse to accept others; (of a girl) refuse, reject, a suitor, 2. refuse to harmonize; fail to unite, stick together; igua mwiri ũkĩ~, (of sb. tired or with vague aches all over the body) feel disjointed; irto cia nĩra~ na ni, the food in this country disagrees with me; ~ na watho, be beyond the natural law, be an exception. reganira, app. rec. 1. refuse together with sb., refuse things together, refuse for sb. 2. (of a girl) refuse a suitor because of. reganiria, caus. app. rec. refuse, reject, together. regithia, d. caus. make sb. refuse; get sb. rejected. tregithia, refl. d. caus. pretend not to want sth.; refuse pretentiously; cause oneself to be rejected (for slyness, laziness, &c.). regeka, st. pot. be rejectable; horo an irrefutable matter. regera!, app. refuse, reject, because of, for, at; e gũkũ, he has refused to leave here. refl. app. 1. exert oneself unflinchingly for, deny oneself over sth.; be abstinent, because of, at. 2. refuse on one’s own behalf, refuse to leave a place.

regera?, v.i. (1), 1ĩ. be loose, slack; wobble. 2. (of eyes) be weak; be languid, become hungry; tũrutĩte wĩra tũka~, we have worked so hard that there is no more strength left in us; cf. raara, enycnya. regaregera, dim. regaregeria, caus. dim. make loose a little bit more. regaregerithia, d. caus. dim. regaregereka, st. pot. dim. regaregeria, caus. app. dim. regaregerũra, conv. dim.; see regerũra. regaregerũka, neut. dim; see regerũka.. regeranga, diff. regerangia, caus. diff. regerania, caus. rec. make each other, others, loose, weak, wobbly. regeranĩra, assoc. become loose, &c., at the same time as, together. regeraniria, caus. assoc., make (things) loose, weak, languid, at the same time, together. regeria, caus. I. loosen, enfeeble; ~ mũndũ nga, free a person from restraint or obligations. 2. be undecided; waver, shilly-shally. ĩregeria, refl. caus. free, loosen, oneself; work oneself free (by wriggling;) regerithia, d. caus. regereka, st. pot. regerera, app. be loose, at, for. regereria, caus. make loose, for, at, fregereria, refi. caus. app. regerũra, conv. loosen. regerũranga, diff. conv. regerũrana, rec. conv. regerũrania, caus. rec. conv. regerũranira, app. rec. cony. regerũraniria, caus. app. rec. conv, regerũrithia, d. caus. conv.; ~ nga, get sb. loosened, pay part of a ransom to effect loosening of bonds. regerũrika, st. pot. conv. regerũrira, app. cony. regerũriria, caus. app. conv. regerũririra, inten. cony. loosen some more. regerũka, neut. be, become, loose, get loose (after being tied tight). regerũkanga, diff. neut. regerũkangia, caus. diff. neut. regerũnkangithia, d. caus. diff. neut. caus. app. diff. neut. regerũkana, rec. neut. regerũkania, caus. rec. neut. regerũkanĩra, assoc. neut. regerũkaniria, caus., assoc, neut. regerũkia, caus. neut. regerũkithia, d. caus. neut. regerũkika, st. pot. neut. regerũkira, app. neut. regerũkiria, caus. app. neut, regerũkirĩria, caus. inten. neut.
mũrega!, a-, n. (111), one who refuses. ~ ndaregaga akĩhetwo, see under heta. Cf. mũregi. [rega]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. cause to break Joose. 2. cause to be self-willed, rebellious, wayward. refi. break loose, break free (from an anchored control)
FULL_ENTRY: regenyũra, v.t. (1), I1. cause to break Joose. 2. cause to be self-willed, rebellious, wayward. refi. break loose, break free (from an anchored control); cf. ĩnyegenyũra.

regenyũranga, diff. regenyũrana, rec. regenyũranira, app. rec. regenyũria, caus. regenyũrithia, d. caus. regenyũrira, app. regenyũka, v.i. I. (N.K.) be wayward, rebellious; cf. genyũka. 2. get free from. regenyũkana, rec, become completely free from an anchored position. regenyũkia, caus. regenyũkira, app.
mũregi, a&-, n. (111), one who refuses to obey; (in religious sense) Protestant. Ũregi, n. cl. 14 only, refusal, rejection; dissent. [rega]

rehe [reehe] / reha [reeha], v.t. (11), bring; cf. giira, iyira. frehe, refl. bring oneself; come on one’s own, in person; come without being called; come unaided, without a guide, by one’s own effort; rĩmwe thĩna niguo wĩrehage, sometimes distress (poverty) comes uninvited. reharehe, dim. bring nearer, a little closer. rehange / rehanga, diff. rehana / rehane, rec. come together, cause each other to come, accompany one another; bring others, each other. rehanira / rehbanire, app. rec. bring together; bring, to, for, each other. rehaniria, caus. app. rec. bring (things) together, simultaneously; ~ ng'ombe na gacan, bring the cow along with the calf. rehithia, d. caus. 1. have brought, assist to bring. 2. (of cow) bring forth milk; cf. gaithia. reheka, st. pot. rehere / rehera, app. bring, for, at, through; ~ mũtokaa haha, drive the car along here. irehere, refi. app. bring on oneself, cause sth. to happen to oneself; cf. reherere, inten. bring some extra (ones); bring very close to, bring to the brink (of a fight), bring upon. ĩreherere, refl. inten. bring oneself to the brink of; bring upon oneself sth. causing distress. rehereria, caus. inten. come towards, draw near to; rĩu horo niwarehereirie gũthira, now the matter was getting near to being settled.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: make a gap in sth., tear, rend
NEW_VALUE: make a gap in , tear, rend
FULL_ENTRY: rehũra [reehũra], v.t. (1), make a gap in sth., tear, rend; cut a piece out of sth.; cf. tehũra. frehũra, refl; ~ thithino, wipe one’s face after profuse perspiration. reharehũra, dim. rehũranga, diff. tear into pieces, make numerous tears or gaps. rehũrana, rec. rehũrania, caus, rec. tear, into two, apart, completely. rehũranira, app. rec. rebũraniria, caus. app. rec. rehbũria, caus. rehbũrithia, d. caus. rehũrĩka, st. pot. rehũrĩra, app. rehũrĩria, caus. app. rehũririra, inten. rehũka, neut. get torn, gape, be broken on the edge or rim.
mũreigi, see mũraipi.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: let, allow, permit, give a chance, a turn, to
NEW_VALUE: let oneself fall into a state of neglect
FULL_ENTRY: reka / reke, v.t. (1), let, allow, permit, give a chance, a turn, to; reke mũndũ aarie, give this man a chance to speak; ~ tnthi, let us go; matingĩ~ tuone, they will not give us a chance to sce. freka, refl. rekareka, dim. rekanga, diff. rekana, rec. give one another a chance. rekanira, app. rec. forgive one another, others. rekanirĩria, caus. inten. rec. leave (people, animals, fighting) alone to keep on fighting, give freedom to fight until they are tired. rekera, app. forgive. rekereria, caus. inten. leave alone, give freedom, to do sth., cease to restrain; ~ mbũri mũgũnda, let the goats go free in the garden. ĩrekereria, refl. inten. 1. let oneself fall into a state of neglect; become dirty, unshaven; see hunyũ. 2. abandon oneself to, let oneself go; give oneself free rein.

rekia!, v.t. (1), drop down, lay (eggs); release from custody; relinquish, let go, release the grip; ~ njira, cause sb. to die on the way from exhaustion, starvation, &c. irekia, refl. 1. knock oneself down. 2, (of fruit, &c.) fall down on its own. rekarekia, dim. 1. let go a little,
loosen one’s grip. 2. lower, let down.

-$ rekangia, diff. rekangithia, d. caus. diff. rekangĩria, app. diff. rekania, rec. 1. release the grip of sb.; release sb. from custody. 2. (of fat) melt; (of ice) thaw. 3. (of people) release each other; separate; (of things joined) come apart. 4. (of people wrestling, &c.) throw down with force. rekanĩria, app. rec. release each other, others, because of, at; let go because of; release sb. at; drop sth. with force at. rekithia, d. caus. have sb. released, have (trees) chopped down. rekeka, st. pot. rekeria, app. let sth. drop, at, on, upon; release sb. at; (of a hen) lay (an egg), at, on.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: guessing game in which a number of objects are touched and a hint of some sort is given to indicate the right one (started by sb.
NEW_VALUE: guessing game in which a number of objects are touched and a hint of some sort is given to indicate the right one (started by sb
FULL_ENTRY: earekia [kaarekia], n. cl. 12 (x), guessing game in which a number of objects are touched and a hint of some sort is given to indicate the right one (started by sb. saying to the guesser wa ~, nĩndakũruma, Mr. Guesser, I lay a curse on you).

nũrekio, a-, n. (11), messenger, ambassador; cf. kĩrekio, i-, n., a loosing, letting loose, sending away; thenge ya ~, scapegoat. [rekia] nũreko, mĩ-, n. (1), branch, stem, stick; long grass which obstructs one’s way through bushes, on an overgrown’ path, &c.; hra mĩreko, push one’s way hastily through such obstructions b athĩte cia mĩreko, he has gone off the track (through the bush). nareko, n. cl. 6 only (1), first beer drawn from container; cf. matina; see kĩnyita. ai [reekondi], ~, n. (xv1), record. ema, v.i. & t. (1), 1. be disobedient, unmanageable; be obstinate, stubborn, immovable. 2. be too difficult for; be undoable, impossible; kandũ hau nĩha~. kũrĩmĩka, it was too difficult to cultivate that place. 3. be too big; kĩrĩra gĩake kĩaigana gĩa his load of sweet-potato vines was simply gigantic. remwo, pass. be unable (to do sth.). rremwo, refi. pass. be lazy, uninterested, apathetic, disinclined, indolent. remarema, dim. remanga, diff. remangia, caus. diff. remana, rec. remanwo, pass. rec. I1. disagree on a price. 2. (of a fight, wrestling match, &c.) end in a draw; (of people) be unable to overcome one another, come to a deadlock, fail to get along together. remania, caus. rec. encourage each other, others, to disobey. remanira, app. rec. remaniria, caus. app. rec. make (people) disobedient at the same time. remia, caus. make disobedient, wilful, undisciplined. fremia, refi. caus, I. hold out against, resist, withstand successfully, be too strong for; cf. kĩria kinya. 2. become disobedient for no reason, become obstinate, obdurate, difficult. remithia, d. caus. remeka, st. pot. remera, app. I. be disobedient, obstinate, stubborn, to, towards, because of, for. 2. remain at stubbornly, refuse to. move from; ~ rima, ger stuck in a hole; cf. regera. remeria, caus. app. make get stuck, make (sth.) adhere so that it cannot move. remerera, inten. become impossible of completion, be too much for, resist efforts to dislodge, fail to move; (of an old or sick man, a motor-car in mũd) get stuck. remererũo, pass. inten. be stuck; be too sick to move about; be unable to finish a task; nĩnda~ ni kũrĩkia wĩra, I am unable to complete the work; mũthuuri the elder can no longer get about. remereria, caus. inten. make get stuck, make fail to move on; make stay on longer; cause to be slack, lazy; cf. gondereria. fremereria, refi. caus. inten.; ~ ho, kuo, refuse to come out or budge from a spot; ~ kari ng'ania, hang on to sb. i-, n. (111), 1. scar; part of a wound which does not respond to treatment. 2. monster; ugly, illformed child; freak child which cuts its top teeth first; cf. krũgũ, mwitakio. karema, tũ-, n. malformation, deformity, infirmity, defect. rema / ũremwa, n. cl. 14 only, disability, defect, blemish; cf. Rũgũ, mĩ-, n. (1), 1. any tree with hard, resistant wood. 2. small tree (Combretum guernizii); cf. mũkũra.
rema, a. (?) (1); (coll.) ti? ~, it is not difncult. [rema]

NEW_VALUE: that is too hard even for strangers
FULL_ENTRY: karema-ageni, tũ-, n. (1-1), sth. that is too hard even for strangers. [rema]
karema-hiti, tũ-, n. (1-1v), knee-cap, patella. [rema]

mũrema-njogu, mi-, n. (1-1), a kind of liana. kĩrema-njogu, i-, n. a sp. of grass. [rema]

ũremi / ũremu, n. cl. 14 only (1), obstinacy, disobedience, refusal, wilfulness, intractability. [rema]
-remu, a., (11), obstinate, unmanageable. [rema]

NEW_VALUE: rough wooden handle used to hold a spear while it is being made
FULL_ENTRY: kiremwa, i-, n. (1), rough wooden handle used to hold a spear while it is being made.
ũremwa, see Ũrema.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. patch, patch an opening, cover with a patch
FULL_ENTRY: remba!, v.t. (1), I. patch, patch an opening, cover with a patch; cf. kunĩka. 2. seal off (an opening); fix the bottom (of a container, beehive, &c.) rembaremba, dim. rembanga, diff. rembania, caus. rec. rembanira, app. rec. rembaniria, caus. app. rec, patch, seal off, (things) at the same time. rembithia, d. caus. have patched, assist to patch sth, rembeka, st. pot. 1. be patchable. 2. (used inten.) close a hole by putting sth. over or against it; close for a moment by covering with the hand; patch; put the lid on (barrels, honeypots, &c.). rembarembeka, dim. st, pot. rembekanga, diff. st. pot. rembekana, rec. st. pot. fight, hit sb., with a sizeable lump of dough or sticky mud. rembekania, caus. rec. st. pot. seal up completely. rembekanĩra, app. rec. st. pot. rembekanĩria, caus. app. rec. st. pot. patch, seal, (things) simultaneously. rembekithia, d. caus. st. pot. rembekeka, d. st. pot. rembekera, app. st. pot. rembũra, conv. take a piece out of, remove the bottom of; cut off a patch; cf. tembũra. rembũrana, rec. conv. tear in pieces, take flesh off bones. rembũrania, caus. rec. conv. rembũranira, app. rec. conv. caus. app. rec. conv. rembũria, caus. conv. rembũrithia, d. caus. conv. rembũrira, app. conv. tear in pieces, for, at. rembũka, neut. (of a patch, bottom of container) come off. rembũkia, caus. neut. cause a patch to come off violently, with a snap. rembũkira, app. neut. rembgkĩria, caus. app. neut. |
kĩremba!, i-, n. (1), turban. KILEMBA] |
rembecũka, v.i. (1) come off, away; be torn off. rembecgra, v.t. tear a piece off; cf. themberecũra.

FULL_ENTRY: mũrembeko, mi-, n. (11), modern song of women and children in clubs. [remba]

FULL_ENTRY: kĩrembui, i-, n. (viir), 1. remnant of food, cloth, burnt garment; ~ kĩa nyam, a partly devoured kill. 2. yaws; cf. mũcarĩ. karembui, tũ-, n. dim. of above; see remba.

renda!, v.i. (1), be sticky. rendarenda, dim. rendanga, diff. rendana, rec. rendania, caus. rec. rendanira, assoc, rendaniria, caus, assoc, rendeka, st. pot. rendera, app.

rigai, v.i. & t. (11), 1. be unintelligible, incomprehensible; be a mystery. 2. perplex, baffe, bewilder; be too diffcult for, incomprehensible to; horo cio nĩwa~, that matter is perplexing. rigwo, pass. fail to recognize, understand; fail to know, be ignorant, be nonplussed. frigwo, refl. pass. be dazed, unconscious, muddled (e.g. with sleepiness}; be at a loss to know what to do; nderigagwo, he is always prepared (with food or cash). rigariga, dim. riganga, diff. rigana,) rec, be baffling to a number of people; be unknown to people. riganwo, pass. rec. I. fail to come to an agreement; be estranged. 2. be unintelligible to one another. riganira, app. rec. 1. (of things or people) be unable to be recognized, remembered, together. 2. be forgotten, be left out. riganirũo, pass. app. rec. (of people) fail to, know, recognize, forget; riganiria, caus. app. rec.; 1. ~ distract attention from the main argument. 2. cause sth. to be forgotten. iriganiria, refl. caus. app. rec. I, make oneself be left behind, hang around after everybody else has left. 2. pretend to forget, keep sth. behind deliberately. rigithia, d. caus. make baffied, perplexed; confuse; ~ gĩtkaro, unsettle. frigithia, refl. d. caus. 1. pretend not to, know, understand; feign ignorance, stupidity. 2. cause oneself to be unrecognizable. rigithania, rec. d. caus. cause misunderstanding between people; ~ horo, confuse issues. rigika, st. pot. rigira, app. baffle, perplex, bewilder, because of, at (time, place). rigirira, inten. become stranded half-way while saying sth. rigirirũo, pass. inten. become confounded in the middle of sth., fail to do sth. while others are watching or doing it well (e.g. in dancing). rigara, stc. be unrecognizable; be disguised. rigaria, caus. stc. make unrecognizable, make look different; ~ cira, give a dispute a new aspect, frigaria, refl. caus. stc. make oneself unrecognizable; ndangĩ~, he is recognizable anywhere. iga?, v.t. (1) 1. singe, remove hair, wool, fur, by singeing and scraping; ~ mũtwe, singe the head of slaughtered animal before cooking it. 2. burn bark (off sticks, poles, &c.)}so that it comes off easily, char ends; scorch. rigariga, dim. riganga, diff. rigana, rec. singe sb., singe each other; put people in a place where they cannot move away from the fire. riganira, app. rec. singe things, for each other, at the same time. riganiria, caus. app. rec. singe, remove hair, wool, fur, bark, together. rigithia, d. caus. rigika, st. pot.; mũtwe wa ngũrũe ndũngĩ~ nĩ kũnora, the head of a pig cannot be singed because it is so fat. rigira, app. remove (hair, bark, &c.) for, at. rigirĩrira, inten.; ~ mũtwe, singe the head further.

[riga]
kĩriga?, i-, n. (v11), klipspringer.
mũriga, mĩ-, n, (vir), charred fur or
m bark; odour of singeing or of burnt hair. [riga?]
