NEW_VALUE: rinsings from a cooking-pot (liked by goats because of the salty flavour)
FULL_ENTRY: maringo, n. cl. 6 only (111), 1. rinsings from a cooking-pot (liked by goats because of the salty flavour); water in which a woman rinses her hands while making gruel; ringa ~, put water into sth. to rinse it out, 2. remains of gruel after the bulk has been drunk; cf. (N.K.) mathambia-njara.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) the opposite bank or side of river or water
NEW_VALUE: () the opposite bank or side of river or water
FULL_ENTRY: mũringo?, mi-, n. (11), (N.K.) the opposite bank or side of river or water; cf. mũrimo, (N.K.) mũrima. [ringa?]
ring’i, ~, n. (1), taxi-rank, taxi-cab. [E. RING]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be silly, be childish, foolish (as a drunken person).
NEW_VALUE: be silly, be childish, foolish (as a drunken person)
FULL_ENTRY: ring’ong’a [ring’oong’a], v.i. (1), be silly, be childish, foolish (as a drunken person). ring’aring’ong’a, dim. ring’ong’anga, diff. ring’ong’ania, caus. rec. ring’ong’anira, app. rec. ring'ong’aniria, caus. assoc. ring’'ong’ia, caus, app. be silly, to, at. ring'’ong'eria, caus. app.
rinye, ~, n. (vir), secret, sth. confidential; umbũra ~, give the show away. karinye, tũ-, n. core, nucleus.

irio, n. cl. 8 only (11), 1. food, food crops; ~ cia imera, seasonal crops (cereals, legumes); ~ cia menja, permanent crops (roots, bananas, &c.); handa, getha, ~, plant, harvest, crops; ~ cia mro wa mbere, first fruits. 2. thick stodge made from whole maize, peas, and beans to which are added potatoes and green bananas; ~ cia nyeni, food so prepared with addition of green vegetables. tũirio, n. cl. 13, a little food. [rĩa]

rira, v.t. (1), whisk, flap, away, off sth.; protect by driving sth. off; protect from; ward off, parry; ~ mwere nyoni, scare birds off millet; ~ nguo ng'ombe, keep cattle from (drying) clothes. irira,! refi. protect, defend, oneself against (a blow or missile by parrying or feinting); drive off, ward off, from oneself. rirarira, dim. riranga, dif. rirana, rec. r. protect, shield each other, others, from attack. 2. separate, keep apart; cf, teithũrana. rirania, caus. rec. riranira, app. rec. ririthia, d. caus. assist to ward off, protect from, whisk off; have sth. protected from. rirĩka, st. pot. ririra, app. protect sth., for, at. iririra!, refl. app. guard oneself against. rirĩrĩra, inten. frirĩrira, refl. inten. guard, excuse, oneself.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: also ~-inĩ (ma mbere), the time of year when birds have to be scared off the millet crop (usu. in November)
NEW_VALUE: the time of year when birds have to be scared off the millet crop (usu
FULL_ENTRY: marira, n. cl. 6 (1), also ~-inĩ (ma mbere), the time of year when birds have to be scared off the millet crop (usu. in November); cf. krya-kĩamwere; see mwaka. [rira]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: decorated quiverlike case for storing and carrying the long white ostrich plume for the top of an ostrich-feather war-head-dress.
NEW_VALUE: decorated quiverlike case for storing and carrying the long white ostrich plume for the top of an ostrich-feather war-head-dress
FULL_ENTRY: maũrira, mi-, n. (1), decorated quiverlike case for storing and carrying the long white ostrich plume for the top of an ostrich-feather war-head-dress. kĩrira, i-, n. small tree (used for making quivers for arrows).
riri [riiri], ma-, n. cl 14/6 (1), splendour, beauty, prettiness.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who keeps sth. from (birds from eating growing millet, &c.)
NEW_VALUE: one who keeps from (birds from eating growing millet, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũriri, a-, n. (1), one who keeps sth. from (birds from eating growing millet, &c.); ~-ngi, one of six guards at a homestead in which a circumcision ceremony is in progress; see mbato. [rira]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: any thin thread or thread-like material, hence used of
FULL_ENTRY: rũriri ndiri, n. (vnr), any thin thread or thread-like material, hence used of: 1. wire-netting. 2. lank, weedy, grain-plant grown in shade of tree. 3. weak, down-trodden set of people. 4. chaff of grain; cf. mũngũ. irikana, v.t. (11), remember, recall, recollect. fririkana, refi. remember oneself (e.g. when dishing, dividing, out things). ririkananga, diff. ririkanira, app. rec. ririkaniria, caus. app. rec. ririkania, caus. remind sb.; tiga kũndiirikania morire, do not remind me of those who are dead. ririkanithia, d. caus. ririkanĩka, st. pot.
kĩririkanania, i-, n. (1v), memorial. [ririkana]

NEW_VALUE: a reminder. karirikania, tũ-, n. of above
FULL_ENTRY: kĩririkania, i-, n. (1v), a reminder. karirikania, tũ-, n. dim. of above. [ririkana]
kĩririkano, i-, n. (111), memory. ũririkano, moririkano [mooririkano), n. memorial; cf. kĩririkanania. [ririkana}]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. draw tight, knot, close up tight
NEW_VALUE: become closed up completely, be drawn together tight, become closely knotted
FULL_ENTRY: ririma / ririma [riirima], v.t. & i. (1), I. draw tight, knot, close up tight; cf. thing’a!. 2. be dense (mentally, &c.). 3. (of rain) be very heavy. riraririma, dim. ririmana, rec. I1. become closed up completely, be drawn together tight, become closely knotted. 2. become completely dark. ririmania, caus. rec, fririmania, refl. caus. rec. 1. hold oneself tight, discipline oneself severely. 2. ~ na, appropriate, make free with (somebody else’s property) in assumed ignorance or stupidity. ririmanira, app. rec., ririmanĩria, caus. app. rec. draw (things) tight together. ririmia, caus. fririmia, refl. caus. make oneself appear stupid. ririmithia, d. caus. 1. assist to close up, draw tight. 2. protect from ill hap, have protected from evil. ririmithania, rec. d. caus. tie together tight, make closely knotted; ~ ũthin, pull a face; cf. thithinaria. ririmithĩria, app. d. caus. have sth. closed up for sb. ririmitharia, stc. d. caus. make to be completely closed up, watertight; ~ frown. ririmika, st. pot. ririmira, app. 1. shut sb., sth., into a place. 2. sink into; see riiriima. ririmiria, caus. app. ĩririmiria, refl. caus. app. I. live apart

l~} from other people. 2. ~ indo ciene, make free with another person’s property by assumed ignorance or denseness. ririmiriria, inten, ririmara, stc. 1. (of door, &c.) be shut tight; become completely closed up; horo nĩwa~, the matter has become confused. 2. become pitch dark. 3. be stupid, befogged; be a blockhead. ririmaria, caus. stc. 1. make stupid, confuse mentally. 2. shut tight; ~ kĩroto kĩnru, expunge a bad dream from the memory by spitting on charcoal and rubbing it between the hands; cf. cicingaria.

ririma [riirima], ~ / ma-, n. (v), 1. bunch, conglomeration; (of cattle) large herd, drove; (of stars) mass; cf. ngũriga, ngong’orua; mbũmbũthi. 2. anything big, strong, fierce, e.g. bull or ox. 3. (N.K.) importance, personality, majesty.

NEW_VALUE: nguo ya ~, () elder’s long robe or kaross of skin or fur
FULL_ENTRY: ririmba, n. (1); nguo ya ~, (N.K.) elder’s long robe or kaross of skin or fur; cf. gĩthii.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: place from which birds are scared off crops.
NEW_VALUE: place from which birds are scared off crops
FULL_ENTRY: kĩrirĩro | kĩriro, i-, n. (1), place from which birds are scared off crops. [rira]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: very strong gum made from rock plant (used to fasten feathers to shaft of arrow). mi-
NEW_VALUE: ram of brindled colours, red and black
FULL_ENTRY: ũriru [ũriiru], moriru [mooriiru], n. (111), very strong gum made from rock plant (used to fasten feathers to shaft of arrow). mi-; irirũ, ma-; n. (111), stripe, band of contrasting colour, on cloth, flower, &c. ; kari-mĩrirũ, skunk, cf. ngarũri. kĩmĩrirũ, i-, n. usually with cl. 9/10 concords, ram of brindled colours, red and black. nyamirirũ, ~, n. ewe of brindled colours. [see rirũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. sth. unusual, causing sensation, a mystery
FULL_ENTRY: ũrirũ, morirũ [moorirũ], n. (111), 1. sth. unusual, causing sensation, a mystery; cf. gothe. 2. matter for consternation, marvel, wonder, calamity, catastrophe. [see rirũkał

rirgka, v.i. (11), I1. scintillate, gleam; be iridescent, shimmer, glisten, ripple, flutter; mwere nĩũra~, the millet is rippling in the breeze. 2. (of flowers, buds) burst into bloom, unfold. 3. appear and disappear from sight; ndona ũkũ~ o harĩa mitĩ-inĩ, I have just seen sb. popping about there amid the trees. rirũkanga, diff. rirũkanira, app. rec. scintillate, ripple, at the same time. rirũkaniria, caus. app. rec., rirũkia, caus. rirũkira, app. scintillate, ripple, flutter, at. rirũkiria, caus. app. rirũra, v.t. cause to flutter, shimmer.
mũrirnngũ / mũriũngũ, mi-, n. old type of handmade glass bead in the form of a ring (dark blue or green in colour).
(i-)_ maritati [maritaati], n. (111), finery, decoration. [Sw. MALIDADI]
iritii [iriitii], ma-, n. (v11), rocky scarp, escarpment, cliff.
kĩritu [kĩriitu], i-, n. (1v), blockhead, simpleton; cf. kĩgegea, gĩthayo, kĩnunwa, kĩmoi, kĩrimũn. ritu, n. cl. 14, stupidity, foolishness, ignorance; cf. ũrimũ, ũkĩgu. [rita]
-ritu f[-riitu], a. (1v), stupid, unintelligent. [rita] a (1), heavy; difficult; (of women) pregnant.
ũritũ, [moorita], n. (1), x. weight. 2. dullness, heaviness of brain, stupidity.

ritũha, v.i. (1), 1. be heavy; be heavy. with child, be pregnant. 2. be dificult to manage; (of sums, examinations) be difficult to do, 3. be slow in learning; ~ kĩongo, have a heavy head, be unintelligent. ritaritũha, dim. ritarithia, caus. dim. ritũhanga, diff. ritũhangia, caus. diff. ritũhania, caus. rec. ritnũhanira, assoc. be heavy at the same time as. ritũhanĩria, caus. assoc. ritũhia, caus. make heavy; make dificult; ~ ranio, make the examination difficult. ĩritũhia, refl. caus. make oneself heavier (by being clumsy or weighing down on sb., sth.); be clumsy (as when children carry one another at play). ritũhithia, d. caus. ritũhika, st. pot. ritũhĩra, app. be too heavy, on, for, at; weigh down; nĩaritũhiirũo, she is heavy with child. ritũhiria, caus. app. make heavy, difficult, for sb., at a place.

NEW_VALUE: African Land unit or reserve, rural area
FULL_ENTRY: rithabu [rithaabu] / rĩthabu [rĩthaabu], ~ | ma-, n. cl. oj1o or 6, African Land unit or reserve, rural area. [E. RESERVE]
rithathi [rithaathi] / rithathi [rithaathil, ~ / ma-, n. cl. or 6, 1. bullet; ball-bearing; cf. biribiri?. 2. soldering-iron. [Sw. RISASI]
ritheni, see retheni.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: head ornament made from ndibai fibre (worn by Masai Guild initiation candidates).
NEW_VALUE: head ornament made from ndibai fibre (worn by Masai Guild initiation candidates)
FULL_ENTRY: marithiani, n. cl. 6 only (vi), head ornament made from ndibai fibre (worn by Masai Guild initiation candidates).

NEW_VALUE: small side-entrance into cattle-enclosure through which only calves can pass
FULL_ENTRY: rithii [riithii] / (N.K.) ribii [riibĩ}] / ruthĩ [ruuthii], ~ / ma-, n. cl. 14/6 or 10 (V11), 1. small side-entrance into cattle-enclosure through which only calves can pass. 2. U-shaped guardhut for sheep, open at one end. karithii, &c., n. dim. of above. [cf. Ma. O-RRIPIE]
rithiti [rithiiti] / rithiti [rĩthiiti], ~, n. (111), receipt. [E.]

rithũra, alt. form for thiithũra, see under thiitha. [mũriũ], a- [aa-], n. (1), 1. (his) son; ~ cio wari mũkũrũ, mũnyinyi, the elder, younger, brother. 2. (used by father speaking to other people) ~ wakwa, my son. 3. (used by an elder to a younger person, not between equals) sonny!; (also by father) my son. 4. (cl. 2) arin a ithe brethren. Cf. mũrũ. nyamũriũ [nyamũriũ], ~, n. cl. 1/2, young friend taken into family but treated as of lower status; aa ~, members of family named after members of father’s family; cf. nyamwarĩ, kũrũngũrũ.

NEW_VALUE: come back to life, be revived, be resurrected, rise up from the dead
FULL_ENTRY: riũka, v.i. (1) come back to life, be revived, be resurrected, rise up from the dead; (of plants) sprout after drying up or withering; cf. rigua. riũkariũka, dim. riũkariũkia, caus. dim. riũkanga, diff. riũũkangia, caus. diff. riũkangĩria, caus. app. diff. riũgkania, caus. rec. revive sb., people; (of a mass of withered plants) come to life together after a fall of rain. riũkanĩra, assoc. riũkanĩria, caus. assoc. riũkiaa, caus. resurrect, revive, raise from the dead; bring forth a child and name it after a late grandparent; cause to sprout after a dry spell; resuscitate. iriũkia, ref. caus. riũkithia, d. caus. riũkika, st. pot. riũkĩra, app. riũkĩria, caus. app. bring back to life, for, at. riũkirĩra, inten, riũkiriria, caus. inten.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: spring which only flows in the rainy season.
NEW_VALUE: spring which only flows in the rainy season
FULL_ENTRY: kĩriũka, i-, n. (1), spring which only flows in the rainy season. [riũka]
riũngũ, mariũngũ, see under rũariũngũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. weed, weeds in general (contrasted with cultivated crops)
NEW_VALUE: weed, weeds in general (contrasted with cultivated crops)
FULL_ENTRY: riya / (N.K.) riia, ma-, n. cl. 5/6 (111), I1. weed, weeds in general (contrasted with cultivated crops); ~ rĩa anything edible in its natural place of growth, forage for livestock; ~ yia njoko, second growth of weeds. 2. (cl. 6) patches of weeds of different kinds, masses of uprooted weeds.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. n., a., p.c., & p. subj. prefix, cl. 5. 2. when duplicated this prefix forms the ‘near’ dem., a. & p.» cl. 5
FULL_ENTRY: rĩ-, conc, prefix (U), 1. n., a., p.c., & p. subj. prefix, cl. 5. 2. when duplicated this prefix forms the ‘near’ dem., a. & p.» cl. 5; ithanwa rĩrĩ, this axe; (freq. used without a noun to indic. time) o rĩrĩ ndoka, just at the moment when I had come; rĩrĩ rĩngi, the next moment after that, suddenly.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. used with present and past tense signs to express the v. ‘to be’
NEW_VALUE: used with present and past tense signs to express the ‘to be’
FULL_ENTRY: -ri, v.i. (U), 1. used with present and past tense signs to express the v. ‘to be’; also together with the assoc. part. na to express v. ‘to have’. 2. (rare) with -ngĩprefix; nĩngĩ~ ũthũre, it would have been better if vou had gone, you would have done better to go. 3. (rare) with -ngaprefix; nĩnga~, see under anga.
iri, see iri,

rial, v.t. (II), I. eat, consume; rĩria ng ombe ĩ~ga mĩramba, mid-day (when cattle eat banana palms); cf. ũndũra. 2. with vv. nouns in fig. sense: ~ g0ro, be exorbitant; ~ ho, agree, comp!}y; ~ karĩko, make an agreement; ~ kĩagarĩra, see kĩagarĩra; ~ magego, talk nonsense; ~ marĩgu (rĩgu), waste time, linger; ~ mĩkori, (of a goat) be foolish; ~ (mond) na ũgĩ, beguile (sb.); ~ mwaki, go on the spree; ~ mbeca, waste money, win a lottery; ~ ndereti, converse; ~ do sth. which brings evil consequences; ~ ng be fraudulent; ~ thiiri, get into debt; ~ thĩna, experience the pangs of poverty; ~ Niritu, wanake, enjoy the fruits of girlhood, young manhood; ~ make a fool of oneself; ~ ũru, experience hard times; ~ wana, act childishly; ~ wega, enjoy oneself. 3. be the death of, bring to a bad end; mwĩhĩtwa yũ nĩguo ũgakũ~, this vow will surely kill you (if you break it). 4. cause pain, hurt; nĩ nda ĩraknũ~?, are you having a stomach-ache? 5. defeat (in a game); nĩkaamũ~, (the cards) have defeated him, the result has come short of his expectation; see cũtha. rĩo [rino], pass., I1. be eaten, consumed. 2. be hurt; ~ nĩ motwe, ngoro, nda, have a headache, backache, stomach-ache. 3. be defeated (in a game of cards, argument, &c.); nĩaa~ nĩko, he has lost the game (he has failed to get what he expected). riaria, dim. eat a little more. rianga, diff. continue eating for some time; ~ ngeni, stay (a little while) longer away. rianganira, app. rec. diff, rianganiriza, caus. app. rec. diff. riangithia, d. caus. diff. help sb. to eat for a while. riangĩka, st, pot. diff. riangira, app. diff. riana, rec. 1, (of animals) sport with one another, gambol; fight. 2. devour, prey on, one another. 3. take advantage of sb., defraud; spring on. 4. (of fire) be fierce. 5. (of two things, joints, &c.) fit in each other., rĩanwo, pass. rec.; nĩkaa ~, an agreement has been reached. rĩania, caus. rec. 1. eat all sorts of things that do not go together., 2. eat anyhow. rĩanĩra, app. rec. I. eat together, eat from the same dish; (of relatives) eat together after they have been separated for a long time. 2. (of animals) fight at a place. rianiria, caus. app. rec. eat different things at one time; eat sth. in addition to. irianiria, refl. caus. app. rec. bring ceremonial pollution on oneself, bring destruction on oneself through one’s folly. rĩthia, d. caus. 1. assist, force, sb. to eat sth.; ~ ruuo, cause pain. 2. herd, look after stock; act as a shepherd, cowherd; keep an eye on, watch, oversee, supervise (people); ~ raise chickens. friithia, refi. d. caus. watch one’s step, look after oneself, be careful of what one does in relation to another. rĩthariithia, dim. d. caus, rĩthariithithia, d. caus. riithariithiria, app. d. caus. rĩthangia, diff. rĩthangania, rec. riithanganiria, assoc, rĩthangithia, d. caus. rĩthangiria, app. riithania, caus. rec. oversee working people; ~ na, (of a detective, &c.) watch sb.’s movements. rĩthaniria, caus. assoc. riithithia, d. caus. assist sb. to look after stock. rĩthĩka, st. pot. rĩthiria, app. look after stock for, at; cause stock to eat sb.’s crops. riika, st. pot. be edible; (of crops, &e.) get eaten (by stock}. riikanga, diff. st. pot. rĩkana, rec. st. pot. Rũomwe no kũ~ rũri~gwo, members of a family do not lose one another, but they quarrel over the family possessions. rĩkithia, d. caus. st. pot. cause to be devoured by; ~ mũgũnda ngũrge, be responsible for letting the garden be eaten up by pigs. riira, app. I. eat, at, for, from, in a place of. Mũria rũnene (rũthanju) ndarũrĩagĩra, he who asks for too large a strip of garden does not eat of it. 2. ~ thĩ, use underhand methods against, take advantage of sb., be disloyal to, play false; ~ ayenge. 3. match, suit; (of a pole in a hole, &c.) fit; prosper, flourish, live long. riirũo, pass.; ~ thtiri, be liable for payment of debt contracted by sb. else. |

ORIGINAL_VALUE: stem of dem. a. and p., all classes except 8 and 10, used as follows
FULL_ENTRY: -ria, p.a. (U), stem of dem. a. and p., all classes except 8 and 10, used as follows: 1. as a selector; ũ~ mwega, the good woman, the woman who is good. 2. as the pre-relative in a relative clause; ĩta mũndũ wĩ mũgũnda, call the man who is in the garden. 3. with the vowel of the preceding p.c. lengthened ad lib. that (yonder); those (yonder); see also -ria. 4. with the loc. prefix cl. 16; o ka~ nĩndĩthĩ, wherever I go. 5. with the cl. 5 prefix, when; rĩ~ rĩothe, whenever; rĩ~ rĩngi, the next time.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. one who eats. ~ na ~ na kaara niahĩaga, he who eats with a stick is not aware that he who eats with his fingers gets burnt. 2. freq. used in compounds
FULL_ENTRY: mũria, a-, n. (11), 1. one who eats. ~ na ~ na kaara niahĩaga, he who eats with a stick is not aware that he who eats with his fingers gets burnt. 2. freq. used in compounds: (a) ~-goro, one who holds out for a certain price, cheat, extortioner; see goro. (b) ~-irĩndĩ, one who lives by sponging on others; see kĩrĩndĩ. (c) ~-ĩmwe [mũriemwe], salutation between friends who have shared kidneys at a meat-feast, one each. (d) ~-ma, deceiver, cheat, one who denies what he knows to be true, one who having taken an oath goes to the medicine-man and is immunized from its effects; see ma. (e) ~-muuma, one who is untrue to his oath, renegade, traitor; see muuma, (f) ~ng one who strikes a hard bargain, extortioner, one who takes, claims, more than bis due; see ng 'ũrũ. (g) ~-thenge, one who is untrue to an oath taken; see thenge. (h) (N.K.) one who obtains, tries to obtain, by deception; see ũhinga; cf. ndĩra-thĩ, ndĩa-mathi. ũria, n, cl. 14 only, (only in compounds) 1. ~-goro, extortion, demand for higher price. 2. ~-ma, deceit, craft, dishonesty. 3. ~-ng'ũrũ, treachery. [ria']

NEW_VALUE: he found us just starting our meal
FULL_ENTRY: iriangiro [iriyangĩro], n. cl. 5 only (111); ~, he found us just starting our meal; driangiro-ini rĩa irio, during the course of the meal.
mũriano [mũriyaano], mĩ-, n. (111), 1. defrauding, cheating; ~ wa magũrũ, subterfuge, trickery. 2. taking advantage of sb., sponging on people. [ria!]
rĩbii, see ruthii.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one of a series of corrugations. (mũ-), mĩribi, n. unevennesses, roughness of ground or other surfaces
NEW_VALUE: one of a series of corrugations
FULL_ENTRY: iribi, ma-, n. (11), one of a series of corrugations. (mũ-), mĩribi, n. unevennesses, roughness of ground or other surfaces; cf. mathong’othong’o, mang’'ereng’ere, mahigahiga. kirĩbi, i-, n. hole, hollow. karĩbi, tũ-, n. dim. of above.
riboti, see riboti. mi-, n. (11), self-willed, wayward person.
mũricia [mũriicia], n. (v1), whip, lash. a. (11), eaten; ng'ombe nĩ ndie, the cow has been eaten. [rĩa1]
-rĩe? | -rĩe, a. (11), intoxicated. [rĩa?]

rĩgarĩga, v.i. (11), 1. (of goats) scamper, run about; scurry, trot. 2. walk hurriedly, quickly, briskly; cf. cũacũa, cũga. rĩgarĩganga, diff. rĩgarĩgania, caus. rec. chase each other, chase sb. about; cf. korakorania. rĩgarĩganira, assoc, rĩgarĩganĩria, caus, assoc. rigarigia, caus. 1. make scurry, hurry. 2. hurry (the hearing of a legal case) unduly, give a slipshod decision, scamp. rĩgarĩgithia, d. caus. rigarĩgika, st. pot. rĩgarĩgira, app. scurry, hurry, at, for. rĩgarigiria, caus. app.

FULL_ENTRY: mũrogori, a-, n. (1), medicine-man who can counteract the effect of black magic, exorcist, remover of spells. kĩrogori, i-, n. 1. that which removes the effects of black magic, unbinds the spell. 2. a powerful exorcist. [roga]

rogota!, v.t. & i. (1), 1. simmer, boil; cf. togota. 2. roll tobacco, make one’s own cigarettes; spin thread; cf. thogota. 3. strike with a cane on the legs, strike with a whip; cf. camũra. rogarogota, dim. rogotanga, diff. rogotana, rec. strike each other, others. rogotania, caus., rec. spin thread, roll tobacco, anyhow. rogotanĩra, assoc. and app. rec. rogotanĩria, caus. assoc. rogotithia, d. caus. rogoteka, st, pot. rogotera, app. 1. speak a foreign language, at, to; speak to in unintelligible language; cf. thogotera. 2. roll tobacco into cigarettes, for, at. 3. rail at; cf. negenera.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: confused sound of talking (in a crowd).
NEW_VALUE: confused sound of talking (in a crowd)
FULL_ENTRY: irogoya, ma-, n. (111), confused sound of talking (in a crowd). [rogoya]
mũroha, mi-, n. (VIIL), (N.K.) tall tree (Cussonia holstii); cf. mwenyere, mũthariti.
roho [rooho], ma-, n. (111), 1. (cl. 1/6) soul, spirit; ~ Mũtheru, the Holy Spirit. [cf. Sw. ROHO]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. come, go, first thing in the morning
NEW_VALUE: come, go, first thing in the morning
FULL_ENTRY: roka [rooka], v.i. (1), 1. come, go, first thing in the morning; go early in the day; ~ haha, come here first thing tomorrow morning. 2. (followed by infin.) start the day by doing; ~ go home tomorrow morning; ~ kũnjĩta, call me tomorrow morning. rokania, caus. rec. go with sb. to claim his debt early in the day; come, go, together first thing in the morning; ~ na, visit sb. in the morning together with sb, else. rokanira, assoc. visit sb., others, each other, first thing in the morning; have a row in the morning; perform circumcision operation; ~ na, get to grips with, meet together in the morning with. rokaniria, caus. assoc, send sth. to sb., pay a visit to people, in the morning. rokia, caus. take to, send, bring, in the morning; ~ mũtino, bring misfortune in the morning; wa~ kĩ? what do you bring so early ? rokitbia, d. caus. cause sth. to be brought, taken, early. rokeka, st. pot. rokera, app. visit sb. early in the morning, do sth. in the morning; ~ njohi, drink an early draught of beer; ~ mwere, go to scare birds off millet early in the morning; ~ ibuku, go and fetch a book early in the day; ~ mũndũ, visit sb. in the morning to collect a debt. rokeria, caus. app. bring, take, send, sth., sb., to sb. else in the morning; send for; ~ Kamau ng'ombe send cattle to the river for K. in the morning. rokerera, inten.; nĩũkũũ~ Njeri mwere?, will you scare birds off the millet for N. tomorrow morning?
mũrokero [mũrookero], mi-, n. (x1), early in the morning. [roka]
pa [rooketi], ~, n. (xvr), rocket.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one in the habit of visiting people early in the morning.
NEW_VALUE: one in the habit of visiting people early in the morning
FULL_ENTRY: kiroki [kĩrooki], i-, n. (1), one in the habit of visiting people early in the morning. karoki, n. name given to sb. with this habit. [roka]
rokiconi [rekĩconi], ~, n. (xvui), location, ward, division, of district. [E. LOCATION]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. early morning activity, an early morning call
NEW_VALUE: early morning activity, an early morning call
FULL_ENTRY: mũroko [mũrooko], mi-, n. (1), 1. early morning activity, an early morning call; ng'ania arĩ ~, so-and-so bas risen early for a purpose. Rũcini rũtirĩ mĩroko nirĩ, the morning has not two early calls (you cannot delay and yet hope to do sth. early). 2. the morning on which the second phase of the initiation ceremonies begins; see ndonyo. kĩroko, i-, n. (mainly N.K.) morning; ~ tene, early in the morning; cf. rũcini; see mũthenya. [roka]

NEW_VALUE: locomotive repair workshops (esp. Nairobi Railway Workshops)
FULL_ENTRY: roko [rooko], n. cl. o (1), locomotive repair workshops (esp. Nairobi Railway Workshops). [E. LOCO(MOTIVE)]

mũromo, mĩ-, n, (1) 1. doorway, entrance; (of calabash, &c.) mouth; (of bed) side farthest from the wall, see rũgito; ~ wa ihu, gullet (oesophagus) and its entrance into stomach, (of ruminant) reticulum. 2. (cl. 4) lips (with the surrounding area); mĩromo ya ihu, entrance to the oesophagus together with the oesophageal groove. kĩromo, i-, n. 1. lip. 2. (cl. 8) (more polite than mĩromo) lips. nyamũromo, (nyamiromo), n. with cl. 1/2 concords, nickname for a liar.
marombo (N.¥K.), see marobo.

rombora, v.t. (11), 1. peel off (scorched, bruised, burnt, skin); cf. rambũra. 2. break off plaster, &c. 3. chip, jag, break (metal, knife blade, &c.). rombarombora, dim. romboranga, diff. romborana, rec. scorch people, bruise each other’s skin. romborania, caus. rec. peel off entirely so as to make look romboranira, app. rec. romboraniria, caus, app. rec. romborithia, d. caus. romboreka, st. pot. romborera, app. romboka, v.i. be, become, peeled off; be bruised, scorched. rombaromboka, dim. rombokanga, diff. rombokana, rec. become peeled off entirely. rombokania, caus. rec. rombokanira, app. rec. rombokaniria, caus. app. rec, rombokia, caus. cause to peel, bruise, scorch. rombokithia, d. caus. cause sb. to be bruised, scorched, hurt. rombokera, app. be scorched, bruised, at a place. rombokeria, bruise sb. at a place; peel paint off, at, for; chip (off) at.
-rongo, a. (1v), false, unfaithful.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: curse, pour forth ritual, comminatory utterances, invocations
FULL_ENTRY: rongora [rongoora], v.t. (1), curse, pour forth ritual, comminatory utterances, invocations; make repeated appeals, say incantations. rongarongora, dim. rongoranga, diff. rongorana, rec. expect sb. rongoranira, app. rec. rongoraniria, caus, app. rec, rongoria, caus. I. expect sb. 2, direct one’s wishes, ambitions, desires, &c., towards a certain goal; set one’s face towards work with sth. in view, follow up; cf. tanya, erekera. 3. direct magic, witchcraft, towards sb., sb.’s family. 4. seek, after, for. Ngware ĩrongoragia mũngwethe, one partridge seeks after another (of a different type but same species) (birds of a feather flock together). Cf. rahira. rongorithia, d. caus. 1. direct evil omen to sb. 2. take sb. to medicine-man for ritual interrogation and purificatory invocation. rongoreka, st. pot. rongorera, app. say incantations over, for, at. rongoreria, caus. app. 1. say incantations; speak ritual words in an exaggerated way to ward off evil or disease; lead antiphonal worship, make supplications following an offering or libation. 2. express desire that sth. may befall sb.; invoke a curse upon, direct a blessing or curse to sb.; direct magic to, make magic effective by saying things; ~ gĩkũ, invoke death (on sb.); ~ mũnyaka, wish (sb.) good luck. [mũrongoorithia] / mũrongorithĩria ria}, a-, n. (1v), leader of chant or incantation; ceremonial conductor, master of ceremonies. [rongora]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. trace, direction in which to proceed
NEW_VALUE: trace, direction in which to proceed
FULL_ENTRY: rũrongoro [rũrongooro], n. cl. rir only (X1), 1. trace, direction in which to proceed; ndikũmuona ~, I cannot see where he has gone. 2. report, news; tũtiacokire kũmũigua ~, we heard no more of him. [rongora]
irong’a, ma-; karong’a, tũ-; n. (1), Grantt’s gazelle.
rong’o, ~, n. (1?), (N.K.) boy’s cudgel; cf. rũng’ũ.
kirong’oe / kĩrong’oi, i-, n. (vI), (N.K.) tufted tail of animal; cf. gĩting’oe.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. talk confusedly, burble, chatter. 2. be in tumult
FULL_ENTRY: ronja, v.i. (11), 1. talk confusedly, burble, chatter. 2. be in tumult; cf. thonja. ronjaronja, dim. ronjaronjia, caus. dim. ronjaronjera, app. dim. ronjanga, diff. ronjania, caus. rec, ronjanira, assoc. ronjia, caus. make (people) talk confusedly. ronjithia, d. caus. ronjera, app. chatter, burble, at, for. ronjekera, app. st. pot. be suitable for chattering, in, to., ronjeria, caus. app. cause to burble, chatter; speak confusedly, at, in, for.
ironja / ironjo, ma-; kĩronjo', i-; n. (11), 1. bubble of conversation, chuckle; glib, inconsequent, talk. 2. uproar, commotion, disturbance. [ronja]
kĩronjo?, i-, n. (11), long tail of fattailed sheep.
kĩronye, i-, n. (1x), indolent, apathetic person; one disinclined to exert himself as the result of physical weakness or disability; cf. kĩgnũta, gĩthayo. ũronye, n. cl. 14 only, indolence, apathy.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. slip away, slip out of one’s clutches, escape
NEW_VALUE: slip away, slip out of one’s clutches, escape
FULL_ENTRY: ronyoka, v.i. (11), 1. slip away, slip out of one’s clutches, escape; cf. monyoka. 2. (of metals) be shiny. ronyora, v.t. make shiny.
roo, ~, n. (1x), low gear. [E.]

rora, v.t. (1), 1. look, behold, see; look at, look after, take care (of); ~ irio itigacure, see that the food does not get burnt. 2. face, proceed, towards, to; ũrorete ka?, where are you going? 3. find out, inspect, taste, try; ~ kana wĩ na mbeca, see if you have got money. frora, refl. r. take care of, be careful about, oneself. 2. be conceited, self-regarding. rorarora, dim. roraroria, caus. dim. rorarorereria, caus. inten. dim. roranga, diff. rorangia, caus. diff. keep con trying on clothes to see if they will fit. rorangithia, d. caus. dim. assist sb. to tend livestock for some time. rorangĩra, app. diff. look (after livestock) for (for some time). rorana, rec. I1. look, stare, at each other. 2. visit a sick person, go and see how sb., sth., is. 3. watch, guard others; oversee, supervise workers; invigilate (an examination). rorania, caus. rec. I1. make things face one another; compare things. 2. inspect, examine, minutely. frorania, refl. caus. rec, compare oneself with. roranira, app. rec. roraniria, caus. app. rec. roria, caus, 1. make sth. face, proceed, towards; turn, send, direct, towards; ~ maitho na gũkũ, turn your eyes this way. 2. try out, test, experiment on; ta ~ nguo ĩno, just try on this dress., rfroria, refi. caus. I1. try on (a dress). 2. direct oneself towards. rorithia, d. caus, 1. make face. 2. have sb. look after sth., assist in looking after sth., sb. frorithia, refl. d. caus. pretend to be looking, after, at. roreka, st. pot. be examinable, be that which can be looked at; rina rĩtingĩ~, the sun is brilliant, too bright to be looked at; nyarari a woman of dazzling beauty. rorera, app. look, for, at; look after, for, at; inspect, examine, for, at; ndorera mwana, look after my baby for me; ndorera mathabu, see if my sums are right, correct my arithmetic paper. frorera, refl. app. look for oneself, look at, watch, gaze upon; ~ mũbira, watch a football match. roreria, caus. app.; ~ Kariũki ng'ombe na gũkũ, make the cattle move this way for K. rorereria, caus. inten. 1. peer into the far distance for; look intently, expectantly; be on the watch for. 2. take care, charge, of; cf. menyerera.
ũrorero-thi, n. cl. 14 only (1-11), view, prospect. [rora]
rori! [roori], ~ | ma-, n. (1), lorry. kĩrori, i-, n. big lorry, motor lorry. [E.]
kĩrori, i-, n. (I11), seer, prophet, predictor; one who receives a message from the Deity (usu. in a dream); cf. mũrathi. ũrori, n. cl. 14, prophecy. [rora]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: cut of meat, part of the hind leg which is the perquisite of young men.
NEW_VALUE: cut of meat, part of the hind leg which is the perquisite of young men
FULL_ENTRY: roriceti / (N.K.) rũriceti, ~, n. (1?), cut of meat, part of the hind leg which is the perquisite of young men.

kĩrorio, (i-), n. (1), test-case, pretension; ke na ~, give sth. to test whether it will be received; pretend to give sth., then withdraw the offer; erirũo eke na na ~, he was told to do a certain thing to try him. [rora]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) a wonder, matter for astonishment, marvel
NEW_VALUE: () a wonder, matter for astonishment, marvel
FULL_ENTRY: maroro, n. cl. 6 (1?), (N.K.) a wonder, matter for astonishment, marvel; cf. ũrirũ, magothe, magegania.
kiroruha, see kihoruha,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. dream, have a nightmare. 2. have a wet dream. 3. (of a river) rise. rotarota, dim. rotanga, diff. -rotanĩra, assoc. rotanĩria, caus. assoc, rotia, caus. cause to dream
FULL_ENTRY: rota? [roota], v.i. (11), 1. dream, have a nightmare. 2. have a wet dream. 3. (of a river) rise. rotarota, dim. rotanga, diff. -rotanĩra, assoc. rotanĩria, caus. assoc, rotia, caus. cause to dream; twamũgweta niegũtũ~, if we mention him we shall dream about him. rotithia, d. caus. cause to have dreams. roteka, st. pot. be imaginable. rotera, app. dream, at, about, for. roteria, caus. app.

rota? [roota], v.i. (11), x1. rust, tarnish, corrode; cf. gunda. 2. (of crops) be mouldy, develop rust. rotarota, dim. rotanga, diff. rotanĩra, assoc. rotanĩria, caus. assoc. make (things) rust at the same time. rotia, caus. cause to rust, rotithia, d. caus. roteka, st. pot. rotera, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (primarily of cow) ribs, side. ũrote |/ ũrutĩ, n. cl. 14, (ref. to humans) side
FULL_ENTRY: mũrote | (N.K.) mũrutĩ, mĩ-, n. (1x), (primarily of cow) ribs, side. ũrote |/ ũrutĩ, n. cl. 14, (ref. to humans) side; komera ~, lie on one’s side.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. dry smear (as of saliva on the mouth after sleep)
NEW_VALUE: dry smear (as of saliva on the mouth after sleep)
FULL_ENTRY: marotero [marootero], n. cl. 6 (11), 1. dry smear (as of saliva on the mouth after sleep); mucus; semen. 2. stain, incrustation, rust, discoloration; mark of scorching. [rota?]
kĩroto [kĩrooto], i-, n. (111), a dream, [rota!]
rothi! [roothi], n. (11); nyama ya ~ roast meat, joint. [E.]
rothi? [roothi], ~, n. (1?), almond. [Sw. Loz]
rũru [rũru], see rũru.

rua, [ruwa], v.i. (1), be circumcised, undergo initiation, cliterodectomy; ~ ngoro, become mature spiritually, become wise. ruanĩra, assoc. þe ’ circumcised at the same time as sb. else. ruithia, d. caus. 1. circumcise, perform circumcision; have one’s child circumcised. 2. be a host to neophytes, offer shelter to initiates after circumcision (usu. the first eight days). rruitbia, refl. d. caus. (of youths) act as if one was circumcised, be obstinate, disobedient, rude, defiant. ruitharuithia, dim. d. caus. ruithangia, diff. d. caus. ruithania, rec. d. caus. circumcise each other, others. ruithaniria, assoc. d. caus. I1. circumcise together; have one’s children circumcised at the same time as sb. else’s. 2. be a host to neophytes at the same time as sb. else. ruithithia, d. caus. have circumcised, assist in circumcising. fruithithia, refi. d. caus. x. get circumcised on one’s own initiative, get oneself circumcised accidentally. 2. (of children) be bitten on the genital organs by brown ants, &c. ruithĩka, st. pot. d. caus. ruithĩria, app. d. caus. circumcise, at, for; have one’s children circumcised at; hold a circumcision rite, at, for. rruithiria, refl. app. d. caus, (of youths) act obstinately, rudely, disobediently, to seniors. ruira!, app. be circumcised, at, for. ruirira, inten. be circumcised, initiated, in the next age set; nao magĩtũ~ mwaka Ũyũ ũngi, they were initiated after us in the following year. rũra, conv. cause to be uninitiated, do sth. extremely annoying to sb., behave like an uninitiated person towards sb. rũka, neut. become uncircumcised, revert to an uninitiated state; ndunke ingĩtema ũyũ, (as an oath, for emphasis) may I revert to the uncircumcised state if I cut down this tree (I am determined never to cut it down).

rua, n. (11), 1. (rare) recency, proximity of time; mahindi maya nĩ ma ~, these bones have recently been cleaned by scavenging birds or beasts. 2. used adv., more freq. with na written conjunctively, quickly soon; ndangiũka na~ (or ~), he will not be coming for some time; nka na~, come on, hurry; ica ~, see ica.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (1), dung mixed with rubbish that collects in a cattle or goat shed
NEW_VALUE: dung mixed with rubbish that collects in a cattle or goat shed
FULL_ENTRY: rũrua [rũruwa], (marũrua), n. (1), dung mixed with rubbish that collects in a cattle or goat shed; fresh dung used for wall plastering; rĩa ~, act stupidly; cf. mai.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: dress sb. up, prepare sb. for a ceremony. iruaca, refl. r. prepare oneself for a dance. 2. (of a girl) protect oneself
FULL_ENTRY: ruaca, v.t. (1), dress sb. up, prepare sb. for a ceremony. iruaca, refl. r. prepare oneself for a dance. 2. (of a girl) protect oneself; see nguiko. ruacanga, diff. ruacana, rec. ruacanira, app. rec. ruacaniria, caus. app. rec. ruacia, caus. ruacithia, d. caus. ruacika, st, pot. ruacira, app. iruacira, refl. app. prepare oneself, at, for a ceremony. ruacũra, conv. undo oneself, undress. ruacũka, neut. be, become, undressed, ungroomed; (of make-up) come off.
ruai, ~, n. (1), (N.K.) Whistling Thorn (Acacia drepanolobium) see mũgaa; cf. mũthi, mũgaa.
mũruano [mũruwano], n. cl. 3 only (1); andũ a ~, recently circumcised or initiated people; cf. muumo. [rua]
rubae, ma-, n. cl. 14/6 (v1), (of foot, &c.) swelling. mi-, n. (1x), shrub (Piper capense), roots boiled for inhalation against colds.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. put leather lid or base on honey pot. 2. put a lid on a beehive
NEW_VALUE: put leather lid or base on honey pot
FULL_ENTRY: rueka / ruĩka, v.t. (11), 1. put leather lid or base on honey pot. 2. put a lid on a beehive; cf. hengera. ruekarueka, dim. ruekanga, diff. ruekanĩra, app. rec. ruekaniria, caus. app. rec. ruekithia, d. caus. ruekeka, st. pot. ruekera, app. ruekerera, inten. ruekũra, conv. take the lid, base, off a honey pot. ruekka, neut. (of a lid, patch) come off; (of a honey pot) become lidless or baseless. ruekũkĩra, app. neut. (of a honey pot) have the lid, base, come off, at, upon sb., sth.

ruga, v.t. (11), cook; (specifically) boil; ~ njohi, brew beer; ~ cai, make tea; ~ bitina, provoke misunderstanding, fight; ~ maheni, concoct lies; ~ ndundu, call a private meeting; cf. hagira, therũkia, kima, thiora, keera, teng’ũra, ihũra, uruga, hiũhia, rinda, mata. rruga, refl. 1. get cooked easily. 2. (of a person) mumble to oneself, complain in undertones, grumble; consider, meditate. 3. think highly of oneself. 4. (of a sore) itch, become worse, fester, suppurate. rugaruga, dim. ruganga, diff. rugana, rec, rugania, caus. rec. ruganira, app. rec, cook for, each other, others. ruganiria, caus. app. rec. cook together. rugia, caus. rugithia, d. caus. 1. assist in cooking; have cooked, brewed. 2. use a pot in cooking; nũ wa~ thaburia ĩno?, who cooked with this saucepan? rugithania, rec. d. caus. cock together, help one another in cooking. rugithiria, app. d. caus. have food cooked, beer brewed, for, at; have a feast prepared for. rugika, st. pot. 1. be cookable. 2. fester; ~mwĩrĩ, be inflamed, suppurate. app. st. pot. be possible to be cooked, in, at, for. rugira, app. 1. cook, brew beer, for, at. 2. tell sb. where beer is being drunk. rugirira, inten. cook sth. in addition to. rugũria, caus. conv. use (a pot) for the first time; ~ nynmba, brew beer in a new house, have.a ‘house-warming’ party.
mũrugano, n. (111); -a ~, freshly cooked, boiled; maguta ma ~, new (castor) oil, newly made vegetable oil of any sort. [ruga]
mũruge, mi-, n. (11), sp. of sorghum, eaten raw; cf. mbaria.

NEW_VALUE: one who boils or cooks in a pot, a cook
FULL_ENTRY: mũrugi, a-, n. (111), 1. one who boils or cooks in a pot, a cook. ~ ndangĩaga mŨria, a girl who is a good cook will never lack a suitor. Cf. mũbici, mbici. 2. a common female name.

NEW_VALUE: of stirring a pot or other vessel, cleaning out a gourd, flask, or calabash, &c
FULL_ENTRY: rugu-rugu, ideo. (1), descr. of stirring a pot or other vessel, cleaning out a gourd, flask, or calabash, &c. ruguta [ruguuta] (N.x.), see huruta, mũruguti (N.K.), see mũhburuti,
kĩrugutĩro [kĩruguutĩro], i-, n. (1), (N.K.) hearth; furnace for smelting iron-sand. [ruguta]

NEW_VALUE: interpret riddles, translate into a different language, solve
FULL_ENTRY: rugnũra, v.t. (1) interpret riddles, translate into a different language, solve; cf. taũra. rugarugũra, dim. rugũranga, diff. rugũrania, caus. rec. translate entirely, anyhow. rugũranira, app. rec. translate to; translate, interpret, together. rugũranĩria, caus. app. rec. rugũrithia, d. caus. assist to translate, interpret; have translated, interpreted. rugũrika, st. pot. rugũrĩra, app. translate, to, for, at. rugũka, v.i. be, become, understandable; be translated, interpreted; (of riddles, proverbs, puzzles) become solved.
mũrugũrio, (mĩ-), n. (x), housewarming; ceremonial process of using sth. for the first time; njohi ya ~, beer prepared for house-warming (consumed by old men too old for sexual intercourse). [ruga]
mũrugũrũ, n. cl. 3 (x); ~ wa mbaki, mild snuff.
mũrui, a-, n. (1) neophyte before circumcision. kĩrui, i-, n. neophyte after circumcision and until the completion of initiation; cf. gĩciairi, kiumiri. [rua]
karuithia (-arũme), tũ-, n. (1-111), silverfish.

ruira? / ruera / rũera, v.i. (1), 1. be, become, moist, damp; (of colour, dye, in garments) become set. 2. (of land) be left to lie fallow; (of path) become overgrown, contracted, narrow, through disuse. ruiraruira, dim, ruiranga, diff. ruĩranĩra, assoc. become damp at the same time as sth. else. ruĩrithia, d. caus. ruirira, app. become damp at; (of dye on cloth) match, become set. [ruwira], v.i. (1), (of a matter) be ascertained; be concluded amicably and finally. ruiria, caus., ascertain sth.; bring (a matter) to a satisfactory settlement. ruĩrira, app. (used inten.).

mũruki, mi-, n. (1), 1. smell, pleasant or unpleasant odour; na ~, be on heat. 2. (cl. 4 only) fresh air, breath; ya maĩ, steam; ĩkĩra mĩbira mĩrukĩ, blow up tyres. kĩrukĩ kĩmũruki, (i-), n. bad smell, foul air; cf. kĩrumbea.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. piece of colobus skin worn by men as leg ornament at dances, generally black round ankle or leg, with projecting piece of white at back. 2. colobus skin cloak with a white point at the back
NEW_VALUE: piece of colobus skin worn by men as leg ornament at dances, generally black round ankle or leg, with projecting piece of white at back
FULL_ENTRY: iruku, ma-, n. (v11), 1. piece of colobus skin worn by men as leg ornament at dances, generally black round ankle or leg, with projecting piece of white at back. 2. colobus skin cloak with a white point at the back; cf. nguyo, (N.K.) matere. 3. (N.K.) a kind of bead; cf.
mũrukwati [mũruukwati], mi-, n. (vin), loquat-tree. rukwati, ~, n. loquat (fruit). [E.]

ruma!, v.i. (1), 1. (of bulls, &c.) bellow, roar, grunt; cf. ania. 2. resound, reverberate, echo; (of blacksmith’s anvil) give a dull sound; ~ ngoro, give a dull sound from the lungs as result of blacksmith’s bewitchment (probably T.B.). 3. (of doves) coo. rumaruma, dim. rumanga, diff. rumanĩra, assoc. bellow at the same time (as). rumaniria, caus. assoc. rumia, caus. ~ rũimbo, sing loudly, heartily, enthusiastically., rumithia, d. caus. ~ mũgoiyo, lead in a vigorous dance by singing the solo. rumira, app. bellow at, for, to. rumĩriria, inten. prolong low bass tone for serious effect; intone in chanting. rumũka, neut. (of combined voices in a song, dance) be very loud; cf. gonoka. rumũkia, caus. neut.

ruma?, 1. abuse, affront, insult, swear at; curse (esp. on a death-bed); put burden on. 2. publish abroad; ~ fix a day for a visit. rumaruma, dim. rumanga, diff. rumana, rec. I. abuse, curse sb.; call sb. names. 2. abuse each other. rumanira, app. rec. rumaniria, caus. app. rec. abuse, curse, people together. rumithia, d. caus. rumika, st. pot. rumira, app.
karuma, see under numa.,
mũruma [mũruuma], mi-, n. (VIII), (N.K.) underground bachelor’s club house; cf. ndomo, gĩturi kia nyamũ. ũruma, n. cl. 14 only, (of wild beast) lair; (of porcupine, &c.) burrow.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: small tree (Bersama abyssinica) (distinguished by disproportionate flower-spike)
FULL_ENTRY: mũruma-andũ / mũrumia-andũ, mĩ-, n. (1-1), small tree (Bersama abyssinica) (distinguished by disproportionate flower-spike); see a&-, n. (11), one who curses; an abusive, insulting person. ũrumani, n. cl. 14, use of insulting language, vituperation. [rumazz]
-rumanire, a, (iv); mĩgũnda a garden that has a curse upon it against people trespassing or stealing. [ruma?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: snipe, kingfisher, dabchick or lesser grebe, moorhen or waterhen.
NEW_VALUE: snipe, kingfisher, dabchick or lesser grebe, moorhen or waterhen
FULL_ENTRY: karuma-ndiyũ [-ndiiyũ] / karumandihũ [-ndiihũ], tũ-, n. (10-11), snipe, kingfisher, dabchick or lesser grebe, moorhen or waterhen.
karuma-nđgunũ, see karogi-ngunũ.
mũruma-thirio, mi-, n. (III-VII), a bush (Turraea mombassana).
irumi, ma-, n. (1), grey, comparatively soft, building-stone.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: knob of wood, boss on a tree.
NEW_VALUE: knob of wood, boss on a tree
FULL_ENTRY: kĩrumu!, i-, n. (1), knob of wood, boss on a tree.
rumu, ~, n. (1), room., karumu, tũ-, n. dim. of above. kĩrumu?, i-, n. aug. of above. [E.]

rumba, v.i. (1), 1. muffle, cover up; speak in a cryptic manner. 2. look glum, unhappy. rumba, refl. muffle oneself up completely to keep off severe cold; cf. ĩririmba, rumbarumba, dim. rumbanga, diff. rumbana, rec. dress (children, &c.). rumbania, caus. rec. rumbanira, assoc. rumbaniria, caus. assoc, rumbithia, d. caus. rumbika, st. pot. rumbira, app. rumbũra, conv. uncover sth. rumbũria, caus. cony. uncover (sb., sth.). #rumbũria, refl. conv. uncover oneself. rumbũka, neut. ; see humbũka, umbũka.
mũrumbae, see mũrumbea.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: bush about 3 ft. high (roots used in broth). irumbea, mi-, n. leaf of this bush (used as eye-medicine
FULL_ENTRY: mũrumbea / mũrumbae, mĩ-, n. (x1), bush about 3 ft. high (roots used in broth). irumbea, mi-, n. leaf of this bush (used as eye-medicine; also boiled and patient’s head held over the steam as remedy for influenza).

mũrumbi, mi-, n. (11), 1. (of birds) flight; (of locusts) swarm; (of dust, flies, bees, &c.) cloud; (of fish) shoal; (of cattle) mob, drove; (of people) moving crowd. 2. cloudy effect of wealth of blossom on bush or trees, flourish, irumbi, ma-, n. 1. vapour rising after rain, fine misty spray rising from waterfall; ~, have thin spray of beer blown over one by mouth in blessing ; cf. ndihũ, grthambio kĩa ũgĩmbĩ. 2. haze from smoke or heat of sun; steam from pot. 3. flying swarm of bees, flies, gnats.
(ki-) (ma-) rumbi, see under marumbi,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. second partt, second instalment, 2. younger brother or sister, next-born
FULL_ENTRY: mũruna, mĩ-, n. (1), 1. second partt, second instalment, 2. younger brother or sister, next-born; ~we, his younger brother; cf. itiema.

runda, v.i. (11), 1. become unfathomable, unintelligible, obscure; the dance (song) is unintelligible (one cannot make head or tail of it). 2. (of a path, road) come to a dead end, peter out, come to a standstill, disappear; njĩra nĩya~, the road has come to a dead end; cira wa~ ikara thĩ, if you are unable to argue your side of the case, sit down. rundarunda, dim. rundanga, diff. rundana, rec. come to a dead end, peter out, become dark. rundanira, assoc. rundanirira, inten. assoc, act foolishly to one another; fail to have the slightest recollection of. rundaniria, caus. assoc. rundia, caus. ĩrundia, refl. caus. adopt a bashful, stupid, pose; cf. ĩkĩgia, ĩgoiya, ccicia, ĩrigithia, rundithia, d. caus. rundira, app. become to know, at, for. rundirĩra, inten.; it was extremely difficult for him to make head or tail of the dance (song). rundĩrirũo, pass. inten.; ~ nĩ ũhoro, lose the thread of the argument.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. hit (usually playfully). 2. submerge in the water. irundika, refl. submerge oneself in water. rundarundika, dim. rundikanga, diff. rundĩkana, rec. 1. hit sb., hit each other. 2. submerge sb.
FULL_ENTRY: rundika, v.t. (1), 1. hit (usually playfully). 2. submerge in the water. irundika, refl. submerge oneself in water. rundarundika, dim. rundikanga, diff. rundĩkana, rec. 1. hit sb., hit each other. 2. submerge sb.; become submerged, drowned; cf. hindĩkana. rundĩkania, caus. rec. rundikanĩra, app. rec. niria, caus. app. rec. submerge (things) together at, for. rundĩkira, app. put under water, at, for.
-rundu, a. (1v), obscure, unintelligible, unfathomable. [runda]
mũrundu, mi-, n. (1v), 1. timber-tree (Celtis kraussiana). 2. small tree (Turraea abyssinica); cf. mũti-mũirũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of mist, fog) come down, become thick. 2. (of light) become dim. rundarunduhara, dim. runduharanga, diff. runduharana, rec, become pitch dark during the day. runduharania, caus. rec, runduharanira, assoc. rundukaraniria, caus. assoc. runduharia, caus. runduharithia, d. caus. runduharĩka, st. pot. runduharira, app. become dark for, when sb. a at a place
NEW_VALUE: (of mist, fog) come down, become thick
FULL_ENTRY: runduhara, v.i. (11), 1. (of mist, fog) come down, become thick. 2. (of light) become dim. rundarunduhara, dim. runduharanga, diff. runduharana, rec, become pitch dark during the day. runduharania, caus. rec, runduharanira, assoc. rundukaraniria, caus. assoc. runduharia, caus. runduharithia, d. caus. runduharĩka, st. pot. runduharira, app. become dark for, when sb. a at a place; be, become, dull, for, to, sb.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. dimness (of eyes when waking up from sleep, when sight is failing, &c.)
NEW_VALUE: dimness (of eyes when waking up from sleep, when sight is failing, &c
FULL_ENTRY: marundurundu, n. cl. 6 (v), 1. dimness (of eyes when waking up from sleep, when sight is failing, &c.); ona ~, be dazzled. 2. obscurity of expression. [runda] n. (1m1), God; cf. ngai, mwene-nyaga.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: name given to hornless female goat or cow. karungu, tũ-, n. dim. of above. kirungu, i-, n. (usu. with cl. 9/10 concords) male goat, ox, or bull of above type
NEW_VALUE: name given to hornless female goat or cow
FULL_ENTRY: mũrungu?, mi-, n. with cl. 9/10 concords (v1), name given to hornless female goat or cow. karungu, tũ-, n. dim. of above. kirungu, i-, n. (usu. with cl. 9/10 concords) male goat, ox, or bull of above type; cf. mũria?.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: snip, cut a little bit off, bite a small bit off.
NEW_VALUE: snip, cut a little bit off, bite a small bit off
FULL_ENTRY: runya, v.t. (1), snip, cut a little bit off, bite a small bit off. irunya, refl. snip oneself (with scissors, &c.). runyarunya, dim. runyanga, diff. runyana, rec. runyania, caus. rec. runyanira, app. rec. runyaniria, caus. app. rec. runyia, caus. runyithia, d. caus. runyika, st. pot. runyira, app.

rura, v.i. (1), 1. be green, fresh, succulent; be dark green. 2. have tender, smooth skin after using oil; (of snuff) be softened with oil when grinding; be moist and oily (not dry as dust). 3. (of sun) become less intense, be cool, cool down; rina rĩa~, when the sun cools down (usually between 3 and 4 p.m.); see mĩrarahũko. rurarura, dim. ruraruria, caus. dim. make a little greener; ~ mbaki, make snuff softer and smoother; ~ mwĩrĩ, make skin smoother with oil. rurarurĩria, caus. app. dim. make softer, smoother, greener, for, at. ruranga, diff. rurangia, caus. diff. rurania, caus. rec. make each other’s skin smooth by massaging with oil. ruranira, assoc. ruraniria, caus. assoc. ruria, caus. 1. make deep green; mbura ya trio, rain to make the crops green. 2. make (tobacco) smooth, tone down with fat; smear oil on dry skin, leather. iruria, refl. caus. eat fat tasty meat; ~ iromo, wet dry lips. rurithia, d. caus, rurĩka, st. pot. rurĩra, app. ruriria, caus. app. fruriria, refl. caus. app. make one’s body smoother, softer, at, for; make sth. smooth, better, for oneself; cf. rurũra, conv. make rough. rurũka!, neut. become rough; (of skin) become dry, change from smooth and soft to dry and rough.
rũrura, (ndura), n. (VIII), 1. evergreen plant (Adiantum spp.) 2. Asparagus fern (Asparagus africanus). 3. Smilax (Asp. medeoloides). [rura]

mũruru, mĩ-, n. (VIr), 1. sticky, viscous substance; foetal water, mucous-like fluid; ~ wa itumbĩ, white of egg; ~ wa clear liquid honey, cf. ~ ria albumen. 2. lochia, post-parturial flow; climacteric flow. 3. (cl. 4) exudation, oozings, tricklings; tua mĩruru, cease to bleed, staunch flow of blood after child-birth; haata mĩruru, cleanse a hut after a child-birth away from home; igongona rĩa mĩruru ya mwana, ceremony of cutting a child’s umbilical cord; ikia rũi, bathe at the river during menstrual period (a serious offence involving ceremonial uncleanness).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: lushness, greenness, freshness, dampness, damp spot.
NEW_VALUE: lushness, greenness, freshness, dampness, damp spot
FULL_ENTRY: ũruru, n. cl. 14 (11), lushness, greenness, freshness, dampness, damp spot. [rura]
-ruru, a. (11), 1. green, fresh, succulent; nyeki nduru, fresh green grass. 2. (of sun) cool. 3. (of well-greased body) smooth, shiny. [rura]
ruru! [ruuru], n, cl. 11, pain in goats’ hooves; cf. ragurumo.
ruru? [ruuru], ~, n. (viu), pearl. [Sw. LULU]
ruru?, see riri,

NEW_VALUE: of quick walking, swift falling, gushing out of water
FULL_ENTRY: ruru, ideo. (1?), descr. of quick walking, swift falling, gushing out of water.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. speak in a deep resonant voice
NEW_VALUE: speak in a deep resonant voice
FULL_ENTRY: ruruma'!, v.i. (1), 1. speak in a deep resonant voice; thunder. 2. (of the voice of an adolescent boy) break. 3. (of an old parent or relative) mumble (thereby causing sickness to daughters, daughters-in-law, grandchildren). 4. (of an engine) start, hum, tick, rumble, roar; make a din, a deep murmur; cf. gamba. rurumaruruma, dim. rurumanga, diff. rurumangia, caus. diff. let (an engine) run a little longer. rurumangira, app. diff. rurumania, caus. rec. 1. talk, to each other, others, in deep voices. 2. have prolonged thundercłaps. rurumanira, assoc. 1. mumble endearments to each other. 2. roar, rumble, at together. 3. (of engines) start at the same time. 4. mumble curses, cause illness to younger relatives by such curses. rurumaniria, caus. assoc. make engines start, rumble, race, at the same time. rurumia, caus. 1. make roar, hum, rumble. 2. start an engine. ĩrurumia, refl. caus. (of an engine) self-start, roar by itself. rurumithia, d. caus. rurumika, st. pot. (of an engine) be capable of being started. rurumira, app. 1, speak in a deep voice, hum, to, for, at; (of an engine) roar, start, at. 2. cause sickness to by mumbling. rurumiria, caus. app. I. start (an engine), cause to hum, at, for. 2. prolong an incantation in deep resonant voices to make it effective. conv. make roar loudly, bring (an engine) to life, drive off in a car hurriedly with a roar. rurumũka, neut. begin to roar, rumble (after being silent).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: x1. (of pain, &c.) revive, recur. 2. (of tree stumps, deciduous plants) show signs of life
FULL_ENTRY: ruruma?, v.i. (1), x1. (of pain, &c.) revive, recur. 2. (of tree stumps, deciduous plants) show signs of life; (of buds) come to life, sprout, be revived from a withered condition, germinate. rurumanga, diff. rurumania, caus. rec. 1. (of vegetation) grow luxuriously, riotously. 2. (of pains, diseases, &c.) recur, revive together; cause each other to recur or begin being active. rurumanira, assoc. rurumaniria, caus. assoc. cause to recur simultaneously. rurumia, caus. make sprout, cause to recur., refl. caus. 1. meditate, commune with oneself, go over things, recall. 2. (of stumps) bud, sprout, by themselves. rurumithia, d. caus. cause to begin to be active, make sprout. rurumika, st. pot. rurumira, app. recur, at, because of. rurumũra, conv. make spring to life again, activate strongly. rurumũka, neut. recur suddenly, begin to be active forcibly; sprout out again, begin to grow after a drought. irurumũkwo, refi. pass. neut. have sth. come back into one’s mind, revive an old memory. kania, caus. rec. neut. (of crops and weeds) grow vigorously and rapidly. rurumũkanira, assoc. neut. rurumũkaniria, caus. assoc. neut. rurumũkia, caus. neut. revive, cause to sprout again; raise (a matter) again; ~ cira, bring up a case again. rurumcũkithia, d. caus. neut. rurumũkithĩria, app. d. caus. neut. rurumũkira, app. neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. anything big and flat, bulky or lumpish, e.g. a hill, a grade bull, certain types of motor vehicle
NEW_VALUE: anything big and flat, bulky or lumpish, e
FULL_ENTRY: mũruruma [mũruuruma], mi-, n. (viii), 1. anything big and flat, bulky or lumpish, e.g. a hill, a grade bull, certain types of motor vehicle; ~, taciturn, unresponsive, person. 2. carcass skinned or unskinned, corpse; cf. krimba, mũrũ
iruruma, ma-, n. (1), talk, rumour. [ruruma!]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. thunder-clap (formerly regarded as the noise of the fight between Black and White gods). 2. (cl. 6) rumblings
FULL_ENTRY: irurumi, ma-, n. (v), 1. thunder-clap (formerly regarded as the noise of the fight between Black and White gods). 2. (cl. 6) rumblings; cf. ngwa. kirurumi, i-, n. murmur, rumble of voices; distant murmur of rapids, motor-cars, &c. [ruruma!]

mũrurumo, mi-, n. (1), 1. a rumbling, roaring, booming sound; any heavy, prolonged sound or noise; ga na ~, fall heavily; 2. time, turn, try; ~ Ũngi, another go. kĩrurumo, i-, n. roaring of waters, rapids, waterfall; cf. ndurumu, ndururumo. [ruruma!]

rurutĩka [ruruutĩka], v.i. (11), (of sb. who has been hit hard, a sick, hungry, person) stagger, wobble, be unconscious, dizzy. rurutĩkanga, diff. rurutĩkanĩra, assoc. rurutikaniria, caus. assoc. rurutikia, caus. make dizzy, cause to stagger with a blow. rurutĩkithia, d. caus. rurutikira, app. feel dizzy, stagger, be unconscious, at. rurutikiria, caus. app. give a staggering blow to sb., at, for.
kĩrurutha [kĩruruutha], i-, n. (vin), (N.K.) taciturn, boorish person.

NEW_VALUE: (of crops} dance beautifully in the wind
FULL_ENTRY: rurũka?, v.i. & 1. (of crops} dance beautifully in the wind. 2. slip, slide, skid; cf. tenderũka, tirũka. 3. ignore, disdain, despise. rurarurũka, dim. rurũgkanga, diff. rurũkana, rec. rurũkania, caus. rec.; nĩ wega kuona mwere Ũki~ ũ, it is beautiful to watch millet dancing in the wind thus. rurũkanira, assoc. rurũkaniria, caus. assoc. caus. d. caus. rurũkika, st. pot. rurũkira, app. rurũkiria, caus. app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. remove (out of), take out (from), take off, subtract
NEW_VALUE: remove (out of), take out (from), take off, subtract
FULL_ENTRY: ruta', v.t. (1), 1. remove (out of), take out (from), take off, subtract; extract, put out of, take away; ~ nguo, undress; ~ kĩhuti, extract a speck (from the eye); ~ nja, take outside, make public; ~ mũndũ wĩra, discharge sb. from employment; ~ guoya, encourage, take away sb.’s cowardice; ~ thoru, talk to sb. so that he does not feel lonely. ti gũcokia, taking out is not putting back (you cannot eat your cake and keep it). 2. pay; produce, give out; ~ pay tax; ~ put forward a suggestion; ~ horo nja, tell news, let the cat out of the bag; ~ ndũgũ na, make friends with; ~ thĩnjo, be fined a goat (to be slaughtered by elders); ~ wĩra, work; ~ igongona, sacrifice; ~ ndũngũmi, covet sb.’s food, &c., be insolent; ~ (mũndũ) njira, travel with sb. so that he does not feel lonely. ĩruta, refl. 1. offer oneself, volunteer. 2. extricate, remove, oneself (from a situation); rid oneself of, take off (a garment); get rid of (jiggers, &c.); ~ ng'aragu, eat all without minding others. 3. ~ wĩra, work for oneself (when others refuse to work for one). rutaruta, dim. rutarutania, caus. rec. dim. disjoint a little more. rutarutithia, d. caus. dim, rutarutira, app. dim. take out of (the fire), for, at. rutarutũrũra, inten. (conv.) dim.; see rutũrũra. rutanga, diff. rutana, rec. take each other, others, out of; ~ na, begin a journey, with, at. rutania, caus. rec. I1. take apart, dismantle, disentangle, disjoint; ~ ndigi, unravel threads. 2. begin friendship or enmity with sb.; maarutanirie o hau, their co-operation began there; arra mararnũa maa~ kĩ? what caused those who are fighting to fight? 3. ~ igongona, perform a ceremony. rutanĩra, app. rec. take out (things) together; take, out, off, for each other, others; maa~ hin, they unsheathed their swords against one another (they almost fought); ~ wira, work, with, for; maa~ mbũri ya kĩama, they gave the council one goat jointly. rutanĩria, caus. app. rec, 1. bring, put, take, out (things) at the same time. 2. take to pieces together. frutanĩria, refl. caus. app. rec. exert oneself intensely, redouble one’s efforts; cf. ĩgũmira. rutia, caus. 1. begin, at, from; start to deal with; ~ kũrĩma haha, start cultivating here; mbura yarutirie kuura o iyo, it has been raining since the day before yesterday ; ~ cĩhĩ, be meticulous; wama~ kĩ? how did you provoke them into a quarrel? rutithia, d. caus. I. get taken, out, off, from. 2. ~ wĩra, assist sb. to work, have some work done, supervise work being done, make (sb., sth.) work. 3. have sb. discharged from employment. frutithia, refl. d. caus.; ~ wĩra, work alone away from others, have oneself discharged from employment, pretend to be working and so avoid other people’s attention. rutithania, rec. d. caus. work jointly, together; help one another at work. rutithanika, st. pot. rec. d. nĩguo wĩra Nrutithanĩke wega, that the work may proceed harmoniouslły. rutithaniria, app. rec. d. caus.; ~ wĩra harĩa, make people work over there, supervise work yonder. rutithiria, app. d. caus. have sth. taken out for sb. else; ~ Kamau irio, have K. given food; ~ Kamau wĩra, assist K. by having his work done by sb. else, rutĩka, st. pot. be easily removable, extractable; wĩra nĩũkũ~, work can be done (e.g. after rain). rutĩkĩka, d. st. pot. be easily and successfully workable, be capable of accomplishment. rutikira, app. st. pot; ithandũkũ rĩrĩ rĩtingĩ~ this box will not go out of the doorway. rutira, app. 1. take out, off, for, at. 2. ~ mũndũ wĩra, work for sb., work as substitute for a friend. rutirira, inten.; ~ mbembe, get some more maize out. rutirĩria, caus. inten. begin at, with; ~ horo haria ũtigũire, begin the story where you left off. rutũrũra, inten. (conv.), evict, expel, drive away from; ~ andũ camba, evict people from the farm; ~ mbũri gĩthaka-inĩ, get the goats out of the thicket. rutarũranga, diff. inten.; ~ mbũri, get the goats out of the bush quickly. rutnrũrana, rec. inten. expel people (from), drive one another away. rutũrũranira, app. rec. inten. rutgrũraniria, caus. app. rec, inten. expel, evict, drive away, together; ~ mbũri cianyu na citũ, drive. both your goats and ours out of the bush. caus. inten. d. caus. inten. have evicted, driven away, expelled; assist in getting goats out of the bush. rutũrũrithania, rec. d. caus. inten. rutũrũrithiria, app. d. caus. inten. have evicted, &c., for, at, because of, st. pot. inten. be evictable, expellable. rutũrũrira, app. inten. evict, expel, drive away, for, at, because of. rutũrũrĩria, caus. app. inten. rutũrũrũka, neut. inten. be all cleared, driven out, expelled ; come out of as a group or in a line; mbũri nĩcia~, finally it was possible to get the goats out of the bush, rutũrgrũkia, caus. neut. inten. rutũrũrũkithia, d. caus. neut. inten. rutũrũrũkika, st. pot. neut. inten. rutũrũrũkira, app. neut. inten. caus. app. neut. inten.

ruta?, v.t. (1) teach, instruct. fruta, refl. learn, study, teach oneself s wĩrute! have manners! learn, study for the examination. rutaruta, dim. rutarutana, rec. dim. rutanga, diff. rutana, rec. teach, instruct others, each other. rutanĩra, app. rec. teach, in, at, for. rutaniria, caus. app. rec. teach two classes together, teach people who are at different levels together. rutithia, d. caus. rutĩka, st, pot. be teachable. rrutĩka, refl. st. pot. be easy to learn, study; mathabu matingĩ~, one cannot teach oneself mathematics; gũting~ nĩ frugarĩ, one cannot study because of the heat. rutĩra, app. teach sb., a class, at, in (a classroom). refl. app. study, at, in, for; ~ kĩgeranio, study for the examinations; ~ mũti-inĩ, study under a tree.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. a handing-out, subscription, customary payment, contribution, levy
NEW_VALUE: a handing-out, subscription, customary payment, contribution, levy
FULL_ENTRY: iruta | rĩruta, ma-, n. (1), 1. a handing-out, subscription, customary payment, contribution, levy; tribute paid to one’s own or a senior age-group; levy by local district or clan. 2. ~ rra wĩra, a spell of work. mĩ-, n. (1), sp. of wild running plant (grows in fallow gardens, used as goat fodder).

NEW_VALUE: teacher, instructor. ũrutani, morutani [moorutani], n. teaching
FULL_ENTRY: mũrutani, a-, n. (1), teacher, instructor. ũrutani, morutani [moorutani], n. teaching. [ruta?]
irutaniro, ma-, n. (1), training centre. [ruta?] a-, n. (1); ~ wa wĩra, workman, wage-earner, labourer,

NEW_VALUE: make sick, cause to be ill
FULL_ENTRY: rũara, v.i. (11), be ill, be sick; cf. tutua, thũthũnyũo, tambwo. dim. rũaranga, diff. rũarangia, caus. diff. caus., rec. make (people) ill. assoc. become, be, ill at the same time as sb. else. rgaraniria, caus. assoc. make sick together, have people become ill under one’s care; nitũrũaranĩrie ciana, we have our children sick (in two or more households). rũaria, caus. 1. make sick, cause to be ill. 2. have sb. sick under one’s care, nurse, tend. ĩrũaria, refl. caus. pretend to be ill; nurse oneself when ill, look after oneself. rũarĩka, st. pot. rũarira, app. become ill, at, for. rũariria, caus., app. have sb. ill at; ~ kaana ũgeni, have one’s baby sick away from one. refl. caus. app. pretend to be sick (as an excuse for sth.).
maũrũarano, n. (11); -a ~, recently become ill. [rũara]
kĩrũaro, i-, n. (111), 1. illness. 2. lesion, bad place; kahiũ ge ~, ruooro rũtiri, the knife makes a bad place, the branding-iron does not. [rũara]
mũrũaru, mi-, n. (1v), 1. sick person, patient. 2. golden-crested crane; cf. cũacũ-wa-njoka, naitũri (nyaitũri), hũgau. Ũrũaru, n. cl. 14, sickness, complaint; cf. ndwari. [rũara] a. (1v), ill, sick.
rũba (N.K.), see rũha.
kĩrũbĩ, see

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be busy, wander about in search of, be restless. rũgarũga, dim. bustle about, hustle, run
NEW_VALUE: be busy, wander about in search of, be restless
FULL_ENTRY: rũga, v.i. (11), be busy, wander about in search of, be restless. rũgarũga, dim. bustle about, hustle, run; cf. cũacũa. rũganga, diff. rũgania, caus. rec,; ~ na ng'ombe, chase the cattle that are restless. assoc. rũganiria, caus. assoc, rũgia, caus, cause to be restless, wander about; rina rĩa mũgaa mbũri, the hot sun (of the months December- January) will make goats restless; ~ matũ, (of animals) prick up ears, be on watch, scamper away. rũegithia, d. caus. app. rũgiria, caus. app.; ng'ombe wert-inĩ, the cattle are making him wander all over the

rũgama [rũgama] / (N.K.) rũngama, v.i. (1) 1. stand up, be upright (two alt. forms of the perf. aspect are in common use, -rũngii and -rũgaime); see also rnga. 2. stand still, halt, stop walking; ~ hanini, wait a moment. 3. pass water. 4. (of moon) be new; mweri ũkirie kũ~, a new moon is about to appear. 5. (of a bride) stay unnecessarily long before having a baby. rũgarũgama, dim. stand up for a while, wait for a short while. rũgamanga, diff. rũgamangia, caus. diff. delay, cause to stand, wait, for a while. rũgamana, rec. 1. stand up alongside each other (as when comparing heights); face one another. 2. stand together, with; nĩngũka thaa mũgwanja cia~, I’ll come at one o'clock. rnũgamania, caus. rec. I. make others stand, halt, pause; halt people at the same time. 2. make sth. lean up, make things stand alongside one another. rũgamanira, assoc. halt, stand up, stop moving, at the same time as sb. else. rũgamanirira, inten. assoc. supervise, oversee; be a substitute for a guard, supervisor, while he is on leave. rũgamaniria, caus. assoc. make stand up, halt, &c., at the same time; make halt, stand still, stand upright, at, for; make lean upon one another, at, for; make stand alongside, against, each other, at, for. rũgamia, caus. make stand up, stop, halt, bring to a standstill; stand sth. upright. rũgamia, refl. caus, stand in one’s strength, stand aside from others, come to a stop by self effort. rũggamithia, d. caus. have stopped from motion, have put in an upright position, assist in bringing to a stop, help in raising to an upright position. frũgamithia, refl. d. caus. stop moving (disobeying the order to move on). rũgamithĩria, app. d. caus. have stopped, for, at; help to erect sth., for, at. rũgamĩka, st. pot. be capable of being stopped, halted, erected, raised position, d. st. pot. app. st. pot. be capable of being raised upright, vertically, at a place. ragamira, app. 1. halt, stand, at, for. 2. cease to bear children for the time being, stay longer than usual before having a baby. rũgamiria, caus. app. stand upright, for, at; make halt, for, at. inten. r. stand by, watch over, sth. 2. take charge of, superintend, manage, look after. 3. be a substitute for sb. else, act on behalf of sb. over sth. 4. be best man, bridesmaid, godparent to sb. 5. be engaged in, pursue one’s occupation. rũgamiriria, caus. inten. 1. look after for, cause to halt for sb. at. 2. stay long, be of use temporarily until sth. permanent is found; cf. ikarĩrĩria. rũgamũka, neut. (of sb. standing near another) move away; ~ mũthuuri, do not stand near an old man (sitting down).

FULL_ENTRY: mũrũgama-njira [mũarũgama-], a-, n. (XI-11), robber who lies in wait for travellers, a highwayman. [rũgama]

(1), (of sb., animal, trying to get away) make a sudden leap; cf. rũga, guthũka. rũgithũkanga, diff. rũgithũnkania, caus. rec. leap away together with sb. else, make people leap away. rũgithũkanĩra, assoc. rũgithũkaniria, caus. assoc. caus. make leap, run away. rũgithũkithia, d. caus. rũgithũkira, app. run away through, at. rũgithũkĩria, caus. app. / kĩrũbi, i-, n. (1v?), clumsy dancer; cf. kĩrũbĩri, kĩrũgi. n. cl. 4 (1v), bustle, busyness, activity; cf. mĩhang’o, mĩhũgo. [rũga] mi-, n. (1x), 1. long-tailed Whydah bird. 2. tail feather of Whydah. i-, n. male Whydah bird. nyamirũgũ, n. 1. cow with all-black hump which is high and jumps about. 2. person or animal of excitable nature.

FULL_ENTRY: rũha [rũnũha], v.i. (1) run around singing for joy, frolic, jump about excitedly, gambol; cf. taũha. rũharũha, dim. rũharũhia, caus. dim. caus. app. dim, rũhanga, diff. rũhangia, caus. diff. rũhania, caus. rec. rũhaniria, caus. assoc. caus. I1. jump about with joy, shout with joy. 2. sing a song of triumph after a successful raid, lift up voices in rejoicing; frolic, rejoice; atumia makenete no ki~ mara~, the women are so happy that they are just frolicking. rchithia, d. caus. make frolic, rejoice, gambol. rũhira, app. rũhĩria, caus. app. frolic at, exult, gambol, shout with joy, for, at; triumph over.
karũhagi, sce under ihapi.

[rũha] mi-, n. (11), protuberance, excrescence, boss, knob; cf. thũkũ, iguku.

NEW_VALUE: cause to come unfixed, come off
FULL_ENTRY: rũka, v.i. (11), (of a joint) come unfixed, unhafted; come to pieces; (of nails) be pulled out. 2. pop out, escape; tũtina~ ũmũthi nĩ mbura, we remained indoors all day today because of the rain. rũkarũka, dim. rũkanga, diff. rũkana, rec. become unhafted entirely; zcembe the hoe has come off the handle entirely. rũkania, caus. rec. get a jointed apparatus disjointed. rũkanira, assoc. rũkanĩria, caus. assoc. disjoint together, at, for. caus. pull out, pull to pieces, unhaft, loosen, cause to come unfixed ; ~ mĩcumarĩ, pull out nails with a claw hammer; ~ mbeca, produce money; ~ ngwacĩ, pull out potatoes (esp. a good crop). rũkithia, d. caus. 1. cause to come unfixed, come off. 2. defeat in a lawsuit. 3. (of a bull) defeat and chase the vanquished bull. 4. assist to loosen and pull out sth. rũkika, st. pot. rũkĩra, app. become unhafted, at, for. rakĩria, caus. app. pull out, for, at; take joints to pieces; ~ ngwacĩ, pull good potatoes out of the ground for.
mũrũka, mi-, n. (vi1), (N.K.) unattached person; mũtumia wa ~, wayward woman or wife, woman of Joose morals. [rũka]
karũkunyũ, see under rũkunyũ.
mũrũkia, a-, n. persistent, pertinacious person; one who perseveres with a task or in a cause. [rũka]

karũkũ, see under rũkũ. v.t. (1) rub clothes; cf. kumutha, tanduka. rũkũthanga, diff. rũkũthana, rec. rũkũthania, caus. rec. app. rec. caus. app. rec. rũkathia, caus. rũkũthithia, d. caus. rũkũthika, st. pot. rũkũthira, app. caus. app.

rũma', v.t, (11), 1. bite, bite off, eat; take a handful, spadeful (of grain, sand, &c.), reduce by a handful or small half-calabashful; ~ tkano, make a fist with the thumb between the first and second finger (during initiation); ~ ndereti, converse, hold a conversation, discussion; ~ tĩri, make an oath by touching the earth with the lips, get a spadeful of earth; ndirĩ ndĩra~ kĩndũ, I have not eaten a thing today; ~ ngeni, be hospitable to visitors, be on the way to visit; ~ ithaga, karĩnga, bite an ornament, trinket (on the right side of the neck only) (for luck, to remember sth. or to preserve prosperity after taking inventory of one’s wealth); ~ nginyo, follow closely on sb.’s heels, stalk sb. 2. end at, reach the limit, be up to a point (including that point), come to an end, terminate; ndoro ya~ o Gĩwe, the road is muddy as far as G.; kinya kũrĩa thĩ ya~, to the utmost corner of the earth; ithuothe kũ~ Njoroge, all of us including N. rũmwo, pass. xi. be bitten, be reduced by a small measure; ~ ngoima, be fined a ram; 2. (of a woman) be in labour. rũma, refl. bite oneself; ~ kĩni, redouble one’s efforts; set one’s teeth, be determined to do or not to do sth. Mwana kaara akũraga narua, a child who does not suck his thumb grows quickly. ramarũma,. dim. bite off a bit more; reduce (grain, sugar, &c., being measured), take some away. rũmanga, diff. rfrũmanga, refi. diff. bite one’s lips in vexation. rmana, rec. bite each other, bite sb. rngmania, caus, rec. give sb., each other, a bite of. rũmanĩra, app. rec. bite one another, at, for; bite, eat together, at the same time as. niria, caus. app. rec. 1. bite things off together, get handfuls of different things together; ~ mbembe na mboco, get small measures of maize and beans together. 2. do repeatedly. rũmanirira, inten. app. rec. follow sb., follow each other. rũmaniriria, caus. inten. app. rec. 1. take sth. after sb., follow sb. to give him sth. he left behind. 2. do things repeatedly. rgmia, caus. give a bite of food to sb.; ~ kaana irio, give the baby a bite of food, rũmithia, d. caus. make bite, cause to bite; ~ mũndũ ngui, make a dog bite sb. rec. d. caus. r. make (joints, &c.) fit. 2. cause animals to bite people, each other; ~ magego, clench, grit, teeth, shut teeth tight in determination; ct. hagarania. ramitbiria, app. d. caus. cause sth. to bite sb., at, for. rũmika, st. pot. 1. be capable of being bitten. 2. (of grain, &c.) be reduceable, obtainable in handfuls. ramira, app. 1. bite, bite off, at, for. 2. (of one selling grain, salt, &c.) add a little bit above the measure; ~ Njeri irio na guoko, give N. a handful of food. irũmira, refl. app. I. bite oneself, at, for. 2. shovel a measure of (grain, &c.) for oneself; help oneself, take a handful of for oneself. rũmirira, inten. 1. add still some more, put in other additional measures over and above what one has already added. 2. follow, walk after; nao makĩmũ~, and they followed him. rũmirĩria, caus. inten. make follow, take sth. after sb. who has left; ~ thoguo kĩnya kĩa mbaki, follow your father with his snuff container. conv. eat, devour, munch, consume, with gusto. rũcaus, conv. devour, eat, large quantities, with gusto. rũmũka, neut. (of sth. caught up by a vice, &c.) be released, get loose, be liberated. rũmũkia, caus. neut. release from the grip of, a vice, teeth, &c.; let go, liberate from a grip. rũmũkiria, app. caus. neut. let go from a grip at.

mũrũmaniriro, mĩ-, n. (111), procession, following, train. [rama!]
rgmande, ~, n. (iv), remand. [E.] i-, n. (111-1v), that which sticks to the pot, burnt arrowroot.
kirũmati, i-, n. (1x), after-pains of childbirth.

mũrũme, a-, n. (11), 1. husband. 2. (cl. 2) male persons; cf. mũndũ. karũme, (tũ-), n. (used by women) dear little husband. kamũrũme, tũ-, n. 2nd dim. (used only in a derogatory sense) a poor sort of husband; cf. kamũndũ. Ũrũme, morũme n. quality of being male, masculinity, virility; bravery, valour, daring; fierceness, ferocity; cf. camba.
-rũme!, a. (Iv), bitten. [rũma!]
-rgme?, a. (111) male; nĩ mũndũ mg~ ma, so-and-so is a very brave, manly person.

mũrũndi, mi-, n, (11), shrub (Lasianthus kilimandscharicus).

NEW_VALUE: forest plant characterized by jointed stems with a long space between the joints
FULL_ENTRY: irũngo, ma-, n. (1), 1. mũrũngũrũ, mĩ-; (in folk-tales) wamũrũngũru, wamirũngũru; n. (1v), weasel, black mongoose. ~ wa njamba na ime, the weasel of merit kills in the dew (early in the morning). karũngũru, tũ-, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. song sung by boys on the way from bathing in the river to the place of circumcision.

FULL_ENTRY: tararĩka3, v.i. (1), 1. (of smell) spread strongly, pungently, sweetly. 2. (of locusts) fly. tararĩkanga, diff. tararikanĩra, assoc. tararĩkanĩria, caus. assoc, tararĩkia, caus. cause a smell, spread a smell; make a locust fly. tararĩkĩra, app. tararĩkĩria, caus. app. mĩ-, n. (XI), strong smell, odour; cf. monungo, mũruki; see ndarariki. [tararĩka?] | mũtarata, mi-, n. (V), row, line, column of people, row of huts; cf. mũrandaranda,

NEW_VALUE: (), (of girls) make a feast for boy friends and their companions
