mũtarathini, mĩ-; ndarathini, ~; n. (xiv), cinnamon. [Sw. MDALASINI]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. general name for a bramble ( Rubus sp.)
NEW_VALUE: general name for a bramble ( Rubus )
FULL_ENTRY: mũtare, mi-, n. (1V), 1. general name for a bramble ( Rubus sp.); blackberry-, raspberry-, bush (used as a fuel in certain rituals). 2. ~ kĩgombe, sp. of bramble (R. volkensii) (with reddishyellow fruit; sweet and excellent for cooking). ndare, ~, n. bramble; blackberry, raspberry, wild strawberry.
gatari, see under ndari?,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: tendon, ligament (at back of knee, ankle, &c.)
NEW_VALUE: tendon, ligament (at back of knee, ankle, &c
FULL_ENTRY: maũtari | mũtaritari, mĩ-, n. (Iv), tendon, ligament (at back of knee, ankle, &c.); tinia mĩtari, hamstring; rũgia mĩtari, (in contemptuous sense) move off; cf.

-tarii, v. defective, contracted perf. (1) be like, look like, resemble; Ũcio a~ atĩa?, what does the man look like?; ni nyone gĩ~, let me see what it is like; ga~-gũ~, ga~ na gũ~, (as adv.) thus and thus, such and such is the way of it, it is like this; ndũngĩenda kũmwĩra gĩ~, would you not like to tell him about it?; cf. haana.
mũtarimbg, mĩ-, n. (1), crowbar. [Sw. MTAIMBO]
(ga-) (gĩ-) tario, see under ndario.

NEW_VALUE: of flapping of woman’s skin skirt, loose sandals, &c
FULL_ENTRY: tari-tari, ideo. (1), descr. of flapping of woman’s skin skirt, loose sandals, &c.
matari, n. cl. 6 (1x), (N.K.) mental derangement (usually slight); cf. mbũgi,
itariri, ma-, n. (x1), 1. matter worth considering; gra ~, be of some account. 2. special group, company, category; cf. itari. [tara]
tarĩta! [taarĩta], v.t. (1), make a beadwork anklet.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.x.) go to the rescue of, aid
FULL_ENTRY: tarĩta? [taarĩta] / tarĩta [taarĩta], v.t. (1), (N.x.) go to the rescue of, aid; advise, counsel; put in order. taratarita [taarataarita], dim. tarĩtanga, diff. tarĩtia, caus. tarĩtithia, d. caus. tarĩtĩka, st. pot. tarĩtira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: large collection, conglomeration, mass of sth.
FULL_ENTRY: gitaro | gĩtarũ?, i-, n. (111), large collection, conglomeration, mass of sth.; large village; cf. gĩtegenyo.
gĩtaroharo, i-, n. (1?), secretary-bird.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: boat, ship, pontoon ferry, raft, canoe.
NEW_VALUE: boat, ship, pontoon ferry, raft, canoe
FULL_ENTRY: f ma-, n. (Iv), boat, ship, pontoon ferry, raft, canoe. gĩtarũ!, i-, n. aug. of above, gatarũ, tũ-, n. dim. of above.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: ornamental coil of wire round neck, arm, or leg of women.
NEW_VALUE: ornamental coil of wire round neck, arm, or leg of women
FULL_ENTRY: rũtarũ [rũtaarũ], ndarũ!, n. (11?), ornamental coil of wire round neck, arm, or leg of women.
gatarũa, see under ndarũa.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of cattle) go for a long time without water.
NEW_VALUE: (of cattle) go for a long time without water. see ndarũna
FULL_ENTRY: tarũna [tarũna], v.i. (11), (of cattle) go for a long time without water. see ndarũna,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: pull (plants, root crops, &c.) up by the roots
FULL_ENTRY: gĩtarũni, see gĩtaũ?, v.t. (11), pull (plants, root crops, &c.) up by the roots; uproot, tear up; tear (clothes) apart; tear (human, animal, body) to pieces, wound, rend, lacerate; flay, strip (animal carcass); cf. munya, cehũra, tararia, ombora. ĩtarũra, refl. taratarũra, dim. taratarũka, neut. dim. targũranga, diff. tarũrangithia, d. caus. diff. tarũrangika, st. pot. diff. be torn to pieces. tarũrangira, app. dim, tarũrana, rec. (of thorns, claws) scratch, wound, make a woundlike cut; wound sb., each other with thorns or claws, tarũrania, caus. shred up completely, wound badly with claws or thorns; pull up by the roots anyhow. tarũranĩra, app. rec. tarũraniria, caus. app. rec. tarũrithia, d, caus. tarũrĩka, st. pot. tarũrĩra, app. tarũka, neut. tear, split; be, get, torn, split, ripped, shredded up. tarũkanga, diff. neut. targkana, rec. neut, be torn, be severed completely. tarũkania, caus. rec. neut, tear up entirely, finish up what is left by tearing up. tarũkanira, assoc. neut, tarũkaniria, caus. assoc. neut. tarũkia, caus. tarũkithia, d. caus. neut. tarũkika, st. pot. neut. tarũkira, app. neut. nguo nĩramũ~, his clothes are getting torn on him, he is in rags. tarũkĩria, caus. app. neut,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: winnowing tray, made of mũgio (q.v.) &c. bast. gatarũrũ, tũ-, n. 1. dim, of above. 2. basket-work teatray
FULL_ENTRY: gitarũrũ [gĩtaarũrũ], i-, n. (vur), winnowing tray, made of mũgio (q.v.) &c. bast. gatarũrũ, tũ-, n. 1. dim, of above. 2. basket-work teatray; cf. tiree.
matata! [mataata]j, n. cl. 6 trouble, perplexity; entanglement; -a ~, funny, puzzling, fierce, troublesome. [Sw. MATATA]
matata? (N.xK.), see mateti,
tatane, n. cl. o (xĩv), (N.K.) righthand division of circumcision ageset; cf. ngitienye. [cf. Ma., TATENE]
gĩtati?, i-, n. (11), a girl’s wide beadembroidered hip-belt.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: joint ownership, partnership, association for trade, &c.
NEW_VALUE: joint ownership, partnership, association for trade, &c
FULL_ENTRY: gitati!, i-, n. (1), joint ownership, partnership, association for trade, &c.; ngondu ya ~, fat sheep suitable for a feast; wĩra wa ~, communal labour; cf. ngwataniro.
gitati?, see gicaci,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. tree (Polyscias kikuyuensis) (trunk straight and branchless, wood soft and white, easy to work, used for mole-traps, beebarrels, and honey pots). 2. (of persons) a giant
FULL_ENTRY: mũtati, mi-, n. (11), 1. tree (Polyscias kikuyuensis) (trunk straight and branchless, wood soft and white, easy to work, used for mole-traps, beebarrels, and honey pots). 2. (of persons) a giant; ~ wa mũthuuri, a giant of an elder. i-, n. mole-trap, made out of short hollowed-out section of above tree; cf. mũtego, kĩrangi. i n. cl. 14 only (1v), (Bibl.) Trinity.
gatathano, see under ndathano.

itatha, ma-, n. (11), 1. bulging side, flank, stomach; ikia matatha, (of animals) be replete (with bulging sides). 2. cheek of a wide shield. gĩtatha / gĩtathwa, i-, n. potbellied goat or person. [see tathũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. give food or drink to famished person
NEW_VALUE: give food or drink to famished person
FULL_ENTRY: tathara, v.t. (1), 1. give food or drink to famished person; cf. tangira, tang’atha, tathaiya. 2, save from danger of fire. tatharanga, diff. tatharana, rec. tatharania, caus. rec. tatharithia, d. caus. tatharika, st. pot. tatharĩra, app. itatharira, refi. app.; ~ maũndũ, manage one’s own affairs, be enterprising, cf. ĩteithia, ĩtabarĩra, rigania.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a shrub (Clausena anisata) (the twigs have an aromatic smell and are used as tooth-cleaning sticks).
NEW_VALUE: greeting between men who have exchanged the gift of a leaf or bunch of leaves of this shrub
FULL_ENTRY: mũtathi [mũtaathi], mĩ-, n; (11), a shrub (Clausena anisata) (the twigs have an aromatic smell and are used as tooth-cleaning sticks). itathi, ma-, n. I. leaf of this shrub (pleasant scented, used in a bunch as part of the insignia of ruling grade of elders, also as a handkerchief, placed in the groin when sitting). 2. (cl. 6) greeting between men who have exchanged the gift of a leaf or bunch of leaves of this shrub. ndathi, ~, n. black or purple fruit of this shrub.

NEW_VALUE: the noise of a stick hitting a person or beast
FULL_ENTRY: tathi, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1. the noise of a stick hitting a person or beast; gambia ~, give sb. a good whack; cf. tithi. 2. the sound of bursting.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: small bush, Kikuyu wormwood ( Artemisia afra).
NEW_VALUE: greeting used by girls and women
FULL_ENTRY: mũtathia [mũtaathia], mi-, n. (vI), small bush, Kikuyu wormwood ( Artemisia afra). itathia, ma-, n. 1. leaf of this bush (very sweet-scented, used as a stomach medicine). 2. (cl. 6) greeting used by girls and women. [cf. Ma. OL-MATASYA]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: strike, make an audible whack, give a sharp cut to (with a stick). tathatathikia, dim. tathĩkangia, diff. tathĩkania, rec. 1. whack each other, others
FULL_ENTRY: tathĩkia, v.t. (11), strike, make an audible whack, give a sharp cut to (with a stick). tathatathikia, dim. tathĩkangia, diff. tathĩkania, rec. 1. whack each other, others; fight. 2. (of rain) come down hard in a storm. tathikanĩria, app. rec. I1. strike each other, others, at, for. 2. (of rain) rain hard on people. tathĩkiria, app.
taurg, ~, n. (111), towel. [E.]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. a kind of food made from boiled bananas mixed with ripe bananas and roasted bananas, then mashed up with sugar-cane, specially prepared for boys. 2. a big sore or ulcer
NEW_VALUE: a kind of food made from boiled bananas mixed with ripe bananas and roasted bananas, then mashed up with sugar-cane, specially prepared for boys
FULL_ENTRY: gitaũ!, i-, n. (1), 1. a kind of food made from boiled bananas mixed with ripe bananas and roasted bananas, then mashed up with sugar-cane, specially prepared for boys. 2. a big sore or ulcer; cf. gĩtegenyo.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. name of one of the early age-sets, about 1850. 2. common male name, given to a male who speaks his mind, one who calls a spade a spade, one who is indiscreet and blurts things out
NEW_VALUE: name of one of the early age-sets, about 1850
FULL_ENTRY: gĩtaũ?, n. cl. 1/2 or 7 (1), 1. name of one of the early age-sets, about 1850. 2. common male name, given to a male who speaks his mind, one who calls a spade a spade, one who is indiscreet and blurts things out; aria ~, speak plainly; cf. gĩtarũni. [taũra]
taũni [taaũni], ~, n. (xv1), town. [E.]

taũra!, v.t. (1), translate, interpret, expound, explain (dreams, &c.); cf. rugũra. taũranga, diff. taũrania, caus. rec. translate, interpret anyhow; reveal, expose, entirely, taũranira, app. rec. taũraniria, caus. app. rec, taũrithia, d. caus, help sb. to translate, interpret; have sth. translated. taũrika, st. pot. taũrira, app. explain, interpret, to; translate for. taũka, v.i. be expounded, explained; be, become plain, clear, undefstandable, explainable. taũkanga, dif. taũkania, caus. rec. explain fully. taũkanira, assoc. (of dreams, ideas, puzzles, &c.) become ‘clear, be understood, together. taũkira, app. become plain to sb.
gĩtaũri, i-, n. (1), I. interpretation, sth. that explains. a know-all. ũtaũri, motaũri [mootaũri], n. translation, exposition, explanation.
ũtaũro, see ndaũro.

tawa [taawa] / taa, ma-, n. cl, 14/6 (11), lantern, lamp; gwatta, mundia, akia ~, light a lamp. gĩtawa, i-, n. I1. aug. of above. 2. perforated tin with a handle used by herd-boys as a portable stove during cold seasons; cf. thagiri, nũgĩ. [Sw. TAA]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. fiat roof of sticks covered with leaves or grass built to afford shelter for a person, or over a seed-bed. 2. rough shelter or screen built round an open fire
NEW_VALUE: fiat roof of sticks covered with leaves or grass built to afford shelter for a person, or over a seed-bed
FULL_ENTRY: gitayaya [gĩtayaaya], i-, n. (vur), 1. fiat roof of sticks covered with leaves or grass built to afford shelter for a person, or over a seed-bed. 2. rough shelter or screen built round an open fire; cf. boi. 3. rough guardhouse outside stockade of homestead; see kĩhingo. 4. portable platform on which a sick person can be carried; stretcher or frame on which heavy objects can be carried; cf. ndayaya.

tebeba [tebeeba], v.i. (1), be nervous, frightened, terrified. tebatebeba, dim., tebebanga, diff. tebebangia, caus. diff. tebebania, caus. rec. frighten sb., each other. tebebanira, assoc. tebebanĩria, caus. assoc. tebebia, caus. intimidate, frighten. ĩtebebia, refl. caus. show fright when one is not expected to, tebebithia, d. caus. tebebeka, st. pot. tebebera, app. be frightened at. tebeberia, caus. app.

NEW_VALUE: that causes fright or terror, a frightening or terrifying act
FULL_ENTRY: itebebia [itebeebia] / itebebio [itebeebio], ma-, n. (1), sth. that causes fright or terror, a frightening or terrifying act. [tebeba]
tebeta, v.t. (11?) ostracize, avoid, shun sb. tebetanga, diff. tebetana, rec. avoid, have nothing to do with, one another. tebetania, caus. rec. tebetanĩria, caus. app. rec. tebeteka, st. pot.
mateeti, see matiti-matĩti.

tega!, v.t. (11), 1. set trap for, trap; lay, use a trap; ~ mata, listen carefully. 2. (in football, &c.) trip, trip (sb.) up. tegatega, dim, tegategũra, conv. dim. teganga, diff. tegana, rec. trip sb., one another. tegania, caus. rec. teganira, app. rec. teganiria, caus. app. rec. tegithia, d. caus. have trapped, have a trap laid; help to lay trap. tegeka, st. pot. be capable of being trapped. tegekera, app. st. pot. tegera, app. lay traps, for, at; trap an animal for sb. tegerera, inten. crowd in. tegereria, caus. inten.; ~ mbara mwaki, go close to a fire in cold weather. ftegereria, refl. caus. inten.; ~ rita, sit and bask in the sun. tegũka, neut. 1. (of trap, trigger, &c.) spring, go off, become uncocked. 2. (of water in a dam), be released. tegũkanga, diff. neut. rec. neut. spring off. tegũkania, caus. rec. neut. let go off. tegũnkanira, assoc. neut. tegũkanĩria, caus. assoc. neut. tegũkia, caus. neut. tegũkithia, d. caus. neut. tegũkika, st. pot. neut. kĩra, app. neut. ORIGINAL_VALUE: take sth. as a present to (a bride, woman after childbirth, another person as a mark of esteem)
NEW_VALUE: take as a present to (a bride, woman after childbirth, another person as a mark of esteem)
FULL_ENTRY: tega?, v.t. (1), take sth. as a present to (a bride, woman after childbirth, another person as a mark of esteem); ~ njohi, take beer to sb. else’s home. teganga, diff. teganira, app. rec. teganiria, caus. app. rec. tegithia, d. caus. help (a woman) to carry presents to sb. else; have presents sent to. tegeka, st. pot. tegera, app. send, take, presents to.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: present of food, &c., taken on special occasions to a house as a mark of friendship or respect. [tega?] mi-
FULL_ENTRY: itega, ma-, n. (1), present of food, &c., taken on special occasions to a house as a mark of friendship or respect. [tega?] mi-; gitegenye, i-; n. (xu), herb (Cyathula polycephala) (stems and leaves densely clothed with hair); cf. kĩramata, mũtenyo. ndegenye, ~, n. fruit or seed-pod of the above which attaches itself as a burr to anything passing such as the hair of goats and sheep; ~ ndĩgana guoko, (used when selecting an animal to mark it for possible purchase) the burr is not the equivalent of the hand (a smack of the hand concludes a purchase).

tegetũka, v.i. (1) dwindle away, sink down; subside; (of stock, grain) decrease when drawn upon; (of crowd of people) decrease voluntarily. tegategetũka, dim. tegetũkanga, diff. assoc. tegetnka-niria, caus. assoc. tegetũkia, caus. diminish, cause to decrease, have one’s property decreased. d. caus. tegetũkĩka, st. pot. tegetũkĩra, app. decrease, because of, at. tegetũkiria, caus. app. tegetũra, v.t. make dwindle, diminish, subside, decrease. dim. tegetũranga, diff. tegetũrana, rec. tegetũranira, app. rec. tegetũraniria, caus. app. rec. tegetũrithia, d. caus. tegetũrika, st. pot. tegetũrĩra, app. reduce, for, at.
mũtegi', a-, n. (111), a trapper. [tega]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who takes or sends a ceremonial present of food, &c., to another person
NEW_VALUE: one who takes or sends a ceremonial present of food, &c
FULL_ENTRY: mũtegi?, a-, n. (1), one who takes or sends a ceremonial present of food, &c., to another person; see itega. [tega?]
tegithi [teegithi], ~, n. (xvr) country bus, taxi; cf. mbaathi. [E.]
oO tũ-, n. (xiv), catechism.
gatego, (tũ-), n. (111), venereal disease esp. syphilis. [Sw. TEGO]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. large hole, depression, cavity, e.g. depression where water collects during the rainy season, deep hole in the bed of a river, big cavity in a tree trunk
FULL_ENTRY: gĩtegũ, i-, n. (VII), 1. large hole, depression, cavity, e.g. depression where water collects during the rainy season, deep hole in the bed of a river, big cavity in a tree trunk; any hollow, pocket, niche on or among rock(s), empty space in a dense forest or bush; pitfall; cf. irima. 2. a large earthenware pot with a big cavity.
(ga-) (gĩ-) (ma-) tegwa, see under ndegwa.
mũtehũko, mi-, n. (11), gasping. [tehũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. that which causes loss, malign influence which brings misfortune
NEW_VALUE: that which causes loss, malign influence which brings misfortune
FULL_ENTRY: gitei, i-, n. (111), 1. that which causes loss, malign influence which brings misfortune; cf. kĩrii, kĩniini. 2. a careless person who loses things. [te]
ũteithania, n. cl, 14 only (1v), assistance, aid, help to others. [teithia?]
ũteithanio, n. cl. 14 only (u1), mutual assistance, aid, help. [teithia?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: help, assist, aid, put oneself at the disposal of
NEW_VALUE: come apart, becomee disconnected, be disunited, separate from each other. 2
FULL_ENTRY: teithia?, v.t. (11), help, assist, aid, put oneself at the disposal of; assist with, in. refi. r. help, defend, take care of, oneself; put up resistance. 2. relieve oneself, defecate. teithateithia, dim. teithangia, diff. teithania, rec. teithaniria, app. rec. teithithia, d. caus. teithĩka, st. pot. teithĩkĩra, app. d. caus. teithiria, app. teithĩrĩria, inten. teithũra, conv. disconnect; separate (contestants), teithũranga, diff. conv. rec. conv. make peace between parties (in a contest), separate, pull apart. teithũrania, caus. rec, conv, teithũranira, app. rec. conv. teithũraniria, caus. app. rec, conv., teithũranĩka, st. pot. app. rec. conv. be capable of being separated, severed. teithũrithia, d. caus. conv. cause to be freed, severed, rescued, teithũrika, st. pot. conv. teithũrira, app. conv. separate, make peace, act as an arbiter, at, for. teithnũka, neut. come free, disconnect, teithũkana, rec. neut. 1. come apart, becomee disconnected, be disunited, separate from each other. 2. die, expire. teithũkania, caus. rec. neut, teithũkanĩra, assoc. neut. teithũkanĩria, caus. assoc. neut. teithnkia, caus. neut. ĩteithũkia, refl. caus. neut. break away, free oneself. teithũkithia, d. caus. neut., teithũkĩka, st. pot. neut. teithũkĩra, app. neut. teithũkiria, caus. app. neut.

NEW_VALUE: helper. Ũteithia, n. cl, 14 only, help, assistance
FULL_ENTRY: mũteithia, a-, n. (Iv), helper. Ũteithia, n. cl, 14 only, help, assistance. [teithia?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sth. stodgy or gelatinous, sth. wet and soft (e.g. mud, dung, &c.)
NEW_VALUE: stodgy or gelatinous, wet and soft (e
FULL_ENTRY: gĩteke, i-!, n. (1), sth. stodgy or gelatinous, sth. wet and soft (e.g. mud, dung, &c.); (of food) any kind that is too thick or too watery; stodgy stodge, ill prepared food, gruel, or porridge; stodge, thick gruel made from millet and milk; cf. trio, ngima; see tendeka.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: make muddy, beat in a fight, trample wet clay to make daub
NEW_VALUE: make a doughy substance at, for. 2
FULL_ENTRY: tekea, v.t. (1), make muddy, beat in a fight, trample wet clay to make daub; cf. terema. tekatekea, dim. tekeanga, diff. tekeana, rec. fight, fight with lumps of mud. tekeania, caus, rec, trample on mud; mess with a doughy substance. tekeanĩra, app. rec. fight, for, at. tekeanĩria, caus. app. rec. 1. make a doughy substance at, for. 2. beat (things) simultaneously. tekeithbia, d. caus. tekeeka, st, pot. tekeera, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1 small tree or shrub (Strychnos usambarensis). 2. tall tree (Strychnos sp.). i-, n, (1), a trampled place

ema, v.t. (of goats, &c., standing massed at a barrier, water behind a dam) stop short, come to a standstill; (of donkey} pull up with a jerk and refuse to budge; cf. thindima, hindima, tindatindima, dim. tindimanga, dif., tindimana, rec. tindimania, caus. rec. tindimanira, rec. app. tindimaniria, caus. rec. app. tindimithia, d. caus. tindimithĩka, st. pot. d. caus. tindimithĩra, app. d. caus. tindimũra, conv. tindimũrana, rec. conv. tindimũrania, caus. rec. conv. tindimũrithia, d. caus. conv. tindimũrika, st. pot, conv. tindimũka, neut. tindimoũkanĩra, app. rec. neut. tindimũkanĩria, caus, app. rec, neut. tindimũkanirira, inten. rec. neut. tindimũkaniriria, caus. inten. rec. neut.

tindira, v.i. (1), 1. (of solid sudd supported by water, &c.) quake. 2. (of a tree) be flat-topped, rounded, widespreading. tindatindira, dim. tindiranga, diff. tindiranira, assoc. tindiranĩria, caus. assoc. tindiria, caus. tindirithia, d. caus. tindirika, st. pot. tindirĩra, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. ancient tree with spreading branches. 2. aged, venerable, man full of years and wisdom
FULL_ENTRY: itindiri, see ndindiri. mĩ-; gĩtindiri, i-; n. (x1), 1. ancient tree with spreading branches. 2. aged, venerable, man full of years and wisdom; old and trusted counsellor; cf. ndindiri. [tindira]
tindirũka, see tendergka.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: place where people spend the day (or a large part of it)
NEW_VALUE: to occupy one’s time, work, occupation
FULL_ENTRY: maũtindo, mĩ-, n. (111), place where people spend the day (or a large part of it); market-place, village green, talking-place, place where the council hears cases. gĩtindo, i-, n. place where one or more persons or animals spend the day; haunt, lair; cf. gĩrikaro, kĩraaro. ũtindo, n. cl. 14 only, sth. to occupy one’s time, work, occupation. [tinda]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sth. partly full, portion (left over), remainder, sth. slightly more or less than half remaining
NEW_VALUE: partly full, portion (left over), remainder, slightly more or less than half remaining
FULL_ENTRY: gitingia [gĩtingiya], i- / ma-, n. (X111), sth. partly full, portion (left over), remainder, sth. slightly more or less than half remaining; reke cuba ũria ~, bring the bottle which is not full up. gatingia, tũ-, n. sth. less than half full, a small portion. [see tingiara]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of liquid in full container) be reduced, halved. tingiaranira, assoc. tingiaraniria, caus, assoc. tingiaria, caus.
NEW_VALUE: (of liquid in full container) be reduced, halved
FULL_ENTRY: tingiara [tingiyara], v.i. (11), (of liquid in full container) be reduced, halved. tingiaranira, assoc. tingiaraniria, caus, assoc. tingiaria, caus.; cf. una. tingiarithia, d. caus. tingiarĩka, st. pot. tingiarira, app. tingiarĩria, caus. app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (esp. of liquid) state of being halved
FULL_ENTRY: ũtingiaru [ũtingiyaru], n. cl. 14 (1v), (esp. of liquid) state of being halved; lack, deficiency. [tingiara]
gitingĩ / giting’i, i-, n. (11), pole, fencing-post, pillar; cf. grtugĩ. gatingi, tũ-, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. peg, stick (esp. one used for marking a position); cf. grkĩngi.
(mũ-) (gi-) ting’oe, see (mũ-) (gi-) tong’oe.

ting’oing’a, v.i. (1), walk, move, erratically (usu. in search of sth.), loiter. ting’ating’oing’a, dim. ting’'oing’anga, diff. ting’oing’ania, caus, . rec. ting’oing’anira, assoc. ting’oing’aniria, caus. app. rec. ting’oing’ia, caus. ting’oing’ithia, d. caus. rting’oing’ithia, refl. d. caus. ting’oing’ika, st. pot. ting’oing’ĩkanga, diff. st. pot. ting’oing’ikang’ira, app. diff. st. pot. ting’oing’ira, app. ting’oing’'iria, caus. app.
giting’ũ, i-, n. (vI11?), 1. stub, stump. 2. person lacking a hand.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be firm, strong, sturdy, hardy, brave, dignified. rec. secure each other. ting’ũranĩra, assoc. ting’ũria, caus. fting’ũria, refl. caus, embolden oneself, have confidence
NEW_VALUE: be firm, strong, sturdy, hardy, brave, dignified
FULL_ENTRY: ting ’ũra / ting’ara, v.i. (1), be firm, strong, sturdy, hardy, brave, dignified. rec. secure each other. ting’ũranĩra, assoc. ting’ũria, caus. fting’ũria, refl. caus, embolden oneself, have confidence; be bold, confident. ting’ũrithia, d. caus. ting’ũrika, st, pot.
giting’ũri, see under nding’ũri.

tira!, v.t. (1), clip (trees, &c., when pruning); pick (vegetables, flowers, &c.); ~ gĩthukĩ, split bits off a stump (to make it less dangerous or to obtain firewood); ~ rũgano, cut a story short; ~ nyeki, cut grass with a blunt knife. tiratira, dim. tiratiranga, diff. dim. tiratirithia, d. caus. dim. tiratirĩra, app. dim. tiranga, diff. tiranĩra, app. rec. tiranĩria, caus. app. rec. tirithia, d. caus. tirĩka, st. pot. tirĩra, app. tirirĩra, inten.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: stub, stump of a tree, stock of a tree, plant, &c.
NEW_VALUE: stub, stump of a tree, stock of a tree, plant, &c
FULL_ENTRY: sĩtira, f-, n. (1), stub, stump of a tree, stock of a tree, plant, &c.; -a ~, of old, original stock; nĩ wa ~ rũgongo rũrũ, he belongs to the original residents of this ridge; cf. gĩtirũ, gĩtuĩku.
tiragita, see karagita,.
gatirahũgi, see under ndirahũgi.
tiree, ~, n. (I11), tray, tea tray. [E.]
tiri?, ideo. (1/11), descr. of: 1. poking with sth. pointed. 2. sound of a sharp blow; cf. turu.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of jerky or bumpy movement
FULL_ENTRY: tiri? / tiritiri, ideo. (1/11), descr. of jerky or bumpy movement; gamba ~, bump, move up and down in jerks; see tiratira. [tira?]

gĩtiriho, i-; (N.K.) rũtiriho, ndiriho; n. (111), 1. sewn border, edging, hem, binding; edge bound with bast to give strength. 2. line, rank in dancing; rũtiriho a row of sorghum, [tiriha] v.i. (1),skid due to a push or on slippery, dusty, ground. tirihũkanga, diff. tirihũkana, rec. (used inten.). caus. rec. tirihũkanira, assoc. tirihũkanĩria, caus. assoc. tirihũkia, caus. tirihđkithia, d. caus. tirihũkira, app. tirihũkika, st. pot.

tirika, v.t. (11), (of people, &c.) forsake, abandon, desert; give up in impatience, lose patience with, reject. tirikanga, diff. tirikana, rec. tirikanĩra, app. rec. tirikithia, d. caus. tirikĩka, st. pot. tirikĩra, app.
tirima, see turuma,
ĩtirimia, v.i. (1), walk with aid of stick.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. (of an excited crowd, frightened animals, &c.) move, dash, forward
FULL_ENTRY: tirimũka, v.i. (1), I1. (of an excited crowd, frightened animals, &c.) move, dash, forward; slide downwards; cf. titimũka. 2. (of dancers) move forward in a short, abrupt burst; cf. tirihũka.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of a pointed implement, crow-bar, nib, &c.) be blunt, lose pointedness
FULL_ENTRY: tirimbara / turumbara, v.i. (11), (of a pointed implement, crow-bar, nib, &c.) be blunt, lose pointedness; cf. tuuha. tirimbarana, rec. used inten. tirimbarania, caus. rec. tirimbaranira, assoc. tirimbaraniria, caus. assoc, tirimbaria, caus. tirimbarithia, d. caus. tirimbarĩka, st. pot. tirimbarĩra, app. tirimbarũra, conv. tirimbarũka, neut.
mũtirimbo, see mũtarimbũ.

tiringa, v.t. (11), poke, touch, with sth. blunt; ~ na gĩcinga, poke with a firebrand; cf. ciringa, turunga, turuma. itiringa, refi. tiringanga, diff. tiringana, rec. tiringania, caus. rec. tiringanĩra, app. rec, tiringaniria, caus, app. rec. tiringithia, d. caus. tiringĩka, st. pot. tiringira, app.
mũtiringiiya, see mũcĩrĩngiiya.
ũtiriri [ũtiiriiri], n. cl. 14 only (v11), nonsense, chicanery. tiriri, ~, strife, trouble; onia ~, see under ona; cf, tia.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of a sick, aged, person, &c.) walk slowly, with care, caution, deliberation
FULL_ENTRY: tiritha, v.i. (1), (of a sick, aged, person, &c.) walk slowly, with care, caution, deliberation; move slowly, with difficulty (as when carrying a heavy load). tirithanga, diff. tirithania, caus. rec., tirithanĩra, assoc. tirithaniria, caus. assoc. tirithia, caus, ; ~ mbũri, make the goats plod along. itirithia, refl. d. caus. tirithĩka, st. pot, tirithĩria, caus. app. ftirithĩria, refl. caus. app. ~, n. (v), care, circumspection, slow pace, deliberateness. [tiritha] [gitiirũ], i~, n. (VHI), 1. stump, maimed limb. 2. eaten down butt of sweet-potato leaves hung up in a bundle for the goats; nyitĩrĩria itirũ icio, tighten up that bunch of sweetpotato leaves.
gitite, see ndite.
titia!, see tuta.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: make spots on. rtitia, refl. titithia, d. caus. 1. rub, dip, sth. in powder or pigment to make it adhere. 2. ~ itina thĩ, touch the ground with your buttocks, sit down
NEW_VALUE: rub, dip, in powder or pigment to make it adhere
FULL_ENTRY: titia? ftiitia], v.t. (1) dab on (pigments), make spots on. rtitia, refl. titithia, d. caus. 1. rub, dip, sth. in powder or pigment to make it adhere. 2. ~ itina thĩ, touch the ground with your buttocks, sit down; cf. kiritithia.

titimũka, v.i. (1), (of a herd, &c.) rush madly, stampede; cf. hahũka. titatitimũka, dim. titimũkanga, diff. titimũkana, rec. (used inten.). titimũkania, caus. rec. titimũkanira, assoc., titimũkanĩria, caus. assoc. titimũkia, caus. titimũkithia, d. caus. titimũkĩka, st. pot. titimũkira, app. titimũkĩria, caus. app. titimũkirĩra, inten. titimũkũra, conv, titimũra, v.t. frighten, chase, make stampede, rtitimũra, refil. titatitimũra, dim. titimũranga, diff. titimũrana, rec. titimũrania, caus. rec. titimũranĩra, app. rec. titimũraniria, caus. app. rec. titimũrithia, d. caus. titimũrĩka, st. pot, titimũrĩra, app. titimũrũra, conv. used inten.
gatitimũki, see under nditimũki.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (1), sudden stampede (of a herd of cattle or goats, people, birds)
NEW_VALUE: sudden stampede (of a herd of cattle or goats, people, birds)
FULL_ENTRY: mũtitimũko, (mi-), n. (1), sudden stampede (of a herd of cattle or goats, people, birds); mbũri ya ~, goat given to former near-neighbours by a man who has removed his homestead and then moved back again to the original site. [titimũka]

NEW_VALUE: of stampede, running away in panic
FULL_ENTRY: titimũkũ, ideo. (11), descr. of stampede, running away in panic. [titimũka]
gatitimbũ, see under nditimbũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: r. daub, smear of red ochre, white earth, fat, oil, paint, &c.
NEW_VALUE: daub, smear of red ochre, white earth, fat, oil, paint, &c
FULL_ENTRY: gĩtitio [gitiitio], i-, n. (1), r. daub, smear of red ochre, white earth, fat, oil, paint, &c.; patch of powdered ochre on shoulders or part of neck as part of toilet when preparing for dance; cf. gĩtia. 2. (of blood) spot, patch.
gatitirn [gatitiirũ], tũ-, n. (111), (N.K.) hedgehog; cf. karangatiti, gathangaita.

NEW_VALUE: sound of blow with a stick or club
FULL_ENTRY: tithi 1 ‘tithi-tithi, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1. sound of blow with a stick or club; gambia ~, give sb. a hard whack with a club; cf. tuthi, tuthu, tathi, mu. 2. joining up, completeness; rzgicĩria kĩhaaro ~, make a complete fence round the open space.
mũtithitithi, see mũtothitothi.

mũti, mi-, n. (11), 1. tree. 2. piece of shaft, handle; stick, post; -a ~, wooden; ~ wa mbere ni nũyũ, (in a court case) the first point is this; cuuka mĩtĩ, cast lots; (N. K.) sth. sacred, set apart, forbidden, an oracle; mĩtĩ ya ngingo, necklace worn at Ituĩka ceremonies; ~ wa gũt, conical lump of wood for stretching ear-lobes, cf. kĩrĩngiti; ~ wa Kenya, a sturdy fellow; aakenire (ithanwa) rĩgĩkua ~, he rejoiced exceedingly (so much that the axe-handle broke). 3. (cl. 4) herbs, herbal medicine; mĩtĩ ya thubu, an assortment of herbs put in soup to flavour and strengthen it; rra ta mĩti, taste bitter, be extremely angry. kĩmnũtĩ, i-, n. a huge tree., gĩti, i-, n. stool, chair, seat; ~ kĩa mũrumbo, see ~ kĩa magũrũ, traditional stooi; ~ kĩa nnene, throne; mwene ~, chairman; cf. gĩturua. kamũti, tũmĩti, n. a small tree, stick. gati, tũ-, n. 2nd dim. 1. a tiny piece of stick. 2. a small chair. mati, n. cl. 6. only, wood, brushwood, undergrowth; wild plants (in the mass). rũti, ndi, n. 1. woody plant ( Buddleia salvifolia) (the long withies are used for making grain-stores); cf. 2. rough plank stool used by women as birth-stool.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: esteem, respect, honour, reverence. ftia, refl. think highly of oneself, be proud, presumptuous, conceited, impertinent
FULL_ENTRY: tia v.t. (11), esteem, respect, honour, reverence. ftia, refl. think highly of oneself, be proud, presumptuous, conceited, impertinent; cf. ĩtũgĩria, tĩatia, dim. tianga, diff. rftĩanga, refl. diff. tiana, rec. tĩanira, app. rec. ftianira, refl. app. rec. tĩthia, d, caus. ĩtiithia, refi. d. caus, tĩka, st. pot. tĩkana, rec. st. pot.; tiikaine mũno, a much respected person. tĩra, app. respect, esteem, &c., on account of, for.
gĩtiano [gĩtĩyano], (i-), n. (111), mutual esteem, respect, consideration; cf. gĩtio. [tia]
tĩbĩka, v.i. (1), (N.K.) become settled, decided, fixed. tĩbĩkia, caus. set down carefully.
gatibiri, see ndĩbiri.

NEW_VALUE: (of a moving person or object) come to a stop
FULL_ENTRY: tima [tĩma], v.i. (1) (of a moving person or object) come to a stop; hesitate. timatĩma, dim. timanga, diff. timania, caus. rec. tĩmanira, assoc. tĩmanĩria, caus. assoc, caus. tĩmithia, d, caus. tĩmira, app. pause, come to a halt; hesitate; (in throwing, thrusting) feint; (of sb. making a false start in a race or facing an obstacle) pull oneself up, check. timĩranga, diff. app. tĩmiranira, assoc. app. tĩmiraniria, caus, assoc. app. timiria, caus. app. bring to a sudden stop, check. refl. caus. app. tĩmĩrithiaa, d. caus. app. tĩmirika, st. pot. app. tĩmĩrira, inten. tĩmũka, neut.
mũti-mũirg, mĩti-mĩrũ, n. (11-111), I. forest under-shrub (Galiniera coffeoides); cf, mũgicũ, mhige, 2. shrub (Turraea abyssinica); cf. mũrundu.
mũtimbii, see mũtembei.
itina, ma-, n. (1), a sitting, time of sitting. [tina]
gatingiri, see under ndĩngiri.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: block of tree that has been cut up.
NEW_VALUE: block of tree that has been cut up
FULL_ENTRY: gitĩngiti, i-, n. (X11), block of tree that has been cut up.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be absent, fail to attend sth. according to routine, stay away for a day
FULL_ENTRY: tira [tira], v.t. & i. (1), be absent, fail to attend sth. according to routine, stay away for a day; fail to eventuate, be deterred, cancelled; fail to keep an appointment. tĩratira, dim. tĩratiria, caus. dim. tĩranga, diff. tĩrangia, caus. diff. tĩrania, caus. rec. tiranĩra, assoc. / app. rec. tĩranĩria, caus. assoc. / app. rec. tiria, caus. defer. ĩtĩria, refi. caus. tirithia, d. caus. tĩrĩka, st. pot. tirĩra, app.; ~ njira, cancel going to a place while already on the way. tĩrĩria, caus. app. 1. be irregular in attendance. 2. (of sth. moving fast, e.g. a blow, car, &c.) be very close to striking the object and then stop, tĩrũka, neut. tirĩrira, inten. tĩrirĩria, caus. inten.

tĩri [itĩri], ma-, n. (1), 1. terrain, locality; ~ rranyu nĩ rĩrĩkũ?, what district do you belong to?; coka ~, (used as an oath) do the impossible; Ngai mwene matĩri, Lord of all the earth. 2. place where circumcision takes place attracting a large crowd; ~ rĩria ni rĩa kĩ?, what is that crowd collected for? 3. dancing-ground, meeting-ground for assemblies; (N.K.) communal threshing-floor; ~ itũgiru, (Bibl.) high place (for sacrifice). mũitĩri, a-, n. one who is native to a place; minene ~, chief person in community. ĩtiria [gĩtĩria], i-, n. (1), that which causes people to be absent from sth.; a putting off of some ceremony or appointment; ĩkĩra ~, postpone a matter. [tĩra] nũtiri, n. (1v); mond wa ~, solitary person without children or relatives, person of no importance. wamũtiri, n. nickname for small type of hyena found on the plains; cf. wabii, wahuri.
gatiri, see under ndiri,

tĩrĩtha, v.t. (1), 1. smear, paint all over, cf. haka. 2. tan, dye. ĩtĩrĩtka, refl. tirithanga, diff. tĩrĩthana, rec. tirithania, caus. rec. tirithanira, app. rec. tĩrithanĩria, caus. app. rec. tirĩthĩka, st. pot.
ititi | itita, see ĩteti.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of a matter, dispute, &c.) become settled, concluded, finalized
FULL_ENTRY: titira, v.i. (1), (of a matter, dispute, &c.) become settled, concluded, finalized; be well analysed; cf. gaacĩra. titiranga, diff.; reke ũhoro let the matter be thrashed out a bit further. tĩtĩranĩria, caus. app. rec. titĩria, caus. make sure, ascertain; speak slowly with deliberations. tĩtĩrithia, d. caus. inquire into closely, make quite sure of about, confirm, attest. tĩtĩritkĩria, app. d. caus. assure, certify, to. tĩtĩrĩka, st. pot.; horo nĩwa~, the matter is settled.

tĩthĩba, v.t. & i. (1) I. (N.K.) go into thoroughly, ascertain conclusively; cf. rĩngĩtba, tĩtĩrithia, haarĩria. 2. have enough food, eat enough. tĩthbibanga, diff. tithĩbanĩra, app. rec. tĩthĩbanĩria, caus., app. rec. tithĩbia, caus. tĩthĩbĩka, st. pot.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of a person, tree, or bush) sth. short and stocky, squat
NEW_VALUE: (of a person, tree, or bush) short and stocky, squat
FULL_ENTRY: gitithĩrĩ, i-, n. (1), (of a person, tree, or bush) sth. short and stocky, squat; cf. gatĩthĩ. gatĩthĩrĩ, tũ-, n. clump, tuft.
to, part. (U), gen. interrog.; I. is it not just?; cf. o. 2. written conjunctively with the indep. pron. we and all cl. 3—16; ~guo, see under guo.

toa, v.i. (1), 1. be a daredevil; be violent, dangerous, blood-thirsty, murderous. 2. (N.K.) be greedy, gluttonous. toatoa, dim. toanga, diff. toanira, assoc. toanĩria, caus. assoc. toia, caus. toithia, d. caus. toera, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of liquids being poured out from inverted container) gush out intermittently, come out in jerks.
NEW_VALUE: (of liquids being poured out from inverted container) gush out intermittently, come out in jerks
FULL_ENTRY: tobatoba, v.i. (1), (of liquids being poured out from inverted container) gush out intermittently, come out in jerks. tobatobania, caus. rec. (of rain) come down heavily (as if sb. was pouring it out of a container). tobatobanĩra, assoc. tobatobaniria, caus. assoc, tobatobia, caus. make water jerk out. tobatobithia, d. caus. tobatobeka, st. pot. tobatobera, app. tobatoberia, caus, app.

NEW_VALUE: fll (a narrowmouthed container) by dipping it into liquid
FULL_ENTRY: tobereria, v.t. (1) fll (a narrowmouthed container) by dipping it into liquid. tobatobereria, dim. tobangiriria, diff. tobaniriria, caus. app. rec. tobererithia, d, caus. toberereka, st. pot. toberereria, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of people) enter a river, pool, the sea
NEW_VALUE: (of people) enter a river, pool, the sea
FULL_ENTRY: toboka'’, v.i. (1), 1. (of people) enter a river, pool, the sea; place the feet in water (or any other liquid). 2. (of an initiate) join the other initiates on the morning of the initiation (without previous preparation or participation in the ceremonies). tobatoboka, dim. tobokanga, diff. tobokangia, caus. diff. dip in quickly; immerse some more things in liquid. tobokangira, app. diff. tobokangiria, caus, app. diff. tobokana, rec. fali into water. tobokania, caus. rec. tobokanira, app. rec. tobokaniria, caus. app. rec. tobokia, caus. make go, dip, into water. rtobokia, refl, caus. tobokithia, d. caus. have (stock, &c.) put into a river (when crossing), assist in dipping. tobokithannia, rec. d. caus. tobokithĩria, app. d. caus. tobokeka, st. pot. tobokera, app. enter a river, pool, &c.

tobora, v.t. (1) bore a hole through; puncture (a tyre, football bladder, &c.); ~ nda, open up the stomach of an animal (when slaughtering). toboranga, diff. riddle with bullets; fill sth. with holes. toborana, rec. toborania, caus. rec. let (a hole) go through to the other side. toboranira, app. rec. toboranĩria, caus. app. rec. toborithia, d. caus. toborithĩria, app. d. caus. have holes bored through sth. for sb. toboreka, st. pot. toborera, app. toboka?, v.i. (of a tunnel or other hole being bored) come through. tobatoboka, dim. tobokanga, diff. tobokangia, caus. diff. bore more holes, bore holes through sth. quickly. tobokangira, app. diff. tobokangĩria, caus. app. diff. tobokana, rec. (of a hole, tunnel) go through. tobokania, caus. rec, tobokanĩra, app. rec. tobokaniria, caus. app. rec. tobokia, caus. tobokithia, d. caus. help to bore a hole through. tobokeka, st. pot. tobokera, app. become holed at. tobokeria, caus. app. [Sw. TOBOA]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: descr. of gushing {e.g. of milk when grade cows are being milked)
FULL_ENTRY: tobororo, ideo. (1), descr. of gushing {e.g. of milk when grade cows are being milked); see tobora.

NEW_VALUE: a handing over or ceremonial transfer of responsibility from one generation-set to another
FULL_ENTRY: ituika, (ma-), n. (111), a handing over or ceremonial transfer of responsibility from one generation-set to another; in addition to the annual circumcision or initiatory age-sets, (see marika ma kahiũ) there were generation-sets (also called marika) each covering a period of thirty years or more, during which the duly qualified representatives were in authority and responsible for the conduct of public affairs; the transfer of responsibility involved the contribution of a goat or sheep from every accountable member of the incoming generation towards the redemption of the country from the outgoing set (see kũra bũrũri); this process took many years. The best remembered of these ruling generation-sets, in receding order, are: Mwangi, Maina, Iregi, Ndemi, Mathathi, Cĩra, Cuma. The ‘Maina’ generation handed over to the ‘Mwang? about 1900; the latest, the ‘Trũngũ’ or ‘Maina Kanyi’ set, which is said to have been preparing to succeed the ‘Mwangi’ sometime between 1925-32, was prevented by the social and political changes brought about by europeanizing influences and by prohibition of the ceremonies by Government. [tua1]
