FULL_ENTRY: ituiro, ma-, n. (111), 1. place to which elders retire to consider their verdict on a case. 2. decision, verdict; onia ~, (coll.) punish sb. who deserves it. gĩtuiro, i-, n. 1. aug. of above. 2. pen for hand-fed beast; nja ya ~, stallfed calf; cf. gĩcegũ. [tua!]

tuka, v.i. (11), x1. (of sky) get dark, overcast; gwa~, after sunset, when the night falls. 2. (of people) lose the natural brightness of the skin; ~ ũthiũ, look gloomy; ~ mwĩrĩ, look pale, have goose-flesh. tukatuka, dim. tukatukanga, diff. dim. tukatukĩra, app. dim. tukatukiria, caus. app. dim, tukatukiriria, caus. inten. dim, tukatukũka, conv. dim. tukanga, diff. tukangira, app. diff. tukia, caus. tukithia, d. caus. itukithia, refl. d. caus. tukika, st. pot. tukĩra, app. tukĩria, caus. app. ftukiria, refl. caus. app. tukirĩra, inten. tukĩrĩrno, pass. be overtaken by darkness, be benighted. tukiriria, caus. inten. rtukĩrĩria, refl. caus. inten. delay oneself until it is (late at) night. tukũra, conv., (of people and animals) make or cause to look bright, healthy, clean. tukũka, neut.
matuka!, see nduka,
ituka, ma-?, n. (111), nightfall. [tuka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: become mixed. tukatukana, dim. tukatukania, caus. dim. tukanangia / tukangania, caus. diff. tukania, caus. mix (up together)
FULL_ENTRY: tukana, v.i. (1), become mixed. tukatukana, dim. tukatukania, caus. dim. tukanangia / tukangania, caus. diff. tukania, caus. mix (up together); cf. bococania, thombocania. tukanithia, d. caus. tukanĩka, st. pot. tukanĩra, app. tukaniria, caus. app. ĩtukanĩria, refl. caus. app. tukanĩrĩria, caus. inten.
mũtukanio, mĩ-, n. (1) mixture, compound. [tukania]

NEW_VALUE: of the thud of a log thrown down, of he-goats butting horns together, &c
FULL_ENTRY: tuku, ideo. (1), descr. of the thud of a log thrown down, of he-goats butting horns together, &c.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: smear (oil, red ochre, &c.) roughly (over the skin)
FULL_ENTRY: tukunya / bukunya, v.t. (1), smear (oil, red ochre, &c.) roughly (over the skin); rub (clothes) together when washing; ~ riya, weed with zeal; cf. haka, cthirima. rtukunya, refi. besmear oneself (with fat, perfume, &c.). tukatukunya, dim. tukunyanga, diff. tukunyania, caus. rec, smear haphazardly. tukunyanĩra, app. rec. tukunyaniria, caus. app. rec, tukunyithia, d. caus. ĩtukunyithia, refl. d. caus. tukunyika, st. pot. tukunyira, app. tukunyiria, caus. app. tukunyirĩra, inten.

tukuya, v.t. (1) dig over ground lightly; dig over old sweet-potato patch in search of overlooked potatoes. tukutukuya, dim. tukuyanga, diff. tukuyania, caus. rec. tukuyanĩra, app. rec, tukuyaniria, caus. app. rec. tukuyithia, d. caus. tukuyika, st. pot. tukuyira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: night, night-time, hours of darkness from sunset to dawn
NEW_VALUE: days, periods of day and night from dawn to dawn
FULL_ENTRY: ũtukgũ, motukũ [mootukũ], n. ch 14/6 (111), night, night-time, hours of darkness from sunset to dawn; ~ midnight; ~ mũaũru, mũraya, a bad, long, night; ~ mũnyinyi, a short night, with sound sleep; for names given to early hours of the night see under hwaĩ; for early hours of the morning before dawn see under rũcini. matukũ, n. cl. 6 only, days, periods of day and night from dawn to dawn; ~ maya, nowadays; ~ maingĩ, live long; ~ atatũ, ana, on the third, fourth, day from now, ndukũ!, ~, n. day; cf. ndaaro.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: plant (crops), close together, compactly. tumanĩra, app. rec. tumaniria, caus. app. rec. tumia, caus. I. close lips, keep silent, stop talking
FULL_ENTRY: tuma, v.t. & i. (11) 1. sew, knit, weave; ~ kĩondo, weave a string bag; ~ mũnyoro, make a bead belt; ~ make friends (with); ~ ũthũ, Ũmuku, make an enemy of; ~ ũthoni, (of families) establish relationship through marriage; ~ rĩgu, give sb. food to eat while away from home (on a journey, looking after cattle, &c.). 2. (of blood) be staunched; (of a wound) unite, close up. refl. tumatuma, dim. itumatuma, refl. dim. tumatumana, rec. dim. tumatumania, caus. rec, dim. tumatumithia, d. caus. dim. tumatumira, app. dim. tumatumiria, caus. app. dim. tumatumũra, conv. dim. tumatumũria, caus. conv. dim. tumanga, diff. tumangia, caus. diff. tumangira, app. diff. tumangiria, caus. app. diff. tumangithia, d. caus. diff. tumangirĩra, inten. diff. tumana, rec. 1. (used inten.) (of trees, crops) be planted, grow close together. 2. (of a person) be stupid. 3. become pitch dark. tumania, caus. rec. 1. join (two or more pieces of cloth) by sewing them together. 2. knit, weave, haphazardly. 3. bring together, close (legs). 4. place (objects), plant (crops), close together, compactly. tumanĩra, app. rec. tumaniria, caus. app. rec. tumia, caus. I. close lips, keep silent, stop talking; cf. kira. 2. (of wound) close, stop bleeding. tumithia, d. caus. I1. help to sew, knit, weave. 2. have (suit, dress, shoes, &c.) made (usually to measure). #tumithia, refl. d. caus. tumithangia, diff. d. caus. tumithania, rec. d. caus. tumithaniria, app. rec. d. caus. tumithika, st. pot, d. caus. tumithĩria, app. d. caus. itumithiria, refl. app. d. caus. tumika, st. pot. tumĩra, app. ĩtumira, refl. app. tumirĩra, inten. 1. sew, knit, weave on to. 2. attach, hang, add, on to. rtumĩrĩra, refl. inten. 1. (of people, animals, attach oneself to company one does not belong to, intrude; (of parasitic plant) fasten self upon. 2. (of a person) take upon oneself, assume, undertake; cf. ĩthiga. tumũra, conv. remove stitches; unknit, unweave; open, unwrap (envelope, parcel, &c.); ndangĩ~ kanua, he will not open his mouth. tumũranga, diff. conv. tumũrana, rec. cony. tumũrania, caus. rec. cony. tumũranira, app. rec. conv. tumũraniria, caus. app. rec. conv. tumcũria, caus. conv. tumcgrithia, d. caus. conv. ĩtumnũrithia, refl. d. caus. conv. tumũrika, st, pot. conv. tumũrĩra, app. cony. itumũrĩra, refl. app. conv. tumũririra, inten. conv. tumũka, neut. 1. come unstitched. 2. (of ear of grain) burst through sheath. tumũkana, rec. neut. (used inten.) (of anything sewn up, gunny bag, calabash, &c.) burst open. tumũkania, caus. rec. neut. tumũkanira, app. rec. neut. tumũkira, app. neut.
ituma, ma-, n. (111), I. crowd of people; bunch of animals. 2. a sewing. [tuma]
tumamu, see timamu.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. condition of being newly sewn, woven, made by joining
NEW_VALUE: condition of being newly sewn, woven, made by joining
FULL_ENTRY: mũtumano, n. cl. 3 only (111), 1. condition of being newly sewn, woven, made by joining; nguo cia ~, new clothes. 2. (of crops) condition of being packed, crowded, thick, (of forest) dense. [tuma]
ũtumanu, n. cl. 14 only (iv), 1. ignorance, stupidity; cf. ũrimũ. 2. density, thickness, darkness. [tuma]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (vir), I. sp. of banana (small in size, with specially rich flavour, growing in compact bunches on a v. short tree)
NEW_VALUE: of banana (small in size, with specially rich flavour, growing in compact bunches on a short tree)
FULL_ENTRY: gatumia [gatumiya], (tũ-), n. (vir), I. sp. of banana (small in size, with specially rich flavour, growing in compact bunches on a v. short tree); see irigũ. 2. sp. of weed; cf. mkengeria.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a sewing on to, an addition, sth. extra, added on to, attachment
NEW_VALUE: a sewing on to, an addition, extra, added on to, attachment
FULL_ENTRY: mũtumiriro, mi-, n. (111), a sewing on to, an addition, sth. extra, added on to, attachment; cf. muongerero. [tuma]

rũtumo, ndumo, n. (111), 1. seam; ita na ndumo, (of calabash) leak at the seams (where it has been cracked and sewn up). 2. njira ya ~, highway, through path from one district to another; cf. njira ya agendi. 3. a long ridge; cf. rũgongo. gĩtumo, i-, n. sewing class, sewing. [tuma]
tumu, n. cl. 14 only (1v); ~ wa mũhia, time of year (usu. end of December to early January) when the millet bursts its sheath and harvest is near; see mwaka. gatumu, n. cl. 12 only, as above.
tumuma [tumuuma], v.i. (11), r. be blind; cf. ũra maitho. 2. be obstinate, stupid, unwilling to be convinced. tumumara, stc. be totally blind. tumumaria, caus. stc.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a blind person. gĩtumumu, i-, n. I1. aug. of above. 2. blind end of a ridge where it ends abruptly (as between two converging streams)
FULL_ENTRY: mũtumumu [mũtumuumu], a@-, n. (vu), a blind person. gĩtumumu, i-, n. I1. aug. of above. 2. blind end of a ridge where it ends abruptly (as between two converging streams); cf. gatuĩkĩra. gatumumu, tũ-, n. I1. dim. of above. 2. as gĩtumumu. ũtumumu, motumumu [mootumuumyu], n. blindness. [tumuma]

NEW_VALUE: be pounded fine, be reduced to powder
FULL_ENTRY: tumbetheka [tumbeetheka] / tumbegetheka [tumbeegetheka], v.i. (11), be pounded fine, be reduced to powder,

NEW_VALUE: a short, twisted plait or ringlet of hair, sometimes anointed with red ochre paint and fat
FULL_ENTRY: itumbi?, ma-, n. (1); ~ rĩa njuĩrĩ, a short, twisted plait or ringlet of hair, sometimes anointed with red ochre paint and fat.
tumbunyĩka, see under tubunya.

tumbutha, v.t. (11), 1. break up into fragments; cf. umbutha. 2. scold, upbraid. 3, bustle. tumbuthanga, diff. tumbuthana, rec. tumbuthania, caus. rec. tumbuthia, d, caus. tumbuthika, st. pot. tumbuthũra, conv. tumbuthũka / timbi
thũka, neut, 1. (of sb. hearing news

w TA and rushing off without waiting for details) rush impetuously. 2. (of loose earth, sand, water, herd of goats) run, rush; roll down; come, fall, away in bits; powder. tumbuthũkira, app. neut. (of earth, sand, flood, felled trees, &c.) roll down on to, rush down upon, overwhelm.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. cl. 14/6 (x1), dry sediment of soil, &c., fine shavings, scrapings from sticks or pieces of wood, sawdust
FULL_ENTRY: tumbuthi, (ma-), n. cl. 14/6 (x1), dry sediment of soil, &c., fine shavings, scrapings from sticks or pieces of wood, sawdust; see uumbuthi.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: finish off, give tke coup de grace to (a seriously ill, wounded, creature, &c.), cause to die, administer euthanasia. ĩftumbũra, refl. diff. tumbũrana, rec, tumbũrania, caus. rec. tumbũranĩra, app. rec., tumbũrithia, d. caus. ĩ#tumbũrithia, refi, d. caus. tumbũrika, st. pot. tumbũrĩra, app. rtumbũrĩra, refl. app. tumbũka, v.i. die, give up the ghost, pass away
NEW_VALUE: finish off, give tke coup de grace to (a seriously ill, wounded, creature, &c
FULL_ENTRY: tumbũra, v.t. (11), finish off, give tke coup de grace to (a seriously ill, wounded, creature, &c.), cause to die, administer euthanasia. ĩftumbũra, refl. diff. tumbũrana, rec, tumbũrania, caus. rec. tumbũranĩra, app. rec., tumbũrithia, d. caus. ĩ#tumbũrithia, refi, d. caus. tumbũrika, st. pot. tumbũrĩra, app. rtumbũrĩra, refl. app. tumbũka, v.i. die, give up the ghost, pass away; cf. kua. tumbũkana, rec. tumbũkania, caus. rec. tumbũkanĩra, app. rec. tumbũkanĩria, caus. app. rec. tumbũkithia, d. caus. tumbũkika, st. pot. tumbũkĩra, app.
situne, see under ndune. [ũtuune], motune [mootuune], n. (111), 1. redness, red colour. 2. menstrual period.
tune [-tuune], a. (111), 1. reddish, warm-coloured, red. 2. menstruous; e mũ~, she is having her sickness; cf, mũgongo, mweri. -tunatune, a, darker red, redder; inclined to red, warm, colour.

uniha [tuunĩha], v.i. (11), be, become, red. tunatunĩha, dim. tunatunihia, caus. dim, tunĩhanga, diff. tunihangia, caus. diff. tunĩhania, rec, tunĩhanira, app. rec. tunĩhanĩria, caus. app. rec. tunĩhia, caus. make red. ĩtunĩhia, refl. caus. tunĩhithia, d. caus. ftunĩhithia, refl. d. caus. tunĩkika, st. pot., tunĩhĩra, app. tunĩhiria, caus. app. Ftunihiria, refl. caus. app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. a narrow strip of land dug up (usu. one of many). 2. strip, weal, elongated swelling or mark on a human or animal skin (resulting from a whipping or caning)
NEW_VALUE: a narrow strip of land dug up (usu
FULL_ENTRY: mũtunda, n. (IV), I. a narrow strip of land dug up (usu. one of many). 2. strip, weal, elongated swelling or mark on a human or animal skin (resulting from a whipping or caning); cf. mũtuuru. 3. (of millet, coffee, trees, &c.) row, line. 4. variety, kind; cf. mũthemba. 5. line of argument. kamũtunda, tũmĩtunda, n. a small strip.
gaturndu, see under ndundu.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: i-, n. 1. squatness, bushiness of plant or tree
NEW_VALUE: squatness, bushiness of plant or tree
FULL_ENTRY: gitunduguru (vi) / gĩtunduru (x11), i-, n. 1. squatness, bushiness of plant or tree; clump of trees or bush. 2. tendency to throw out lateral branches; umbrella-like tree or bush; cf. gĩtithĩri. 3. thick-headed person.
tunduma, see tindima.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: make sth. by bending, curving, connecting two ends
NEW_VALUE: make by bending, curving, connecting two ends
FULL_ENTRY: tunga, v.t. (1), make sth. by bending, curving, connecting two ends; fasten, tie; form, organize (a club, company, political parties, &c.); ~ ta, string a bow; ~ mbara, make a (stick) hoop; ~ gĩthiũrũri, (of people) make a circle; ~ rũgano, create, tell, write, a story; ~ rimbo, compose a song; cf. thondeka. tunga, refl. stoop, bend. tungatunga, dim. tunganga, diff. tungania, caus. rec. tunganira, app. rec. tungithia, d. caus. tungika, st. pot. tungira, app. tungra, conv. let loose, unstring (a bow, string, snare, &c.). tungũka, neut. become loose, unstrung.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: ring (of twisted sticks, &c.), circlet (for neck or head), wreath.
FULL_ENTRY: itunga, ma-, n. (1), ring (of twisted sticks, &c.), circlet (for neck or head), wreath. [tunga?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: r. be in work, perform service
FULL_ENTRY: tungata, v.i. & t. (11), r. be in work, perform service; labour for, serve (sb., an establishment) (without reserving energy in excess of expected or proised reward). 2. tend (livestock). tungatungata, dim. tungatanga, diff. tungatana, rec. tungatanira, app. rec. tungatithia, d. caus. tungatika, st. pot. tungatĩra, app. serve, work for; minister to.

NEW_VALUE: (Presbyterian usage) ordained minister. ngatĩri, motungatĩri [mootungatĩril, n. priesthood, guardianship
FULL_ENTRY: mũtungatiri, a-, n. (111), (Presbyterian usage) ordained minister. ngatĩri, motungatĩri [mootungatĩril, n. priesthood, guardianship. [tungata]

NEW_VALUE: large billet, log of wood, stout log for the fire
FULL_ENTRY: -tungu, a. (111), (of things and people) fat, plump; thick, stout, big, brawny; heho ndungu, intense cold; mbura ndungu, heavy rain, see kĩboboto., ndungu, n. (1m1), large billet, log of wood, stout log for the fire. motungu [mootungu], n. stoutness, girth.
gatung’ang’a, see ndung’ang’a.
matunya [matuunya], n. cl. 6 (vur?), violent anger, rage.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) linger, put off time. tunyeria, caus. delay (sth.), cause to linger. tunyerera, inten. linger in, at. tunyereria, caus. inten, delay
FULL_ENTRY: tunyera f[tunyeera], v.i. (1), (N.K.) linger, put off time. tunyeria, caus. delay (sth.), cause to linger. tunyerera, inten. linger in, at. tunyereria, caus. inten, delay; cause to linger, loiter.
rũtunyo [rũtuunyo], ndunyo, n. (111), robbery. [tunya]
mũtuo, a-, n. (1v), one named after sth. or sb. [tua1]
tuoko, see under guoko.

tura!, v.t. (11), forge, work, hammer (metal, &c.); work as a smith, work iron; ~ icembe, manufacture a hoe; ~ mũtokaa, manufacture a car; cf, thondeka. turatura, dim. turaturanga, diff. dim. turaturania, caus. rec. dim, turanga, diff. turana, rec, turania, caus, rec. turanira, app. rec. turaniria, caus. app. rec. turithia, d. caus. have made, manufactured. ĩturithia, refi. d. caus. turĩka, st. pot. turĩra, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of a path, &c.) come to a dead end
FULL_ENTRY: tura?, v.i. (11), (of a path, &c.) come to a dead end; come to an impasse; (of a forest stretch) come to an end; nĩwa ~, the matter has become a deadlock; roimbo nĩrna ~, (spoken in despair after some effort) the dance (song) is just impossible. rtura, refl. sit, stay apart; keep to oneself; cf. ctũmba. turatura, dim. turanga, dif. turana, assoc. turania, caus. assoc.; ~ na, come against, run into each other, encounter, make contact with, meet. turanira, app. rec. mix up together, combine; (in arithmetic) add up. rturanira, refl. app. rec. make common cause with. turanĩrira, inten. app. rec. turaniriria, caus. inten. app. rec. turia, caus.; ~ maru, ndu, kneel, kneel down; ~ make an appearance at a place. turĩra, app. turĩria, caus. app.; ~ ndu, kneel down before. iturĩria, refi. caus. app. tuririria, caus. inten. come upon unexpectedly; cf. korerera.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: to no purpose, without result, fruitlessly.
NEW_VALUE: to no purpose, without result, fruitlessly
FULL_ENTRY: tura, ideo. (U), to no purpose, without result, fruitlessly. [see tura?]

mũtura, mĩ-, n. (1v), 1. lower bowel, large intestine; ~ wa ihĩ, caecum; ~ wa kagĩri, kĩboroboro, gĩtungati, (of cow) colon, rectum. 2. sausage made of pieces of large intestine, stuffed with meat, fat, and blood; black pudding; cf. ndũndiro. itura, ma-, 1. small piece of goat’s rectum (used in doctoring livestock). 2. glutton.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of women) sit of pose carelessly, exhibit private parts
FULL_ENTRY: turama, v.i. (1), (of women) sit of pose carelessly, exhibit private parts; cf. tengũka. turamania, caus. rec. I1. stand (a cooking-pot, &c.) upside down. 2. knock (a woman) down, roughly (with intent to expose her private parts). turamĩra, app. expose private parts (esp. to an arrogant man); (said by a gravely wronged woman as a threat or curse) I’ll expose my buttocks to you!

ORIGINAL_VALUE: collection, assemblage of different things, conglomeration.
NEW_VALUE: collection, assemblage of different things, conglomeration
FULL_ENTRY: gĩturaniro, i-, n. (r1), collection, assemblage of different things, conglomeration. [tura?]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a perennial shrub (growing to about 8 ft.), cf. mũcatha, iturangũrũ, ma-, n. leaf of this tree (soft like flannel
NEW_VALUE: a perennial shrub (growing to about 8 ft
FULL_ENTRY: mũturangũrũ, mi-, n. (v), a perennial shrub (growing to about 8 ft.), cf. mũcatha, iturangũrũ, ma-, n. leaf of this tree (soft like flannel; bunches carried by inner circle of elders as symbol of office; see krama).
mũturi, a&-, n. (11), blacksmith. Ũturi, n. cl. 14, smithing, the blacksmith’s art. [tura!]
mũturiri, mĩ-, n. (111), (N.K.) Sodomapple bush (Solanum); cf. motongu?. nduriri, ~, n. Sodom apple. [cf. Ma. EN-DULELE]
gĩturiririo, i-, n. (111), obstacle, limit. [tura?]

NEW_VALUE: thick iron bracelet worn on upper arm
FULL_ENTRY: gĩturo, i-, n. (111), 1. thick iron bracelet worn on upper arm. 2. bracelet made from thick wire worn only by men. gaturo, tũ-, n. dim. of above. [see tura]
turu / tiri, ideo. (1), descr. of: x. striking. 2. poking, pushing with the end of sth. [see tura!]
gaturu, see nduru!.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: rub dry ochte all over oneself in patterns.
NEW_VALUE: rub dry ochte all over oneself in patterns
FULL_ENTRY: turuba, v.t. (1), rub dry ochte all over oneself in patterns. turubia, caus. turubũka, neut.
iturubari, see itubari.
turuhũka, see tirihũka.
gaturuku, see under nduruku,

NEW_VALUE: thrashing, punishment, meted out to scoundrels
FULL_ENTRY: ituruku, ma-, n. (111), 1. mark from blow, bruise; cf. ¿turutu. 2. (cl. 6 only) thrashing, punishment, meted out to scoundrels.

turuma / tirima, v.t. (1), 1. bump into, prod, poke; place in contact with, touch with pointed object. 2. touch (sth. liquid) with the lips, sip. turaturuma, dim. turumanga, diff. turumangana, refl. diff. turumanganĩra, app. rec. diff, turumana, rec. come in contact, collide; ~ na, meet with. turumania, caus. rec. stand (a pot) upside down; cf. kurumania. turumanĩra, app. rec. turumithia, d. caus. place, press the end of sth. in contact with, put against; ~ thi, touch the ground with the forehead. turumithania, rec. d. caus. turumĩka, st. pot. turumira, app.
gaturumiri, tũ-; ndurumiri, ~; n. (1), sp. of mole-rat; see huko.
tururnbara, see tirimbara.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. shoot with blocked arrow. 2. poke, rap, tap, beat (repeatedly) (a bag, calabash, &c., to remove remains of dust, flour, food, &c., clinging to it)
FULL_ENTRY: turunga / tiringa?, v.t. (11), I1. shoot with blocked arrow. 2. poke, rap, tap, beat (repeatedly) (a bag, calabash, &c., to remove remains of dust, flour, food, &c., clinging to it); cf. komonga. turunganga, diff. turungangira, app. diff. turungangirira, inten. diff. turungana, rec. turungania, caus., rec, turunganira, app. rec. turunganiria, caus. app. rec., turungithia, d. caus. rturungithia, refl. d. caus. turungika, st. pot. turungĩra, app. rĩturungira, refl. app. turungirĩra, inten. ~, n. (11), brown calico; nguo ya ~, woman’s dress made from above material; cf. thiturungi.
turungi?, tma-, n. cl. 14/6 (11) tea without milk.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be dazed, wander blindly, grope about like a blind person. turaturura, dim. tururanga, diff. tururana, rec. (used inten.). tururania, caus, rec. tururanira, app. rec. tururaniria, caus. app. rec. tururia, caus. ftururia, refl. caus. tururithia, d. caus. ĩtururithia, refl. d. caus. tururika, st. pot. tururikia, caus. st. pot. tururira, app. tururĩria, caus. app. tururũka, neut.
NEW_VALUE: be dazed, wander blindly, grope about like a blind person
FULL_ENTRY: turura [turuura], v.i. (11), be dazed, wander blindly, grope about like a blind person. turaturura, dim. tururanga, diff. tururana, rec. (used inten.). tururania, caus, rec. tururanira, app. rec. tururaniria, caus. app. rec. tururia, caus. ftururia, refl. caus. tururithia, d. caus. ĩtururithia, refl. d. caus. tururika, st. pot. tururikia, caus. st. pot. tururira, app. tururĩria, caus. app. tururũka, neut.; ~ ngoro, be confused, perplexed.
gatururu', see under ndururu',

NEW_VALUE: flashes of indistinguishable objects in the dark
FULL_ENTRY: iturutu [ituurutu], ma-, n. (111), 1. stroke, bruise, contusion; flash of blinding pain; onia ~, beat sb. 2. sudden calamity; ~ rĩa Ngai, plague, stroke. 3. (cl. 6) flashes of indistinguishable objects in the dark; state of near darkness when one can see only the shape of objects.
turutu, n. cl. o violence, blows; kora ~, get bruised.
turuturu, ideo. (1), descr. of muffied noise. turu-turu, ideo. descr. of ineffectual hacking with a blunt knife.
ituruturu [ituurutuuru], ma-, n. (v), 1. stroke, black-out. 2. dizziness, giddiness; onia maturuturu, make sb. see stars; cf. iturutu.
mũturũ [mũtuurũ], mi-, n. (11); ~ wa maĩ, water-furrow, mill-lade.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of persons) nod, fall forward with sleep
FULL_ENTRY: turũka, v.i. (11), (of persons) nod, fall forward with sleep; turn somersault; (of things, chairs, &c.) topple over, fali backwards or sideways. turatudim. turaturũkia, caus. dim. turũkanga, diff. turakana, rec, (of motor-car, &c.) overturn, turn right over, topple over. turũkania, caus. rec, turũkanĩra, assoc. turũkaniria, caus. assoc. turũkanika, st. pot. assoc. turũkia, caus. disappear, at a distance, above or beyond a ridge or hill. turũkithia, d. caus. rturũkithia, refl. d. caus. turgkika, st. pot. app. turũkĩria, caus. app. turũkĩrĩra, inten, disappear down a steep slope, into a deep valley, among hills. caus. inten. turũra, v.t. 1. make (sth.) topple over; make (sb.) turn a somersault, dump a load (e.g. from a wheelbarrow). 2. shave (sb.’s) head completely. turaturũra, dim. turũranga, diff, turũrana, rec. turũrania, caus. rec, (used inten.) topple (sth. things) over. turũranira, app. rec. turũraniria, caus. app. rec. turũrithia, d. caus. turũrĩka, st. pot. turũrĩra, app. tip in. rturũrira, refl. app.; (N.K.) ~ na maithori, start to weep; ~ na mĩtheko, roll about with laughter. turũrũka, neut. get turned over.

tuta [tuuta], v.i. (1) x. (of person, animal, &c.) prance with rage, be truculent. 2. (of millet, maize, &c.) turn yellow in colour as a sign of ripeness; (of a half-healed wound) become dry, granulated, powdery. rtuta, refl.; ~ na, rub oneself against. tutatuta, dim. tutanga, diff. tutania, caus. rec. tutanira, assoc., tutaniria, caus. assoc, tutia, caus. r. rub hands in dust to improve grip on implement. 2. feint at (to cause amusement). 3. (N.K.) dance up and down. tutithia, caus. ftutithia, refl. d. caus. tutĩka, st. pot. app. tutũra, conv. wear, make (ground, grass, an animall’s coat, &c.) bare. tutũka, neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: r. bare, sandy, dusty, place which has been worn by people dancing or children playing. 2. pitch where ox digs with horns or donkey rolls
NEW_VALUE: bare, sandy, dusty, place which has been worn by people dancing or children playing
FULL_ENTRY: gituto [gĩtuuto], n. (1), r. bare, sandy, dusty, place which has been worn by people dancing or children playing. 2. pitch where ox digs with horns or donkey rolls; cf. gĩtuumo, 3. yellow soap stone; smear of dry red-ochre, chalk, yellow-earth, &c.; taama wa ~, cloth dyed by rubbing in red-ochre. [tuta]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: crowd, mass, bunch, clump, group, large collection
NEW_VALUE: a grey kind of sugar-cane which grows fast
FULL_ENTRY: tutu, n. (1); kĩgwa gĩa ~, a grey kind of sugar-cane which grows fast; cf. bobo. i-, n. (1v), crowd, mass, bunch, clump, group, large collection; ~ kia mboco, a lot of beans; marũngĩ itutu, people are standing in groups. gatutu, tũ-, n. dim. of above.
gĩtutu?, see under ndutu.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. any kind of powder, sawdust, dry dusty soil, crumbled salt. 2. small crumbs of food. ratutu, n. cl. 11 only, 1. fine earth, powder, granular substance, e.g. sawdust
NEW_VALUE: fine earth, powder, granular substance, e
FULL_ENTRY: mũtutu [mũtuutu], mi-, n. (1), 1. any kind of powder, sawdust, dry dusty soil, crumbled salt. 2. small crumbs of food. ratutu, n. cl. 11 only, 1. fine earth, powder, granular substance, e.g. sawdust; cf. rcitĩki. 2. small crumbs from food, bread; cf. matu.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: that which has turned reddish-white or yellow as it ripens.
NEW_VALUE: that which has turned reddish-white or yellow as it ripens
FULL_ENTRY: -tutu [-tuutu], a. (1v), that which has turned reddish-white or yellow as it ripens. [tuta]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. converge, 2. eat large lumps of food quickly and greedily. tutungangiria, caus. app. diff. tutunganĩra, assoc, tutunganiria, caus. assoc. tutungĩria, caus. app. (of clouds) gather, collect
FULL_ENTRY: (tutunga) / (N.K.) tutuma, v.i. & t. (11), 1. converge, 2. eat large lumps of food quickly and greedily. tutungangiria, caus. app. diff. tutunganĩra, assoc, tutunganiria, caus. assoc. tutungĩria, caus. app. (of clouds) gather, collect; (of rain) be about to fall; (of people) gather in group(s) from various directions. tutungirithia, d. caus. app. rĩtutungirithia, refl. d. caus. app. tutungirira, inten.
gitutunga, see under ndutunga,
tutura, v.t. (I1), 1. force a way through bush. 2. hit.

tutha, v.t. (11) break; burst; break (rope, tether, &c.) by pulling apart; ~ mũrango, force a door open; ~ (mondũ) ngundi, give (sb.) a heavy blow with the fist. tuthatutha, dim. tuthanga, diff. tuthana, rec. tuthania, caus. rec. tuthanĩra, app. rec. tuthanĩria, caus. app. rec. tuthithia, d. caus. tuthĩka, st. pot. tuthĩra, app. tuthũra, conv. tuthũka, neut.

NEW_VALUE: the sound of a blow with a stick on an animal or human being
FULL_ENTRY: tuthi / tuthu, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1. the sound of a blow with a stick on an animal or human being. 2. the snapping of a rope or string; cf. tithi, tathi. tuthu-tuthu, ideo. descr. of a succession of blows. [tutha]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: clump of trees, tuft of grass, bamboos, &c.
NEW_VALUE: clump of trees, tuft of grass, bamboos, &c
FULL_ENTRY: gĩtuthu, i-, n. (1), clump of trees, tuft of grass, bamboos, &c.; cf.
mũtuthũko, see mũthnundũko.

tuuba, v.t. (1), file, sharpen with a file. tuubatuuba, dim. tuubanga, diff. tuubania, caus. rec. tuubanira, app. rec. tuubaniria, caus. app. rec. tuubithia, d. caus. tuubika, st. pot. tuubira, app. tuubirira, inten.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of cutting or pointed instruments, teeth, &c.) be blunt. tuuhatuuha, dim. tuuhanga, diff. tuuhania, caus. rec, tuuhanira, app. rec, tuuhaniria, caus. app. rec, tuuhia, caus., refl. caus. tuuhithia, d. caus. tuuhika, st. pot. tuuhĩra, app. tuuhiria, caus. app. tuuhũra, conv.
FULL_ENTRY: tuuha, v.i. (11), (of cutting or pointed instruments, teeth, &c.) be blunt. tuuhatuuha, dim. tuuhanga, diff. tuuhania, caus. rec, tuuhanira, app. rec, tuuhaniria, caus. app. rec, tuuhia, caus., refl. caus. tuuhithia, d. caus. tuuhika, st. pot. tuuhĩra, app. tuuhiria, caus. app. tuuhũra, conv.; ~ horo, explain a matter, make plain; blurt out, let the ‘cat out of the bag’. tuuhũka, neut. be frank.
-tuuhu, a. (1v), (of knife, &c.) blunt.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: x1. (of prospective initiates) dance the ceremonial dance on the day prior to circumcision
NEW_VALUE: (of prospective initiates) dance the ceremonial dance on the day prior to circumcision
FULL_ENTRY: tuuma, v.i. (1), x1. (of prospective initiates) dance the ceremonial dance on the day prior to circumcision; cf. (N.K.) hĩra. 2. trample ground or grass so that it looks worn. 3. (of heart pulse) throb, beat heavily; (of blood), pulsate; (of male sex organ) tumesce; (of kites) weave about, circle round. Hũngũ i~ga ha muoni, hawks haunt the home of the one who has livestock., tuumatuuma, dim. tuumanga, diff. tuumanira, app. rec. tuumaniria, caus. app. rec. tuumia, caus. tuumithia, d. caus. tuumira, app. tuumĩka, st. pot. tuumirira, inten. conv. trample sth. so that it looks worn or dusty. tuumũranga, diff. conv. tuumũrania, caus. rec. conv. tuumnũranira, app. rec. conv. tuumũraniria, caus. app. rec, cony. tuumgũrithia, d. caus. conv. tuumũrĩka, st. pot. conv. tuumũka, neut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. a girls game or dance
FULL_ENTRY: ituuma, ma-, n. (1), I. a girls game or dance; cf. nduumo. 2. palpitation. [tuuma]
gituumo, see under nduumo.
mũtuura, mi-, n. (1v) weal; cf. mũtunda.
ituuru, ma-; mũtuuru, mi-; n, (Iv), (N.K.) weal; cf, moatuura, motunda.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. a very large tree (Myrianthus arboreus). 2. a creeper. ituya, ma-
FULL_ENTRY: mũtuya [mũtuuya], mi-, n. (111), 1. a very large tree (Myrianthus arboreus). 2. a creeper. ituya, ma-; nduya, ~; n. fruit of 1. above (resembling a large mulberry).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. n., a., p.c., & p. subj. prefix, cl. 13. 2. when duplicated this prefix forms the ‘near’ dem. a. & p., cl. 13
FULL_ENTRY: tũ-!, conc. prefix (U), 1. n., a., p.c., & p. subj. prefix, cl. 13. 2. when duplicated this prefix forms the ‘near’ dem. a. & p., cl. 13; see also tou, under -u, tũria under -rĩa, twa under -a, tuo, under -o.
tũ-2, subj. pron. (U), 1st pers. pl., we; see ithui.
-tn-, obj. infix. (U), p. ref. to n. cl. 13.

gũtũ, ma-, n. (1), 1. ear; ear-lobe; aga mat, be disobedient; aga gũtuĩka ~, (of a herd of goats, &c.) be complete; nyũmba yao ti nduĩku ~, their household has had no death in it; inia ~, see under ina; kunya ~, see under kunya; wa ~, see under mũti; mbũri cia mat, see under mbũri; ndari matũ, he pays no heed; ũri ~?, have you taken in what I say?; nyitĩra ~, see under nyita; rũgia matũ, see under rũga; tega see under tega; temwo nĩ ~ kwa mwana, see under tema; ũra mat, become heedless, disregard advice deliberately; cf. mbuguiro. 2. lug; handle of earthenware pot, jar, or cup; barb of triangular arrow-head ; cotyledon of bean plant. tũ-, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. Ũr ~, a well-known person, one in the public eye.
gitũbũnyiko, see gĩtubunyiko.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: mound, bank, rise in the ground, hillock.
NEW_VALUE: mound, bank, rise in the ground, hillock
FULL_ENTRY: gitũbũrũrũ, i-, n. (v), mound, bank, rise in the ground, hillock.

NEW_VALUE: (of ears) stick out, stand up perpendicularly
FULL_ENTRY: tũga, v.i. (1) x. (of ears) stick out, stand up perpendicularly. 2. (of livestock, people, &c.) move restlessly from place to place. 3. (of people) wander in search of sth. or sb. lost, search all over for a runaway person or animal. tũgatũga, dim. tũgatngia, caus. dim. tagatũgĩra, app. dim. caus. app. dim. tũganga, diff. tngangia, caus. diff. tngania, caus. rec. cause people to wander all over in search of sb., sth. lost. tnganĩra, assoc. tũnganiria, caus. assoc, tOgia, caus. (of a frightened animal, &c.) lift up the ears, be on the alert. 2, cause people to hustle; be the cause of people wandering and being worried in their search. tũgithia, d. caus. tũgithania, rec. d. caus.; ~ na, run after a runaway animal. tũgithĩria, app. d. caus. tngika, st. pot. tngĩra, app. wander in search of, at, for; be quick, make haste, get a move on, at, for. tũgirira, inten. tũngarara, inten. stc. r. stick up; rise up; 2. (coll.) walk, make a journey; walk briskly. tũgararanga, diff, inten. stc. tũgararania, caus. rec. inten. stc. tũgararanĩra, assoc. inten. stc. tũgararaniria, caus. assoc. inten. stc. tũngararia, caus. inten. stc. raise up. tũgararithia, d. caus. inten. stc. tagararika, st. pot. inten, stc. tũgararĩra, app. inten. stc. tnũgarariria, caus. app. inten. stc. tagararũka, inten. stc. neut, (of sb. who has been raising himself up) resume normal position, come down.
ũtũgi / ũtũgĩku motngi [mootũgi] / [mootũgĩku], n. (1v), a raised edge. [see tũgira]

tũgĩra [tũgĩra], v.i. (11), (of a place) be raised up; (of a person) be uplifted in stature or status. tũgatũgira, dim. tũgatũgiria, caus. dim. tũgiranga, diff. tũgĩrania, caus. rec, 1. uplift, speak well of, sb. 2. give sb. higher status. tnganĩra, assoc. tnganiria, caus. assoc. lift (things) up at the same time. tũgĩria, caus. 1. give sb. higher status, raise up, make a platform; 2. (of place, distance, &c.) be far away, be a long distance off. itũgiria, refi. caus. give oneself higher status, put oneself into leadership. tũagirithia, d. caus. tũgirĩka, st. pot. tũgĩrĩra, app. rĩtũgirĩra, refi. app. tũgĩrĩria, caus. app. itũgirĩria, refl. caus. app. put oneself into higher status, at a place, in front of people. [-tũnũgiru], a. (1v), elevated, raised up. [tũgĩra]

tũgũga [tũgũga], v.i. (11), 1. stagger, reel about, go wanderingly; cf. tororeka, tururĩka. 2. (in speech) wander away from the facts, be garrulous and irrelevant. tũgatũdim. tũgatũgũgia, caus. dim. diff. tngũgangia, caus. dim. tũgũgania, caus. rec. stagger others, one another; tell irrelevant stories, tease. tcgũganira, assoc, tOũgũganiria, caus. assoc. tngũgia, caus. stagger sb., cause to stagger; beat about the bush in telling a story. refl. caus. make oneself stagger; wander about of one’s own accord. tũgũgithia, d. caus. itũgũgithia, refl. d. caus. pretend to be staggering, allow oneself to be staggered. tũngũgika, st. pot. be capable of being staggered; wander away from home, roam away because of mental sickness. diff. st. pot. tũgũgikania, caus. rec. st. pot. tngũgĩkanira, assoc. st. pot. tagũgikaniria, caus. assoc. st. pot. tagũgikia, caus. st. pot. cause sb. to be a wanderer from his home. refl. caus. st. pot. tũgũgikira, app. st. pot. tũgũgikiria, caus, st. pot. tngũgira, app. stagger, at, upon. tũgũgĩria, caus. app. cause sb. to stagger at a place, for sb. itũgũgiria, refl. caus. app. pretend to be tottering, staggering.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: sth. that has collapsed, a prostrate corpse. n. (1v), 1. (freq. used as adv.) (to) no purpose, uselessly, without cause, in vain
FULL_ENTRY: mũtũgũtũgũ, mi-, n. (v), sth. that has collapsed, a prostrate corpse. n. (1v), 1. (freq. used as adv.) (to) no purpose, uselessly, without cause, in vain; horo wa ~, a futile affair; wa ~, a useless person; cf. mana, becũ, tthaũ, buri. 2. freely, for nothing; nĩndaheo marigũ ~, I have been given bananas without payment.
mũtũhũ a-, n. (vir), person of no account.

mũtũnkia mĩ-; ndũkia [ndũkia]; ~; n. (1v), I1. a form of yaws. 2. a local and limited infection of syphilis. gatũkia, tũ-, n. 1. dim. of above. 2. a creeping plant from the leaves of which a paste is made to cure the sores of yaws and syphilis.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: inherited likeness, family likeness, inherited qualities. ~ gatithiragĩra hene, inherited qualities do not disappear merely because one is living with other unrelated people. Cf. gatũkũrũ, gataũrũ. [tũkia] tũ-, n. (1), 1. origin, breed, descent
FULL_ENTRY: gatũko, n. (1), inherited likeness, family likeness, inherited qualities. ~ gatithiragĩra hene, inherited qualities do not disappear merely because one is living with other unrelated people. Cf. gatũkũrũ, gataũrũ. [tũkia] tũ-, n. (1), 1. origin, breed, descent; nina ũhoro ~, finish up a matter entirely; ngwacũ ĩno ti ya Gĩkũyũ ~, this type of sweet potato is not of Kikuyu origin; kĩkumo ~, his original descent; cf. gathirange, gatnũrũ. 2. case history, detailed account; law of legal procedure,

tũkũya, v.i. (1) be stiff, rigid, with anger; be very angry. tnũkatũkũya, dim. tũkũyanga, diff. rec. swell, become swollen with anger. tũkũyania, caus. rec, tũakũyanira, assoc. tũkũnyanĩria, caus. assoc. tnkũia, caus. d. caus. tũkũĩka, st. pot. tũakũĩra, app.

tma, v.t. (11), 1. send; ~ kaana, send a child to convey information to sb.; cf. rekia. 2. (foll. by v. in subjunctive) cause; nĩaa~ ngire, he caused me to be silent. ĩtama, refi. (esp. of children) do sth. on one’s own initiative and bear the consequences. taũmatũma, dim. diff. tũmangithia, d. caus. diff. tnũmangĩra, app. diff. tamana, rec. send sb. somewhere, send a messenger, messengers; ask people to go and help on a certain job of work. tũmanira, app. rec. send a messenger to sb. or to some persons; send (sb., sth.) to sb., people; send to one another; ask people to help sb. else to do a piece of work. tanmanĩria, caus. app. rec. send together. tũmithia, d. caus. have sb., sth., sent. tamithĩria, app. d. caus. send, for, to. tamika, st. pot. tũmira!, app. send on behalf of sb. else. rtũmĩra, refl. app. inten. send additional things; ~ marna, send a reminder. rtũmĩrĩra, refi. inten. send sb. else in one’s place. tũmirĩria, caus. inten. send on to, forward by a second or third hand.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: r. stem of edible arum plant. 2. rifle, shot-gun
FULL_ENTRY: mũtũma? mi-, n. (V11), r. stem of edible arum plant. 2. rifle, shot-gun; cf. mocinga. ndũma, ~, n. edible arum (Colocasia) taro, cocoyam, arrowroot; ~ ya mwanake, sp. of edible arum with dark green leaves; cf. thangari, ndir, mweũ wa ragai. itũma, ma-, leaf of this plant (used to cover cooking-pots containing yams, also as a cup); cf. irũtũ, gĩtemani.
mũtnũmiki, a-, n. (111), (R.C. usage) acolyte. [tũma]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. use, spend money. 2. be accustomed to
FULL_ENTRY: tũmira?, v.t. (I1), 1. use, spend money. 2. be accustomed to; cf. hũthĩra, menyera. ftũmira, refl. mira, dim. tũmiranga, diff. tũmirithia, d. caus. help sb. to spend money, supervise money being spent. tũmirika, st.t pot. tũmĩrĩra, app. spend sb. else’s money, spend money on.

tũmba, v.t. (1) isolate, set apart. itũmba, refl., sit oneself down alone; ~wtiki, sit apart from others; cf. ĩũmba, ĩnuna, cikaria. tambatũmba, dim. toũmbanga, diff. rtnmbanga, refl. diff. tũmbania, caus. rec. itũmbania, refl. caus. rec. tũmbanĩra, app. rec. ĩtũmbanira, refi. app. rec. tũambithia, d. caus. itũmbithia, refl. d. caus. tambika, st. pot. tombira, app. see under ndũmba!,
itũmbi, ma-, n. (1), group, knot of people.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: green unripe sorghum (pounded in wooden mortars, cooked to a stiff gruel, eaten by men).
NEW_VALUE: green unripe sorghum (pounded in wooden mortars, cooked to a stiff gruel, eaten by men)
FULL_ENTRY: mũtũmbũrũ, mi-, n. (1v), green unripe sorghum (pounded in wooden mortars, cooked to a stiff gruel, eaten by men).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. powder, dust, flour. 2. (cl. ro) fine shreds, small particles
FULL_ENTRY: taũmbũ-tũmbũ, ~, n. (v), 1. powder, dust, flour. 2. (cl. ro) fine shreds, small particles; cf. ndũmbũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: pamper, treat (a child) with special favour. tũnatũnia, dim. toanangia, diff. tũnania, rec. tũnaniria, caus. app. rec. tonithia, d. caus. tOniria, app.
NEW_VALUE: pamper, treat (a child) with special favour. tũnatũnia, toanangia, diff
FULL_ENTRY: tũnia [tũnia], v.t. (1), pamper, treat (a child) with special favour. tũnatũnia, dim. toanangia, diff. tũnania, rec. tũnaniria, caus. app. rec. tonithia, d. caus. tOniria, app.; see ongithia kĩreru.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a favourite, spoilt, pampered child. v.i. (I1), come to a conclusion, be completely finished
FULL_ENTRY: gĩtũnio [gĩtũnio], t-, n. (1), a favourite, spoilt, pampered child. v.i. (I1), come to a conclusion, be completely finished; cf. niinũka. tũnũtanga, diff. tOũOnũtana, rec. tũnũtanira, assoc. tũngtia, caus. finish, bring to a conclusion. tũnũtithia, d. caus. tũnũtika, st. pot. app.

tũnda, v.i. (11) be covered with pimples, pustules; have measles, &c. 2. (of beans, maize, when boiling) become wrinkled, swollen; swell. tũndatũnda, dim. diff. tũndanggia, caus. dim. tũndangiria, caus. app. dim. tũndania, caus. rec. tũndanira, assoc. tandaniria, caus. assoc. tũndia, caus. cause to have small swellings on the body. ĩtũndia, rec. caus. (of a limb or part of the body) be covered with small swellings without apparent reason. toandithia, d. caus. tũndika, st. pot. tandira', app. swell, have small swellings, at. tũndũrũrũka, neut, inten. (conv.) be covered with swellings all over the body. tandũrũrũkanga, diff. neut. inten. (N.K.) swell up, be bursting with pride. tũndũranga, dif. conv. tũndũrana, rec. conv. tũndũrania, caus. rec. conv. tandũranĩra, app. rec. conv. tũndũraniria, caus. app. rec. conv, tũndũria, caus. conv. tandũrithia, d. caus. conv. tũndũrika, st’ pot. conv. tandũrira, app. conv. tũndũriria, caus. app. cony. neut. conv. (of sth. bound up, tobacco, &c.) become untied, be loosened. i-, n. (1v), foetal membranes, bag of waters; cf. ratũndũ. n. cl. x14 only, swelling; waters; cf. mokamo wa mwati. / mũtũndũtũndũ, mi-, n, (ujv), tree (Neoboutonia macrocalyx); ~ wa njora, tree (Croton macrostachys). itũndũ, ma-, n. leaf of above used in covering cooking-pots, also for papermaking.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: clump, bunch, matted clump of sth., tuft of weeds pulled out by hand from overgrown garden
NEW_VALUE: clump, bunch, matted clump of , tuft of weeds pulled out by hand from overgrown garden
FULL_ENTRY: itũngo, ma-; i-; n. (H1), clump, bunch, matted clump of sth., tuft of weeds pulled out by hand from overgrown garden; ~ rrna mara, a cut-off section or portion of the small intestine.
(ga-) (gĩ-) tũngũmi, see under ndũngũmi.

mũtũngũrũ, mi-, n. (1v), 1. mediumsized timber-tree (Mitragyna rubrostipulacea); cf. mũkũndũ. 2. a tall tree (Anthocleista zambesiaca) with leaves nearly 2 ft. long. 3. sth., long and stretched out; cf. mũtongoro.

gitũngũrũ, i-, n. (XIII), 1. round, compact, object; sphere, hard spherical ball; mara ma ~, part of large intestines where excrement is formed into small balls; cf. ndũngũrũ, ndũngũmi, gĩthũnũkũrũ. 2. onion, leek; ~ kĩa ngoma, a wild plant (similar in appearance to an onion, bearing bulbs); ~ kĩa ngwacĩ, bulb onion; ~ gĩa thumu, garlic. gatangũrũ, tũ-, n. dim. of above. tũ-, n. (1v), 1. last position, extremity. 2. (used as adv.) last; thutha o ~, very much behind.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: hasten feebly, try to catch up with those ahead
FULL_ENTRY: tũng’atũng’a, v.i. (11), hasten feebly, try to catch up with those ahead; trot, hop along. tũng'’atũng’anga, diff. caus., rec. tũng’atũng’anira, assoc. tũng’atũng’aniria, caus. assoc. tũng’atũng’ia, caus. tũng’atũng’ithia, d. caus. toũng’atũng’ira, app. tũng’atũng’iria, caus. app. mi-, n. (111), 1. smallpox; cf. mathundo. 2. name of an age-set. Nyamũtũng’ũ / Nyandũng’ũ, n. name given to a woman with a pockmarked face. Ndũng’ũ, n. I. name given to a man who is pock-marked. 2. name of the age-set of 1891.

tũra, v.t. & i. (1), 1. puncture, bore a hole through; ~ riitho, put sth. into sb.’s eye; ~ ndũgĩra, have holes punctured in one’s upper ear for ornaments. 2. (coll.) go, start and bolt away. refi. tũratũra, dim. diff. dim. tũratũrithia, d. caus. dim. tũratũrĩka, st. pot. (of salt in fire, &c.) crackle. tũranga, diff. tũrana, rec.; ~ maitho, set eyes on each other. tũrania, caus. rec. 1. bore, puncture holes through, anyhow. 2. (of persons angry with each other) come, meet, face to face. tũranira, assoc. niria, caus. assoc. tũria, caus. (of sb. prohibited from appearing at a place) appear, put in an appearance at; cf. thũndia, comoka, thandũka, umira. tũrithia, d. caus. r. help to bore holes through, have holes bored. 2. (coll.) chase away in a fight (like a victorious bull). tũrithania, rec. d. caus. r. bore holes mutually. 2. chase one another. tũrithĩria, app. d. caus. tũrĩka, st. pot. 1. (of a tunnel, hole, being bored) come through. 2. (of a whip) crack; (of sth. in the fire) explode, burst out; (of a tyre) be punctured; (of a charcoal bed) cave in (causing charcoal to burn to ashes), 3. (of children, youths) be shameless, impertinent, destructive; transgress against social morality in words, have evil intent. tũrĩkanga, diff. st. pot. tũrĩkangia, caus. diff. st. pot. 1. bore holes through quickly. 2. crack a whip for some time. 3. make a child naughty. tũrĩkangiria, caus. app. diff. st. pot. tũrĩkana, rec. st. pot. be completely bored through. ` tũrĩkania, caus. st. pot. 1. bore a hole through completely. 2. cause one another to be naughty, shameless. assoc. st. pot. explode, &c., together, simultaneously. tũrĩkaniria, caus. assoc. st. pot. caus. st. pot. 1. bore a hole through sth,; ~ mũtita, pass from one side of the forest to the other; ~ matumbi, (of hens) hatch out eggs; ~ mũbira, puncture a bicycle tyre; ~ mahira, burst a suppurating boil. 2. crack (a whip). ĩtũrĩkia, refl. caus. st, pot. tũrĩkithia, d. caus. st. pot. tũrikithania, rec. d. caus. st. pot. tũrĩkithĩria, app. d. caus. st. pot. tarĩkĩka, d. st. pot.; mũtita ndũngĩ~, this forest cannot be gone through. tũarikira, app. st. pot. 1. be punctured, for, at. 2. be naughty to sb. at a place. tũrĩkĩria, caus. app. st. pot. refl. caus. app. st. pot. tũrĩra, app. I1. bore holes through, for, at. 2. run away, through, at. tũrĩrĩra, inten. bore additional holes. tũrĩrĩria, caus. inten.; cf. turĩrĩria.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: piebald cow or she-goat, black and white or red and white in colour. grtũri, i-, (usu. with cl. 9/10 concords) piebald ox or ram
FULL_ENTRY: tari [tũaũrĩ], ~, n. (1), piebald cow or she-goat, black and white or red and white in colour. grtũri, i-, (usu. with cl. 9/10 concords) piebald ox or ram; cf. gĩthũriũ. itũri, ma-, n. Ii. piebald, fiecked pattern; spot, blotch, fleck; mbũri ĩ na matũrĩ, goat with piebald skin pattern; cf. manyaga. 2. (cl. 6) cattle.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of a whip) crack. [tũra] a. (1v), punctured, pierced, burst. [tũra] mi-, n. (1), 1. rut, gulley
FULL_ENTRY: mũtũriko, mĩ-, n. (111), (of a whip) crack. [tũra] a. (1v), punctured, pierced, burst. [tũra] mi-, n. (1), 1. rut, gulley; cf. mũkuru, 2. raised edge, ridge, stop-wash of grass, &c. mi-, n. (1), 1. (N.K.) boiled bananas or potatoes, unmashed and unmixed with other food. 2. an only child. 3. person bereft of children, left desolate, stripped of possessions; cf. mũting’a. rũtũrũ, n. unmashed, unmixed bananas.

tũrũ, ideo. (1), descr. of: 1. the noise of a whip, the report or explosion of a gun, the burst of a tyre; gambia mĩcinga ~, fire guns; gambia rũhi ~, slap with the open hand. 2. piercing or boring of peg holes (in a skin), earlobes, cloth, paper, corrugated iron. tũrg-tũrũ, ideo. descr. of: 1. crackling, popping, bursting, e.g. of roasting maize. 2, repeated blows; hũra ~, beat with countless blows, hammer. [tũra] i-, n. (vi), thong, strap, fastened below the neck of an ox to prevent it withdrawing its head from between the skeys of the yoke. motũrũbũ n. (11), 1. poverty; cf. thĩna; see mũndũrũbũ. 2. difficulty, trouble.
(ga-) (ma-) tarũme, see ndũrũme.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. live, be alive, exist, remain alive
FULL_ENTRY: taũra, v.i, (11), 1. live, be alive, exist, remain alive; take up abode, dwell, at, in; ~ muoyo, (may you) remain alive for ever!; ~ na nĩ live and let me live (apart) (lest we fight). 2. (as aux. v.) continue, go on, be in the habit of, spend one’s days (doing); ndũire nĩmaga, I have been cultivating all this time. dim.; ~ gũkũ, remain with us longer. tũranga, diff. tũrangia, caus. diff. tũrania, caus. rec. I. live together, remain together a long time; ~ na, live with. 2. preserve one another. tũranira, assoc. be contemporaries, tũranĩria, caus, assoc. live together, at, for; keep sth. for sb. tũria, caus. 1. live, with, near, sb., sth. (on guard); spend one’s life with. 2. keep sth. longer than allowed; preserve, keep at a certain place, support, maintain. itũria, refl. caus. live alone, be independent; maintain one’s existence, abode, at; keep oneself alive; cf. rikaria. tnaũrithia, d. caus. st. pot. taũrĩra, app. live in a house for the sake of. 2. (of sth. thrown or kicked into the air) get stuck up (on a tree, &c.). caus. app. I. keep house for sb. 2. throw, kick, sth, and get it stuck (on a tree, &c.). riria, refl. caus. app. tũririra, inten. 1. (of a mass of people, swarms of locusts, &c.) remain, at, with, temporarily. 2. take a long time over. tũririria, caus, inten. refl. caus. inten, remain at a place, temporarily, a long time, even when unwanted. ma-, n. (viir), village; collection of homesteads (usu. of people connected together by some family tie, formerly stockaded for strategic purposes, with a certain amount of pasture and gardens for use in an emergency); cf. nyũmba,

Gathoni. = of broken glass. 2. a large edible arum that has to be scraped after roasting. Ndende ĩrehage ~, a small immature arum produces a large one. [thuta"]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: drizzle accompanied by light driving wind.
NEW_VALUE: drizzle accompanied by light driving wind
FULL_ENTRY: rũthutũ thutũ, n. (11), drizzle accompanied by light driving wind. [thuta?]

thutha, v.t. (1), 1. crush by downward pressure; smash; break into fragments; ~ gĩthaka, push (one’s) way through thick bush or forest; cf. memenda, bebera. thuthathutha, dim. thuthanga, diff. thuthana, rec. thuthania, caus. rec. thuthanĩra, app. rec. thuthithia, d. caus. ithuthithia, refl. d. caus. thuthĩka, st. pot. be crushed, smashed, broken into fragments; ~ ngoro, be heartbroken; (as a swear word) may you be irrecoverably crushed, ruined ! thuthikanga, diff. st. pot. break up, be smashed up; cf. unĩka. thuthĩra, app.

thutha [thuutha] / (N.K.) thuha [thuuha], n. cl. 14 only (1), 1. behind, back, rear; (with ref. to relative positions in a geographical sense, depending on the speaker’s thinking about the object referred to, confusion being avoided by context) behind, after, this side of, at the back of; atũraga ~ wa Thĩka, he lives this side of T.; -a ~, last; tiga na ~, leave behind; tua, uma, ~, follow sb. ~ wa arũme nduoyagwo ruoya, where men have been it is impossible to pick up a feather. Cf. mbere. 2. (of future time following, subsequent time, posterity ; freq. as adv. and prep. of time) behind, after, subsequent to; ~ wa irio cia hwaĩ-inĩ, after supper; ~ũyũ, Ũcio, after that; ~ ũr? o mũhiũ, in a little while, shortly, immediately after; ~-~, a little later. gathutha, n. cl. 12 only, a little while after; a little way behind; posterity.
mũthuthi, mi-, n. (11) 1. Barberry (Berberis holstii) (thorns used in surgery); cf. mũkekenye, mnkururi. 2. bush (Gymnosporia luteola); cf. mũthithioi. 3. shrub (Rhamnus deflersii); cf. mũbura.

gĩthuthio (gĩthuuthio), i-, n. (11), company, companionship; keeping together, travelling together; maatinda ~, they have spent the day together. mi-, n. (11), removal, dispersal; ruin; cf. muunĩko. [thutha]
thuthu (N.K.), see nduthu.
rũthuthu, thuthu, n. (1?), (N.K.) a little bit; cf. kang’orui.
thuthua [thuthuwa], v.i. (1), I. rain lightly, drizzle; cf. thuta, cicia. 2. be indisposed. thuthathuthua, dim. thuthuanga, diff. thuthuira, app.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: be finished, complete, ready, all in order. thuthuania, caus. 1. do completely leaving no gaps. 2. smooth over, leave tidy, level
FULL_ENTRY: thuthuana [thuthuwana], v.i. (1), be finished, complete, ready, all in order. thuthuania, caus. 1. do completely leaving no gaps. 2. smooth over, leave tidy, level; cf. cocorania. 3. reconcile, bring into harmony; cf. iguithania. thuthuanithia, d. caus. thuthuanika, st. pot. thuthuanira, app. thuthuanirĩra, inten.

NEW_VALUE: disease characterized by very swollen stomach (a form of dropsy or diabetes)
FULL_ENTRY: mũthuthuma [mũthuuthuma], (mi-), n. (VIII); ihu rĩa ~, disease characterized by very swollen stomach (a form of dropsy or diabetes).

NEW_VALUE: nag, backbite, find fault with, gossip about
FULL_ENTRY: thuthunga, v.t. (1) (esp. of women) nag, backbite, find fault with, gossip about.

thuthuta, v.t. (1), (N.K.) strike a heavy blow. thuthutana, rec. thuthutania, caus. rec. thuthutanĩra, app. rec, thuthutanĩria, caus. app. rec. thuthutithia, d. caus. thuthutĩka, st. pot. thuthutira, app.

NEW_VALUE: of: spitting rain, light persistent drizzle
FULL_ENTRY: thuthu-thuthu, ideo. (1) descr. of: I. spitting rain, light persistent drizzle. 2. perpetual nagging; see thuthunga. 3. ambiguous, equivocal speech.
rũthuthũ [rũthuthuwũ], thuthũg, n. (v), drizzle, showers. [thuthua]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of young man to young giri whom he wishes to marry, husband to bride, young boy to sponsor at initiation) give present(s)
NEW_VALUE: (of young man to young giri whom he wishes to marry, husband to bride, young boy to sponsor at initiation) give present(s)
FULL_ENTRY: thuuga, v.t. (11), 1. (of young man to young giri whom he wishes to marry, husband to bride, young boy to sponsor at initiation) give present(s); cf. tuha. 2. appoint, choose, commission, depute. thuugathuuga, dim. thuuganga, diff. thuugana, rec. thuugania, caus. rec. thuuganira, app. rec. thuuganĩria, caus. app. rec, thuugithia, d. caus. thuugika, st. pot. thuugira, app. thuugiria, caus. app.

thuuna, v.i. (11), 1. (of bulbs, suckers, &c.) sprout, have shoots; bud, multiply by propagation; cf. therema. 2. be multi-stemmed; see gĩtina. 3. be prosperous. thuunathuuna, dim. thuunanga, diff. thuunanithia, d. caus. rec, thuunanira, app. rec. thuunaniria, caus. app. rec.

thuuna / thuuno, ~; gĩthuuna, i-; n. (vi), 1. offshoot; shoot springing from base of established tree or plant; sucker of plant propagating by division. 2. gill at base of pineapple. gathuuna, tũ-, n. dim. of above. [thuuna]

thuura, v.t. (1), 1. choose, pick out, select, elect; sort out; ~ pick out husks and grit from rice. 2. investigate, discuss, unravel an affair; ~ mũndũ, interrogate sb. concerning sth. thuurathuura, dim. thuuranga, diff. thuurana, rec. thuurania, caus. rec. put into categories, discriminate, sort out. rthuurania, refl. caus. rec. make up one’s mind, decide for oneself, make a personal resolution. thuuranĩra, app. rec. 1. pick, choose, the best; pick out what is inferior leaving only the good; select, arrange in order. 2. apportion, allocate, to. thuuraniria, caus. app. rec. thuurithia, d. caus. thuurithia, refl. d. caus. thuurĩka, st. pot. thuurira, app. ĩthuurĩra, refl. app. thuurĩrira, inten. thuurũkania, caus. rec. neut. sort out, sift, investigate, analyse.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: particular, fastidious, person, gathuura, tũ-, I.
NEW_VALUE: particular, fastidious, person, gathuura, tũ-, finicky, particular, person
FULL_ENTRY: maũthuura, a-, n. (1), particular, fastidious, person, gathuura, tũ-, I. finicky, particular, person. 2. a personal name. ithuura, ma-, n. choosing, selection. [thuura]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: selection of a particular person, a particular selection.
NEW_VALUE: selection of a particular person, a particular selection
FULL_ENTRY: ithuurana, ma-, n. (x1), selection of a particular person, a particular selection. [thuura]
-thuuranire, a. (1v), well arranged, sorted out, ordered. [thuura]
ithuuranirio, ma-, n. (1), 1. place of sorting out, judgement. 2, (Bibl.) the last judgement. fthuura]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: a sorting out, selection. gĩthuurano, i-, n. a choosing of sb. or of some persons, a choice
FULL_ENTRY: ithuurano, ma-, n. (X1), a sorting out, selection. gĩthuurano, i-, n. a choosing of sb. or of some persons, a choice; an election. [thuura]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (R.C. usage) a saint. [thuura] a-, n. (1), elder, married man with children or of an age to have children, councillor
FULL_ENTRY: mũthuure, a-, n. (IV), (R.C. usage) a saint. [thuura] a-, n. (1), elder, married man with children or of an age to have children, councillor; ~ wakwa, ~guo, ~we, my, your, her husband; ~ ũy, sir (a respectful form of address); ~ mũkũrũ, elder of advanced age, old man; ~ wa kĩama, an elder who has paid the fee to the council; ~ wa mbũri elder who has paid one goat as a fee (towards membership of the council of elders). gĩthuuri, i-, n. I. big, clumsy sort of elder. 2. (N.K.) isolated hill of some prominence, large mound, dome, n. cl. 11, company of elders, elders in the mass. kĩmũthuuri, i-, n. a big, old man. gathbuuri, tũ-, n. dim. of mũthuuri, usu. complimentary. kamũthuuri, tũ-, n. znd dim., usu. in derogatory sense. Ũthuuri, n. cl. 14, 1. condition of being an elder; qualities attaching to eldership. 2. age; cf. kũrũ. fthuura]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: process of investigating a case before the elders. mathuuro, n. cl. 6, things picked and rejected as bad or inferior
NEW_VALUE: things picked and rejected as bad or inferior
FULL_ENTRY: thuuro, ~, n. (1), process of investigating a case before the elders. mathuuro, n. cl. 6, things picked and rejected as bad or inferior; ~ ma mbembe, small cobs of inferior quality maize. n. cl. 14, fastidiousness; choiceness, selectness; ĩca-~, a fastidious person; irio cia ~, choice food. [thuura}]
thuuti, ~, n. (1), (of clothes) a suit.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: v.t. & i. (11), sing to a baby, rock a baby to sleep. thuuthathuutha, dim. thuuthathuuthira, app. dim. thuuthathuuthangira, app. diff. dim. thuuthanga, diff. thuuthanira, assoc. thuuthira, app. croon a lullaby, sing a lullaby to a baby, whistle to
FULL_ENTRY: (thuutha!), v.t. & i. (11), sing to a baby, rock a baby to sleep. thuuthathuutha, dim. thuuthathuuthira, app. dim. thuuthathuuthangira, app. diff. dim. thuuthanga, diff. thuuthanira, assoc. thuuthira, app. croon a lullaby, sing a lullaby to a baby, whistle to; ~ mbũri, whistle to goats or sheep while herding; ~ gĩcukia | njukia, lead this particular dance; ~ mũndũ, entreat sb. earnestly, persuade sb. to change his mind; ~ kaana, sing a child to sleep, cf. kũya. thuuthĩranga, diff. app. thuuthĩranira, app. rec. thuuthĩraniria, caus. app. rec. thuuthirithia, d. caus. thuuthirika, st. pot. thuuthirira, inten. (in common use for thuuta).

ORIGINAL_VALUE: grow out of the ground. thuuthathuutha, dim. thuuthanga, diff, thuuthũka, neut. start to grow
FULL_ENTRY: thuutha?, v.i. (11), grow out of the ground. thuuthathuutha, dim. thuuthanga, diff, thuuthũka, neut. start to grow; cf. thundũka.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. wooden arrow-head on millet stalk shaft, used by Masai guild in circumcision ceremonies
NEW_VALUE: wooden arrow-head on millet stalk shaft, used by Masai guild in circumcision ceremonies
FULL_ENTRY: thuuthi, ~~, n. (11), 1. wooden arrow-head on millet stalk shaft, used by Masai guild in circumcision ceremonies; see irui, ciumiri. 2. the same, with cane shaft, used for shooting colobus monkey; see ithara. rũthuthi, thuthi, n. arrow-head, pointed for use against birds, otherwise slotted to receive iron tip; see mũgui, kĩano.

NEW_VALUE: songs sung by herd-boys and men while herding their charges in the bush
FULL_ENTRY: (mũ-), mĩthuuthiro, n. (XI), songs sung by herd-boys and men while herding their charges in the bush. [thuutha]

thũ, ~, n. (111), enemy, adversary; cf. mimuku. ũthũ, n. cl. r4 only, enmity, hatred; state of feud, animosity; grĩaaa ~ na, be at daggers drawn with; cf. kaũthũ / gothũ [goothũ], n. cl. 12 only, a small degree of ill-feeling.
thũ, interj. (U), a greeting. [cf. Ma. SOBA]
mũthũ, mĩ-, n. (Iv), fruit-eating mousebird.

thga! [thũwa], v.t. (1), 1. scratch (body with nails); burnish (with hard stone); rub; put edge on spear, sword, &c. after forging; cf. hara, guta. 2. hunt for; cf. caria, tuiria, rutũrũra. ĩthũa, refi. scratch oneself (because of irritation). thũathũa, dim. rira, dim. inten. thũanga, diff. thũangithia, d, caus. diff. thũana, rec. thũania, caus. rec. scratch oneself carelessly, anyhow. thũithia, caus. fthũithia, refi. d. caus. scratch oneself by rubbing oneself against sth.; 2. irritate, ruffle, rile, nettle; provoke sb. to anger, evoke a retort. thũĩka, st. pot. thũĩra, app. thgirira, inten. 1. bring trouble on; 2. go on scratching at (an itch, &c.); ~ thambo, disturb little black tree-ants (ask for trouble). fthũĩrira, refl. inten.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (of sun and moon) set, go down. thũithia, d. caus., mabebe maya (ng’ombe) rina, these maize stalks will not last the cattle tili sunset. (mĩ-), n. (1v), white ants, termites
FULL_ENTRY: thũa? [thũwa], v.i. (1), (of sun and moon) set, go down. thũithia, d. caus., mabebe maya (ng’ombe) rina, these maize stalks will not last the cattle tili sunset. (mĩ-), n. (1v), white ants, termites; cf. nguya. thũa, ~, n. sponge-like nest of termites; (N.K.) inner nest of a papiermache-like substance. githũa, i-, n. I. colony, nest, hill, of white ants; queen ant. 2. any large mass; ~ kĩa njũkĩ, swarm of bees; mboco imerete ~, the beans have germinated in a bunch. gathũa, tũ-, n. 1, dim. of above. 2. andũ ~, masses of people.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: shake up, clean a bottle or calabash by shaking or with switch.
NEW_VALUE: thoca, v.t. (1), shake up, clean a bottle or calabash by shaking or with switch
FULL_ENTRY: thũca / (N.K.) thoca, v.t. (1), shake up, clean a bottle or calabash by shaking or with switch. ithũca, refl. thũcathũca, dim. thũcanga, diff. thũcana, rec. thũcania, caus. rec. thũcanĩra, app. rec. thũcaniria, caus. app. rec. thũcithia, d. caus. ithũcithia, refl. d. caus. thũcĩka, st. pot. thũcĩra, app. ithũcĩra, refl. app. thũcĩrira, inten.
thũgũ, ~, n. advance guard.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: gnaw (a bone or C pah et maize cob, &c.).
FULL_ENTRY: thũggũna, v.t. (1), gnaw (a bone or C pah et maize cob, &c.). thũgathũgũna, dim. thũgũgũnanga, diff. thũgũnanira, app. rec. thũgũnithia, d. caus. thũgũnĩka, st. pot. thnũgũnira, app. thũgũnĩrĩra, inten. cony. inten., ~ ihĩndi, tear off flesh from bone violently and ravenously. thũneut. conv. ~, n. (111), glutton.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: procure food, &c., from a distance in time of famine
NEW_VALUE: food, merchandise, brought from a distance to relieve distress in time of dearth
FULL_ENTRY: thũgũra, v.t. (1), procure food, &c., from a distance in time of famine; go trading a long distance away. thũgũranga, diff. thũgũrana, rec. thũgũranira, app. rec. thũgũrithia, d. caus. thũgũrira, app. thũgũririra, inten. fthnũgũririra, refil. inten. bring upon oneself; cf. ĩgĩrĩrira, ĩreherera. (ma-), n. cl. 14/6 (v1), food, merchandise, brought from a distance to relieve distress in time of dearth; act of searching for food or merchandise in time of dearth; thĩ, ~, go in search of, bring back, food, &c., to relieve distress; aheanaga irio ta arĩ cia ~, she gives people food sparingly as if it were extremely scarce. [thũgũra]

NEW_VALUE: buyer, fetcher, of things, esp. food, from a distance or under adverse conditions
FULL_ENTRY: mũthũgũri, a-, n. (1), buyer, fetcher, of things, esp. food, from a distance or under adverse conditions. [thũgũra] see thogota,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. hard black stone or pebble used for putting a new grinding surface on a stone or as a missile
NEW_VALUE: be, become, bad, inferior, ugly, unbecoming
FULL_ENTRY: hũi, ~, n. (1), 1. hard black stone or pebble used for putting a new grinding surface on a stone or as a missile; cf. ngomongo. 2. one who stirs up; ~ ya ita, an instigator of war. [thũa!] nũthũi [mũthũi], a-, n. (1), honeygatherer. [thũya] athũita, tũ-; thũita, ~; n. (11), the blue-breasted wax-bill (Cordon Bleu). nathũithia, see mahũithia, thũiro [ithũwiro] / thũĩro, n. cl. 5 or 9 (1), where the sun sets, west; cf. rũgũrũ. [thũa?] hũka [thũka] / (N.x.) cũka v.i. (11), 1. be, become, bad, inferior, ugly, unbecoming; 2. be, become, dirty, repulsive; 3. be, become, spoiled, useless; 4. be, become, badtempered, fierce, violent, dangerous, harmful. thũkathũka, dim. thũkanga, diff. tĩũkana, rec. be completely spoilt, ruined. thũkania, caus. rec. thũkanĩra, assoc. thũkaniria, caus. assoc. thũkia, caus. spoil, make bad, injure, disfigure; foul, defile, render dirty. thũkithia, d. caus. ĩthũkithia, refl. d. caus. thũkĩka, st. pot. thũkĩra, app. tell sb. off; act violently, fiercely, towards sb.; be unbecoming to; ~ mũndũ, (coll.) scold sb. thũkĩria, caus. app. spoil, injure, dirty, for. refi, caus. app. thũkiriria, caus. inten. detract, defame; speak slightingly, damagingly, of. thũkũra, conv. take away badness, inferiority, ugliness; | change bad to good, reform.

-thũku [-thũku], a. (1v), bad, unbecoming, dirty, repulsive; spoiled, injured, disfigured; bad-tempered, fierce, violent; dangerous, harmful; cf. -cũaku. [thũka] ~, n. (1), roll of fat at the back of a ram’s head; cf. kamba. 2. name given to a female goat with a white tuft of hair on the head. 3. a fruit-eating bird, distinguished by a white crest. gĩthũkũ, i-, n. 1. crest of hair, feathers, &c.; (of birds) comb, tuft of hair. 2. bulge, knob, boss on a gourd or tree; rough, unshaven part; cf. gĩtuiririro. v.i. (11), work for wages; cf. tungata. diff. thũkũmanira, assoc. thũkũmithia, d. caus. st. pot. thũkũmira, app. v.t. (11), push hard; cf. tindĩka. thgknmanga, diff. thũkũmana, rec. push each other; be packed. thũkũmanĩra, app. rec. thũkũmithia, d. caus.; ~ mũndũ mũtokaa, help sb. push a (when stuck or with a run-down battery). thũknũmĩka, st. pot. thũkũmira, app. [Sw. SUKUMA]
githũkũmo, i-, n. (x), work for wages (manual). [thũkũma:]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: swelling, bump (as from a blow), spherical lump of sth., bulb, boss
NEW_VALUE: swelling, bump (as from a blow), spherical lump of , bulb, boss
FULL_ENTRY: gĩthnakũrũ n. (X1), swelling, bump (as from a blow), spherical lump of sth., bulb, boss; cf. thũngũri, tũ-, n. knob, tumour.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: n. (iv), dance of old men and women, replaced, after 1898, by mathũnngũci. [thnũnmũka], v.i. (11), 1. sigh
FULL_ENTRY: mũthgmũ [mũthnũnũmũ], (mĩ-), n. (iv), dance of old men and women, replaced, after 1898, by mathũnngũci. [thnũnmũka], v.i. (11), 1. sigh; cf. ikia mĩen, (N.K.) hũmũka. 2. (N.K.) (of girls in ndumo dance) jump and make panting, sighing, sounds simultaneously; give a shuddering sigh, start.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. person named after member of mothers family (sometimes used in a jocular sense)
NEW_VALUE: food or other recompense given instead of pay
FULL_ENTRY: thũmba, v.t. (1) 1. sponge on, earn favour by working for sb. or performing minor tasks (e.g. in harvesting, receiving part of crop as wages). 2. seek; ~ njuukũ cia mũndũ, hunt for grounds of complaint against sb.; ~ ndiiro, be busy with other people’s affairs. thaũmbathũmba, dim. thũmbanga, diff. thũmbanira, assoc. thnũmbia, caus. thũmbithia, d. caus. thaũmbika, st. pot. thanũmbira, app. thũmbirĩra, inten. i-, n. (1), I. person named after member of mothers family (sometimes used in a jocular sense); cf. nyamwari. 2. one who lives on the charity of another, a hanger-on, dependant; cf. gĩthnũmbi; see mwene ũthgmba, n. cl. 14 only, reward (generally food) received by a servitor or casual helper for services rendered; cf. [thaũmba] ~, n. (vI), small blackcrested eagle, martial hawk eagle. i-, n. (1), one who works for food instead of pay (e.g. a poor or disabled woman who helps in harvesting a crop and is given the rejects as her reward). ũthũmbi, mothgmbi [moothũmbi], n. 1. food or other recompense given instead of pay; cf. ũthnũmba. 2. service, state of servitude. [thũmba]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. feathery end of branch, topmost branch. 2. headdress of ostrich feathers
NEW_VALUE: feathery end of branch, topmost branch
FULL_ENTRY: thũmbi, ~, n. (1), 1. feathery end of branch, topmost branch. 2. headdress of ostrich feathers; lion’s or baboon’s mane, colobus-monkey skin worn in war to denote rank; no ~ na just a waving of feathers in the breeze, nothing in particular; cf. thitai. 3. crown; ~ ya mĩgua, crown of thorns; kũigĩrĩrao ~, coronation. ma-, n. top of tree; blade of spear; ma mũtĩ, the crown of the tree; cf. mathimbirĩrĩ. i-, n. the round unshaven patch on the back of a woman’s head.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. toss spear, club, in air with a spinning motion while dancing
NEW_VALUE: toss spear, club, in air with a spinning motion while dancing
FULL_ENTRY: thnmbũya, v.i. (1), 1. toss spear, club, in air with a spinning motion while dancing; cf. hambũya. 2. (of gruel) boil, froth up. thgmbũiya, caus.

thũna [thũna], v.i. (1) be blotchy; (of wounds) swell up with pus; form kelloid; cf. thea. thũnanga, diff. thũnania, caus, rec., thũnanira, app. rec. thũnithia, d. caus. thũnika, st. pot. thũnĩra, app. thũnũra, conv. neut. (of blotch) disappear.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. a roughly made shelter, temporary hut
NEW_VALUE: a roughly made shelter, temporary hut
FULL_ENTRY: githũnũ, n. (1v), 1. a roughly made shelter, temporary hut; cf. kĩganda, gĩtayaya. 2. initiation candidates’ hut built adjacent to the women’s sleeping place of the senior co-wife’s hut.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. protrude. 2. be, become naughty, depraved, vicious
FULL_ENTRY: thũnũka?, v.i. (11), 1. protrude. 2. be, become naughty, depraved, vicious; cf. nana, tũrika. diff. thnũnũkana, rec., thũnũkia, caus. spoil (e.g. a child) by giving too much attention, make naughty. thũnũkithia, d. caus. thũnũra, v.t. 1. turn inside out. 2. deprave, make naughty or vicious. refl. conv. put on airs, make a show of being welloff and genuine; (of a child) attract attention by crying; cf. yata. thũnũranga, diff. conv.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. showing off, putting on airs, demand for attention
NEW_VALUE: showing off, putting on airs, demand for attention
FULL_ENTRY: mũthũnũko, mi-, n. (1), 1. showing off, putting on airs, demand for attention; cf. mwithũnũro. 2. (N.K.) naughtiness, shamelessness, impertinence; cf. monanũko, ũtũrika. [thũnũka?]
githnnũnũ, see gicanũnũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: come into view, appear, emerge (from hut, bush, &c.)

ORIGINAL_VALUE: stem of dem. a. & p., cl. 5, (6), 7» II, I2, 13, I5, and 16, that, those, (near the person addressed, near or in sth., previously mentioned). With the prefixes of cl. 5, 15b, and 16 there are special meanings
FULL_ENTRY: -u, p.a. (U), stem of dem. a. & p., cl. 5, (6), 7» II, I2, 13, I5, and 16, that, those, (near the person addressed, near or in sth., previously mentioned). With the prefixes of cl. 5, 15b, and 16 there are special meanings: 1. cl. 5, rĩ~, now; o rĩ~, here and now, just a few moments ago; o na rĩ~, even now, certainly; kĩrĩ~, see sep. entry kĩriu. 2. cl. 15b; ki~, there; thit na kũ~, get away with you; gũtiri mũndũ, there is nobody there; gĩkn~, after the manner of that place, local. 3. cl. 16, ha~, just there, thereabouts; thĩ na ha~, go over there, in that direction; kĩrĩ ha~, (an evasive reply, when repeatedly asked where sth. is, to close the matter) it is somewhere there; one nĩ ha~ haranduura, that’s where I feel the pain.
nũ (N.K.), see mũhu,.

FULL_ENTRY: uma, v.i. (I1), I. come out, go out from, emerge, leave a place; ~ thutha, follow; mbũri cioima, when the goats leave the house (about 8 a.m. depending upon weather); ~ na nja, come out into the courtyard; ~ nja, go out, come out; ~ tha, be merciful, lose hope about sb. or sth.; ~ ngoro, (of a person) sigh deeply after crying; ~ nda, (of women) have a miscarriage; ~ come down, come from heaven, climb down; ~ tũathendũ, see thendũra; cf. girika, huma, ng'ethũra. 2. (of a dream) come true. 3. be bought for, be worth, cost, produce, yield; result in, from; be produced by; ~ mbeca, cost money; ~ goro, be dear, expensive, exorbitant; ~ irio (of garden) be very productive; mbaara yoima kĩ?, what caused the fight? 4. (used as Immediate Past Tense of v. ‘to be’); ny~ na mbeca, I had some money just now. §. (used as aux. V. with adv. force) nearly; u~ kũgũthwo, you were nearly struck. 6. (used as prep. preceded by cl. 15a prefix) from; ku~ tene nginya rĩu, since the remote past up to now; ku~ haha nginya Naikuru, (coll.) from here to Nakuru. 7. (in conjunction with no) must; no ku~ (no gũki~) we must go; cf. no nginya, no mũhaka. umwoo, pass.; ~ nĩ ngoro, be under emotional distress; ~ nĩ muoyo, be at one’s last gasp, expire, die; cf. tuĩkana. umoima, dim. come out a bit more. umanga, diff. umana, rec. come apart, come out of each other; nguo nĩ yoimana, (of tattered dress) the dress has come to pieces; ~ na, originate in, proceed from; ~ thutha, follow one another. umania, caus. rec. cause to come apart, tear up; come out together, emerge and scatter; ~ na, go out with; result, issue, in; aki~ na gũthii, he finally decided to go. umanira, assoc. paths through bush, &c.) lead into one another. 2, come out at the same time or place; ~ na mbari ĩrĩa ĩngĩ, go in and come out on the other side. 3 umanirira, inten. rec. meet unexpectedly ; ~ ngoro, be friendly with one another. umaniria, caus. assoc. make go out, take out, together. umanũka, neut. rec. become completely torn apart; become undented. umia, caus. 1. take outside, carryout; put, pushout; expel; ~ mara thutha, send a letter after sb. 2. yield, produce, multiply; produce profit; be profitable; irio icio nĩcioĩmia, the food has turned out to be more than it appeared to be. iumia, reĩl. caus. go out, climb down, by one’s own energy, of one’s own accord, umithia, d. caus. 1. have taken out, help to take out. 2. make profit, increase, cause to produce profit, multiply by trading. umithania, rec. d. caus. umithiria, app. d. caus. umika, st. pot. umikira, app. st. pot. umira, app. 1. come out of water or bush, emerge, protrude, show; ndangi~, (of a sick person) he will not recover. 2. come out, off, down, at, through; ~ ndirica, come out through the window. umiranga, diff. app. come out of water, bush, quickly. umĩrangia, caus. diff. app. take out of the bush or water a bit more quickly; ~ mwako, bring building materials to the site quickly, for some additional time. umirithia, d. caus. app. have brought out of. umirika, st. pot. app. be capable of being picked, lifted (out of water); be expellable (from the thicket); cf. rutũrũrika. umirĩra, d. app. 1. come out upon, appear suddenly to; present oneself to sb. unexpectedly, catch red-handed. 2. come out of the water or bush at. umirĩria, caus. app. take out of the water, bush, at, for. umũrania, caus. rec., conv. break the stitch (of a dress, calabash, &c.) apart. umũraniria, caus. app. rec. Conv. break the stitch at, break the stitch of sb. else’s calabash, &c. umũka, neut. I1. become outspoken. 2. appear. umgũgkana, rec. neut. 1. (of tattered clothes) be torn into shreds, be broken and separated. 2. be clearheaded, intelligent, direct, straightforward, open, clear-spoken. 3. become revealed. caus. rec. neut. 1. (of a child) begin to speak clearly, fluently, and intelligibly; enunciate words very distinctly and clearly. 2. speak out, declare what is in one’s mind clearly and distinctly, make a clean breast of sth. umũkanira, app. rec. neut. umũkaniria, caus. app. rec. neut.; ny~/!, (of a mother to her child angrily) speak to me clearly ! umũrũrũka, neut. inten. (of people, ants, &c.) stream out. umũrũrũkia, caus. neut. inten. drag towards (the exterior, courtyard, &c.) umũrũrũkira, app. neut. inten. stream out, at, through, across, upon, toward, for.

FULL_ENTRY: umbika, v.t. (1), stick (potatoes, yams, bananas, &c.) into hot ashes to bake; hide by sticking into the ground, under a heap, among grass, &c. Maũthii ndoimbikaga irigũ, a traveller does not stick a banana into hot ashes. rumbĩka, refl. thrust a stick, needle, sharp-pointed knife, into oneself; hide by thrusting oneself into the bush. umboimbĩka, dim. umbikanga, diff. umbikana, rec. thrust, inject, a needle into sb. umbikania, caus. rec. stick into haphazardly; embezzle, defraud. fumbikania, refl. caus. rec. keep out of view, dive back into the water. umbikanĩra, app. rec. thrust into hot ashes for sb., each other, others; ~ kũihũ, stick a mongoose for each other (set a secret trap for each other’s capture). umbikaniria, caus. app. rec. umbikia, caus. umbikithia, d. caus. help cover (logs for wood distillation, &c.)}. umbikika, st. pot. umbikira, app. stick into, for, at. umbikira, refl. app. thrust oneself into hiding at; stick sth. into for oneself. umbikirira, inten. stick additional things into. umbikiriria, caus. inten. thrust into (in order to conceal). rumbĩkĩriria, refl. caus. inten. dig oneself into in order to hide.
