ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. stir up, disturb (fire, bees, animals, hidden in the bush) by poking with a stick
FULL_ENTRY: umbutha, v.t. (1), 1. stir up, disturb (fire, bees, animals, hidden in the bush) by poking with a stick; ~ nyũmba, thrust (a spear, stick, &c.) through the wall of a house; ~ ikwa, ngwacĩ, turn over yams, potatoes, in the fire so that they may be ready for eating; see gariũra; ~ njũkĩ, provoke bees to sting (stir up trouble for oneself). 2. provoke sb. to quarrel; press repeatedly for information; ~ cira, spoil a case, by excessive talk, irresponsible remarks; cf., thogotha. umboimbutha / umboumbutha, dim. umbuthanga, diff. umbuthania, caus. rec. stir, disturb, anyhow. umbuthanira, app. rec. umbuthaniria, caus. app. rec. umbuthithia, d. caus. refl. d. caus. umbuthika, st. pot. umbuthikira, app. st. pot. umbuthĩra, app. umbuthirira, inten. rfumbuthirira, refl. inten. umbuthiriria, caus. inten. rumbuthirĩria, refl. caus. inten.; cf. rumbikirĩria. umbuthũra, conv. scare (an animal in hiding), drive out of concealment; cf. rutrũra, guthũra, hahũra. umbuthũranga, diff. conv. umbuthũrana, rec. conv. startle one another into flight. umbuthũrania, caus. rec. conv. umbuthũranira, app. rec. conv.; moimbuthũranĩra kĩ?, what have they been fighting about? umbuthũraniria, caus. app. rec. cony. umbuthũria, caus. conv. umbuthũrithia, d. caus. conv. umbuthũrĩka, st. pot. neut. umbuthũrĩria, caus. app. conv. umbuthũrirĩra, inten. conv. umbuthũka, neut.; come away in fine dust (as a rotten tree); make appearance as sediment when water is stirred up; cf. tumbuthũka, tumbetheka. umbuthũkania, caus. rec. neut, turn (compost, manure, rubbish, dirty water, &c.) over. umbuthũkanira, assoc. neut. umbuthũkaniria, caus. assoc, neut, umbuthũkia, caus. neut. umbuthũkithia, d. caus. neut. umbuthũkika, st. pot. neut. umbuthũkĩra, app. neut. umbuthũkiria, caus. app. neut. uumbutha, see koimbutha. (mauumbuthi), n. cl. 14/6 (x1), 1. sediment in remaining dregs; cf. kĩnyita. 2. dust falling from ceiling; refuse, rubbish, litter; cf. thumuthumu, tumbuthĩ. koimbuthi, tuumbuthi, n. dim. of above; see umbuthũka; cf. tumbuthi.

umbũka, v.i. (1) 1. become known; (of a secret) leak out. 2. emerge, come to the surface (of water). umboumbũka, dim, umbũkanga, diff. umbũkana, rec. surface, emerge suddenly and entirely. caus. rec. (of water at the bottom of a waterfall) surge violently, well up vigorously. umbũkanira, assoc. be known, leak out, simultaneously. umbũkaniria, caus. assoc. umbũkia, caus. bring into the open. rumbũkia, refl. caus. umbũkira, app. be known to, for, at. umbũkĩria, caus. app. make known to, for, at. rumbũkĩria, refl. caus. app. umbũra, v.t. confess, make confession, announce, declare, give away a secret, divulge. umbũrana, rec. squeal, blackmail, others, each other. caus. rec, dig up a bone, anything buried, hidden; dig up sth. anyhow; let out a secret entirely. umbũranira, app. rec. announce things together, give out a secret. umbũraniria, caus. app. rec. 1. make known together. 2. dig up (bones) together. umbũria, caus. r. dig up, unearth, uncover (buried things); turn up; cf. rindũria. 2. divulge, discover, dig up and reveal secrets, fraudulence. fumbũria, refl. caus. come out of hiding of one’s own free wil. umbũrithia, d. caus. conv. umbũrĩka, st. pot. be revealable. umbũrira, app. reveal, divulge, confess, to, at. umbũriria, caus. app. uncover, dig up, unearth, for, at. umbũrũria, caus. inten. dig up, out; dig up completely, eradicate; cf. ombororia. umbũrũrũka, st. inten. be dug up and dispersed, scattered, over a wide area; (of insects) stream out of their nest when disturbed. caus. st. inten. bring every detail to light; cause to stream out of the ground; cf. ombororokia.
muumbũri, oimbũri, n. (1), one who confesses or reveals. [umbũra]

NEW_VALUE: take away very early in the morning, hurry away into hiding (from danger or enemy attack)
FULL_ENTRY: una, v.t. (11), I. snap, break in two; break (things other than globular, glass, pots, &c.); ~ split firewood; & kĩrĩti, gĩthaka, cut down forest, virgin land, for a new holding; ~ mũndũ thimo, mũti, talk about sb. (who is present) in proverbs, pick a quarrel, find fault with sb.; ~ kĩrĩti kĩa ngoro, know inmost thoughts; ~ mũgumo, (of initiates) collect the leaves of a fig-tree; ~ njohi, put half of the beer into a second container; ~ nyũmba, commit a burglary, break into a house; nĩruoina, the river has burst its banks; ~ break open a box. 2. bend, bend double, fold over (bed sheets, &c.), bend round; ~ nguo, turn up a hem, fold the ends of a dress, see heta; ~ iru, bend a knee, take a short rest; ~ riitho, wink; ~ mĩromo, pout; ~ kĩrĩma, go over the top of a hill, ridge. refl. x. make a turn, turn a corner. 2, bend oneself; change one’s mind; break one’s limb. unoina, dim. break off short, abbreviate (words); cf. tinatinia, kuhakuhia. unoinanga, diff. dim. unoinanira, app. rec. dim. unoinithia, d. caus. dim. unoinira, app. dim. unoinĩrĩra, inten. dim. unoinũra, conv. dim. unfold a little bit. unanga, diff. runanga, refi. diff. be ill at ease, appear embarrassed, uncomfortable; be fidgety in the presence of others; beat about the bush, prevaricate. unangithia, d. caus. diff.; ~ irto ng'ombe, allow cattle to trample on crops. unangika, st. pot. be broken, breakable. unangira, app. diff.; ~ ngũ riiko, break firewood into an open fire. unana, rec. I. break, share, sth., among; divide (sugar-cane, maize cob, &c.) between selves by breaking it into pieces. 2. break sb.’s limb, bone, finger. 3. speak about people (in proverbs), blackmail others in their presence. 4. (of a crowd of people) run into, away, en masse; cf. omoka. unania, caus. rec. break in two; fold into two equal parts; divide into two. unanira, app. rec. r, bend, fold, over, upon; fold, together, up. 2. break sb.’s limb at. unaniria, caus. app. rec.; ~ njohi nyanja ĩno, put half the beer into this calabash. unanirira, inten. app. rec, fold down one upon another, for, at. unia, caus. lead, take, away from danger. funia, refl. caus. take refuge in flight; betake oneself off unobserved, by a back way; go into a corner. unithia, d. caus. 1. take away very early in the morning, hurry away into hiding (from danger or enemy attack). 2. divert (a stream, &c.); irrigate land. 3. help in breaking, have broken up. 4. (of a man in whose home neophytes live) hold initiation ceremonies; see una mogumo. runithia, refi. d. caus. 1. pretend to be gathering (firewood). 2. have (a leg) broken through carelessness. unithiria, app. d. caus. 1. divert to, into; ~ rOG igwa-inĩ, irrigate, divert the river into sugar-cane plantation (for |irrigation). 2. hurry away into hiding. unika, st. pot. 1. be broken, be breakable. 2. run away early from danger, go early, flee to safety, move off because of famine; ndokire ku~ ruoro rũtanatema, I got away before dawn. unĩkanga, diff. st. pot. (of |4 wood) break into shivers, be shattered; (of a spear) be broken into pieces. unikangira, app. diff. st. pot. be shivered at. unĩkana, rec. st. pot. be broken entirely. unikania, caus. |4 rec. st. pot. 1. break off completely. 2. take people to safety very early. unĩkanĩra, assoc. st. pot. I. snap, lf break, at the same time as others. 2. run to sb. elses home for safety. unikanĩria, caus. assoc. st. pot. take | 1 to safety together, for, at. unĩkika, d. st. pot. unikira, app. st. pot. I. run to, immigrate to, for safety. 2. break one’s limb at. 3. (of a tooth, &c.) be broken when being, pulled out, uprooted. unĩkĩria, caus. app. st. pot. unĩra, app. break for, at; ~ riitho, wink at. ĩfunira, refl. app. st. pot. unĩrira, inten. st. pot. 1. break |r up in addition to. 2. avoid sb. by taking to the roadside; divert one’s talk or walk. 3. leap in and out in a kĩbaata dance; dance round the ground in triumph after killing an enemy. ungra, conv. unfold; cf. runũra, refi. conv. (of a dress, &c.) unfold itself, stretch, straighten, itself. unoinũra, dim. conv. unũranga, diff. unũrana, rec. conv. unũrania, caus. rec. conv. Uungranira, app. rec. conv. ungraniria, caus. app. rec. conv. unũrithia, d. caus. conv. unũrika, st. pot. unũrĩra, app. conv. unfold, stretch, straighten, lengthen (a dress, &c.) by unfolding, for, at. unũririra, inten. conv. un- | n fold, unroll, stretch, in addition to what has already been unfolded, &c. unũka, neut. be unfolded, unbend.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. peel off, remove skin (of potatoes, bananas, fruits, &c.)
NEW_VALUE: peel off, remove skin (of potatoes, bananas, fruits, &c
FULL_ENTRY: ũa [ũwa], v.t. (1), 1. peel off, remove skin (of potatoes, bananas, fruits, &c.); shell beans. 2. strip plants for stringmaking. 3. remove fat of skinned animal from meat, &c. rũa!, refl, peel off easily, shed one’s skin; (the animal) is so fat that the fat peels off by itself; ta njoka, you look as fresh as a snake that has just shed its skin. ũoa, dim. ũoanga, diff. dim. Nũoana, rec. dim. ũoanegithia, d. caus. dim. keep on assisting to peel, strip, off; have some more peeled off. noangira, app. dim. ũoangirira, inten. dim.; ~ mboco, keep on shelling beans out of the pods. ũanga, diff. rec. diff. ũangithia, d. caus. diff. nangira, app. diff. inten. diff. peel off, shell, potatoes, beans, &c., in addition to what has already been shelled or peeled off. nana, rec.; ~ ciero, punish (small boys, &c.) by pinching their thighs. ũania, caus. rec. shell (beans, &c.), peel off, haphazardly. ũanĩra, app. rec. shell (beans, &c.), peel off, at the same time, for, ũaniria, caus. app. rec. shell, peel, together. ũithia, d. caus. assist to shell, peel, strip, off; have shelled, peeled off, stripped. fũithia, refl. d. caus. pretend to be shelling beans, stripping plants for fibre, peeling potatoes, when one should be, or is, doing sth. else. ũĩka, st. pot. ũĩkĩra, app. st. pot.; mboco ici itingĩ~ haha, it is impossible to shell these beans here (the container being too small or there being no time). ũira, app. shell, strip, rind, peel, for, at, into. ũĩrira, inten. shell additional beans, &c.; peel some more.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: take shelter (esp. from rain or sun), sit in the shade (on a hot day). diff. fũanira, assoc. take shelter together. iũanĩria, caus. assoc. caus. ĩũithia, d. caus. pretend to be taking shelter
NEW_VALUE: (11), take shelter (esp. from rain or sun), sit in the shade (on a hot day)
FULL_ENTRY: tũa? [iyũwa], v.i. (refi.) (11), take shelter (esp. from rain or sun), sit in the shade (on a hot day). diff. fũanira, assoc. take shelter together. iũanĩria, caus. assoc. caus. ĩũithia, d. caus. pretend to be taking shelter; take shelter when one should not do so. fũĩka, st. pot.; rina rĩting~ nĩ kchigha, it is so hot that it is impossible to shelter oneself. ĩũĩra, app. take shelter at.
riũa, see riũa.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. a peeling-off, shaving, strip, shred of meat, 2. fatty tissue between lean meat and outer skin
NEW_VALUE: a peeling-off, shaving, strip, shred of meat, 2
FULL_ENTRY: riũa [riũwa], moa [mooa], n. cl. 5/6 (u1), I. a peeling-off, shaving, strip, shred of meat, 2. fatty tissue between lean meat and outer skin; maguta ma ~, pure liquid fat obtained from the fatty tissues immediately underneath the outer layer of skin; romanĩrĩra ta gĩthita na ~, follow one another closely (like tail and fat); cf. ngai, mũroti, [ũa]
rũa!, n. (11), 1. hide, skin of animal; cf. ndarũa, 2. skin of human body; cf. grkonde. gĩcũa, i-; icũa, ma-, n. large hide. karũa, tũ-, n. membrane.
Ũcũ!, see
Ũcũ?, see inũ,
ŨcŨkũ, see cOkũ. see cawe.
mũgiti, ogiti [oogiti]; kĩũgiti, ci-; n. (1), aggressive, truculent person; cf. kĩnyogondi, mcthoga. n. cl. 11 only, provocation; aggressiveness, pugnacity; cf. ruurugi, rũnyũi. [ũgita]
-ŨGgigu, see under -Ũgi.
-ŨGgogi, see under -ũpgĩ. see under guo.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. soft meat at back of cow’s hind leg. 2. very special greeting between men who have shared this cut. ci-, n. (1), freak, monster
NEW_VALUE: soft meat at back of cow’s hind leg
FULL_ENTRY: mũgũ, mi-, n. (1v), 1. soft meat at back of cow’s hind leg. 2. very special greeting between men who have shared this cut. ci-, n. (1), freak, monster; any being or animal with a congenital deformity or abnormality (formerly used of albinos}; see kĩrema, mwĩtũkio. maũgũ, n. abnormality, disability, defect, blemish (of congenital type); ndarĩ na ~, he is industrious; cf. wathe, rema,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: Nandi rubber vine. 2. any stout liana or strong supple creeper used for ropes and for making hurdle doors for huts
NEW_VALUE: climbing shrub (Landolphia ugandensis), Nandi rubber vine
FULL_ENTRY: mũgũ, mi-, n. (1v) 1. climbing shrub (Landolphia ugandensis), Nandi rubber vine. 2. any stout liana or strong supple creeper used for ropes and for making hurdle doors for huts; cf. mokamwe.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: ri. be unbalanced, wander about, be unsettled
FULL_ENTRY: ũgũga [ũgũga] v.i. (1), ri. be unbalanced, wander about, be unsettled; cf. bũgũga, ũrũra, Ũngũtũka. 2. be beyond measure; athĩnĩte akoggga, he is very poor. Ũgogũga, dim. ganga, diff. Ũgggania, caus. rec. Ũgũganira, assoc. caus. assoc, caus, cause, to wander, to be unbalanced mentally. ũgũgithia, d. caus. app. wander about at, become unbalanced in.

NEW_VALUE: one who wanders, an unstable person
FULL_ENTRY: mũgũgi [mũgũgi], ogũgi [o0gũgij, n. (1), (usu. of mental disturbance) one who wanders, an unstable person.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. (of knives, &c.) be sharp, keen. 2. (of people) be clever, wise, educated, sensible, intelligent
FULL_ENTRY: ũhbiga, v.i. (11), 1. (of knives, &c.) be sharp, keen. 2. (of people) be clever, wise, educated, sensible, intelligent; be crafty, cunning. ũhohiga, dim. ũhohigia, caus. dim. make sharper, wiser. caus. app. dim. make sharper, for, at. Ũhiganga, diff. ũhĩgangia, caus. diff. app. diff. ũhĩgania, caus. rec. make clever. ũhĩganira, assoc. become clever, wise together; (of boys, &c.) grow together. inten. assoc. I1. swindle one another. 2. act clever (when sb. is doing the same to you). ũhiganiria, caus. assoc, gia, caus. make sharp; make clever’ educate, fũhĩgia, refl. caus. make oneself clever, pretend to be cleverer than one really is. ũhĩgithia, d. caus. make clever, sharp. ũhigika, st. pot. ũhigira, app. be sharp at, for; grow up at, for. ĩũhĩgira, refl. app. be clever for ones own advantage. ũhigirira, inten. 1. be able to foresee what sb.’s reactions will be so that you catch him unawares; use one’s wits, be wily, crafty, over; be wise about, clever at.

ũi / ũi-ũi / woi-wũi, interj. (U), exclamation of sympathy or dismay. meceũici, see maũigo, see Ũigo. maũiguano, see Ũiguano. maũikariri, see Ũikariri. maũikaro, see maũingihu, see ŨGingihu. ũiro, ~, n. (1), lubricating oil. [E. o1L] maGũiru, see Ũiru.

ũi [ũwi], v.t. (defective) (11?), know, recognize, assume, suppose; ngũ~ ati, I thought that. refl. have a good opinion of oneself. [ũiyo], pass. be known. Ũyana, rec., yaine, we know each other; mwĩyaine na n kuo?, whom do you know there? ũĩkana, rec. st. pot. become known; nĩkũikaine, it is common knowledge; nĩoĩkatine, he is a famous person.

NEW_VALUE: river. karũi, tũ-, n, stream, brook, runnel
FULL_ENTRY: rũi, njni / (N.K.) ndũi / njii, n. (111), river. karũi, tũ-, n, stream, brook, runnel. gacii, tũ-, n. 2nd dim. rivulet, brook.

(of people sitting or lying on others) get. off one another., ũkia, caus. tumefy, tumesce, erect (penis). ũkika, st, pot.
FULL_ENTRY: Ũka, v.i. (11), 1. come, come to (a place). 2. ~i muone!, (indicating wonder, surprise, disappointment, &c.) come and see (ye spirits)! ũkoka / okoka, dim. come near, nearer, closer. okokia, caus. dim. bring closer, nearer. ũkanga, diff. hurry up. Ũkania, caus. rec. ~ na, come with. ũkanira, assoc. come together, at the same time, with. (of people sitting or lying on others) get. off one another., ũkia, caus. tumefy, tumesce, erect (penis). ũkika, st, pot.; nĩ mbura, it will not be! possible to come because of the rain. ũkira!, app. come, through, eross, a place; pass by a place in coming.
gĩũkabi, see under mũkabi.
maũkamini, see Ũkamini.,
mũkano, n. cl. 3 only (111); -a ~, newly come, recently arrived, new; ũhoro wa ~, a new development; kĩndũ kĩa ~, a novelty, a recent importation. [ũka]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who has recently come to a place, a new arrival, a stranger.
NEW_VALUE: one who has recently come to a place, a new arrival, a stranger. [ũka]
FULL_ENTRY: mũki, oki [ooki], n. (11), one who has recently come to a place, a new arrival, a stranger. [ũka]
mũkia, see muokia.
ũki, maũki, n. (u1), 1. honey, honey mead; beer; cf. moruru, mũ- 2. (cl. 6) separate lots of honey, &c.

OŨkira?, v.i. (11), 1. stand, get, rise, up; rise, wake, up in the morning; ~ toro, rise up from sleep. 2. prosper, get rich, succeed in business. ũkokira, dim. rise up a little bit; (of a hill, &c.) be fairly high. ũkiranga, diff. rise quickly. ũkĩrangia, caus. diff. wake sb. quickly; assist to raise upright quickly. ũkĩrania, caus. wake sb. up, assist (sb., sth.) that has fallen down to stand up; ~ mũagũnda, make conditions so hard that the owner of a garden cannot continue cultivating it, evict sb. ũkiranĩra, assoc. rise, wake, up at the same time as. Ũkiranĩria, caus. assoc. cause (people, animals) to rise, wake, up at the same time., Ũkiria, caus. make rise, wake up; ~ mbũri, send goats to graze after the midday rest. rũkiria, refl. caus. (of a sick person, children, &c.) raise oneself up into a sitting position. ũkirithia, d, caus. ũkirĩka, st. pot. ũkirĩra, app. get up at, for; rise up against, attack (people). ũkĩriria, caus. app. I1. rise up from (one’s seat), give (a seat) to sb. else (to sit on); give up (one’s chairmanship) to sb. else. 2. be on a higher level, be elevated. 3. raise up a little. fũnkĩrĩria, refl. caus. app. regain mastery in wrestling, get rid of a weight which has trapped one; raise oneself up.
rĩũkĩro, mokĩro [mookĩro], n. (111), time, route, process, of coming. [ũka]
maũkuui, see
mũkũ, mi-, n. (1v), worthless fellow,
ũknũmũ, n. cl. o (11), 1. sentence, verdict, judgement. 2. insubordination; punishment, correction. [Sw. HUKUMU]

ŨCma, v.i. (11), 1. dry, harden, up; be become, hard, stiff, dry; wither, be parched; (of meat, sugar-cane, yams, &c.) be tough, 2. ~ ngoro, be difficult, unyielding, brave, hard-hearted; ~ ihu, be a dare-devil. ~ tha, give up, despair; ~ za indo, be hard to get anything out of; kĩkooto noko kioma, the truth (justice) is the criterion; nĩ kii kĩoma?, what is the nn woma?, who did not flinch (during circumcision operation)}?; gĩtaranio nĩkio kĩoma, worry is the worst thing for anybody. ũmoma, dim. ũmomia, caus. dim. harden a little more. d, caus. dim. thiria, app. d. caus., dim. ũmomirĩria, caus. app. dim. hang on a second, keep going; have patience, courage. Ũmanga, dif. ũmangia, caus. dif. refl. caus. diff. ũmangĩra, app. dif. ũmangiria, caus. app. diff. Ũmangiriria, inten. caus. diff. refl. inten. caus. diff. ũmana, rec. mania, caus. rec. Ũmanira, assoc. 1. dry at the same time as. 2. (of litigants, opponents, &c.) refuse to yield to each other. ũmaniria, caus. assoc. make many things dry together. rũmaniria, refl. caus. assoc. pluck up one’s courage and do one’s duty. ũmanirira, inten. rec, r. become drawn together into a hard, dry mass, shrivel up, become stiffened up together; solidify on, become a solid whole. 2. (of plants, crops, &c.) be stunted, dry up; (of people} be wizened, barren; (of liquids, e.g. gruel) stick to, solidify. ũmaniriria, caus. inten. rec. encourage, one another, each other. rũmaniriria, refi. caus. inten. rec, pull oneself together, brace oneself. ũrnanithĩriria, d. caus. inten. rec. be irresponsible, insensible, to. mia, caus. make stiff, stiffen, harden; let dry, make dry. refl. caus. 1. (of plants, &c.) dry up without apparent reason. 2. harden oneself; put on a bold face, pluck up one’s courage, stand firm, make a stand against, put up a resistance. Ũmithia, d, caus. have sth. dried up, assist in drying sth. up. Ũmithiria, app. d. caus. have sth. dried up, for, at. st. pot. ũmira, app. 1. be hard, severe, to, upon; be unyielding, inflexible towards; withstand, resist. 2. (of things) be too hard for. 3. become inured, resigned to; ~ tha, despair (of finding sth. one has been looking for). inten. harden, dry (out) on to; stiffen, harden up; (of flowers) get hard and stay on a tree without shedding petals. ũmirĩria, caus. inten. 1. harden oneself to, bear pain, meet danger; be brave, cheer up, take heart, endure. 2. be bold, forward, presumptuous, insolent, audacious. 3. encourage, instil courage into; stiffen, give firmness to. riria, refi. caus. inten. cheer oneself up, take courage, take a firm grip, stand firm, persevere, endure.

mũma, oma [ooma], n. (11), (used only in compounds): r. ~-iku | ~ rhu, hard-hearted, pitiless person. 2. ~-tha, callous person. n. cl. 14 only, (in compounds) 1. ~-iku, desperation. 2. ~-tka, callousness. [ma]
-ũmirĩru, a. (1v), 1. firm, stiff, bard. 2. brave, courageous. 3. forward, bold, audacious, insolent. [ũma]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: thenge ~, he-goat with fore-quarters of lighter shade than rest of body (light red compared with brown or dark red).
NEW_VALUE: thenge ~, he-goat with fore-quarters of lighter shade than rest of body (light red compared with brown or dark red)
FULL_ENTRY: kiũmũ ci-, n. (11), thenge ~, he-goat with fore-quarters of lighter shade than rest of body (light red compared with brown or dark red).

ũmũ, (maeũgũmũ), n. (11), 1. hardness, dryness; endurance, valour; mũthaiga wa ~, medicine to stimulate bravery; gĩtĩ kĩa ~, a seat of the mighty; cf. -ũmũ. 2. hard stodge eaten on first day of preparation for circumcision; cf. ngima. 3. uncooked gruel blown from mouth over women by male neophytes at circumcision. 4. dun-colour. [ũma]
-ũmũ, a. (11), 1. hard, stiff. 2. dry, parched, withered; (of meat) tough. 3. (coll.) mũnda big-wig, brasshat.
kiũmũcii, see under mũcii.
ũmũthi, see GmMŨAthi.

ũmba!:, v.t. (11), 1. mould, shape, form; ~ nyũngũ, make pots; ~ icua, form a heap with refuse (from sacrifice) for burning ceremonially; ~ guoya, apply skin, fur (to a burn); ~ ndua mũhũra, mould wax on to the lip of a beer gourd. 2. (of God) create. rũmba, refl. sit apart, alone; keep to oneself, be concerned with one’s own affairs; be content, satisfied, with one’s lot; cf. rnuna, rikaria. ũmbomba, dim. ũmbombana, rec. dim. ũmbombia, caus. dim. ũmbombithia, d. caus. dim. ũmbombirira, inten. dim. ũmbanga, diff. mbangia, caus. dif.; ~ njohi mũhũra, put a wax funnel on a large beer calabash quickly. d. caus. diff, have sth. moulded quickly, assist sb. to mould for some time. ũmbangiria, caus. app. dim. ũmbania, caus. rec. mould anyhow. ũmbanira, app. rec. mould at the same time as sb. else, mould for sb, else. ũmbaniria, caus. app. rec. mould (things), at the same time, together. ũmbithia, d. caus. st. pot. ũmbira, app. ũmbirĩra, inten. mould some more in addition to; crowd around sb. or sth. ũmbũra, conv. undo a moulding before it dries; ~ r&hĩ, give a heavy smack. ũmbũka, neut. (of a moulding) disintegrate. v.t. (11), 1. (of birds, &c.) alight, settle, perch, upon; (of bees) mass, cluster, together in a swarm (on a tree, &c.). 2. (as aux.) settle down to; tũkĩ~ o kwĩrorera, (having nothing else to do) we began to watch. ũmbomba, dim. ũmbombũra, conv, see ũmbũra. rira, app. conv. dim. neut. dim. ũmbombũkia, caus. neut. dim.; see ũmbũkithia. ũmbanga, diff. ũmbanira, app. rec. I. alight together. 2. (of two or more persons) side together against one person, fall upon sb., attack or argue against one opponent; unite over a task and do it, lift a heavy object together. ũmbira, app. alight, perch, at. inten. (of a crowd, &c.) crowd round, attack (sb., sth.); flock after; pile at, beside, upon; swarm on to. rũmbirĩra, refl. inten. app. set off, run away, be frightened away. ũmbũra, conv. r. make fly, raise (dust). 2. (of boys) dance from household to household to get food and to warn parents that they are ready for initiation. 3. eat and finish suddenly. 4. strike, threaten to strike, a hard blow; akĩmũ~ iteke, he kicked him hard. 5. ward off evil by drinking from last-filled gourd of beer. ũmbũro, pass. conv. Mũmbia na njũgũma, he who refuses food offered by men is chased away with clubs. rũmbũra, refl. conv.; rghia ria kw~, the first horn of beer to set one on one’s feet. ũmbũranga, diff. conv. ũmbũrangira, app. diff. conv. ũmbũrana, rec. conv. I1. (of birds, &c.) make one another take wing, startle each other to flight. 2, (of dust) arise. 3. fight each other vigorously; ~ ngundi, box hard. ũmbũranira, app. rec. conv. fight, because of, at; cause to fight at the same time; raise dust for each other, others. Ombũraniria, caus. assoc, cony. 1, make (objects) take off, fly up simultaneously. 2. strike hard together. ũmbũrithia, d. caus. conv. 1. make fly up, take wing; have dust raised up. 2, chase a vanquished victim away. Ũmbũrithania, rec. d. caus, conv. chase one another along, across, in flight. ũmbũrithiria, app. d. caus. conv. r. defeat and chase (bulls, &c.) at. 2. have dust raised up upon sb.; have birds made to take wing for sb. ũmbũrĩka, st, pot. conv, ũmbũrĩra, app. conv. make take wing, fly, take off, at, for; raise dust, for, at. ĩũmbũrira, refl. app. conv. ũmbũriria, caus. app. conv. inten. conv. ũmbũneut. inten. (of a large number of birds, locusts, &c.) take wing gradually from one side of the field to the other. ũmbũrũrũkira, app. neut. inten. ũmbũka, neut. (of birds, aeroplanes, persons, &c.) fly up, take wing, take off, set off, rise up and go; (of dust) rise up into the air, be blown up. ũmbũkanga, dif. neut. ũmbũkangia, caus. diff. neut. raise dust rapidly, quickly; winnow (millet) rapidly for some additional time. ũmbũkania, caus. rec. neut. ŨmMbũkanira, assoc. neut. 1.(of aeroplanes) take off together. 2. (of dust) rise up into the air at the same time on to sb. Cmbũkaniria, caus. assoc. neut. 1. make (aeroplanes, birds, &c.) take off simultaneously. 2. raise dust on to sb., at the same time. caus., neut. I1. cause to fly. 2. raise dust. ũmbũkithia, d. caus. neut. let fly accidentally, cause, help to cause to fly, frighien (birds, &c.) away. ũmbũkĩka, st. pot. neut. ũmbũkira, app. neut. 1r. fly from, at. 2. get up suddenly and start on a task. ũmbũkiria, caus. app. neut. pake fly at, for; raise dust on to sb., sth.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I. anything moulded or constructed, a roughly built hut, framework of hut roof constructed on ground and lifted into position, hutch trap for serval cat or leopard
NEW_VALUE: low ridge to prevent rain-water entering hut door. 2
FULL_ENTRY: kĩũmba, ci-, n. (11), I. anything moulded or constructed, a roughly built hut, framework of hut roof constructed on ground and lifted into position, hutch trap for serval cat or leopard; cf. kraga, krĩna; see nynũmba. 2. enclosed nest of Weaver bird, &c. 3. (mod.) room in house. nyũmba?, n. 1. low ridge to prevent rain-water entering hut door. 2. low mound, ridge of ploughed furrow. komba, tũmba, n. dim, of above.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: one who is attractive to the female sex, who seeks and wins their favours
NEW_VALUE: flirting, gallantry, love-making, success in wooing
FULL_ENTRY: mũmbani, ombani; / kĩũmbani, ci-; n. (111), one who is attractive to the female sex, who seeks and wins their favours; a gallant, fiirt; cf. monyanya. ŨGũmbani, man. 1. flirting, gallantry, love-making, success in wooing.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. creature, handiwork, created thing, natural object
FULL_ENTRY: kĩũmbe, ci-, n. (1v), 1. creature, handiwork, created thing, natural object; ~ kĩa Ngai, God’s creature; cicmbe na maheni, real and counterfeit, human and superior objects contrasted with inferior types. 2. (used adv.) expressly, really and truly; nĩ ũndũ wake ~, expressly for him (her); nĩ yake ~, it is his very own; nĩ mũndũ ~ nyũ, he is a real man, he is; ~, I myself, cf. -ene; ĩ, ti ithera ngi ~, so you have actually eaten flies. tũmbe, n. cl. 14 only, creation, being created.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: I1. potter, maker, moulder (usu. a woman in case of clay pots). 2. a name for God. 3. a woman’s name
FULL_ENTRY: mũmbi, ombi, n. (111), I1. potter, maker, moulder (usu. a woman in case of clay pots). 2. a name for God. 3. a woman’s name; name of the mythical mother of the Kikuyu people whose ten daughters give names to the Kikuyu clans; see Gĩkũyũ, mũhĩrĩga. ũmbi, n. cl. 14 only, forming, shaping, creating. (ombia), n. (111), one who refuses kindness, &c. ~ arme ombragwo na one who refuses sth. offered by men (an insolent act) gets beaten by clubs. rũmbia, nyũmbia, n. boycott, strike. [ũnmba?]
Gũmbo!, n. cl. 14 (111), creation, formation. [ũmba:] n. cl. 14 (111), (of a bird) place to settle. [ũmba?]
rũmbo, see rũnũmba? under rũ
rũmburura (nyũmburura), n. (v), (N.K.) I. gnats, midges; cf. rĩmuru. 2. spider’s web.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: (N.K.) fine large tree with big blunt barnacle-like protuberances on the bark and a wealth of green leaves
NEW_VALUE: () fine large tree with big blunt barnacle-like protuberances on the bark and a wealth of green leaves
FULL_ENTRY: mũmbũ, mi-, n. (11), (N.K.) fine large tree with big blunt barnacle-like protuberances on the bark and a wealth of green leaves; roimba ta ~, may you flourish like the ‘green bay tree’; cf. mogumo, moruri.
Ũni, maũni, n. (11), heart of tree, pith. [ũnũra]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: act uncharitably, spitefully, cruelly, towards sb.
FULL_ENTRY: ũnũha, v.t. (11), act uncharitably, spitefully, cruelly, towards sb.; spoil, destroy wantonly, injure, deface, damage; ~ mũndũa kindũ, destroy sth. so that a person may not profit by it; ~ mũndũ, deprive sb. of his life by magic or witchcraft. fũnũha, refl. I1, commit suicide. 2. spite oneself (by wantonly destroying one’s own property). Ũnonũha, dim. ũnũhanga, diff. ũnũhana, rec. r., deprive a young person of his life (so that he may not profit by living longer). 2. conceal profitable information from each other. 3. cause mutual destruction. ũnũhanira, app. rec. niria, caus. app. rec. destroy together. ũnũhbithia, d. caus. hika, st. pot. ũnũhira, app. destroy at, for. refl. app. I1. commit suicide, at, for. 2. destroy one’s property, intentionally, unintentionally.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: extravagant, that which, he who, wastes.
NEW_VALUE: extravagant, that which, he who, wastes
FULL_ENTRY: -ũnũhi, a. (I1), extravagant, that which, he who, wastes. [ũnũha]

kiũnũhu, ci-, n. (x), meanness, niggardliness; ill will, spite, uncharitableness; destructiveness, wanton mischief; wa ~, malicious, spiteful, person; ~ kĩa indo ciene, covetousness. ũnũhu, n. cl. 14 only, mischief; destructiveness.

ũnũra, v.t. (1), peel, strip, bark (off a tree, &c.); remove skin (from a wound). refl. 1. (of reptiles) cast off old skin; (of paint in a house, &c.) peel off; remove skin (from one’s wound). 2. show off, be conceited; cf. ũnonũra, dim. ũnũranga, diff. ũnũrana, rec.; ~ na tiboko, whip, strike, each other, heavily with whips, &c. Ũnũrania, caus. rec. peel entirely, anyhow; ~ mbembe, take the husks off the maize cob. ũnũranira, app. rec. I. peel for each other, others; peel at the same time as sb. else. 2. (coll.) fight, at, for. ũnũraniria, caus. app. rec. 1. peel, strip bark off, at, for. 2. (coll.) buy together, simultaneously. ũnũrithia, d. caus. have peeled, assist in peeling. ũnũrithiria, app. d. caus. Ũnũrika, st. pot. Ũnũrĩra, app. peel for, at. refl, 1. peel oneself, at, for; cast old skin off. 2. (of child) cry (to mother, &c.) for nothing, cry for the moon; tiga kwĩ~, stop crying (for nothing) to me. ũnũrũrũka, neut. inten. become widely, completely, horribly, peeled, skinned, off. Ũnũka, v.i, peel off. a, (11), peeled. [ũnũra]
rĩũnũro, monũro [moonũro], n. (x1), (usually cl. 6) peeling; bark peeled or cut off a tree. [ũnũra]

ũnda, v.t. (11), 1. make up into lumps; make, form, mould; knead (dough) into a lump. 2. smelt pig iron, make (iron) into a pig; mould (an axe, &c.); eat with gusto, stuff food into the mouth and munch it with satisfaction. ũndonda, dim. ũndanga, diff. Cndana, rec. ũndania, caus. rec. ũndanira, app. rec. caus. app. rec. d. caus. ũndika, st. pot. ũndira, app. 1. mould at. 2. stuff (food) into the mouth. Ũndĩria, caus. stuff (food) into the mouth. ũndũra, conv. I. eat hungrily, eat with gusto. 2. smash. ũndgria, caus. conv. enjoy eating food.
ũndi / ndi, num. (u), (arch.) (used only in counting) one; cf. -mwe.
mũndiriri, mi-, n. (v), train, string, queue of people, &c.; cf. niriro, mũtongoro, mũgokoro, mũkwĩri.
(ka-)(ki-)(ma-)Gndũ, see under Ũndũ.

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. swarm of bees, locusts, or warrior ants on the march

FULL_ENTRY: mũndũ, mi-, n. (1), 1. swarm of bees, locusts, or warrior ants on the march; cf. gĩthũa. 2. lump, fistful; lump of prepared clay; cf. rrnmba. 3. lump of smelted iron; cf. gĩkama. 4. ball of cotton; burst head of bulrush. 5. lump of cooked food; cf. moũndwa. 6. substance exuded from pores of skin when rubbed. 7. mundia ~, (N.K.) go in file, procession. [ũnda]

(esp. of women) fight each other hard. caus. rec. 1. beat sb. haphazardly. 2. gobble food. ũndũthanira, app. rec. fight each other, at, because of. ũndũthanĩria, caus. app. rec. Ũndgũthĩra, app. I. beat sb. at. 2. cat, at, for. ci-, n. (vinr), heap, pile. [ũndũtha]

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. lump of cooked food prepared for a journey. 2. lump of prepared pot clay. 3. pig of smelted iron. 4. mundia ~, go in file
NEW_VALUE: lump of cooked food prepared for a journey
FULL_ENTRY: mũndwa, mi-, n. (v), 1. lump of cooked food prepared for a journey. 2. lump of prepared pot clay. 3. pig of smelted iron. 4. mundia ~, go in file; cf. moũndũ. [ũnda]

(1v), ĩ. anything which attracts, a magnet; mũthaiga wa ~, a magical powder or medicine for attracting good fortune, wealth, admirers, &c. 2. acquisitive, greedy, rapacious person, kĩũnga, ci-, n.; ũrotuĩka ~ Ũngage airĩtu, may you be a gatherer and attract the young ladies (part of the blessing of initiates). kiũngungui, see ngungui,

ORIGINAL_VALUE: 1. refuse from winnowing grain, chaff, husks P crushed dry leaves

FULL_ENTRY: mũnjũ, mĩ-, n. (1v), sp. of banana (yellow when ripe and with harder and drier fruit than usual).

Oragania, caus, rec. break in entirety. ũraganira, app. kill, sb., each other, for, at; break sth. for each other, others. ũraganiria, caus. app. rec. Ũragira, app. kil, break, for, at. Ũragirĩra, inten. break some more (and add to what is already there).

mũro, mi-, n. (111), 1. running away, flight. 2. perdition, destruction (of life). [ũra]
